reviews.comment: Gorgeous and tall
She is a great teacher and when your'e online you never really see her at all but we only receive a few assignments and we have a month to finish them!

This class was intense. It is VERY lecture and exam heavy. Hoursssss of lectures each week, maybe if the lecture wasn't online I would have enjoyed it more but unfortunately it wasn't. He says he doesn't like to trick with exam questions but felt pretty tricky sometimes. He was really nice and funny though. Office hours were helpful!

One of the funniest professors I ever had. I failed this class the first time and when I came back there was no judgement, just pure enthusiasm for me trying again. I was really encouraged in this class. Lovely TA's too. Put in the work and you will pass!!

Where do I begin? In all of my years of going to school, middle school, high school, college etc., Peter is without a doubt one of the best teachers I've ever had. Do yourself a favor and try to get Peter. I had for Human Anatomy and was genuinely one of the most fun I've ever had in class.

Peter is such a great professor!! Really cares about students success and gives lots of extra credit opportunities in lab AND lecture. Very fast paced and heavy workload for a summer course but manageable if you stay on top of it. He is very funny and friendly and tests are do-able if you really pay attention to the lectures and take notes/study.

Peter made this course very enjoyable and doable! Took it over the summer (6 weeks) so super fast and intense but he made it a great experience. He truly cares to get to know his students and help them succeed. He gives many opportunities for extra credit and drops lowest quiz, etc. Lab uses models and cadavers, lectures are online. HE'S AMAZING!

The amazing professor taught both Anatomy/ Physiology. Hardest classes I have taken as a student in DSPS. OFFICE HOURS SAVED ME! The best teacher wants you to learn! You have to put in the work! As a student in DSPS, I studyed 6 hours a day, every day. Had to quit my job for Anatomy, but worth it! :) The Kindest and Funnest Professor!

Peter is fantastic. Hands down the anatomy professor to take at SBCC. Extremely engaging and fun in lectures and lab. Very fair grading and exams all were representative of the material covered in lecture. Tutors are all great in his sections. Really enjoyed this class and would 100% take him again.

Peter is an amazing teacher. This class is difficult, thats what youre signing up for but he will do his absolute best at making sure you learn it all. Not only is he hilarious but you can tell he puts his heart and soul into his students. He is very engaging and tries to make sure everyone succeeds. Be ready to laugh and learn with him.

My favorite professor at SBCC. He is very organized and gives outlines for each lecture. His exams are difficult, but he gives you all the resources to succeed. He is super personable, knowledgeable, and just a funny guy. You will have to dedicate much time and effort to the class to get an A. Overall, highly recommend taking Peter!!

Peter is such an enthusiastic&engaging professor making Anatomy SO much more fun! He understands that this course takes alot of work& provides students w/ multiple resources to get help that they need.He's passionate abt material & always finds a way to make students laugh in lectures&lab! Drops a couple of yr lowest grades,but always give yr 100%!

If you are looking for an easy A, this isn't it. Peter's class is absolutely hard, and you have to put in a lot of work to get an A. That being said, he makes his lectures interesting as well as his labs. Expect multiple quizzes a week. He also offers lots of extra credit and is very understanding towards personal situations.

I miss having class with Peter, he is easily the best teacher I've ever had. He makes anatomy seem like an easy course. I personally liked a lot the summary classes he did before the exams, those were gold! He is an awesome teacher and a great person!

Best professor ever. Cried my first day of this class because I felt so unprepared and I tried to drop- ended up with an A. He makes a horribly difficult class the most enjoyable thing ever, wish every professor was like him.

I'm going to admit I'm a bad test taker and I didn't do well in that area but that's on me so be prepared for that when taking his course. Other than that he explains the material very well, in a way you could understand and makes it fun too. One of the best professors out there fr, I would take his class again 10/10

Peter is not only a terrific professor, but also a kind and understanding person. Labs are fun, and lectures are long but engaging. I definitely recommend this class if you are disciplined and willing to work hard. The material is hard but he offers a ton of help and really wants students to succeed. One of my favorite classes at SBCC.

Peter is easily the best professor I have ever had at SBCC. Physiology is not an easy course, but he makes the material fun and interesting. Labs are super fun, and he is always accessible outside of class during office hours. Getting an A is doable as long as you put in the work. Go to OH, see the tutors, and you will do great!
Great professor! Lectures are pretty information heavy, but outline what you need to know for tests and in general, so the class is not awful if you take the time to study.

Peter is an amazing person and professor! He is super helpful with explaining during lectures. When I would have questions he simplified it and made it easy to understand. Always rewatch lectures because a lot of what he says during lecture and almost all of what is on his outlines are on the exams. There's ec in all of his lab quizzes too!

Anatomy is a very hard class but I am so glad I took it with Peter. You WILL need to study!!! I took hybrid so the lecture is online and there are 3 exams (each worth ~100 points) and they are proctored. Also like 6 WUQs which are 15 points and proctored based on the lectures. Study lab material bc every class you have a lab quiz worth 15 points.

Peter is nice and the class is easy enough. However, he often went over time on lecture and still wouldn't get to all the material. He'd tell us to "study on our own" for the sections he didn't have time to teach. Felt like poor time management and feels unfair to test something you didn't make time to teach.

Peter is hands down the best professor I have ever had! He really wants his students to succeed. His lectures contain a lot of information so you have to make sure that you are paying attention and never miss a class. If you put in the effort this class can be not only very interesting but also a lot of fun.

I took BMS 107 last semester with Peter and he was an amazing teacher. This class is definitely test heavy and point based. If you don't procrastinate and organize your time well you can definitely succeed. There will never be anything on an exam or quiz that he does not include in the lectures.

Amazing teacher, really cares about his students succeeding. Although the class is very difficult, he does offer a lot of help. Lab quizes every week and lecture quizes every other week. Don't miss class, you won't pass. There are 3 huge exams, tests pretty much reflect your grade.

Simply put, the best professor and one of the best people I've ever met, IF HE'S AVAILABLE TAKE HIM!!! Seriously changed my life and made a class that's supposed to be stressful one of my all time favorite courses, Peter is straight up the GOAT!!!!!
Peter is great, probably my favorite professor I've had. Class in itself is very difficult but he is funny and cares and is a great teacher

Very tough class and material, but Peter does everything in his power to make sure that students can succeed and understand the material. Expectations are clear. Organization is spectacular and he really cares about his students.

Peter was so caring! Anatomy is a tough class but he made it so much fun and has an amazing sense of humor. You have lab quizzes every week and lecture quizzes every two weeks with a couple exams. STUDY!! you have to be willing to put in the work! great professor and such a cool class, 10/10.
He was a great guy who was always willing to help

Peter is engaging, enthusiastic, and excels at explaining material in a digestible manner. The class is challenging, but, the materials to succeed are provided. There is a team of tutors that are available on a daily basis. Peter def puts in a lot of work to give students an engaging course; he expects them to put in the work to succeed. 10/10

peter himself is a really cool guy. BUT DONT take this class if you're a bad test taker, your grade is determined by test performance. Not very clear with what will be on exams, especially in the online format. I know many in my class who did WELL on quizzes, lab reps and the HW and barley passed/ didnt pass at all. very test heavy.
Great teacher, especially good if you're passionate about film, Fun classes while also learning a lot.

He's amazing. He gives so much flexibility (don't take advantage) and is so caring to every single student. The discussions he brings into class are so interesting and I wish I had participated more. He bought coffee and biscotti to our classes. Such a cool guy and down to earth!
Learned a lot, had a great time. Really recommend this professor if you like film.
You watch a movie a week and get a question to answer. Pretty easy and simple structure. Albright is a good teacher.

Fun teacher, poorly executed class structure. In-person class is entirely slideshows, with all discussion being online. Would be a good class if there were structured in-person discussions. If you want a free A, this class is for you. If you want a free A and the chance to have real discussions with other movie lovers, you'll be disappointed.
I very much enjoyed his class. Very thorough and explanatory. His lectures were easy to follow and assignments were fun in ways. Basically just watched movies and wrote about them based on a prompt.

Although I found the coursework not that interesting, Professor Albright does a great job making it engaging and thought provoking. Barely any classwork besides a midterm and a final paper. Would recommend for an easy A.

I had a bad family emergency, and he let me make up my late work during the week I had to take off. I recommend this professor, he is a great man and I really enjoyed the movies and material he teaches! 10/10 professor, just chef's kiss!

Very clear week by week set up. Essays weren't too challenging and he is a fairly easy grader. Some of the assigned articles were a little long but nothing awful. Great selection of films, he has a good mix of classic older movies and recent ones as well.

I don't know why Professor Albright has such good reviews. His documentary film class is extremely boring, with the majority of the films being 2 hours. Lots of them are in different languages or silent, making them very tedious to get through. I am currently registered for another one of his classes, but think I'll drop.

Recommend Professor Albright 100%. Took the class fall of 2022 as an elective (online). Understanding and not a hard grader. Quizzes are straight forward. Course was only "time consuming" because you must watch the films. Easy discussions involved.
Take him he is awesome!
Great professor, nothing else to say. Take his class and you won't regret it!
He is great!
Film 101 was a fun class! I enjoyed the movies Dr. Albright had us watch as well as his online format of the class.

He was really nice and chose great films to show us. He never made you feel like your interpretation was wrong and actually encouraged new perspectives. The structure of the class was great and easy to follow.

This was a great class! This class was excellently structured. You knew what to expect every week. Since everyone saw something different in the films, this allowed for interesting conversations and no wrong answers.

Michael is an absolutely amazing professor. He is super clear and lays everything out in a powerpoint for you to read and digest. There is a weekly discussion about the movie and a final paper at the end of the class and LOTS of extra credit. Super easy and super fun.

Great and caring professor. Lenient with due dates and gives a week grace period before docking pts for late work. Assignments were all discussion posts or 3-question-quizzes; they were fairly easy. Gave good feedback for thesis assignment so that you can see what he's expecting for your final essay. Be aware that you can't choose what you watch.

I took FS101H and FS107 with Albright and I absolutely loved his classes. The films we watched were great and the homework was always pretty easy. He made me have a new appreciation for filmmaking. Each class had a final paper that was based on past discussion posts so it was pretty easy. I'm thinking of taking another class of his. BEST PROF EVER!

I really think Professor Albright cares about his students and what he teaches. He is extremely flexible in turning assignments in and getting the work done, especially with COVID. He picks educational but interesting films to watch and I really felt like I learned a lot from his class. Would take all of his classes if I could!

Michael Albright is an A + teacher- He is the Best. He is respected and he is respectful to his students. He grades quickly too. But what I liked best of Prof. Albright is he is totally present with you. He is sincere when he engages you. I give him an A for absolutely wonderful, amazing and astounding to learn from. Zangi

Not many bad things to say about Albright! Clearly very passionate about what he is teaching, lectures can be a little dry sometimes but manageable. Three papers he grades fairly throughout semester. Answers to weekly quizzes can be found in slides or textbook and very understanding dude

Prof Albright is amazing. I took online summer class with him and he is very organized and aware with students who do the work. There is no final or essays and the quizzes are so easy he tells you where everything is located at IN the question! you have presentations and light readings and YOU NEVER USE THE BOOK! Perfect class for an easy A.

By far the most caring professor I've ever had. He is passionate about film which makes his class very easy to follow along in. He made things so much easier during the crisis this year by checking in through email and extending deadlines. He will personally email you if you're falling behind. He really wants his students to succeed. TAKE HIM!

Albright was an awesome professor and guy! Before coronavirus I probably went to his lectures like half the time and still had an A+ cause he puts everything online. Make sure you really put effort into your final paper and don't forget about lecture quizzes and you'll be totally fine.

Mr. Albright is a really caring teacher who wants the best for his students and provides plenty of EC assignments. The lectures can get pretty boring, however, and sometimes he trails off from his point. There's not a lot of collaboration with peers, and a lot of the work is busy work. You basically learn everything in the first 2 weeks. Great guy.

I loved going to Professor Albrights class. He cares about his students and is always willing to go the extra mile to help them succeed. His lectures highlight interesting aspects of the film industry, eras, and/or the in-class screenings. You wont regret taking his class!

Professor Albrights passion for Film is remarkable. He always provides his students with fascinating and relevant readings and films. I highly recommend taking his class(es) if you have the chance to!

Professor Albright is a great teacher and has a doctorates degree. His class is fun and you will learn interesting things about film studies. Lectures can be boring sometimes, but if you attend most of the time you can get an A.

I never thought that I would find a really good professor . He is very knowledgeable, he helps his students a lot. He puts all his effort to make sure his students become successful, Thomas is strict but he has a very good sense of humor. It is sad he is not teaching grammar 5. I miss the way he taught.

I am an international student. Although Thomas is my esl writing teacher, but i learn a lot of things from him, not only writing but knowledge. I like him very much, he is strict but humor. I feel depressed that he doesn't teach the class that I am gonna take next semester, otherwise I definitely will take his class for sure. Like him so much haha!
he is awesome. very nice, helpful and you can learn a lot from his class but you have to work for it
He is an excellent teacher, explain very well.
excellent teacher!!!
The best english teacher ever!
He is the best English teacher ever. You learn a lot with him. He makes many emphasis in pronunciation. His classes are just in the night. And he is hot!!
The best teacher!!!

IF YOU WANT TO LEARN GRAMMAR ...HE IS THE ONE! he's really helpfull and very educational. Carefull !!! because for this class you need to study a lot !!! He forced us to do our homework everyday, you wouldn't want to not attend his class because he takes it personally....

He's the best professor. IF YOU WANT TO LEARN GRAMMAR ...HE IS THE ONE! he's really helpfull and very educational. Carefull !!! because for this class you need to study a lot !!! He forced us to do our homework everyday, you wouldn't want to not attend his class because he takes it personally....

He's the best professor ever he's really helpfull and very educational. At the same time he uses his book as a bible....carefull !!!, because for this class you need to study a lot !!! He forced us to do our homework everyday, you wouldn't want to not attend his class because he takes it personally....IF YOU WANT TO LEARN GRAMMAR ...HE IS THE ONE!

He's the best professor ever he's really helpfull and very educational. At the same time he uses his book as a bible....carefull !!!, because for this class you need to study a lot !!! he forced to do your homework everyday , you wouldn't want to not attend his class because he takes it personally....IF YOU WANT TO LEARN GRAMMAR ...HE IS THE ONE!
Love the guy. Doesn't assign too much work in Marketing 101 Class. Enjoyable and informative experience overall.

Wouldn't recommend this professor. He doesn't know his content and is basically just reading information from the internet or Lynda. The assignment instructions are veeeeery unclear, and he does not give you back any feedback
I love this professor!
Jeff has a lot of knowledge in the finance field. Had a great time in his class and would love to take another class taught by this instructor.

Jeff is a great professor that is very knowledgeable in his field study. Cares a lot about his students and has a great sense of humor. You'll gain a lot of valuable information from his lectures and class. Always keeps the class alive and going. His class is not for slackers, so expect to do some work.

A Young professor who can relate to students. He works hard to get things done but makes you wait very long to hear from him, therefore, you are given the chance to ask and communicate with him during class hours. If you do the homework and attend class, you are good to go. It is not an easy A, you have to earn it. learned a lot! Jeff is awesome!

Jeff is unique in the way that he is also a student, works in his field of study, and teaches about it. He is extremely easy to get along with, and his experience in finance is well utilized especially when answering questions. The weekly homework is simple, and a large part of your grade. If you want to get the most out of the class, ask questions

This professor is not technologically inclined and had to ask students for help in setting up the class. He doesn't update assignments or syllabus just lazily slaps everything together and then when assignments are not there, says "If it's not there don't do it". Assignments are not clear and useless. Does not respond to email or moodle.

Horrible teacher. He makes the class seem like it's the easiest thing in the world the first day of session. Then we get to the homework, and it does not relate to students' life at all. I took this class to better understand finance, and I've walked out of it not understanding anything. Horrible, horrible, horrible
CIS258: Not the most interactive, but that doesn't mean hes a bad prof. The grading scale gives you basically 10% extra credit, which is incredibly helpful.
Took CIS258: Great professor with an extra credit system that basically gives you a 10% boost. Stay on track and you'll do well.

this guy is ridiculous with his tedious, strict grading. Every assignment is posted online, and there are so many little details you must pay attention to. He is a good person, but his grading is just abysmal.
It's a good class,but there's a lot of assignments. Also, some of the assignments are answering short answer questions where you need to provide a short write-up or summary.

will give you a zero on assignments for the smallest errors even if you did the assignment and did it correctly. The work load is EXTREMELY overwhelming and very time consuming. If you are taking a full load DO NOT take this class, you are expected to spend 16 hours and more on this course
Grades extremely overly harsh and will give you points off even if you fulfill the rubric criteria. Makes assignments super confusing with unclear instructions.

Very quick to give 0 points for the most minor of differences in interface despite functionality. Cookie cutter course with outdated text/typos left in videos and assignments. Strict and with little feedback and takes every opportunity to withhold as many points as possible.

Lectures were presented as a video with doodles on the first class of week then the rest of the class time was spent as working periods. Learned the material by practicing with it which I enjoyed. If you need this class for your businesss major, I'd recommend this one. Probably 2-3 hours of work/week which I completed in class.

Very responsive to questions, including evenings and weekends. Approachable. Graded assignments/tests timely. Put effort into making an online class personal. Helpful how-to videos. Expert knowledge. Great instructor.

This teacher has a terrible way of setting up his assignments and it is all a web discussion based class. On one of the final assignments someone did something wrong in the discussion the made me get a worse grade. Easy class terrible teacher

First real online course and Professor Bek made it a wonderful experience. Really puts a lot of time into his lesson plans, and wants to make sure you're understanding the material and succeeding. He supplies a lot of useful material to supplement the reading. Weekly assignments are pretty easy, but midterms and final is difficult.
Great caring professor. Knows his material and work very hard to help you understand the material and assignments.

I was pretty unsure if I could start CIS since I have background in completely different area. I am a foreigner so it was very important for me to know that I could switch my educational direction. Not only that he helped me in the early phase in SBCC but has always been extremely supportive. I am sorry I dont have to much space here to thank him.

Some of the work seems like busy work and the software downloads can be daunting but he knows a lot about a lot of useful things. The exercises do give you experience with skills that programmers and database managers need . Despite the downsides, I've taken many classes with him and learned a lot, despite the occasional aggravation.

Professor Bek is a very smart guy, but he's also eager to sound smart. In this, he loses a lot of people because he talks about things that make him feel smart instead of teaching the material. He's not understanding when it comes to working with students as he tacked me a grade whenever my grandmother died and I couldn't attend class.

His class isn't bad at all, pretty darn easy. Yes his power points can be boring but he gives you good examples of the material you cover. His test are open book which is awesome! The HW you do is for the most part all done in class when your in the lab and he helps you go through it step by step. Easy class!!!

There were several occasions where I had to break down my answers so that a 5 year old could understand because he didn't understand. He'll give you and automatic 0 if you forget to cite a page number. He's clueless. Most of the students in my class were frustrated with him. Everyone will be better off with another professor. He's not worth it.

I had issues downloading the required software. The information was confusing and the videos he had posted didn't help either. Basically you're on your own. It's the type of program where he puts in 30% and you're supposed make up the other 70%. There are also no perquisites to prepare you for these problems. The CIS dept was no help either.

I had issues downloading the required software. Lucidchart would have solved the problem if that information was given ahead of time. He suggested I drive 54 miles one way to campus to use the computer labs. There are no perquisites to prepare you for these problems and the dept chair was no help either. You're basically on your own. Good Luck!

Professor Bek is a new face at SBCC this semester and, after taking CIS 117, I'm glad he's here. He provides prompt and detailed e-mail help for questions. The homework is long but really helps reinforce the concepts he teaches. His discussions are interactive and his videos are very clear. I'm looking forward to taking more classes with him!

The good: Very passionate about her subject. You will learn things and she will make sure that you do. The bad: Exceptionally strict. There's little to no leeway for how you should do things. Prepare to write things by hand, electronics are forbidden in class. The teacher is rude if you disagree with or inconvenience her. Meant for English Majors.

No hate, we were homies. I think that she has helped me a lot as a writer. I also think she makes you work harder than any other teacher at SBCC. It's great for your life, but I also believe it's hard for students who are planning on transferring. You should be rewarded for your hard work, and you simply are not. No hate fr. But eh.

don't take this teacher for english 101. i left early in the semester because of how rude she was. honestly no idea why sbcc hasn't hired teachers with more modern ways of thinking and common kindness.

This class was challenging and had me stressed out a lot of the time, but despite that i enjoyed it.I feel like i was able to broaden my essay writing and reading skills a lot. She makes the essays sound really complex, but if you make sure to do research, they're not that hard. I also found Professor Bell to be an enjoyable and interesting person!

worst professor I've ever had. always sounded like she was on the break of lashing out. d1 yapper about the most irrelevant stuff as well as always rude no matter what. she also allows no late homework at all
It's a hard class get ready

Barbara Bell is not qualified to teach in today's world. Her class is behind times, only presents work on the white experience and dismisses, ignores relevant work from people of color. She asked a student of hers to see another colleague's course page and tried to get them fired. Her power trips are reason alone to stay away from her class.

Professor Bell doesn't make this class very easy and it requires a lot of effort. However, she truly has your best interest in mind and improves your writing skills. She can be intimidating at times, especially during the first week or so but don't let that deter you. To succeed make sure to go to her office hours and become a familiar face.

This professor is hard to please especially when it comes to essays. I did essays and I had to redo them because she wouldn't like the words I would use or didn't like my ideas. She expects you to be a professional writer. If you ask her something she will reply with something different. Don't enroll if you can't handle someone with tough grading.
no
Barbara Bell was a strict professor who graded heavily. Make sure that the essay topics are appropriately chosen to her delicate opinions.

Professor Bell has to be one of the worst teachers I have ever had. She assigns so many essays and expects you to be a professional writer. She will not give you an A. If you think your 8 page paper that you spent 40 hours working on is good she will think otherwise. Do not take this class by all means.

Prof. Bell could be very nice at times, and yet very mean. I felt as if she targeted me, scolding me specifically for things that the rest of the class was doing. While she did tend to upset me, she really pushed me to improve my work. By the end of the term she pulled me aside and gave me good advice for my career based on my essays and writing.

She tries to scare everyone off, it seems, during the first week of classes. Lots of homework (long-term papers, reading assignments, etc..). She is my favorite English teacher OAT, and I wish she taught more classes. Don't procrastinate, make sure to participate in class. She truly wants her students to succeed, as long as they want to.

Barbara is the worst teacher I have ever had. She is an absurdly tough grader. On my classes first major paper that was worth 15% of our grade and the highest grade she gave anybody was 85%. The average for the paper was under 70%. She post work on Saturday that is due Monday. I'm convinced any good review on here is her posting about herself.
Great person, cares about students if you put the time into showing her. Lots of papers so be prepared for that.
She cares for her students. She is always kind when I visited her for a questions during her office hours. She provide interesting resources and the course was very helpful.

Awful professor, total mean person. She doesn't make the instructions for assignments clear and switches up what she is saying constantly. She talks in circles and doesn't answer questions, also is not available over email or anything. In the height of covid her class was in person and had no online resources. Do not take her!

She is a good teacher if you make yourself a familiar face. She respects seeing you try, and wants to see you succeed. Her papers are definitely toughly graded but it has set me up for future papers pretty well, so a good teacher to have to prepare you for the rest of your college career.

She is a great person but grades damn hard. I talk to her after every damn class but my grade does not reflect that. I'm not an idiot she just has two essays going on at once and has you read a book at the same time. Lots of homework. Lots of essays. Mandatory attendance. You can't screw up even a little on the homework without getting points off.

I will never take this class by this professor again. The class is very technical and time consuming. She is a very tough grader and there is a ton of writing in this class, and multiple papers on the same subject. She will even tell you to stop doing work for other classes and instead do work for her class because you will end up falling behind.

Professor Bell is by far one of the best professors I've ever had. I went into this class expecting a challenge, but not something that I would have to work very hard at. Boy, was I wrong. After getting my first essay back and getting a C, when I'm normally a straight A student, I knew I was in for it. But, with her help, I was able to get an A-!

If you want the easy A don't take this course, but if you want to truly better your writing and make the rest of your college English experience's easier/better I suggest this course. Barbara is really willing to work with you on your papers and help the process. Plus you will get experience writing a variety of papers which is real-life helpful.

Professor Bell is a really tough grader, so if you want to succeed in her class you're going to have to really work for it. She is SUPER helpful in every paper and really wants you to become a better writer, which is why her tenacious criteria is needed! If you're willing to put in the time and effort to improve your writing skills, take her!

Although she was easily one of the toughest teacher on campus she is also the best. She made me such a better writer and was very helpful during the writing process. I would take her again in a heart beat, she's amazing! She is NOT easy but she made every class after hers easy. It's college if you wanna succeed take her, push yourself.

Professor Bell is by far my favorite that I've had so far. She really wants you to understand the material and as long as you turn things in on time you'll be okay, although you get one free pass. Don't text in class if you want to stay in her good graces though. Email her or go to her office hours and she will always be ready to help you out. :)

Professor Bell was a very nice women, but her class was very difficult for me as a student. She's a tougher grader, but if you plan on transferring into a competitive school then i suggest taking this class. She will teach you so much and prepare you to a 4 year school. She really cares for her students and is willing to help anyone.

There were 3 major essays, a big research essay at the end, and usually homework for every class (easy homework). Focus on the essays! Bell is a tough, TOUGH grader and if you don't go to her for office hours for help you can forget about even getting a B. I learned a lot in this class, i also worked very hard but didn't get an A.
#ILITERALLYCANT #DONTEVEN

I have never ever failed an english course ever in my life. She is incredibly difficult and picky about how you write. I had such a bad time in her class that I became incredibly depressed and I felt like I beat myself up so much for trying so hard. I wouldn't even recommend her to my worst enemy.

Gina is AMAZING! She has a heart for her career and is well spoken. She is all ears and understanding. She is properly strict with deadlines, so if you're having a hard time with an assignment, she appreciates communication. Treat her the way you want to be treated.

Gina is a chillerrrr she acts “scary” in the beginning but genuinely if you just go to class you'll get an A. she's very participation and attendance heavy but truly cares about her students. her demeanour is off putting at first but as more time goes on her work gets easier and she gets more lax. def recommend for an easy A
Goodhubecuhbr74

She expects a lot and is intimidating at first but is very understanding. The workload isn't heavy, there is only one assignment per week. She is also not a harsh grader and gives really good feedback on essays. Overall she is very caring, genuine, and a good teacher!

Gina Bell is genuinely the best professor I've ever had. She's so understanding, beautiful, kind, and sweet. I could not ask for a better psychology professor. She's genuinely one of the best people I've ever met. You will never meet somebody with a more beautiful soul. Gina Bell, I love you.

Gina Bell is an great professor! While she may come off as intimidating at first, it's clear that this is because she truly cares about her subject and wants students to engage with and enjoy the material. Her passion for teaching makes her classes both challenging and rewarding. I highly recommend her for anyone looking to dive deep into any Psy.

I took Gina for PSY 100, and I could've done better, but her due dates were confusing because she had a quiz due every Monday, a discussion post due every Wednesday, and homework due on Sundays. Gina Bell is very understanding and caring, and if you can stay on track, then I recommend taking her course. She doesn't tolerate using AI or chat GPT.

Prof Gina is passionate about what she is teaching which made the learning experience entertaining. She also is constantly engaging with students in her lectures and loves it when students speak up in her class. However, she was always understanding of the quieter students and made accommodations for them. Amazing teacher, and the sweetest woman.

Gina is one of the best teachers you can really ever ask for, I say this because Gina is a really understanding teacher inside of class and outside to the point you can actually build a connection with her, she is also super funny. she also gives you two personal days for the semester if you need them. Im telling you guys TAKE HER CLASS!

I love Gina! Her lectures are decently interesting, and she makes them funny. She really truly cares about her students and as long as you respect her, show up, and put in effort, she will respect you. The homework schedule is consistent, which is helpful, and assignments are never too hard. I would definitely take her again.
Gina Bell is an amazing professor! She really cares about her students and is passionate about teaching. She is also very funny. I would definitely recommend taking her class to anybody.

Bell very much cared about the AI to creative writing ratio. Every assignment u have to use a quote to support your answer from the book but then you can't use to long of a quote or else she will flag you for 'suspicious" writing". Besides that, the course was more scientific that I thought and it was really fast because it was in the summer time.
Really goood teacher!! Interesting lectures and very passionate about her teaching and her students! Love learning in this class.

I don't recommend this class at all. She assigns too much homework without regard for other classes. Each week you're required to write at least 2 essays while also doing extensive reading and watching videos/lectures and/or discussion work. This is Psychology, not English! You'll be drowning in homework by week 2. Beware!!!!

Gina was incredible! You have to show up to class to earn points, but the classes are fun and engaging. Gina is passionate about psychology and it really shows. I couldn't have picked a better professor for psych, she is empathetic and funny and picks really interesting articles and videos for assignments! She also uses free online textbook! 10/10
She was very caring, and helpful. She was easy to reach and responded to my questions in a short amount of time. Great professor!
The most kind and caring and passionate woman. Cares so deeply for each individual student. Content is so interesting! Taking her again:)

Gina is a wonderful and caring individual. She gets to know her students on a personal level and always encourages and inspires her students to think deeper about her prompts and psychology as a whole. I'm very grateful for Gina and I will remember her forever as a very important part of my education. LOVE YOU GINA!!

Gina is the best teacher I have ever had. She is passionate about the material she is teaching, and able to relate it to her own life. She does an amazing job of creating a safe, environment where the class can share as well. She genuinely cares about all of her students, she's open, funny, kind, and always willing to go above and beyond for them.
goated. nice human being.

Prof Bell is a great teacher! She is very accessible outside of class but get ready to do lengthy assignments every week! She is very kind and if she feels like you are struggling with something outside of class she takes the time to check in on you. I would highly recommend taking her class if you need to take intro to psych!

Gina was a really nice teacher, easy grader, good feedback, at times if not all points are achieved will give option to redo Class was taken online, every week there was a short lecture slide show, 1-3 short videos, a writing assign(around 3 pgs), and a short multiple choice quiz. There was 3 multiple choice exams throughout the whole course
She is so understanding and allows you chances to fix your homework. As long as you clearly follow the criteria for her assignments (which is mostly about using sources) than you will pass
Take her class!! She is such a caring teacher that cares about your success. You will not regret taking her course!
She is the sweetest prof I've ever had. She really cares about her students and always gives such good feedback.

I am telling you to take PSY 100 with Professor Bell. I took her 6-week summer course online and it consisted of 2 discussions and quizzes every week, 3 exams in total, and 3 mandatory meetings with her. This may seem like a lot but it's honestly not bad at all. Literally the best teacher I've had so TAKE HER !!!

She is such an great professor! The textbook is a free online pdf. The workload is pretty light- 1 assignment, discussion, and a quiz every week. She sends out a voice-email every morning with clear expectations. Her lectures are powerpoints, but she also has the options for voice-infused lectures. 3 mandatory Zoom meetings throughout the semester.

Gina Bell is one of the best professors I have ever had! Taking her class made me switch to a psychology major. She is so caring and wants you to succeed. She makes it clear that she is always available to meet with too. The material itself is so easy if you stay on top of the reading. I highly recommend her class!

So nice and caring especially during COVID times. Always wanted to make sure you're doing okay both with grades and mental health. Easy quizzes and tests, all multiple choice. A really doable amount of work every week and clear grading criteria. Definitely take her class if you get the chance, she's such a gem and we need more profs like her.
Great teacher! She really cares and is very personable. I loved her class, and so will you if you are willing to do the work!

Doesn't respond to emails. He isn't a bad person, but he is a bad teacher. Makes tests as hard as possible and gloats about how the test average is a 50. If everyone is getting low scores, it's time to reevaluate your teaching skills. I only did well in the class because I taught myself the material. Almost everyone fails the final btw.

He is very nice and is a good teacher, but gives no practice problems besides homework, and the homework isn't useful for studying for exams. The topics are sometimes overcomplicated and not very straightforward, so it takes a lot of time outside of class to learn. Exams are pretty hard.

Honestly an amazing human being and very passionate about chemistry, honestly I got the grade I did because I had a tough start, there is A LOT of homework which does help when it comes to studying. Stay on top of it though because it piles up quick, don't miss class! So much gets covered in lecture missing even a couple days will set you back.

he is very millennial. he is very nice though. but his lectures are poorly made and he overcomplicates topics. He often time will show a slide and say "this is super important and you need to memorize this" then quickly skip the slide so that nobody has time to write down/process what he said. it is definitely a LEARN IT YOURSELF class. good luck

Lectures felt rushed and sometimes unclear, he brushed over a lot of things and if you fell even a little behind you were basically screwed because he didn't really slow down or go back over things at all. The tests were pretty hard and the final was HARD, labs were an easy A tho.

I really liked taking this class. I would say it is definitely a challenging course but Chris gave us all the tools and practice we could ever need. The exams were a direct reflection of his lecture problems so make sure to attend those. it also does require you to study outside of class and put in the effort. I feel like i've learnt a lot from him

Bris is Awesome! His lectures are challenging but engaging and packed with everything you need to know. the examples he goes over in class and homework problems are the exact same ones you'll see on the tests. plus, his brainrot references are top tier and he loves when students show him chemistry reels. 10/10 would recommend.

Chris is honestly such a fire professor for this class. CHEM 155/156 suck in general and are very hard classes. He makes the class less intense and makes some funny jokes in class and in lab! Youi should definitely take this class with him if you can.
Accidental double post oops

I've really enjoyed his class! Go to lecture+watch his videos (there's a bonus pic of a pug). Study his slides/videos NOT the hw for calculations, or it'll mess you up (I got a C+ on an exam by doing this). He drops your lowest exam grade, has snacks during office hours, and is a super nice guy! Keep his lab clean and he'll go easy on grading labs

Chris is such a sweet man, his lectures definitely move fast but he is such a joy to talk to and is always down to chat about anything chemistry or non chemistry related. He is genuinely so passionate about chemistry, and it shows in his lectures. His tests can be a bit confusing, so definitely pay attention to how he writes problems in class.

Chris is the best. His explanations of things made concepts I have been learning since high school finally make sense! He is the kindest, most approachable, and most understanding type of professor. If you want to succeed in this class, he is there to make that happen. He is so easy to talk to about chemistry and life. Chris is awesome!!

Chris is a great professor. Everything was very clear, and if it wasn't, a clarifying question cleared up any confusion whatsoever. If you don't know what is happening in this course, all you have to do is ask. Chris obviously cares about his students, and I really enjoyed having him as a teacher. My favorite chemistry professor thus far.
Chris is one of my favorite teachers at SBCC. He made his lectures easy to understand and I really enjoyed having him for this class!

Professor Bernt is one of the best professors I had encountered during my academic journey at SBCC. He is passionate about what he teaches and makes it as easy as possible for you to understand complex material given in chem 156. Make sure you prepare for labs and lecture before hand and do the homework in a timely manner

Professor Bernt is a very helpful and caring professor. He explains each lab in detail at the beginning of class and answers any questions you have as you go. I am not good at chem and I struggled with some of the material at times, but he always helped me understand anything I had trouble learning.

This class sucked. Chris is a good guy but man his lectures are the worst things I've ever experienced. They are very short and he doesn't really know how to explain things. You need to teach yourself. DON't TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU NEED TO LEARN FROM AN INSTRUCTOR. Trust me, I didn't listen to the other reviews on this page, now I am suffering.

I personally liked Professor Bernt's lectures. They were nicely structured, short, and packed with a good amount of information. I liked taking the class asynchronously and he was very flexible with assignment due dates. I would have wished that he was more organized because I often did not feel fully supported or prepared for exams.

Professor Bernt is super nice. He's lenient about homework deadlines and you can tell he's trying his best. I also love that he adds a picture of his pug at the end of lectures. However, the material is tough. Chemistry has always been really hard, but now even more so since classes aren't in person. Tough class but nice guy.

Currently taking Chris for Chem 101. NO ONE in the class is doing well. Everyone is failing the tests. There is barely any hw. Tests take up like your whole grade. Lectures are rarely given. Tests don't contain material from the lectures. Lectures take forever to understand since they are pre-recorded. There are no live zoom classes. Classawful

Professor Bernt seems like a very caring teacher, but that doesn't make up for his poor lecturing skills. I'm currently taking his online intro to chem course, and his inexperience as a teacher is evident. His lectures are short and unclear. Thorough textbook study is ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY to do well. This is the worst online class I've experienced.

Professor Bernt is a great professor. Listening to his lectures was the highlight of my semester. He also really cares about his students, he is super polite, understanding and helpful overall. The material itself is tough, but manageable.

Taking his class is basically watching lectures then you're on your own with a test that contains questions you've never seen like before; 20 hard/ambiguous questions for an hour time limit. No instructor for labs and he constructs labs that nothing similar tocould be found online, like we need to learn some way or another? He's not caring at all

The only REAL reason why Jennifer still has a job at SBCC simply because she's BLACK. The department of Biology is DEI WOKE IDEOLOGY. They care more about people with different skin color than people who are a good fit for the job.

Don't listen to the positive reviews they are written by the teacher or her tutors so sad. WORST teacher you'll ever take. She can't run a class. I recommend taken a different cell bio teacher from another college.

I went into this class prepared to ignore everything I had heard about her, but the negative reviews are all true. Here are some words of advice: Don't let her get to you. It's her not you. A good lab group matters. DO NOT take with OCHEM if you can avoid it. It will be hard, but if you keep your head down and do your work you will survive.

Great lectures brings bio to life, req TIME and effort. Communicates DISRESPECTFULLY w/ her students in class- unprofessional. Doesnt facilitate an environment where students feel comfortable asking questions. Show up prepared to lab. You can do it.

dr b liked having off topic convos w students to come off as nice and friendly, but in class and during discussions, she is not afraid to grill you on if ur wrong. she embarrasses people in front of the whole class. once told me to use my brain and i will never forget that. it mentally effects so many students. she needs to do better.

should've been 2 separate classes. final grade is based off points you get in lecture and in lab. overall i had a C. but i was 2 POINTS OFF FROM THE LECTURE PASSING GRADE SO I GET AN F. this is gonna tank my GPA. she will not round up. hardest class i've ever taken. also group projects are very hard to work in, LOTSS OF ARGUMENTS…

DONT TAKE WITH OCHEM! Harder than Ochem, takes over your life if you want to do well. Consider that when you make your schedule. Only option at CC (unless you take it at Ventura) so get ready to grind. I wish I hadn't gone in expecting Dr B would be awful, it just stressed me out more. Shes a great lecturer but has a short fuse sometimes

Never missed a single lecture. Attended almost every tutoring session. If you don't go to tutoring, you will fail. I love her lectures, but she is an incredibly tough grader and can be snappy/unprofessional to students. She was never rude to me, but seeing how she interacted with other students made me feel sick. When she isn't evil, she is great.

Should be 8 credits. One of the hardest classes I have ever taken. She expects a lot out of her students and gets unnecessarily angry when we can't keep up. She is "trying something new" this year, where the students teach themselves instead of her teaching us. Why am I paying hundreds of dollars for a course where I teach myself the material?

The professor is extremely strict with rules, sometimes in ways that feel discouraging. More understanding and flexibility would help create a better learning environment. The little moments do matter.

Tragic she is the only teacher for part three of a three part biology series. I already finished calculus and chemistry with As and Bs and even got accepted at uc as a biology major, but her class was so bad I changed my major and now have another year at cc before I can transfer. Subject should have been good, but she was impossible to please.

"Exams riddled w trick Qs designed to confuse rather than assess true understanding, excessive busywork made it hard for wrong reasons." Totally agree—aren't we all here to learn, or some of us are meant t be filtered out by not having ability to tolerate a power abuser? People work in cell bio later will think this is a joke.

I got an A in this class, but not because I “became a better scientist” or whatever she claims the goal is. It's mostly just copying dense material onto notecards and hoping the formatting doesn't annoy her. You can skip all lectures and still do fine if you figure out her very specific grading quirks. It's mindless busy work rather than learning.

Has had many neg. reviews deleted from the past few months-image>nontoxic learning environment. No exag. that 9/10 students hated her, those who don't have Stockholm syndrome. Faux “warm" act flips to BPD style rage. Exams riddled w trick Qs designed to confuse rather than assess true understanding, excessive busywork made it hard for wrong reasons

Most I've ever had to work in a class. DO NOT recommend taking with other STEM classes. Toughest prof you'll meet but is inspiring and prepares you for ANY future classes. The class itself added to my love for bio, but I didn't get to enjoy any of it bc of her. Almost impossible A, easy to pass if you just do the work. I love and hate her so much.

I have never worked so hard in a class and it feel so thankless. The lecture and lab should be two different components since you need to pass them individually. Prepare for it like a 10 unit class imo. Also I got a 79.96% (796.5 points) -800 is a B… It's the first C on my college transcript in the last 5 years. It hurt. GL!

Interacting with this woman was like dealing with a bipolar parent. You never knew which “Dr. B” you were going to get. Condescending, constantly overstimulated, smiley to some but instantly rude and an obvious disdain/ lack of patience for others. I cannot believe this type of behavior is permitted in 2025.

Dr. B doesn't just fail to inspire; she actively drains the passion that quality profs cultivate. If another prof taught this course, I believe I'd have loved lec+lab. The workload is brutal if this were your only class; with a full load you'll drown. Many capable people fought to maintain their grade only to barely scrape by, if pass at all. RUN

A petition called for her removal in spring ‘20 (pre-tenure) yet, she is still here. The BioDep Chair/Dean should be held accountable for years of inaction. It has been too many years w/o repercussions. Check her UCSC reviews, NO character dev since then. A disgrace that an overwhelming amount of students experiences were not taken into account.

I respect Dr. B and I learned a lot but this class was the hardest class I have ever taken. The only way to pass is to get a good lab group where everyone wants an A, turn in EVERY assignment (there's a LOT) do your note card early, and never skip tutoring, lecture or lab. This class alone should be worth 10 credits. Take it by itself if you can.

Dr. B is the most organized and inspiring professor I've had. She clearly lays out what expected as well as offers you plenty of time to clarify. Her assignments seems long but they truly deepen your understanding for the subject(interest if you have any). Class never felt like a tense or toxic environment. Dr. B made me better at studying

Dr. B prepared me for upper division and then some. Her class was moderately difficult but endlessly enriching. Her lectures are perfectly organized, and they're what my current university professors should be striving for. I've learned far more from this class than anything from university so far. I'm very thankful to have had Dr. B as a professor

Dr. B is everything a professor should not be-profoundly childish & uninspiring. Yes, the class is challenging but not in a fair way She doesn't teaches exactly what she wants to see on exams and in my opinion, makes success unattainable. If you're passionate about biology and willing to work, DO NOT TAKE HERE.

Dr. B is everything a professor should be—profoundly knowledgeable and inspiring. Yes, the class is challenging and time-consuming but incredibly rewarding. She teaches exactly what she wants to see on exams and in my opinion, makes success attainable. If you're passionate about biology and willing to work, she'll deepen your love for the field.

I have never taken a class, anywhere that has such an insanely high work load. She thinks it's reasonable for you to drop your other classes just to focus on hers. Had I not dropped her class I wouldn't have gotten into UCSB. I took molecular/cell bio there after transfer and it was much easier. This class is absolutely insane. AVOID at all costs.

Choose lab group wisely!! If you don't you may end up doing all the work yourself (happened to me). There is a lot of discussion presenting, so make sure you're comfortable being grilled on the spot. The tests are very similar to practice tests tutors send out. Focus on memorizing!

This class is no walk in the park, but with the right study tools like unstuck AI, it's manageable. The labs can be tedious, but the professor makes an effort to engage students. Worth taking if you're interested in the material, but be prepared to put in the work.

One of the most unsatisfying classes you'll ever take. She doesn't give you everything to know to succeed, you just need to find another cell bio teacher. Textbooks are useless but the sad part about this class is even participating in not enough to pass. Don't bother going to here office cause she HATES seeing students with her CHILD BEHAVIOR.

One of the hardest yet most rewarding classes ever. She gives you everything you need to succeed, you just need to be detailed in your work & following instx. You don't need to read the txtbook, but if you want to succeed GO TO CLASS! & participate, I swear it will help you. & go to office hours- she loves to see students putting in the effort.

I've scored an F on my MCAT. If you're pre-med I HIGHLY DONT recommend you spending any time on this class. Jennifer will not prep you for any kind of MCAT or anything. After taking this class you'll realize how bad Jennifer is as a teacher & a person at self. If you want an adequate cell bio teacher take another college.

The workload mainly consists of individual filming projects outside of class, which I thoroughly enjoyed. He tells you what he wants for the project, shows some examples, and gives you a week to film about a minute of content. The lectures were sometimes dull, and I would lose focus, but other than that, this class was a lot of fun.

I enjoyed the lectures and I learned a lot. He does not post example assignments online, so try not to miss class. I had a lot of issues when I didn't attend for one period. Tests are simple if you study and he has a good sense of humor. Just make sure to take this seriously because the grading can be harsh, but he gives good feedback.

Curtis explains everything very detailed and is an all around fantastic professor/person, but he also moves extremely fast. Get ready to write notes faster than you thought possible. He is very strict with late/missing assignments so make sure you give yourself plenty of time and don't put off work.

Curtis clearly knows what he is talking about and can really help you get into Cinematography if you want to. He also is a very tough grader, expect weekly shooting/lighting projects that can easily take a few hours each. His lectures also move very fast and can be tough to keep up with, although he keeps them engaging.

do not take this class for fun if you are not a film major. the weekly projects are a lot of work and a lot of the time I felt like I didn't have the time or resources to complete them well, he has no sympathy for that though and will give you a bad grade, made me no longer like film as a hobby
Take his classes, you guys wont regret. Curtis knows his stuff.

Curtis Bieber is a tough grader and asks questions that are subjective and grades them harshly if you don't answer exactly as he thinks. He marks you down for little things. I would not recommend taking his class and he takes the enjoyment out of writing.

I like Curtis as a person, I think he's really funny and it's clear that he cares about film. However, he grades this class like it's an advanced class... almost nobody ever got full points on an assignment. It's a fun class and I learned a lot, though. Just be prepared to not get an A if you're new to cinematography.
Tough, but fair.
Over Critical of submissions, does not give good instructions.

The Motion Graphics and Compositing class is all about taking what he teaches in class and putting it into the assignments you hand in. He gives out the assignment in a timely manner before they are due, so be prepared to start working early. Curtis gives great feedback, but requires you to make an effort with your assignments.

He makes learning fun and also has a fun sense of humor. Hes tough but clear on projects, but is easy on written assignments as long as you do them as asked. He also gets back to emails quick and is helpful when checking out equipment if Chien-ie requires some permission!

Curtis is the best cinematography teacher you will ever have. He is super passionate about cinematography, and so knowledgable. He teaches in a very straight forward way, and you will learn a lot by paying attention in his classes. He does not settle for mediocrity! Forget about turning in late or not showing up to class on time.

Curtis is a great teacher, he is passionate about teaching and you will get a lot from his classes. Every class I took from him I learned more than any other teacher I have taken class with but you have to work hard. He doesn't accept late assignment or extra credit, also you can't miss classes. Curtis doesn't settle for mediocrity!

Cinematography I requires a lot of time and effort. If you are a full time student, be prepared to stay up a little late because Curtis doesn't settle for mediocrity. Oh, having your own camera and tripod is a huge benefit if you can afford one. Overall great professor, if you'd like a challenge definitely take one of his courses.

Curtis is definitely a great guy and extremely knowledgeable. However, he is a bit of a tough grader in each of his courses. As long as you go into the class with prior knowledge, you will do fine. If not, I would recommend becoming familiar before entering his classes. Overall, I did learn a lot.

Curtis was my favorite teacher last semester. I really enjoyed his funny kind of teaching. But if you take him, be prepared that not turning in an assignment or coming late is a absolute no go. If you're into it and show it, it's gonna be a great semester for you!
Enthusiastic and inspiring teacher!

Curtis is a wonderful educator and respectable human being. If you have the opportunity to learn from this vetted professor, take it seriously. The more effort you put into your creativity, the more effort he'll put in to you. Dream Big and Good Luck!!!

Curtis is awesome! He is very clear and makes sure that everyone is following along and understanding what to do. I loved taking this class, it was a lot of fun and i recommend it to anyone. Projects are due every 3-4 weeks and as long as you follow the requirements you will get a good grade.

Cool guy. Speaks fast with clarity while showing things on final cut on an overhead. Assigned hw due every 3-4 wks. Otherwise you could show up, learn a little in a matter of 2-3 hrs and leave. class was once a wk, 5-10, we got out by 8 or earlier. He grades you pretty seriously, but just do what the paper says and your fine.
Best teacher I have ever had, he is funny and you learn a lot, but you have to show up to class to get a good grade, You have a project due about every 3-4 weeks.
Excellent teacher, extremely bright and knowledgable, and seems to genuinely care about what he does. You will learn from him.
Best film professor there is at SBCC. He is extremely good at passing along his knowledge with a sense of humor to his students. Take every class available with this guy!

His teaching is absolutely astounding! His grace and knowledge in all aspects of film, inspires me and i believe he's the best there is at sbcc! Wish every class could be taught with his intellect - If you have the chance, take all his classes! You'll learn a lot from him, plus he looks like a director!

This guy is actually funny. And very talented. On our midterm a question was "Contrast is good, but too much of a good thing can sometimes turn bad - like heroin" and then some question about it. Just a genuine & nice guy. Just want to underline again that he IS talented and is a big inspiration.
Savage! I have taken all his classes and he is freaking hilarious. He makes you want to go to class
The best teacher ever. He is really talented and have the passion for what he talks about. He is not to easy and not to helpful, but that is for your own best. TAKE ALL CLASSES WITH HIM!
Great teacher and great class learned alot.

hilarious guy who doesn't bs his students. All his information is interesting and has practical application towards whatever sort of media production you are interested in. If you enjoy films and film-making, take this class!
Terrible take someone else

Managerial finance; thought we'd be mostly working with numbers/calculations. No, more than 1/2 the time spent writing pointless papers and multiple discussion forums. Unclear grading criteria/directions on writing. Gave SO much effort, and the grading DID NOT match up. Been at SBCC for 2 years, never experienced this before with another professor

Susan is a great professor and is is always available and willing to give you help when you need it! She provides extra resources too, but the reading is still crucial for succeeding in this class. Summer classes have a rep for being difficult, but if you put in the time+effort, youll do great. Dont put off the final project though!

This online class was a fairly light load. There is an assignment (500 words), discussion, and a quiz every week. The practice test questions are used in both her quizzes and exams. She is very caring and responds quickly to emails. If you don't do the assignments, she might drop you out of the class, so make sure to participate!

I took Professor Blocks summer course, and I really wish I had the chance to meet with her in person because she was so great. Seriously such a caring, helpful, and understanding professor. Responds to emails quickly, work load wasnt too much by any means, and she lets you work at your own pace. 10/10 would recommend!!!

She was one of the nicest professors I've ever had. You definitely have to work for your grade, but in the end it is all worth it because Professor Block really knows her stuff and cares about each and every student. 10/10 would take one of her classes again.

She is THE EASIEST GRADER. Just do the assignments, turn them in on time, meet the word count, and you're FOR SURE going to get an A. She is super nice and emails you back quickly if you have any questions. It may seem tough but put in some effort, you'll get an A. I had no idea what I was talking about in my posts, but she is an easy grader.

Susan is alright, she is a bit boring and it's quite easy to fall alseep to the sound of her calm and soothing voice. She knows a lot about finance but has lost some Money by investing herself so don't be to sure of Everything she says ;) Sometimes she gets confused by the textbook which is quite funny but a bit sad. Be sure to make assignments.

Amazing professor who uses her professional career in finance to enhance the class. This is a must take class and will teach you valuable life lessons that will apply to any major. I highly recommend that you take this class. You won't regret it. Work hard and you will do great.

What is great about this class is that there are no surprises on the exams. Just make sure you do the study guide. I went in wanting to learn how to make a budget and that is exactly what I got. Finance is not my major but I am happy that I took the class.

This teacher is incompetent. She rarely responds or clarifies and there is ZERO participation within the class, when she creates forum posts and assignments no one does them because she is so vague and doesnt answer questions. Not to mention, none of the material to her study guide is ACTUALLY on the test. Please do NOT take her classes.

No student questions answered 10 days into the class. A quiz was required, it couldn't be opened. She has all the email addresses of her students, yet was indifferent or lazy. I wrote her three times - I figured she was in a car accident. Nope. Later I heard she just needed tech help. She could have let us know, we'd have hung in there. I dropped.
She is an amazing educator. Kind and focused. She is he best Finance teacher I have taken in this country.

Professors assignments are not clear, will give poor grade without feedback. Does not use email to communicate which is super annoying, and falsely accuses students of using AI. I am an english major, and I was accused of using AI on my final of all things…. I received A's in all other classes, wrote in person essays, etc… unbelievable

Honestly the worst experience ive had with a professor. Really questioning how she got a job as a teacher, takes 2-3 weeks to respond to emails. Grades papers horrendously and falsely accuses for AI. WOULD NOT TAKE AGAIN!!!! 100% RECOMME

Im shocked at the amount of people saying negative things about professor Boggs. There are times she takes a while to respond to emails but if you continue to update and bug her about it she is more than willing to help you out. She is incredibly kind and reasonable and understands that life can get in the way of school. 10000% recommend

You would think that since it is an online class, the professor would have more time to dedicate to actually teaching the class since they don't have to go anywhere? Wrong. She responded to emails 2 weeks after I sent them, literally the only job as a teacher. Terrible teacher dont take this class with her. Also a very tough grader.

She was an excellent and informative teacher. She gave just the right amount of work, enough to keep us engaged and excited to come to class. She was very genuine and really listened to what we had to say, unlike some teachers who don't take students input seriously.

I had her for summer which goes by super fast. It was online so I never saw her in person. But for the most part her assignments were easy and clear. She's not the fastest grader but she seemed nice and seemed to be a fair grader. I finished the class with an A.

Do not know how this person was hired as a teacher. No sense of direction on every single assignment and never responds leaving the student absolutely clueless on what to do. Literally the directions she does try to give make 0 sense. A monkey would do a better job teaching this class

This professor will not communicate with you. Her TA was the only one who responded but was not helpful. She would grade things incorrectly and unfairly, like she had barely looked at the work turned in. Do not recommend!

There is alot of busy work that isn't worth anything for your grade and doesn't grade papers until the last minute. She is late almost everyday and spends the first half hour of the class talking about politics/her opinions on things that are irrelevant to the class. She's a sweet person and means well so as long as you try you'll get a good grade.
Sarah is an amazing professor and is a good teacher to take if you want an easy A. I'd say the only significant downside is she can be unorganized and take weeks/months to give you a grade.

An easy and manageable class with good structure. There is 3 major papers (5-6 pages) that are 60% of your grade but she does give you about two weeks to do each one so nothing too crazy or hard. Pretty easy grader and seems very sweet. An easy class and professor I would recommend.

Lots of discussions and quote gathering, feels like any other English class except she doesn't give clear feedback on your grade for your essays which are worth 60 percent of your grade. Some of the content doesn't feel necessary for the essays assigned but overall it's a very mediocre class with tedious/monotonous work.

This is one of my hardest subjects. She makes it fun and interesting.. I feel empowered because I didn't have the skills and now I have improved. I never feel put down. Take her class if you need English 110 she is caring and can help you with your goals to move past sbcc.

I dreaded taking an English class because it is my least favorite subject. Professor Sarah Boggs completely changed my perspective about it. Not only did she reply to my messages within 2 days all the time but she also graded very generously. I did all the work, followed all the rules, read all the books, and earned an "A".

All her bad reviews are from her online class: ignore it. In person she is AMAZING. Funny, witty, kind. We only had to do three papers total and she is an extremely generous grader. People with English as their fourth language passed with an A. Turn in papers and you pass with flying colors. She brought snacks for all of us. Miss you Doctor!!
Very laid back and super nice. Very understanding.

Sarah was honestly one of the best professors I've ever had. Shes so nice, caring, and humble. She grades super easy, always offers help, and has taught me a lot without even trying. She makes me want to come to class. You write like three essays. read one book and its super easy. I love her and i feel like i can go to her for anything. TAKE HER!!!
Sarah is one of my favorite professors ever! She is so nice and funny and you can tell that she really cares about her students. I wish all my professors were like her

She was literally the sweetest English teacher. She seriously cares about her students, and see's everyone equally no madder their level of writing. She sets up the class so the assignments up to the full essay basically write the essay for you. Content we read and wrote about was all very interesting, never boring, and never busy work.

(ENG 110 Enhanced) Sarah is a really god professor and she really cares about her students and get to know each one and really opes up in class. I enjoyed everything we read and she was really good and took time to explain if you didn't understand the assignment. Realy involved in the process of doing the work with you, super supportive and awesome
Extremely empathetic, Clear, and concise teacher. Teaches you how to structure your arguments properly and encourages thoughtful discussion. Would recommend for any class that you must take.

Boggs is by far the worst teacher I have had. She is very disorganized. She used to get to class 30 minutes late, and it takes her a while to grade your papers. She was also very messy in her online courses. She never explained or uploaded a video to explain what she wanted us to do.

Though she is a nice lady, it was the same work over and over again, each class being the same old thing. She didn't know what she was doing most of the time and seemed to be making it up along the way. I would say if you did the work, it would be an easy A, but times she forgot what she had assigned and penalized US for having done it "wrong".

Mrs. Boggs has gone down of one of my absolute favorite teachers, she cares so much about her students and gives so many opportunities for you to get your grade where you want it. You can feel that she respects you. I would definitely recommend anyone taking English 110 take her she will definitely get you excited to go to class and write.

I attended two of Sarah Boggs' classes and came away feeling like I hadn't learned anything. She's a nice lady, but never did her own preparation, gave copious amounts of busy work, and was extremely vague about the grading criteria. (In fact, I didn't know what my grades for any projects were until a couple days before the end of the semester.)
One of the best teachers I have had. She will work with you if you are having a hard time. Definitely recommend her!!

Sarah is a very nice prof. I would love to take her again if I could. The lessons taught in class were very fun and interesting, they were mainly about news events happening recently like mass shootings, politics, racism etc. Very easy to learn. The events talked about in class were in the books we read and essays. You also get to work in groups
She's doesn't explain how to do your papers the way she wants them done

Signed up for ESP just to ensure a good English teacher for ENG 110/120, but after ending up in Sarah's class, I really wish I hadn't. Even if you're great at English essays, this class is a chore. Wrote 3 essays and 10 short papers, printed over 100 pages in total. Doesn't grade anything. Very biased, scatterbrained. Learned nothing whatsoever.

Sarah Boggs is by far one of the best English teachers I have ever had. She was the first ENG class I ever took my first semester and her class was very enjoyable. We focused a lot of topics on very interesting/controversial current events which made the class very fun. You do a lot of essay writing but is fairly essay.

Ms. Brinkman really cares about how well you do in the class and the material you're learning. She's always willing to help students whenever they need providing great feedback and ways to improve your writing.

Ms. Brinkman was a good professor who is both approachable and supportive, although she is a fairly tough grader. Her classes never felt too long. She was kind and went out of her if you needed help. For example, she allowed me to resubmit an assignment even when it wasn't typically allowed, which I really appreciated. Overall i'd recommend.

Very clear grading criteria, she wants you to succeed and always checks up with people who are behind. She is very kind and sweet. Her class is a relaxed and warm environment. Her topics for research papers are genuinely very interesting and fun to research. When grading essays she has a 1 on 1 conference with you and gives helpful feedback.

My favorite teacher so far at SBCC. She gives such thorough, step-by-step instructions. She is also an easy grader and gives you weeks to revise assignments. I saw someone else comment that the assignments in this class practically write themselves, and I 100% agree.
Her class has a very clear instruction. Workload is manageable. She is great at feedbacks and always try her best to help students. Funny lady too!
Course outline is very clear and simple. Class is an easy A if you show up and do your part. Caring and sweet lady! Learned a lot.

Prof. Brinkman made writing the three essays in the course very simple and easy to understand. Showing up to her class and absorbing the information she lays out will get you a good grade in her class. This class went by quickly due to the rapid pace of her teaching methods. She made it clear what makes a good essay and teaches the format well.

Mrs. Brinkman is a great professor, but you have to be willing to do your part in her class. She makes writing essays so clear and grades the essays with you. She gives oppourtunities to revise the essay for more points. She wants to help students succeed. If you have to take eng110, take it with Brinkman, pretty easy A and great teacher.
