Amazing!! I never missed a class, she showed that she cares how well we do in our future and that we actually learn in her class instead of just getting through it for the credits. Grades assignments with you to ensure you understand what mistakes were made, but also to receive praise! Wants participation from class so be ready to talk.

She was very helpful in revising essays and helps you through writing them step-by-step. She only gives out 3 essays and is very clear on what she wants from them. She makes you participate in free writing at the beginning of class, but it is super easy. Class is an easy A if you go to class, and she's pretty lenient on due dates.

Debra is an amazing and caring professor that gave me an amazing sense of confidence in my writing ability that I didn't possess before. She is accessible during the day quite a bit and is willing to work with all her students in order to improve their writing skills

I really enjoyed Eng 110 with professor Brinkman. She gives clear feedback on every assignment that helps improve your writing! She's easy to get in touch with and she's overall really nice. This class does have busy work but nothing that's unbearable. 10/10 recommend.

Ms. Brinkman is truly one on my favorite professors. She is caring, has a passion for teaching, and is funny. The discussions we had in class got students really involved and kept us interested. She takes the time to discuss the essays individually, which is awesome if you like feedback on your work. I would definitely take her class again!

Mrs. Brinkman is hands down the worst English professor I have ever had. She lacks so much student teacher interaction that it cause confusion as to what is required for the curriculum and is EXTREMELY unclear as to what she expects from her student. There is no homework so you never know how you are doing or making progress. Shes terrible.

She can be funny at times, but if you dont show up to class you wont pass. She also is very specific on what she wants. there is homework everytime you have class. It was confusing for me because i would change my essay according to her suggestions, but then when she would proof read it the 2nd time & she would question her own suggestion

Debra is an amazing and caring professor that gave me confidence in my writing ability that I didn't possess before. She is accessible during the day quite a bit and is willing to work with all her students in order to improve their writing skills

Amazing teacher! My writing definitely improved thanks to her class. She works very closely with students and really gets to know and understand her students strengths and weaknesses to help them further develop their writing skills. She lays out the class so beautifully that the essays she assigns almost write themselves. Definetly recommend:)
Very nice and helpful teacher. She wrote a good novel called Birds of the Central Coast!

Her class is great. She teaches you how to write good essays and is super nice. I normally didn't like english but i love her class. The pink sheet she hands out is so helpful for writing essays in other classes too.
She's ok. I don't recommend taking her. The other english 100 teachers are better and she doesn't fully revise your essays.
Amazing teacher! So easy to understand her directions and is so helpful! Take her, you wont regret it!

SHES A NICE LADY BUT SHES A VERY CONFUSING TEACHER I DEALT WITH, SHES ALL OVER THE PLACE W/ HER ASSIGNMENTS, HAVENT BEEN CONFUSED IN A ENG CLASS BEFORE. SHE DOESNT FULLY REVISE THE ESSAYS SHE EXPECTS YOU TO FIGURE IT OUT! THE ONLY POSITIVE THING IS THAT I HAVE IMPROVED MY WRITING. I RECOMMENED GETTING ANOTHER TEACHER, DONT KNOW WHERE I STAND!

I'm surprised to see people say she was unhelpful and late or disorganized. That was not the case in my class. She is an awesome teacher and so helpful, every single student in our class passed portfolio grading and her one-on-one conferences are extremely helpful. I highly recommend her to anyone who actually cares to learn proper essay writing.
I like her class.She taught me how to use ciatation and write essay. I improved my English writing a lot.Apprecriate it! You can learn lots of useful knowledge from her. Very EXCELLENT TEACHER.
Great professor who really cares about everyone there. one-on-one conferences help to improve writing. she makes the class as easy as possible and loves to help
She is very much into politics. Im not a politics person but I learned a lot from her. Very friendly and super understanding if u communicate with her.

Ms.Brinkman is a great teacher and I would highly recomend her to anyone taking English! I took Eng100 with her and loved it. Prior to this I hated English but she made it fun and gives the support and help a student needs, with that said I no longer hate english! She trully cares about the student's success and goes the extra mile to help out!:)
I thought she was very helpful. While she was late she always tried to get us ready for the essays and best as possible.

class writes 5 essays and when they are due she gives each student a conference which really helps if you go to them. she is very helpful, and make sure u have ur text book because thats what you use for the readings
Easy A for me. Not impressed by her at all. Usually arrived late, unorganized no passion for teaching. That being said, I put in minimal effort, and got A's on all the papers.
Class itself was pretty easy but Brown made it feel like a high school course, and sometimes would treat you like a high school student. He wasn't mean maybe slightly condescending though.

He's a very nice professor. Not the most organized of people, definitely takes a long time to grade assignments. He is very lenient, however, with due dates. If you put an effort and participate during class, you will do well. One of the easiest classes I have ever taken.
Literally the best professor I've ever had. Was super helpful, super sweet, very fun and enjoyable class. You want to take his class!

I took a class called ENG110E (Enhanced) cuz I'm not good at writing and needed extra support. The class is twice a week and there're many assignments, but his lectures were very rational and I learned a lot and nothing was wasted. He's a great prf who explains in detail the structure of the essay and he cares well each students. I'm so glad!!!!!!

Professor Brown is a nice guy but he grades EXTREMELY slow. There are a total of three essays and it takes him weeks to just grade one. He grades everything else last minute during the finals week. The third essay (which was due a week BEFORE finals week) was only graded AFTER finals week was over so I couldn't even see the grade I got on it.

Fun teacher, insightful discussions, and a somewhat easy grader. He makes for a light hearted and fun environment where you can always participate in a discussion and know that he'll always expand on your ideas and help convey points to rest of the class. Assigned readings are boring, and weirdly similar to each other. Good and easy English class.

Professor Brown is so amazing! He is an icon and I would love to take another one of his classes. Classes consisted of class discussion, within groups and the class. He was hilarious and the class was a comforting place. I personally didn't like the readings he assigned but despite that, his class was amazing.

Really good class, lectures were unique in that theyre are more like discussions and are helpful in seeing different interpretations of the text. Three essays, very nice with grades, allowed for late work, not many assignments. It took him a while to grade the last two essays, didnt know my actual grade until after final grades, fav eng teacher fr.

Overall his class was nice. Class time was more like a group discussion about what we read the night before. There are three essays, each are 1,200 words. He gives us plenty of time to complete them. There are also journals about the reading but those are light. He was very accessible outside of class and provided a lot of feedback.

He waited until the last minute to grade a bunch of the assignments, but other than that it was a nice class and wasn't too difficult. There were three essays and we had lots of in class discussions. There were also a few opportunities for extra credit which was nice.

took english111 Honors, and I was disappointed. the canvas page is extremely disorganized, he has very few assignments. there are 3 essays and combined they are worth 86% of your grade... 650 of 750 total points in the semester. harsh grader of essays, grades slow, class discussions go off topic. normal english 111 is prob better
good books, not a huge work load, class was just discussing the reading which was nice, Chris is a good prof

I would highly recommend taking his class. Professor Brown picks cool readings, and the homework is not hard. He wants each student to do well. Sometimes class can be a bit boring but overall really great.

I LOVE MR. BROWN! He is an awesome, smart, and really caring teacher. He has tons of office hours and has given me very helpful advice throughout the course. The readings are interesting and he offers plenty of time for analysis and questions during class time. Homework is usually reading and responding to questions with three essays!

Dr. Brown is incredibly supportive, patient, and brilliant. It is evident that he loves to teach and his student's success matters to him. The material he has selected was engaging and interesting. The thought provoking discussions he initiated left me well prepared to write an organized essay about that unit. Take his class!

Professor Brown's class was very enjoyable and easy going as long as you do the work and participate and talk in class you will succeed there are 2 books you read and a few short stories. His essays are straight forward (3) and if you struggle at all he is there to help you and is understanding TAKE HIS CLASS you won't regret!!

Professor Brown is a good choice for English 111. There are 2 books we read, and an essay to go along with each of them. Also, there's a third essay based on a few short stories that we read at the end of the semester. He is always available to help you and gives great feedback on your work. As long as participate, you'll get an A in the class.
Loves to hear opinions based on assignments that are being given and answers all questions. Gives enough time to go over essays or what your reading in class before going home to do it as homework.

Best English teacher Ive ever had. Only 3 essays And he gives awesome feedback before theyre due. Super caring. Class attendance isnt mandatory for online, but is much better than the alternative assignments. 10/10 recommend! But dont take my word for it, register and see for yourself!
TAKE HIS CLASS!! I took his class for ENG 110 this semester and am planning on taking his ENG 111 class in the fall. You will not regret taking his class.
Mr. Brown is a caring professor. He took the time to really listen to me and my concerns (personal that were effecting professional education). I appreciate him!

Not the greatest essay writer, however took the time out of his day to go through each essay of the students in his class. Really helped me out a lot. Really an amazing teacher. Participate in the zooms! I did assignments on time so don't know how he is on late work.

Genuinely cares about his students and what he teaches in class. He finds a way to make English interesting and makes writing essays a lot less of pain and most importantly less stressful. TAKE HIS CLASS 11/10 recommend!! He is super funny and easy to talk to and he is by far my favorite teacher.
truly great easy class could not have asked for a better teacher a class i enjoyed comeing to.

Professor Brown clearly cares about her students and is willing to spend one-on-one time during office hours. She is a tough grader!! Her lectures are interesting, but you will need to study a ton outside of class to do well on exams. Extra credit opportunities do help improve your overall grade.

Professor brown and I had good conversation outside of class. She is clearly willing to help students who need it. Although, she is a tough grader. you need to put in effort to this class and it is not an easy A. Exams are difficult and require a lot of knowledge about the subject and her grading is quite harsh. she does good give extra credit

I'm unsure at how this professor still has a job. I am absolutely baffled at how she grades. Criteria is so insanely unclear. On the exams, she wants you to answer questions that she doesn't ask, then when you don't answer them, she penalizes you. Don't walk, RUN from any class with Brown as the professor.

Super disappointed with Professor Brown. If you want to just pass the class, and you don't care about your grade / GPA then by all means take her. Otherwise, run. This professor will leave you super frustrated after every big assignment. She really sucks, and she grades harsher than any professor I've ever had. If you wanna keep your 4.0, run.

Please listen when I say this, you don't want Brown as your professor. If you are thinking about taking a class of their's, don't. If you are enrolled in one of their classes already, drop it. And if you just finished a semester with Brown, I'm sorry.

I'm gonna keep it real, this professor was pretty terrible. Super duper tough grader. You can write a ten paragraph answer to one question and it still isn't good enough for her. I'm not exaggerating. I'm sure many of my peers feel the same way. Save yourself the stress and hurt, don't take a class with Professor Brown.

If I had known what the professor was like, I wouldn't have taken the class. Please don't make the same mistake I did by taking a class taught by this professor. Save yourself. Your grade is dependent on 2 exams and 1 paper which she grades extremely harsh on. It's close to impossible to get an A in this class with this professor. Don't take it.

The midterm and final are based on SO MUCH oversaturated information. The course content is interesting, but I feel like im set up to fail because the course is extremely dense and there are not manyy outside resources, there's no way i can memorize everything. She's an okay teacher, but this class isnt a good fit if you're bad with memorization.

I regret to say that my experience in this course was deeply disappointing and frustrating. Rather than fostering a respectful and engaging learning environment, the professor's approach often felt malicious, unprofessional, and counterproductive… I feel this course did not provide the educational value it promised.

This is the worst teacher I have encountered in the past two years. The final grade is based on two exams and the final paper. Especially the paper, it is almost impossible to complete. You need to spend a lot of time interviewing, and the requirements are not clear. It is almost an impossible assignment. And the exam grading is very strict.
Her lesson plan was so elegant. If history classes are supposed to help you contextualize the present, she nailed it. Every time.

spare yourself the mental stress and don't take this class. Brown's grading system makes no sense and she has unrealistic standards and you will stress by finals. Her final is a 5-6 page essay due the first day of finals WEEK not final DAY. I liked the material but I would not take HER class again. If mid was a class, this is the class.

Worst Professor I have had, doesn't upload any resources from lectures online, grade is based upon two in class tests which require writing multiple essays. Very vague study guide and students are required to set up a review session in which she "may or may not be able to make it" (one she was at she just sat in the back on her phone). Good luck

Taking this class id say it's difficult because this class only depends on mid-term and final on free wright essays about topics you learned through the first couple of weeks. This was difficult for me because she is strict on certain things on the free write essay. No homework to get a grade up and only 2 extra credits worth 5 to 10 pts. Good luck

I love this professor! She is incredibly organized, which is so appreciated and the assigned work is interesting. I feel like I learned so much from this class. That said, it is a lot of work, lectures, videos, and assignments. However, if you stay on top of the weekly assignments, you will do great. I love this class!

imma keep it simple: discussions are 1pt each, midterm is an in-class essay, and final is an essay where a requirement is to interview someone in that particular field. tough grader aswell...great information displayed in class but teacher was not the best.

I took her class and the material was interesting but Brown's way of grading was very strange to me. Every discussion is 1pt compared to everything else is 20-30% of your grade. I liked that she made us interview people who work in the African American industry for our final project which I learned a ton. Recommend the class not the teacher.

I took this class because it sounded interesting and indeed it was. The midterm is an in-class essay in which she gives you pages to fill out to help you. Her lectures are very informational and intriguing. There is a final project at the end which is a 5-6 page essay that you have to interview someone in that field. I recommend taking this class

Professor Brown is very passionate, kind, accommodating, and wants everyone to do well. Definitely take as many notes as you can because you'll need to apply a lot of the homework and sources to discussion posts/essays/finals. I recommend corresponding via email or zoom with her as much as you can especially for the final project.
just no

She doesnt know how to use canvas and is a very tough grader. She was not very clear on how she wanted our work to look like when we turned it in. She also doesnt know how to use canvas. Even though we went online in 2020, she still acted like we were IN class. She made our final exam to where we could only take it at a certain time of the day.

I look up to Dr. Brown because she is a strong female intellectual! Lectures are not hard to go to and everyone becomes friends by the end of the semester. A truly eye opening class. TAKE NOTES on the lecture as you're graded on the midterm and final (and participation), which are based on what she says in class.

Shes a passionate and great teacher. She listens to what students have to say and respects all opinions. Im sad I dont have class this semester with her, I miss it! Dont doubt it and take a class with her!!

I have so much respect for Dr Brown, she is an amazing professor, teacher, and person. I looked forward to going to her lecture every week. Participate every class, not just because it counts towards your grade, participate because you'll have interesting conversations with your peers and Dr Brown herself. Take good notes, and DO NOT SKIP CLASS.

One of my favorite professors ever. Extremely engaging and knows what she is talking about. You need to show up to class in order to do well on the midterm and final. It is a discussion based class so she leans towards that in her material. I really liked the way she taught because we were able to learn from each other.

The grades are based on midterm, final, and participation. The two tests are essay based and if you leave a piece of information out she marks you down. Must be willing to participate in discussions and go to class

An expert in black studies, Dr. Jamece Brown truly cares for the well-being of her students. For that reason, her academic guidance inspired me to transfer to the University of Hawai'i at Hilo, where I received a B.A. degree in history (3.87 GPA). Also, I am presently in the M.A. Program in History at Cal State Sacramento. Awesome professor!

Dr. Brown is such a great educator and she really knows what she is talking about which is why attending class and taking good notes during lectures is crucial to passing and getting a good grade in her course. I rarely used the textbook, and some classes mostly consisted of watching films that were about what the book says. I recommend her!

Although Dr. Brown is nice and cares about the subject, the structure of her course is very poor. The only grades are the midterm and the final, and there are way too many documentaries and TV show segments, many of which aren't significant towards these two tests. I felt like I was wasting my time showing up; I wish she could lecture more.

She is a nice person. There are multiple readings for HW. Your grade is 30% participation, 30% Midterm, and 40% of an 8-10 page research paper which is your final. Overall, the class is not difficult if you go to class and take notes from her lectures in order to study for the midterm (no multiple choice test).

This rating is actually for her MKT 203 course. Overall, I liked her class the assignments were fairly easy to do as long as you put in the effort. The majority of her class consists of quizzes and discussions. She's very accessible through email/canvas and is really sweet. Some of her assignment instructions are a little confusing, however.

Great online course with manageable hands-on projects, creating a fictional company (or focusing on your own real company) and its Web site and social media accounts. Really good practical experience understanding how to identify and target an audience and reach them online and through social media. Inspires me toward a Marketing career.

Took this class ONLINE and got an A+. She assigns one chapter to read a week, one quiz a week (which you can take twice), and gives extra credit weekly. DO the extra credit! She always replies to questions in a timely manner. As long as you read, do the weekly extra credit there is no reason not to get an A. Learned a lot about internet marketing!

Very responsive to issues with appropriate clarification. Provided feedback in a timely manner. Created a challenging virtual classroom allowing creative solutions to emerge. Made effective use of case studies to stimulate lively discussions. Fostered a positive minded atmospheric of excellence. Highly recommended.

Jennifer Brown is a very knowledgeable Professor. Her class "Online & Mobile Marketing" was more challenging than I expected. She really expects you to learn with the same amount of enthusiasm she has in teaching. I worked hard and was happy to earn a "B". She really knows her stuff. Final project was easy however. I'd take her class again.
No test yet (less than one month left of semester), unless quizzes every week but the answers are to find online.

Jennifer is super amazing. She is incredibly friendly and is always there to help you out if you need it. She's clear on what she wants and doesn't ask for too much. Her class is incredibly simple and well informed. She is also very cool as a person. Just talk to her!
Smart and on top of online directions to help students do well. I will take her again.
Loved her! She never pushed us to feel inadequate with technology or social media (important for 1st online students). Make us tell her what we know. Smart.
Big class of 78, teachers first real regular class and it was good. I learned a lot. She cares about us and trusts us.

She doesn't explain everything, but I think its because in the real world- everything isn't explained, we have to figure things out on our own. Several students had difficulties not having detailed assignments. I can't blame them, but I liked having the freedom. I was online, she responded to my emails promptly.

I took Jennifers class this semester but she's really confusing with homework and tests and how to do stuff. Theres no structure in the class. I do not recommend taking her class, maybe online is better...

Jennifer is super nice teacher:D she is young, so for me i feel really friendly! She invited 2 professors in this semester to lecture. If you want to know online marketing, the class is helpful and it absolutely works! She has teaching also online class. But i recommend to take in class:) i really enjoy the class with her XD Love and miss, Jennifer
She is a great teacher. Very clear in what she expects. Wish she taught more classes.
Taught online and mobile marketing. Learned the basics and got me ready to take more marketing classes. Looks from internet searching like a fun and not old teacher.
Social Media taught my Jennifer was an online class that was great in content and scope. This teacher in "linked in" and shows us how to get the same social media ties. Young and cute, too.

Professor Brown overall made the curriculum easy to understand and complete. Even when there was a misunderstanding, she worked to fix it and was super nice! Overall, an easy A and would definitely take again!

One of the kindest and most supportive instructors I've had at SBCC. She explains concepts clearly and genuinely cares about her students' success. Her classes are well-organized and easy to follow, and she's always willing to help when you have questions. I highly recommend taking her class if you want to learn and feel supported at the same time

Pretty interesting class. Huge workload during the summer; 3 essays & 3 discussion posts weekly. No tests, though. The content is pretty easy though, and you get full credit as long as you hit the word count. Professor Brown is super accessible and super nice. You can expect an email reply in like 10 minutes and she is so, so willing to help.

Professor Brown was extremely helpful with questions about the class. She responds to emails fast and cares about her students. The class was difficult (heavy workload) but very interesting and necessary for Business students. Would take again.

I was pleasantly surprised by this class. Julie does an amazing job of making the coursework relevant and interesting in our everyday lives, and what we aspire to be. She takes business fundamentals, and engages the student to challenge their own understanding and viewpoints. She cares about what we think, and how we think, and gives great feedback

I appreciated how Julie approached the course. She created materials using ChatGPT as an assistant, like we do as students. The class moves fast (16 weeks in 5), but she stayed clear, organized, and supportive. Everything was open-book/open-note, and she wants students to succeed. One of the most transparent, student-centered instructors I've had!

Chat GPT wrote the textbook used; she didn't cite it either. Julie doesn't care if your house caught on fire and will not let you turn in any assignments late. She says "You have 24 hours in a day and 7 days a week to complete the assignment." Be ready to write 3 essays + 3 200-word discussions with an additional response to 2 students each week.

I've had the pleasure of taking 2 classes with Professor Brown, both online and in-person/abroad. She takes her teaching to a level that other professors don't bother to do. She's caring and is willing to work with you/support you on your journey to success. She has hands down been one of the most influential professors throughout my time at SBCC.

Julie Brown is a professor who demonstrates a clear understanding of and appreciation for marketing. She goes above and beyond in showing support for her students' development and future endeavors. The structure of her course is well-organized and thoughtfully designed. I found the assignments and course materials to be relevant and informative.

Class is very easy but my god the material is so mind numbing. Assignments are poorly structured and if your hoping to take this class to get a better understanding of business you will be disappointed. Today we spend and hour copy and pasting recipes for our favorite deserts. Professor Brown seems like a great lady but would not take again.
Professor Brown is an amazing teacher who makes the class engaging and meaningful. Assignments are creative, and she provides great feedback. unstuck study was super helpful for study prep.

Marketing 101 with Professor Brown was a fantastic course! If you stay on top of the course load, you will do just fine. There is a book that correlates with her lecture notes and PowerPoints. Between those three, the tests and assignments are fairly straightforward. All assignments are relevant to the course.

Julie was BEYOND helpful. She is very accessible outside of class, and her lectures and information are very straight forward. If you really put in the work in her class, she takes the time to notice. She may seem like a tough grader, but if you turn your work in on time and participate it's an easy A. Absolutely would take this course again!
Great professor. One of the easiest teachers I've had. Assignments were creative and were a variety of learning about concepts and their application within marketing.
Very flexible with homework and assignments. Deadlines are negotiable.

Julie Brown is an amazing teacher. She is easy to reach and always gives feedback on your work. Such an amazing professor that cares for her students success. She has a very clear grading criteria. Just make sure you do the reading and all the work and you will get a good grade. I highly recommend her class. She's awesome.

Professor Julie Brown in PD100 class is wise and very understanding toward her student. She has no qualms about sharing her life experience with her students which creates a sense of trust between students and an educator. I only feel bittersweet because her PD 100 class ends too soon, and if I can I would participate in all her other classes.

I was so honored to have Mrs. Brown as my PD110 teacher. She is an amazing,funny,caring and loveing person . She is one of the best teachers I have ever had and I'm gonna miss having her as my Profesor. Thank you For being such an outstaning Profesor Mrs.Brown your so amazing wish you nothing but the best :)

I took Professor Brown's PD100 class this semester, and had a great time. All of the instructions for each assignment were very clear. I never felt confused or didn't know what was going on, and she always took the extra time to respond to any questions. I couldn't have asked for a better professor to kick off my education.

Professor Brown had an outstanding course outline that was easy to follow. I was blessed by the fact that she was there guiding and teaching the material with excellent PowerPoint lectures that made Professor Charles Hill's textbook easier to grasp. Professor Brown also gave me feedback on my assignments and many times on my peer-to-peer comments

Do not take her in person classes! She will drop you for no reason. No late assignments. If you give her a legit excuse she will take points off your final grade. 10% of your grade will be taken off per class missed. Her syllabus is wrong so you don't even know what to expect. She does, however extend due dates randomly.

Julie Brown has a true passion for business and marketing. It shows in how she takes pride in her class and encouraging her students. I took her online class this summer and she provided great comments and feedback on the assignments, and she was very encouraging. I look forward to taking another class from her again! Highly recommended!

Professor Brown is one of the best teachers I've ever had! I was strongly recommended to take her MKT 101 class and I'm so glad I did, I've learned so much from her class. She gives feedback on every assignment and she's an easy grader if you make an effort. TAKE HER CLASS.

Loved her! Amazing teacher. Strict on deadlines but gives proper time to complete all assignments. Overall, assignments are really fun and aren't extremely difficult. I would absolutely take this class again with her.
Probably the best class and teacher I have ever experienced. I wish she was teaching more classes so I could have her again. Awesome way to learn business in an online format.

One of my favorite Profs I have had. I have taken multiple classes with her and she is a very responsive and caring person, and her grading criteria is very clear. She is strict on deadlines but gives you a lot of time to complete the work and quizzes. She also helps you create a plan for your educational goals.

If you get the chance, I recommend taking her classes because they are entirely worth it. She takes the time to delve into the course subjects and explains further how to implement them into our everyday lives. I learned so much in this course that is applicable to my life, which is so important. She is incredibly kind and cares about your success!

Professor Julie Brown is absolutely amazing! She is smart and very passionate about how the curriculum is received by us as students. Her subtleness & delivery is easily accessible and helps a lot if you ask for it. She is willing to explain things in multiple ways until you understand. In my experience, she has coined the persona of greatness...

Julie Brown is an amazing professor. I have taken multiple classes with her, online and in person, and enjoyed each one of them. She has been so supportive and I can truly tell she really cares about her students. The lectures are inspiring and I have learned so much that will help me with my career. Would highly recommend!

I took many traditional online courses with Prof. Julie Brown and right now I'm in MKT205. She has been very adaptable during this pandemic - so instead of office hours on campus (#covid19) -- she is using Zoom for office hours. Also, she is super reachable, practically 24/7 and has been an amazing, inspiring, mentor to me - I love her classes!
DO NOT USE AI. You will be caught if you do so. She is a good teacher and you can reach out for help anytime and she is understanding. Fair grader.
Incredibly talented teacher and too cute for school. Wish I could have her as a teacher every year
She is the best! I had her last year and still recommend her to all my friends. Such a caring person. She is the kind of person that would make a great friend.

Professor Bryan is one of the best professors I have ever had. She cares so much about her students and really wants you to succeed and learn from her class. You will read some very interesting books and I feel like I became a much better writer and reader in her class. She is so kind and engaging, I really recommend her/her class.

Professor Bryan is known for her tough grading, but she provides great feedback to help improve writing skills. The class workload is manageable, and she's quick to respond to questions. Unstuck ai proved to be a valuable study tool alongside the provided readings.

Best teacher I've ever had in my school career. Tough grader, but very clear on what needs to be done. She encourages her students to be as social as possible and made this class worth coming to. She will sit down with you and tell you what you need to do to pass. Very interactive and easy to contact outside of class. Would 100% take again!

Bryan is strict in grading papers, gives lots of feedback which is helpful in improving writing skill. A typical week would be 1-2 discussions + essay, and each discussion requires your knowledge from some articles she provided. She is very clear in grading critiques and always has clear instructions. She's a quick responder too.

As long as you complete the work on time and put effort into your assignments, you will get an A. There is a clear rubric for every single assignment if you want to get full credit. If you have any questions, her response time is very quick. One of the best professors I've had at SBCC.

Very unclear on feedback and is a very tough grader. If you do take this class make sure you don't mess up a single part of the assignment or she will give you a terrible grade. Check your essays similarity scores prior to submitting because she will fail you if it has over 10%. Would definitely recommend a different professor.
She's very nice and very helpful. The pacing was great and it was never overbearing

Professor Bryan is an amazing teacher, you can really tell she cares about her students and makes the effort to get to know them, even in an online setting. Definitely wish I could've taken a class with her in person!

I had Professor Bryan for my first semester of my freshman year and she was the best teacher I couldve possibly asked for. The work she assigned was never too difficult but it gave me the chance to be creative. She is extremely understanding and caring towards her students.
Best English prof at sbcc. She checks up on you if you are slacking (in a nice way) and is very caring.

Professor Bryan is SUCH a great teacher! I took her online Eng 110 class and it was great. There are usually 2 assignments due a week. One being a smaller assignment and one being a bigger assignment. The pace of the class was wonderful. Professor Bryan is a very helpful teacher and cares about her students. The work load I would say is medium.

Great professor, extremely caring, and really wants to see you succeed in her class. Very organized for her online class and her homework was reasonable. Take her feedback on your papers seriously and implement it to improve your next drafts. Extra credit is offered and she is easily accessible outside of class. Top pick for English courses.

Professor Bryan was a great teacher. She gives you every opportunity to get the best grade possible, but the hard work is up to you! There's no textbook but she does assign a book that ties with with your final essay, so it's important to READ. She meets with you between drafts to give pointers and wants to see you succeed! Best class at SBCC!
She is very caring towards her students. She has very flexible office hours. I definitely would take her class again.

She treats students with love and respect. An amazing professor, improves your writing but doesn't want the class to be overwhelming. 2 manageable assignments per week, 3 essays, one final. (offers a fourth optional essay to improve your grade) Fair grader, fun, lively, amazing. I love her so much and I am so sad she isn't teaching 111

Professor Bryan is amazing. If you truly wan't to succeed and see changes in your writing I highly recommend taking her English course. She makes you feel comfortable and offers honest feed back. She want's you to pass her class and is willing to help any way possible. The only way to fail this class is not putting in the time and effort.
Professor Bryan is probably the most caring professor I've had. She genuinely seems to care about her students and is always so enthusiastic!!

I loved Bonnys class. She is so nice and really cares for her students. The homework for her class consists of a short response to whatever we were supposed to read before each class period (super easy). The essays in her class are not too intimidating and she gives amazing feedback. This class oddly made me enjoy going to English class.

Bonny Bryan is an amazing, down to earth, and funny woman. I had her for both ENG 110 and 111 and I had a great time in both classes. She gets along with every single student she has and makes this subject fun. Although she is a tough grader, she wants to work with you so that your essays are the best that they can be. I highly recommend her!

she is a very goofy but efficient English teacher. very helpful when it comes to big assignments. she does one on one for all papers that she assigns. she is also very understanding of other things going on outside of class. but mostly I would say read when assigned and don't miss class and you'll be set for her class.

Bonny is a very down to earth teacher who genuinely cares about her students. We had a student who was obviously from a different way of life becuase he would constantly disrupt class, but she handled it incredibly professionally and never once lost her temper. She wants you to pass and is always willing to talk outside of class to make it happen

Because the class was small, there was a sense of community. I think if she really warms up to you, you'll do better in her class. You will actually need to read the textbook to do well and she does have several things assigned in class. So show up, participate and respect her and she will have your back.

Great professor, first English class at SBCC. I am taking her class again for ENG111, she gives really good feedback on essays and made me a better writer. Attendance is important because there are a lot of in class assignments.

Professor Bryan is excellent. Her material is thought-provoking and fun, and her lectures are very interactive. By the end of the semester, the whole class felt more like a community than any other class I have taken. She gives great feedback on essays and is kind, helpful and approachable. One of the easiest and most rewarding classes I've taken.

Bonny is my favorite professor on this campus. She is caring and very understanding! Oh and shes super funny! I took her 110LC class and it was phenomenal! Be ready to write a few essays though they are not hard what so ever. Highly recommend.

I took Bonnys 110/89 combo course and she is amazing! Be ready to write a few essays but she is very useful and an easy grader. She loves listening to her students! I would highly recommend her as a teacher, she is amazing!

I took her online English 110 class during summer. I highly recommend this option for people who want to avoid the theater of in person English classes and just want to focus on writing papers. She gives decent feedback on papers and is a fair grader. The work load is extremely manageable. We had 4 essays, each about 1000 words.

Took online PHOTOGRAPHY 109. Do the assignments & turn them on time, you're guaranteed an A! Assignments are easy- just take pictures per instructions & upload. As long as you put some kind of effort into the photo, you'll get 100%. Quizzes are open book. Just make sure to read the lectures.

Lots of opinions about cropping but overall an awesome class. Bruce is helpful, understanding, and gives creatively challenging assignments. Brooks educated (I think) and a really nice guy! The course is great if you've never touched a camera or have been using them you're entire life!

I took Digital Assets Management (DAM) class from Bruce in the summer of 2015. Great class for those serious about getting their photos organized so that they can find them. Bruce starts at the beginning and works his way through to the end in an easy to understand, building block fashion. The class is only 6-weeks. Don't miss a class.

Extremely tough grader! However, she does give second chances for late work or to make corrections if you don't meet (all) the requirements. Gives an extraordinary amount of hw for a class worth only 1 credit.

This class was manageable to complete over the summer, when I had nothing much to do. Virtually no interaction between professor and student, but he seemed to be an easy grader. Material was a bit dry and instructions could be vague. There is a LOT of reading and writing, so be aware of that. Overall, he seemed alright. Wouldn't take again.

Two exams, consistent workload (forum discussions and short essay each week). Text was interesting, but the lecture videos weren't always clear. Videos and articles other than the book were relevant to exams. Didn't respond to emails. Friends received the same grades for each type of assignment, even in different courses. Pretty easy overall.

Gave zero feedback on essays and discussions, even if I was marked down. Uses an old textbook and probably hasn't changed his course material in years. Extremely lazy professor with harsh grading, and no reasoning provided as to why you receive the grade you get. Avoid if you can.

Dr. Carrasco's class has lots of reading and writing. Homework is based on the textbook. Weekly posts (400–450 words) and replies (150–200 words) are required. Finals are timed essays. He's fair and funny. Lectures can be dry, but show up. I hate reading and still got an A. If I can do it, you can too.

I recommend taking this course online. It involves writing a 400-word discussion post a week (plus replying to two people's 150-word posts) and an essay that's 2-3 single-spaced pages long. This is a lot of writing and time-consuming, but I liked it because it was only an 8-week course that went by quickly. Thomas was an easy grader as well.

The response time was unacceptable. As the semester ended, I was unable to take my final exam online and immediately emailed Mr. Carrasco about it. Despite reaching out to him and others in the department multiple times, I did not receive a response until four days later, by which point I was no longer able to take the exam.

Worst professor I have ever experienced. Doesn't respond to emails or post rubrics or through guidelines for assignments and expects you to write ~1000 words a week with loads of reading. Doesn't care about students or teaching. Avoid at all costs.

The material is interesting, but most of it is self-learn. There are no lectures to solidify the learning of the material. His grading is fair, but be sure to cite all references. I would not retake this instructor.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. Yes, the material is very interesting, and honestly worth reading on your own time, but NOT for this class. The workload is INSANE. You will be reading whole chapters, writing about 800-1000 words total for discussion posts, and have 2-3 page essays required in ONE week. Not to mention you never get feedback. AVOID!!!!!!

While some have commented that attendance for this course is not mandatory, I strongly urge you to take this course with the intention to attend each lecture and be willing to put in your best effort. Mr. Carrasco clearly want to see his students succeed and is accommodating and reasonable with any personal struggles that may come about.

If you want an easy A, take this course. His courses are quite easy to navigate and relatively straightforward: 4 Assignments (Essays), 4-5 Discussion posts on the chapter reading, and 2 Exams. Assignment directions are very unclear, but as long as you cover the main points of each item, you'll be fine. FOLLOW DIRECTIONS AND YOU WILL SUCCEED.

Every week you will have a 400 word discussion and will have to write a response of 150 words to two classmates. There are 3 essays (minimum 2 pages long) 2 midterms (7 written in questions and have only 2 hours to complete) and lots and lots of reading! He gives no feedback, doesn't check in, but ultimately an easy grader.

He's an easy grader. With that ootw, prepare for an incredibly monotonous online class. Boring discussion boards that are way too long, essays and essay-heavy midterm and final with no feedback at all. He responds to emails, but his responses seem copy-pasted from a masterlist. Long readings from book and some chapters arent listed in the syllabus.

Writing prompts often ask us to "define and discuss X" which leads to little critical thinking or innovation. Syllabus is inaccurate, test questions and discussion prompts sometimes ask about material not assigned to us, and everything is based on documentaries, a single lecture video, and book chapters. ZERO feedback on essays or assignments. Run!

This class is an easy A as long as you show up to class but he is so vague. There are never instructions on what we should be doing. We just read from a book, then he lectures for 30 minutes and lets us go early. I learned a lot from this class but just from the readings, he is not very engaged and does not respond to emails.

This was the most disconnected professor I have ever had. Although the course was online, he didn't respond to emails, didn't have weekly lectures, had extremely vague prompts, and had no grading rubric to know why we got points deducted or could improve on. Very writing heavy so be aware that nearly everything is essay based.
Prompts for assignments are kinda vague

Unresponsive, no feedback. Absent as a professor, which is awful online. Writing 800 words and a long essay on a simple concept. The prompts are bare minimum, "define and discuss _" is what every assignment prompts. His policies and expectations are unclear, still don't know if he takes late work even after an email.

not a responsive professor. gives us what would be 6 double page essays every week. makes us read about 3 chapters a week. only lectured once at the beginning of the semester. no feedback on our work. he's an easy grader though.

This class is amazing, the syllabus is very well explained, it tells you everything you are going to see, the forecasts that will come in the exam and it explains very well each part that you are learning.

Assigned readings are from a book which was quite interesting. About three chapters a week. Very doable. I got A's on all three essays. There is a midterm and a final exam with seven written questions-open book. Would take again, but he is not very responsive. You need to ask for feedback on your essay, he does not provide feedback after grading.

Was so excited to be taking this class because I have little to no knowledge of it and was super excited to learn new stuff. I listened to his online lecture for 10 minutes and could immediately see this man was a horrible teacher. Yes he very clearly knew the material but showed no interest in explaining what anything actually meant. dropped class

Professor Carrasco is not good at online classes. He has no grading rubric, where I wouldn't get 100% on an essay, but had no way to know what I needed to improve on. The work load isn't too much, but there is no clear instructions on any of it. He will contradict himself, saying for it to be MLA but single space. Don't take his online class!

Dr. Carrasco is absent and unspecific. The textbook by Dr. Acuna was full of grammatical errors and unprofessional diction. Consistently did not provide enough guidance on what to study in order to succeed. Reading and studying the study guides will still leave you unprepared. His lectures seemed good, but there was only one available.

Don't enroll with him in summer because he uses outdated, blurry material that he doesn't update or contextualize. He doesn't give you study guides for material, nor does he give you a preview of what to look for. If you do enroll with him then, don't expect him to be "present" or caring...or inspirational. He's a totally bland & apathetic teacher.

I've taken multiple course from Dr. Carrasco; expect lots of reading and self teaching. If you follow his exact discussions and essay instructions, read the textbook and go to his study zoom's; easy A. He accepts very late work, and honestly, it's the easiest class. He doesn't give any feedback and isn't personal online.

Be prepared to read a lot. Much of this class was self-taught through the various articles, textbooks, and films. I took it in 8 weeks and did ETHS 101 the second half of the semester. It worked out well for me. He responds to emails and questions quickly. There are 4-5 essays plus midterm and final. The essays are not very long though. great class

CHST 101 was an Online course half semester long. It was a pretty easy course. As long as you do the discussions youll get full credit for them. The essays arent too bad just use alot of quotes from the book to take up space on them. For the Midterm and Final they are essay style format, he is a really easy grader though so its not too bad a class.
He's genuinely a great professor and he teaches the material really well, gives great feedback, and won't hesitate to help you if you ask!

I took his summer online class. It basically just consisted of lots of reading, discussion posts (make sure you meet the word min), papers, videos/films, and the final and midterm. Overall I did learn a lot, but I wish there was lectures and the course wasnt so textbook heavy. Hes a tough grader on papers so exceed them minimum if you want the A!

Juan is one of the most engaging professors I've met, he's fun and professional and really cares about people individually. Weekly quizzes are done on canvas, and labs are usually under 2 hours. Highly recommend.

This is the easiest class I've ever taken. I actually understand chemistry now. He was very understanding and always accepted late assignments with no penalty. He did have a lot of lecture videos but they were easy. The tests and quizzes are easy. He really cares about his students and wants them to succeed.

Juan is amazing. I'm in his Chem 101 class currently. It is the 6th week and the lab is easy and pretty fun. We usually get out an hour to 2 hours early. Lecture is boring, but Juan is really funny with his dad jokes. He is very personable and encourages you to do better. He wants you to succeed more than anything and will personally help you.

Juan is a great professor! He really wants you to succeed in his class. Its not easy but if you put in the effort you'll do great. He is also very understanding. If you have a problem with the an exam time, he is more than happy to give you the test when you can take it.
One of the best teachers I have ever taken at SBCC. He is really nice and cares about his students passing. Explains material very clearly. I would definitely recommend taking him.

had the worst attendance in class, maybe showed up 15 days out of 16 weeks, communicated to her and she reassured me along as I do good on exams I will do good, she clear with how she grades and empathetic, owe her alot. she's the best!
great professor!

Professor Elizabeth Chisholm is the best math instructor I've had. She explains everything clearly and makes the class easy to follow. There's also an in-class tutor for extra support. Highly recommended for anyone who learns slowly or struggles with math.
she was very nice and there was so much help available when things were confusing. She also went very slow and reworked problems for those struggling.
makes it easy to succeed in her class my best teacher ive had

LOVED IT. She makes class time optional and you receive extra credit if you go. She posts lectures online and you can use class time as a free period to ask her questions or do homework with classmates. She also has group quizzes around every week and tests maybe once a month and you are allowed a notecard to use during tests.

she has a lot of attitude/ doesn't really bring a positive presense in the class which made it hard for me to ask her for help on anything. Shes friendly to some students and then passive to some. Whenever I did ask for help she was very responsive/quick however she did make me feel stupid a lot. Shes not accomadating or compassionate.

took her for 160 and 200, both were very flexible in that attendance was only mandatory for tests. this was my first summer math class and she did an excellent job in making it manageable. there were three tests and they were the majority of your grade. overall really great teacher, gonna miss her class :(

Math 200 over summer is quite compact and difficult, however Ms. Chisholm made learning easy and made the class extremely manageable considering the short amount of time summer classes span over. She's a fair grader and doesn't throw curveballs on the exams. She's also very kind and always eager to help. Class was textbook optional.

Hybrid class. The lectures were all online and were just videos going over notes. Really good teacher, some of the videos had mistakes but she quickly corrected them by sending a canvas notification. She also responds quickly to canvas messages. there was a mini quiz at the end of each unit and a final. She did allow for a note card on each quiz.

Best math teacher I've ever had if you can take her class with the support course involves a ton of review of old concepts you may have forgotten. Best class structure of any professor. Do notes before class and stay on top of online assignments this class is an easy A. You get extra credit just for showing up. Highly reccommend!!!

She is the best I wish I could take her for every math course I need!! Great teaching style that keeps very clear & organized. Even if some basics are gone over, it helped to refresh me on things I have forgotten! A tutor in class was helpful also, & it was beneficial to have us try our own problems at the end of class and be able to ask questions
She teachers math well and clearly likes teaching math. The workload was very reasonable with no graded homework. I helped 2 friend sign up for her class and I would take one of her classes again.

my favorite math professor so far, helps a lot in class when you are stuck on question, allowed to use note cards for exams. no homework outside of class. definitely show up to class everyday because if you miss one class you'll get very behind. other than that class is fairly easy and teaches very well would 100% recommend to take this professor.

Terrible. Frequent mistakes on the videos she sends out. these are sometimes corrected, but never to redo the videos. Once gave me a completely wrong exam. Often doesn't respond to emails. Very unprofessional in communication. Seems completely bored. Essentially like not having a teacher at all, as you'd be better to learn material on your own.

Very doable class and many resources provided to teach concepts. Very anti-cheating as it's an online class and to be honest, cheating's not at all necessary if you just put in a bit of work. I worked 2-3 days a week in total for the class (took it in spring over 16 weeks) and that was plenty of time for an A. No more homework than necessary imo.

MATH137 is generally an easy course anyway but her teaching style is absolutely amazing for a math class. She gives an example of the problem, makes you attempt it yourself, and then allows you to converse with the classmates at your table. This really helped me understand what I was learning rather than her just talking the whole time.

I took Professor Chisholm for MATH160 this semester and while the class had a lot difficult concepts, Elizabeth did the best she should to explain the concepts clearly. She's super available outside of class and let us take half the test individual and the other half as a group. If you need to take MATH160 take it with Elizabeth, it's the best shot

Elizabeth is a decent professor who gives good grades and lets you do half of the tests in groups. If you care about the grades and easiness of the class, definitely choose her! On the other hand, her lectures aren't as fun and she's not the best explainer. You might not learn as much in class, and sometimes would be better off watching YouTube.

Chisholm is great. Makes a great grading system where you can redeem grades you don't like. Quizzes are done with groups, and you can use notes. Tests are solo with no notes. 3 of each in total. Not an overwhelming amount of homework. Goes over things thoroughly and welcomes all questions. Fun class to take, would take again.
best math teacher ever! loved her class and makes class an easy A.
Would not take her class in person again.

she was okay but i definitley dont think deserving of the 5 star rating. I got through the class and it wasnt too hard but i have had multiple math professors at CC which were much more engaging and better at explaining the subect in an understandable way.

Elizabeth has a very mellow way of instructing. There is something about her lectures that make math simple and easy to understand but my favorite part about her is that she makes herself really accessible to her students. Take her, she is amazing!

Even tho I got a low grade in her class i would 100% take her class again. The class is very simple as long as u do the online homework and the everyday class notes/worksheet the class is an easy pass. For tests she allows one page of notes so make sure your in class notes are very well detailed. Also there is a chance for test corrections.

Exams are the main part of your grade, which could help or hurt you. She drops your worst score, which was a life saver for me. Watch the videos, and when studying, try to look back to your written assignment, she reuses some of those questions, or slightly changes them. She is a great teacher who makes math not seem so scary!

If you're taking Math 150, take it with Elizabeth. She is such an amazing teacher and super laid back and personable. Her lectures are very clear and if you do all the homework and participate in the discussions, you will be able to succeed in her class. She assigns review quizzes before tests that are very similar to the test material.

had prof chisholm for calc 1 and calc 3 (multivariable). Great professor, gives worksheets and tells you what example in the notes they relate to which helps understanding of doing the problem. Also very good at explaining stuff in her lectures, she explains stuff in simple ways and does it well.
I dislike math, and this professor got me through it. She is pretty amazing.
Professor Chisholm is one of the best math professor Ive had so far. She makes her lecture videos detail and easy to follow. As long as you do the work and ask for help you will pass the class .

Committed professor. She gave really good feedback, making the assignments feel worth my time. It was pretty hard because there was a lot of work assigned but I definitely learned a lot and enjoyed the class even thought I don't really like school. I took ENGLC1000

Professor Clark is really caring of her students, she wants you to succeed and she creates a really thoughtful course. I enjoyed the course material and the setup of her class, just be sure not to fall behind because the work can get overwhelming. It's mostly easy as long as you make the effort to keep up.

Professor Clark was a caring teacher, and is always willing to help you! She is very behind on grading all the time, and also is a hard grader! You will succeed if you show true effort and make sure to make a connection with her by asking her questions and FEEDBACK! I wouldn't take the class you are looking for an easy online class

The most genuine person I've ever met. Professor Clark really wants you to succeed! She has a lot to grade each week, nonetheless, she can devote 2 hours to help you. One of my favorite professors! I would love to have her in Eng111, but she only offers Eng110. Just be sure to not procrastinate, otherwise, you will not succeed.

I learned so much in her class. She's a tough grader but my writing abilities greatly improoved throughout the semester. BE PREPARED TO READ A LOT!!! There is a lot of assigned reading but trust me it's good stuff. Diverse readings (Malcolm X, MLK, and texts written by Mexican American immigrants). There are 3 papers and 1 research paper assigned

I loved having Esther Clark as my Professor. Going to her zoom class was the highlight of my week (especially because everything is online and life is tougher). She is very personable, caring, and wants to help you as much as she possibly can.

I would strongly recommend to avoid this professor. Lectures organization is weak. A lot of homework. Checking essays takes about a month what is unacceptable because you will make the same mistakes in your next essay. Deadlines for students strict but for professor there are no deadlines. The worst professor I had so far.
I loved Esther. You must be prepared to read a lot and do much work, but it is worth it, because you will learn! She is also very fair and nice as a person.

Truly cares about her students. Ive never had a teacher that actually cares this much about her students. Shes extremely relatable and understands we have other stuff going on and have crazy lives and just overall very understanding. She makes everyone so comfortable and sets the perfect environment to succeed. LOVED having her.

Avoid at all costs if you can. An unbelievable workload for such a basic English class(115). Terrible at entering grades and always behind on grading the 5 essays in the semester. Temperamental, goes from joking around to yelling for no reason. Any attempts at giving a different opinion will be shot down by her immediately.

This class is really easy, especially if you need to fulfill a graduation/transfer requirement. The lectures and assignments are pretty easy and interesting. He does not accept late work, but since it is an online class, it's hard to be late. I would definitely take it again!

His online class was really easy and covered interesting music topics. He was a little confusing at times with certain instructions, but overall seems like a chill guy. He also makes sure to tell you when you did a good job and is a very quick grader. Definitely reccomend his class.

Prof Clark is okay. Some of his instructions aren't clear, and when I was docked points for not adding something (because it wasn't in the instructions), I tried sending him a message on canvas and his response basically just said "just follow instructions". Be prepared to watch lots, and lots of videos. Grades quickly though.
This professor was extremely rude via email and canvas comments. I was honestly surprised teachers could speak like that.

This is an easy and enjoyable class. It is well structured with every unit and assignment laid out ahead of time (allowing you to work ahead). The material was very interesting and you can tell his is passionate and knowledgeable on it. Most assignments are opinion based and fun and easy to do. He gives great feedback. Great professor and class!
I took it online and it had good video lectures. Assignments and tests were very reasonable. Not late work accepted. Overall good, would take again

He is one of the best professors I have ever had at CC. There is one assignment every week and a quiz here and there. His lectures and assignments are heavily based on opinion but if you want facts the textbook is a great option. If you love music and want an easy and fun A, take his class. He is also hilarious in the comment section of assignments

This class is very interesting, especially if you are interested in music! I took this course over the summer and the workload was not too heavy. Every week there was a lab, quiz, and discussion, and every other week had a short essay. The material Prof. Clark provides is very informative. I definitely recommend watching the documentaries!

Such a fun class! If you like music you should definitely take this class. Dr. Clark is a really nice guy. We had discussions, essays, quizzes, and labs. Each were worth 25% of our grade. No exams. One textbook required but I bought it online for cheap. We watched movies, read, and listened to a lot of music. Great class and I totally recommend it.

Prof Clark is AMAZING! I took this class 100% asynchronous and loved it, I wish it was in person. The assignments were fun and mainly watching performances and writing about them. If you like music, you will love this class so much. No tests, easy quizzes based off textbook and listening portion. Easy A if you do the work (about 2 hours a week)

TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!! Professor Clark is absolutely amazing and the songs he chooses are always great. I had him last semester in Mus115 and I'm glad I took him again for Mus112. There are quizzes, discussions, labs, and mini-essays. He is an easy grader as long as you put in some effort. Also grades really quickly.

Professor Clark is amazing! You should definitely take his mus115 class. I really enjoyed the class and the music he chose was really good. There were so many songs that I personally listen to which made it even better. I made a playlist on Spotify with all the songs he wanted us to learn and it's such a cool playlist. Taking him again for sure!

PLEASE TAKE THIS CLASS. He is such a cool guy and is very informative. He practically gives you the answers to the tests in the study guides. Listen to the music and get to know it and youll get an A. Loved learning more about the songs I grew up listening to. 100/100 take this guy.

Its 1am and im writing this review about professor John Clark. I was listening to The velvet underground album and it reminded me of one of the classes he taught us about it. He completely changed my relationship with music. Please take his class, and show up! Make the most of it! You will never listen to music the same way ever again.

Definitely my least favorite professor Ive had. Didnt keep track of turned in assignments well, and is difficult to communicate with or ask questions to. His lectures lack structure, and he seems to just ramble about random rock history facts that he knows off the top of his head or puts on some old concert films.

I loved Music 115 with professor Clark. Such a fun class and always lets you go a few minutes early which is awesome. He gives you ALL the songs for the tests on the first day so you have literally all semester to study. He gives you key info for exams so make sure to go to class.. it'll pay off in the end too...

Not even a month into his class-already over it. Tried to ask multiple questions, at appropriate times, & have been publicly disrespected & treated like a nuisance. I can't recommend a teacher that won't consider different ways of teaching & believes that if you don't get an A, you didn't put in any effort. More demanding than a 5 unit course.

Purposely manipulates questions "that you can achieve, on with effort... I'm not just going to through out a softball for you." He is so proud of the fact that if you're not getting an A in his class, then you're not putting in effort. He is test heavy, note heavy, and not very productive during his lectures and just likes to hear himself talk.
Such an awesome class. It was a good start to the morning.

John Clark is the BEST professor I've ever had. He completely changed and expanded my world of music. I only took the class to fulfill some requirements, but it turned out to be the best class I've ever taken. Be sure to go to class, take detailed notes to study from, and do all the assignments on time. Everyone should take a class of John Clark's!
i lov clark :)

John is awesome! Has great stories of iconic rock and pop legends. Tests are primarily on the info he gives at the beginning of the week. Rest of the week is up to you to go to, if you want to show him respect, I suggest going, you might learn some things you never knew. Great teacher, would definitely recommend or take again.

John Clark is perhaps the best professor you will ever have. Just listen to what he has to say, study it, and do his work. Should be an easy A if you study for tests and do all the work. Watch out for the first test cause its the hardest.

This dudes great! If you enjoy Rock n Roll itself, then youll honestly just have a fun time, the guy really know everything about the stuff and it makes his lectures really fun and entertaining. If your not a huge fan, no worries. Just do the work, and put in effort for the tests and your looking at an A.

Clark is THE MAN. Awesome mustache, funny guy, and fun lectures. He appreciates a good joke and always leveled with his students about life and music. Told us great personal stories, gave clear lectures, graded fairly, assigned little homework and created a perfect classroom atmosphere to make friends and have fun while learning a bunch.

He is a great teacher. He gives you the answers to the tests all you have to do is study them. 5 tests and one final total. There's 1 big project and 4 movies on music that you watch. Easy A overall if you do the work.
sarcastic rude too fast

TAKE this class. COOL teacher ever! Go to class to get information of the song lists, and you just remember them and listen the songs, and song listening will be used on the exam. Do the lab work&concert review, and answer his questions on your own words, turn in on time, easy score: 100%

5 tests with max 15 songs on each to memorize. Show up for the first two days introducing each new test and just memorize it key info. You don't need the book and you don't need to go to class every day. Tests were easy and theres only 5 easy hw assignments. If you get an 85% or higher, you don't need to take the final. Textbook is a waste of $
Dr. Clark is tough, but he's good and he knows what he is talking about. If you miss classes, don't expect to get a good grade.

The type of teacher who sticks with you for the rest of your academic journey. Yes, he is a tough grader & there is a lot of material but he's genuinely the coolest teacher I've had. He provides ways to grow beyond academics. You will not regret his course, the material is so interesting & he is such a kind & authentic man. This the GOAT!
Weekly discussions & bi-weekly short essays (3ish pages). His grading scale is sometimes unclear but not that tough. Put in effort and participate in class and you'll succeed.

Great guy. Corny, but he cares. Only issue I had was I had friends who would use chat gpt and get a 39/40 and I'd get a 37 when I put my soul into it. But typing this out I think I'm realizing I'm just not as good as a writer as I thought.

Professor Clark is awesome! He is a tough grader when it comes to discussion posts and essays, but he gives thorough feedback so you can improve. Definitely attend his class and pay attention as that's where most of the information is given. No textbook is required but you do need to buy a few books.

Professor Clark was awesome for my semester! He was clearly enthusiastic about teaching, and always answered questions that students had. His lectures don't really follow a certain set of notes, so you really do have to pay attention to what he says in class to really be able to succeed. That's not too hard thought, he always keeps class fresh.

Ive never commented on this but I had to add Steven to this website because he was seriously the best. Very professional but also really down to earth. I took his fall political Theory class and was actually finding myself raising my hand constantly becuase the topics he talked about were so genuinely interesting. Not too much work too. Essay final
She's amazing. I actually learned so much in this class and she was so helpful. You can definitely see how much she cares and wants you to do well. My favorite professor at sbcc so far!

She was such a great and caring professor! The workload was manageable, and her exams weren't too bad because she let you use a sheet of notes (hand-written). Understanding about accommodations, and will do her best to see you succeed. Definitely go to lectures to understand the material!

Incredible Professor! Genuinely wants to see every student succeed. Will always want to help you work through your homework. Test corrections also help you boost your grade. Put in the work, and you will pass.

TAKE HER CLASS! She is such an amazing professor, genuinely my favorite at SBCC by far. She cares about her students and clearly wants you to get a good grade in the class. Easy A as long as you put in minimal effort. Tests are pretty easy, never took a more chill math class (first math class I got an A in). She is super helpful and accommodating!

Andrea is a great professor. She wants you to succeed and is willing to help you do it. She stays after class to answer questions and is pretty accessible otherwise. Statistics is a stressful subject for many students, but Andrea makes it pretty easy to navigate and understand. I highly recommend you take her for STATS
Extremely patient, empathetic, and considerate professor.

One of the best professors I have had at SBCC. She will work with you and help you understand the material. She is easy to reach and responds within a 24hrs. For exams she gives study guides are great to study with, syllabus, modules are very organized and easy to follow. she uses a free online textbook, but also can buy the textbook from library.
Absolute gem of a teacher. Take her.

Really supportive and kind! Always willing to help and makes sure not to put anyone down when they're struggling. If you've taken any math courses recently, this is basically just a reading comprehension course. She gives an understandable workload, and provides practice tests before every exam.

Although I didn't ace the class that had nothing to do with Professor Cullinen. My lack of effort in the lectures is what led to that but I will say Professor Cullinen was one of the nicest and most kind teachers I've had in a long time. Her work is similar to what we would learn regardless but she actually cares and provides multiple supports!
Amazing teacher and as long as you put in the effort you will definitely pass this class. She's super kind and attentive to giving feedback and wants all of her students to understand the concepts.

Professor Cullinen gave many resources for statistics and was very sweet and took the time to give back feedback and contact students as soon as she could whenever we had any questions. Some of these reviews that have complained about her make me think they haven't experienced a math college professor before. Put in the hours and you will get an A.

THIS IS A PROFESSOR THAT WANTS YOU TO SUCCEED!!! Lots of homework, but very good at teaching; she always provided step by step videos of her lessons which helped a lot. She is very understanding and you can tell she cares about her students. Just don't fall behind because assignments will add up fast!

She has a Master's in Statistics & is an AMAZING PROFESSOR WHO WANTS YOU TO SUCCEED despite Math not being everyones' strong suit. I recommend taking her class if you have math anxiety & would benefit more from a dedicated professor who is willing to take the time to help students. You won't regret it!

I loved taking statistics with this professor. It is easy to mix up the difficulty of this math level with her teaching however I do have to say she made difficult material easier. She would also provide us with many resources to use for additional help such as youtube video tutorials on what we were learning. Overall she wants you to be successful

Prof Cullinen is by far the best math teacher I've ever had! Although it's a lot of content, being attentive and responsible (studying, going to class) will for sure earn you an A. I've passed all my math courses with a C and in this class I found it very easy to follow Andreas lectures and earn an A (:

By far the most bland professor I have had at SBCC, she stands at her desk and talks (to herself it seems) for 2 hours almost every class! She has a significant lack of ability to keep students involved in lecture and is very subpar at explaining how certain materials apply to the course. Absolutely miserable experience, you have to teach yourself.

Professor Cullinen is my favorite professor I've had yet. I'm someone who math does not come easy to, & my major required statistics which I dreaded. However, Professor Cullinen is extremely supportive & caring. Her office hours are amazing & she always makes time for her students. Stats is hard but she wants students to pass & gives extra credit!!

I love Professor Cullinen! She was the first math teacher that made me feel comfortable and confident with math material. She was VERY patient and made sure that if she was explaining something we were actually retaining it. She provides a little bit of extra credit, but homework is very crucial. Textbook is super helpful.

MATH 117 is one of the hardest and dropped classes at SBCC, so some reviews are a reflection of the course and not as much of the Professor. I was impressed at how she handled the course during her first year. Do her homework, understand the lecture, ASK QUESTIONS, and youll be fine. I know a few who made As, and most people who stayed passed.
