Cullinen is a super sweet professor but her lectures don't reflect her tests. It's hard to keep up with her, since there is so much material every class. Homework is graded and helps keep your grade up. Sometimes pop quizzes but they're graded on completion, so you have to show up. If you're not confident in statistics, find a different professor.

She didn't grade anything all semester. If you take her, do the extra credit she offers because you will have no idea what your grade is and whether or not you need it. The course was laid out fine, but she was quite literally non-existent even in the grade book.

The class consists of a weekly lab and quiz, with a comprehensive (hard!) final. Some labs have a short video explaining them. The rest of the labs, you are on your own to search the web and look for information about how to figure it out. There was almost no instruction and I found the class very frustrating as a result. My least fav class at SBCC

Wonderfully clear & succinct. Provides a worksheet packet that's reasonably completed in lab. Cons: Grades very slowly & sometimes explains the lab outdoors in unfavorable conditions (ex: pointing things out on a small plastic map in the wind and sun, etc.). Would've preferred if she'd introduced all topics in lab, before hikes/field trips.

Danielle is a really good professor, one of the most clear and concise instructors in her delivery of core concepts and material. She is super accesible, very understanding and will make time for any student in need. Very fair grading, easier if you are actually interested in the material.

Professor D'Alfonso was super awesome and treated us like people rather than students. Although only two of us showed up to her weekly lectures I would always be able to ask her for help and she would be able to explain it in a way that I could understand!!

Danielle is one of my favorite professors I've had, and it was just a lab class. Super approachable, really cool, and you can tell she loves her field of work! No outside work, I just came to class with a pen and that was enough. We had two tests, but we studied in class together for an hour prior to it. Very easy if you show up! Would take again.

BEST professor I've ever had! I've learned more in her class than most of my other classes combined! Her lectures are engaging and her assignments are fun! I transferred to UCLA and none of my professors come close to being as good as her. Most of the other SBCC geography teachers are way too easy and you barely learn anything.

Outstanding professional and Danielle dose zoom meeting once a week. Basically the work is broken down and covered in the zoom meeting's. It's physical geography lab.... Ask questions during the discussion's. ����������

Although Danielle seems like a sweet professor who cares about geology, this was a very stressful and draining class. The labs took way longer than previous lab classes I've taken and most questions were complicated or very detailed. This 1 unit class took the same amount of effort as my 5 unit classes this semester. Two long exams & tough grader

I took Professor D'alfonso's lab online due to co-vid and it was not the best. The lab assignments took hours, very detailed and you have to use a computer that has google earth. The exams were difficult and she never replied to any of my Canvas messages. She also didn't grade any of the assignments until the last week, which was stressful.

Without hesitation the WORST professor I´ve ever had. The only reason why I struggled with this class was that she was so unclear about everything. She would say one thing and then do the complete opposite. She posted the study guide at 11 pm when we had the test 11 am the day after. Said she would post a study guide for the final and never did.

Danielle is super passionate about the subject she teaches and you can tell she cares for her students. I believe this was her first time lecturing, when she is used to doing labs so she may be getting used to it. She has study guides for the tests, but only a couple questions from the study guide that end up being on the test, the rest is random.

Prof. D'Alfonso is just as excited about her lab class as her students. She is very enthusiastic and full of energy which tends to make all of her students excited too. A lot of amazing field trips to look forward to almost every week. She is mellow and makes sure that everyone understands the topics by thoroughly explaining them.

I generally dont like science classes but I really enjoyed taking Danielles class. Its once a week for 3 hours which is normally a long time for me but the time goes by fast in her class and generally after you get your lab done you can leave. Average I could leave around 2-2.5 hours of class time. Danielle is super caring and helpful.
Worst teacher I've ever had. Doesn't teach anything and to be quite honest just a cornball.
Avoid taking his class at all costs. If you can take it his class with Curtis or Osiris
Creepy and mean

I chose the course to learn how to produce a movie. Instead,he talked a lot and didn't get to the point, weekly quizzes, lots of material.Only made one movie with entire course, outside of class sometimes 13h on Saturday. At the end we had to evaluate each other, but there were only 7 A;B;C. Don't take his class FP 175!

He doesn't actually teach cinematography or show you how to use a camera. He just plays movies for a couple hours and explains why certain shots are so cool and hangs out with his favorite students. I stopped showing up after two months and learned how to film, utilize lighting, and editing by myself. I have no regrets.

The only reason Steven Davega's rating is over a 3 is because RMP keeps taking down negative reviews of him because he's SO BAD that it's nearly impossible to talk about his class without being derogatory. This man is an awful teacher, an awful person, and students deserve to be warned about him. He's a creep and a bad professor, plain and simple.
A lazy man, who will creep on the attractive girls in the class. Left his wife and kids for his student after he knocked her up

Take this class with another teacher if you can- seriously!! He's genuinely such a hard person to work with and only gives 2 A's per production group which is why I got a B+ despite being very involved with my production and getting an A on all assignments. He didn't like me bc i was slightly confrontational (bc he says the most ridiculous things)

Davega is a great professor, if you are interested in pursuing film. One can not expect to be accepted into the film industry, simply because of interest and dreams. Davega builds that work ethic from the start, you want him to respect you or like you, you need to prove yourself and pay attention. How to succeed in the class is simply do your part.

Stephen Davega is a bad person. He left his first family for a student right after her graduation, and it is rumored he was sleeping with others. He is always political in class, and excessively rude, especially to the foreign students, going as far as to tell them they wasted his time because they spoke a non-english language in his class.

I ignored this professor’s reviews and I seriously wish that I had not. Do not enroll in his class if you want to keep your sanity. This man will argue about absolutely everything and berate you for the simplest things. He does not teach you anything and instead sends you into a production with no preparation. Doesn’t ever answer emails.

Davega is absolutely hilarious. However, if you have thin skin and cant take corrections, this class as well as film in generally is probably not for you. He prepares you well for the future and is a pretty easy teacher. He does criticize you and your work, but only to better you for the future. Trust me, he doesnt ACTUALLY hate you.

If you are sensitive to criticism, and can't stand having your work dissected, then not only is Davega not the professor for you, but you should probably switch to a non Film Production major. Expect to go above and beyond outside the classroom. If you can thrive (or merely survive) Davega, you have a shot at the industry. Period.

I liked Davega, I wish I could've taken an in person class with him, His lectures were long but taught a lot and focused on the important items. I unfortunately was dealing with someone in the industry who was causing me hardship in my life and fell far behind in the class and was dropped. Not Davega's fault, I blame COVID and toxic personalities.
Tough grader and favors individual students which creates a bad class environment. Lectures are mostly watching videos, which I could find myself as well. not my favorite
worst class and professor Ive had at sbcc

Be ready to devote a lot of hours outside of class. As with every other class DaVega teaches, make sure to show interest. Participate in the discussions, and ask questions. Read everything he posts to the class, he gives it to you for a reason. If you want a good grade, you also have to impress your production group. Be on top of your game!

Make sure to attend class, and pay attention. Spend the assigned periods to work. Show him that you are interested, ask questions and have discussions. He will take better care of him if you show your interest. Don't just wait if you wonder about something. Figure it out or ask him. His hard shell is just to scare people off. He is a cool guy.

Stephen has a lot of knowledge but tends to go off topic a lot. He will have you do a lot of assignments on your own time so make sure you can set time aside. He will call on you in class and call you out if you don't know the answer. He also tends to teach and involve stuff from other classes as well.

The only reason I got an A is because I already knew some editing. He goes through it too fast. He says he gives points for participation but only ask questions for the same 3 people. He ASSUMES you're taking his other film classes. You feel stupid for asking questions IN CLASS. It's not "showing you like it is", it's simply being a bad professor.

The best professor for Film Production I. Make sure to show up, do the hand ins, and put in the extra effort during all of the production phases. Go beyond your assigned role, do not wait for people in your group to tell you what to do, be ahead of the game, and you will be graded thereafter.
He does not care about you. Doesnt tell you your grade. Acts like a frat boy. Wears shirts that are way too tight. And he does not care about you. He will fail you even if you do everything he asks.
Clear expectations and great sense of humor. Put in the effort and you'll do well!! Easy to get in contact with outside class and is willing to provide feedback.

This industry isn't for the faint of heart. This industry isn't for kids who think that their scripts actually matter to people who aren't their friends. If you thought DaVega was too hard for you or unfair I cringe at the thought of how you will react when a real producer breaks your will down. DaVega is the real deal. Work with him, not against.

No clear grading criteria. His lectures are over stuffed. Do not take his classes especially if Film is not your passion and just want to take a class to learn basic skills like editing a youtube video.

Be ready to spend a lot of free time on the film project. He tells you that if you skip class twice he will drop you. He doesn't give a damn about if students like him. I did everything I had to do for this class and still got a B. He is also the reason why I didn't take film as my major.

That was a really fun semester. I didn't appreciate it until I went to work and was the only one who had all the knowledge. Practical test in the end of the semester in the best thing that happened to me in this school-you really learn things! He's not a sweet person-he'll make fun of your mistakes so the class is not for sensitive people.
You can tell that he has a lot of experience but he can't listen and has trouble communicating with his students.
I learned a lot from his class. The videos he shows in the class about cinematography was super helpful as well.

Tears down students work/ideas/passions/dreams in front of entire class as a way to "teach" them then says he is "just telling it how it is." Very unfortunate. Then wonders why so many people drop his class.

One of the best professors I've ever taken. She is so sweet and genuinely cares for the well-being of her students. There is a decent amount of homework, but if you're on top of everything, you will easily pass.
Professor Davis is great! Workload was beyond manageable, and the coursework was relatively easy. Definitely take her class if you have the chance!
Professor Davis has been the best professor I have ever taken. Take her if you have the chance. A lot of homework but a reasonable spread out. She cares about the student success
Very relatable teacher and is very graceful with her workload and grading.

I enjoyed taking this class with Davis. As long as you put in at least a little effort you will pass no problem. In class and homework assignments are easy but can be time consuming. She seems to be kinder to those who participate and show up often, but is overall very kind and understanding for late work. Would take again.

Professor Davis is one of the best professors I've taken. She's a spectacular person + very understanding. Would most definitely take her again. Not heavy reading and grading is based off your understanding of the lecture. Such an amazing professor.

Easiest class I've ever taken. She's super clear on the assignments, you just have to complete the work. Due dates are a bit weird, and you can't access work until the day before it's due. Not reading heavy at all. As long as you put a little bit of effort in, you get full credit on everything.

Worst professor I have ever had. She dropped me from her class 4 weeks in. I had two missing assignment and no absences. I had emailed her and explained my situation and why those assignments were missing, but without warning, she dropped me anyway. She gives the longest "in-class lectures" and homework assignments with little to no feedback

Very understanding about assignments. If you have any trouble or something to say about your grade, reach out to her. My class was online and she gave a decent amount of homework for one class. I had four other classes, but all the work was manageable. The work is not too difficult for the majority of the semester. I highly recommend her class!

You have to complete every single assignment with a passing grade or she fails you, often these assignments will be multiple 3-5 page essays a week on top of "in class work', I have a high grade in the class and missed one assignment and she almost failed me right at the end of the semester. This class criteria is ridiculous.

Dont take this class. If you miss one day of anything she will plane out drop you. For no reason. If you want 6 assignments a week with weird due dates and the toughest grading criteria ever existed then thats for you! Worst teacher I have ever experienced.

I took her class for Fall 2021 and it was one of my favorites! Her lectures are informative and she gives nice comments on assignments. I'm not the best at English but her class was pretty easy. There was always homework and mini essays due in a week but it's doable if you manage your time correctly.

The course contents are inspirational & reflective. But you should not take the course unless you have really sufficient time for it, especially if you take the online form in summer, which all the lectures were made into essays. Workload for 3 days of this 3-credit ENG111 exceeds the workload for an entire semester of the 4-credit ENG110 I took.

I took this class as an online summer course, so it was condensed over 6 weeks. I know now that was a huge mistake because there were no actual zoom meetings, and for 2/3 assignments that were always due the next morning, you had to write multiple pages for each. I felt like the professor was taking multiple-page essay writing very lightly.

Professor Davis is one of the kindest professors I have ever had! She is so accommodating and you can tell she truly cares about her students. The readings she provided were super interesting, and she bases grading off of the effort she sees that you put into your work. I really enjoyed this class and would 100% recommend taking her!

I took this class during COVID and so I had her for online and it was pretty bad. She never uses canvas and had us doing so much reading and writing work everyday. I think I may have liked this class if it was in person but I wish I hadn't taken it online. She seems like a nice person but NOT equipped for online.

I'm taking this class during the covid closure. She doesn't use canvas. The class is completely unorganized. Sends at least 4-6 emails a day starting at 6am and gives daily writing assignments due w/in 24 hrs. Very unclear with instructions and hard to locate assignments in emails! New assn'ts every day but they're all due at the end of the week.
Shes a great professor and youll get an A if u do the work and exceed the minimums! Be sure to participate though !

Took her class for summer hybrid, and OMG DO NOT TAKE HER. She does not use canvas, so you will not know your grade until literally the end of the semester. I am going to drop it without a"W" (luckily I was on time for that), because I do not want to chance getting anything lower than an A-. DO NOT TAKE. I would rather take with better prof.

Dont even get me started on how she handled the switch over to online, it was awful! She obviously is not technologically sound and she didnt know how to use canvas. If sbcc is closes down for the fall I definitely wouldnt take her! I wouldnt even take her if sbcc opens back up for the fall. She was a bad experience in the classroom too.

I am going to give you the most honest description of how my semester went with this professor. In the beginning everything was fine. She is nice with her students/ lenient at times. The class was honestly very boring and personally I didn't learn much. She never uses canvas to post grades or assignments which is the most frustrating thing ever.

I love Cynthia so much! She's a very easy grader, sure, and likely there are people who didn't learn much in ENG 111, but I did! She certainly provides the tools to learn effectively. Her historical background lectures are interesting and she's open to students' opinions and actively seeks them out. 1-2 page informal essays twice a week.

Used to like her but with all the coronavirus news she spams us with emails and then gets upset that we don't look at every single email. There are days she sends us 12 emails, starting at 6am. Homework takes about 80 minutes and get ready to write tons of papers. She's nice but itd be even nicer if she learned how to post grades to canvas.

Great Professor, only downfall is the complete lack of online usage. There is no class on Canvas, so you cannot check grades or anything online without asking her in person. Lots and very frequent papers, typically a 2 page essay every week. But this sounds more daunting than it really is.

This teacher is hands down the worst teacher I have ever had in my life! She is very old fashioned and doesn't know how to use Canvas. I couldn't check my grades myself and had to rely on her to check her "grade book" in order to constantly see where I was at. She is also a late grader which makes it worse!

Cynthia is a very nice woman. She is accessible outside of class and even gives students her cellphone #. I was often bored in class because it was so easy but that is not a reflection on her teaching as much as the curriculum. She is very understanding as long as you're honest and try hard.

Her personality can be very disrespectful some days and sometimes she can be nice. Most days she is disrespectful and passively aggressive towards some of her students; especially to an international kid in our class where english wasn't his first language. Instead of being patient with him she constantly raises her voice at him and frowns at him.

Professor Davis has been nothing but the sweetest, most comprehensive instructor I had this semester. I went through personal struggles and she went above and beyond to reach out and help. And when I say beyond, I mean she came to me, to help me, and not the other way around. She is supportive, understanding, and will do her best to help you!

DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS. She is the worst professor I've ever met. Very old fashioned and definitely horrible at teaching. Two 2 page papers every week, usually a summary following a prompt or an analysis. The workload isn't the worst part tho. She goes over the material after we've written the essay. Rude, unreasonable, wish I could give a 0 rating

If you are an international student, don't choose this teacher. The teacher's requirements for international students are the same as those of Native American students. I don't think it's fair. In your homework and exams, if you make mistakes in your grammar, you will get zero.

She's great! I highly recommend going to the tutor as soon as possible. That made my retention and understanding way better. She lets you bring your textbook and notebook to class and is great at explaining concepts. Genuinely don't miss more than like 1 or 2 classes though.

Profesor Davis is such a great teacher. As someone who was terrified of taking stats due to failing in high school, she is fantastic. She lectures so clearly using humor to engage you. All exams and quizzes are open book and open note. highly reccomend!

The most down-to-earth teacher I've had at SBCC. She is an open book as an individual and it really helped everyone feel comfortable and engaged with her. I've spent my entire adult life thinking math was simply an impossible subject, but believe me, you can ABSOLUTELY ace this class if you put forth some effort - it's not as scary as you think!

She's amazing. A really good person. Gave me a ride one time. She's dope. Also try's her best to help students who don't have a passing grade up to the final. Just have to pay attention and do the reading honestly. I hate math with my whole heart and she honestly made stats my favorite class this semester

Honestly the realest professor I have EVER had at SBCC, she makes this class interesting and really makes you engage i love how she shared life experiences with her students and is also very funny. She always helped me when I had questions outside the classroom and very easy to get a hold of. Of course if you put in the work you will pass :)

I had Professor Davis this spring and she was awesome. Lectures are aligned with the book and all assignments/quizzes/tests were online. Really funny and understanding, also very helpful! Loved her lectures and would definitely take a class from her again.
Professor Davis is so hilarious and makes class so interesting! She really cares about her students, when I needed extra help she would stay after class with me till I understood the work. Amazing!!

I did not find Rebecca to be all that helpful as a teacher. She's a nice lady and fun, but I can't say I learned any statistics. Although, I gave up trying at some point because the class moved so slow--that might've had something to do with it.

Professor Davis, is awesome! Always willing to help, easy to get in contact with and will always do whatever she can to ensure students pass the course! She offers extra credit. You. do have to do the usual text book reading on your own prior to going to class but she does her absolute best to make sure everyone understands!! take her class!!
She is the sickest teacher at SBCC....

P. Davis is by far my favorite teacher ever. She is so funny easy-going and understandable. The actual criteria is a bit difficult and we had hw just about every class, but if you pay attention and ask her for help you will get an A. Due to Covid-19, we only ended up having two exams including the final, super accommodating! Would 100% recommend!!!

I took SOC 125 to fulfill my math requirement, but I am so glad I took it with Rebecca Davis. Out of all my classes, this one was one of my more fun and engaging classes, and she makes everything make sense. She also made the transition to online classes (due to COVID) smooth, yet still engaging. If she taught other classes I would take them!

I haven't had any luck with other math professors during my time at SBCC up until Professor Davis. Hands down, one of the best professors! She's extremely caring+supportive. Maths tough for most, but she's willing to help those who need it and is available outside of class. If you attend class, do the homework, and ask for help you will pass!

I hate math, but I LOVE Professor Davis. She is flexible, funny, and just wants to help you get through the class. She is accessible outside of class too if you have any questions. I dont know how, but she made intro to statistics fun. She made the transition to online due to COVID-19 seamless. You wont regret taking this course!

Professor Davis is one of the best professors I've had!Never liked math before, but she was helpful and accessible. She is also hilarious and shares a lot of fun stories, which made me enjoy the class. You can tell she cares about her students and wants them to do well. Make sure you do the homework, put in the work, ask for help and you'll pass!

I actually LOVED professor Davis. Funny, unique lady. Wish there were more professors like her. Told a lot of great stories and gave a lot of truthful advice. Offered extra credit. If you had an A going into the final, you didn't have to take it. She understood the class was early in the morning and wasn't unkind to those who wandered in late.

I am taking SOC125 this semester, and she is my favorite teacher ever! She is super nice and funny. She wants her students to be the best they possibly can, and gives them opportunities to higher their grades. Easy A if you do the homework (not a lot of homework). Would definitely take her class.

Theres not a lot of work in this class, so do whatever she asks of you because it counts for everything. Offers a lot of extra credit. Class is not by the book, but shes a funny lady with a lot of interesting stories, and trust me youll learn more from those stories than some old $100 textbook.
I LOVE MICHELLE.

Ms. Diaz is a petty grader. She took off 2 points from an assignment, because I didn't use a "standard" font, like New Times Roman. I checked the syllabus & there is no font specification. Also, there was no rubric for this assignment, denoting the font specification. Her grading is subjective & shows her inability to provided meaningful feedback.

She can be rude. I sent her an email telling her that I wasnt able to complete an assignment due to a busy schedule at the time. I asked politely if I could have an extension and she quickly denied it. She is quick to judge. I would not recommend taking her if wish for this teacher to make you feel like a failure. I repeat do not take her class.

You should be aware of a few things before taking her class. She's a strict grader and doesn't give any feedback when she grades something. She doesn't tell you why you got the grade you did which isn't helpful.
She seems like a sweet person and all but she was a strict grader. A little not clear with what she wants and clearly prefers some students to others but fine I guess.

Sarah Diaz is a tough grader, and will make you work for that A. When receiving feedback on work, ask for specific details and more feedback again. Adjust as necessary to her liking to make that B+ an A+. Whether grading criteria is fully covered by your work or not, depending on her likes, quality work will not receive an A. Ask for more feedback.

was the hardest ECE class i have taken! a lot more work than i thought it was. attendence matters for this class, never be absent on a presentation you automatically lose 2 grades for assignment. she was very thorough in her lectures and the group activities. be sure you do the reading for the class there are pop quizzes!
Sarah makes the class very welcoming, comfortable, and enjoyable. It's not difficult at all. Pay attention, do the few assignments, and you'll get an A. Amazing teacher!!!!
Love her! Such a great ECE prof . She is passionate about her both jobs. Gives awesome feedback and grades super quick so you always know where you stand in the class
Sarah is amazing she is so knowledgable and she is a pretty easy grader. She does have a great personality but the class material is a little boring and thick

I took her ECE 102 class not knowing what to expect. Turns out it has been the best class I have taken so far. All the reading material can be found on Moodle. This class is something you can look forward to.

Sarah is awesome especially if you know her background. This class is a lot of sitting through powerpoints the first couple weeks but the assignments are easy if you do not procrastinate. Fun class and pretty easy if you go to class, let us work on assignments in class as a group and you do the majority of work in class besides observations

This course was challenging for me . I am not fond of documentation. This instructor did a great job of making the class more interesting and avoided monotony. When I was struggling with an assignment she was there to help me. She has a vibrant and jovial personality.

Unhelpful, would not take again. If this was still my major and I HAD to take her again to finish a degree I would switch majors just to not come in contact with her teaching again. Honestly lazy as a teacher, websites, student involvement, video watching, and other people's texts are what make up the class. She acts like she's a PhD.

Sarah is very unclear about what she expects of you. Told our class we could use the 8th edition of our book and weeks before the end of the semester says we need the 9th edition. Isn't responsive to e-mails. She treats her class as if it's the most important class you have and piles on the assignments. Get ready for lot's of tedious work.
Bill is such a wonderful professor. I go to UCSB now, and his lectures on mineralogy are still better than any I've received so far at UCSB. He's really knowledgable and kind.
This was one of my favorite classes. If you love rocks, this prof can teach you everything you want to know. I would definitely recommend this class!

He makes you think about problems he gives, which can be challenging, but are also really fun. Go to tutor hours because the tutor is nice and they are really helpful. We work in teams, so be prepared to collaborate.

This was his first time doing a microgrid project for his energy class, we were his guinea pigs and we felt the consequences of that. This class was fun, and you can tell Bill is really passionate and knowledgeable about the subject. He has teambased learning which can bite you in the butt or save you -- depending how good of a teammate you are.

Bill uses the flipped classroom setting; students read relevant chapters before each quiz, ~6 thru the semester. Bill then spends class having you apply your knowledge with your team that he places you in day 1. The class isn't hard, just a tad time consuming before each quiz. Participate, read, and you'll get an A. Lots of EC opportunities too.

Bill Dinklage has been on of the most inspirational professors I've had the privilege of learning from. He takes the time to explain the answer to your questions or tells. If you go talk to him he is very understanding and will do anything he can to help you. He is very straightforward with you and talks to you on eye-level.

Bill teaches this class in teams, which can't always be reliable, but the teams are big enough so that you're not the only one participating. He teaches at a somewhat high level for an intro course, but I really appreciate it. His tests are difficult but you have to just read his questions carefully.
The class isn't easy but it is very worthwhile. Quite a lot of your grade depends of group work, so having a good group definitely makes things easier. Great lectures, fascinating subject material.

He expects his students to understand super complicated information, with basically no introduction at all. Every class is just group work or quizzes, with a lesson scattered here or there. He needs to do more teaching in his class.

Prof. Dittman was an amazing professor and I highly recommend taking his class. He is very knowledgeable and his lectures are very engaging. Even if Philosophy is not your major this class is very eye opening and interesting. Professor Dittman is also very understanding and helpful.
Loved this class, such a chill guy. Super interesting, great teacher, great lectures, easy grader, not too many assignments, got to go on a cool field trip. Definitely would recommend.

He's so smart, respectful, & kind. Well-paced fascinating lectures! Wish more professors lectured like this! Grades based on short weekly discussion posts with clear directions on interesting topics, online quizzes, 2 easy multiple choice tests, and a short final paper. Allows late work and is happy to help! Loved having this class!
Genuinely one of the smartest people I have ever come across, and he is so sweet and caring. Would definitely recommend, I learned a lot!

He's super cool, super chill. He is very knowledgable about religion. You write a final paper at the end of class. Classes were always enjoyable, since he made the content interesting and easy to understand with plenty of videos.
I loved this class so much and the professor made it super fun and easy to learn the material. There is one paper at the end of the class, other than that the work is easy and informational.

Definitely an easy class to take online! No office hours or live zoom classes to learn anything from. Only a midterm, test , and final paper are graded. He gives you answers to the tests beforehand. Overall, you're not really learning anything. Easy class

I took him online and I feel like it would've been super different in person- with that being said... you basically had to watch like a 3 hour lecture video and read a whole chapter every week- the lectures are incredibly boring and he uses a lot of words that no one knows. But he basically gives you the answers to the tests so that's a plus.

Great guy! I took his 6-9 and never really dreaded it, although he lectures for a full 3 hours, you are able to do homework for other classes throughout it. Only a midterm, finals and 6 page research paper! If you are interested in religion then this class will be very interesting!! He is very educated on religion and knows how to present it. EASYA

LOVE this professor! He lectures his entire class which can be boring if you aren't interested the subject. But you don't even have to pay attention! Before the midterm and final he tells you all the answers to the tests in order so it's just memorization, I got 100%. There's 1 paper but he's a super easy grader. Do yourself a favor by taking him!

He knows his stuff. The text is great and the class lectures are very good. He teaches with passion and critically engages with religion fairly and respectfully. I would recommend him for anyone trying to fill the cultural requirement!

Awesome teacher who really cares about his students and about the material he teaches. The text (The World's Religions) is a great read, and lectures supplement the material with more detailed explanations and facts. 8am class was v difficult to make, but attendance isn't mandatory. He is also very accessible if you have any difficulties.
Prof. Dittman clearly knows his stuff. Not only does he truly care about the material he teaches, he also truly cares about his students. If you have the chance you should definitely take his class.

Comparative world religions was my favorite class last semester. Take it with Prof. Dittman. He makes the topics super easy to understand and interesting. He really knows his stuff, plus goes over what will be on tests and grades fairly.

Awesome teacher! Highly recommend him. Really passionate about what he teaches and really interesting lectures. Low workload and gives lots of feedback. If you're thinking about taking him, do it! You won't regret it.

Professor Dotson is great and its very clear how passionate she is about art. Shes very understanding that it is many peoples first time doing art at a college level and more so grades on your progress and effort in your art than how perfect it turns out
took 140 and 190 loved both, awesome projects, she is just caring and funny, fun classes, not a crazy amount of work but depends how dedicated you are
has a good amount of assignments, art projects and discussions. But many of the projects are quite simple with clear explanations.

This class was a lot of work, but that's how you become a better artist. I initially thought some of the assignments would be lame, but ended up loving them! I gained a lot of knowledge and developed my technique in different mediums. She is encouraging and leaves good, detailed feedback. I would definitely recommend taking a class with her!

Stephanie is intense in her classes and although she is a bit more on the stricter side, she is also super chill. I will say that this class takes up a lot of hours. I spent 1/2 of my time finishing projects outside of class so keep that in mind because it will hurt your grade if you don't take time out to finish the projects.

This class has limited space, too many students enrolled and the competitions for resources is intense! You have to devote a TON of hours outside of class and even then its like chickens w/their heads cut off in the lab. She is not welcoming to creative differences, she has favorites. But did help push my work. She can be cold and moody at times.

Stephanie is one cool cat! From an art student who knew only a little about printmaking she taught me a lot about many different types of it. She does require a lot of work and you will have to spend a lot of time in the studio in order to complete your work. But she is fun, interesting and interested in your work! Highly recommended.

She changed my life! She is incredibly talented and smart. I learned to much from her and really appreciate the structure of her class and the way she explains projects. I also loved the projects. I ended up with a lot of really great work at the end.
Excellent communication skills and energy. Teaches new technologies for printmaking in addition to traditional techniques.

Took Printmaking as a requirement for an AA in Studio Art, so I had some vested interested, but Stephanie sparked my interest in printmaking and is very inspiring and insightful. I only wish I had more time in my schedule to take more classes from her. Plus she is supa hawt!

hands down THE coolest hippest quirkiest art teacher ever! shes easy to relate to, she really helps you, and you become more creative every class.the assignments were very interesting and she gives you ideas to plant in your head to really come up with a great project. come to class and you will learn more then you ever thought u would. i promise!

Great teacher! Young enough to relate to you. She expects a lot out of her students - assignments turned in on time, make it to class and be awake in class - but she's willing to go the extra mile to make sure that you understand the assignments and concepts. She has great energy and I found her to be upbeat and entertaining. Highly Recommend!!
No Comments

I thought she was a great teacher. The class was more difficult than I thought it would be. She expectes your projects to be very clean and she likes perfection. She likes you to think out side of the box. She is a pretty easy grader. She has an interesting personality and I thought she was pretty funny.
Love her!!!!awesome teacher and relates well to her students

The only teacher I haven't liked. Claims her class is accepting, but is really rude if you ever raise your hand or answer a question. She tries to embarrass you, and I felt confused the whole class. I got an A bc it's not a difficult course, but being in this class was infuriating. She says we dont have enough time, but wastes time with meditation

Dana is an amazing woman who cares deeply about her students and about the things we can learn from literature. You're gonna have to read a lot and write some papers, kinda how English classes are lol. Yeah, it sucks, but we've all gotta get it outta the way. Dana is understanding, flexible, and helpful.

This was by far the worst professor I have ever had. If you are anything short of a perfect writer, you will not receive an A in this class. She has no organization and takes valuable time out of class to "meditate". Do not take this class because you will regret it.
I hope for the sake of everyone thinking about taking her class that she retires soon. Worst teacher Ive had in my entire life. Be prepared to be utterly confused the whole semester

This is absolutely the worst class I have ever taken!!! No real grading or points, she will just assign you whatever grade she thinks make sense to her at the end of the semester. She teaches as if she is teaching a kindergarten class. Meditation replaced valuable class time to discuss prompts/other unclear things. Deeply regret taking. plz retire

Not the most enjoyable class. She cares for her students but, she lacks the organization and transparent grading system you'd want or expect in a class. Honestly felt like she didn't even read our final projects.

Professor Drobny (aka Leelee) is a really good teacher if you actually care about the case. We have about 8 homework assignments over the whole class, one project, and three essays. The work is really easy and she gives you a lot of time to finish everything. Dont try to scam a grade out of her, although shes very nice she doesn't give into tricks.

Do you want clear directions and a good teacher? Dont take her course. She is a very kind person but you will struggle with the very unclear directions she will give you. Every assignment so far Ive guessed on what to do, and although I did earn a B+, it was very not worth it. Kind person, horrible teacher.

super sweet but super disorganized and very unclear with directions. I had to go to her office hours just to understand her essay prompts clearly. very picky about what exactly she wants in an assignment and she does not keep track of grades. you won't know your grade until finals week and overall her class was extremely stressful
She is incredibly unclear with her assignments and expects very unconventional work. Before she walks into the room the entire class is saying how hard her class is and how they are beyond confused.
Lots of extra credit, fun essays, and good lectures! Very easy to pass, Dana just wants to help her students. She's really kind and wants everyone to just feel good in her class.
Disorganized and feels like a high school English class. Get ready to review the same topic again and again with little feedback and direction.
Super unorganized which can be annoying, she doesn't really teach either.. not very helpful but she caring and compassionate Ill give her that

Dana Drobny is an incredibly disorganized, dysfunctional professor. She does not want her students to succeed and encourages all of her students to do the bare minimum. I've never had a worse professor. I will need to add more to this rating later, but for now, Students of Dana beware. She's extremely unclear and hardly "mindful."

Dana gives interesting lectures. Be aware she's not the average english professor. She begins every class with 2 minutes of meditation. Her prompts are not always clear, but she's always happy to help students who show interest. She also makes all her students talk (which is not that easy if you're shy. Just tell her if it makes you uncomfortable).

Dana really cares about the well being of her students. She gives a ton of extra credit opportunities that are actually cool to go to. She starts and ends class with two minutes of silence to be mindful and it really was the best part. Prompts are kinda confusing but if you ask her for help she will.
Dana is an inspiration from day one. You have to be in the right space tho. Be prepared to tap into yourself rather than just having mind numbing readings.
A very sweet lady, values the well-being of her students.

Dana is a wonderful professor. I had her for my first semester here and I'm very happy I was. She cares about teaching english and she cares about the students more. She'll do as much as she can to make sure you're successful. Take Dana!

I took her personal development class in hopes of becoming less stressed. She is a great teacher who opened my eyes up to self-awareness and mindfulness. THIS CLASS CHANGED MY LIFE. I WILL USE WHAT I HAVE LEARNED FOR THE REST OF MY LIFE. Go into it with an open mind and motivation to become a better person.

Dana was not the best teacher I ever had however she was not the worst. Her attitude was not always a good one. She was more helpful towards the end of the semester. The only thing that bothered me is she almost put negative thoughts in her students heads I hated that. Her feed back on appears sucked! Awful hand writing!!!!!

She is very unclear also when you ask her questions she tends to get irritated with you. She is the worst professor I have ever had at sbcc. Very rude she looks very nice and peaceful but she has such an awful attitude she made me feel uncomfortable. I would never recommend her class to anyone

She is a very kind and passionate woman who means well but the prompts are often very confusing and it leaves much room for questions and unnecessary stress. Interesting topics are covered and the class was only once a week.

She is the worst teacher I've had so far in SBCC. She is unclear with almost everything and her lectures is useless. You must have 700 points in her class to hand in portfolio, while other 100 classes don't have that rule. DON'T TAKE HER!!!

Extremely nice professor. I can see how others might think some of her prompts are vague, but I've never had trouble reaching her for clarification/help. She sticks around at the end of class for individual help, and grades very fair. She makes a class I was not looking forward to actually tolerable, and I appreciate taking her over someone else.

WORST teacher I've ever had. She was so unclear and extremely vague with every assignment, even if asked a question she refused to help clarify any instructions,or to even give any sort of feedback on a paper until it's being graded. And her class was so boring! There were literally were students sleeping in her class every day.

Fabulous professor! She really cares about her students well being. She is not an easy grader, but if you pay attention and attend the majority of classes you will do well. I saw myself progress sooo much in this class because of Professor Drobny! Its a mandatory class but I was so glad I took it! Wonderful class, learned alot
Dana is a very nice teacher. She cares a lot about her students and wants them to pass. She has some trouble explaining things so asking a lot of questions is smart to do.

Dana is honestly the SWEETEST teacher ever. She is interesting, funny, and helpful. She cares about each individual student's success and makes it almost impossible not to pass ENG 100. She doesn't drown students in homework, and is always willing to give students great feedback on our essays. You won't regret taking her class!
AMAZING TEACHER!!! she is very fare in how she grades and is very clear. If you work hard and just follow the plan she sets before you, you will not fail!

Roger's lectures are amazing and I was genuinely disappointed whenever I had to miss class. Workload is very minimal, one discussion post a week. The paper assignment is the only thing you should really be concerned about, but he gives you plenty of time to work on it and gives great feedback on the first drafts. By the way, PLEASE take in person.

Roger is literally the BEST professor I've ever had. He is so passionate about film and his class is really interesting and fun to attend. he gives extra credit if you go to one of his free movie screenings on the weekend. he is really funny and the workload is light. 10/10 professor and class
One of the most fantastic professors I have ever had. Really cares about movies and invites his students to super cool early screenings. Take it if you love movies.

My favorite part about Roger's class is that we had extra credit opportunities every weekend just by watching a movie (for free) at theatre, where there is celebrity appearances. We even got to see Adam Sandler do a Q&A with roger in person last semester after watching his movie Hustle. He's a great guy and makes you look forward to his class.

Professor Durling is very passionate and knowledgeable about film. You will gain a far greater understanding and appreciation of film if you take this class. The paper is the biggest assignment and he recommends you go to the writing center. You should definitely go to the writing center about 3 times (can do this virtually).
Great guy. His student's success is a priority. Not too much work given and when it is you have enough time to complete it and clear instuructions. Highly recommend his classes. Great Guy.
Finally transferring looking back at all my professor Durling was hands down the best teacher I ever had. Nothing else has to be said about it, great guy!

I love this guy and loved his class! He is so funny and kind. He really knows his stuff, but is super accommodating and does not make it too difficult at all. Most classes comprise of watching movies and a short lecture, and sometimes taking a short quiz. It was really fun!

Professor Durling is an awesome professor to take Film courses with! I took my first Film class w/ him and he did not disappoint. His lectures were interesting, not too long, and straight to the point. We mainly watched films and broke scenes down in accordance to lecture notes. Attendance isn't mandatory, but don't miss the quizzes! Only 1 paper.
Such a fun class! Do not take this online. It is much more fun and worth it to take in person. He keeps the movies he shares with the class updated and modern.

Roger would expose you to a plethora of clips and films, and then he would discuss it. His class offers an opportunity to attend screenings at the Riveria where he would carry out Q and A sessions with filmmakers and actors. The class is introductory. Any lover of film will do well in this class.

Roger Durling is a kind person and very passionate about what he's teaching. He tries to do his best to teach the basic cinematic concepts, such as mise- en- scene, editing or music. However, I think that he was not very good at explaining his stuff. Presence is not mandatory, but recommended as there are some quizzes. Many EC- opportunities!
Loved his class and was my favorite! Easy grader and the exams in this class are easy (he goes over exactly whats going to be on the midterm and final!)

Roger is such an hilarious teacher! You will have a few quizzes at the beginning of the semester which are 1 question each and you have to choose true or false. The midterm and final are also true or false type of exam. There is one paper assignment which is the most difficult thing in the class because he is a tough grader on that.

Roger During, what a guy. Super interesting dude and is exactly what I would have imagined a Film Studies Professor being like. Tests are pretty easy and there are many EC opportunities. He does give random 'pop quizzes' that really just record attendance. Don't miss those. Super funny and likable professor, would highly recommend.

Easy A class. Would take again in a heartbeat. Usually follow the schedule of watching a movie one day and a short lecture the other. Lectures are life changing - makes you rethink how you watch films and appreciate films in a dif way. They can be boring sometimes but he usually cuts the class short by an hour anyway. Love his jokes & enthusiasm!

Super super easy A, only one paper and no homework. Movies were interesting and lecture almost always ends early. A few pop quizzes but super easy, mostly just for attendance (although I missed a ton of them and still got an A). Plus sometimes fun extra credit screenings with Q and As with the actors.

Roger Durling is one of those special teachers that you can tell from the second they start speaking that they are in love with what they teach. My time in his class was the highlight of my summer. His lectures were very interesting and he was very open to discuss film if you approached him after class. If you love film he is a phenomenal teacher.

Really cool professor who you can tell, loves what he teaches. You basically go in and watch a movie for about 2 hours, and sometimes class will be cut short. Easy A if you just show up, also an interesting class to learn too.

I loved being in Roger Durling's film class. Attendance is not mandatory but there are random quizzes so be prepared for those, in case you think about skipping class. There is one midterm, an essay, and a final. We watched films and discussed them in class, he is a very funny teacher and made it extremely enjoyable.
Easy A

Seriously one of the best classes you can take at CC. Roger is hilarious and his class rarely feels like you're in a lecture. You can tell how passionate he is about film just through the way he talks about it. Pretty easy but super enjoyable class.

Roger is a fantastic man and even better teacher. He truly knows what he's talking about when it comes to films. He opens a level of depth to watching movies that is just incredible. Very funny, very approachable.

He is such a great and funny guy. There is no way to fail this class, only if you miss the paper, midterm and final. He literally reads the answers of each exam in the class before the day of the exam. Plays new and interesting movies in the class, but attendance is not an issue. Great teacher, EASY A, just take him dude.

Roger Durling is the plug for sure. I highly suggest taking his class. You watch a lot of movies and when your in class listen to how he analyzes the movies. You will realize how many things you have missed while watching movies from before. Roger is a super funny, passionate, inspirational, and well respected teacher. He enjoys teaching class

Just show up to class and write down what he says. The midterm and final are literal gimmies and if you dont get As on those you shouldnt be in College. Only hard thing in the entire class is the paper, but he is extremely helpful in pointing students in the right direction. Great teacher, super funny, easy A.

I really liked this class. Not only do you get to watch a new, up-and coming movie once a week in class but Mr. Durling makes his lectures very entertaining. There's no homework except studying for the mid-term and Final and there's one paper. I was bummed it was over. Make sure you show up to every class because his pop quizzes are notorious!

The best way to describe Roger Durling is, entushiasm! There is never a boring second while you take his class. The films you get to see are the newest oscar worthy films. It is with joy I went to his classes and I would hihley recommend him for future students.
Midterm and final were extremely easy. He grades harder on essays. He randomly takes attendance; if you miss an attendance quiz then you can make it up by attending an extra credit event.
Very fun class to take. Easy A if you show up. Its about a 2 hour class but letcrures are only an hour

Loved this class so much! Not a huge fan of history, but he made it enjoyable. There were around 3 assignments each week where you had to write, so if you like writing, this class is definitely for you. Don't think there was any extra credit, but if you follow the grading criteria, you'll be fine.

Great class! Took for my history credit for my 4-year university. Prof Elliot made this class manageable to do alongside a busy summer schedule, how summer classes should be! The class had 2 essays and then just weekly videos to watch, and a paragraph response on each one, super doable! will take again!!
this dude is a G
I love him so much, his lectures are amazing and he throws his little jokes sometimes, very easy to understand and easy grader. Definitely worth taking a class, especially in person
Best teacher i've ever had.
I went to study abroad with him in Japan. One of the best teachers I have ever had. He is very passionate about history and a caring person. 100% would take his classes again.

Professor Elliott's lectures are engaging and informative. The workload is manageable, and Unstuck ai was a helpful study tool alongside the course materials. Overall, an interesting and rewarding class.

David Elliot has got to be one of my favorite professors at SBCC. His lectures on YouTube make you feel like you're in the classroom in real life, he adds humor to whatever he can, which I really appreciated. I took a summer class so there was a lot of work in little time but he was able to make it manageable and even fun.

This was one of my absolute favorite SBCC profs. He is so down to earth and send out long lectures videos but they are SO interesting and he makes them funny. His classes tend to go over the lecture homework so if you forget to watch it's okay. So understanding and thought history like no other. You HAVE to take his class!!
I liked this professor. Homework was the exact same every week.

This dude LOVES what he does. Incredibly knowledgeable and more than willing to help you out, inside or outside the classroom. His lectures are most of the notes, so if all else fails you can look at those and get a good idea of what you need to know. His in person classes are wayyy better than online ones, I highly recommend him.

History 113H was a very well taught course. I took it online but there was a weekly in person discussion. He makes it super easy for students to do well in the class. The lecture videos he recorded were also very well taught and easy to understand.
1,000 words minimum for a weeks worth of assignments
The class was very lecture heavy.
Easy grader and interesting lectures

Professor Elliot has a lot of lectures in his courses. About 3-4 45 minute long videos per week with an assignment attached to each. However, these lectures are honestly great at providing good insight into the topic he's covering without giving word for word answers to the questions. If you are interested in the material then his courses are great
The lectures ended up killing me at the end cause I wouldn't even bother watching them anymore. I would just use the transcript and take what I needed.

This class is really straightforward. He has a really great late work policy, and he is very responsive. His lectures are interesting and the short answers after the lectures end up helping for the papers later on. Make sure you stay head otherwise you will fall behind, because there are typically four lectures per week.

I have had three of his classes and I would take them all again he is an excellent teacher. His homework consists of three or four video lectures that are very interesting and easy to digest and an extra assignment alternating between an essay, document review or discussion. The work is very manageable and he is a fair grader.

His lectures are amazing. He is a fountain of knowledge and he brings history to life. I am so glad I signed up for this class. He presents a few videos each week and students write a reflection essay about it, plus one weekly discussion with the whole class. Manageable amount of work and it's all super interesting. Grateful for this teacher.

Overall, I enjoyed this course and think professor Elliott is an excellent instructor. If you are good at paraphrasing material and don't mind writing a bit, then you will not find the workload to be particularly challenging.
Definitely a very nice teacher! Grading policy is very generous, and the assignments were very light.
a lot of work and long videos which were boring

He is a great professor and he makes it very easy to understand the material. I would recommend him for an easy and interesting class! Also no tests, quizzes, or exams. I took it over summer so there was a lot of discussions but not too bad!

Professor Elliott is great. He has a clear and generous grading criteria and is very lenient with late work. There are a good amount of assignments each week, but nothing too extreme. 3 essays for the semester but they only have to be about 900 words. Overall, great professor!

In my opinion, this class consists of a lot of busy work. I would only take this class if you are interested in history because there is a lot of homework each week, it is easy just time consuming. Prof. Elliot is very nice and gives good feedback. There are weekly videos, discussions/ document reviews, and 3 essays instead of exams.

No exams, only like 2 papers. Very easy papers though. Readings that he gives are only a couple of pages long. Was not a fan of history until this class. Lectures are so interesting and he tells us all the juicy drama. Professor is very sweet and approachable. You can tell he is very passionate about the subject and about teaching.

Absolutely loved this class. It was completely online and up to the student to work at their pace. However, Dr. Elliots lectures were super interesting so it never felt like too much of a chore. He taught history how it should be taught with an importance on cause and effect and motifs instead of dates and names. Absolutely take this class!

I took this class all online and it was great. His lectures are easy to understand and he makes them interesting. There are no tests, just two essays. Make sure to do good on them, they count as a big part of your grade. No zooms or anything, do everything at your own pace. Stay caught up and you'll do great.

Mr. Elliots class is different than other history courses Ive taken before. In other history courses you read so much but with his class there were only small sections to read, which didnt make me feel like I was reading a lot. He didnt really have specifics dates, other than just turning it in by the end of the weekend.
Work was pretty easy but there is about 7 assignments every week to complete. He takes late work up to a week for almost full credit which I loved!

I like this professor; he is an intellectual. Unfortunately, I was disappointed by the course content. Creative writing barely had any opportunities to write creatively. Instead, we were often responding to poetry, essays and art that I honestly disliked. I was really excited to produce a lot of original writing. Instead, I was disappointed.

I took ENG 111 as an online class in the summer with Professor Escobar. Although there was a fair amount of assignments per week, they were very encouraging and easy with grading. If you do the work, they will give you an A. I would 100% recommend taking this class with Josh!

Gave confusing instructions, never responded to emails, didn't answer questions, & lectures weren't material-based. He also gave quizzes at the start of every class and only ever entered one of them in the grade book. The class as a whole was easy however the difficult part was trying to understand the work due to unclear instructions.

People on here saying he's a tough grader must have summited blank documents. I wrote every essay in less than 30 minutes and never got anything less than a 90. If you show up and do all the work (maybe 10 assignments TOTAL) you will get an A. He also gives generous late work grace periods so there's really no excuse to get a bad grade.

Professor Escobar is an extremely harsh grader. The essays count for most of your grade and he took off 20 points claiming I didn't have a thesis when I clearly did on a 50 point assignment. You are able to turn in all assignments late with only a 0.5% penalty per day. However, there are so many assignments every week its impossible to keep up.

Professor Josh is an amazing professor. I took his summer class, and he was understanding, funny, and I learned so much. The way he connects the readings (super fun books) to the real world made me critically think about how I live life and view the world. I highly recommend this class to everyone. The essays were also reasonable and fun to write.

He's nice but this was such a demanding class. Maybe since this was a summer class but it was around 15 assignments every week with many essays with min 1500 words. I was going to have a B but he marked every extra credit assignment as a zero which brought my grade down to a C and said I shouldn't have waited til the last minute to complain. What!?
He is the best!!! In every assignment he was super clear on the instructions and even would come up with helpful sentence starters! This was literally the best class and he was so nice!

I really enjoyed his class. All the reading material was fun and interesting to read. I learned a lot from this class, from how to become a better writer to world issues we are facing as a country. As long as you actually read and turn in your essays, you'll be able to pass.

Professor Escobar was one of my favorite english110 teachers, and it's pretty easy to get a good grade in the class, but you have to turn in the assignments and do the readings. We had essay's but most of them are just graded for participation as long as you are proving that you are participating in the class. Really good teacher, 100% recommend!!

I have to admit that Professor Escobar's class consisted of a lot of reading and writing but the books we read in the class were very interesting and I enjoyed reading all of them. There were 3 essays in total but we were allowed to use previous assignments and essays in writing the final one. He leaves very good feedback too! Overall, 10/10.

Has no compassion, understanding, or empathy for students. Does not care about his students, only cares about keeping his job. Told me to drop the class when I got sick with coronavirus and missed the mid term. Informed me there was zero chance of me passing because I was sick. I should've taken it to the dean but I couldn't stand the guy anyway.

Professor Escobar is a great teacher who really helped me explore writing and so many insightful topics! Yes, there is quite a lot of work in this class, but I promise it help you so much in the long run. He really cares about his students and is always encouraging them to be prepared for the future. Definitely take this class he's super cool!!

Professor Escobar cares about the students and is passionate about what he teaches. Yet, he uses the worst grading system. You don't see your grade throughout the whole semester, until after the final exam. Even if you have completed it to the best of your ability, it will be marked as incomplete and he will not help you to make it complete.

Ive taken both Eng 110 and Eng 111 with Prof. Escobar with absolutely no regrets. He is kind, more than reasonable and flexible, as well as full of information. His class is very much what you put into it. He isnt afraid to tackle the hard stuff, like racism and inequality in our country. This isnt just an easy A class, but youll be enriched.

Professor Escobar is very nice. His grading for the class is very strange but I don't mind it. He is very accommodating and will let you redo an assignment if its incomplete. overall this class wasn't my favorite but also wasn't the worst.

I took Professor Escobar due to a recommendation from a friend who took his 110 class. Kind of regret it due to being stricter on students and required readings/journal entries for each chapter. Also with every paper, youre required to complete two drafts before your final draft. Seems like a great guy, but can be awkward at times.

After taking his English 110 and receiving an A, I decided to take his 111 class because, as a new professor at SantaBarbara City College, he seemed kind and approachable. Wrong choice. I'm not sure what happened to him from one semester to the next, but now he has a condescending demeanor towards his students that is discouraging and uninspiring.
Holly is a nice lady, She let me turn in a packet after my bosss had left. She listens and understands and trys to fix your problems.
Manageable workload and a relatively easy class to take as long as you complete the work.

I enjoyed the class and learning the material, though there's a lot of reading involved and we actually didn't have many discussions about the films we watched. There's a lot of focus on the final paper but at the same time I feel like we didn't have much time to prepare for it, we only really got started on working on it in the last 2 weeks.

The material is interesting but the course is not set up well online. Videos are slow withno visuals. You have a lot of busy work but no discussions whatsoever about the films which is odd for a film class. He does not respond to emails, making it tough. There's far too much focus on the final paper and little to no focus on learning the material.

"Your Mid Term consists of multiple choice questions, short answer questions, and 1 clip analysis question. There are a total of 50 points available. Pay attention to the questions. " Those are our midterm instructions, no review or any hints on what it would be on, studied all the wrong stuff just to be set up to fail. DO NOT take this class.

I love the topic, but am disappointed that this class is rote memorization with no actual learning or meaningful assignments. The quizzes test nitty gritty details of things that if you asked 100 film professors, none of them would know! I am being tested on small things instead of actually learning in a meaningful way. It's disappointing.

Do yourself a favor and do not take this class. A terrible class with an even worse proffesor. Sent a total of five emails throughout the semester to him and received 0 in return. Lectures are absolutely awful. The average score for the entire class final was a D and Midterm a C. Which both weigh heavy on your grade. Believe me, DO NOT TAKE IT.

I took his Contemporary American Film Studies class and really enjoyed it. His lectures are super interesting and he really cares about and knows what he's talking about. We rarely had homework or used the book, making most of the work in-class aside from the two papers. He also picks great films to show! And I love his dry sense of humor.
There isn't much to say about Richard Feilden. He's British, he's hilarious, and he always show the best films. 4/5 would take again.

Does not (rarely) answers emails, is dishonest about not giving assignments and then gives them, no extra credit opportunities to help achieve a higher grade, ambiguously worded final from ALL OVER the course material, and taking FOREVER to give you your final grade,(and no I'm not lazy I got an A in the class), but besides that great class !!!

If you ask for help, he will help you in any way you can. At first I was intimidated but I wish I had done it earlier. Really knows his stuff and shows good examples. I also love his English accent and dry sense of humor!

Feilding is AWESOME! I've taken 3 classes from him, all of which I've greatly appreciated and enjoyed. He is passionate and extremely knowledgeable about film and film history. Complainersbad reviewers are just lazy students, honestly. He's really understanding and totally willing to help 1 on 1 if you put in the time.

Great course layout with lectures every Tuesday and film screenings every Thursday. There were supposed to be 4 papers and 10 quizzes but there were only like 2 papers and 3 quizzes, and the essays like 2 pages each. Super chill. For the midterm and final you just have to have paid attention during lectures. His accent was no problem to me.

the subject content is easy but the midterm and final are 100% memorization so that was annoying. there's really no point in going to class because you will fall asleep every single time. chill guy, but incredibly boring. definitely wouldn't take anything he teaches again.

i was really excited about taking this class but he isn't a very good teacher and doesn't explain things very much. when i've tried to email him he never replies. the midterm was all memorization of every single detail of the notes and didn't really help us much before midterm
His lectures are pretty boring unless you are very interested in the subject. Midterm was all powerpoint memorization. He is not that interesting and I would not take his class again.

Amazing film teacher with a great sense of humor. Has a great passion behind his lessons and makes it feel almost like a stand up routine with his snarky humor. His tests aren't the easy but only require good note taking and studying, it feels like I got it wrong because I wasn't paying attention NOT bad teaching. He is also extremely helpful

Boring class, difficult midterms unless you memorize every bit of notes. Only good thing was that I was able to watch the movies at home and just come to lectures. Lectures are horribly boring though.
I took his Film Genres class. He's very passionate about the subject and he tries to be helpful. However, since the class is very short, he doesn't have much time to answer questions.

I don't think he's that great of a teacher. I tried reading the book, and came to every lecture but when it came to tests I was like "when did we learn this?". Once I came in to review my paper and get extra credit and when he explained the topic to me then and there, I understood it. But I wish he was more clear in class.

I took Feilden at Moorpark. The class was interesting and I enjoyed it but when it came to the tests, he didn't give an overview well of what would be on the test so you have to study every little detail because he doesn't clarify. I found this weird because for the essay he clarified exactly what he wanted. He also is bad at answering emails.

I had Feilden for FS 101 and he was a great teacher. He explains this clearly and he knows quite a bit. HIs lectures are usually entertaining, but he has a strange taste in films. None of the films he showed were bad, they were just boring, and a lot of the movies didnt really relate to what we were talking about. Overall he was a good teacher

Had Feilden for 101 and 120. He's a great teacher. He is extremely knowledgeable and insightful when it comes to film and he explains things in a way that gets you to really think about them. I've learned a lot about film in just two semesters from his classes and I plan to take 110 with him next semester. Great professor.

Great film prof,especially for students new to film. I never had trouble understanding him, in fact, he is the clearest film prof I've had. Whenever I approached him for help he was friendly, and had a very evident passion for film. The key is GO TO EVERY CLASS. That's what I did, and I got an A without any extra studying/reading outside of class.

This guy is a NEWB. In every sense of the word, and to make matters worse, he takes his rage for his shortcomings on students for, and he would not admit to the student for inscrutable questions on the mid-term/final instead he will blame the him/her for not understanding the meaning behind the twisted wording, typos, and grammatical mistakes.

Feilen is a great film teacher, sometimes it is hard to hear because of his British accent, but you'll get used to it. He is well prepared for every class and he is very humorous. After class you just have the feeling that you know everything about the subject, but you still want to learn more. One of the classes I'm exited for every week.

He is the best film teacher at SBCC. He lives and breathes films and you will have one of the most interesting and insightful classes with him. Oh, and he's hilarious with a snarkey British sense of humor. No matter what your interest in films and movies is, when you leave the class you will be wanting to learn more.

He's a very insightful teacher. He knows a lot about the cinema world, and he is well-prepared for every lecture. His accent may be kinda hard to hear, but it's still totally fine. He's cool! Take his class!
one of the best film teachers i've had at santa barbara city college (and i've taken a lot). This guy really knows his stuff and goes that extra mile in his lectures. take him!

He will say the textbook is related to the lectures - NOT at all related. Lectures NOT great, can be helpful SOMETIMES. If you rely on lectures to learn, DON'T for this class. ALSO, there was NO CANVAS (at the time I was taking the course) I did not know my grade at all or how I was doing throughout the semester. I did a NP/P. I passesd but barely.

Ron's super chill you might be scared on the first weeks since physics is hard but don't withdraw you'll learn why. Do homework problems day before quiz and you'll be set. Labs are easy n chill. Lectures are recorded, sometimes his lectures don't make sense if you follow along with his textbook online you'll find equations to match his lecture.

I actually took physics 102 but that wasn't an option for rating. His class is a pretty easy A, but you won't learn much. The work is busywork and he's not very good at teaching. I was scared into taking this intro class by my counselor because I need to take higher levels of physics, but it's such an unnecessary class.

Ron is such an amazing professor. Honestly so far my other college professors have just seemed apathetic and I was starting to worry that it was me. Ron is actually enthusiastic to be lecturing and talking to students. He is hilarious and has so many cool stories. I am starting to enjoy going to class and being a college student.

Ron (aka Ron Burgundy) has a unique aspect as a teacher. Hes clear and very intelligent in the subject of Physics. I have had the pleasure of being tutored by him in the Stem Center on campus and he really helps students understand the theory and conceptual aspects of Physics. He is the one of the best instructors I've met.
The bombskies Ron is a great professor. Good Lectures talks clearly and makes sure you understand the material. Any question you have he will answer it without a doubt. He is one of a kind.

Ron The Man, didn't take him for Phys 101, but he's in the lab and in STEM and always helps out all levels of Physics for students, this includes Mechanics (Physics 121), Electricity & Magnetism (Physics 122), and Optics (Physics 123). Very knowledgeable guy, knows his stuff very well. He's also currently working on his PhD on Astrophysics!CoolGuy!
Funny guy. One of the easiest courses I've taken at CC.

I took conceptual physics with Ferril over the summer. Classes were long and we went over multiple chapters in just one day. However, that is normal for a summer course. His test were not hard at all and we were able to use notes and calculators during his test. He always had experiments in class to go along with his lectures. Overall, really cool!

He is a very nice guy and super approachable. It was his first year teaching the course and first time teaching in the school too. So expect a bit of confusion here and there. Overall, very nice but not that easy. Textbook isn't even needed that much.
