Take surfing! If you are a beginner this is a great class to meet people and learn the basics. Participation matters and if you want to actually learn you have to go! It's so nice to be in the ocean twice a week! He is a really cool guy with a lot of knowledge. Highly recommend!

Negative man, no care to talk things out with students and is unforgiving. He does not like being questioned or people clarifying things. He's harsh and not at all understanding. Not worth paying for this class I'd just learn from a friend.

Show effort in his class and he'll respect you. He's not the most bubbly guy, but he's not mean either; just serious. I liked the class but we only surfed 3 times because it was an especially bad semester bc of weather. Don't take if you already know the basics bc he doesn't give feedback to improve. Study for midterm, it's harder than u think.
no additional comments

Easy A, but such an unpleasant guy. He kept complaining about everything he hated about the world, and it seemed like there was only one good person in the world according to him.. and that was himself. Always criticized everyone's work, rarely complemented. Didn't really show how to do the exercises either. Tests were weirdly difficult. A mean guy
canceled class half the time and wasn't very nice
amazing professor. took surfing and it was so much fun. class was canceled a few times due to mother nature

Scott can be summed up as a military-esk dude with a vendetta against the plastic and beef industry haha. Really easy-going class and I learned a lot about surfing despite going into the class with experience and had a lot of fun. Seriously tho, talk to him about pollution, nutrition, or running and you will be his best friend.

Yeah you would think ohhh surfing fun!! yeah mmmhmm NOOO! not at all. Probably one of the least fun classes I took. He kept canceling classes and I didn't learn anything. So yeah. Waste of my money and time. :)

Took beginning weightlifting. Don't sign up if you're afraid to get swole. Why? That's what's expected. No show = no pass. Good coach. Demo's the basic technique and will give more pointed and specific guidance, but you have to ask! That's what great coaches do - put out what you put in. Ask him to critique & advise. You'll get more out of it.

Coach Scott is a really cool person and this class has been amazing. I signed up for this class to learn how to surf (which I now can, and I love it), but one of the best parts of this class turned out to be Scott's ecology lecture. It was truly inspirational. This class actually changed my life.

It's a workout class; You're expected to be there on time and get to work, because that's what you signed up for. There are a few quizzes (5, 5 questions each) that you need to pay attention to his lectures for, but nothing too difficult to absorb. I really enjoyed my time in the gym, including the extra credit days offered.

Scott is a very caring professor and he will work with you if you reach out to him. Every class is a lecture! One thing that I did not enjoy from this class is that he basically makes you work for a study guide, meaning that if the entire class slacks off during lectures you will not be granted a study guide.
Great guy!

He is v strict about attendance. Even if it should be excused (doctors apt, field trip with another class, etc), he will NOT excuse it. You are allowed 3 without grade penalty - after that your grade is reduced by 3% each day missed I recommend a later class than 8am. Beware it takes min 15 min to shower/change after class (for course scheduling).
I really like him. Hes a good surfing teacher! And many of my friends and colleges likes him very much! He knows what hes taking about and doing a really good job!

very disappointed with this professor. he preached a very strict attendance policy and then proceeded to cancelling class every other week. oh well, typical gym teacher. he lectured a bunch, you definitely don't need to listen though. easy A.

This class was very fun, Fickerson is an awesome teacher. Before surfing you have to pass a swim test (swim one lap in a pool), You have to buy your own wetsuit, the water is cold so the wetsuit helps A LOT. I thought it would be kind of an awkward class but since its a small group (lots of people drop so crash if u can!) its more fun! 3 skip days
"Go Vegan, I mean I'm not telling you to go vegan, but you could die otherwise."

This guy teaches a one unit surfing class yet requires a 3-5 page essay as well as an exam, come on dude. Honestly not sure why everyone likes this guy, he is not funny and does not make the class fun. If you have a friend that knows how to surf ask himher to teach you instead. I am in this class right now and wish I was not, take a different PE.
He's so cool. Like I want to be him when I grow up.
hard but fun class if you want to get into a good shape. I think his grading is a little bit strict.

Fickerson is an amazing coach. He really knows what he is doing, and is nice about it. He is very understanding, and cares about each individual person. Which not many coach's posses. Just do what you are told to do and it will be an amazing experience in cross country :)
a great person and an extremely helpful and dedicated coach!

Genuinely the best teacher I've ever had. He's incredibly kind, & he sets up every class that its impossible to fall behind. His lectures are clear & organized. You always know exactly what is needed and he never tries to trick you. Put in even a little effort & easy A. He truly wants his students to do well. I wish every professor is like him.

Professor File was a great teacher and I would 100% take another one of his classes again! His lectures were very easy to follow, he gave a reasonable amount of homework, the essays were chill, and he was overall a very caring and compassionate teacher. He really wanted his students to succeed and he did everything he could to help

Professor File is a great professor. There were 3 books we had to get but they were cheap and you could access them all online if needed. There were 3 essays, regular homework and an in-person final essay. Each class is set up to help with each essay and he tries to make the content connect to students' lives. Definitely recommend.

Mr. File is an extremely caring professor who is passionate about teaching and his students. I love(d) his class and is very essay based in terms of grading. His classes help you prepare for those essays as they build on certain aspects of writing a paper. Would take this class again in a heartbeat.

Definitely an amazing teacher. Could not have asked for a better English teacher to start the year with. Super easy to get in touch with and always willing to help. He truly cares for his students and their success, I had some issues outside of the class and he was willing to adjust deadlines. His lectures are really people participation focused.

Professor File is truly so kind and generous and wants to see his students succeed. He is extremely patient and will always help kids out with deadlines and things they are struggling with. There are 3 definitely doable essays and a regular amount of homework. If you do the work and come to class you can easily get an A+. Such an amazing teacher!!
Mr. File is such a nice teacher who wants his students to succeed. He never refuses to answer any questions regarding homework and essays and always tries to give detailed feedback.

I usually dread the subject of English, but Professor File made it such an enjoyable class! I really liked the materials we read, as they were relevant and relatable. I also found the essays engaging and not boring. Truly loved his chill, caring, light-hearted, and informative nature. He was kind, understanding, and just such a good human being.

Professor File is very passionate and funny and makes sure that everyone understands the assignments and in class work given. He engages with the class in group discussions and is very easy to talk to. There's 4 essays through the semester, but they are all relatively easy to write about if you read the assigned material and do your work.

He is literally the best, no joke. Just phenomenal. I learned more in his English class than in any other. The chosen books were very enlightening and captivating. Writing my essay was a wonderful experience. You should absolutely have him teach you. Have written 3 essays and 1 final. He is constantly accessible and very attentive to his students.

really funny and chill guy who cares abt u and ur success in the class, only got a C+ because I turned in assignments super late but he's even super lenient on that (turned each essay in a month after they were due, only got around 4 points docked off). would take a class with him again in a heartbeat

This was an online class because of Covid. I feel we were all adjusting. Prof File made us weekly check-in videos which I appreciated. He is very fair with grading and is always available to help his students. I worked really hard for this B this semester. I actually registered to take his next Eng class this Fall. I wholeheartedly recommend File.
A bunch of essays that is 60% of your grade but if you do good its a fun class and easy.
He is the sweetest professor I have ever had! Such a sweet guy and really cares about his students! He is so cute! SO definitely take his class!

His class was really good! If you are someone who needs a supportive professor, take his class. There were 2 essays, 1 research essay, 1 final exam essay. Be sure to meet with Mr. File during his offers hours if you want a better grade on your next essay because he will tell possible improvements which will help!
i loved this professor. i took his last year and he really helped me out a lot with my essays. Goshh i would love to take him again! he' awesome and cares for his students! easy A! (bonus: he's cute)
Jason is a great professor he is very nice and will take time to talk to you and help you, the class is a lot you miss a day and you get lost but he is a good professor and if you try you will pass

Probably the nicest and most caring teacher on campus, he always feels like a long time friend. Don't get me wrong he's a strict grader and make it clear what he is expecting but coming from someone who hates english I would seriously take his class again.

Took Jason File my first semester at Sbcc. His class was great he is a respectful and caring teacher. Gives great feed back and is always there to help you. Its an easy A as long has you do your homework and show up to class. Would recommend taking him.
Amazing teacher he really cares about his students and if English is your second language feel confident taking his class since File will respect you and help you.

Profesor file is an amazing teacher. He's a very open minded, caring guy and does not discriminate. He sees everyone equally weather you're a fluent English speaker or it's your second language. He meets every student at their level and makes each of his student feel important, smart and heard. And he's very HANDSOME so definitely recommend him!!

It was my first year here at SBCC and Professor File was amazing! He's a really cool guy. He gives great feedback on essays with emphasis on peer review. He gives respect when respect is given back. so if you are always disrupting class you will get called out. He is always encouraging students to not give up and is always open to questions.

Jason really cares about his students and just wants the best for you. He is very reasonable. The class isn't too hard, if you choose not to show up or do your part of the work then don't expect a passing grade.

Jason File is the sweetest teacher ever. He is very understanding and wants you to do well. You will do well in his class if you show up, take his feed back and do the homework. Never felt bored in that class. I wish I could take him again.
Mr. File is very nice and funny. He lets students work in groups and even read outside as a group. Homework and quizzes are enjoyable, especially projects. Take his class and have fun. Mr. File rocks

The class was extremely difficult. Don't waste your time on ANYTHING but the lectures. Record them and re-listen to them. He said on the first day that it was not his job to pass people just so they can move on. He considers himself the last barrier to entry to get into these upper medical programs.

The material is interesting, but the class is unnecessarily difficult. Exams have 50 Multiple Choice & 50 pts of short answers, and the grading is very harsh. No study guides, but gives a sheet of potential questions but only about 4ish show up on the exam. No extra credit. Record lectures!! Be careful with tutors, they often give out wrong info.

Not impossible to pass but will definitely make you work for it. He is a tough grader and honestly you never know if you are correct or not. It's up to robbie's interpretation if he thinks your answer was good enough. Interesting and fun class but the rough grading made it unbearable.

As a professor, he is tough; he wants you to think. His grading is a mystery; he could like your answer or not. He is fun. Lectures are full of personal stories that are entertaining and connect the dots. I wish it was a longer course; so many things were skipped because of the timing.

Exams were multiple choice and short written answers. Did not emphasize on what he wants you to show you understand on written questions. Even after going with tutors, wasn't very helpful after asking on what could be done to get a better grade on exams or how to study for them, just gave general response, didn't seem to care. seemed biased.

Don't be scared of the reviews, but get ready to study! If you put the time into understanding the material, you will do fine. You don't need the textbook, but you do need to record and listen back to lectures. Tutoring and office hours are super helpful. If you do badly on the first exam, keep going! There is room a lot of room to make up!
This was the toughest class I have taken yet. I appreciate how hard it was because it did prepare me for how other institutions test. I gained lots of knowledge. Be ready to study hard.

Professor Fischer is AMAZING!!! If you are expecting an easy A, this is definitely not that class. However, no bio class is meant to be that way and will never be that way. He is caring & very particular about responses, however he gives great feedback and guidance. What you put into this class is what you will get out of it! All about perspective.

I LOVED this class. So much that I switched majors to microbio. Definitely record lectures, but he explains things so well and keeps it interesting to the point where I could recite his lectures by heart. My advice is to write down as much as possible, rewrite your notes and read/teach the material to someone else (I taught my cat)

SBCC's worst professor. Poorly prepared, relies on textbook images, rambling throughout class, prompting students to record. Fischer displays racism on ESL student quizzes (unsubstantiated cheating accusations) favoritism evident through arbitrary extra credit for selected students, lacking a clear grading rubric. Take this class anywhere else.

As a pre-nursing major who has straight A's in all other BMS classes, this was by far the hardest one, especially with Robbie. He does not provide any slideshows, notes, extra credit, or study guide for the VERY hard exams. You have to record lectures and listen back to them otherwise you will not pass the exams. TAKE AT ANOTHER CC he is AWFUL.

Probably the hardest class I have ever taken. Only thing counted towards your grade are pop quizzes and midterms/ finals. He isn't specific on telling class what to study, and most feel unprepared. Straight A student as a pre-nursing major, but most likely have to re take this class. Try to take micro with a different professor or at another cc.

Don't let reviews scare you!!! This class is HARD ... harder than some of the classes i'm taking now in a well respected ABSN program. But, I can tell you that this class single-handedly made me a better student and prepared me for nursing school. First exam is the hardest. STICK WITH IT!! RECORD LECTURE.

I loved this class! It was interesting and engaging and Robbie is a great professor. He cares about your success and tells you exactly how to do well in the class. He is very much available to answer questions during lecture, lab, and during office hours. I did not find the class to be difficult, but studying is somewhat time consuming.

this class is HARD! but I like a challenge, and it felt good to be pushed academically. you don't need the book, but you do need to record lecture! re-listen to them before every exam. literally everything he says word for word is the exam, especially the short answer questions, which he grades really hard! GO TO OFFICE HOURS AND TUTORING!

I was thinking that if I would have liked to see this kind of review before taking the class because I know it's helpful to get good insight before: he's a great professor and don't freak out just bc you have heard it might be a scary class. There's lots of room to make up if you do poorly at the beginning. Just try hard!

His lectures and what he said would be on the exams never added up. Take good detail notes and make sure you understand what he is teaching. Thankfully I passed but barely, he is a tough teacher, and refused to let us see what we got wrong on the final due to " potential cheating" which makes zero sense. I would not take him again....dead or alive

There are no rubrics and there is clear bias in grading. Took this class while everything was still online and despite open note exams, he does not have a clear grading criteria nor provide good feedback. Very unapproachable. Seems to relish in student failure.

Don't be intimidated by this class, it is very interesting and as long as you watch the lectures and take good notes you will be fine. Robbie is a great professor and truly wants you to succeed. All of his lectures are super interesting and he is happy to answer questions and explain concepts again. I really enjoyed this class, Robbie is great!

Robbie is by far one of the best instructors I've ever had. He is warm, kind, and really good at explaining complicated concepts in simple terms. If you are interested in medicine, this is an incredible class to prepare you and pique your interest. If Robbie taught other courses, I would most definitely take them just to have him again! 10/10!!!

Microbiology is a tough class, but Robbie makes it very doable! Re-watch the recorded lectures and yes, write down EVERYTHING! It can be time consuming, but the exams are open note/book and you will thank yourself. Buy the textbook for exams questions. Hes a really sweet/funny guy. Extremely intelligent. Wont baby you, but wants you to succeed!

Took his class based on previous ratings... yeah that was a MISTAKE! he is a good person but just not a good professor. I learned nothing, his class is stressful and as many students have mentioned his quizzes and test are HARD! I challenged some of the quizzes answers and he got offended, he is arrogant! helpless on your success
He gives many quizzes. Takes up a lot of your time. Would recommend forming a study group. Make sure to keep up on deadlines.

Im a very average student. This class is doable for you but its extremely tough. LITERALLY write down EVERYTHING he says, not what you think is important, EVERYTHING. Skip the live lec. and watch the rec. so you can pause and write Tests/quizzes are not proctored but you will not find any of the answers online, you have to have outstanding notes.

Robbie is one of the least compassionate teachers ever and is quite frankly very rude. He quickly goes over material them makes extremely hard questions on it. You will never see what you got wrong because he does not actually care about students learning. Do not bother going to him for help because he will do nothing to help you improve your grade

Terrible terrible terrible. Unprofessional, degrading, and shows clear bias towards certain students. Does not follow his own rubrics/instructions or grading system and when confronted, denies and does not take responsibility. Rushes through material instead of ensuring students are understanding concepts. Would not recommend AT ALL

Do not recommend this class. Test and quiz wording are ridiculous. I have gotten an A in all other BMS class cc but this one was something else. lectures and labs seem easy until its quiz or test time. Robbie will set you up for failure. Do yourself a favor and take the other teacher.

His quiz and test questions are ridiculously difficult to answer. He will say hes not trying to trick you but as an A+ student in all other BMS classes, I strongly disagree. Even worse, he wont let you see quiz and test answers after you submit, so you never learn what you missed so you cant improve! This was a very discouraging class. :(

Robbie is a nice man but does not care about students success. Went for help and gave me general advice that did nothing to help me. Lectures quizzes are all over the place and wording can be extremely tricky. Grade is pretty much based on two five point quizzes a week so even if you get one off youre already at a B.

Robbie is very unorganized and his lectures are all over the place. Take notes on EVERYTHING he says. Tests are much harder than what you actually learn in class. He is accessible outside of class and seems nice. Very tough on his criteria and unclear on grading. Don't take this with another class.
Grades hard, but is very understanding with extensions and easy to contact. Lots of discussions and peer review stuff, lots of reading and annotation, but overall not bad

She's very sweet but there were so many little activities in between essays that got pretty annoying. She did pick out some really interesting material to read though and she's very flexible and understanding.
One of the best professors I ever had. She really cares, and I would 100% take her classes again. If you really put in the effort and stay consistent, you will feel content with your grade.

such a sweet lady, you can text or email her for information. She gives SO many EC chances, zooms and meeting with librarian. I maxed out 20 points. Her class takes time but its not hard. 3 big essays and another timed for Final. Interesting course and I think I actually learned more than other English classes.

There are weekly discussion posts that are pretty simple to complete, in which Professor Foege provides feedback on your response and help build you as a writer. The workload is a doable amount. Also, the purpose of the discussion posts are to help you with the larger assignments.

Prof Foege is very sweet and is very easy to get a hold of if you have any questions of concerns. She cares a lot about her students and gives you good feedback. There is a lot of work and reading articles and we have a word count for everything we do. The class can be hard but it is doable.

Great professor! She is very organized and you know exactly what is expected of you. If you have another dominant class like science or math, just know that she is going to take up quite a bit of your time and you may be a bit stressed. We had a lot of small assignments within larger assignments that add up to be a big chunk of your grade.
He's very good and amazing. .. ... ...

Great and approachable. The course itself is difficult, but that's not Folsom's fault. If you are rusty on your math/algebra skills I would recommend brushing up and getting comfortable before taking his intro physics course. If you need help he makes himself available, but there's only so much he can do if you don't know your math skills.

Weekly quiz, a lot of your grade, did allow you to use resources on canvas. The class was hybrid. The lab was also hybrid, some in person labs were lacking. Lectures were technically online you could go in person for a more indepth view of the material. Going in person counted for 'discussion' credit. Would recommend.

i had to learn the entire course on my own because what he taught in lecture was entirely different to the tests. I would have no idea how to solve the problems on quizzes, regardless of doing all the homework problems he sent. Not even the physics tutors could help me solve the online quiz problems.
kind and helpful
Dr. Folsom is a great person and a decent professor. You'll have to figure out most of the content on your own. He helps a lot in discussions if you are confused.
My favorite asynchronous class experience! Professor Folsom was very helpful and easy to reach out to for questions. The class format and structure allowed for easy engagement and learning.

The formatting of the class kept changing so that added some confusion. But I blame that on covid. Doug is awesome, super nice. I felt more stressed about getting good grades rather than enjoying the learning unfortunately but that could be just me. Doug is really nice. Had one exam all semester. One quiz per week with homework and discussion post.

Folsom was great. Took in person for Spring 2022; course remained Canvas-based. Whatever other people were complaining about, his Canvas shell has been fixed or was being exaggerated in the first place. Accomodating/understanding instructor. Caring and thoughtful lecturer. I excelled despite not having strong background. You got this

I took Folsom online and he was surprisingly disengaged my entire class was confused whether or not his teaching style was even considered teaching. He had us post any and all questions on a discussion board and he wouldn't allow one-on-one student zoom. He also didn't have a TA until halfway through the semester. However, generally an easy class.

Doug is the best! With covid, he has been very understanding if you reach out. Responds quickly and grades in a fair way. Only one test (the final). Reach out to people and solve homework problems together. It will make the class a lot more understandable and enjoyable.

I took PHYS 121 online. Prof Folsom is forgiving and understanding with assignments and grading. Final grade calculation is a good mix of homework, quizzes, and the final. Final was hard but graded well. I prefer reading textbook, but the lecture I watched was clear and thorough. This is a difficult class, but Professor Folsom understands that.

Doug is awesome!! SUPER smart - you can literally ask him ANYTHING and he ALWAYS has a good answer. Off the charts IQ with this professor. Make sure you attend lecture because he gives hints of what will be on exams. Real exams were similar to practice exams. He makes tough material very doable AND interesting! Thank you Doug!

Doug Grades his 102 courses in a leaderboard of sorts. All hw(homework is optional), tests, quizes, and Labs are worth different amounts but add to your total points. there are give or take 10 slots for +A, A, -A, +B, B, -B grades each and you get ranked against classmates anonymously. Overall he is a great professor and I loved his class
Professor Folsom is great! Physics 101 wasn't that hard! If you do homework and go to the lectures you can get an A pretty easy! Lectures are fun, he is a great professor!

Fun, engaging, quirky professor who brings the material to life. I took Conceptual Physics purely to fulfill a prerequisite, but ended up enjoying the class. Not too much work for a 6-week class, and very doable to get an A. Definitely recommend Doug Folsom!

Professor Folsom is the best science teacher I've had. He is very passionate about physics and is interested to discuss anything you bring up in class. Insanely easy for a non science major. No math at all. Very understanding, helps with assignments/quizzes, and easy grader.

I took Doug for PHYS102 in Fall 2019. Basically the easiest class ever. He 'curves' your grade from most points to least points in the class. If I remember correctly the top 10 in the class get an A+, next 11 A, next12 A-, and so on. The vast majority of your points come from online HW, so you can brute force your way to an A w/ ok test scores.

He's a very enthusiastic professor who know's what he's talking about. He leaves a far amount of time at the end of class for homework help (about 10-20mins), so most of the time you can get out of class early. The labs are fairly quick so that also leaves time for homework help. You can tell he really wants you to succeed which is always great.

Lectures are great, essential since there is no textbook. Assigns large amount of online homework, but you choose how much you want to do. Grading is all points based so do more hw to increase your grade. Tests and quizzes are decently difficult so be prepared for them. Do lots of hw if you want a good grade in the class. Overall great professor.
Entertaining lectures and displays of physics. Such a dad. Easy class, no math used, just concepts, just do the easy quizzes and hw. Tests are slightly more difficult than quizzes.

Folsom goes over Algebra concepts during lecture, but the quizzes and exams require Trigonometry and Precalculus. SBCC advises Algebra 2 as the prerequisite, although, I strongly recommend Precalculus before taking this class. I only showed up to lectures on quiz days, but got an A because of the homework and final exam. You can skip the midterm.

Honestly love this dude, he's really understanding and a great teacher. Just kind of fast paced but all the notes you need he posts online for later review. He helps everyone to the fullest extent. DEFINATELY RECCOMEND

It seemed that he started the lab before the scheduled time but his class was at correct time. Don't take this class before completing required math class. If you are taking Math 150 or higher then no problem. Do outside research so understand the concepts so you are ready for quizzes, homework and exams.

Folsom is just great. Take him! He is very energetic and respectful. He helps you a lot through the lab and tries to help you the best he could when you ask anything related to the course. JUST TAKE HIM!! YOU WON'T REGRET IT!!

The lectures are EVERYTING!!! Go to lecture, take diligent notes, ask question (or go to office hours) and do the homework and you will pass. He tests on material from the lecture and homework, labs are complimentary to the lecture material. His lectures are interesting and help make the subject fun and straight forward, no surprises.

Professor Folsom is an amazing physics instructor. He reminds me a lot of Dug, the happy-go-lucky dog, from Up. Always so positive and energetic! He made physics simple to understand and fun to learn. Tests were difficult but not impossible. Labs were pretty striaghtforward, although stressful at times. Doug's there to help!

Doug is an amazing professor, but the class itself is just pretty difficult! I'm not a big science person. During lab, Doug is always there to help. I do always dread the exams and that's just because I don't completely understand the material. I'd recommend this class if you've already taken a physics class. I never did! Don't buy the book! :)

Lectures: 20 min spent going over the HW problems, interesting and engaging conceptual introduction of the new topics followed by examples Book(free)HW online. testsquizzes match HW HW not collected *PRO TIP* Use the HW to study for exam, but solve to all variables. Ex. if given D, T, V, and asked to solve A. Practice solving for D, T, V.
Pat is a nice person but her classes are so excruciatingly painfully boring.

She has great skills by giving people with disabilities extra time to work on their homework and to provide all the materials that they need to get the full units and a siutable standard letter grade.

Pat is a great professor, she cares about her students and shows it. Her module structure is sometimes confusing so I suggest checking your grades for final dates of assignments if you are not sure. The material is all pretty fun to learn about with interesting resources to read/watch along with. Highly recommend pat!

I took her TA 103 class last year. She is a nice enough person and she loves what she does. That being said it is an extremely boring, lecture heavy class with multiple group projects that are a huge pain. Only take the class if you have to.

Pat is a pretty good professor. This class has a lot of boring lectures, but also a lot of interesting ones. If you show up and put in a little effort, it should be an easy A. The only thing that you do in class is the midterm (quizzes and final are take home). Overall, I'd recommend taking this class.

She's a good teacher, really friendly and experienced. Because she's an easy grader, it's not hard to get an A. And so long as you show-up fairly often, passing is easy. Everything (but the midterm) is take home, even the final.

Class is pretty bland. Lot's of group work, which is fine. It was basically pretty mediocre. Got an A, which was nice. Just pay attention to the techinical terms, write them down, and make an effort on presentations and you're fine.

Decently easy class, mainly just show up and youll do alright. Be prepared to write some assignments which shell be fair on grading. Put in the effort to show up and try on the tests and your looking at an A.

Quizzes and final open note at home. Easy because most is in the textbook and you can google the rest. Some answers are impossible to find anywhere though but unlimited extra credit with reports on plays. Frustrated with lack of clarification on assignments and response to questions. I had to wing it a lot but she grades fairly leniently.
She is a good teacher, but she says 'alright' and 'okay' way too often.

She's extremely annoying. I counted how many times she said ''Alright'' and ''Okay'' after a sentence during 10 minutes - 29 & 36 times. But I guess that if you're really into what she's teaching she's okay. I just found her boring and annoying. Tests are not super hard, and the painting we did was kind of fun.
Easy A. Show up to class occasionally, turn in your stuff on time, and don't miss the play performances and you will get an A. Take the class, don't be an idiot.
Really easy class with not that much homework. She takes attendance, but you really don't have to pay attention to do well.
No Comments

Chef Charlie is a breath of fresh air when compared to other staff members in the department. He takes a different approach to teaching, letting you work and learn in your own way. Rather than yell at you in front of everyone, he turns your mistakes into learning moments. He creates a welcoming and educational environment in the kitchen.
Charlie is the most interactive, understanding, educating teacher I've ever known. You will absolutely love any class you take with him! Hilarious too
he is the man.
I love Chef Charlie's class :D. He made class worth going to. I've learned a lot :D

He is an awesome teacher!! I would highly recommend him as a teacher!! He is funny and very helpful! I don't find anything negative about him. Just be on time and in uniform and you will be fine in his class.

Charlie is a great teacher and very patient. He loves what he does and communicates well with his students. I wish he wouldn't let us out early from lecture class because I would love to hear more from him on a personal level in regards to real life experience in the culinary world. Or, it would be great to use the extra time as q and a.

I wouldn't say the class is easy. If it was, you wouldn't appreciate what you learn. Charlie is very upbeat, positive, and willing to help anyone. Be on time and in uniform, in other words, a responsible adult. Watch his demonstrations, take notes, help others and this class will be great and one you will remember. Charlie makes education work.
kinda cocky, but it makes him sexy. knows his stuff.
great!
No Comments
Charlie is THE coolest techer in the program. He is very talented, very receptive, and always has time to chat.
No Comments

He's a great teacher in class. not too boring, he likes to get right into things and skip all the useless material. keeps class as short as possible. he hates authority. in his lab class at 7am he gives us a quick demo and all the info we need and we crank out full coarse meals. the only problem is either you puctual and in uniform or your dropped from the class.

If you're truly interested, a strong writer, and not just trying to pass, IGNORE the negative reviews. Larry is brilliant & funny, replies to email well - it's truly refreshing to learn from him. Some essays are tough, but you'll come away with a deep understanding. Strong scientific vocabulary recommended. Write more than the word minimum, ~3-4k

All his classes are the same format: 4-5 quizzes and 3-4 essay exams. Doesn't post grades on canvas. Quizzes are hard because his book (required to purchase from his site) doesn't make sense. Can't do this class unless you pay $63/book. Not very clear what he's looking for when he grades so it's tough but doable. Pewpew 2 my head, might take again

Must buy his books for his classes because everything is on his website. Website is a hot mess, his book doesn't make sense and is hard to read, his exams are hard -- you literally have to re-read the book to answer the questions (basically writing 4-5 essays per exam). Doesn't give clear directions or advice. Worst class/professor ever. He sucks!

Principles of bio 140 is easy peasy. 4 essays one each month… granted the essays are massive. Set one day aside before the exam to do about 15 hours of writing and studying and you'll get an A. Would take again

But the teaching style is 100% detached, there is no canvas, the grades are on a (code names tho not real names) spreadsheet which I did not like because it compares the students against each other. Its all reading and writing sadly. The teacher seems kind but i would not pay to take another of their classes if its like this.

Class was hard and time intensive but super interesting. Prof responded quickly and seemed helpful to students who put significant effort in. But this class did take SIGNIFICANT effort. Also, baller move to make us use the textbook he wrote. Seems genuinely interested in bio, very clear rules to get an A.

AWFUL CLASS! I am a straight A student and I would never recommend this class. Save yourself and don't take it. It was a semester of tears and confusion. Ineligible textbook, unclear instructions, and god-awful essay exams. DON'T DO IT EVEN IF YOU THINK THAT YOU'LL BE FINE BECAUSE YOU'RE A GOOD STUDENT. THAT WAS ME.
You have to be very good at writing. Even if the entire class gets a quiz question wrong, he will not add a point. Expects you to deal with his crappy textbook. You won't learn much.

DO NOT take this class! He asks you to write four 2,000-word essays (expects them to be longer) based entirely on his textbook.There are no lectures, and nothing is on Canvas. He is wildly sensitive to any possible use of AI. He gave me a perfect score in every category, then gave me an F because AI was detected (I only used it to edit for grammar)

He doesn't know how to use Canvas You cannot do this class unless you buy a $70 notebook to get into his website and do the work Also, he only uses this website for everything, so you might miss something if you don't check it every day It is very messy as well he does not know how to clearly describe what needs to be done and how or where to do it

Get ready to write 8,000 -12,000 words (four exam papers). Every detail counts! The class is self-guided. Pretty intense workload but I learned A LOT and the professor was nice + responsive! If you're a bad writer or a slacker, don't even bother...you will not do well!! But, if you're motivated and keep up with the work, you can totally get an A..

Had to drop because of how rude professor is. I had a few emails where I asked clarifying questions and was belittled by him. I am a good student, I've taken other difficult biology related classes with professors that were poorly rated and deemed "difficult", however, the exchange I had with him made my decision very easy. Please quit already.
I am generally a good student but DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. When there is a normal asyn

This class taught me how to pretend I understand biology without learning anything. Be prepared to write 20,000 words with your main source being a confusing typo-ridden $60 online textbook; I would suggest taking literally anything else. Not even my UCSB bio major roommate could decipher this professor's wording.

Just shouldn't be a teacher. I'm sure he's a great science guy but teaching is just not for him. He grades insanely difficult on his exams which are 2000 word minimum papers. Feedback is of a fifth grader proof reading your work. Save yourself time and money and don't take his class.

Wackest teacher on the whole west coast. Never heard his voice or saw his face the whole school year. The whole class material is based off of his personal textbook website. I'm sure he's a smart guy but he's definetly cut out to be teaching at any level. Don't take his class.

I took bio 140 as a dual enrollment highschooler. The class was a lot of reading and notes. I took online notes, you need to stay on top of it. There is some extra credit which is nice. For the exams write way more than you need to. I got 89 with 2600 words, 98 with 4716 words, 100 with 5947,100 with 5714. The minimum word count is 2000 btw -_-

The whole class is 5 quizzes and 4 exams with many opportunities for extra credit. The exams are not in a typical test format: 1 exam a month, 2000 words each, writing answers to each question. You don't even have to read the entire textbook! Just read what is required for the exams. It's easy to get an A as long as you try.

This class was insanely tricky due to the course being extensive in material and the way everything was organized. I found myself consistently getting confused. Exams are very lengthy, and the textbooks (that the professor wrote and forced you to buy to even participate in the class) do not help.

All this class seems to be is reading off of his textbook website that is both outdated and written at a level beyond a beginner to the subject, and you are expected to comprehend 4+ subjects for each written exam with said website. The way the text is all shoddily clumped together and presented gives me no passion for this subject.
The Textbook is very confusing, it is written by him. The only good thing is that he answers emails fast but gets ready for a lot of writing, quizzes, and labs. Not going to lie his class is hard.

Prof doesn't use canvas everything is on this terrible outdated website you have to pay to access. He puts NO effort in the class, not a single recorded lecture. In a nutshell you're handed a bad textbook and expected to teach yourself enough to write 4 specific and detailed papers on the material or you fail. Save yourself <3

This guy refuses to be incorrect about anything and does not care enough to spell check or reread his "textbook". While being not only unsympathetic, unapproachable and narcissistic, the man makes students purchase his own (self-published, I assume) textbook. TAKE AT YOUR OWN RISK.

Don't take this class or anything with Prof Friesen unless you want to hate your life. No lectures, the only assignments are quizzes and exams. Exams are 2000 word papers graded harshly. No lectures or any resources besides his own terribly formatted website/"textbook." I failed the first exam, dropped the class, and changed my major.
No lectures, no information other than his "textbook" which is his own poorly written website. Half of the class failed the first exam. Waste of money and time.

Ive taken ecology and natural science from Professor Friesen and I really enjoyed the material he covered. The information was provided through his own textbook, which is well written and very informative. I highly recommend his classes if youre passionate about science and want to be challenged. I learned so much I highly recommend his courses

Do not take this class. He has his own textbook that's absolutely ludicrous. The man is obviously a biology genius but when it comes to writing? Complete gibberish making it incredibly difficult to get any conclusive information from the textbook. Exams are incredibly long and require a lot of writing, textbook quality doesn't help.
This professor has a very weird class and has zero sympathy for students struggling. This class is not with taking if you're considering it, you will most likely fail this class.

Mr. Friesen is a really knowledgable professor, and I am really glad to have learned from him. The class is super reading heavy, and the exams take a fair amount of brain power. You will definitely work for your grade. The best advice I have to give is take your own notes, do outside research, and ask questions. He is accommodating & available.

Professor Friesen seems like a great guy, but he needs to make some changes to his class. Everything is done through a separate website using your personal email which makes things much harder. I work for days at a time on my essays and have never scored above 60%. Each essay is 2000 words and so hard to write. On my last 2500 word essay I got 28%.

I was having internet issues and was 2 minutes late to turn in a project I had spent hours doing, Gallegos wouldn't accept it, I had to take another course with him only because there was no more available classes except his and yet again faced similar issues, avoid his courses if you can. Pick someone better.

Professor cares little about students and artwork, poorly understands photoshop, often confused and lost during lessons. Actively punishes graphic designers and artists, gave me significantly lower grades on hand drawn vectors, then poorly compiled photoshop images with obvious errors. Class is extremely inaccessible, and extremely expensive.

If you want a professor who truly cares about his students, the questions they ask, and what they learn about then DO NOT TAKE Michael's class. None of the work is clear and if you ask for more info he gives you a vague response, telling you to find out on your own. if you want to learn about marketing, or graphics, this class won't teach you.
Do not take this class if you are not interested in coding and computer science. Not an art class really at all.

There are very clear expectations published about this class by the prof: online class, read the textbook, must be organized to get through content. I found that professor Gallegos ALWAYS responded to my emails, his instructions were EXTREMELY clear, and he was so helpful. He even offers you the ability to re-submit projects for a higher grade.

Professor Gallegos's MKT 164 class is a course I wouldn't take again. He is very unclear on assignment instructions and he's a tough grader, he doesn't provide any feedback especially if you do poorly on an assignment. This course is very project heavy which makes up most of your grade. The projects, discussions, and quizzes take up a ton of time.
Would not take professor again.

This professor has no idea how to teach. At most, this class is a showcase for his mediocre photoshop skills of the underwater photographs he has taken. His "lessons" consisted of him playing old videos featuring long expired versions of photoshop. The professor also regularly made chauvinistic remarks about women. Avoid this course, use YouTube.

Michael is my favorite teacher at SBCC without any doubt. He is very warm, helping and respectful. His tutorials are very helpful and it's relatively easy to get an A. He's not only a teacher but a friend as well. There's honestly nothing bad I can say.

I took both MAT 153 & 154. I was about to graduate with just a graphic design major, but these classes inspired me to pursue web design. From informational lectures to actual useful projects, the class material learned from him has been irreplaceable. Professor Gallegos went above & beyond my expectations - always helped me outside of class!

There was nothing especially great or horrifically terrible about Gallegos's class. The weekly assignments can add up quickly, but they are fairly quick aside from two or three that took 3+ hours. He does not grade regular assignments, but his TA Emily is extremely nice, and very accessible. Don't waste your money User: MAT131, PASS: photoshop

Took her class in person and she was awesome! She is very passionate about teaching and she makes you work hard for the grade that you want! She was a tough grader in the beginning but slowly became a little more lenient. She starts class from the second it's supposed to start and finishes right on the dot. You have to work hard for your grade.

Ms Liz was my lab instructor for BIO100 I could write an essay about how amazing she was as a professor and in general, but Ill keep it short. If you can have her as your lab instructor, DO IT. Shes extremely passionate about what she teaches and it helps you, in turn, with understanding material and absorbing it. Ms Liz is wonderful!!!

Liz is a very caring teacher. She gives you chance to fix the unsubmitted homework. When you have the questions, just come forward to her and ask. She is really warm-hearted and responsible. I love this teacher.

She's not the teacher that's name is on the course, but she taught my class. She is very passionate about the class and happy to teach. Lab and 2 mini quizzes are each week. 4ish exams. They are proctored. You have to work for your grade, but I enjoyed the overall atmosphere.

Liz is such a good professor! She really cares about her students and wants them to succeed. Recommend going to the live lab reviews and intros if you can, they are really helpful and make the labs less overwhelming.

I took concepts of botany with Liz Gaspar in 2018. It was so rad to learn all about plants. The professor was so awesome; She really loves plants and is so fun to be in class with. Really a kind human! I hope she teaches the course again so other students could stoke out about plants with her.

Really really amazing professor. He's hilarious, great at teaching, he explains math in a very elementary school level way, he makes everything easy to understand. He's very very strict about attendance, and he gives quite a lot of homework, but regardless, I enjoyed his class a lot and I learned a lot from it.

I struggle with math, so this class wasn't easy for me, even though I understand it's pretty elementary stuff. If you need a math class to pass, take this one. I'm horrible at math, yet I still managed to get a B, and I had no clue what was going on. Therefore, anyone with a basic understanding of math should be able to achieve an A.

I took this class in the fall semester, and I still think about how great it was. I'm not amazing with math, but after a few of his lectures something just clicked and it made perfect sense. Excellent teacher, and truly cares about his students

Georgakis keeps it entertaining by throwing in jokes and explaining math by connecting to the real world. All tests, quizzes and even the final are completely open note and his study guides are extremely accurate to the actual exam material. He is very caring and supportive. The course load is extremely chill. Would recommend!

Peter Georgakis is the worst teacher I have ever had in my entire history of 16 years of schooling. This man is straight up mean, intentionally tried to embarrass me when I would ask questions, graded me unfairly based on the fact that he didn't like me (he made it clear). His class was 2 hours long starting at 8 am. may his karma catch up to him

Great guy with many interesting stories. Calculus was complicated for me, but he made it simple and broke down the steps in his lecture. His practice tests and exams are very similar. Studying is a must, and you'll be alright if you do your homework. You must put in the work if you want to pass. I suggest you show all the work and all the steps.

He's a good guy but on certain days the dude is hella mean and got mad at kids just for asking a question. 95% of your grade is tests and the other 5% is homework which he gives an insane amount of. He is very hard to get in touch with as he is never in math lab for his "office hours", the class is not for the weak-minded, I had to work very hard.

Class is incredibly easy and easy for students who are not focused on math. Course material is taught using three different methods, and the tutor is allegedly available (I could never get a Zoom meeting with her.) There are very few reasons to fail this course because all you have to do is put in the bare minimum.

This guy is the most passive aggressive human I have ever met. However, he explained everything so thoroughly and in easy alternatives to traditional math. Every test/quiz was completely open note including the final.

Says he wants you to pass and is there to help you. I found that he was very nonunderstanding when it came to any personal matter. Strict when it comes to attendance. If you don't do the work on time you wont pass period. NO tollarence for late work what so ever.
not a good professor, wasn't very helpful and doesn't teach good.
Horrible Professor, wouldn't take again. Not understanding or reliable. Don't take this professor

Best math teacher I have ever had! I was so nervous about college math but Professor Georgakis is so patient and a great teacher. He keeps people engaged and interested in math. He also has so much life experience doing all different jobs and i really respect that.

Georgakis is the best math teacher I have ever had. As long as you go to class and put the work in you can absolutely do well in this class. I have never enjoyed math before taking his Math 130. He is willing to help and he takes note of what the class is not understanding. Take him if you can!

I had to drop his class because I had a really bad family emergency, and he would not allow me to make up the work I missed in the one week I had to leave. My grade took a huge drop to where I had no other choice but to drop the class with a W! I do not like how he would grade also, be prepared to fail this class even with turning in all your work!

Get ready to work hard! Professor Georgakis was alright. He graciously allowed open notes on exams and was a fast grader. The homework load was absurd. Kind of a negative class environment at times... wish he was more positive and complained less during class lectures. Sometimes he makes you feel stupid, but overall he can be pretty fair/decent

I adored this professor. He was so caring and such a good teacher. I'm not good at math, and he taught so well that I got an A in CALCULUS. He was phenomenal and really, really wanted us to pass and made the class fun, exciting, and relatively easy. Show up to class, make an effort, and he'll make sure you do well. DEF TAKE HIM :))

Professor Peter Georgakis is the best professor you could truly ask for. Not only is he funny and tells great stories in class he does an even more amazing job at lecturing and explaining Math 114 to his students.

Professor Georgakis truly cares about his students wellbeing and success. Will help you as much as he can. Any questions you have, he will make sure to answer them thoroughly. He wants you to understand and he does everything he can to support you. Great practice tests. Clear lectures. He will go over real questions from the quizzes/exams. 11/10 (:

Go to class, follow examples he solves, take time on tests. He wants students succeess! Will lead you to water, but can not, nor desires to make you drink. He lends every opportunity to students to succeed: no time limits on tests, SHOWS test in class, practice tests are the slightly altered test questions. His stories=hilarious. Accent=legendary.

1. He offers no assistance in class other than a very mediocre tutor. 2. His grading is absolutely terrible, hell mark you off on things and leave no explanation for it. 3. The class grade is made up of only quizzes and tests, no homework to be graded. 4. Its clear that he doesnt treat online classes any different than in person. PICK SUM1 ELSE!
Great guy but if you don't show up to class you will fail.
He is a nice guy but all we do is quizzes. No homework, assignments or anything, only quizzes the entire year.

The class wouldnt be hard if Professor Georgakis wasnt so hot and cold. He can be such a kind human being one day and then remarkably callous and cruel on other days. He seems to simultaneously try really hard to teach well while also sabotage students. Its hard to explain. I hope you just take my word for it and stay away from him.

Peter is the worst Math Professor I have taken at SBCC. His Canvas page is a mess. He barely goes over the material and expects you to know what you are doing within minutes. He makes you take exams at 7 am and gives you only until 1 or 3pm to finish them. Btw this was for his Summer 1 class. Good luck if you take him, or just take another class.

Peter truly cares about his students, and I know he is going through some hard times in his personal life. Math 130 was a difficult class, but he was available during tests to ask questions via zoom, and he had multiple office hours to help. He got frustrated with students using Chegg which is understandable. He is a good guy and wants to help.

Peter gets lots of bad press, but he is a good professor if you're clear about what you need more help with. He teaches fast, so if you have questions you need to speak up and ask. NO mandatory homework, so everything in the grade book is tests/quizzes. He is a good teacher, just gets impatient with students' lack of care for the class.

Pete could be great in person, online not so much. He was very passive aggressive when speaking to students, got frustrated very easily and left no feedback on tests. It was a hard class, he did not make it easier. No homework, or textbook, really only learning for tests. His online structure was awful and he was painful to communicate with.

Peter is a good teacher but I think he struggles with online which upsets students. He is caring and he tries extremely hard to make the class as doable as possible for students. I hate to see the rude comments lefts about him because it is untrue. He is a great professor for in person classes.

Georgakis is the best! I struggle in Math but he is such a great professor that will help you out if you come to class and do your assignments. No homework for the class only quizzes and tests. This sounds scary but it really isn't. The quizzes are basically homework and all quizzes are multiple choice. First-class I have taken with useful math!
One of the best teachers you know!
Math 117 was one of my favorite classes in all of collage, Prof. Gilbert was kind and had some awesome lectures, he was a tough grader though
amazing teacher
He was good but for my learning style not that great. You kinda have to teach yourself I would say go to the math tutors they helped a lot but also if you missed class your grade goes down.

Easiest math class I've taken. First third is Math 150 review; new topics like integration by parts, parameterization, and series are manageable with a solid calculus foundation. Exams are light and similar to homework. Quizzes are group-based, so it's hard to do poorly. Instructor explains well and answers all questions. Highly recommended.
The most indifferent teacher I've ever had. Barely wants to help at office hours and doesn't care if you do well.

Cares for students' success. Quick response time, whether in person or replying to email. Entertaining, fun, easy lectures. Lectures are for the most important; the rest of the chapters are up to the student to study. Clear directions. Homework matters. Fun, easy personality, approachable. I bugged him with so many dumb questions, lol.

The class itself is not particularly hard, just slightly frustrating as his teaching style would be more suited towards in person. He gave us homework during spring break though, that was incredibly frustrating.

I was worried about my grade and asked for help multiple times, but he only said to "keep trying." Despite never missing class, I had to drop to save my GPA. Most students dropped or failed. He ignored emails, admitting he saw mine but didn't reply. My friend, sick on exam day, got no response and was forced to take it with only half the time.

if you are taking this class, be ready to put the rest of your classes on the backburner. professor gilbert is not the nicest in emails, and will also not accept any kid of late work. you will be doing a LOT of reading, but his lectures are helpful and easy to follow, and his canvas has a lot of resources (like videos for each and every chapter)

Prof Gilbert's class is straightforward - show up, pay attention, and you can get an A. He's hands-off but allows flexibility. unstuck ai was a lifesaver for test prep along with the textbook. Overall, a decent math class if you're self-motivated.

Never miss a class. You'll never catch up and he will be no help at all. He barely will respond to emails and if you ask for help he's very inattentive. His class is very notes based with little hands on trial and error. Each test will be able to make or break your grade. Definitely not my style. Most people in my class really struggled throughout.

Gilbert himself was okay. I struggle with math so this class was difficult for me. Lots of homework and quizzes. There's 3 non cumulative exams and then a cumulative final exam. We also had 2 labs, a 3rd for extra credit. I recommend doing those at the math lab, otherwise it can be very confusing. Do the work and participate and you should pass.

He is a great in person teacher. He explains the concepts very thoroughly and is always offering to answer questions or spend time outside of class on subjects. His syllabus policy is very beneficial for students as well due to his willingness of dropping 10 homework grades if scored low and he could use the final to replace one of the exams

I honestly don't know if there is a teacher on this planet worse than David Gilbert. He expects you to teach yourself everything off of an expensive paid course! On top of that, everything your exams are on, have nothing to do with the things you have been learning off that course because he makes them himself. (Terribly I might add.) Dont Do It!!!

Doesn't explain how to solve a problem besides solving examples on the board with little to no explantation. Attendance is mandatory and effects your grade. After receiving a concussion and showing up now more than five minutes late, he marked down my grade even after explaining the situation, causing me to have to withdrawal. Zero empathy.

I would not recommend this teacher. It's as though he wants you to fail. He doesn't teach, doesn't respond to emails promptly. Terrible communicator. I'm surprised SBCC has not fired him He makes learning miserable. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS. I loved college until I encountered him.

I loooved Mr. Gilbert he was so awesome I took Stats and he was able to explain everything clearly and made it straightforward. If you do the homework you'll be fine but if you dont you will not pass. He was so funny and encouraging i'd definitely recommend.

Love his class. Everything is straight forward in his class. Show up and pay attention and it will turn into an A. He allows a notecard on tests. He says "i dont care" and "do it how you want" to many things which allows you to not be confined to just the way solves the math problems. The way he teaches is entertaining and he has a sense on humor.

all of the reviews may have been for online. I had Gilbert in person and he was AMAZING. Probably the best math teacher I've ever had. While class can get boring, his teaching is very straight forward. You get to do quizzes with the whole class, and you get half a piece of paper to write notes on for the tests and final. He loves helping.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. You learn more from the book them him. He gives the "idc" attitude and seems like he does not care about his students. Graded by only three tests and 5 quizzes. tutor is mandatory in order to understand this class. he assumes that you know information about the subject, but in reality, you don't. DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS CLASS

Gilbert is the teacher that never teaches. You learn more from the book than the actual class. He is a very tough grader and your grade depends on three tests and few quizzes. If you miss class, you have to hope that he puts the material on Canvas or else you are screwed. He embraces a "mid" teacher. DO NOT RECOMMEND. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!

David Gilbert is by far the worst professor I have ever. Decent lectures. Incredibly long tests, around 20-25 questions in 2 hours is incredibly unrealistic. Most professors give 10-12 with that time span. Hard grader as well, he barely gives partial credit for tests questions. Its embarrassing that SBCC has a professor like this. Stay away.

Took this course for the summer be ready to not feel like its an actual class. Everything was on Hawkes Learning professor only put out exams and labs using SPSS with poor instructions. Also you have to buy the expensive calculator on top of buying a whole textbook just to get the code for the online Hawkes Learning.

This man is the worst math teacher one can possible get. He truly wants you to get a bad grade. Usually his online tests are available for four hours. On the final (double the size), he only opened it for 3 hours. Truly unnecessary. His online labs also make ZERO sense. It requires you to use Windows on a Mac computer. Absolutely Useless. Avoid!

Literally the worst math 'teacher' I've ever taken. All learning done is through online quizzes and homework with no direct feedback/direction from him. The most hands off teacher I've ever taken and never actually taught anything himself, only the paid online book.

this is the first math teacher I've ever had that asks test questions asking you to define mathematical terms. math has always been about remembering formulas, but he just asks for too much; it's like he wants you to get a lower grade. In a test week, expect the same amount of homework as usual, with an additional lab assignment on top of that.

Terrible teacher. Starting with the lecture that has no explanations of the material, but only straight exercise examples. So one wouldn't be able to understand why are they studying this. Secondly, homework was 8% in the beginning, mid class he changed it to 12%, and then at the end after you already knew what grade you had, he changed it to 18%

Tests were difficult, there were some questions that we only went over once in class that were on the test, but overall not too bad. He uses your grade for the final to replace your lowest test grade which is awesome. This class definitely required a lot of effort to be put in. I definitely suggest making a study group/going to the math lab.

This class was very hard and it was difficult to get in touch with Mr.Gilbert. I was constantly trying to get ahead and it still wasn't enough. The tests and lab were pretty challenging and it was all self-taught. Mr.Gilbert does not respond to emails timely and when he did, it was very blunt and short. If you are good at math, knock yourself out.

I love Timothy Gilmore! I would recommend his class to any freshman, and he is so interesting. His class was super interesting and not only taught me about English but also made me look at life from a different perspective.

Professor Gilmore was both very intimidating and very interesting. I heard that a lot of girls took his class because they thought he was "hot". His lectures were super interesting and made you think in a special way. He is a fairly tough grader but if you like writing he is great. He gives good feedback on essays.

Tim is great and seems to be a very intellectual person. i always looked forward to attending his lectures and would take him next semester if there were more time frames for classes. He is a fair grader when it comes to essays but there are a lot of them. you get lots of time to plan and prepare though. if you put the time in it's an easy A

Gilmore is very profound. He will lecture about things you wouldn't think to think deeply about, topics that will stick with you which is why I say this class is a life class not so much about English. You'll write a few essays but you will get to choose what to write it on which was a great aspect. Don't take his class if you can't sit & listen!

I thought Gilmore was great. I never had an issue with the way he graded, and in fact I thought he was incredibly lax. I turned every single paper I wrote (worth a combined total of 70% of my grade) in late, and never received a penalty. He has great lectures that really make you think, and though I disagree with him on some things, he's great.

long and aimless lectures but by the end i developed a genuine appreciation for the material. honestly left the class with a new perspective on life, he just talks at you for an hour every day but there's not much actual work & if you have the energy to listen it's worth hearing. assignments are just a few essays but very detail oriented

Did not take class because professor was the worst. Tried to join class and he kept blowing me off and trying me he was going to"meditate on it". Literally such a joke of a prof. So many better options he also thinks he's incredibly profound- he's not. Seems like a takes mushies and goes to class - not in a fun way, but a braindead one.

Drones on in lectures for 2 hours every single class about his own antiquated opinions as a middle-aged man about how modern music is terrible and how modern media is less sophisticated than it was when he was young. Nice guy, and has semi-interesting discussions, but he's a tough grader and his grading criteria is vague and difficult to follow.

Take Professor Gilmore!!! I can't recommend him enough. At first I thought he was sort of a fool, but he's so freaking smart its not even funny. He's going to talk about some extremely thought provoking ideas, so having an open mind is essential, because there will be class discussions IRL that are extremely engaging. I can't recommend him enough.

He is a pretty strict grader. I don't understand why his class is said to be an easy A. There were three papers, a 5-minute speech, and lots of readings. Besides the papers, he often asked us to write down our opinions and thoughts on paper without prior notice. The topics for the papers were challenging to understand each time.

Loved this class! While I thought Professor Gilmore was a tiny intimidating in the beginning, he is actually just passionate about his lecture material and a deep-thinker. He cares about his students, listens to them without distraction, and cracked quite a few smiles. Super engaging and interesting lectures! Gets you thinking outside of the box:)

I seriously regret taking this professor. The class is not tough, but the essays and final exams are very difficult. He also does really tough grades. We have 3 essays and essay 2 is an extension of essay 1. He helps students but his points are very vague and hard to understand. If you wanna get good grades, you should choose another professor!

Gilmore's class is pretty easy if you do the work, however, his lectures are beyond boring and often off-topic. There was only a few times I enjoyed his class, most of my time was spent looking at the time. He's very passionate about what he says but I don't think he ever came to class with a plan. He talks and talks but never gets anywhere.

Very easy class. No tests or quizzes just 3 papers and a lot of reading. Class is just lecture for about an hour and half but can get extremely boring if you're not engaged. Very nice guy and easy going. If your looking for an easy A, definitely take this class. However, don't expect to learn much about writing or analyzing literature.
His class is overall easy and simple. Deadlines are clear as well as his grading criteria. His grading does get slightly tougher towards end of semester but still easy.
Hes a really cool dude who is very smart and wants to share the knowledge. Id take this class again in a heartbeat, really made me think outside the box.

Prof. Gilmore is a good teacher that doesn't ask for too much. He doesn't force you to participate which is really nice for those that get nervous. All you have to do is write a few GOOD essays and present once and you will get an A. He talks straight through 99.9% of the class and it can be boring but it's not the worst. Great choice for ENG111

Professor Gilmore is a cool guy, but as a professor its hard to say I learned a lot from him. His class consists of him lecturing about society. The topics that he brings up are great and controversial and very thought provoking. I would suggest this class for people who like new ideas, but not for people who want to improve their writing skills

I really enjoyed Professor Gilmore's class. Lectures are interesting and fun. I definitely had some good belly laughs which made class enjoyable. Readings are long but doable. He is a tough grader though.

He's the easiest professor I currently have. He assigns "homework" each class which are readings, but you don't have to actually do any of them because you aren't required to speak in class. He just lectures the entire class about the reading, but gets very off topic. He's a really cool guy overall though, I'd be down to crack a cold one with Tim.

Participation is mandatory, he allows only a few absences before it affects your grade. Very interesting and introspective class lectures. There are 4 or so papers, which he allows the class a chance to edit for a higher grade and one group project for the final which is fun and interesting.

Extremely intelligent and aware human being. If you want your mind expanded, take his class. His tone of voice is very monotone which takes the importance away of what he is trying to convey to his students on some level but he speaks truth. Very easy class, open discussion based and 4 papers in total.

Prof. Gilmore is a great choice for ENG110. He is extremely sarcastic and can talk for hours, which I enjoy but is definitely not for everyone. I know a lot of people in the class that find him boring. There are only about five things that determine your grade(4 papers & 1 debate) which are all really easy. He is also a lenient grader. Recommended!

I took the class because I heard he's hot. On a different note, he is very good about leaving specific notes on your essays to improve them definitely to developing a rapport with him as he is definitely likely to be a little less harsh with you. (He has a strong personality). Other than that I passed the class with an A!
Great teacher! Makes concepts relatable and is very passionate about his subject!

The only thing that was graded in the class were 3 essays and a group debate. He assigned a book and weekly readings but there weren't any quizzes on them. Its more of a philosophy class in my opinion. LECTURE HEAVY! Be prepared to sit there and listen/talk the whole class. I never needed to take notes and there were never in class assignments.

Timothy Gilmore is a very inspiring, intelligent, and caring professor. His feedback is extremely valuable, extensive, and unmatched. His lectures are long, however very interesting and you can learn a lot from him if you pay attention. Besides the fact that he is indeed very hot, his character and points of view make his lectures even better.

His lectures are long but interesting. He is super helpful during office hours. you will get an A if you do go consult him often about your papers. He is strict about attendance so make sure you go to every lecture. He is very inspirational and tells us a lot of information outside of class. I thought it was just me but he IS super hot. RECOMMEND

Timothy is a great teacher there is no denying that. A lot of people say he is difficult because he does make you work for your grade.I always loved english and that made me not push myself in my work but he gave SUCH GREAT feedback for each paper that i pushed myself to do better. HE DOES make you feel like you're in a philosophy class annoying

Tim is a great teacher and a really cool guy. He knows his stuff and is always ready to debate. His assignments are straightforward and not too challenging. Just show up and do your work and you'll get an A. Hardly any tests. His tone is a little dry but that's because he's so laid back; if you listen to what he says you'll learn a lot.

I love Gonella. The final project was a creative botany assignment which gave students to do whatever they wanted. His lectures are super detailed, his tests are easy and he is super accessible outside of class. HIGHLY recommend.

Took this course to get the credits, would not take again. All assignments were ready from the first day but had due dates. Did not feel like I took anything from this class. Maybe in person would have been different but I took it online and felt bored the entire time. Make sure you do the final papers early because they are worth a lot.

great teacher, responsive, material is interesting, canvas is well organized and easy to understand. weekly quizzes you can keep doing until you get them all right, which helps you actually make sure you learn everything

Love all of the courses he has led! It makes learning fun with the professor enjoys the content they teach and approaches it like an opportunity to knowledge share. Great class, wonderful instructor. So responsive, kind and practical.

He is fantastic! So personable, down to earth and just a great mentor. He has a ton of knowledge, is happy to share it and cares for his students. I love people with purpose and passion and he has both.
Easy lectures, everything in tests is in the lecture. Actually an interesting class!

Mike is great he is always willing to help you out if you don't get the questions or are having a hard time with the class. He loves his work and is very inspiring. Very enthusiastic about answering questions, and has good jokes for the right occasions. He is well connected and can help you in and outside of class. Take his classes!
Pretty chill class, super basic. Does not like students asking questions. Lets out early a lot, can't miss too many classes.
I loved my permaculture class with Mike. He makes information tangible and easy to understand. His humor is great and uses good visuals.

I really enjoyed Mike's class, and from what I have heard, so did my fellow classmates. He is witty and got some good laughs out of us. Sometimes he can be a little up tight when asked a question he expects you to know. Over all I enjoyed my classes with him.

I found Mike Gonella to be one of the most inspiring instructors I've ever had. He's passionate about the subjects he teaches and is quite engaged with his students. (Mike Gonella is actually an instructor for the horticulture dept., not the botany dept., but ratemyprofessors doesn't offer horticulture as an option)
Best teacher ever! She is really helpful and clear. I recommend taking her.
Excelent Teacher!
Very interesting and funny. Gives a lot of advice and tries to help even without asking. My favorite class this semester, and I don't even like English.
Engaging lessons, helpful exercises and feedback. Also very caring and flexible! A lot of little assignments but nothing crazy. Would recommend!
Hands down the best teacher I've ever had. Love her
