Essays were worth an insane amount of the overall grade. I had been getting all 100% on assignments leading up to the first essay. I got a 3.8/6 and it dropped my grade to a D but the percentage showed it should have been a C. I emailed her about it and she could not have been less helpful. Overall very unclear with instructions and unhelpful.
Very nice person and easy to pass class

Great professor! I had her during the summer and let me just tell you she will literally make you change your whole writing technique to fit her style. I had to learn to adapt to her style of writing and completely change my way of writing. It was totally worth it let me tell you! She made me really focus more on analysis and grammar. Get her!

I took her class during summer session 1. She was very understanding and was interested in her students. Does give a lot of homework but it is definitely manageable. Feel free to ask questions because she wants every student to succeed in her class. She likes when students participate. My writing skills definitely improved after taking her class :)

Make sure to show up for the classes, if not you will not become the great writer Miss Handley tries to make you. Do the work she assigns you, and take her feedback. She is willing to work with you to help you get as good of a grade as possible. You have to prepare for a lot of reading and writing, but compared to other classes it's not too much.
She is a little unorganized and expects your writing to be like hers. You could write a perfect paper and she will still find something wrong with it.

She expects you to be as good as a writer as she is. She is very scattered and hard to see where is is taking things. Not my cup of tea but if your already a strong writer and are just taking the class to get it over with shes probably fine.

She definitely wants you to be a better writer and she gives good feedback. This class requires you to read out loud, interact with your classmates, and homework every class. Theres a total of 4 papers and attendance is mandatory.

One of the BEST teachers I've ever had. She is HILARIOUS and caring. She really wants to get to know her students, individually. Lots of homework and I mean LOTS of, but my ability of writing increased so much after I took her class. If I had the option, would definitely take it again. I found myself laughing so hard and so many times!
One of the best teachers I've ever had! She's hilarious and really clear with her essay prompts. If you have the chance, take her ENG100 class! Would take this class again for sure

She isn't a very good teacher, If you try hard in her class she will continually keep increasing the "busy" work you need to do. She once told me to completely do over, a essay I spent over a hour in the writing center with a tutored, only to have her not look at it and reject it.
Professor Handley was one of my favorite professors at SBCC.
One of the best teachers I have ever had! Would take this class again for sure!

She breaks it down and makes all the assignments very clear. Its not a hard class; just do your essays and perfect your papers. Stay off your phone and pay attention. Super nice lady and really smart!!
One of the best teachers I have ever had. Very professional with a great attitude/personality.

She doesn't organize her lectures well so you never know what to expect when you come into class the next day which makes it hard to be prepared. she can also be pretty rude sometimes to students. i wouldn't recommend taking her.

English is not one of my favorite subjects but this teacher totally made the class. she's humorous and makes the class very exciting. I can honestly say if I had any other teacher I probably wouldn't have passed. she's helpful and very understanding.

Elizabeth is very knowledgable, and she knows her stuff. She is very clear on the essays that are required, and makes class very interesting with the TED talks that she plays. If you are unclear on the essays she will make sure to stay with you after class until you understand each assignment. Great professor overall!
Handley is so nice! she is very helpfull and shows a lot of ted talks! her class is interesting for an eng 100, she is funny and chill...but you must attend every class!
Amazing teacher! Very helpful and straight forward! Learned and improved my writing skills. Very nice teacher for ESL students to have.
AWESOME, yet challenging (in a good way), class. Learned a lot about how to be a better writer, thanks Ms. Handley!
THE BEST TEACHER! Makes everything simple and easy, loved the class - it's a must-take! She really created a fantastic learning environment for us and has a great sense of humor too!
She is just the greatest. she makes the complicated simple - direct and helpful.
Great teacher. She made the class as painless as possible. Take this class if you want a reasonable teacher. She's really down to earth and understanding.
SUPER, easy-going teacher. She lets you leave early from almost every class and also lets you turn in assignments late.
great teacher!
No Comments

She was so kind and truly cared for all her students! She made it known that she wanted us to succeed but we would have to make the effort too. If you do the assignments, take the feedback and participate in class you will have a great time and do well! What she gave us to read was very moving and motivational. It was amazing!

She is the best teacher I have ever had and I would be remiss if I didn't choose her as my English110 teacher. She is very kind and attentive. As an international student, I was not confident in my English but she helped me a lot. If you have any problems, she will help you carefully. I feel really lucky to have met her.

She is the best English teacher I've ever had! I love this class so much. She is very caring and respectful, and you just have to do your work. She is very manageable and gives one assignment per week, which is very helpful when it comes to other classes having a lot of work. Overall, she's an amazing person!!

She is the best English teacher I've ever had! I love this class so much. She is very caring and respectful, and you just have to do your work. She is very manageable and gives 1 assignment per week, which is very helpful when it comes to other classes having a lot of work. Overall, she's an amazing person!!

Awesome Professor! Please take her if you get the chance! She is super nice, and really cares about what she is teaching and each of her students. She gives very personalized feedback and gives lots of extra credit! 10/10!

Judy is a professor who made my first semester of college amazing. She was caring, she answered all my questions, and was overall an amazing professor. She made essay writing a breeze, and her feedback was extremely helpful!

Professor Harper is great! She has clear assignments that were thoughtful to the text, group discussions are really fun. Turn in papers before the due date for extra credit and also gives great feedback, would totally take a higher level class with her.

She is very sweet and funny. I enjoyed her class the most out of all my classes I've taken this semester. I'm a freshman so having her as my first english teacher was pretty great since her class is so easy. Her writing assignments are usually custom to whatever you want to write about. She also gives more than one deadline on homework/essays.
Professor Harper is the best ENG 110 and 111 teacher! She really cares about her students

Professor Harper is amazing! She goes above and beyond to have her students succeed. There are many opportunities for extra credit and revisions. She is also understanding if you have a situation come up and will do her best to accommodate. She is truly one of the best professors on campus.

I had Professor Harper for ENG111. The best option if you have to take this class. Her class felt like a one unit course but not for the bad reasons. She wasn't a "subjective" english teacher and encouraged her class to write about anything. Overall a super easy class with a super great professor. Take her if you have to take this course, easy A

Took English 110 and 111 with Judy. Completely changed my life. She is the best teacher I have ever had in my life. Cared about students not only as students but as humans. Incredible human, could not recommend taking her enough.

Judy really cares about her students and makes class fun. She let us a choose a movie to watch for our final, and basically just had us do a free write on it. Almost every single assignment is "what do you think", and you just write (not even in essay format) your own honest opinions about certain subjects. I wish I could take her again for 111 :(

Genuinely such a good and caring professor. She has a very clear grading system that's super easy to follow, and she takes her students' feedback into consideration. Very sweet and has a lot of extra credit opportunities!
Awesome professor. Cares about her students, nice workload, fun lectures, everything about it was great.

Her classes are very short and usually very fun. Her feedback is incredibly sweet but also very helpful. Taking this class will be an easier A than with other professors, but you'll also learn so much.

The best English teacher I ever had. Funny, caring, helpful, and just a sweetheart. She helped me, as well as other students with all the assignments, we had a lot of extra credit assignments and basically, you can make up any essay that you wrote.
best professor i ever had!!!

the greatest professor I've ever had. I moved here less than six months ago and I was not even speaking English fluently but I got an a+. she is inspirational and gives every opportunity to boost your grade. she also has an amazing personality and cares about every single of her students. Recommended.
the most caring professor i have ever had.
I loved her!
She is a fantastic professor; take her, and you won't regret it.

Professor Harper is by far the best professor I've had. She is a very caring person. In this English class I developed my reading, writing, and speaking skills even more, but I also grew as a person. The essays and reading are interesting and relevant to today's problems in society. Attendance is mandatory if you want to be successful.

Best teacher I have ever had. Super caring and really wants to help you do good and improve your writing skills. Assignments and instructions are very clear and even fun sometimes. Makes reading and writing enjoyable! 10/10 recommend

Judy is a fantastic teacher. Very well spoken and captivating, her class has had a profound impact on my outlook in life. The essays will challenge your writing ability, but she gives lots of very helpful feedback. Attendance is also generally required, if you want to succeed at least. A true gem of a teacher, everything you could ask for.

Professor Harper if the best professor I've ever had! She is kind, caring, thoughtful and wants her students to succeed! She gives extra credit and lots of commentary on your assignments, plus is an easy grader! She is always the accessible when you need help and the books you read are enjoyable! 100% would enjoy to have her again!!
Enjoyed this class and would take it again

Professor Harper is by far the best English professor out there, at least she's the best I've ever had. You can tell she genuinely loves teaching and truly cares about her students. Her assignments are always intriguing. She did make us read 2 books but I assure you they are not boring whatsoever. You truly end up loving to read in this class.

According to Sangster No one should teach who is not in love with teaching.. Professor Harper understands this quote very well. She is in love with teaching, she fits her profession. She wants you to learn and reach your goal. She got me on the right track. She motivated me to continue. If you want to increase your English skills, take her class.

Shes the best teacher Ive had in a long time. She really takes time to get to know her students and values conversation to know youre interested in the readings. As long as you do the assignments thoughtfully youll pass with an A or B no problem and RESLLY improve in your writing.
Enjoyed her class, learned a lot!
Recommended professor

Very difficult to work with. In every essay you are required to cite everything that isn't common knowledge which is up to her judgement. Therefore you have to cite every single sentence in order to get full credit. Makes you buy her textbook online, the book is okay but course can definitely go with out it. Her lectures are boring.
Great person, unfair grader

Dr. Haupt is an amazing professor. She provides you all the materials you need to succeed in her class and is always willing to assist students. She teaches with so much passion, you see her eyes light up when she talks about a topic she really likes. She is very accommodating to students. She seems serious but she's funny and kind.

Overall I really enjoyed this class. Professor Haupt is very understanding and gives very clear grading criteria. The most challenging part of this class was the essays which were just time consuming. To do good on them, just use A LOT of CITATIONS and you'll be good. She also offers a lot of extra credit and is very gracious when grading.

A valuable class just EXTREMELY fast passed, you will need to study A LOT outside of class to really fully understand the material because of how fast the class goes. Exams are composed of multiple choice and short essays questions which in my opinion are more about based on memorization. Overall a nice teacher though.

Really interesting class, especially the lectures. It's honestly a great class to grasp the basics about today's politics and political divide. Also, I did not find this class to be biased, but rather neutral. Exams are open notes. Study a little, pay attention to citations in your Discussion posts and then it's an easy A.

The class felt very skewed Left, focusing too much on identity politics and critiques of America without balance for conservative views. Overwhelming for a 101-level course. Not the fair intro I expected. I'd recommend students look for another teacher.

One of the most useful classes I've taken. I feel way more confident talking politics now. Class is fast-paced, but super organized. Lots of topics and (mostly) interesting videos. Lectures are concise. Textbook is just ok. Haupt seemed strict at first, but is actually very accommodating. Lowest grades are dropped. Plenty extra credit. Can't miss.
Vibes were good. There's some material, but it's really not a hard A. Prof's cool and keeps it real with the news. Finally get the whole Trump thing now lol.

Though Mrs. Haupt is understanding, this class was challenging, especially if you missed a lecture. The material is good, but entails tons of reading, studying, & LOTS of note taking. For me, it seemed like tests were more based on memory rather than understanding. Lastly, it is a lot of hrs for summer, & is can be hard to keep up with.

Professor Haupt means well but I did not like her teaching style. Lectures are long and heavy podcasts are 40 min long. Class is based on memorization rather than learning. The final week we had an essay exam midterm final exam Exams are remotely proctored. Her lectures are really long slide shows and she just reads off of them. Take someone else

I've had Prof. Haupt for 3 classes now: POLS 101, POLS 121, and GLST 101. My grade kept increasing as I took more classes. Yes, she's a harsh grader— but quiz answers can be found in lectures/videos assigned. The questions on the final are the same as those given on quizzes lol. Would take more of her classes even though she makes me a bit anxious!
Horrible teacher - That's all I have to say.

POV: you signed up for an online poli sci class thinking it'd be boring but now you're deep in a discussion thread about the power of the president at 10 pm. lowkey obsessed. the content actually hits.

This American Politics class is way better than I thought it'd be. For an online class, it's actually pretty engaging. It's not just memorizing facts. We're actually talking about real issues and how things play out today. Makes me think more about what's going on in the news. Reading amount is moderate and the exams are not that hard.

I took Mrs. Haupt's class in the summer, and it was definitely a good amount of reading. The class structure was perfect, the discussions were interesting and her instruction was well above most other professors!

Took her Pols 121 last semester and I really liked her she was a fair grader and the workload was very manageable, the only issue is we would sometimes fall of track in lectures but other than that, a good, very understanding and concise professor.

This class has been one of the hardest I've ever taken. Maybe it's because my major isn't related and the teaching style I'm not used to. I had a 4.0 GPA until this class came along. Attendance is mandatory since the professor hands out the topic sheets that you'll need for the exams.
I did not like her teaching style.

I loved this class! You definitely need to be on top of your attendance and work but if you are this class will be easy. Dr. Haupt is really kind and helpful. It can get content heavy but it is very manageable and easy to do well in this class.
Professor Haupt runs a tight ship, but if you keep up with the readings and notes, you'll do well. The exams are challenging but fair. Unstuckstudy was a game-changer for my study prep.

such an organized professor, have taken her for 2 online pols sci classes now and her lectures and materials are sooooo easy to follow and incredibly organized. easy to do well if you do the readings and take light notes, plan for the online essays in advance and you will be okay! proctored tests with lock down but not the hardest exams ever

She has a total of 4 Proctorio exams, their isn't a text book. Attendance is mandatory she takes it every day. The one thing i didnt like is she makes you cite everything from the lecture and videos from the minute mark, then all of her documents of the readings and lectures are protected google docs which making using them harder.

She was a very good teacher overall but had some nuances made it much more difficult. Zero technology allowed in class and everything must be handwritten and it takes hours. Her tests are difficult but the study guide was extra credit. She also was not helpful in the sense of even having a stapler. 6/10

I took her online course, which definitely hurt the overall experience. From what I've heard from other students that took her in-person course, it was better. She grades tough, and takes stuff out of her textbook that she wrote pretty constantly. BEWARE: online course requires use of proctorio, which is a massive invasion of privacy.

I learned a lot from Professor Haupt's International Politics class despite it being an online course. Make sure you follow her instructions clearly as she is strict but fair. She is very organized and you shouldn't have trouble following her lecture or modules.

I was really surprised how much I liked the class! The class is not very difficult and really interesting. My major is in the sciences, but this was a fantastic class for general ed. It's well organized, too.

Professor Haupt was wonderful and has made learning about political science extremely interesting. She's extremely knowledgeable on the topics she speaks about and is very clear about what she expects from you as a student. You will do well if you read and understand the textbook and listen to her lectures (they're delightfully brief).

Loved this class! All tests/quizzes were open note, and there were multiple opportunities for extra credit. There is a huge term paper, but it was honestly not bad, as you have to watch a movie and write about it (that's fun-ish for me at least!). She also gives you a lot of time to prepare (I did it in a week and still did good)

Prof hock is absolutely amazing. she really wants to see you succeed in her class and gives you so many resources to help you become successful in her class. her lectures are interesting and you can tell that she is very passionate about what she teaches. her quizzes and exams are based off of what we have learned, so no surprises.

The workload is very light, with a total of about an hour a week for me. As long as you participate in the discussion and do the quizzes, you will get an A. She is terrific and timely with her communication over email and responded to me within 15 minutes every time I had a question.

I've taken several of her courses. Sarah Hock makes me uncomfortable as a trans student. I've witnessed her multiple times misgendering students and other instructors who ask for specific pronouns to be used. She has a history of complaints but because of her power, she won't suffer consequences. Beware of what you're signing up for.

loved professor hock. her classwork is well organized and i like how it's a pattern every week, so you basically get the same assignments every week. she also shows a lot of care for the general mental well being of her students too.
Really nice, I like how she pretty much has us write the paper over the course of a few assignments. She gives notices and reminders to make sure everything stays on track.

Not a difficult class. Lots of discussions and they were pretty interesting. The paper was the only thing that was graded somewhat toughly. The paper was about communication within a film of your choice.

She's really kind and caring, but the class is pretty meh. I loved the material, but she has a TA that gives feedback, not her. So when she graded our term papers, I was very surprised to see her negative feedback. I did not like it she doesn't explain the "why" in the criticisms. You'll have to email. I learned so much tho, reco the course

Very easy to pass this class if you have the ability to do discussion posts each week. There is one very weighted paper in this class. So weighted that it's like 50 percent of your grade. It's not even that bad if you make sure to just do it. Overall, very easy if you can be consistent and/or do well on the term paper.

Prof Hock is amazing! This semester was my second time in one of her classes. The workload is easy until the term paper hits. It's a huge project but she is always available to help you out! I would def recommend her class for communication majors!

Helpful and responds to emails, canvas messages and even texts outside of class. She sends out regular reminders/announcements as well about assignments, the week's modules and whatnot. Overall she is a good prof, one research paper in this class and she gives really good feedback for it.

Professor Hock is soo nice!! I would 100% take a class with her again. The lectures are very clear & helpful. The workload is not too much but you have to do all the assignments ESPECIALLY the term paper.

Professor Hock has probably been one of the best I've had. A lot of the class material is relevant to todays day and age, she knows how to keep students engaged and motivated, and she gives constructive feed back! I wish I could take another one of her classes.

I've taken two of Professor Hock's classes, and they've both been really great. Her lectures are engaging and the content is explained clearly. There's a term paper, but as long as you do the assignments to prepare and reach out for help you'll do well. She really cares about her students succeeding.

Sarah Hock was a great teacher, you can tell how she truly cares for her students and really wants you to get the best grade you can. Overall a very nice person. Every quiz you get 2 attempts and it shows you the answers, discussions are a little lengthy but not hard to write. Lectures are about 20 mins but with notes 40 mins. Reading each week.
Just an all around great profesor, shes so sweet and helpful. Really made me enjoy going to the lecture and learn. Shes the best profesor in the comms department and is incredibly knowledgeable

Sarah was lovely, Her class structure is easy to follow along from day one as it hardly ever changes. The discussions were interesting with good videos/reads. She is such a sweetheart, and will answer any questions quickly. Leaves good feedback, and the midterm/final are very based on the lectures and textbook!

Sarah is awesome, I took her class in the summer online and had a bit of trouble keeping up (summer session goes very fast and I had work too) which is why I only got a B, which made sense. She is very helpful and responsive with feedback. She gets back to you quickly if you need help, unlike other professors here. She made class interesting too!

Hock is a very tough grader but is also the only teacher I had this semester who posted videos/lectures and was accessible to students. Majority of her assignments are VERY liberal. The class is fairly boring and common sense but she makes it difficult as you must do your work according to how she wants it, little room for self interpretation.

Professor Hock was a good professor, you just have to understand that COMM101 itself is kind of boring (since it's just an introductory class). However, she isn't a very tough grader and seemed like a generally nice person. I just don't think she added too much to the class, which made it a little more boring.

Prof. Hock's Comm 101 class was very easy to pass (as long as you do the group discussion each week). Don't put the term paper off until the last minute. She gives plenty of opportunities for her and the tutors to proof read and give feedback (make sure you get their opinion on the film you are reviewing). Easy class, easy pass-as long as you try.

My favorite professor at SBCC. Truly cares about your success and has clear expectations. Can be a tough grader, especially on the term paper, but if you meet all the requirements she lays out you should have no problem. I really like the videos she provides and she makes the topics super interesting. Read textbook and take notes and you'll pass.
This was my first communications class, which made me want to pursue a degree for communications. Overall, she is the sweetest and her grading is straight-forward. You're going to love her!

I loved having Prof. Hock for COMM101 online. She really cares for her students and wants them to succeed. There is a term paper but she gives lots of help and tells you ahead of time. Overall great class!
So much work in such a short amount of time.

She was very clear on her expectations and made sure that all the students had an opportunity to do well in the class. Offers extra credit. The only thing is the term paper, she grades difficult on that.

Professor Hock's class was pretty doable and was meaningful for me. However, you do have to study hard for the midterms and especially for the Final. It was pretty tough, not easy. Other than that, every assignment was reasonable and interesting to learn for me.
Even though your English is not good you still can get the A

There were pros and cons of this class. Pros: offered extra credit, spent a lot of time ensuring her lectures were on topic and up to date. Cons: there was one HUGE 200 point research paper. That was brutal. At least at the end of the semster, she offered one last extra credit assignment. That's what turned my B into an A.

I just took Sarah Hock's online comm class this fall. She's a wonderful. Listen & Watch all her lectures. Do all the discussions, read & respond to 3 of your classmates thoughts on the discussion & share a link related to the topic of your response, quizzes once a week, timed multi choice mid term & final (study quizzes) and one term paper!

Get ready to teach yourself because if you ask him how to improve he'll tell you to study harder. He would regularly not teach 1/3 of the material on exams in the 5 hours a week of lecture. Refused to curve the final with an F+ class avg after telling numerous students he would. He also repeatedly singled out and became upset at a disabled student.

Dr. Horwitz is a great professor overall. He is willing to help you if issues arise and help find a solution that works for you. His classes are challenging and require a lot of work, but they are very doable.

One of the best CHEM professors I've had at SBCC. The only downside is that it's a night lecture, or I would have taken CHEM156 with him. He does prepare you for the final. I found that he prepared students for questions presented by the ACS. The only downside is he cannot hear well but he's a great guy and will take his time to answer any "Ques?"

I love him as a person but be prepared to teach yourself. Lectures don't really make sense, he's not great at explaining and homework on the next lecture is due before the lecture, which doesn't make any sense. As long as you study, do the homework + additional problems on Pearson + his self tests, you'll be fine on the exams. Labs are confusing.

I could have gotten a better grade in this class, but life got in the way and there is always the second semester to this. Horwitz is amazing and cares about his students, no other way to put it. He responds promptly to emails and will always entertain you with a story. He uses the whiteboard alot, so be prepared to follow along with what hes doing

Great professor and great person. Cares a lot about his students and their success. Tests were pretty difficult, and they are not like the practice tests on the homework website we use. He responds quickly and will answer any questions you have. Will change due dates for homework assignments so you can get full points. He just wants you to do them.

Doc is a super cool guy. He never fails to make you laugh in lecture and teaches the content effectively. He is gonna make you work for a good grade, but that's just how chemistry always is. I wish I could have had Doc in-person (class online because of pandemic) as it would've been much more fun and probably less difficult. Awesome teacher though!

This class was tough online. It probably wouldn't be so bad in person. Lectures were hard to understand. The labs were really unorganized too. There were tutors but they didn't help much. LOTS OF HOMEWORK AND PREP WORK FOR LECTURES

Doc is an OUTSTANDING professor. He responds to emails ASAP and is extremely personable, caring, and kind. The harsh reviews here are anecdotal. Chemistry is HARD. Good professors don't give you an easy pass. I earned an A and there is no reason you shouldn't if you put in the necessary effort! He is also SO understanding toward personal situations

Dr. Horwitz is an outstanding professor and a nice guy. With that said, I must admit his class was challenging. Don't worry though, he is a really good teacher who puts a lot of effort and time into making sure you have all the recources you need to succeed. My Best piece of advice for you: put in the effort and Doc. will reciprocate.

It is time for him to retire instead of being looking cool. Take the textbook author, Nivaldo Tros class. It is not fair to pay the same amount of money for this instructor. He justifies his answer based on the instructors answer sheet. He uses 6 years old presentations and blames the tutors. The exams are time attacks, not critical thinkings.
Run!!!!Don't take him. Save your GPA. He doesn't do plus or minus on final grades. He doesn't care. General chem is a hard class and he just makes it worse.

This teacher really needs to retire. I took his class and did the work. I was one point away from a B and I emailed to him about an error in one of the quizzes that could give me a point. He decided not to give me the point and he said he couldn't justify giving me a B because I scored a C on the final. He doesn't do plus or minus on grades.

Professor Horwitz is truly a great teacher. Even though his lectures are long, and his exams are difficult, it's really worth it! I feel really confident going into Chem 156 (He is teaching that class as well). I also strongly, strongly, encourage going to the PAL sessions!! They are super helpful (especially before the final!!)
very excited to teach and very easy to ask questions without feeling intimidated !
Hardest chem teacher in Sbcc, there are 4 exams, and 1 lab fianl and a fianl, if you wanna get an Atake someone else!!!

Dr. H is awesome! While I hate gen chem, Dr. H made class fun. He has a witty dad sense of humor and high respect/care for his students. My lab partner and I were confused about a concept and he retaught it after class until we understood. He curves exams and gives many partial points! Would've gotten an A if I wasn't so distracted with my Bios

In this class, your entire grade rests on the four midterms, lab final, and final. The labs sort of help the grade, but the rest of the department does not do lab finals, and they also gove notes for the last two exams which he does not. I am a hardworking student with a 3.8 gpa, took AP chem, and I hard a hard time... not an easy guy to deal with

Horowitz is a great dude,glad I had him as a chem teacher. When choosing between him and Sally, just keep in mind that Sally does a better job structuring the class, but Doc is really good about helping his students outside of class and does care about you if you care about the class. Be ready though, no walk in the park. DO ALL THE PRACTICE PROBS!
This man is the best.

He is one of the best chemistry teachers at SBCC. He is incredibly smart, and entertaining. He has trouble hearing (has to wear hearing aids) but that doesn't stop him from trying to understand you. That aside, any complaints that students may have are "really" about the coursework and not the teacher. Gen. Chem 1&2 are really really hard classes.

Just awful, had him for the easiest chem yet he taught it worse and made it harder than O-chem. He made his tests quite hard and a lot of times I felt the material he covered was not adequately tested on.

Lectures are interesting, sometimes feel very long though. Tests can be very conceptual and hard, but if you really read all the chapters in depth and prepare continuously you can do this. He is also very helpful with questions regarding lecture or labs and is willing to explain concepts again. I really enjoyed the class!

he does not teach. he just doesn't teach and then expects us to to teach us ourselves. This class was supposed to be my easiest one this semester but i got the worst grade in it because of this guy and his three hour night lectures. i love chemistry but this guy ruined it for me.
Doc Red, the best-ease!

Dr. Horwitz is awesome! Keep up, get ahead. whatever you do, dont fall behind. His quizzes made sure students stay on top of things. Work hard and you should receive a good grade. He is an excellent teacher.

Such a cool guy, he teaches well enough so that you understand the concepts and he is really helpful when it comes to preparing for the final exam which is written by the american chemical society. You'll be happy to take this course.

Doc is the man!! He definitely pushes students to apply themselves. He's the first teacher I've ever had to actually makes you learn a concept rather than memorizing how to do a problem. This makes him kinda hard but you can email him at any time during the day with a question and he'll respond within 20 min. Best physical sic teacher I've had!!

Really enjoyed Dr. Horwitz. Very nice, helpful and cool guy, and he definitely knows his stuff. His lectures are a bit slow and it requires you to read the textbook to know all the test material. Tests are relatively hard and it becomes a challenging class if you don't keep up to date on your work.

He is an okay professor, but his tests are difficult which i enjoy because I like a Challenge! it's half classwork, and half learning on your own. He is always there to assist you if you need any help though!
Chill teacher, reading is a big part of the class, and don't submit late work. Fair Grader.

Summer course- No tests, one short-ish discussion per week, one quiz per week. One research paper that you start working on early in the class. Not too rushed, great feedback. Textbook chapters are long but this is one of the most interesting classes I've taken. Professor sticks to his syllabus, no late work, closes discussions. Recommend!!

If you learn better in person don't take this class! He's nice but assigns SOO much reading, I'd spend days/hours reading because it's the only way to learn. Quiz questions were random, I studied what appeared to important/relevant across multiple chapters (no study guide) but he'd ask about random facts you can't even remember reading.

Little instruction and lots of reading. Many assignments-weekly essays plus quizzes plus papers plus group projects plus a midterm and a final. It's too much! Much more work than my other classes and he does not teach much, just occasional five minute videos. Otherwise you are on your own with lots of work.

Class is super organized online. He posts short lectures and gives you some reading. Textbook reading is super helpful and class is very interesting. 1 discussion post a week, 3 total quizzes, 1 group project, 1 research paper and a final. Really doable class as long as you stay up to date on reading! This was also the 8 week course.

One discussion post + 2 replies per week. A quiz about every month. One midterm and one final exam. A couple of big extra credit opportunities. One group project, and one lone assignment. Very easy class with fun discussions and prompts. The textbook is really interesting. I highly recommend the class.

Professor Huff's class was totally do-able. Quizzes and exams are based on the textbook and his modules which are clearly laid out. Watch the videos he posts they help you understand the material better. Very nice class and professor. Not gonna stress you out if you do the work.

I highly recommend Prof Huff! He gives good feedback and chimes in on the discussion posts which makes it feel more personal. The concepts in this course are very interesting and the courseloud is very manageable. My only complaint is that he gave a group project but other than that I really enjoyed Huff's way of teaching this online course!

Replied to emails or messages sent on Canvas really quickly. Each rubric is mapped out well and he gives back really good feedback. Especially because psy 145 aka human dev goes through like all stages of life, it can be a lot of reading and stuff but overall a good class. would recommend prof huff :)
I have taken three different Psychology classes at SBCC with Prof Huff and each is awesome. Everything is clearly laid out. No confusion on what you need to do to get an A.
Hands off professor. Just do the weekly textbook readings and it's a walk in the park.

Took this class online which made talking about sexual topics a lot more comfortable. Just had a discussion post per week and then a quiz every few weeks. Lightwork if you stay on top of it, but deducts points on late work so don't fall behind. Talked about interesting topics and quizzes/tests weren't too hard.
Professor Huff is just the best. Take him! So sweet and nice and the class isn't hard if you just do the work (which is really interesting!!)

Professor Huff is pretty simple and straight forward. I took 3 of his classes - 100, 110, and 145. They all have weekly discussions & quizzes (besides 110), exams, and research papers. All pretty simple if you read the books.
The professor makes a tough course manageable. You'll need the book but the online course is setup well and clear instructions. Recommended!

I really liked taking Human Sexuality online. I felt more comfortable discussing some of the topics online than I would have sitting in a classroom with other people. I would recommend him, especially if you are shy like me.
Horrible teacher. Unfortunate because the class has so much potential, the teacher is just too lazy.

Professor Huff wants everyone to succeed. This class has clear expectations and a reasonable amount of work. He's very engaged and grades work super quick, especially for an online class. Material is interesting and relevant. Weekly quizzes and discussions as well as 4 exams that are decently long. One final paper at the end.

I am taking his Online class for summer session 1. I previously failed with professor Stanley but Michael is an amazing teacher. You do read the text book and he gives some resources online for you to look over. There are exams and weekly quizzes online. There is also 1 group project and a final research paper. Take Him he's great and easy going

lots of reading but it's super interesting. I took the class online and had no trouble getting everything done. there are a few discussion posts, a group project, and a research paper, but also lots of easy quizzes
Pretty easy class as long as you do all the readings.
Great professor. Fair grader. Offers a lot of extra credit opportunities. Weekly quizzes and a big research paper due at the end of the semester which i recommend starting as early as possible.

Professor Huff was amazing! I took his PSY140 class as a late start online class. The workload was heavy but Micheal made it a blast. He's such a nice professor and any questions you might have, he has a quick response and always wants to do whatever he can to help you succeed!

I like this class to be honest I think its really easy, the one thing that I say wasn't good is that he doesn't really respond to emails and when he does hes a little snappy and gives attitude. I also have a learning disability so I would have to email him to ask certain questions consistently about the assignments and he still was a little rude.
SBCC is lucky to have Professor Huk! He clearly has a passion for reading and writing, which can be contagious for students!

Great teacher. Helped me get an A in the class. a lot of writing and only 2 quizzes in the class. If you understand how to format an essay and do everything to the best of your ability you will get an A or B.

Prof. Huk's class is very easy and interesting. It mainly consists of discussions and essays. You have multiple weeks to turn in your essays. There is a lot of peer review in this class. Only 2 essays, 2 tests, and the final research paper. The final paper can be hard if you fall behind so start early and stay on top of it. Overall great class!

Most work consisted of discussion forums and essays. I absolutely loved all essay prompts, as they were interesting and fun to research and write about. As long as you get your work done on time you'll receive a reasonable grade. Overall I feel that this class has improved my writing, and I couldn't recommend Peter Huk for English 110 more!

Took him for Summer Session II. Mainly relies on peer review which I liked to an extent. He did not provide feedback after he gave you back your paper and only if you ask for it. Clear expectations for grading which I appreciated. Overall an easy class.

Very easy class. He will actually help you with your work. On our essays and discussions he would let us freely discuss the topic. We had one big essay that was 7 pages everything else was short and easy. I learned more from him than I did from any of my high school english teachers.

Suuuuper easy class. Pretty much only 3 assignments for the entire quarter plus a short reflection. 1 optional meeting per week for asking questions and peer editing. You can email him your assignment and ask for feedback, but he's an easy grader anyways. (I think the average was an A-)

Huk is a great prof that responds to emails super fast and very nice. I took online 110 and it was super easy. One discussion a week and a paper every 3 weeks. he will assign rough drafts and outlines so you don't write it all the next day. He is super easy on grading.

I took his online class over the summer. English 110. His assignments were ridiculously easy and as long as you do the work, there is no way you can fail. He responds to all you questions and is super nice !

Worst professor ever. First, he didn't provide add code even though he knows how crowded classes gets and he is never willing to help students to get into his class. He thinks he is a celebrity at school. While he is just a piece of human being that shouldn't be teaching at school such as SBCC. I wouldn't take his class again never not even 4 free

Took course online. LOTS of reading with participation each week. You wont pass if you don't read and participate. 4 essays inc.7-10p research paper. If you are looking to enhance your writing skills this might not be the right class. It is solely student participation and critique, not once did I receive any feed back from the professor.

Great professor, gives great feedback, really pushes you to learn and conduct research. I procrastinated a bit but in the end, because I did my best to interact in discussions and the weekly readings, they helped me keep a B. That and scoring well on our papers. The final can NOT be procrastinated with, AT ALL -advice.

I wish I could take this class again as it was so fun. He uses Moodle effectively and sets up a nice "classroom" vibe, even though the class is online. His reading assignments are themed, which helps with critical thinking and essay topics. This is a very heavy reading and writing class. Keep up or you'll get really behind.

Grading consisted of 4 essays (third one based on book "Born to Run" and the last was an 8 page research paper) and weekly discussions. Those were very simple and not a problem at all. The essays were due in steps which made it much more easy to write and complete them. The grading on the essays seemed lenient to me. All in all, really good class!

Great Professor! Took his online course and there was only 4 essays and I one reflection essay due. This class is very manageable and he lefts you retake the ENG 120 test if you did not pass the first time. Super nice guy! Highly recommend!

There are only 4 assignments due (three 4 page essays and 1 research paper 8+ pages). Lectures are pretty boring and many of the classes will be spent watching videosmovies. He is somewhat a tough grader but gets easier throughout the term. But overall not much work to be done (but do good essays)

Lots of attention given to specific essay texts and very thorough instructions on writing papers. All of the essays built on one another; it made perfect sense and allowed me to want to learn and to practice my writing.

Really nice guy! sometimes it felt though as if classes consisted mainly of watching videos. However, the 4 essays are all manageable, as well as the research paper. He provides you with enough input to revise your work successfully. Interesting class, especially when taken during the summer.
Took this class online and it was very easy and simple. Keep up on the forum posts, and spend time making good essays and you'll get a good grade.

He is an ok teacher. The class was not hard but he was not always very clear with directions. His class was extremely boring and he talks in monotone. I did not learn from the actual class time, but rather by going to the writing center. There are four essays, plus a research paper and he allows you to revise an essay for a better grade.

Peter is pretty monotone which kind of puts you to sleep but he is a witty guy and comes up with some funny jokes. He grades honestly so do a good job on essays and you'll pass. The class consists of many video clips and 4 essays. I enjoyed being in his class.

Professor Huk is awesome. I didn't realize it at the time, but he's extremely easy. I took him for Eng 110 and missed him when I took Eng 111 with a different professor. Take him if you get the chance! Class can be boring with useless video clips, but Huk grades easy on the essays which makes up for it.

I took a long evening class with Prof Huk. He showed a lot of videos to fill the time but somehow tied them to whatever we were discussing. The paper's were not too difficult and you have opportunities to revise them. We also had peer reviewing which was helpful. There wasn't too much reading. Anyway, overall it was a good class I recommend it!
He is overall funny, he will make you want to come to class. he has a lot of group activities which makes the class participate and connect. he understands all levels of learning.

Took this online. As long as you keep up and do the assignments and participate in the forums you'll do fine. Give yourself plenty of time to do the papers! The research paper is pretty intense but you have almost an entire month to do it. The only negative thing was the peer reviews, they get redundant, yet I can see the purpose. Good guy!
hottie for sure, relevant topic discussions, uses various forms of media to keep students interested, very worth your while
I took Eng 110 online. The class was very easy to follow, it was clear what he expected. He was not overwhelming. If you do your work it's easy to get a good grade.
Professor Huk was very helpful and chose interesting material. A lot of exposure to different styles of writing and subject matter. Highly recommended for those wishing to explore English as a major.
I took Prof. Huk's online Eng 110 class and it was a great experience. He is very helpful and didn't overload us with papers/work. Learned alot- take him if you can.

She's not a terrible professor. However, I would take her in person rather than online. The only learning material is the textbook, no lectures. She gives no feedback when grading assignments, so you won't really know what you did wrong on an assignment if you didn't get 100%. Be sure to read her exam questions thoroughly!

Makes you feel bad if you're straight or white. I learned about how privileged I am and how awful I am to others. It's a DEI class that doesn't talk at all about leadership. Makes us fill out a survey called " CISGENDER MALE PRIVILEGE CHECKLIST" and "HETEROSEXUAL PRIVILEGE CHECKLIST." Excuse me, but what does that have to do with Leadership, AWFUL!

She was great, and the class was pretty easy. I took her online class, which was very manageable. There is one presentation required, but she was very understanding and helped people be less nervous. She is also easy to reach, replies very quickly.

I took this class during the Summer B section. It was very fast passed. She wrote the text book and provides a free on line version in the class. She gives helpful tips each week. The tests and quizzes were multiple choice, There is a presentation (3-5 minutes) on a peer reviewed article. She provides a clear rubric to follow. Great professor.

Amazing class! Based on textbook readings only, so if that's not your thing pick an in-person class. The textbook is written by her and very easy to understand. I highly recommend the class and her as a professor!!

I've had very tough courses, like anatomy, etc., and I did WELL, but this extremely easy content class doesn't make me want to continue. this instructor can stereotype you as a procrastinator when you contact her for help. She gives you quizzes with very captious questions to complete in 5 minutes! Ridiculous.

I would NOT recommend this teacher for her ability to use canvas. She doesn't unlock modules until Thursday, and they are due on Sunday. She also directs you to use required sources that are broken, and refuses to change her ways. She is the worst most difficult professor I have had thus far, even if the class is incredibly easy in itself.

Very organized, inclusive and smart, Dr. H creates a positive vibe for students of all backgrounds and abilities. Everyone I have told to take her classes agrees that she is the best. She will have you thinking deeply about the way you show up in the world. I feel like I see things differently and am a better communicator because of her classes.

Very flexible and has a clear structure for the class. The final project was very simple and graded leniently which was helpful as I don't like presentations. She made everyone feel confortable while being online.

I took this class because I needed the credit. I actually learned something and enjoyed it. I think this class helped me become more self-aware, and a well-rounded person. Professor Carrie was very understanding, helpful, and accepting. You do have to do a presentation over Zoom, but it's only a few minutes.

Prof. Hutchinson is super nice and so flexible. One discussion and a quiz every week. There are 2 tests through the semester that are easy. At the end of the semester, there is a presentation that goes with another project from earlier in the semester. Textbook is required, she wrote it herself! Overall, Carrie is great and I would recommend her!

The material mostly felt like a no-brainer. I've never rolled my eyes so hard at a textbook (written by her.) The tests are ridiculous, 1 minute per question and at times felt purposely worded to be confusing. The presentations are not hard but you feel ridiculous doing them. Lots of discussion post replies to create a fake camaraderie.
She is a great professor and it is noticeable that she truly cares about her students and their education. I would recommend!

Honestly, this class is better than therapy. Super easy class and super eye opening. The textbook is written by her and is super insightful and not boring like most other textbooks. She really cares about communication and you can tell. I took the class in the summer, so grading may be different, but she grades quite quickly as well.

She has a great textbook and is really nice in person. Her online format was lengthy. anytime you would email a question she responds with "did you read the instructions" or "did you read the syllabus" and the information would be somewhere in her wall of instructions on one of her assignments or announcements. Read the textbook you'll get an A.
Prof. Hutchinson wants you to succeed. Try hard, turn in your work, and email her with any questions, comments, or concerns. She will give you the support if you give the effort.

The class was a bait and switch in that it has nothing to do with the topic I signed up for. It's a DEI class and has nothing to do with leadership. I don't know how much the teacher has to do with the work, but there was no chance to be authentic in the class, you just have to regurgitate the status quo. I did not learn anything new and that's bad

I absolutely loved this class! Professor Hutchinson is super sweet and very organized. I learned so much from her textbook. You get quizzed on each chapter but quizzes will seem easy as long as you do the reading. You have one public speaking project in front of a few people but she's very understanding of how public speaking can be nerve wrecking.

Dr. H is truly a gem. Wow. The class is organized so well, and she is just a super inspiring person. She's the reason I became a Communications major. Don't miss a chance to take any of her classes. Each one is different and she will have you thinking about things that you take for granted all around.

Took COMM 121 online during the summer and the class was very well structured with clear assignment deadlines. The textbook is written by her and reads as if it were a live lecture making it an easy read. Easy quizzes and exams if you read the book. A few major projects; as long as you don't fall behind and complete the work you'll get full marks

This was a great class. The Professor was great, if I ever need to take another comm class I will for sure take it with her. The reading is interesting. There is a short online quiz, 5 minutes and its 5 questions. Im the kinda guy where I like to read the question a few times and then go through and read the answers, but with a short quiz you can't

She's very nice and very clear about what she expects from you. All the subjects you are tested on are covered in the class. She adjusted her class very well during this tough year and I really enjoyed taking it!

She seriously knows her stuff and is ALWAYS there to help. Make sure to read her textbook bc the answers are clearly there. You're making a mistake if you chose a different prof. for the same course. She even gave a class wide project extension during the election to try to reduce stress. Loved the class, loved her teaching style, loved the grade.

Took her summer 1 comm 122 class. You have a final presentation on zoom and have the whole semester to prepare. Quizzes and test every week along with a discussion post. Tests were hard, but if you read the textbook, you'll pass. She's awesome and class is very interesting and informative.

Carrie is very nice and understanding. Her class is very easy to pass. I was nervous for the presentation that had to be done over a research study in front of a couple peers through a google cam but it was honestly really easy. I enjoyed this class and a lot of the topics discussed are very useful to everyday life.

Hands down one of the best classes I've ever taken at SBCC. And one of the best teachers I've ever had. The book is very useful, clear lectures, easy projects, easy tests, she is caring and available outside of class as well. If you do your part, it will be an easy A. Learned about communication more than I expected.

Much to like about class. Very interesting, fun, informative self-assessment assignments and discussions. Interesting reading from a very well-written textbook that gives real-life examples. Tests tricky, make one think. This class somehow easily brought together classmates in a very supportive, encouraging environment. Great Prof. C. Happy took.

I took her online Comm 121 and we did use a textbook. We had weekly quizzes, 4 online exams and two projects. One we had to show up in class to present the other was online. I totally recommend her class she is amazing and it was an easy A if you read the book.

This class was extremely easy. Do the assignments and you're guaranteed an A. I ended the class with a 96% receiving an A on every assignment and test. There was 2 major projects and 3 exams and then a quiz for every chapter and 4 discussion assignments. That's it. I believe she wrote the textbook, but for once it's a really good read.

Carrie is a great teacher for an online class. She answers really quickly and is really helpful. She is the literal opposite of a tough grader, as the exams and the presentations are so easy to get an A in. Would recommend.

Dr. Elizabeth Imhof is an amazing professor caring, kind, flexible, and truly wise. She teaches with love and positive energy. I joined her class by accident on day one and stayed because I loved her passion. I learned so much about AI and will really miss this class. Highly recommend!

We touched resources like research database and primary resources from lectures led by great historians, philosophers, phycologist, economist, and political scientist. Dr. Imhof is very passionate about her students and class. She makes herself very accessible for student to ask for help and asks students to come see her during office hours .
loved this class. this class was refreshing and was way more interesting than all of my classes this semester.
She is very passionate about wanting her students to learn important skills and making the lectures fun for her students. Would highly recommend and she is easy to work with, very understanding.
Great professor!!
So sweet! Very optimistic and funny :) not too much work but some assignments are last minute so be aware of that!
She cares a lot about her students. She brings in amazing guest speakers. Great person and teacher all around. Fair, understanding. Just wants you to care. Amazing lectures.

Great professor. She's caring, patient, and wants her students to succeed. She explains the material clearly and never makes you feel dumb for asking questions. Her feedback is helpful & she puts real effort into the class. If you show up and try, she supports you all the way. Highly recommend. Easy A if you go to class and take notes.
teacher seemed nice, but the class was super uninteresting and had little to do with social sciences. it's basically all about ai/chatgpt. only take it if that's what you actually want to learn.
Such a great class! This was my first class at SBCC and I'm glad that it was this course. She is such a inspirational teacher and truly cares for her students.

My favorite class. The work was interesting and we studied things that mattered like race conflict in America. The class can be challenging but she helps you and is the best. most dedicated teacher I have ever had. I'm not usually an A student but she inspired me to work hard and I earned an A. I cant believe that i looked forward to class.
Very respectful and grades fairly. SHE IS AWESOME

I took her for HIST 103 (online) and it was an easy A. All of your work will be completed through an online eText Lab, in which you will complete quizzes and misc material. Just complete all the work by each due date and you will breeze by.

I started with Dr. Imhof and this class online. She was so helpful when I struggled. I did not think I liked history or was any good at it. I took it online because I thought it would be easy. I struggled a lot and she helped me. Even online. I liked it so much I took her classes on campus. Online she is great, in person amazing!

This class is amazing. Dr. Imhof conducts it like a seminar. There is a lot of discussing in class. She gets everyone to participate but not in a mean way. She cares about what you think and wants to hear your opinions. There is a lot of work, but if you do it she will help you do really well. This is the most interesting class I have ever h

I loved this class! At the end we used what we learned during the semester to work on our own social justice projects. It was so fun to actually use what we learned in class on something that matters. I think people who wrote the class was disorganized at the end wrote their comments too soon. If you wait until the end of the class it all works

Really passionate about her students and would do anything to help them whenever necessary. Easy grader as long as you tried - you'll get an A. Easy class as long as you participate and show up. Last half of the class is really unorganized and spent a lot of time doing nothing when you could have been working on your research / group project

There are no test in this class only 2-3 writing assignments each week. Overall very easy, and if you are undecided about your major then definitely take the class. Its a combination of all the social sciences and there are a lot of guest lectures. Lectures are usually really interesting. Imhof really cares about her students.

Great teacher. She's really nice and VERY understanding. The class was a little unorganized at times but it is a great class overall. Do all the work and it's an easy A. Perfect class to get a bit of knowledge from each of the social science classes.

137 was a really great class I learned so much very enlightening. Grades are never final she will let you rewrite an exam to get a 75. She is extremely understanding and the nicest teacher ever. She gives exam questions out ahead of time. There is a lot of reading but I never read it and still got a B in the class just go to all her lectures.
Knowledgeable, Prefessional, and fair to all students.

This is one of those mind blowing classes that college is supposed to be all about. Come with an open mind and be willing to learn and you will have your world view shift in a life changing way! Hands down the best class at SBCC! You do have to read a lot but she is clear about what she expects and its not hard to get an A if you do your work.

This class is soooo interesting. She is a fantastic storyteller and holds really interesting and unique discussions. She says that everyone in class is a historian and she respects us all. I can tell how much she loves teaching and how much she cares about her students. There is a good amount of reading and writing but she will help you.

I can't believe Dr. Imhof is not the highest rated professor on this website. She is the most dedicated professor I have ever had. She created this class because she believes it is what every student needs and she is right! I have never felt so inspired from a class before. I actually looked forward to coming to class.
Easy class, no tests no midterm, she writes exactly what she expects and is a fairly easy grader. Would take again.

I have never had a class like this before. I wish I had taken it when I first began at SBCC. Dr. Imhof is the most dedicated professor I have ever had. You can tell she loves teaching and really cares about all of her students. She also brought in many other professors for guest lectures that were so interesting. Class was also fun!
Social science 101 is very useful. You will not regret if you take it. Elizabeth respects all the students.

This is my second class with her and its my favorite! I dont want to tell people she is easy, because she is not. However, she is the most dedicated and helpful teacher I have ever had at CC. If you do your work and ask her for help when you need it, it is easy to do well. The class is so interesting and I am learning so much.

I'm loving this class! She only lectures for part of class and then we get into a big circle that she calls a workshop. We talk about why the history matters to us and what was really going on in the past! So interesting! Its a Hybrid and I wish we had more class time. Can't believe I'm saying that. The computer program is OK too.
This was my second class with her. She really helped me through the semester and all of the assignments worked together to help me on my final paper.

He is super understanding and nice. You only have about 2 to 3 assignments a month and its just a rough draft/essay, and a short reflection on it. This class was pretty easy because you didn't have a ton of assignments so I did a better job on the essays because I would work on it a little every day.

There is only about 2 to 3 assignments a month and its just a rough draft essay, and a short reflection on it. This class was pretty easy because you didn't have a ton of assignments so I did a better job on the essays because I would work on it a little every day. He is really nice and understanding and grades easy.

Overall great teacher who cares about his students. He never provided substantial feedback on essays but grades very generously. Easy class to get an A in if you put in the work. 6 essays total but he will give extensions if the class requests. He is a very giving and kind teacher.

There were a total of 6 essays we needed to write: 5 regular and 1 final. His grading wasn't tough, but he also did not leave much commentary whenever he docked pts. There is a lot of work, but just stay on top of due dates. He formed groups in class so the students could give each other feedback within those small groups, which was helpful.

As long as you put in some effort and apply what you learned in Eng110 you will be more than fine! There are 6 essay's and the last one is a research project. You're able to write more creatively and poetically. There are also workshops, discussions, and journals. Pretty simple class, he is slow on the grading but a somewhat easy grader.

This class was super easy. Only 4-5 essays the entire semester and very minimal work. He's a very caring guy and a fair grader who always highlights the good parts as well as the needs-more-work areas of your work. Super easy course.

Overall I had a great experience taking his class. There were about 6 essays throughout the semester, but the assignments were pretty clear. If at anytime I did not understand something I would reach out to him and he was very helpful. The feedback he gave would highlight your strengths, but he would also provide constructive feedback.

Really nice professor! He has clear expectations and seems to care about his students. However, don't expect a lot of feedback. The quizzes are not "impossible," they are actually super easy if you attend class. Just do every assignment, ask questions, and you'll definitely get an A.
he is an amazing professor who really cares about his students, easy grader, lots of essays but they are all interesting.

I love him! This is the second class I am taking with him. He gives WAY less homework than the other 110 teachers. He is pretty chill about everything and is open for students' input. He adjusts deadlines to the class and you can always talk to him if you have a problem. He is one of the best professors I've had at CC. 10/10 recommend!

I should have taken a different prof. The reviews on Johnston are really misleading. If you are taking english in hope of actually learning anything, skip this class. Pop quizzes are nearly impossible. Lazy grader, gives no feedback or reason just gives you a letter grade. No structure for "lectures" and arrogant guy.

One of the best professors in the English department. Very funny and kind professor, who doesn't grade your papers as harsh, yet gives solid feedback. You will write about 6 essays in total, and have a easy research presentation (basically a class powerpoint), along with some random quizzes early in the semester. Easy A/B depending on your effort
Super mellow. We wrote like 5 essays I think but, it's an English class. Super mellow guy and a chill grader. Highly recommend. There's a 8 page research paper and the in class final is on a play.
Chris is amazing!!!! Be ready to write essays pretty much back to back all semester, but he is an easy grader and a great guy! Highly recommend

Although there are quite a bit of papers and a 10 min research presentation, if you follow the guidelines given you will get an A. He is the easiest grader on campus. I hardly put in any effort and still got 95/100 on every essay. I only did my presentation for 5 min and got 100%. Take this guy if you can, he's the best grader!!

Ok so here's the truth. 6 papers and a 10-15 minute presentation. One of the essays, being the one you present, is a research paper on 8-10 pages. You have to find (5) primary sources (you can't use one you read in class), and 10 secondary sources. You have(??) to have a genuine interest for your author of choice. Worth 15 %. Like the other 5.

Summer Online English 111 with Professor Johnston was a wonderful class! The assigned essays, readings, and assignments were straightforward, fun and interesting. His class made me love and appreciate literature even more than before I took it. Hes a fair grader and the workload isnt bad at all. Youll love English and this class! Take Him!!!

Hands down, the best English teacher I have ever had. He encourages us to actually think about what we are writing and to look more in depth at what we are analyzing. The lectures are always eye-opening to new ideas that you may have not notices when you first did the readings. I definitely feel like my writing has greatly improved because of him.

Love this teacher, he's really there for the students. Participate in class discussions, he loves when people contribute ideas. You are graded by the english department on 3 of the 4 essays you write in that class along with an in class essay. Super easy, I think he told us that around 85% of students pass. Super chill guy, always willing to help.

My class was a hybrid, so we would show up for one class and then do homework online. This format was really helpful for me, as he would always respond to my emails on the same day. The class is also based off of a few essays and an in class one, which he gives you plenty of time to complete. Show up, write, and pass the course. Easy A .

Chris is one of the best English teachers I have ever had. He is extremely laid back and always willing to help his students (make sure to ask questions). I recommend this class to anyone who knows the basics of writing an essay. His essay topics are really interesting and its fun when the whole class joins in on the discussions.

I have been taking Chris for both ENG 100 and ENG 110 and he is such a great teacher. The papers are on interesting topics, therefore it is fun to write the papers. He is also there for help if you need to check on your work. Take this class!
Interesting topics, fun class, cool teacher. Outside of readings, several essays and a presentation there is no homework. Don't need to go to class to succeed.
Participate and try on your papers, and you will pass without a problem. Expect lots of essays!!

Great guy but kind of a boring class. Assigns a bunch of essays but you have enough time to complete them. Plus he's an easy grader. Definitely buy the books because you use them in class and for the final.

Took class/web english 100 very easy! Do all the assignments on there & on time and you will be able to turn in your portfolio. Essays are assigned right after another but it is for him to give feedback on each essay. He gives clear feedback, and looks at each essay at least twice! Wish he was teaching 110 next semester.

Took online and class was very easy but educational of course! Just respond to every assignment forum and do your essays ! and you are sure to receive an A!!!!! The only reason I received a C :( is because I did not do one 100 point essay and other discussion forums! Stupid me ..... Quick to respond to emails and most willing to help you out !!!!

Very straightforward, yet exciting class. I found the topics for essays to be very relevant and interesting. I like how the class ran in a basic routine each week. Read Respond Write Rough Draft Get Feedback Write Final Draft.
Chris is an amazing teacher and he made the class fun. I would really recommend to take Chris for your english class.
Really kind and understanding teacher. Put a decent amount of effort in the papers and you will easily end up with a B or higher. English 120 is unbelievably easy so don't stress about it at all.
Cool lady. Not a hard class if you attend. Never dreaded it You will learn a lot about yourself. All assignments can be revised. Ecryone welcome.
Accepts late work, super sweet, easy and informative!
She's very kind and cares about what she teaches. Wasn't super lecture heavy we did a lot of in class discussions, videos, and activities which made class interesting and fun. Definitely recommend.

interesting lectures. always prepared. lots of writing. no busy work. great for returning students gaining pre recs for grad schools. ask her for a letter of rec. she'll get you in. walks the walk. knows the content backwards and forwards. q and a time is the best. take her every chance you get. she's a role model. an unflinchingly kind woman.

Professor Johnston really cares about her students, listens to them, and is able to create a safe environment that encourages a lot of different perspectives. Each person was treated with respect and openness. I learned so much about children. It was amazing! I highly recommend her and her class Pysch 140..

This is the 2nd class I have taken with Laurel, I want to share that she has been one of the best, most understanding and real professors I know. She truly cares about every student and wants everyone to do their best. I really enjoyed all the group discussions and moments for collaboration in class, it really is a safe space for every student!!

Professor Laurel Johnston's Personal Development class was an excellent experience. She creates an engaging, supportive environment that encourages personal growth. Her teaching style blends real-world insights with humor and compassion. This class helped me develop valuable life skills, and I highly recommend it to anyone who is interested in PSY.

Professor Laurel Johnston classes are engaging, insightful, and relevant to real-world issues. She combines humor, compassion, and deep knowledge in every lesson. I took her Personal Development course as well because I love her teaching style and her ability to connect with students, making her an unforgettable and inspiring professor.

Really glad I've taken multiple of her classes and this one was no different to the great content she presented and her energy in every class. Very available and always make me smile whenever I walked into her class, I hope she keep teaching for many years to come.

Johnston does not read power points. She comes prepared, starts class on time and doesn't do fluff. Her knowledge of psy is very deep and she can explain anything. She lectures heavily. Makes a point to bring in what she calls leading edge research. This is an easy class if you attend. Highly recommend for working people. Very efficient.

Only take this class if you're looking for an easy A NOT if you want to learn anything. We barely had any assignments throughout the semester and attendance wasn't mandatory. Laurel is a very kind and knowledgeable professor, but her class was a little lacking.

combat veteran here. Dr. Johnston sent an email to class thanking all of us vets for our service. Don't understand criticism reaching her. Not my exp, spent many office hours with her so far this term. She will wait until she is sure you understand. Very intense lecturer, maybe do some group work. But I also appreciate she doesn't waste our time.

As others stated before she cancels class a lot on the same day a few hours before class, gets behind in lectures and skips topics. She does not have office hours, won't respond to emails, you need to text her, but won't get back to you. If you are curious about psychology, or is your major and want to learn take someone else. Anyone can get an A

She is by far the kindest most understanding caring teacher I have ever had throughout my entire life. She understands that students can be taking harder classes and can be overwhelmed so she doesn't really assign homework and is super easygoing about assignments. We did a lot of in-class work and watched physiological movies relating to topics
Very sweet lady who cares for her students and their needs. She is very educated in the field of Psychology and has alot of interesting takes to bring to the table. I would take this class again!

Take this class if you want an EASY A and not if you actually want to learn. She cancels class VERY often with poor excuses. If there is class it mainly consists of watching films and lectures, but are often left out early. Only 4 things in the grade book (1 group assignment, 2 take home tests, and final) all of which she gives you the answers.

Her class is the easiest in-person class within SBCC. First, there is no regular homework at all. There are only three test grades.For the tests, you will be given a sheet of paper with the questions on it, and you're allowed to work on them off campus. Surprisingly, she also encouraged the use of chat gpt. That's why she is a popular professor.
Such an easy professor, very laid back and super nice. Anyone can get an A in this class.

Her class was completely stress free making me actually want to learn. As a nursing major this was ideal: easy A and attendance isn't manditory. I took 100 and 145 with her and the midterms came with answer keys or they were self graded. Highly recommend.

this class is a joke so if u want to learn anything or are a psych major take someone else. Canceled class every week, no hw, "self graded" midterm/final, honestly I don't know how she is still employed as a professor, nice woman tho

This class will not stress you out. The teacher is nice and has comprehensible lectures. Most learning is going to be done through lectures and there are very few assignments. The final was also tame with unlimited retakes. Only complaint is that she's pretty much inaccessible outside of class.
