If you need and easy A, this is your class. Professor cancelled class every week, never made a canvas page, and sent midterms and finals with answer keys and said grade the final yourself and input your own score. Ridiculously easy, not a real teacher nor class.

Do not take this class if you are a Psy major. With that begin said TAKE THIS CLASS, so easy only had 1 quiz that we went over in class before turning it in, no midterm. I've been to class maybe half the times and I have a 100%. She's a very nice and understanding teacher as well, fun class.

This was an easy class, that said if you do go through the material you can learn a lot by yourself, but nothing is tested hard. So in a way it makes it enjoyable, I guess. But don't expect to learn a lot. Psych majors should probably take another section unless you badly want that A grade.
Easiest class I've ever taken. Very caring and sweet lady but you don't learn much. She is very understanding.

I took this class for psych 100 and it was extremely easy. If your not looking to go forward with psychology then I would definitely recommend taking but if your trying to learn things then I would probably choose a different instructor. There were very few assignments and all including exams were on canvas with unlimited time and attempts.

This class was enjoyable but I wish the teacher was more present. We only have 4 in-person classes and the rest of the time we had minimum work online. I think there were less than 5 assignments in the grade book. I wish I had learned more but I took this class for credit and it wasn't one of my major classes.

If you're looking for an easy A and not learn much, take this class! She assigned 1 quiz, 1 midterm, and a final exam for the whole semester. This was an in person course, but we only met up a handful of times because she kept making up excuses to cancel class. Although the professor was nice and easy going, I was highly disappointed in this class.

This class is very easy. She assigned like 15 assignments the whole semester. However, she only grades a few of them. 1 whole module was locked even though she has a policy of excepting all assignments up until the end of the semester. Felt like she ignored some of my emails. Easy class just didn't learn much.

Roughly three super easy assignments each month. I had taken AP in high school so this class and the two tests were a breeze. She grades super easy and assigns hardly anything. Need and easy A? Take this class!

Such an involved, funny, and exciting professor, his personality makes the class extremely interesting and engaging. You need to attend class for participation credit + answer questions in class. There are four tests that come up fast, but there are great quizlets with the exact material. Lots of extra credit available!

Sean is so kind and very energetic! His lectures are fast paced but everything is online to look back on. He posts "most missed questions" before every test which is super helpful. I would recommend studying very well before the tests, they can be hard but there's only 3. HE IS THE BEST THO!! take him.

Most condescending and rude person ever. Always make me feel stupid when I ask a question or when he looks at my assignments even if they are right. Makes fun of his students and always has something snarky to say to you.

Sean is the best professor I've ever had! He's super super smart and knowledgable about the subject matter and it's obvious that he not only loves the content but also genuinely loves teaching and his students. I'm not a super science person but his enthusiasm is infectious and it makes the class really enjoyable. I can't recommend him enough!
He's a good teacher that wants his students to do well. Coming to class is a huge part of the grade. Pretty fun class, he makes the stuff easy to remember, didn't have to study all that much.

Had him for ERTH 106 and 104. Dr. Kelly is the life of the Planetary side of the Earth and Planetary Sciences at SBCC. His lectures are great and you can feel the passion and enthusiasm he has for his students. Dr. Kelly is one of the few at SBCC who make it a special place. Commit to your studies/passion and you can go far under his instruction!

Sean is really passionate about what he teaches which is nice, but that also means he expects everyone to be as passionate as him. The lab was off campus which I didn't know about before registering. I also completed everything in class, missed one assignment and it tanked my grade from a 97 to an 84. We also had a tests on stuff he didn't cover.

Hello! I signed up for Rate my Professors just so I could say that Sean Kelly is THE BEST professor/teacher I've ever had. I love astronomy, but even students who weren't passionate like I was loved him and his teaching style. He is so excited and loving. You can tell that teaching is his calling. He is an angel who makes students love learning. ⭐️

One of the best professors I've had. Sean is incredibly knowledgeable and engaging, often cracking funny jokes to keep the class entertained. The lectures are worth attending, and Unstuck study is a great study tool to supplement the course material.
Very fun and cares about the success of his students! There's a lot of material so be sure to go to his review sessions before exams. Plenty of extra credit opportunities to take advantage of.
Probably one of the best teachers I've ever had, he was super funny, very very smart and you can tell he loves his job and school and just life in general type shi

Sean is one of my favorite people in the world. I took a lot of his classes and worked as his tutor, and he genuinely inspires me to be a better person. He not only wants you to succeed in this class, but he wants you to succeed in life. Don't take this class if you just need to fulfill a science requirement and think this will be an easy A though.

Sean is such a great teacher, he really cares about his students. He puts in a lot of time outside of his classes to his work as well, such as the astronomy club. He offers extra credit and always has tutoring available. Tests are long but they shouldn't be difficult if you take notes in the lectures and study outside of class.

Sean is an amaxing professor, he has such an upbeat energy that makes you want to listen to what he is saying. He is very passionate about his sudents and the topic of astronomy, and offers a variety of before test study sessions, tutor hours and extra activites. This class however is extremely hard and the tests are a huge portion of your grade.

If you take Astronomy 101/102, you HAVE TO take it with Sean. He is incredibly knowledgeable and thorough with lectures and will crack jokes that are actually funny constantly. He tries very hard to get to know you and your needs on a personal level. He gives lots of extra credit opportunities, take advantage of that.
The lab was super fun. Love Sean, he explains everything great, and I promise you won't look at the sky the same again after he shows you what you're looking at.

Sean is a really great professor and even greater person. I must admit you will have to spend a bit of time studying for this class as the concepts can be hard to memorize with multiple question exams but he makes learning it fun. I also took 102 and would sort of recommend it, helps reinforce concepts for 101 but a lot of work for a 1 unit course.

You are lucky if you get Sean as your astronomy professor. He absolutely loves astronomy, and his passion is shown in his lectures and the lab. He is funny af, and no class is boring with him. He is not the best grader, and sometimes lectures can be a little confusing, but if you're willing to put up with a lil bad stuff he is more than worth it

Kelly is a good teacher but this class is definitely not as chill as he makes it up to be in the beginning of the semester. He claimed that there'd be no exams, hw, and just one-two quizzes which is not true, after week 4 there'll be quizzes and hw every week. Kelly is not the fastest at responding emails or uploading the grades on canvas.

I loved taking Sean's astronomy class. He clearly has a passion for topic and he goes on and on about cool space facts. His lectures can be a lil boring at times and he rushes through material so taking notes in his class can be a little difficult. He relies on the textbook to teach a lot of the material, and lectures tend to be supplemental.

If Bill Nye the Science Guy was a Professor Sean would be it. Sean loves to share his knowledge about astronomy while also keeping class fun for all. Sean hyped up an exam for us, such a cool guy. This man can probably make rocket science seem like a fun subject to learn. I will 100% be taking more courses with him in the future.

As long as you show up and used the study guide this class is awesome. Beware it is very easy to fall behind so if you're going to miss classes don't take this one. Dr.Kelly was great though, he always shows up ready to teach and brings good energy.
Sean is great! so enthusiastic and by far my favorite professor I have ever had. One of the best and cant recommend this class more!
one of the best prof at sbcc

I love having Sean Kelly as a Professor. I would 100% take his class again. He always put a smile on everyone's faces with his positive attitude. You can definitely tell he is passionate and loves his work and science.

Astronomy 102 was such a fun class! Getting to use the planetarium and the telescopes is such a cool experience! Your attendance is the majority of the grade, so if you attend every lab you'll be able to get an A. Its rough sometimes having a three hour lab, but Sean makes all of his lectures super entertaining and worth attending!

Sean is an amazing professor when it comes to teaching you through lectures about various terms and information regarding astronomy. He is also genuinely a great person as well outside of class and his love for astronomy and physics is contagious. At first, join the astronomy club for extra credit, then keep attending for Sean, he's that cool.

Prof Kelly is so caring, high energy, and hilarious. He is passionate about astronomy which can be seen in his fun and engaging lectures. There is a lot of material to cover in this course so the tests can be tricky but he is a super understanding and gives lots opportunities for extra credit.

Best professor I've ever had! He is so engaging and loves what he does. He cares so much about his students I was actually sad when his class was over. Take this class it is amazing and you will have fun taking it. I took it just because and ended up loving it so much.

Sean is the most amazing educator I have EVER had. He cares so greatly about not only the material, but the students understanding of it AND their enjoyment. His heart is so big and it definitely reflects in his classes and lectures. While this class was in a large lecture hall, he always kept my attention. I would take this class a million times!

I didn't know what to expect in ECE 103 but Kathy has truly made the experience amazing and overall enjoyable. She is consistent with responding to any questions or concerns and she is very supportive of her students. I also appreciate how she emphasizes the importance of self care, especially as a student. Overall, she is very personable!

She is very unclear in what she wants and I think that showed especially after the transition to online classes. Very strict in grading, makes you work for it and even then you might not get rewarded for it.

Dr. King is overall a great professor. She very passionate about educating future educators. That alone to me is important. She makes you work for your grade. She goes through, with detail, what she expects from you. Her online courses are harder than in person. No make-up assignments taken from Dr. King. She wants you to be punctual and on-time.

Kathy's one of my favorite professors. She is big on learning from the class's personal experiences, so get ready to share :). She is clear on what she wants in assignments and is more than willing to help but you have to reach out to her. She makes her lectures fun, 6-9pm flies by!

Kathy is very kind and caring. She might be a bit intimidating at first, but she really cares about her students and wants them to succeed. Shes easy to talk to when youre struggling so dont heaistate to reach out.
My favorite class by far! Kathy is an amazing professor who makes her 3 hour night class fly by. highly recommend. you learn a lot about yourself, ad your classmates.
Kathy is amazing. She's changed my life! She's so positive, funny, and makes class fun. Highly recomend her class :).

I took an online class and I have taken many before. She is extremely confusing on what she wants and the due dates. She doesn't give good feedback. I honestly didn't learn anything for her "lectures".

Kathy tries to be personable, but may come off as rude. She is overall there for you and wants to accommodate to all learning skills. Her personality could be overwhelming and get ready to watch her play with her hair... a lot.

It is a good class to take if you can do the following standards: never miss any actives and finish all the homework on time. She grades your work very slow, so you will not know how you have done until the end of the semester. The standard is that she can delay her feedback to you, but you must do your job on time. Not helpful and accommodating!
Very opinionated . She is also a tough grader and grades you on what she sees which is not actually accurate . This class including 103 will take over your life.
This instructor is very arrogant and opinionated !
She was the LTA in 103, Kathy is amazing, funny, and positive. This class will take over your life, if you don't know how to manage your time. But it is very rewarding

This class is super fun to be in, you learn a lot and if you pay good attention in class and take notes, you will be fine. One test 5 questions and priority is essays. If you like to write that would be helpful, you write a lot. Good discussions and you get to know your classmates and Kathy! No textbook needed!
She made me not want to go to class and would be rude to me in front of everyone which is not professional.

I wouldn't say the class was hard, I'd say challenging. Yes, there are high expectations. Getting an A, takes dedication and full participation. To get full points on papers, they must include a lot of details, and supporting facts. When you get an A, you've EARNED it! She helps make you a better teacher. She is always helpful, but YOU need to ask!

She is so mean. Everyone told me to take a class, so I did...Boy should I have chose another teacher. She grades unfairly, and she thinks just because she teaches that she knows everything. She is not organized.

As an LTA She a lot out of her students but is more than willing to provide guidance, support, and feedback, which helped me tremendously over my two student teaching semesters. A wonderful teacher and mentor who has a true passion for her field.

Really good teacher. Not an easy A though. Half my class dropped out due to bad grades. Tons of projects due weekly. One midterm and one final. As long as you go to class and do the work you'll be fine! Highly recommend.
she is very helpful towards her students

Dude I loved this class. I understand that dance can be a very new feeling, but he is a professor who acknowledges that and still expects participation. Literally just try. He is a great professor with the best metaphors and analogies. He gets frustrated when people don't TRY, which is completely understandable. But yeah, I would 100% take again.
Particularly tough on inexperienced students, seems like someone who is genuinely unhappy with work, and it impacts the class. Intimidating, so learned little.
He is intimidating and we always did the same in class it was boring
I loved this class, it was really really good ! Great and caring teacher.
Such a great teacher and a nice, caring person!! I really enjoyed the class.

Doesn't check his email so get ready to be very confused. Sometimes he posts the videos and sometimes he doesn't. That being said, when he does post them they are very relaxing, and a little bit challenging if you aren't as flexible. Okay class but I wouldn't take this professor if I could help it.

Incredibly relaxing. He knows how to help you push your own limits physically and he really wants students to improve. I've taken beginning modern from Tracy too, and besides being a very talented teacher, he's really fun to talk to, and he learns your names quickly, so he'll check you in before you can even raise your hand by week three or four.
The least understanding and helpful professor I have encountered.
I really like his class, I took Jazz and his choreographies are amazing!
Good dancing teacher but very exhausting.

I love Tracy. His class is fun and it really makes you sore. Intermediate Jazz, as he teaches it, is more of a conditioning class. We do warm-ups for about an hour and only actually dance for the last 20 minutes. Take this class only if you're looking to sweat, not to learn technique!
I love Tracy! Super funny and made us work hard enough and learn, but still a nice and fun class. Just Awesome.

Tracy is a wonderful dancer, he is also very sarcastic and funny! He can sometimes be "moody" but for the most part he is super nice and caring. The mid-term and final are a bit hard for a 1-unit class... But even if you get bad scores on them, you will most likely end up with an A. Overall, he is a fun professor, especially around Halloween! ;)

Compared to his ballet class modern is much easier, less strict and just super fun! He is a professional dancer, a helpful and friendly person and made me want to stay here longer. The midterm is a bit tricky, but 70 (or 75?) % of your Grade is attendance. We all just loved this class so much- Im auditing it this semester. Take it if you can!!!!!!

I just love Tracy! He is super inspiring and his corrections really help you a lot to improve your technique! One of the friendliest people Ive met in my life- his dance class is the reason why I decided to stay at SBCC for another semester. He definitely expects you to study for his class (terms and terminology) but is always willing to help!

Tracy is interesting. He's really sarcastic which can be amusing at times and very discouraging. Not gentle on beginners. I can see how someone who hasn't danced or taken another P.E. class could feel overwhelmed. Doesn't offer alternatives for stretches or correct your form. I would't recommend :-(

I loved Tracy! He knows what he's talking about and is willing to work with you if you talk to him. Only complaint is that I felt the class was a tad ballet heavy considering it was supposed to be a jazz class so if you don't have some kind of ballet background you might be a little lost at times. Awesome guy!
He is an amazing dancer and he really cares about all his students and he makes class fun. Show up for every single class. always.
Tracy is an amazing dancer and passionate teacher. His midterm is fairly easy (no final). Take the class if you can!
A super fun class. If you show up, it is hard to fail.

Tracy is a GREAT professor. Nice but challenging at the same time. The midterm for the beginning ballet class is relatively easy... you will have to know your positions (eg. grand plie, efface devant/ derriere etc). No final. Amazing class overall.

Pretty good teacher. He is a very good dancer and he is passionate about it. He doesn't judge beginners and he is always helpful. The Beginning class are a little boring, you do the same exercices every class. 75% of the grade is participation and attendance so even with a C at the midterm I got an A in the class. And no final!

If you guys want an easy A this is not the class you are looking for. However, it is an okay class. You just gotta put in work. If he teaches you something, he expects you to know it from then on out. Also, DO NOT MISS CLASS!!! The total amount of points in the class is 210 and 160 of those points are based on attendance. Good luck!

Tracy is a great teacher! I was worried that I wouldn't be able to keep up in his intermediate dance class after not dancing for a long time, but as long as you work at it and enjoy dancing he is great. He is also a great dancer himself and is very inspiring. I recommend this class to anyone who enjoys dancing to dance!
He is professional dancer! Also he is the best teacher. I recommend all his classes if you want to know and learn how to dance.
Really nice and understanding guy, just wants you to have fun! If you're sick he'll let you sit out and take notes for credit so your participation grade doesn't drop. Great class.

Tracy is a good teacher but can be very intimidating. Had him for beginning ballet and he was scary! His expectations were really high. Basically, he doesn't treat it like a beginning class... I was really excited to take this class but the midterm stressed so many students out that they dropped out even though it was ballet... an okay ballet class

Good dance teacher. Really cares about his students. Is NOT accepting of actual beginners. Puts abnormal emphasis on midterm. Expects you to spend hours studying for his class. Don't take this class for fun.
Honestly don't take her class if you don't like chemistry. I was confused half of the time and a lot of her quizzes didn't even align with what she taught. Lectures were pretty boring too.
Pretty okay, not very accommodating to disabilities. Online exams are u
Just take someone else's class if you don't want to struggle to teach yourself

Obvious she puts in minimal effort in. She speaks like a robot and doesn't know what she's talking about half the time because she's simply reading off her own hidden slides (robot). Quizzes don't even align with the material half of the time and they are filled with typos. Exams are also sloppy and not much thought goes into them.

Lectures graded solely on few confusing questions at end. Even if you do lectures and take extensive notes, you may get a 50% or even 0% just for getting 1 or 2 questions wrong.The professor is also very condescending and regularly states how she knows at least one of us will fail to follow direction. It is clear she is jaded and resentful.

This professor gives the hardest quizzes and exams. Get ready for fill-in-the-blank and essay questions on things she barely or didn't even go over in the lecture! Her study guides for the exams don't help. I take notes, watch the videos multiple times, and study but her questions are set up for you to fail. Also, very hands-off teacher.

I had her for lecture and lab, I really learned a lot. Be sure to attend class as she writes her lectures on a whiteboard. She always responded quickly when I had a questions. Offered a good amount of extra credit. Not many assignments but had 3 tests and 1 final. Test and grading were fair. Highly recommend

Michelle was a good teacher with interesting lessons but her weekly quizzes were awful. I watched her videos, read the transcript, read the textbook, watched youtube videos, made flashcards, and did my own research and still struggled. When I asked for help or what I could do to improve my grade, she replied simply to "meet with classmates". :/

(online) Her lectures are long and difficult to understand. She is sometimes hard to reach since her business hours are only a few days a week. However, she is super forgiving and caring. She really wants you to understand and pass, the key, reach out. Tell her you need help, tell her you are struggling. Nothing will happen if you stay silent.
Amazing professor, was able to meet with her in person to gain college and career advice even though it was an online class, easy enough material and very straightforward, caring professor

only posts one weeks material at a time so no way to get a headstart if needed. things are due Fri night so u don't even get the weekend. lectures have typos, dates are incorrect, hard to understand when things are due. quizzes are difficult, fill in the blank sentences??? very bad online class definitely would not recommend

Shes one of those teachers who expects a lot out of her students but doesnt reciprocate. You wont get much direction or interaction from her. She will nitpick your answers to death and doesnt seem to really grasp plagiarism etc. Save yourself the headache dont take her

One of my favorite professors! Im not sure why other ratings are bad because she is an amazing professor and the class is very interactive! Shes understanding of all students and a fair grader. If you put effort into her class you will see a great outcome and she cares about how her students perform in the course! 10/10 would love to take again!
My favorite professor by far. She gives a lot of great advice and has a very laid back teaching/grading style. You can go to her about anything. Very intelligent and wise and fun.

Not recommended at all. The first time I wrote a report letter to the college was because of her. Don't think she will answer your question. She is really irresponsible, scoring is irresponsible. Dont use her lecture notes to answer questions, because you will be counted as plagiarism, and you will fail this course directly. I AM Not kiDDING!
Shes not clear on whats she really want for exams or assignments. Tough grader.

Very educated teacher, only thing is her quizzes and tests literally contain questions that have no information in any of her lectures. If it does have some information its literally a couple sentences that dont have any info to help you. Purchased the textbook and its NO HELP AT ALL big waste of money lol. Google is your only resource!
Nice teacher. The class was pretty difficult since I am not good at biology or animal behavior in general. You will get an A as long as you do the quizzes and answer exam questions in your own words.

She sent a total of 5 announcements in the entire semester. Grading criteria not clear. Don't bother emailing her, she won't answer your questions. Every quiz each week is full of typos so they will be confusing and frustrating to complete. Quiz answers will be counted wrong because even her answers are misspelled. Accused class of plagarism.
Terrible.

This has been one of the most difficult classes I've ever taken. She has it formatted very unclear and confusing, I have a hard time understanding when certain assignments are due, the content is hard to understand and it's overall a really difficult class. I have never struggled so hard in a class.

Lectures had typos, and quizzes were formatted terribly. I had to email her almost weekly to get credit for questions that would be marked as wrong even though the answer itself was correct. I did not learn from the class, plus she accused me of plagiarism when Zoology is my major. This is college, not high school.

Michelle is the best professor i've had here at SBCC. Her class is very straightforward and she is incredibly interested in the subject matter which makes the class in general more enjoyable. I am not a science person but I absolutely loved her class.

Michelle is the best science teacher I've ever had! Students respect her because she's brilliant but also because she gets her students. She isn't trying to trick you, she wants you to get an A. Her lectures are easy to understand. Also, you take a trip to the Natural hist. museum and she used to work there so she knows it all! take bio w/ her!!!!!

If you need to take Bio 100, take w Michelle! She is nice, understanding, and funny. Her lectures aren't boring & she loves what she does. Grading is straight forward. Lab quizzes each lab & pop lecture quizzes. Very easy if you keep up with the lectures & review your labs. 2 quizzes dropped & added EC in everything. Book not needed TAKE HER CLASS!

She is one of my favorite professors so far here and easy to get along with. Think subject material is easy but will help you with anything no questions asked, easy A if you just sit down and study the material. Funny professor and easily gets along with anyone. She gives very little extra credit but it's no big deal if you study.
Michelle is a great professor who wants her students to succeed. Put in the effort and it will pay off.

Professor Kramer is a very funny guy. While the course content isn't too difficult, his lectures can be somewhat confusing. There are 3 exams total (including the final) which each have 3 short essays. Grading on discussions/reflections are tough, expect to get a 4.7/5 or 9.3/10 on many assignments. However, getting an A is manageable.

Professor Kramer is a good professor, however he can be a bit of a tough grader so keep that in mind, but he always gives detailed reasons why. Another thing to note is the essays take up most of the grade, by far. I only regret taking this online because his classes seem pretty fun in the recorded lecture. Funny guy.

Wish I could have taken it in person, A great class but the topic seems best in an actual classroom. Even still, lectures funny a "shite" as Kramer might say. Must have changed up quizzes this time around because Quizlets did not help me. But they weren't that hard any way. All essay exams and pretty AI proof, But grades fairly.

for an online class over the summer and a writing class, I was very pleasantly surprised. Great class, and agree with others about the horrors of ENG 111. Take Kramer's 111 class! Way better. more interesting, useful, and you actually learn. He is also very helpful if you get behind and will make sure you do well in the class.

Summer course: 3 Exams (all written). Easy quizzes (2 attempts/takes average scores). Quizlets are helpful. Reflections are easy, but make sure you give critical responses to peers. I thought his grading system was easy and a great class to take over the summer. Make sure you write at least a page and a half for each exam question. Picky on points.

I dropped the Eng 111 class because it was awful. So glad I took Kramers philosophy 111. I don't like writing but this class actually made it interesting, it helps that the readings in philosophy are interesting and Kramer's lectures are hilarious.Allows late work often with no penalty and has early drafts for papers where he tells you what to fix

He skips all in class discussions in favor of talking for the entire period. He also just rambles on and on about random things that aren't even relevant- He makes me want to never take another philosophy class and his ego is off the chart. Also pro tip if you write a paper and choose a topic he'll agree with, you'll get a much better grade.

Very focused on his own style for the class, would not recommend taking it online. He expects a lot and doesn't make instructions clear, instead depending on students to magically read his mind for how he wants you to write. Way too harsh of a grader for the content he provides which is often comedic and strays away from details class"should know"

Best professor I've ever had. Learned a lot and was able to do well despite his grading not being super easy. Kramer inspired me to do well and learn a lot, and he was very available to help/explain things.

Professor Kramer was a good professor and I am sad to have not taken his in person class as I think I would have enjoyed it much more and learned more. Taking philosophy online was pretty difficult for me but overall it was a fairly easy class. Definitely would recommend taking him in person as I am sure it would be easier to understand the content

Super funny guy, always tried to keep us interested in the lectures by engaging with us. Lots of reading though around 5-7 pages average for each assignment. Super approachable, always ready to help if I had a question on a certain topic. Would definitely take his class again.

He's a great teacher and anyone who's heavily into philosophy will enjoy his lectures. To those who have a slight interest in it some lectures may get a little boring. He's very funny and interactive. The workload in this class is very easy and there's only one big project. There's no exams and the quizzes are a majority of group quizzes.

Professor Kramer makes this philosophy class engaging and manageable. The workload is reasonable, and he provides helpful feedback. unstuck study was a great study tool alongside the course materials.

Prof Kramer is probably MORE interactive and accessible in the asynch/online class than any teacher I have had in person. I have had numerous one-on one zooms with him, he sends out reminders about assignments, and has new videos with updated info every week. He made sure I passed. Got an A which I did not think possible. Take kramers class

This class was very interesting. Professor Kramer is very funny and makes lectures interesting. He is not too harsh on grading and I was able to do well in this class despite not always coming to class.

His pre-recorded lectures are long and wander way off-topic. He should consider the ethics of being paid year after year for the same lectures. A professor who cares about philosophy should want to craft fresh lectures and reference current issues to engage students (and be more concise). He expects a lot of effort but doesn't do so the same.
So far best class at SBCC. By far. Fun interesting useful and he knows his stuff. Even written essays in journals on the topic Take any class from Kramer
Awesome teacher, definitely recomend taking his classes. Kramer is a super nice guy.

On one hand, Mr. Kramer is super charismatic and really cares about the concepts he teaches, which I really appreciated. On the other hand, the lecture would sometimes drift off and have nothing to do with the concepts we were learning. I would take this class again, but just make sure you take notes in class.

Chris treats his lectures as a "workout room" for his stand-up comedy. He is pretty funny, but its out of key when compared to how he grades. If you want a good grade in the class remember to take thorough notes and pay attention when for when he hints quiz/test answers, since they are usually squeezed in after he ran out of time doing crowd work.

Kramer is literally one of the funniest people I have ever met in my entire life. His humor alone makes you want to show up to class all the time and even get more invested in philosophy. He assigns a fair amount of reading but it's usually not that bad. I cannot stress enough how funny he is though like truly. Definitely take his classes!

such a good teacher!! take the class in person because he has very interesting lectures and is very engaging! I struggle with paying attention during lectures but I had no problems in this class. he Is sooo funny too so that made the class a lot more enjoyable
Easy class, frustrating professor to say the least.

I am so glad I dropped the Eng 111 class for this one. There was literally (kramer hates that word, ha!) no critical thinking in the english class. It was ridiculous. Kramers lectures are awesome and he makes sure you do well in the class. Video records his comments on some of the assignments--super helpful. Take his class
best prof I've probably ever had

As far as online lectures go, Kramers were enjoyable. Recorded from an actual in person class, so it is interesting to hear students questions and comments. It is a writing class but does not feel like it. Lots of low point writing work that he helps you with and preps for the bigger stuff. Readings are cool and current and he explains them well

Bets prof at sbcc. So helpful and lectures are enjoyable. A decent amount of reading but they are interesting and he explain them well in class. The exams are online and not timed and can use notes and readings etc. No proctorio watching over you either. if you show up you can get an A

Great class, interesting readings though some were hard to follow, but looked forward to the lectures. Clear, helpful. He wants you to do well and helps a lot. Do the readings and come to class and you will do fine. take notes! he does not like when you just sit there
Excellent class. Take anything from Kramer. You will enjoy it, get a lot out of it, and he makes sure you do well

Philosophy is a very hard subject to grasp, but Kramer is very knowledgeable and does his best to keep us engaged with it. He is hilarious, his lectures are easy to follow along with. Great guy but try to keep up with the work and readings. Usually, we had a discussion post every week and an online quiz every other week or so. Pretty easygoing.
I really enjoyed taking her class. She's clear with her instructions and lectures

Overall, a really great professor! She's clearly super passionate about the subject and is very knowledgeable which is always nice to see from a professor. The class itself was pretty easy and none of the assignments were too difficult. You def have to attend the lectures but it's not a big deal unless you skip often (if so then why take it lol)

I loved Prof. Kunz. It's obvious she wants you to pass the class as her quizzes aren't difficult at all. My only issue is her strictness with attendance. You can only have 4 absences, she does not care about excuses, no matter the situation. I missed maybe 6 and received a 0/60 on participation. If you're prone to skipping, take her class online.

Dr. Kunz's lectures were my favorite part of the week throughout the semester. Listening to her is simply delightful; she made the subject super interesting and accessible, and she can be very funny as well. There's also a field trip to J. Paul Getty Museum near the end of the course and it was a very enjoyable experience.

She is a great professor; you can tell Kuntz is extremely passionate about her work; take her in person; her lectures are very clear and well put together; you really just need to show up and listen to do well.

I found myself appreciating art history so much more after taking this class! Great Professor with clear expectations. Kind and super stylish, love her outfits. Essays after each Unit, (there are four), and only 750-1,000 words. Textbook not even needed, showing up to lecture and paying attention is enough to succeed. Her in-person > online class

Professor Kunz is extremely eloquent and her lectures are incredible. Every week you have one exam which isn't hard if you took good notes beforehand and read the textbook. ALWAYS stick to her assignment requirements because even slight deviation from it will cost you points. That said, I have a huge appreciation for art after taking this class!
JUST AWFUL OH MY GOD. if youre taking her class, be aware to d*e.

2-3weeks of lecture videos & followed by an exam each week > 1w of essay, and the pattern repeats. # of videos varies, most are almost 1hr long, expect 4vid at least/up to 8-9vid per week. You'll never read under 15pg of TB. Essay topics depending on your choice. She gives clear instructions on everything! Expect to work on thanksgiving break.

Expected this class to be so boring but ending up loving it. lectures were interesting and I learned a lot. go to every class and you will get an A, work and quizzes were easy if you paid some attention.

I took 101, 103, and 104, and loved all three courses. Her lectures are straightforward but dense- they are great if you're passionate about the subject, but may admittedly be a bit boring or tedious if you're just taking for an art credit. She's competent, kind, and the assignments are easy- quizzes, chapter exams, and a handful of short essays.

Professor Kunz is very easy-going, nice, and accommodating! I didn't find this class to be hard at all, but you should attend the lectures because you won't find the answers to her exams on quizlet or anything. I thought lecture could be boring sometimes, but she's definitely knowledgable on the content she teaches! This course was an easy A.

Absolutely LOVED professor Kunz!She really knows her stuff. The class is lecture heavy so attendance is key.I took her for honors and learned so much.I don't understand why she has some bad reviews.She will let you retake/reopen any two assignments that you may have missed(which came in clutch for me).The sweetest person ever.

DO NOT RECOMMEND. I took this class because I needed the one credit and as someone who is majoring in art, I thought this class would be easy considering it was a 1 credit class. I emailed her about missing assignments and without telling me she opened them and I automatically got a zero but I never told her to reopen them. Subject was okay overall
Be prepared for many reading and takes tons of notes on the lecture videos.

Dr. Kunz is a great professor. Her class can be challenging, but follow her instructions and you will do fine. Group study sessions really help with this class, so this class is a good chance to make new friends?

The class consisted of her talking at the students in class rather than with us, and she was not engaging. The tests were difficult studying rarely helped. The quizzes were easy, and you can take them twice without penalty. If you do not learn well in lectures, I wouldn't recommend this course with professor Kunz
Tough

I enjoyed Prof Kunz's class. It was online and during summer, but she was very understanding to my personal situation. Other than that, tests were pretty good, all based on readings/lectures, but fairly straight forward. Would take again :)

Tests follow the lecture almost exactly, which often leads to questions that seem based on specific wordings and minor details that prove you watched the lecture or read specific materials. Makes it feel like she sidelines grasping the material in favor of memorizing particular details. Doesn't correct errors in tests or give points back.

The work in the class is very manageable and nothing too extreme. She gives exams for each chapter, but if you watch and take notes on the lecture videos she uploads you will do perfectly fine. Her lectures are pretty engaging and interesting so the material becomes rather exciting. She gives back good feedback and is very communicative w/ question

Very good professor if you work hard and do all the assignments on time. Gives extra credit and easy projects for extra points. The majority of assignments are quizzes but as long as you study they are doable.

All the assignments were super easy! The tests on the other hand, even if you study a crazy amount, are very difficult! Tests are the majority percent of your grade as well. Unless you are passionate about art history and care to learn about it, this professor isn't a good choice.

Very organized. I knew when things were due and I knew what I needed to do in order to get them done. I loved the projects at the end. I will say that the exams are NOT easy. I made a study guide and I got 40/50 and I had a hard time even with the study guide. This is not the art class you can just breeze through. There is work but it's doable.

Enjoyed this class. As long as you read the assigned textbook chapters and review them before any exams, you'll be good (open book). There are assignments every week that consist of a discussion and a 400-500 word essay, not long at all. I am relativity good at writing up essays so I had no problem with them but if you struggle,put extra time aside

If you can't keep up with 600-800 word essays with a resource every WEEK on top of your other classes then this class is not for you, she is very demanding on what she wants in her discussions, and her homework assignments. I am taking four classes and even they don't assign the amount of workload this teacher does. Very test heavy too
She is literally the definition of awful. She is not caring at all and doesn't respond to emails. AVOID HER CLASS.

Amazing professor Textbook is a requirement but I got an A in the class by taking notes of everything during the lecture, reading the sources she provides in canvas, and skim reading the textbook. There is an essay for the 4 exams. Offers 35 pt EC for attending a field trip, definitely attend. Lots of information during lectures. Attendance matters

She can be caring if you show her that you are trying, but if you do not show her that you care about her class and don't show up then good luck. You will not be allowed to make up exams if you miss the day because you were sick. Also make sure you check the syllabus so you know when the test and quizzes are so you won't get caught by surprise.

I loved taking her class. The topics that this class offers are really thought provoking and well curated. She offers really good feedback, allows room for improvement, she also gives extra credit assignments. But be prepared to apply yourself, and think critically.
Great prof!
This Professor is really top shelf, she has a no nonsense style, but will courage hard work, and will grade fairly. She is very approachable and will take time to speak with you if she can.
Lectures are boring, she's unclear about the homework and doesn't know how moodle works yet insists on using it.

AMAZING TEACHER! Tests are tough, but she grades on a curve. Easy lectures, everything you need is online, so no need for textbook. Jokes throughout class. Helps students a lot. There's many extra credit opportunities too if you go up and ask her before the lecture. Take this class for an easy A.

Anna Kwon know a lot about business and marketing, and you will realize that from some of her stories she tells you. Although being really sick the last weeks of class, she never failed to show up and make the best of class. I would strongly recommend her if you want to have a teacher that answer all your questions about marketing or business

Anna Kwong is an expert to make hard things easy! She has allot of experiences in this area, so you learn from not only the book but real life, whitch is great, she has allot great storyes. I would recommend taking one of Anna's classes, she makes you wanna do great things!

She is amazing, she is STERN, STERN BUT FAIR! If you do your work, do decent on the tests, there is no reason why you SHOULD NOT PASS! She will help you, she is understanding, she's simply one of the best teachers, if not, the best teacher I have ever had.
AMAZING CLASS!

Ive had this teacher for 3 years straight at sbcc, Ive taken International Marketing, International Business, and now Im in International import and export, overall she will help you if you need it, she wont let you fail if you come to class, great overall knowledge, goes beyond what the textbook can teach, She has real life EXP, no useless hw
The best professor. She is kind, smart, intelligent and outstanding. Too bad that I have taken all her classes. I hope she will teach more at SBCC.

Professor Kwong is a top-notch professor at SBCC. She likes and cares a lot about the students. Her lecture is funny and interesting. I have never wanted to attend classes, until I attended her class. Want to learn how to do business in the real world? Take this professor, she will really open your eyes and bring you to the next level.

Anna Kwong is the best professor I ever have in my entire education life. She is engaging, enthusiastic and extremely knowledgeable. I just love going to her classes. I wish she teaches more classes at CC.
I love this professor. Anna Kwong is very smart and knowledgeable in her subject matter. If you are a serious student who want to learn, she is perfect. I learned so much from her!!!

Professor Kwong is the best professor I ever have in SBCC. She cares a lot about her students. Her classes are interactive with lots of interesting discussions. Her knowledge in the subject matter is extensive. And what I like best is that she always take time to make complicated concepts seem easy and fun.
Great teacher. Her class is always exciting. She explains the material in a way that it is understood easily.
She's a very bad teacher, who always think she is right!
great professor. every class is fun with lots of interactions. she is very helpful. the material can be understood easily because she explains good and gives a lot of examples.

Mrs. Kwong makes sure you know all needed material, not only so you pass the tests, but so you understand the subject. She uses real life examples from her own experiences which makes the class that much more interesting. In addition she has a great sense of humor, keeping you that much more captivated by her lectures. Overall great teacher!

Anna is a great teacher. There is a lot of interaction in all her classes that it makes it very interesting and fun. She makes sure that everybody understands the topics. She has a lot of knowledge and shares it with her students. Anna really cares for her students and makes sure that everybody is treated the same. Best teacher I've ever had

She is so fun and easy going. I actually enjoy going to class because she is such an awesome funny teacher. Nobody can fail her classes because she wants everyone to succeed. She offers lots of extra credit opportunities and is always wanting to help people out. Best teacher at SBCC. I'm going to take more of her classes!!
Not a good professor. Tries to lecture on global affairs. Uses disinformation consistently. In general, doesn't like to talk with American students unless she's trying to get something from them.
Cool and helpful teacher. Wants her students to succeed!
Very easy to get an A. Sticks to the subject every class, which is good. A lot of oral presentations.
If you really wanna learn something, choose Anna. She is the coolest professor I ever met.

Anna Kwong is not only an extremely lively, outgoing, honest & intelligent Teacher, she is helpful, super understanding, and unless you are disrespectful and uninterested in the topic..Id more than reccomend any class of hers.
really experienced and helpful!

She supress student in a bad way. She is arrogance and torture student those who does not keep high score on an exam. She talks about the student when they are not around. Her information is out of data and she force you to learn 10-15 years old story.

This is one the most arrogant people I've ever met. Her classes aren't very hard though. She says she´d like to treat us as adults, but she really doesn't.. I would say it more like a highschool class. and oh yeah... It is suppesed to be international marketing, but I would call it more an Asian culture class.
VERY smart and nice teacher, highly reccomended
She is a good teacher. But sometimes is hard to understand what she is saying. But overall she is great. She makes her classes interesting.

It is a P/NP course. Everything is self explanatory. There is minimal communication with Professor LaBonte because her online class is well organized/ structured. Gives you plenty of time to complete modules. A few discussions involved. Let's you redo assignments and quizzes for a better score.

Pretty easy class, it is a P/NP. The schedule is simple and allows you to submit homework late if you forget or just busy. Quizzes are quite simple, you can just scroll through the lectures notes. Final project is based on the citations you done through the class and you compile all of them into 1 file.

If you are in need of a library 101 class for a GE credit, I would say 100% go with this teacher. I went with her because she didn't have a review on here while other teachers had awful ones. She lays out a clear cut class for you, her website is very organized and straight forward. I was able to log on once a week and finish the work within 1 hr.

Super nice teacher who genuinely wants students to succeed. It's a different learning style than I was used to, as you have to learn outside of the classroom, not during lectures. There are lots of extra credit opportunities, and I would 100% take this class again.

If math's not your strong suit/you can't learn on your own, this may not be the class for you. It's structured to be independent. If you're stuck taking it, go to office hours and you'll be fine. She offers so much extra credit that it's nearly impossible not to pass. If you are decent at math, TAKE THIS CLASS! She's super nice and it's a breeze.

Very little homework and you are graded mainly on 8 quizes, 4 of which you can retake and will get 100% on. All the quizes and final are open note. This class is impossible to fail if you do the homework, labs, and show up for the quizes.

I think the teacher was pretty good. The lectures weren't understandable whatsoever, but the fact that 3.5 out of the 7 midterms you can retake, and you get as many notes as you want, is a great plus. It also helps that you can get more than 100% through extra credit in the assignments. Homework is light. Just form a study group and practice tests.

Allows full pages of notes on exit tickets but no calculators, which I find strange. Usage of the LaTeX equation editor sometimes takes more time then the actual problem itself. Wants you to explain in words how you solved the problem on exams instead of showing pure math, which I think wastes valuable test time. Nice person, but wouldn't recommend

Will not provide examples for concepts. Must learn Maple and LaTeX which is required for tests and wastes valuable time. Super kind lady. Terrible at teaching and lecturing. You will have to teach yourself the calc 2 concepts making the dedication to this class much bigger than it has to be. Success in this class requires much dedication.

First off, you have to get a near-perfect score on the exit tickets for your grade to not drop an entire letter. Second, the class doesn't feel set up very smoothly. I understand the idea behind the discussion posts but I feel like they're not set up very well in execution. Nice lady, but would reccomend taking another prof for 130 if you can.

I personally struggle with math. I have never taken calculus in my life, but she made it easy to understand. If you actually show that you are determined and do your work, she is a very understanding person. She notices when you do your best. Great communication. Best teacher I have ever had.

This course has a lot of material, but the way she has the class set up allows for students to focus on one concept at a time. She provides many opportunities to earn extra credit. Always asks what students want to work on in class. You will have to spend a lot of time outside of class teaching yourself, though, since lectures are just videos.

You have to attend lecture to learn the material, but with it being a college calc course this isn't surprising. There is consistent homework and exams, but she provides extra credit opportunities throughout and structures the class for you to succeed if you are willing to do the work. Highly recommend her!! Would 100% take again.

This hybrid 7credit class requires about 21 hours a week and has tough open-note tests and super low level lecture notes. However, Mrs. Loftus offers so many chances to raise your grade exit ticket retakes, one question test redo which are guaranteed 100%,open-note final, extra credit, and support from the SBCC Math Lab. She's also kind and genuine
100% would take again, class is very supportive and lots of extra credit!!!
Professor Loftus' class can be a mixed bag - some love the setup, others find it too rigid. But unstuck study is a great study tool to stay on top of the work. Overall, a challenging but fair course.

Awesome, easy to talk to, and eager to help. However, the class's absurd reliance on Maple (horrid graphing software) wastes needed learning time. Also, the pace and difficulty of the class isn't met with adequate chances to improve grade. Loftus is a great teacher and enjoyable to be around, but the course is hard* and not well thought-through.

Loftus is an interesting professor. For one if you're a procrastinator then you can submit anything except discussions up to the final exam. On the other hand, if you want a view of your grade well good luck. Loftus takes over a month to grade. Her final is 30% of your grade, the 8 "Exit Tickets" are another 30%. Lectures are long and no examples.

Professor Loftus is kind, but her class NEEDS improvement. Weekly assignments include math discussion posts, Maple labs, and reflections. Multiple online open-note exams consist of several tests with 2-4 questions worth 4 points each- work must be submitted online for credit, leaving little time for math. Average class score was very low each test.

Jennifer Loftus was an absolutely amazing professor. The care and attention she gives her students is above and beyond what I expected from a professor. She not only adjusted the class to our needs but took SO much time out of class creating new practice for us. PS don't let my grade reflect the value of Dr. Loftus; math was never my best subject.

Dr. Loftus is really nice! Difficult class and concepts. While there are practice tests and ungraded "second look hw" there is very little else meant to submit, making self discipline the absolutely necessary. Make sure to do every second look homework, and attend office hours. The test retakes help a lot so don't get demoralized halfway through!

I absolutely recommend Mrs Loftus she is understanding and wants to see us succeed, she has great lectures and is eager to hear our questions and provide solutions judgement free. All her quizzes are open note and she's taken in the feedback because she gave many chances to make up points and receive extra credit. Overall a great teacher and class!

Loftus has figured out how to get a teaching job without doing an ounce of teaching. Genuinely one of the worst teachers I've ever had, doesn't answer a single question that you have, rather insisting on asking your classmates, who are often just as confused as you are. If you take this class be prepared to spend hours at the library with no help.

Please take someone else. She doesn't go through every step and messes up her own answers and zooms through everything. Overall doesn't care to explain what she's doing. She just does it on her own and then asks what we got without going through it with the class. Takes the whole class period to go over 1 problem and it's still very unclear.

Not a huge fan. Endearing, but not the best educator. Has an inexplicable love for Maple, which can be frustrating at times. Almost feels like a Latex class during the quizzes. If she's still allowing Desmos on the exit tickets when you take her, I highly recommend leaning heavily on it, you can copy and paste latex from it into your submission.
Very strange class, using pre recorded lectures from 3+ years ago, does not go over anything on whiteboard in class, only a couple homework problems per week.

Loftus jumps around and mix up the sections and enjoys telling students to check the discussion boards for answers to questions instead of answering your questions. The worst part are her lectures where she rambles on and turns a 40 minute video lecture into a 90 minute lecture video with no examples. If you have her please use Paul's Notes. Better

The Zoom meetings and videos after the Practice Exit Tickets go great with the lectures. The discussion boards give the class an in-person feel, and you pick up a lot of tips from your classmates (including LaTeX). Maple makes MATH 200 easier to visualize, and Professor Loftus keeps showing you how to graph by hand to encourage you to do the same.

I thinker there's a lot of inconsistency between her classes, but I took her during a summer session and the class was absurdly difficult. No extra credit, no curves, a punishing 4-point scale that tanked your grade for small mistakes, and F/D averages consistently on every test. I didn't feel like I learned anything at the end as well

Genuinely don't get the other bad reviews here. Yes, you use Maple but anyone who has ever touched a computer before can learn to use it. The way the class is set up is really cool. A bunch of small tests (around 8) which makes the work load a lot more manageable. Fair grading, lets you retake 2 tests per semester. Really chill and you learn a lot.
RUN!
AVOID HER AT ALL COST!!!!!!!!!!!!!
By far the worst math professor I have ever had beside Ignacio. AVOID her at all cost. Take Gabe he is so much better.

Prof. Lukomski reminded me so much of Sheila from shameless it made me enjoy lectures more lol. Mostly group work, answering questions, making collages, and group presentations. She is so sweet I couldn't recommend her class more!

I'm SO SO surprised by these low ratings. Professor Lukomski is the best English teacher I've had. She truly cares and supports her students. Go to office hours at least once or twice and build a relationship with her, if you show even the smallest bit of effort you will be rewarded in so many ways (especially through your grades). Take her class!

Wendy holds a very engaging English class. She wants you to succeed and is very helpful when it comes to writing. She assigns plenty of work on canvas to do during the week, which is annoying but it's doable. She has us read a memoir and do a unit on it, which I actually enjoyed and I HATE reading. The class is easy if you show up and do the work.

Professor Lukomiski is very nice and caring!! You can tell she wants your writing to improve. There are about 2-3 assignments, very time consuming. She gives great feedback on essays. Do not skip class!! Attendance matters in her class if you miss more than 3 classes she will drop you. Just don't fall behind and do your work and you will be okay.
Not a friendly person

Teaching style is mostly by student examples of essays and/or writings when ever we have an essay/writing assignment coming up. She goes mostly by group and class discussion when overviewing material that we read and has few individual assignments. Strict on deadlines and has a clear grading criteria for all assignments.

Only take if this is your only option. so incredibly boring and slow; she did lessons and quizzes on punctuation, compound sentences, and how to write a summary for literal college freshmen. assignments feel like busy work, just write a lot and throw in some buzzwords. Learned very little this, honestly just using my elementary writing skills.

The class largely revolved around one author's dense and uninspiring memoir. Lecture notes were vague and difficult to decipher; a classmate and I spent too many evenings together trying to decode the professor's instructions so we could pass. Show up late and she'll threaten to kick you out. But if you can regurgitate her opinion, it's an easy A.
Do not take this course online. She will grade your work very hard and then give vague explanations explaining what you did wrong. Take her in-person classes. So much easier to follow.

Professor Lukomski is so caring and wants you to do well in her class and beyond it! I didn't find most of her assignments difficult and she likes to try to give you as much freedom and control over your work as possible. Some thing I think she should work a little more on is being more assertive and stern because she's too nice with everyone!

Prof. Lukomski is a great teacher. If you put in the effort and read the material, you will do well. She expects you to improve your writing and will push you to do so. The class is interesting, and the readings are relevant to current issues. It's funny, most of the negative reviews are poorly written--what a coincidence.

Prof. Lukomski is a nice lady, though can be unclear with her grading criteria. Grade is based on all essays and midterm/final only-which is hard because all other extra assignments don't really count. Her topics are pretty interesting though, and class goes by fast

Wendy means well and is a nice lady. However, she doesnt know how to teach. It will be the day before turning in a big paper and we watch a play for 2 hours instead of going over things to write. in educational class and would not recommend her

I took Professor Lukomski's class Fall 2018 and I actually enjoyed it. Her assignments are easy and so are the essays just a bit time consuming. Listening and interacting makes this class a lot easier. She is really nice and understanding as well. I enjoyed talking to her after class because you can tell she actually cares about her students.

she is one of the best professors I ever had. She expected you to put effort in your essay and she is a tough grader but you can turn in the revisions for the first two essays. You are required to attend one play if you are taking ENG 110 with her. You will be fine as long as you follow her instructions along the way.

Prof is super nice but is veryyyyy boring and trails off on thoughts a lot. I skipped class a lot and would never miss anything but she does take attendance. Grade is relied on essays and short writings only.

Wendy is very friendly and caring. Class isn't hard, but can be unclear and kind of a drag. She expects effort and participation that goes beyond most students desire for a required class (participation points, outside-of-class activities had to see a play). Discussion of boring topics felt very forced. TL;DR: Solid classwork, boring teacher.
pretty nice professor, she is very helpful .

Wendy is a really nice person and really wants her students to succeed but shes also just not a very good teacher. Shes all over the place with her material, shes a tough grader too. If you dont do a essay exactly how she wants it youre screwed. She literally emailed me that I wouldnt succeed in her class. lol

Professor Lukomski is a very respectful women who is passionate about writing, literature, and grammar. Her lectures were always very interesting and the reading she assigned was never dull. She does assign a good amount of papers but she is not a very hard grader so as long as you do what she asks you will be fine!

I took this class bcs of her good reviews but they were so wrong. Her syllabus was always off or wrong. Had to spend $20 to see a play. Her grading was inconsistent and hard. She lectures a lot on stuff that doesn't matter. She has favorites. We had to BEG for extra credit. Everyone regretted taking her class, don't take any class with her!
She is super nice teacher. Do the homework and don't forget to read syllabus because she does not tell you.
Wendy is one of the best teachers! Shes very respectful, super kind and very helpful! Would definitely take any other class she teaches! She makes her English class seem soooooo easy.

She structures the class in such a way that you only get one full day to write essays, normally between 4-6 pages in length. The major downside of this is that she expects final-draft quality from these papers, which is unreasonable in my opinion. She also doesn't upload powerpoints from lectures, so you must take notes if you want to do well.
Who is better than her? None. She teaches very carefully and concisely. U must get in this class if u r gonna take ENG110.
Wendy is the greatest! She's sweet and helpful! Took her in the summer which is accelerated and it's still easy to get an A!

Wendy is a wonderful teacher. She is passionate and really knows her stuff. She's extremely helpful and really wants her students to succeed. Apart from being extremely nice her class is interesting and you have all the opportunities to succeed. I love her.

First class all I could think was that I wanted to drop the class, it seemed so dry. However, after the first few classes I learned to love Wendy. She's super sweet & willing to help you after class. Class never goes for the entire scheduled time. The class is very easy, but at the same time I have grown as a writer from this class.Would recommend!
The class is a little dry, about California Dreams and Realities, the textbook is used a lot for free writes.

I had her in Eng100 & Eng111. most people from my Eng100 class even took her for Eng110 because her class was so much fun and really easy. All you have to do is write a few essays and show some interest. She is one of the nicest persons I have met at SBCC and she will always support you. Just make sure to check the syllabus for assignments
Professor Malvinni was nothing but enjoyable throughout the semester. He responds quick, doesn't assign too much work, and overall made the class intriguing and fun to do.

Prof. Malvinni was really great. Such an easy grader and he let me in the class later in the semester. He says the textbook is required but bought it and never used it. No exams just small quizzes once a week and you will have a final paper at the end of the semester. Most of the quiz answers can be found online through Quizlet.
Best professor ever! He was so kind and made the classwork very clear to understand. He is awesome.
like his lecture and the course content, a very interesting and enjoyable course test may be hard though, but i am taking it P/NP so anyway

There is a quiz and discussion each week, sometimes just one or the other. Midterm and final exams plus a project at the end of the year. Do all the work on time and you're good. He even opened some assignments at the end of the semester to help us out. For tests, make a quizlet with all the quiz questions and you'll get an A like I did. Solid prof

He has a lot of interesting things to say, but they're not always about the topics you have on your test. He is very interesting and I enjoyed his class quite a bit, though he can be all over the place at times and takes a long time to grade or update canvas. Make sure you study and take a lot of notes, and then sit back and enjoy the music!
Malvinni is great, I really enjoyed his class. I learned so much and he is super knowledgeable

Malvinni is a great professor but demands respect in the classroom. He knows what he's talking about but rambles a bit. His lectures are always interesting and his test material is surprisingly fun to study.
Tests and quizzes were super opinion based but easy enough. Really cool information and stuff I actually used in real life everyday. Weird dude but fine and sound minded most of the time.

Dr. Malvinni is a great teacher. I took his MUS115 class and I loved it. He's super chill and always willing to hear our opinions. He knows a lot about music! Easy class, just listen to his spotify playlists and you'll do fine. Would take this class again without a doubt.

Malvinni can be super knowledgable about one genre and then have no idea about a different genre. He is constantly changing how he runs the class so it can get sort of weird. He is also very opinionated about music so don't take everything he says as a fact.

One of the nicest, coolest teachers I've ever had. Reviews the quizzes and exams in class, so they are very easy if you take notes. Also, show up because he grades attendance. Show up, pay attention, do the work and you will get an A. Pretty easy and straightforward course, and the class was interesting as well.

Super funny guy. This is a really layer back class with hardly any homework, but Dr. Malvinni is still extremely passionate about the subject, which makes the class so much fun. Definitely not a class you want to take if you have other majors classes you need to get done, but if you have some free time, this is a great class. Great guy, and prof.

Overall super easy class and a great teacher. Might not be the most exciting class but he puts in the effort to discuss about your opinions on just about anything. Learned quite a lot from that. You get to use notes on all the quizzes and exams. You would literally have to TRY, in order fail this class. Attend class and you'll receive an A.
Basically show up to class everyday and you'll pass. Gives all the answers to quizzes and test prior to taking them. Extremely unorganized teacher but some of the lectures are interesting.

Dr. Malvinni is really nice. This class is very easy to pass, but I recommend keeping up good attendance as it is worth points. You're allowed to use notes on quizzestests most of the time. He's very soft spoken, so if you're hard on hearing, sit near the front. Keep good notes. His lectures kind of jump around a lot. Not the most exciting class.

The class is pretty easy as long as you show up. Note taking is extremely hard as he blows through them with not much time to write them down. He said the text was required but never once was assigned homework or opened it, but really easy class to pass for general ed as long as you show up to class you should pass. Final & Midterm is really easy.

Worst professor I have ever had. Grading and syllabus criteria are vague. He makes up class as he goes along, typing notes into Microsoft Word, while scrolling through so fast it is hard to take notes for what's quizzed and tested on. Part of your grade is attendance, and if you show up after he has called your name he won't mark you as present.
Easy Gen. Ed,, study for listening quizzes and do the few assignments he gives. Easy easy A. No need for textbook

The quizzes and homework are literally the easiest things ever. You're allowed to use notes on the quizzes and half the class got an A on every one. The midterm and final are a little tougher, but you have to be an idiot not to get at least a B in this class. Also, we literally never used the assigned textbook.

Real chill dude! Really enjoyed this class. He clearly knows what he's talking about and if you pay attention to the lectures it's an easy A. All tests were open notes. Didn't use the textbook at all, so that was a waste of money.. Other than that, if you like music this is a really cool class!!

Honestly he is a nice guy but he is extremely disorganized. Required book is rarely used and I didn't even try in the class and I received an A . It can be boring (especially if you know all the music already). Otherwise it is a good filler class that is transferrable.

He is veryy knowledgable in all the history of music and you can tell he's really interested in it! If you're not interested in the subject its relatively easy to get distracted but the class is easy and interesting!
Funny dude! His class is pretty enjoyable and easy. only complaint is the 10 hours you need to spend in the LRC. Other than that its a great class!

The reason he gave to us regarding having to spend 10 hours in the LRC was something to do with the music program and it's funding. I don't think it was just him who enforces that. It's a department decision.

I will agree he does not get back to e mails, but he seems like he is not the type to be in to that sort of technology. It's not an online class though-so just go talk to him. That simple. Slightly disorganized, but I imagine it's that's whole musician mentality. The class is great and the information is awesome.

this class was really easy and it broaden my music horizons. i learned a lot kind of but he wants you attend at the lrc for like 10 hours to listen to the music supplemental for the listening quizzes. he usually gives you the questions and the answers to the test so it's hard not to get an a. he grades really weird too

Often repeats entire lectures. Inconsistent in way he gives prep questions for tests/quizzes and the actual test questions themselves. Told my group to include topics in our group presentation, then interrupted us to tell us what we did wrong in including those same topics when we spoke. Grades do not equal the effort you put in necessarily.

i am suprised at all the positive comments. guy is cluless. expect him to interrupt your presentation. never responds to emails. pipeline postings worthless. seems to make up class as he goes along, typing subjective notes into microsoft word. unable to control talkative students.

he was very unclear.. he seemed very unorganized.. didn't really answer emails and some of his facts were off. All in all it a very easy class and if you already know a lot about rock and roll it will be a cinch.

I honestly think she's the best econ professor at sbcc. The other two had bad reviews so I took her instead and it wasn't too bad! She let's you use a secondary device on the tests and her lecture videos cover all the materials

Honestly, I had a good experience with Dr. Martinez. I took ECON101H online and yes, it was really difficult to acclimate to the faster pace of the class at first. But, having little experience in economics before, Dr. Martinez makes it clear how and what to study. Her course setup is super organized which I appreciate.

Way too much work for a not my major. 10+ hours of work a week and multiple assignments on same subject is a bit overkill. No will to help student with difficulties or seem to care about how you learn to better understand the material.

Her notes and slides in lectures were well-written and readable. Although when I had asked for help, it was ignored, and felt that I was not understood or recognized. I had another event to attend for other programs in the school, and she told me it wasn't important, even though it was part of my career. I didn't feel she acknowledged her students!

Content-heavy course taught in the kindest most well-organized way possible. Posts study guide Qs & lecture slides on Canvas. Explains things clearly. Labs are lecture + mostly independent work. Read labs ahead of time! 3 lab field trips. Grade based on: Weekly lab quizzes, 4 exams, 1 700-word research paper, & 3-4 short assignments. No busy work.

Jen is the best professor ever. Her lectures are always so interesting and they were never boring. If you do all of the review questions and take time memorizing them you will pass this class. She is amazing take her if you can.

Professor Maupin is amazing - super engaging lectures and genuinely cares about student success. Exams are detailed but the review questions are super helpful. unstuck ai was a lifesaver for extra practice!
She absolutely loves her job and she takes great consideration to students by extending deadlines and providing good resources for tests.

If you have the option to take her, DO IT! She is one of the best biology professors at SBCC and makes a relatively difficult subject enjoyable and easy to digest. She gives us extra credit and is always willing to answer any questions or concerns you may have.
Makes me care about bio, genuinely so nice and caring, does the best with a difficult subject
She is the best biology 101 teacher in SBCC. I took her my first year and definitely put me in track for my future. Thank you Jen.

Great teacher. Caring, friendly, and helpful. Group labs were so much fun, if you find a good group. Lectures are okay but really slow. I listened to them on 1.75x speed and still understood what she was saying perfectly fine. Show up for class, listen to lectures, and you'll do fine. Yes the lectures are slow but there is no cheesing the exams.

I loved professor Maupins class. She is super kind and has great lectures that are really engaging. There's a good amount of work but its not too bad, if you just study lecture and lab notes you can easily get an A on every exam. The field trips for lab were also super fun. Take her if you can!

Very sweet professor!!! Cares for her students success. Her exam reviews are long (100+ questions) but they will be exactly like the exams so defineitly do the review questions! (TIP: I would fill them out during her lectures to get more accurate responses)

Jen is an amazing professor. Lots of extra credit. Lab quizzes are the only difficult things in this class so make sure you thoroughly learn the material + refer to what she write on the board in lab. Study guide (review questions) is basically the whole exam so if you know the answer to the questions, you'll do well on exams.

Such an amazing teacher and person! its rare that you find a teacher who is so passionate about their job these days.
