Only thing to worry about is that the tests are very thorough and in detail and some answers are only available during the lectures so its vital to your grade that you come and take notes and draw all diagrams.
I love this woman. Show up to her lectures!

She is so loving and kind and is very passionate about her courses. But pay close attention and take good lecture notes. She does look for VERY particular answers on exams DO NOT GOOGLE ANYTHING! if you study off the slides only, you'll do really well. She is the most genuine person ever and she's very approachable to ask for help/extensions.

I absolutely adore Dr. Maupin! She cares so deeply about the wellbeing and success of her students and is definitely one of my favorite professors of all time! She provides plentiful extra credit opportunities on the exams. The study guide is lengthy, but you will be very well prepared for the exam if you complete and understand it thoroughly. :)

Dr. Maupin is wonderful. She truly loves biology and teaching - in a large lecture she will get to know each of her students by name and make sure they all feel supported by her. She gives lots of extra credit opportunities, def take advantage of them! Her study guide is long, but it is exactly what the exam is, so memorize it and you'll get an A

Jen is awesome. I miss her class. She is very caring and loves getting to know all her students. Her class is nt easy, but as long as you take good notes during lecture and study the study guide you'll be okay. she also offers a good amount of extra credit to help boost your grade.

"MINIMUM SPEND 6-8 hours studying WEEKLY for my class". too many online lectures that are hours upon end long that no one listens to. everyone fails first exam and most people do not do well in her class. class was only offered online. if you have a job or in other extracurricular activities, she does not care

Jen is a wonderful professor! She is very passionate about her field and is very enthusiastic with lectures! She makes classes + labs very interesting and I am not a bio major at all. You do have to study for the quizzes and exams, make sure to watch all lectures because the answers are usually there. I 100% recommend this professor :)

I had Jen for Lab for Bio 101 and she's so sweet, knowledgeable, and really helpful when you aren't sure of something. She takes the classes on walks around campus and down to the beach for some labs. If you can, definitely take her lab section !!!

Jen was my lab professor, and I absolutely loved it. I had never had an in-person lab before and I always looked forward to it every week. She is so knowledgeable on plant biology and her enthusiasm is contagious. Take her if possible!!

Professor Maupin is a kind person, which makes her an even better professor. Take good notes, rewatch lectures if needed, dont be afraid to ask questions, engage with the material beyond just writing notes ( e.g. active recall), DO ALL THE EXTRA CREDIT youll work hard for your grade, but Professor Maupin is a great support and made bio fun.

Jen is passionate, enthusiastic, and interesting! She has done really cool work in the field and her excitement is obvious. You don't really need the textbook. Use the study guides and watch (or attend) lecture. If you put in the work, the exams are very fair and she offers a lot of extra a credit. Jen cares a lot about her students!!!

How to pass this class: do the labs (watch the videos, she gives the answers), take lab quizzes, watch lectures while filling out study guides (answers are gone over in lectures), and write out study guides as many times until you memorize answers. She gives extra credit, as well as a make-up test to replace lowest test score. Awesome teacher.
Outstanding professor, she has a passion for her job.
Jen's the best professor ever <3

Jen is so awesome. She is passionate about the field of Biology, and even though I'm not a bio person- she made it fun! She goes above and beyond to make her students feel supported in class and is always there if you need help.

Professor Maupin is the best professor I have taken at SBCC so far. She really cares about her students and she is always accessible and she will help you if struggle with something. Her lectures are interesting and she explains everything really well. A lot of extra credit opportunities. I definitely recommend taking her!

The lectures are extremely long when the could be 35 minutes. The study guides are extremely helpful in passing exams. She gives extra assignment during exam weeks so plan for that. Likes to discuss what she finds interesting, but it won't pertain to our exams or the labs. There is extra credit.

When I was taking 3 courses at my local community college and this one, this one was equal in time to spent to the other three classes. Each week there was a tremendous amount of material to cover.The teacher is very polite and helpful when asked questions, but the class is teach yourself from slides, lecture notes and text, you need all 3.

I took Maywood's 8 week course, and it was doable. Grade is based on quizzes, discussions, research paper, and final. Quizzes were a bit difficult and lots of material to study in a short amount of time. Grades easy on discussions and research paper. Overall, interesting class but you're basically teaching the material to yourself.

Absent professor. We had weeks when we were assigned 80-130 pages of textbook reading (thick Pathophys reading btw). That's all her class is, reading and quizzes based off the textbook. Professor spends 0 time with students. She doesn't respond well to emails. And her "feedback" is basically copied and pasted.

Not sure how this professor was before COVID-19. Couple things. First off, she lives in Florida and works... full time? I don't what her positive reviews are about because she is absent. There are no class meeting times. Her course doesn't feel like she's a teacher so much a grader: someone who set up the course on canvas and let's it play itself.

Great professor. Detailed lectures and power points provided. Grades easy on HW and research paper. I appreciated her personal weekly communication and motivational words. This is a difficult class and you will need to read the chapters, lectures, and review the slides. Would definitely recommend her.

I took this class online and really enjoyed the material. Professor Maywood was very clear on what was expected in the class and was always very helpful when I had questions. Quizzes and discussion posts every week, 1 paper, 1 final exam. I wouldn't recommend if you can't dedicate adequate study time.

Straightforward class, quiz after each unit and discussions about every other unit. Tests are very difficult and you need to know the tiniest details given in the book to pass them. Class uses Proctorio so you have to give access to your computer to an outside source! Cannot use notes or book on tests since you're recorded. Nice lady though!

I thought that the class itself was really fascinating, but admittedly I learned so much more from reading the textbook than reading her lectures. It feels like she uses the exact same lecture slides that she uses in an in-class session and is presenting because just reading them makes almost no sense. Textbook use is a must if you want to pass!

This is a difficult class. Professor Maywood was polite and helpful. She responded in a timely fashion to inquiries. However, her tests are very difficult. You need to know EVERYTHING you read because the test questions are VERY specific. This was unfair in some weeks because the amount of reading required left no additional review time. STUDY!!!

Lec and ppt not helpful bc she tests on tiny details in book, so just read. Two tries per quiz. Take both attempts bc Q's end up on finals and midterm. To get full pnts you need to memorize everything or get lucky. Every sentence is a new idea and you need to memorize every sentence. Case studies are a good idea of the level of detail on tests.

I would not hesitate to pick Prof Maywood if you are taking HIT 204. This is a HARD class with lots of reading and material. One research paper and forums and quizzes. The material itself is dense and difficult to remember. Prof Maywood's lectures are really good. In terms of an online class, thus far she is the best professor I have had.

I took the online Patho class. Prof Maywood is a good professor and clearly lays out what you need to do in order to do well in the class. If you have any questions, she will reply to your email within a day. Because this is an online class, if you want to learn then you will learn. If you don't want to learn, you can still get an A in this class.
Tough class. Clear grading criteria. One disease paper due. Tough tests

I would NOT say this class is easy. Maywood is very pleasant and responsive. Her lecture notes are very helpful for weekly quizzes. But to get an A, you have to do the weekly study questions using the book although you don't have to read every page. I put a lot of time into this class and got good results. Again, she's a very nice lady!
Linda is wonderful! She is helpful and will explain material you may not understand. You have to study, you SHOULD expect it! It is pharm. put in the effort you will enjoy the class and do well.

Online. Professor is extremely easy to talk to and considerate to students. Directions are clear and concise. 2 exams, weekly quizzes and write ups. One paper. Follow the directions and you'll do just fine. Great class for people who work full time too.

Her class was very easy. Quizzes and exams are easy. She responded my emails within a few days. Her lecture notes are not thoroughly, so you still need to read a textbook. However, you can get As on quizzes without studying hard because there are plenty of time to answer all questions.

Easy A, but time consuming! It's basically a self-study class. She provides BRIEF notes, so you still HAVE to read the book. You do 13 posts and 12 quizzes (2 lowest scores drops), 1 midterm, 1 essay, 1 final for 300 pts total. Use the book to look for answers. You will NOT learn much-- NOT a substitute pharm class for those in nursing programs!

I was nervous for this class, but love Professor Maywood's teaching style! If you study her lecture notes well, you should do fine on her quizzes. Do make sure to purchase the book though, as not all of the quiz answers are in the lecture notes. I personally utilized my book 30-50% of the time to get the information I needed for the quizzes.
pathophysiology sounded like it would be hard, but not with her! she makes this class so super easy. take her if you can!!!
great teacher. Great instructions easy to understand class is fairly easy if you study

Wow I'm shocked to see the bad reviews.I wouldn't say I enjoy talking in front of people, but I can confidently say that his class helped me a lot. Not only is he a good public speaker, but he presented handy tools and tricks . I will say that attendance is mandatory if you want to pass the class. Just do the speeches and you'll be chilling.

This class was great! If you show up to class and do the assignments you will pass the class for sure. This class took my confidence to a whole new level. The instructor is great and really cares about the subject and helping you improve.

I took Mr. Medel's course based off his good reviews. Don't buy into the hype. He is talking to hear himself speak not to teach you. Not only that, he is one of the most unprofessional teachers I have ever met- not in the fun way. He will never give you 100%, he will never let you out of class early even if he has finished lecture-unforgiving. 0/10

A very difficult person to listen to! It was incredibly hard not to visibly cringe during every class I had with him, for several reasons. On the second day of class he made very insensitive jokes about a classmate to the entire room and tried his best to play it off. Best of luck to anyone who ends up having to enroll!

I took Michael for COMM131 for three hours every Tuesday night. The first time I went to class I was thought "oh god this is gonna suck." BUT.... it didn't! Michael is an awesome teacher and his lectures are all super informative and actually helpful! Overall maybe one of the best classes I've ever taken. 10/10 would take again (:

This was a great class that I found to be super fun. He gives great feedback and makes the whole class feel kind of like a weird little family. His rubrics are clear and easy to understand so you always know what's expected of you.

I was so nervous for this class but Michael really makes you feel comfortable! As long as you show that you have put in work to improve & prepare for your speeches you'll get a good grade! This class taught me many valuable skills for public speaking including how to feel more comfortable & confident in front of a crowd! Great teacher!!

He is great for public speaking! Makes it super easy. Follow his guidelines, show up to class, give your input, and you'll have an A. The midterm was hard, but that's because I did not study at all. READ the book for your midterm... the only time you will need it. Otherwise follow his guideline, easy A. He is awesome, and very funny as well.

Great class. Mr. Medel is intelligent and will absolutely help you if you ask him. There are a lot of speeches and one written test so I would recommend not buying the text books and just using the ones at the library (didn't touch mine after the midterm). If you need to take COMM 131, take Medel.
Great Instructor and knows the subject very well. Be prepared to speak briefly about yourself on the first day. He is encouraging and understanding to new public speakers.

I was terrified to take public speaking, but it was mandatory for my major. Professor Medel made this class fun and enjoyable. He is so nice, helpful, and is a credible source for public speaking. He is a lenient grader, and as long as you read and prepare for your assignments, you will do well. He has made public speaking a lot easier for me!
Really Nice Teacher. I was really nervous about this class but he makes it fun and is a really good teacher. I improved a lot. As long as you go to class and read the text you will do fine.

He is a very good teacher. I had a large fear of public speaking and he makes class fun. He teaches the class so you feel comfortable with your classmates. You have quizzes every 2 weeks and as long as you do the reading or pay attention in class you will do fine.
No Comments
No communication. More than half ungraded at the end of Semester. Surprisingly tough grader.
Easy class and was super interesting. She had everything set up so easily and made it fun to do the lectures and homework assignments.

Disappointingly, this class lacks organization, a reliable grading system, or basic communication. Despite its potential for fun and interest, the instructor fails to update grades, provide study guides, or respond to emails. While she may be kind, her teaching style doesn't suit many students, myself include

She barely scrapes the surface, and when she goes deeper (off-track for her) she gets caught up and goes back to just scraping the surface. Would love to see her let herself go on tangents, it is obvious she is knowledgeable about the subject, she just plans her lectures too much and doesn't give herself freedom to talk about other things.

I loved her, I was suprised to see a lowish rating. She is super enthuisatic in lectures the hour or so went by so fast. Super easy to pass with a high grade. Highly suggest her class. Also our midterms were literally at the beach.
Stephanie Mendes is an amazing teacher. I loved her class, and I learned a lot.

This is an 8 week course, so there's only like 8 things in the grade book. ANYWAYS, California Geology (ERTH114) is a great way to learn about... California's geology. Mendes takes us on field trips on Friday all around central California and is really passionate about it. Take an in-person class with her if you can because she is just amazing.

The professor is a space cadet. Very flakey. Could not get the right room numbers for class, wrong meeting dates and times, wouldnt let us have national holiday, terrible disorganized teacher and impossible to follow in lectures too.

I took earth science as a fall late start with Stephanie and i found out it was actually a geology course but it was awesome we took field trips every Saturday and she is one of the coolest and most caring professors Ive ever had, she likes to hike on these trips do some cardio is recommended before you take her class 10/10 would Recomend
Best professor I have ever had. She inspired me so much I changed my major. I have taken most of her classes both online and in person. She is amazing.

Pre Covid? Easily the best teacher I ever had. Super passionate and hands on, her excited disorganization was apart of the charm. Then covid happened and the disorganization was horrible. We were expected to know things she hadnt explained yet and the workload tripled. Her bubbly personality didnt translate thru zoom, highly recommend in person
Im currently taking her Oceanography class online and Stephanie has a very structured online format and the class is very organized!! I highly recommend her as a professor

I took her Spring 2020, the semester that everything changed to online half way through the semester because of Coronavirus. Not great with the technology, but she was very understanding. She was amazing in the face-to-face setting. She gives two midterms and a final. Tests every few classes. Attend lecture and you'll be fine.

I took this class to fulfill my IGETC requirement. I thought it would be a straight forward and easy class, but now I find myself lost and behind. I feel like she needs to restructure the course to fit it into the scheduled class time (she very often goes over) and have lectures that clearly highlight the ideas that she expects us to know.

Stephanie is very passionate about her work and this is shown through her very lively, energetic lectures. If you come to class, you will pass with flying colors. Her lectures are interesting easy to understand. She is unorganized but an all around great person.

Throughout the year she never input any grades and when reaching out, she would not respond to emails. Eventually towards the end of the year, she announced that her computer was the issue and that it somehow inhibited her ability to release grades. There's a quiz every class but the catch is she only collects 6 so if you miss class u may be SOL

Stephanie is super knowledgeable about the material and makes it relevant and interesting. She does a great job explaining the material and if you come to class you will do fine on the exams, quizzes, and homework. It is an easy A if you go to every class. But she doesn't respond to emails and takes forever to put in grades and is very frantic.

Stephanie is passionate, but not a good professor. She is all over the place, constantly forgetting things, she doesnt really give homework and when she does its insanely out of our skill level. She also only gives a few quizzes and 2 exams- not scan tron. Also dont hold your breath for your grade. She has taken over 2 months to grade our midterms.

Stephanie is smart and passionate, but she is not the best professor. Rarely hw, few quizzes, and fill in the blank tests so you better know all the info. She is a very tough grader especially when it comes to spelling. She also takes FOREVER to grade tests! Its been 7 weeks since our midterm and still nothing. When asked she makes up excuses.
She is the worst lecturer I have ever seen but she grades like she actually teaches something important

Stephanie is down to earth and is more than willing to help you learn. She is passionate about her study and it comes across in her lectures. She may be fast paced at times, but is always available through email and her office hours. Stephanie is not only a great resource as a teacher but she is also a great friend!
I love Stephanie so much. She is very smart and knows her lecture very well. One of my favorite professor at SBCC.

Stephanie is a cool professor. She is super nice, gets to know most students on a personal level and really truly cares about her students. She is also very knowledgeable in her field and has some really cool field experiences. The material covered in class is interesting and not too hard. Her office is always open and she is always willing to help

Stephanie is an amazing teacher. She can seem unorganized when she gets really excited but she is alway more than willing to go through material thoroughly if you just ask. Her class is very easy if you come to class and pay attention. Go to the tutor sessions before every quiz and you'll get an A. Also, her labs are AWESOME!!

Stephanie Mendes is the energizer bunny of the Environmental Science Department. You can tell she has a true passion for teaching by her excitement over the course material the personal stories of her education and work experience she shares. She encourages students to be engaged in class. Take her if you want a teacher who truly cares!

Stephanie is a good teacher. She talks very fast and sometimes runs out of time for review sessions or some activities, but you can tell she is very passionate about the material she is teaching. She is super easy to talk to and offers lots of extra credit opportunities! Take advantage of them. Also try not to miss class!

Professor Mendes has been an absolute nightmare. She does not respond to emails and occasionally did not show up to scheduled office hours. She offers a fair amount of extra credit, but does not explain lecture material clearly. Always seems to be in a rush. Also, she was about 1 full month behind on grading assignments during the semester. Avoid.

Responds quickly to emails, super late grader, and uses turn it in for every assignment. Reminder: he doesn't provide your real grade once throughout the semester, turnitin doesn't reflect your real score. You have to calculate your own grade. I ended with a 97% in turnitin but got a b+ (87.75%)on my transcript because of his system.
save yourself, don't take his class.

Professor Metzger waits until the very end of the semester to grade all assignments. It is a week after our final is due and he has yet to grade more than half of the coursework I have completed. Workload is manageable, and assignments are somewhat interesting, but it sucks to not get any feedback throughout the semester.

The books given are a little hard to understand. Everything is on turnitin and he doesnt grade assignments. Its the end of the semester and I still don't know what my grade is. So far he's graded a few things but idk what I got on the rest of the stuff. But if you need help he's super understanding and sweet if reach out. Otherwise not bad.

I genuinely enjoyed taking Professor Metzger's class! His assignments were straightforward, his course well organized and the work load was fair. He is very understanding and it is clear he is passionate about his job, as well as his students.. If you put in the good effort, expect a good grade and good feedback.

Professor Metzger is the most caring teacher I have ever had. I took his class online and he never failed to offer extra help when needed and was super understanding anytime I was struggling on an assignment and needed extra time. He went out of his way to make sure the resources he provided were helpful and always gave great feedback on essays!

Very easy going, has his own textbook so its not accessible on sites. Only a couple written papers yet you can choose to not write one. He takes forever to upload grades but grades the 3 assignments you have weekly. Super easygoing and understanding.
He have very good and interesting lectures on material. I usually do not like English classes but he made it enjoyable. His grading offered lot of good feedback. Overall a very good English teacher.

Lot's of work! you have to want it in order to pass. His class isn't as difficult as it might sound. If you go to office hours, attend class, and turn in everything on time you should pass the class with a B or better! Also, Metzger tells you how it is so if you find yourself slacking off best believe he will let you know! Overall great teacher!

Professor Metzger is a great teacher.. He really does want you to pass and become a better writer. His feedback on papers is great but his explanation on the papers is very limited. You're pretty much on your own in this class. He's not that open minded. Take another Professor if you can. Overall great person but his class sucks.

Nice guy and seems to care about students, but really doesn't relate well. Hypocritical in that he expects 100% organization but is very slow in getting papers back, puts very little on moodle, and has extremely unclear expectations for papers. Very tough grader, but gives thorough feedback. Don't expect to do well without engaged participation.

Mr. Metzger was fantastic. He really genuinely cares to help his students pass. By no means is he easy, but if you show up every day and prove that you care about his class, he will make getting an A much easier. Don't slack off, and be sure you do the readings! If you don't you are in trouble for the quizzes!

Listen. PLEASE let me warn you against this professor. I hired an outside tutor at the beginning of the semester to prepare for this class. Never missed a class, was never late, turned in every single assignment. No notes are allowed for his tests, which are extremely hard. Studying is not enough. He failed me at a 69.7. Learn from my mistake.

While the homework problems can take quite a long time, they're meant to help you learn the content. The group quizzes are a nice since you can collaborate with your peers. Each exam has a practice exam which helps you gauge what will be on the exam. Great prof!

Miner is knowledgeable and fair, but consistently arrogant and sassy to students. Questions are often met by condescension rather than guidance. I take full responsibility for my grade. His rude and ungrateful demeanor creates an unwelcoming and discouraging learning environment. Don't push yourself to try and be friendly to him as he does not care

His lectures can depend on what mood he is , very straightforward and cold. If you're not very good at math be prepared to study a lot. Homework's are important to understand material and exams. Group quizzes once a week. Make sure to do review packets before exams, as exams can mean a lot between an A or C

I really enjoyed his class! There are quizzes at the beginning of class but they are collaborative, and the review quizzes before exams are too. He tests you on exactly what he has taught you and what is on the study guides.His canvas has his online lectures if you miss a class. He always keeps you updated on the schedule so you know what to expect
One of the best professors I have ever had, very nice, has more than sufficient knowledge about the topic and is very good at explaining topics.

The lectures are up to 2.5 hours with no break. You start class with a group quiz which is fine, and he grades fair. Although during his lectures he tends to get sidetracked. He's belittling and often very sassy when you answer a question. He makes you feel small and expects you to immediately understand calculus when it is a very difficult course.

He's a good teacher attendance in class is needed to do well as every class started with a short group quiz and there a homework once a week. It could be a lot of homework problems but his grading is far. Overall classes can be a lot of lecture, but he's a good teacher and if you work hard, you can do well.

Very lecture-heavy which goes on for so long (no break) and often gets sidetracked or goes on random tangents. Not very nice when you ask questions and can be a bit rude with his responses. He gives group quizzes weekly, often with 2 questions, and homework weekly. Exams are just like the reviews, review quizzes are done in groups as well.

Class is a regular Calc class and attendance is mandatory since something is due in class every day as well as a quiz every week. Dude is a good teacher and knows his stuff ,but an ahole. He in a way talks down to you and when you have a question tells you it's due to your poor algebra and doesn't answer your question.
Engaging lectures with real-life examples. Drops quizzes and homework grades, making the workload manageable. unstuck ai was a lifesaver for practice and exam prep. Would take this class again.

Minor is a very enthusiastic teacher and makes himself widely available to students. He seems to really care about the subject and taking his class makes you feel as if you really learned something. Exams and group quizzes are key so be sure to keep an eye out for test dates. Grading is fair and good grades are earned. Solid calculus teacher 10/10.

He was a good teacher and I would take him again. Almost every class started with a short group quiz and there a homework once a week. It could be a lot of homework problems, but he graded them very favorably. He dropped the worst 2 HW and quiz grades and had the final replace the worst exam. Homework is turned in person, not online.

The first week I talked to him and asked him for advice and said I was a little "scared" of taking his class since I knew calculus could be difficult. He told me I should be scared and discouraged me from learning. I immediately dropped. I'm not working with anyone that encourages that kind of math phobia. He made me feel small.

Professor Miner was born to be a math teacher. How he is able to repeatedly discuss the same exact problem with the same level of enthusiasm to student after student so that each is able to comprehend it is beyond me. If you want a straightforward calculus course that will ensure you know the material for later STEM courses, this is it.
Group quizzes very helpful. Doesn't go over material that should ahve been covered in other courses, so make sure you know your math.

Jason Miner has very straightforward teaching and very easy to understand. He's kinda a blunt and straightforward guy but he's hilarious at times. Very clear with his assignments and expectations. Was great for Math150. Lots of group quizzes that makeup your grade. Let us out early some days.

Professor Miner keeps it real. He is straight forward with his teaching style and incorporates lessons into real life news making it that much more important to learn. Drops two quizzes and two homeworks, and replaces lowest exam grade with the final exam grade. One of the best math teachers, but make sure you ask good questions if u tryna learn.

Funny guy. Has lectures on YouTube so you can watch on your own time. Splits the lectures up into different pages of his guided note packets, so you don't have to watch a 1hr lecture all at once. Super good at explaining how to do problems and defining terms, especially ones heard in the business world. Fast graders. Overall, GREAT professor.

Jason Miner is a GREAT calc professor! He makes math easier than most SBCC profs & his lectures are easy to follow! (Also, he is a funny guy.) He is always available during office hours & is on his email 24/5 if you need help! He gives weekly quizzes, but they are good at preparing you for exams. If (online) math is hard for you, take his class!!

Professor Miner is an amazing math professor. The class layout is super clear and organized, even online. Miner is a great teacher and gives you every tool/resource you would need to succeed in his class. I took him for both my calculus classes as I had such a good experience the first time and his classes yield genuine learning.

Miner is great and the class is relatively easy. Quizzes almost every class day. Easy points through homework, discussions, and review quizzes. Drops two lowest quizzes and homeworks. Replaces one test grade with final, if better. Genuinely funny. He is very organized and accessible. Prepares you well for exams. Overall, he is super great!

Loved his class! Despite being completely online, he did a great job with online lectures and makes the work understandable. He is also quite funny and cracks a few jokes during lectures and makes them enjoyable. Has weekly quizzes so you are on track and understand the material. Has study guides that are exactly like the test. Def recommend!

Hands down the one of the worst teachers I have taken during this pandemic. Hes obviously better in person from what I observed in previous reviews, but online he is terrible. He keeps giving me low grades and when I ask him why because I did the hw with a tutor he gives me very vague answers or no answer at all.

I am coming back 4 years later to give this review. Miner is the best math teacher I have ever had in the 12 years of public school, as well as the 5 years it took me to get my Math degree from UCSB. I am so glad I took him when I was at SBCC. He is quite literally the best teacher I've ever had in my entire life. Do your work and you'll pass.
Show up to class and pay attention, and do the homework each week and you will do well. Funny guy, and he was great when we had to switch online.

Great professor. He gives you so many materials, more than needed, to do well in his course. Super smart and will give you good lectures. You definitely have to work in his class but he provides a great foundation for all students to succeed.

I had a blast in Miners class this semester, even though we had to transition to online after the whole outbreak, he was still very caring and patient. Great professor, group quizzes every week! Exams are just like the review quizzes so study those at least twice!

Decent teacher. Does do +/- grades so beware of that. Attendance isn't mandatory, but he does collect HW once a week and gives in-class quizzes (2 lowest grades dropped for both). Note: The other Math220 teacher, Gabe, does not do +/- and does not require a textbook.

This is my first semester to study math. He is literally an interesting guy. Sometimes he is strict, but his lectures are clearly. One thing I want to mention is there are many homework have to do. Taking your notes and study, you will get good grades. If I have a chance, I better take his class again.

Josh is genuinely the best professor I've had throughout college. He engages heavily with students and jokes back and fourth, if you enjoy talking sitting in the front is the move. Great explanation of journalism and journalistic values and the big assignments are very enjoyable coverage of current events. wish he offered higher courses to take.
Josh is one of the best teachers I have ever had. He's funny, nice, understanding, and caring. We also get taught based on first-hand experience he goes through as a journalist. 100% recommend.

In J101, you learn from the best and get real world-experience. You must go to class to understand the course material. Josh loves what he teaches, and he creates a positive classroom environment by interacting with his students. Don't procrastinate but put time and effort into your work.

His class was so much fun. He takes time to get to know the students and what makes them individually special. The work consisted of writing various journalism stories, which was enjoyable. You get to go out in the world and do real reporting. He is so caring and fun, and it's clear that he has a genuine passion for journalism, which shines through
Be prepared to be engaged the whole time.

Molina is super fun and really good at engaging with students. He will pick on you no matter how quiet you are. The assignments are fun and you learn a lot about journalism so it's important to show up to class and pay attention, if you do that you will get an A. -ME

Professor Molina is keen on having back and forths with students. His course is based on in-class lectures and out of class events to cover and write about, so I wouldn't recommend it to those with poor mobility or emergency prone. I found the course educational and not too difficult. -Samuel Gonzalez

One of the best teachers I've ever had, class is mandatory but if you make sure to do your work and come to class you will see the results. Not a very hard class and gives you an idea of what journalism is really aboit

Joshua Molina is an AMAZING teacher. He is SUPER FUNNY and makes sure that every student feels like they belong. The homework load is actually pretty light and not difficult. He is one of the best teachers I've ever had and I strongly recommend him to anyone who wants to learn about or study journalism. I had a BLAST in his class!

As long as you make an effort and show you care about what you are working on, Josh will help you get to the next step. He likes to include everyone and won't let you get away with not doing work but in a caring way. I'm glad I took his course.
Goofy. Knowledgable. Fun. Easy going.

Professor Molina is funny, approachable, and passionate about journalism. His attempted inside jokes and engaging teaching style create a welcoming atmosphere. He illustrates concepts with anecdotes, making learning enjoyable. Molina's really enthusiastic and wants to connect with the students. Just show up to class and you're assured an easy A.

Josh Molina is by far one of the best professors I've ever had. Not only was he hilarious and personable in class, but we really formed a connection outside of the classroom that I hope to sustain through adulthood. He truly cares about his students in a way that's unmatched by others. 100% take this class, if only to hang out with Molina!

Josh is a funny passionate professor and he's exactly what you want if you're looking to walk away with more than a passing grade. He will make sure that you know that he knows your name and always keeps class fun and engaging with light-hearted jokes and banter. As long as you engage honestly with the course material, you should have no trouble.

Journalism 101 with Professor Molina was easy and super fun. If your interested in Journalism, Molina is great at engaging your interest further. Engagement and attendance matters in this class. He's nice and is a lenient grader. If you are engaged and turn your assignments in on time, this class is an easy A!

Mr. Molina was one of the best professors that I've had. You can tell that he's really knowledgeable and passionate about the subject, which makes it easier to learn. He tries to be relatable to students, which is hilarious, and his "lectures" are very conversational. He is a super fair grader and is understanding with make-up assignments.

Molina is a good teacher and knowledgeable on all things journalism. But his people skills... basically nonexistent (saw him make 2 different students cry on separate occasions.) Be sure not to contradict or inject your own opinions on any subject he lectures on. DONT expect to meet outside class 1-on-1 have him explain concepts to you personally

Prof Molina is one of the best teachers and mentors I've ever had! He takes a sincere interest in each student's growth and wants to see you do your best. He expects a lot from you so his class can be intense, but in the end, I was so grateful he pushed me so hard. If you work your hardest, you will get so much out of his classes.

I personally had a fantastic time in class, and learned a lot about the fundamentals of journalism while making great friends. Josh jokes around pretty heavy, but is serious once it comes to the work. He makes the assignments fun and they dont feel challenging if you take notes and enjoy writing. Dont be late and expect to participate.

He has a tendency to pick on certain people in class. If you are on the slower side of the learning spectrum be prepared to be messed with. He is very passionate about what he teaches and clearly loves reporting. Also constructive criticism is not his strong suit. I would describe it more as just blunt criticism.

Can see why people rated him negatively if not a JOURN major or shy. Likes to joke with/roast his students especially if you leave class often, don't show up, don't do assignments or if he thinks you have great potential :) Passionate about journalism and those who feel the same. I had a lot of fun with his assignments because it's my career goal!

He started off really awesome, but throughout the semester he became increasingly more rude. We had a kid in my class who might have had a social disability, and I guess Molina lost his patience because he was very mean to this kid. Telling him to be quiet and such. Overall good teacher, but is very particular about what he wants.

Instead of making someone feel dumb about not understanding something, how about you try to just simply educate them instead. Picks on everyone. If you seem on the less brighter side come prepared to get picked on. He grades easy just do the work. Treats classes like its high school. Marked me absent when I signed in cause I left class 20min early.

Professor Molina is one of the best teachers I've ever had. His grading style is very straight forward (never tries to trick you on anything). If you go to class and engage in the work you will get an A. He is a very passionate and knowledgeable journalist. You will learn so much!! He is also funny, which makes class that much more interesting.

Not recommended for non-journalism majors or those not passionate about the subject. I discovered he had lost a solid amount of my turned in work halfway through the semester. He hadn't documented any of it and although I tried working with him to straighten this out and felt confident I had- I ended up with a D in the course. Ridiculous

Josh Molina is seriously amazing. One of the best teachers I've ever had. He really knows the subject and makes lectures fun. Do ALL the homework, and pay attention. I've learned so much in this class! He makes journalism so fun and interesting. Take the class, you won't be disappointed.

Molina is awesome! He's funny, approachable, interactive and knows what he's talking about. He provides students with very practical knowledge and skills regarding journalism, and draws from many of his own experiences which is helpful too. Take him!

Molina is one of the best teachers I have had at CC. Super nice and knows what he is doing. Pay attention and participate in his class and you will do fine. Definitely take him if your interested in journalism!
Mr. Molina is a great teacher. He cares and he will work with you when you need help or don't understand what he wants you to do. Thanks for being so awesome!!!
Best teacher I've had at city college. Just a really cool guy, easy to talk to and a great teacher. Homework is minimal. Perfect for aspiring journalists

I took her last summer and I liked her a lot! yes! a lot of essays and typing! (because i took her in the summer and it goes by fast!) I was also struggling because i was pregnant and was getting morning sickness often! really nice outstanding teacher! but i passed! she grades by P: Pass or N: No pass

Mrs. Kathy Molloy was an amazing professor. She genuinely cares about each student individually. There are about 5 papers to write, but there are many options on the topics. She gives great feedback and is always available for extra help outside of class. She was my absolute favorite professor. TAKE HER!
Molloy is one of the best teachers ever! She lets you revise all your essays, accomedments with you If needed overall a great teacher

Truly cares about you and your grade, but her assignment structure can be confusing, which is why I wouldn't take her again. Expect 4 to 5 essays, reading/annotating, and group discussions. She gives great feedback and is understanding about late or missing work, but her essay process wasn't for me. Someone said she gives off mom vibes and I agree.

Professor Monda is by far the best English teacher I have ever had. She gives off mom vibes and is the kind of person that you don't want to disappoint. She cares about her students success and extends deadlines. I was failing a majority of the semester but she helped me to pass the class with an 89% within the last 4 weeks of school. Best teacher.
Professor Monda was amazing! She is very kind and considerate, and always willing to help her students out. The assignments aren't too tough but it can pile up if you decide to procrastinate.

TAKE HER COURSE!!! I loved Prof. Monda, she provides such good feedback and is such a kind and inspirational teacher. She is super accommodating and will give you extra time on assignments, especially if you need it :)

Professor Monda is super sweet and caring. I loved her class, it was manageable and interesting and helped me become a better writer. There were weekly reading and easy writing assignments. Total of 5 papers, including the final.

Professor Monda is one of the best professors I've ever had! I took her Shakespeare class, and I'm not a huge fan of that type of reading but she's very funny and made it entertaining. Very caring and knowledgable and provides helpful feedback. Also very accessible outside of class and really works with you to make sure you succeed! Take her class!

Prof. Monda is amazing! I've taken 3 ENG classes with her. There is lots of reading and required homework based on the reading, and two papers in the class. She can go off on tangents sometimes, but they're usually relevant to the topic at hand. She really cares about her students, and will give you an extension if you ask and have a good reason.

Professor Monda is easily the best English teacher I have had. I had her for two semesters, she is passionate about the subjects, teaching, and her students. The care she puts in is genuinely admirable. She has built-in deadlines for papers that really make it so you're not writing the essay the night before it's due. Such an incredible teacher.

Monda is one of the most passionate and fun English teachers you will ever have. If you're an English major the skills she teaches you will make every other English class a million times easier. That being said her classes are not easy. The reader is dense and she actually makes you think about the material

Professor Monda is a diligent and kind. I went into her class really not excited about Shakespeare but in the end found that Monda made Shakespeare interesting. I have had her for 111 as well and really like her in that class as well. Get ready to work, but it will reward in a good grade!

I took this class last semester and it was awesome. I only really did it because Monda was the prof and I knew I already liked her. We read a lot of Shakespeare's plays and had to write essays on some. They were pretty straight forward and she always gives chances to redo essays to improve!! Highly recommend her no matter the class!!!

I took Monda's ENG 110H class and it amazing. She is very understanding and will teach you a lot while still keeping the class fun and entertaining. We had 5 papers to write and had a chance to redo most of them for a better grade. There were no tests and the homework was pretty easy and had clear instructions. I highly recommend her course!!

Professor Monda is so diligent with giving detailed feedback to literally ALL of your writing. She cares so much about helping you succeed, making you a better writer and all around student. I fell behind, and reached out(SO BEYOND HAPPY I DID)she helped me through the rest of the semester. I wouldn't have made it if it wasn't for her.A true gem!

Really tried to jam a lot of work into a short amount of time. Lot of work that she grades very in depth whether it is to your benefit or not. A very nice person but very to the book with how she runs her course. Did adapt somewhat to the pandemic but did nothing to lower the load then.

This class is EXTREMELY organized and what is expected is clear. It is a ton of reading, annotating, and writing. There are a ton of papers and pre-writing. It is worth it though! You will come out a better writer, thinker, and be prepared for future papers in other classes. She gives detailed feedback on your work in a constructive way.

This class is hard. I got an A in AP Lit, AP Lang, and ENG111, but a B+ in this class. There is SO MUCH homework and it is very easy to get behind. Expect at least 3 long homework assignments/week and an essay due every few weeks. The second you turn in an essay your next paper's rough draft is practically due. Lectures are too long and so boring.

It is not an easy A, but that is the point of education. You have to work hard in her class, but you will grow tremendously. She is the most involved professor I have ever had as an online teacher. She works SO HARD for her students. She gives amazing feedback, and is caring and thoughtful. If you care to learn, you will 100% benefit from her class
Worse teacher ever for an online course doesnt make any sense on the work that is provided. Also makes you do an outline when other teachers dont for English 110.

This class is hard but Monda is great. She wants you to do well and you will want to do well. Take this class if you want to be a better writer. The skills I learned in this class have carried over to every writing assignment I have had to do since. So worth it. Be ready for a lot of homework and a lot of papers but she is always available to help.

Monda is a really knowledgeable professor with amazing writing skills. We have 4 required essays and I am such a better writer because of it. Her office hours are super helpful, she'll practically write an outline for you. As long as you submit the homework on time and work hard for your essays, you will pass, for sure!

Expect to have a heavy load of homework every night with Monda. The best English teacher I have ever had without a doubt. She really cares about developing her students as better writers and it shows. You can rewrite 2 papers for a better grade. You have to get a 94% though to get an A as a 93 is graded as a B+ (it is like that for every letter)

Professor Monda is a great teacher and I would take her again. There are 4 papers and an in class essay for the final. Essays are back to back so be prepared to write a lot. She gives great feedback and wants her students to succeed. She is very clear with her directions. Overall I would take her again

If you're an English major I would stay away from her classesespecially if you have an essay writing style that works for you. It is her way or the highway when it comes to essay writing. She admits to wanting to mold her students into writing essays the way she writes essays. Reading heavy and writing heavy, tough essays and tough exams.
Defiantly take this class. She is an amazing professor and wants everyone to succeed.

Professor Monda is one of my favorite professors! Se really cares about getting her students to the next level. She gives great feedback. She has a lot of assignments but they are easy. take her class, you wont regret it!

Dr Monda is a tough grader but is by far the best and most helpful english teacher I've ever had. May not have been the easiest class but was worth taking with her because she made me a better writer in a few months than all other english teachers I've had. She really knows what she is doing and cares about her students. Hard work but worth it

Taking ENG 89, 110, 120 with Dr. Monda helped me to be a better reader, writer, thinker & graduate with A. She is a wise professor who makes students succeed in their education & life. I recommend you to take her classes & you will be satisfied 100 %. I thank my professor Monda for her high standards and the system she uses to teach her students.

The class is a lot of hard work, but honestly worth it. You'll do fine as long as you read and do the assignments. Study hard for the midterm and final. Monda genuinely wants you to improve as a writer. Classes are discussion based around the text.

Lot of reading involved but a solid class anyways. Very difficult with the sheer amount of reading you have to deal with but she's an inspirational teacher and is really dedicated to her job. Clear to see she loves what she does and will certainly help you anyways she can if you ask. Would recommend if you have the motivation and REALLY love lit.

I recommended to take Professors Monda English 110/120 class. She is very understanding that we have things going on outside of school and wants every student to pass and be successful. Her work is not hard as long as you put in the effort and she is always available during office hours for help! TAKE THIS CLASS, trust me, you will pass!

This professor is very passive aggressive. He has a very confusing way of teaching. Everything is due mid week, and the instructions are very unclear. There is a lot of work and a lot of the assignments are super repetitive. The material isn't tough to understand but he makes it really hard to learn. I would take it with different professor.
Took this class for a GE requirement. Not the most enthusiastic about history but Dr. Mooney made learning fun! I would absolutely recommend this class to anyone thinking about signing up!
I took history 101 and 102, he is really fast at responding and super helpful. He gives extensions no questions asked and he does give a lot of work but the work is easy. Definitely take this class!

I was stressed with how much work we had at first, but overtime I realized how much he really cares about the subject and how much I was learning. It's a lot of reading, but you can get away with just finding what you need. Mooney is such an understanding professor, and you can get extra credit on every assignment if you turn it in early!

Speaks to students with contempt. Very tough grader with confusing grading criteria. Makes one think about the dynamic of their own intellect through belittling feedback on assignments that revolve around his own textbook to an unlikable degree.

The only good thing about Mooney is that he is available at practically any time to give feedback. Although, that being said his feedback is hard to follow and when you try to follow it he gets frustrated and seemingly belittles you for doing it wrong even though his feedback is often unclear. Not only that, but his class feels like a self promo.

I had a wonderful experience with Dr. Mooney's online history course. He leaves great feedback and always leaves comments. He emails between 12-1am to say that he has graded all the homework submitted by midnight. I find it amazing how much he dedicates his time to his students. He even prefers to communicate with text so he can reply more quickly.
He was a very lenient teacher and I highly recommend taking this class.
Prof Mooney has a lot of random rules for his assignments which make them difficult, especially due to him deducting points if you miss one of the many he has.

very easy class. if you have any questions you can ask him he will respond, sometimes a few days but always he will respond. always accomodates extra students, I joined late and he extended all the missed assignments a few weeks. you can choose how long to extend, he will accomodate. easiest class and most understanding I have had from a class ever

TAKE THIS CLASS. Such an amazing professor, wrote the textbook himself and his own videos. I took this class online and he genuinely made me learn so much and think deeper about what I know. Very doable workload and super organized class. Same format of work every week. He takes late work and has a lot of extra credit. No exams or papers.

Great professor and a even greater person! I had to drop for personal reasons, but if you need to take a history class, take this one. Definitely buy into his teaching methods and you will learn a lot, and have some fun doing it

You need to read a lot but the assignments themselves are very self-explanatory, they're in the same format every week so you'll get used to it quickly. He replies to phone messages fast but sometimes his answers can be quite vague. He is extremely generous with grading and gives extra credits for every assignment as long as you follow instructions

Mr. Mooney is very clear in his expectations in the class, he always responds to assignments and emails within 24 hours which I appreciate. There is extra credit offered with every assignment and there is ample amount of time to complete assignments each week.

Great class if you're willing to put in the work. Lots of reading but manageable essays. Use unstuckstudy and other study tools to stay on top of it. Professor is understanding and responsive. Overall, an enjoyable and rewarding experience.

He has a very specific outline for what you need to do to be successful in the class but if you read all the material and the syllabus you'll be fine. There is a lot of reading and writing but his discussions were interesting and were helpful to get your assignments done. I almost dropped it but theres just a large learning curve. Overall great.

Dr. Mooney's online class has a specific ruleset that seems overwhelming at first, but once you get it down to your weekly routine, it became an easy A for you. He's also super open to communication, even providing his phone number to all the students at the start of the class. The most important things are time management and self-discipline.

Mooney is one of the best professors at SBCC! His assignments are super straight forward and there's so many extra credit opportunities. Sometimes the assignments can feel repetitive but the content is great and I learned so much! I really recommend taking this class.

I haven't had a more caring professor. I explained my situation & he was incredibly accommodating. His lectures are interesting. it's easy to do well, do the readings & assignments. My grade is the result of issues on my on behalf but trust me he is amazing. I never give ratings but he went above and beyond my expectations. Ask for help & he will!

take advantage of the extra credit opportunities in this class. take good notes during class meetings, and while you read the assigned pages in the textbook. putting in a lot of time and effort will make a huge difference for your success in this class.

I finished the class with over 100% in both 101 and 102. Fire professor if you aren't lazy and are willing to actually turn things in. Mooney has very straightforward grading criteria. As long as you meet the requirements and do the assignments, you've got an A. If you've got academic anxiety, take this class. Super straightforward + extra credit!

By far the worst professor Ive ever had, all his assignments are just copy pasted, so he doesn't have to do anything. If you ask for assistance there's no chance you'll receive any from him. Would not take a class with this professor unless it is your VERY last resort.

I took his asynchronous summer course. Textbook is necessary for every assignment and there's a lot of reading but I would still recommend this class. I generally don't like history class but I enjoyed this one. His class is extremely well-structured and as long as you follow the criteria, you'll get an A. Also gives feedback and extra credit.

Took his summer class, super interesting but a lot of work and a lot of reading, which is required if you want to be able to succeed in this class But overall a fantastic teacher who really cares about his students

If you need to fulfill your history requirements, TAKE THIS CLASS! Professor Mooney has extremely clear grading criteria. As long as you follow the requirements and do the work, you will earn an A in this class. Good class for people with academic anxiety since he throws no curveballs as long as you keep up with the assignments, you will succeed.

As long as you're able to construct a decent, short essay (~300 words) every week, and can manage your time well, this class is an easy A. The way this class is structured is you write 3 short essays, based on the assigned chapter and your classmates work, every week. If you're unable to turn your work in on time, he offers extension forms.

Worst professor I have ever had, not only is he cold-hearted he is not compassionate whatsoever. The assignment load is miserable and he has no ounce of kindness while grading. His responses are rude and degrading. Would not recommend it to anyone, run as far as you can from this course and be prepared for a tough workload and an attitude.

The most well-structured class I have ever taken. Professor Mooney does an outstanding job with all assignments, and they are tailored for each student. ZERO tests or quizzes. It may seem overwhelming at first but you will quickly and easily adjust. Professor Mooney is by far the best professor I have ever had. Follow the criteria and you get 100%.
This professor is great and is always on top of grading and answering emails. If you submit something he always will have it graded within about 12 hours.

Took this course online and he was amazing at teaching online. Instructions were very clear and there was no confusion about the grading process, the assignments, or due dates. He sends lots of notifications through canvas though which can seem a tad overwhelming.
3 assignments a day, 3 midterms and a final. Teacher does a good job in lectures at explaining, but put in 2x speed it'll make a huge difference. Tutors are always available and helpful.
Gives 20+ hours of homework a week even on thanksgiving. Not helpful with curving or giving extra credit. Bad communication def would not take again

This class has an insane workload, at least 3 assignments 5 days a week, BUT professor Bronwen allows for all of those assignments to be retaken on zoom with tutors for a 100% which saves your grade. She also drops a lot of your lowest grades at the end. The exams aren't hard if you've been keeping up with the lectures. Take with her!!!

the comments saying she cares about her students are lies. her summer class requires 45 hours a week just to pass the class more than that for a good grade. what kind of teacher does that she says in the syllabus you can't pass if you have a full time job and she assigns 4 assignments each day that are all the exact same and just a waste of time.

I took 21 credits last semester two 6 credit math classes and this summer class took much more time then all those classes combined. She expects 8+ hours a day of work most days its more like 10+ hours her lectures are terrible her style of teaching is terrible don't take it unless its a 16 week course. The stuff is easy to do but takes so long

You are going to want to self harm if you take this over the summer. 4.5 week class with about 12-15 assignments monday through friday. each assignment can take from 10 minutes to an hour and about 4 of the assignments each day are lectures that are an hour long. I had to pay $250 for a proctor because she requires you find one within 3 days

Her online class has a lot of homework, but it's not hard, and the videos overexplain everything in the homework. There are three midterms and a final, but she gives practice midterms that are very similar to them. Take her if you have time for at least an hour a day of work.

Be ready for a workload, but if you can manage your time well it's totally doable. She and tutors are available nearly every day for help, she's very accommodating and understanding, and the material is easy. Overall I really enjoyed her class and she makes stats fun!

This class was the only reason I was able to pass math, and it's the first time I ever earned an A! Bronwen is an amazing teacher, and her lectures are incredibly clear. You absolutely need to complete every assignment, but if you do, you'll understand everything you need to know to get an A.

Nice professor, she cares about her students and gives out a lot of office hours to work on things. However, don't take this class if you want to have a life. 3-5 assignments a day that take a couple hours to complete. If you get one assignment behind, good luck. It takes a while to catch up. There are finals every month.

I'm taking this class online over the summer and the workload is unbearable. She assigns 8-15 assignments every night and they are so repatitive and not useful. You have to take all notes by hand while following along her hour long lectures. It's impossible to do anything while taking this class because the assignments are easily 4+ hours a day.

Bronwen cares so much about her students success. Be ready to dedicate a lot of time to this class. Going to the zoom tutoring sessions makes a huge impact, they are there to help you!! Keep up with the work, put in effort, ask questions and you will be fine!

I took her summer class and it was not a good time! She gave us 7 assignments everyday for the 8 weeks duration. She would give us an exam every ten days equaling to 5 in total including the final. If you miss a day you will fall behind!!! She gave us as much assignments as she would a fall semester class would get. DO NOT RECOMMEND

This is a very demanding and time-consuming class, be aware of that. I wish the lectures were more concise & I recommend watching on 2x speed. However, despite the reviews, Bronwen does care about her students and wants them to succeed. She offered online help with tutors every day of the week, and many assignments had no time or attempt limit.

When I approached Professor Moore regarding having night classes and a full time job, limiting me to working on the 3+ hours a night homework from 7 days to 4 days, she brushed it off and said that means that you have to use 5+ hours on the nights you can and then you can still get it done. If you miss assignments, you WILL fall behind.

If you need credit for an early education degree, take this class. Her coloring and storytelling skills are unmatched! In all seriousness, her notes are more like a coloring book and extremely unhelpful on tests.

If you are working a full time job or taking more than 12 credits, I would not recommend this class. If you take it, expect to have 3 hours of homework every night, including weekends. Also, there is little to no compromise on date regarding midterms and finals. Class is structured to the traditional "learning process." Be ready to sacrifice time.

If you have ANY other responsibilities like work, kids, family, other classes, do not take this class. She has homework due EVERY day. You have to watch her YouTube videos that are AT LEAST 1 hour every day. You have to take notes on her video because you have to scan notes and submit them daily. WAY too much work.

Math has never been a subject I did well in, but Bronwen will go out of her way to make sure you understand the material and do well in the class. She does go through the material really slowly though and assigns a lot of homework. But as long as you show up to class and do the work, you will pass. She also drops a lot of low scores at the end.

She has one of the biggest hearts of anyone at SBCC. She really cares about her students and student learning. It's a difficult course to take during 6 weeks of summer. You're often watching videos for an hour or two every day to cover the material. Probably much more effective as a regular semesters class when you have more time.

Bronwen is a lovely woman. She has great beliefs a good sense of humor, and genuinely cares and wants her students to succeed. This course is a LOT of work though, approx 36 hours a week. If you fall behind it is hard to catch back up, and it's certainly hard to balance. Lectures are an hour a piece (about 3 a day) and concepts can be tricky.

Ms. B is a pretty good teacher, but not very understanding when it comes to missing class. If you miss even 1 class your great will go down and you will be so lost. Also when in class you usually do not finish the worksheets and will have to do them at home for homework.

I don't even know why these reviews are so positive. Ms. B's class is so slow, tedious, and a waste of time. Lectures are incredibly boring. She talks for hours about information irrelevant to the class. She assigns an absurd amount of work for what the class is. Prepare to spend 10+ hours for this class. Assignments/tests are beyond frustrating.

This class was the first time in my entire life that math has ever made so much sense. Ms B is kind, funny, and doesnt make you feel horrible if youve made a mistake or are confused. Shes very easy to talk to and makes you feel comfortable enough to be honest and ask for help! If youve struggled in math before, take Ms. B! Shes the best!!!
Very passionate about her students and shes hilarious.

I really like Bronwen. She's inspiring, kind, smart, and caring. Yes she's a teacher, but she's so much more than that. She really takes her time to care for her students. Also she explains the math really well to where it's not just memorization. Take her class, she's cool. All quizzes can be retaken. Average quiz grade replaces your worst exam.

She is such a sweet teacher she cares about her students, She stays after class to help you do your homework and really explains the material well. Just put effort in to the class and it will pay off.

The best teacher I have had in my life. You won't have to take time to get to know her because she will put that work in herself. She cares about her students like I've never seen from another teacher. Plus she lets you retake quizzes and then replace your quiz grade with your worst exam grade. She makes success very accessible to those who seek it

I took Bronwen's class a couple years ago, but it still is the most valuable class I've ever taken. She teaches more than just math - she taught me how to study, how to prepare for exams. More than anything, she had confidence in me when no one in my life ever did. I am still so thankful for everything I learned in that class.
Bronwen is an amazing math professor. She has different teaching techniques that suits everyone's learning type. She truly cares for all her students. Nicest math professor you will EVER have.

Im in his Fin-101 class all online and i love it. We use McGraw Hill for homework and activities and the activities are very helpful with the course material. If he notices you lacked on one assignment he will reach out to make sure you understand the material and will let you have another chance at the assignment. Very informational and helpful!
very straightforward class. Very clear with expectations. There is a lot of assignments that require a lot of research and writing. Only complaint is that I wish there was a more time to do them.

I took this class online as I can't get to campus and we have a physical journal we need to maintain that is worth a lot of points. She refused to accept digital journals and, if we can't get to campus, we had to ship the journal to her. I'm already paying to take quizzes through Mcgraw and now I have to pay to turn in my homework. Awful experience

I took Kathy's class thinking it would be easy vs other classes. WRONG! She assigns so much homework it interfered with my other classes. Every module in her canvas is disorganized. And be prepared to take WRITTEN exams on that information specifically. Lastly, required $80-$90 Mcgraw Hill Textbook. Need I say more?

This teacher is terrible to say the least. She assigns so much homework, I guess assigning so much homework is supposed to be beneficial. somebody needs to remind her this class is optional. Professors like these think we are robots, being a good teacher isn't synonymous with assigning so much work. This is community college.

Completely unorganized. Everything is all over the place she made simple things so difficult and was curious to know why are we doing several group projects for a health class??. Felt very similar to English class. wouldn't recommend. Greatly impacted my work flow for other classes.

Doing way more then the usual health class should need impacted my other classes significantly and as a class i was expecting the least out of she made things extremely difficult and everything is completely unorganized with work flow and syllabus. Why are we doing several group projects for a health class??????

I liked kathy as a person outside of class, but in class she is very straight forward and doesn't take anybody's crap so she can be tough at times. Everyday coming to class I wasn't looking forward to coming to this class but I'll say im glad I met kathy because she really is just a tough lover that's all.

AWFUL DONT TAKE THIS CLASS I had done great in school till I took this class IT'S HORRIBLE I read reviews before taking it and still gave her a chance worst decision ever don't waste your money or time
work is very confusing, doesn't seem tech savvy AT ALL, takes for ever to reply
Just don't take her online classes. I don't know if she is just as bad in person as online but I suggest you avoid her.

She is the worst teacher I have ever had. The workload is ridiculously a lot, and most of the assignments are there to waste your time. Or to divert your attention away from the assignments that actually matter. She gives awful and vague advice, she is far from being tech savvy. we literally have to mail our final papers in. Not e-mail... mail

She is beyond archaic with how she sets up her class. Her canvas page is EXTREMELY disorganized, she is a very hard grader, and she gives a large homework load. To sum it all up, I spent more time figuring out what I was supposed to do vs actually doing what I was supposed to do. Awful teacher, I would HIGHLY recommend not taking her class.

This is the most difficult class I have EVER taken. The class is filled with huge assignments that you don't get an introduction to or barely any reminders about. There is so much reading in very short periods of time and it is just very unreasonable. The amount of work that she assigns doesn't allow any time for other classes or free time.

Literally the hardest teacher I have ever had in my life. She is not clear on anything and makes everything so much more difficult than it has to be. I would never recommend her to anyone. Im not good at technology and she expects you to be so advanced in technology use.

I was VERY hesitant to take Kathy because of her reviews here, but I think shes one of the best teachers Ive had at SBCC. The assignments were laid out in advance, easy to follow, and she was always responsive and nice if I had questions. 3 runs (or jogs, biking, etc) a week, a goal assignment, and short answers quiz at the end of the semester.

Kathy is a fine teacher but my god please don't take this class if you don't have to. The homework load is ABSOLUTELY RIDICULOUS. Im talking 7 hours a week if you don't procrastinate. This level class should not have this much work. Long essay questioned tests(35q), two book reports, group presentation, journal, on TOP of 7 hours of assigned HW.

it's a waste of time, it is spilt into four units, but each one has like 20 things to do. her exams are short essays questions and they are proctored, which is ineffective for short essay responses. Some of the data and information she provides is outdated. I didn't find it helpful or educational

Kathy is a great professor. If you follow the syllabus and complete the assignments, you will get a good grade. She is short with emails but wants you to succeed in the class. Honestly one of the easiest college courses I've taken.

Ive taken professor OConners class twice now because I dropped the first time. Her tests are very long and tedious; mostly short answer. She says clearly where to get the information but Ive still had to look most of it up instead. She rarely gives feedback on how you can improve. Also, some assignments and due dates are NOT clear.

HORRIBLE. I took Kathys class and it was a terrible experience. She is so so rude and gives unclear directions, not to mention when you try to email and ask for clarification she just asks you to read the directions, and complains/criticizes when it is submitted incorrectly. Dont bother taking her class she isnt fully invested into her work.

Worst online class I've ever taken. The professor doesn't keep your grades updated at all throughout the semester so you have no idea how you're doing. She sends a ridiculous amount of homework that is worth only 6 points (4-6 hrs a week)! She expects a lot from her students but gives very little. Feels like teacher vs. students.

DO NOT TAKE this is by far the worst teacher I have ever been in contact with. Im going to report her to the school as well. She is very disorganized and extremely rude to ALL her students. You ask for help with an assignment or a question and she makes you feel so stupid and makes fun of you. I wonder why she even became a teacher.

Worst teacher Ive ever had. SAVE URSELF. exams are LONG & all essay questions, no MC. The homework load is cruel - treats the class like its the only one her students are in. Way too much work with bad explanation for an intro health GE class. Also super disrespectful to students - she will give insults and swear at you. AVOID AT ALL COSTS

This class is not set up properly for an online class. We actually have to mail in our journals and book report. Many of this can and should be done online. We have to finish assignments earlier just to be able to mail and she can receive on time. Online quizzes are a lot of work for only 6 points. These take 2-3 hours per chapter.

some piece of advice for Kathy would be to knock down a few of the chapters, how is the tests due before the chapters? that's proof in the pudding right there that most student's aren't even able to finish the chapter's before the tests meaning most aren't prepared for the tests. Knock down the homework a good amount and your class would be better

Gives a ton of homework and makes a 5 credit class feel like a 10 credit class the chapters are SUPER long and SUPER fast paced in my opinion one of the harder classes. DO NOT take this class if you are not ready to spend at least 15 hours a week on it.

Worst teacher I've had at SBCC so far. Extremely rude and judgmental of her students. She treats us like garbage, especially if we don't know the answer to something, or get and answer wrong when trying to participate. She announces assignments or tests last minute and barely explains what we have to do.
Kathys class is super easy and a super fun class! the info is really useful! She literally gives you the answers to the tests but you have to go to the review sessions. Super chill class.

Kathy is such a sweet person! She gives you a planned out class schedule and you have weeks to complete the quizzes online. You'll have to do a few book reports, but again she gives you plenty of time. HW is usually due the next class but it is fairly easy. Highly recommend Kathy she's amazing!

Kathy OConnor is the best Professor I have ever had. She is such a funny, sassy lady and I really enjoyed her class. She is an easy grader on assignment, and the tests are easy as long as you go to the TEST REVIEW bc thats where you get all the answers to the test. There is a test review a few days before every test & that makes it an easy class

This online class made me feel like I was back in high school. Lots of pointless homework that doesn't even help you prepare for the test, which are proctored and consist of short answer questions. Homework isn't necessarily hard but it takes a long time. Makes this 3 unit class feel like 5. Avoid at all costs!!!!!!!!!!!
Michelle Paddack is the GOAT
