Michelle is one of the best teachers I have had. She is such an inspiring and motivational woman. She is highly knowledgeable and down to earth. I studied abroad with her in Bali and I felt safe the entire time. She really has taught me a lot and makes her students fall in the love with the ocean even more than you already might

Lectures are fascinating, funny, and clear! Prof. Paddack is approachable and inspiring. Class syllabus provides an outline of topics to be covered in every lecture, lab, and when quizzes and tests will be. Some labs are in the classroom, others include short field trips (ex: whale watching, mudflats, intertidal zone). Fantastic!

A fun & fascinating class! Dr. Paddack is lively & transmits her love of the ocean while being organized yet flexible. Clear lectures on interconnected topics; study Q's provided. Grades based on quizzes & tests (based on lecture material), 1 presentation on a topic of your choice, and lab worksheets. Inspirational professor!

Disorganized. Grading criteria is subjective and varies throughout the course. Takes points off for things that she was not even testing on. Her powerpoints do not even show anything, just the pictures rendering them essentially useless. Typos everywhere, it is so annoying. Mediocre quality from a PhD. Lots of busy work that is pointless. AVOID!

Prof. Paddack is wonderful! Her lectures are so in depth that if you pay attention, you do not even need the textbook. She sets students up for success by providing study questions for each quiz that correlate to the tests. She is passionate about marine bio. and she is one of the best teachers I have ever had. I appreciate the ocean so much more!

Fave teacher so far. She really cares, presents info in a way that makes sense. Gives lecture questions to use as study guides for quizzes and exams. Offers extra credit for learning about the ocean in different ways. I changed my major because of her. Take her if you can! I didn't really need the text b/c her lectures were so great.

Overall great professor. I had the class online and all of the online labs were interesting and helped me understand the material for that module. Lectures were thorough, but never boring. The information was always presented in a way to keep you entertained and engaged. Definitely would recommend this class especially if you love the ocean.

Ok, I took this class because I was interested in marine biology but ended up changing my major just because of this teacher. She shows you that you can make a difference in the world and is just downright a jolly, funny person to have as a professor. With this professor, If you're not interested in this class, you're not looking hard enough.

Best teacher Ive ever had, shes super nice and passionate about what shes teaching. I wish I talked to her more. Material is kinda hard if you dont study, so study and study some more. No homework besides studying.
Best professor I've ever had. Great labs, amazing person, research is relevant to the course material, and her lectures are interesting. Would recommend highly.

Michelle was super nice, and made her lectures easy to understand. There wasn't really a study guide for the midterms but there was for the final. The lab is really simple (be prepared for lab quizzes) and although the homework is optional, it helps you understand the information. I thought I'd get a bad grade because I suck at Bio but i got an A+!

I'm not a science person at all but I loved this class. Michelle is understanding, knowledgeable, and presents everything in an accessible format. There's definitely some work to this class, but there are never any gotcha questions and she's available to speak to outside of class so it's entirely possible to get an A even if bio isn't your fave.
Great teacher!

She gives you an outline of the class before you start which has the dates of all of your quizzes and exams so you know what will to expect. She also provides study questions after each lecture and those questions are exactly what you should know for the quizzes and exams. As long as you have a good understanding on them you will succeed.

Michelle is so passionate about what she teaches, however sitting in a lecture hall for two hours listening to her go on and on reciting endless facts gets pretty boring. The labs were usually pretty interesting and she definitely teaches well, but you'll only pass if you actually pay attention and do the reading.

Michelle has been one of my favorite teachers by far. Shes one of the sweetest, bubbly people in the world. Her lectures arent boring and she makes you want to go to every class. The labs are super long but so fun, I would definitely recommend taking marine biology with her.

MIchelle is a great teacher and very interested in biology, it is a tough class but if you attend class every day and do all the lecture questions you should get an A. no need to buy the book. she is amazing and i would recommend her class to everyone

This class was very challenging but rewarding. You really have to pay attention to the lectures as well as write everything she writes on the board. WRITE EVERYTHING DOWN. everything she mentions is on the quizes/tests. The labs are fun but very long. One presentation in lab and lots of quizes but no hw.
One of the most amazing teachers I've ever had! Great lectures and labs! She has inspired me and I plan on taking more of her classes in the future.

Michelle is such a fun and exciting teacher that I recommend everyone take one of her classes. She is so passionate about what she studies and it really inspires everyone else in the class. Her lectures are engaging and she makes it interesting, and is very fair when grading. Super down to earth person and super engaging professor!
Michelle was a good teacher, I enjoyed her class. There were a couple occasions where she was moody, and abandoned us at the beach during our lab class, but it's too be expected - she is human.

She is sooo disorganized! Hardly explains anything, she just reiterates the book with much less detail. I really exceed in science, but she is making me lose interest in it completely. Should have taken this class online with Larry Friesen!

Michelle is an amazing teacher who is extremely passionate about marine biology. Her class has taught me a lot, but you really have to study and go to class if you want to pass. You REALLY need to study to get a passing grade. There are also tough quizzes every week. STUDY FOR THEM. Other than that I loved this class and recommend it!

YOU HAVE TO STUDY. A lot of people drop because of this. Barely studied for my first midterm..D. Studied for my second midterm..B+ (I had to memorize the entire study guide but I guess it paid off) Don't over think the creature feature, its easier than she makes it out to be! Hardestmost time consuming assignment was the periodical report

Michelle is an amazing professor. Her lectures are great, very entertaining as well as detailed, as she is extremely knowledgable in her field. Labs are super fun, most of which involve spending time outside in the tide pools or somewhere near the ocean. This class requires studying, but her reviews are very helpful, and she is always there to help

Tests are hard. I would study the study guide until I could almost repeat it word for word and would STILL get a C. Lecture notes are kinda messy so taking notes on your computer really helps. Quizzes every twothree weeks,2 midterms,1 periodical essay,1 lab report,1 big project & final. You have to put in the work to get a good grade

Really knowledgeable about the subject and passionate about what she does. The labs can be a bit dry but sometimes they are so cool. Went to the beach almost every week to collect samples. She can be a hard grader on tests, but quizzes and projects are ridiculously easily graded. Got an A based only on lecture notes, never read textbook.

Very enthusiastic about the course/marine biology! The course has a lot of content but if you focus on studying the study questions shes provides at the end of each power lecture you should have NO PROBLEM GETTING AN A OR B. You do have to put in work for the class, but it is definitely worth it!

"Great teacher" becuase she's knowledgable and enthusiastic about the subject (especially when she gets to talk about worms and their p****) Is she great at BEING a teacher? No. Her overall help towards her students is lacking. It becomes discouraging for those who are interested in the class, but not taking it as a major.

If you have a passion for the subject of English, do not take her class. She will drain any passion in the subject away with extremely strict and arbitrary grading. The essays will be expected to be adherent to her own beliefs in exclusion, and according to instructions that are written in a rambling and barely coherent way.

She is very harsh in her grading. She will start and end an essay and then the next class you will be working on your Rough Draft for Essay 2. She will lecture the whole class which leaves no time for student participation.

Most boring class I have ever been a part of. The entire course is just her talking, she gets extremely upset if you get up to go to the bathroom because it "loses her train of thought." I feel like the essays I have written in her class have been some of my best work but still only get 80%. She locks your overall grade the entire semester

Terrible feedback on all of my assignments. Very close minded and not open to alternative viewpoints which can be very convenient if you and her share a lot of the same ideas. We did not and it made this class extremely difficult both to complete work and listen to her.

Classes are super boring. She lets you revise all essays doesn't offer extra credit. 4 essays throughout the semester, weekly journal writing about a short story, and one in class essay about a book. Doesn't really use canvas. Always gives extra time take this if you're a procrastinator. Can be challenged for better grade. Don't agree? You're wrong

Genuinely the worst teacher that I have ever come across in my time at SBCC. You can spend 8-10 hours on each of her assigned papers and its still never good enough. Half of the time it's impossible to understand what she is saying and she often goes on tangents talking about things that are totally irrelevant to the class material. Don' take her

Dr Pages was very passionate about the material, and the class was doable as long as you work for it. She responds very well to class participation, and as long as you listen to the things she expects in your essays, you should be able to pass.

I learned a lot from Dr. Pages. She's very insightful and the reading material is interesting/thought provoking too haha. Pretty hard essay grader though. I would even go to the writing center for help and start writing papers days in advance and get solid Bs on all of them. Overall an interesting class, it makes you think about life differently

Dr. Pages was an interesting teacher. She's a really difficult essay grader, but if you go in and meet with her to ask about your essay she is likely to let you rewrite your essay. She's understanding for the most part, but can be strict at times. Don't expect to learn anything about writing, but rather expect to expand your mind with literature.
Tough grader and is never satisfied. I rewrote a paper for her FOUR TIMES, and she was never happy even though I followed her requests each time.

Amazing English professor. Really makes you question where you stand on x topic. Engaging and fun. Beware of cellphones she will go into frantic speeches about cell phones (why are they out?). She can be a tough grader but she gives you the chance to rewrite your papers for a better grade.

She's a fine teacher. The reading was interesting and discussions were engaging. She doesn't like students with bad attitudes. If you put in the effort, and are polite, you will have no problem in this class . The course load wasn't too bad and she was kind enough to allow me to turn in my final paper late although she could have easily said no.

She is not that hard of a grader and will pretty much always let you re-write an essay if you did poorly. She makes you keep a going journal, but its pretty easy. I did most of them the day before. She is very philosophical, so class discussions can be interesting. Overall a pretty relaxed english class.

Probably the most boring English class I have ever taken. With that said, it was a joke how easy it was. She really does care about her students but my god is she bland. If you can deal with how slowly the class goes by take her! Easy A!

Horrible professor, worked very hard and received extremely low grades. Have never gotten less than an A in English before and i am barely passing her 111 class. Do not take this class, nice person but if you dont agree with her opinions you are SOL.

Great person outside of class. When it came to class, all of discussions would be off topic and her explanations for assignments would be different from the syllabus (but she wouldn't know). If you can maintain a good relationship with her, I highly suggest it. She cant really take control of a class very well either. Good Luck!

Dr. Pages was not my favorite professor. Oftentimes discussions had nothing to do with English, and she was also very close-minded in her views without taking into account that there might be someone who disagrees. She never gives clear guidelines in what she is looking for in assignments. Get another professor if you can, if not, she is doable.

She is horrible, take another teacher. She contradicted herself all the time and openly expressed her views against people in the class. There were many times when i went to her 3 hour class and did absolutely nothing. I didnt learn anything.

Dr. Pages is a pretty cool open minded teacher. i am not that good at english and i pulled off a B. I had the 3hr class once a week and it wasnt bad at all. the discussions were entertaining and i laughed a lot.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!! Prior to taking this class I was an English major and Had never gotten less than an A- even in AP English in a competitive high school. I finished her class with a D due to the fact that she would not accept my doctors note or let me make up what i had missed while out with a fever.
Easy pass.
she's a real nice lady, you can always go to her for revision before you turn in your final draft. 5 papers + final. she'll drop your lowest grade
she's absolutely a great professor. she's very nice and very helpful, not a very hard grader, and you can always make up your late work. sounds strict, but actually really nice
Professor Pages is a really great teacher. She pushes you to write well and she will help even an "A" paper find room fo9r improvement. I pay alot for my education and she gave me my money's worth.

Page was undoubtedly the worst English teacher I've ever had. When I put massive effort into a paper, it got bad marks, when I barely cared, she loved it. OH, AND MY FAVORITE! She assigned us to read the Awakening, which I drudged through, only to have her change the final project to a different book after I FINISHED THE DANG PAPER!

She's an ok teacher, but a really nice lady. She is a hard grader on essays, and can be pretty anal about how she wants them and what style of writing she likes- she even gave everyone grades like B-- (I mean who gives two minus's) But when my book was stolen in the last 2 weeks of the sem. she did let me borrow her extra that had her notes on every page- even during an open note paper.

She's an ok teacher, but a really nice lady. She is a hard grader on essays, and can be pretty anal about how she wants them and what style of writing she likes- she even gave everyone grades like B-- (I mean who gives two minus's)
She is an okay english teacher, but grades essays on the difficult side. Just make sure you do all the homework in her class.

Her teaching style is terrible. She has no valuable information to give and her assignments aren't relavent to the class. It turns into a poli-sci class everyday. Don't even consider her for a teacher, it will be a waste of your money.
Bad professor would not take again

I took this class fully in person because I heard good things about the hybrid version, I walk into class on the first day and the first thing out of his mouth was he had no idea how to run math 160 fully in person. This definitely shows- Lectures are extremely boring and you have to memorize a LOT of trig formulas which other classes give on tests
The exam was quite easy, with only a little quiz adding a bit of difficulty. A good teacher

He's a nice professor! Make sure to do the homework on time and submit it EXACTLY how he wants, or you'll get points off or no points at all. Calc is just a hard subject, so study hard. He gives you a worksheet and quiz every class for credit, so there's a lot of opportunity to raise your grade. Do HW to avoid falling behind, study day before class

I'm giving Professor Panza 2 stars only because he's kind and caring. As a Professor, I'd give him 1 star. He goes over material too quickly and teaches as if it's just review. I recommend watching Organic Chemistry Tutor videos to actually learn, then use his lectures just to review.

Took this hybrid class this past spring, almost entirely self-taught. Learned pretty quickly I had to rely on YouTube for instruction not his "lectures" which is him filling out a notes sheet. The exams were not bad but final was harder than exams. Lowest exam replaced with alternate score, very helpful can save grade. Would recommend full F2F.

Pretty chill hybrid class, lots of self study. Lowest exam and two lowest quizzes dropped so if you bomb the first exam like I did it won't screw you. He's particular about neat organization which honestly builds good habits. Homework problems could be pretty hard but exams and quizzes were a bit lighter. Grind it out on YouTube and you'll be good.

I took his hybrid class and the online lectures are easier to follow than in person ones. The quizzes are generally not too bad and the exams are also okay because of follow through points. Overall I'd say he's a good professor and very chill. Only thing to look out for is how neat your work is. He sometimes takes points off for messy work.

He's OK, but when you're taking an asynchronous online class it's kind of hard to say he's bad. He should voice an audiobook not teach math, lectures weren't v helpful to me. You're basically self-learning. Seems like a good guy, but class is OK; just okay, not extraordinary or great. just know what you're in for.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS - worst prof at SBCC 110%. Does not care about your success. He made fun of me in front of the entire class for having OCD. Said we had no time for clarifying questions then made irrelevant rambling connections to his personal life. Gives 0 resources if you are struggling. Does not explain math in a way that can be understood.
Goes through examples quickly and does not teach information well, I had to reteach myself everything by going to the mathlab because his lectures are not helpful. Don't take his class.

Be prepared to spend a good amount of time in the math lab. He likes to yap quite a bit too. Lectures are total information overload, while going over ~1 or 2 examples somehow not always related to the homework. I think the lectures would definitely be improved if he could figure out time management.

He does not teach you how do to math. He expects you to have taken only math classes from him and does not accept other styles of doing math. He does not break down problems in lecture and does not offer good help in class, only the tutors would respond on canvas or help outside of class. Do not take him if you want to learn.
Pretty chill class, with reasonable tests and grading policies. Not absurdly difficult

he can be a little confusing in lecture but maybe that's cause I had a small crush on him so didn't pay attention as much, otherwise would have gotten a better grade. quiz every week and cumulative final, pretty hard

Chris is a good professor the material is easy to understand if you actually try. Not an easy A. I def could have an A rn if I tried a little more but overall not bad. Hes a tough grader though and I recommend reading the textbook for every chapter he covers and watch his lectures!! Itll help you tons. He's super chill too :)

Professor Panza is a good teacher. I didn't have a strong background in Trigonometry and Pre-Calculus going into his Math 138 class but through lots of notes, youtubing, and going over quizzes multiple times I am confident in my abilities. He provided us with the files to our exams and quizzes so we can practice our skills. Overall, pretty good!

If you want to prepare yourself for Calc I, take his class (ME major). He assigns a lot of assignments, but helped retain info. If you want to succeed, do the hw, go to office hours OR the tutor (tutor rarely was available tho), and read/skim the textbook before you do the lectures so youre familiar with the material and the lecture runs smoothly

At first I was annoyed doing the online hw, but you get used to it. Super helpful when studying for a test because you can retake it. His lectures are streamlined pretty well, I just wish he had a better tutor (always had overtime). But luckily Panza had office hrs I could attend to (2/week). A lot of assignments, but it helped master the material.

I am so sorry that it was my only option for Math 138. Uninteresting lectures were problems with gradation on online textbooks and I often had to ask for corrections because there is a difference between "." and ",". Get ready to work on your side because I had troubles to understand even If I love Math. Maybe he is better in person...

DO NOT TAKE HIM!!! He is the only calc teacher I know that DOES NOT OFFER PARTIAL CREDIT. The platform tests are taken on make it impossible for students to succeed, and when I asked if I could take the tests in person because I believed it would allow me to do better, he offered no help or guidance. Other students who have had him also hatted him.

Panza is a straightforward and clear professor. His lectures, homework assignments, quizzes, and tests all maintain a logical learning progression. If you understand the homework the quizzes and tests are very manageable. He is also a caring professor and was consistently open to hearing feedback. Keep in mind he was using a flip schedule for 160.

Openly admitted to abusing his puppy in front of the whole class..obvious "correlation" to his harsh, unsympathetic teaching and grading styles. Makes you wonder about the implications for others who may be under his care.

Not accessible outside class. Other students and I would line up outside of his office for office hours, he would not show up. Doesn't respond to messages on Canvas, instead makes an announcement to the class. Doesn't provide flexibility with attendance, yet was absent for 2 weeks. Frequent mistakes in study guides, test grading and lecture videos.

DO NOT USE THIS TEACHER. He has caused depression in me and all my friends. This is the worst professor I have ever taken, One small formatting error will ruin your grade. On top of that, he is an extremely harsh grader on the written assignments and exams because he is extremely strict on spacing and exact format.

The notes are on pdf and are very structured, which is nice. The problems on the quizzes and exams are usually on the more basic side just to make sure he knows that you understand the concept, so don't expect any ultra-hard curveball problems. Also, very generous with partial credit. Stay on track and you will do well. Highly recommend.

Professor wasnt very willing to work with me and all my feedback was extremely critical and negative even when work was done properly. For example I missed 60% of an assignment for putting 2 steps of work on the same line and on another assignment missed 9-10 points for typing instead of writing even though all the answers and work was correct.

I took MATH 150 after not taking a math class in over four years since I graduated high school.. wasn't advanced in math either; just luckily placed into MATH 150. As my first collegiate math class I was daunted. I halted him plenty of times during lecture and he agreeably stopped and explained stuff. Try hard, use the math lab, you can succeed.

Hard grader 4 HW, easier on tests. Hard to understand- he mumbles a lot. Tutor for class just gave answers, but no help on how to do problems. Other tutor commented on how hard the questions were. Uses webassign for hw and quizzes, not tests. Drops 2 lowest hw quiz and written assignment scores. In summer Calc sesh 2 now-so much easier than Panza!
Took M138 over the summer, the pacing was good and his online lectures got the job done as much as they could have. He clearly cares about those taking his class, and wants them to do well.
Wonderful teacher i hate math bur she explains it well

Professor Parmely is a caring instructor who genuinely wants her students to succeed. The class has a lot of flexibility - you can retake quizzes unlimited times and the final can replace a low exam score. unstuck ai was a helpful study tool alongside the open-note exams.

Fantastic teacher & class. Anna is kind, approachable, and easygoing; her administration skills could use some work and she can be slow to grade, but she always follows through & is open to questions. Does her best to connect with & help every level of student—very relaxed coursework with an understanding, sweet teacher. Couldn't recommend higher!

Really lousy professor. Test grading takes forever, lectures are unhelpful, and doesn't seem to care much. Despite all this, the content of the class is really easy and getting a solid grade isn't too bad.

Professor Parmely is the best math professor I've ever had. She's so caring of all of her students and truly wants everyone to succeed. All of her exams are open note w/ a calculator + several extra credit opportunities. All quizzes can be retaken an unlimited number of times too. If you've had trouble in math before, she is wonderful!!!

Very nice and helpful teacher but the course had 3 midterms, 1 of which was the week before finals which just did not make sense. The work leading up to the midterms was also a lot. Overall the course was fairly easy and she was a really nice teacher

Professor Parmely is the best math teacher at SBCC. Her grading is the best, you can take quizzes as many times and you can take unit tests twice! She dropped my lowest quiz and unit test score at the end of the semester which saved my grade! I highly recommend her class!

Shes a good teacher! shes great at communicating and shes very approachable. You can ask her something that youre unsure about and she will help you without making you feel bad about it. She also explains certain math topics well and shes patient for those who dont understand certain topics.

Prof. Parmely was easy to talk to and would reply within a reasonable time when emailed and was always very encouraging. She herself was not difficult, but the class as a whole is very well planned out and the syllabus was defined. LISTEN to the lectures, take lots of organized notes and put in the effort and you will be ok. Recommend!

Was pretty easy does have a lectures what does clarify what is being learned that week. Does have a textbook but I have never used it. Have lots of test and three midterms and the final. I would definitely take this class again.

Anna is wonderful. Great, clear lectures and responded quickly to my emails. The class has lots of work but lots of chances for redemption. The 110C part helped me pass the class. I felt supported and really learned math for the first time in my life.

Take this class! I have never been good at math and failed it in HS but I actually did well in this class. Anna really wants you to pass, she has a tutor and the program she uses makes sure you learn the material. Many extra credit assignments. 3 exams and 1 final but if you don't do well on one exam, your final replaces that grade. Amazing class!!
Her class is simple and she's very straight forward. I took Math 114. Has a few extra credit opportunities throughout the course. Her class used the ALEKS program.
Super organized and flexible math assignments. very generous on grading and late work. It was HARD to get a low grade in this class, and I am a C average student

I hated math my whole life. I was DREADING taking a math class and was so scared to even start. I just finished the class with an A. Something I never ever thought would happen. If you hate math take this class. Anna is incredible, she presents the material so clearly and cares about her students. TAKE HER CLASS!!!

Take this class if you can. Anna has video lectures that are crystal clear - she is super supportive in office hours and responsive to emails. The 110C portion of the class was helpful and didn't add unnecessary work. I'm not strong at math and was worried I wouldn't pass, but I got a B! I learned so much that was actually useful to my life.

Best math class I have ever taken, and I used to hate math. The online course was organized really well - I always knew what I was supposed to be doing that week. It's not easy, but there were helpful reviews for every exam and lots of chances to improve my grade. Anna was really responsive when I had questions - I would highly recommend!

I'm only a week into the class, but I can already tell it'll be a good experience. I'm taking the class online, as a refresher before taking Intermediate Algebra later in the summer, and if all goes well.. trig or pre-calc in the fall. If you have questions, just reach out to her. She's really responsive and encouraging. SBCC hired a good one!

Anna Parmely is a great professor! She explains everything very clearly and makes the class enjoyable. The homework is online using ALEKS so you don't really use the textbook unless you use it for reference; I went the whole semester without it. She does expect you to keep your class binder organized.
Ana is so sweet ! She is super helpful. She really wants you to pass so if she sees your putting in the effort it will go a long way i love her so much

One of the best teachers of all time! I loved her 95th math class, and I am very happy to have chosen her class because math class is a very difficult subject in itself, but some teachers with their teaching method make it seem more difficult than it really is.

Anna Parmely is a great instructor that makes learning the material easier than expected. Attendance is mandatory, plus if you skip class you probably won't learn the material and will fall behind quickly. The tutors in the class are very helpful. Sit in front, do your homework, and most definitely show up for an Easy A/B.

Excellent professor and person in general! Makes learning a challenge but not impossible. Strict grader! Doesn't have a lot of office hours... but is available by email. Also has great tutors. Overall if you want to you will learn a lot of math and be very well prepared to take on future math classes. Make sure you go to class! Easy to fall behind.

Honestly for easiness I would have given an 5 but I think that was just for me because I kinda knew most of the stuff from highschool and had a great study setup. She was an awesome teacher who made math fun for me and I enjoyed her class. Had normal quizzes to make sure you knew your stuff which I appreciated and had group tests before big tests
she is the best teacher. I love her class. You have to study and do your homework and do good on the test, you get an A easily.

Best math teacher I've ever had!! This class isn't an easy A... but go to class, do the work, take notes and study and you'll do just fine. MAKE SURE TO DO THE HOMEWORK. I messed up and got lazy, it ended up dropping my A to a B. She's sweet, funny, and kind of sassy. Most importantly she's a really great teacher that makes it easy to learn.

I've never been so impressed by a prof! The thought of math used to literally put me in tears, but Anna knows how to teach in a way that can help anybody. Take it from someone who used to suffer from severe anxiety when it comes to math, you won't be sorry taking any class with her! She's the absolute best.

Amazing teacher. easy to understand and get along with. her one petpeave it dont be late for class or getting back from break. She will lock you out. It is easy to learn a subject when someone so hot is teaching it.

She is a good teacher & knows how to get the information clearly to the students, but she lacks a professional attitude. Its her way or the high way, if you try to test her she will take your grade into her own hands and make your grade less about your actual work and more about how you at and respect her in class. Take her class with caution
Tough class for me but teacher is top notch. Aleks helps as long as you do it, exams are graded hard but if you keep up and study you can do great.

Loved this class and professor. Was an easy A with very little workload but I genuinely learned a lot about myself from this class. It helped me decide on my major and explore career options within it. The website we use to take self-assessment was super fun to explore. Highly recommend!
A great class if you aren't sure about what to do regarding careers/majors. Very inspirational and keeps you thinking about what interests you and what you want to do later on in life.

Professor Phillips is the man! He is super nice and is great with helping you find whatever it is you want to do for a career. His personal development class was one of the most helpful classes I have taken at SBCC and completely recommend it for anyone having doubts about their future.
Very friendly and if u go to class and do all assignment, u can get A very easily, also all hw are VERY EASY..I would like to take this class again
He is super kind and friendly.

She's very funny, very laid back, very caring. She sometimes isn't clear with last minute assignments but she's always available to ask after class for more clear instructions. She's does a lot of class activities that include everyone's participation, it's maybe awkward at first but by the end of the semester the class feels more connected.

Betsy is passionate, well-versed, and hilarious. Textbook is easy to read. The most important thing to do is to show up. The in class quizzes are a bit rushed, but not quizing for a grade, just to see if your doing the reading. Don't get discouraged; she will love you. The in-class discussions and activities are so fun. TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!

Professor Phillips is one of the most caring, loving professors I've ever had! She barely gives any homework, gives lots of extra credit. Attendance is super important and you won't do well if you don't show up. As long as you're interested in sociology and doing your assignments this class is so so easy and one of my favorites I've taken so far.

Such a lovely professor! Not always on course with the textbook, but funny and sweet in her lectures. Attendance reflects your grade. The tests and assignments are pretty easy as long as you try. Not a heavy work load, and a great class to talk to people.

Professor Phillips is the most amazing professor I've ever had. Some of the things she's said in class really inspired me to keep going in my education. Not only this, but she's incredibly empathetic and very insightful. She's genuinely interested in and cares about her students' well-being. I absolutely loved her and wish I could take another clas

Professor Phillips changed my life. This class was extremely fun and interesting and Betsy really is one of the best people I've ever met. This class is the best class I have ever taken in my life. She has absolutely changed my life and inspired me to go into sociology. I cannot recommend this class enough. TAKE IT!!!!!

Betsy is an amazing professor! She cares so much about her students, is always willing to help, and always gives great feedback. Always puts effort into her feedback on each assignment, and does it with kindness. She truly wants her students to enjoy the class and makes the material interesting.

Betsy is an amazing professor! She made her online class super organized and easy to navigate. This class is really interesting and I learned so much from her! You can tell that she truly cares about her students and wants them to succeed, and leaves really helpful feedback. There is a lot of reading, but the material is easy to read.

Betsy is a really great teacher, shes very kind and understanding. I took her class online and it was structured in a way that made it really easy to track each assignment and not miss anything. She also gives really thoughtful feedback on most assignments!! Highly recommend her and this class

I believe this is Betsy's first time teaching online, and she does it so well! She's incredibly caring, frequently encouraging her students to meet with her in office hours and giving great feedback on assignments. You will be asked to write a lot in this class, but the topic is very interesting so you will not have trouble doing so.

This is the best class at SBCC. For the final everyone makes their own game and it allows for a lot of creative freedom. For the first 8 weeks you learn the basics of Unreal and for the last 8 weeks the assignments are almost all progress updates on your final project. If you want to make something cool try to start early and stay consistent!

Prof Price was awesome. He's newer to teaching, but clearly loves his students and wants to help each one succeed. Don't hesitate to ask for a meeting with him if you get stuck! He's always patient and emphasizes teaching strategies for success in the workforce. Left me feeling better prepared to be a professional.
I thoroughly enjoyed this class!

TAKE THIS PROF! One of the best and most caring person. Breaks down work so it's understandable and not super overwhelming. Mostly group work and 2 research papers which he breaks down over the semester and makes it not overwhelming! Recommend!

He uses a very broad vocabulary and is very matter of fact but that's part of his charm. He is super funny and makes sure his students are well taken care of. All test questions were posted before the actual test so you had plenty of time to prepare. The exams were also completely open note.

Truly a great professor! Cares about his students and really makes sure we all understand the material. If you show up to the labs and put in the work you will be very capable of getting an A & learning a lot! Its important to keep up as its v group oriented, but he is also very understanding and does what he can to allow you to makeup missed work.

This class was very time consuming. However, you are actually learning in this class with the lectures he gives us. This class is hugely based on group projects that you must participate in to receive a grade. Tests were hard but manageable if you put in time to study. Tutor he provided was phenomenal! I always went to her for extra help.

I wouldn't take Professor Ramirez again. I found his class to be disorganized and his feedback to be confusing. When asking him questions, he tends to go off on tangents that kind of have to do with an answer but he doesn't really say anything. Furthermore, I found that he tends to speak a lot and not really say anything in his lectures. Hard pass.

Professor Ramirez made me actually like psychology. This class is very interesting and a little bit scary at first. Most people are afraid of how it is a research class, yet he makes it very easy for you to understand. You can tell he actually cares about his students and their mental health. I would 10/10 recommend taking his class.

I loved being in Prof. Ramirezs class! I took PSY 125 during Covid 19, and each class, he presented a PowerPoint and explained everything in detail, making material much easier to understand. We had unlimited time when the test was open to take (over multiple days). He is passionate about psych., kind, accessible out of class, and a fair grader!

Professor Ramirez is a great teacher. He really cares about what he is teaching and wants you to succeed. The coursework is medium to heavy but a lot of the work is done in class so its easy to manage. He is really nice and understanding as long as you reach out and let him know if you need help/have a conflict.

I took his class when everything went online and it was somewhat easy. There is A LOT of work to do but if you attend every lecture, you're in good shape. I did not even purchase the textbook. There were about 5 quizzes to complete (with 3 takes). There were 3 exams and we worked on the questions in class. Make sure to not miss an assignment!

I would give Dr. Ramirez a 2 for his teaching skills but I gave him a 3 because he is really nice and wants you to succeed. He just talks WAY too fast, assumes you know what he's talking about without giving context, and can be a little judgey if you don't get an answer right. He's also a bit awkward and asks you q's then interrupts you.

He has a pompous demeanor and his lectures are very lengthy since he rambles on. But if you stay the course, you will pass as long as you participate, complete the group projects, and keep up with his lectures. He's not the greatest educator, but he will do.

Solid choice for Psychology! He is passionate about what he teaches and his online lectures are his own, not recordings of someone else - like some other online lectures. Interesting content if you are pursuing psychology or a health care profession. Group project final, but he is able to see who contributes and is very understanding and helpful!

Taking this course with Professor Ramirez was a trek, especially given the pandemic. There is lots of work involved in the research paper assignment however, you work on it throughout the duration of the course. Professor Ramirez meets with group individually to give feedback on projects which provided to be very helpful.

He's a good guy who really cares about the subject he just talks SO MUCH. Every week you're reading 2-3 entire chapters along with his 3+ hr long lectures where he just rambles. I was watching everything in 1.35x speed. Lots of little activities, 3 midterms, quizzes for each week. If you're gonna take this during summer, pair it with something easy

PSY 100 was my first ever online class, and a great first experience. While the class is not a breeze, it is an easy A if you do the work and are self disciplined. He posts his lectures and you are expected to watch them and sometimes reply to them for assignments, and use info from them on your final. Very organized and great professor who cares!

If you are determined to get an A in his class, you will get one. He is accessible and wants every student to succeed. It is not an easy A but a totally attainable A. He offers second chances and recognizes effort. Highly recommend him if you care about your GPA. Some professors will hurt yours no matter what you do. Hes not like that.

This was a tough online class bc it was only 6 weeks! There is a lot that he packs in, but PLEASE watch the screencast lectures because they're interesting and provides all the info that you need to know. If you're struggling, reach out and chat with him! he appreciates effort and overall just wants you to understand these fascinating concepts.

Professor was super chill, I took his 8 week course and its was double the work per week, you must be super disciplined to do well in this course. This course almost ripped my corpus callosum apart, but no pasa nada:)

If you put aside the constant self deprecating jokes and him being an extremely slow grader, Dr. Ramirez isn't THAT bad. He is a very tough and thorough grader so you're not going to be able to slide through the class with ease. And DONT MISS CLASS OR LAB. That's where all your points come from. Don't buy the book either. He never references it.

His online class was engaging even though he's lecturing from online. You have to submit participation responses and write 3 papers which are doable. I would take advantage of his office hours as he's very helpful in guiding you in the right direction. Easy A class as long as you go over his notes while writing papers and responses.

Honestly the best teacher at SBCC . He makes class so interesting that I was excited to have his class as an 8am . Before every Midterm he has Zoom calls and its where everyone in the class has a big FaceTime call and we help each other with study guides and all that . He even goes over what will be on the test . Honestly such an amazing person !

Probably one of the best professors I've had so far at SBCC. There is participation and homework for this class, but it's doable as long as you keep up with the class everyday in discussions and extra credit opportunities. He made the class very intriguing and relative through his lectures and great feedback. He's ALWAYS available for help as well!

Professor Ramirez is an okay professor he just teaches this class as if everyone is a psych major which became pretty annoying since a lot of kids take it since it's a requirement. That being said the class is easy enough if you put in the work, it's just frustrating if this really isn't a field of interest since he assumes it is for everyone.

I really liked his class. I don't like waking up early but I took his 8 am class and it wasn't hard at all. He made it fun to come to class because the material is very interesting. Explains in a clear manner and makes sure everyone understands. Jokes a lot, which is fun, and gives you good feedback and good resources. Sets you up for success!

He's probably one of my favorite professors I've taken at SBCC! He makes things so interesting and if you feel like you don't understand something and ask questions he'll try anything to help you understand! It's a pretty easy class as long as you pay attention and you'll pass if you just do the hw and show up! AMAZING!

He really does care about his students and wants them to succeed. He is funny in his own way and wants to interact with as many students as possible (I have close to 150 people in my lecture). My only pet-peeve would be that he sometimes in lecture will give important information verbally and not have it written in his lecture slides.

Ramirez really knows his stuff, but personally he moved too fast during the lectures for me to be able to absorb all the information. There was so much information taught in his lectures that I fell behind. I would recommend taking his iPath class because taking this class as a big lecture class wasn't a good choice i made.

Very disorganized, but it was his first time teaching the class. I was not sure what to take notes on ever, or what was graded. Exams are incredibly detailed so make sure to study literally everything. All multiple choice questions have the "none of the above" option.

This professor I absolutely amazing! Took him for a summer session and was so fascinated by each one of his lectures. He is funny and approachable, but you do have to work for your grade. He truly wants his students to learn.

Hands down one of the best professors I've had. Clear grading system on assignments and exams, making it easy to understand what is expected. His lectures are engaging and packed with real-world examples. He has a friendly and approachable attitude, which creates a comfortable environment to ask questions and participate in discussions. 10/10 rec!

I was going to drop this class then after reaching out there was a lot of support offered. Ended up being one my favorite classes. The work is easy, just tedious. The professor relied on the one tutor a lot. Get your work done ahead of time, there are multiple opportunities for success.
Jennifer is very helpful and thorough, she explains everything you need to know and always responds to emails in a timely manner. She sends reminders on upcoming due dates.

COMP 101 is a straightforward class. If you follow the directions EXACTLY you will pass just fine. She gives you all the all the information you need to pass and is quick at responding to questions. She also offers extra credit.

Jennifer is frikin AWESOME. You do the work you pass, it is that simple. She explains everything clearly, very easy to follow. If you think her class is hard you should probably just quit college all together because this is easy as it comes. you literally have no homework because she gives ample amount of time to do it in class. oh and she is hot.

I had Professor Roman for English 110 and would say it's a very doable course for those who don't enjoy or aren't very good at language arts. She is very personable and fun teacher. Chose a great selection for reading and we only had to purchase one book that we got to keep.
Super sweet and interesting material. I have never enjoyed an English class until this year.

I really liked professor Roman and she gives good verbal feedback on how to make your papers better. She is an easy grader and basically only cares about completing all of the requirements for the discussion posts. This class was not hard and I would have her as a professor again.
Only one text-book is needed and the class is pretty easy. Assignments due every monday and thursday but she accepts late works sometimes. I like her, very understanding for online classes.
this was a really good online English course to take and she was a great professor to have! definitely reccomend
She didn't explain clearly and gave a low grade.

I hate English classes and I am not good at them either, but this was the best English class I have ever had. She gives two discussions and two quizzes a week but it is really simple and easy. If you read the textbook the discussions and quizzes are easy. She is a fair grader and always gave me a 100% on discussions.

She is extremely unreasonable. Assigns so much work and doesnt care that students take more than 3-4 classes. I am a DSP student with a learning disability and she told me that "some people can't handle online classes" and should consider dropping the course. The most feedback she ever gives is a couple of words. Worst professor. AVOID AT ALL COST.

Prof gives good feedback but slow to return emails. Be prepared to type roughly 1200 words per week on discussion boards, but this will help when it comes time to write your papers. Overall structured well. Do the reading and participate (like all classes) and you will get a good grade.

After reading good reviews about this professor I felt at ease taking her English 111 online during summer. However as a 3.5 GPA student I have never had a professor that grades so harsh with no explanation and does not provide feedback on how to improve until days later.

REVIEW IS FOR CSUN. I love this professor so much! If you need this class, take it with her. Four essays with very clear grading, I got an A+ on all of them. Journal entries as weekly homework, but very easy. One presentation that you do with a classmate, easy. She is very knowledgable about English and cares about her students. Highly recommend

Took her English 101 class at Pierce... literally the EASIEST class I have ever taken. A lot of assignments but it's all busy work. Plus, she literally explains every single thing that you have to do in detail so it's hard to get anything less than A on any assignment. Got a 98% in the class just for following instruction. Easy grader. TAKE HER!

She is a very sweet person. Her class does have a heavy work load but if she notices the class is struggling, she adjusts the schedule. She is very understanding and gives good feed back on essays, I wish I could have a class with her again :(

Took Kathy's ENG 111 class online and it was very rewarding. Her class does require a lot of work but it is definitely do able! The workload may seem a bit heavy, but its not bad. She is a super sweet lady and gives great feedback on essays. There were a total of 4 essays.. 2 critical analysis of poems, 1 research paper, and one timed final.
I loved this class! She is super sweet and helpful! with any english class there is homework and essays! But she is clear on what she expects and I loved her as a teacher

Great class. I learned a lot about nutrition. Her videos are easy to follow and material is easy to understand. Very quick replies when you reach out to her and she's very good at communicating if she might not be available. 3 paper, quizzes for each chapter, many playposit videos with 3 questions each. Super easy A and you learn a lot!!

Dr. Rose was one of the best professors I have had. She explained every concept very well and presented information in a very digestible format. Dr. Rose makes her expectations clear for what is needed to get an A and, in my opinion, has very reasonable expectations. The tests were fair and straightforward.

I am currently working on a doctorate in medical sciences and I can tell you that after all the years of academic life Heather is by far the worse professor I have encountered. Think about your future, a professor like her can stop your career, your livelihood and your future. Choose wisely!

She is no longer a professor at SBCC but if she returns DEFINATLEY take her class. Her teaching style fits best with students who are passionate about the class. If you are just trying to get a good grade take another teacher. Its 100% possible to get an A as long as you put in the work!! RECORD ALL LECTURES

This woman is fantastic. Yes, her class is really hard to take. BUT she cares so much about every single one of her students. She wants to see you succeed but you have to put in the work! She has so much knowledge and knows how to answer questions with so much depth. Truly, the most inspirational professor I've had thus far.

She does not have many good reviews on here but I thought she was a great professor to take for such a hard class! I am a nursing major and had to take her class for a prereq and got an A. Memorize everything in her lectures. I would record her lectures and rewrite everything too. The final is really hard.

The worst professor I ever had. She is not a good instructor. She doesn't care about her students. When you asked her a question related to the material she did not have a clear explanantion for you. Please don't teach physiology anymore.

Please don't take Physiology with her if you want to ruin your GPA and fail her class. She makes the class so hard and with her crazy drawing. She will ask you to draw whatever she draws on the board in her exam. Stay away from her if you want to pass Physiology and going to nursing school.

Heather is very intelligent when it comes to anatomy, but her class was the hardest class I have ever taken. I would recommend taking anatomy with a different professor. She has her PhD in neuroscience so she focuses A LOT on the nervous system which can be challenging. Her study guides are nothing like the tests and she's a tough grader.
So hard, shes very nice as a person, but her class is extremely hard. I would recommend trying to get a different teacher.

She is very insightful to her lectures and tries to help as much as she can. The lectures have slides Important Questions, she chooses 5 questions from that set of IQs to be in the lecture quizzes. She randomly collects them 9 times but drops 2. Study the IQs before class and you should be fine for lecture. Study a lot for Lab its really hard!

Professor Rose is one of the most competent and receptive professors I've ever had and I feel so lucky to have taken Anatomy with her! She has high expectations for her students but takes pride in her students' successes just as much. I learned to much this semester!
Do not take this teacher. If you have to take cell biology, transfer somewhere else. very unfair grading and sometimes rude.

Dr. Rose is extremely intelligent and sweet. Her lectures are jam-packed with info, and you need to makes sure you are able to attend every lecture because the exams are 100% lecture material. This class is HARD and Heather's only pitfall is that because she's so smart I think she fails to see to see that with the students its not always as easy.

I had a bad experience taking Heathers Anatomy course. Her study guides don't cover ANYTHING that's on her exams and she is not clear at all during her lectures. She'll lecture one thing and you'll be tested on information she hasn't covered. Strongly reccomend to take Anatomy with a different professor.

Heather is my favorite teacher thus far. She's VERY smart, but explains things clearly to us who are just hearing it for the first time. The material isn't ridiculously hard, but there's so much of it that the class can seem overwhelming at times. I took a small hit to my GPA, but I enjoyed her teaching and learned so much that it was worth it.

The material does not align with the tests. I happen to be a student that wants to learn. Heather will tell you to study her lecture notes that all the information you need is there for the midterms and final. Not true, the test questions almost seem deliberate with information you do not cover in class. At least, give your students a heads up.

She is a knowledgeable teacher. She explains everything clearly and expects you to be focused and always caught up with the material. Hardest thing about this class there's a lot to learn, the material isn't hard but lots of memorization. use index cards, record lectures, use office hours, and study at least 2-3hrs/day. You learn a lot from her.

Professor Rose is an amazing professor. She will really get you interested in anatomy. However, she is very hard and this class will require you to study A LOT. Make sure you aren't over-packing your schedule. Pay attention to small details and attend every lecture because notes are rigorous. Quizzes every time you come in for lab and lecture.

Dr. Rose is one of the best teachers I've had at SBCC. She has a quiz every lecture and it forces you to keep up, which I actually learned to be a blessing. All the neg. comments about her are about her tests being hard, but honestly if you attend every class and pay attention this class is a LOT of info, but she is more than willing to help you.

This is a very interesting but challenging course with a lot of material to memorize. Plan to study for many hours every day. She's a very clear lecturer, but her exams are hard. She posts the lecture slides online before each class, but you still need to attend lecture because not everything you need to know is on the slides.

She is a difficult professor. If you want easy, take cell w Doohan. I took molecular w him and it was significantly easier. I wasn't ready for her first exam, requires extremely intricate answers (ie. chem structure w bond angles of AA, ATP, etc). Once I realized what she was looking for it went well. Extremely difficult but you learn a lot.
There is a lack of communication in what the expectations are to be successful in the class. Exercise extreme caution.

Dr. Rose is an extremely kind person. She continually offers to help those who need it. Expect minimally 3 hrs of study time per hr of class. She will cover EVERYTHING so expect mass amounts of studying. Overall, she is fairly straight forward, and has her students best interest in mind.

Heather is insanely smart! Tests are graded hard & you must understand the concepts but its all from lecture. Write discussion Qs at a 5.5 unit level. Dont take Bio 103 with any other hard classes! Heather is approachable & always there to help. Half the class dropped but if you put the work in you will do fine. She became my favorite teacher!

Heather is a great teacher! Her lectures are clear & the information in the lectures is what you are tested on. Wants you to go to office hours regularly. Remember its 5.5 credits. Its very time consuming & you MUST put effort into it. VERY hard grader on exams. Use practice tests & know concepts. Definitely feels like 5.5 units. Don' procrastinate

Heather's class is very demanding, and you have to be willing to actually study. but, her lectures are very clear and easy to follow. She throws a ton of information at you, but she makes it as understandable as possible. I feel like I have learned a ton in her class, and really enjoyed it. I really recommend her if you're interested in the subject

Her tests are insanely difficult. her drop failure rates are extremely high. squeezes all the information she can into the 1hr20min classes, when my class had an average higher than usual on the first test she decided to make the second test way more difficult. not funny at all either. i had experience in the field and studied and still got a C...

Heather is a great professor, probably the best I've ever had. If you don't need to take anatomy, i don't recommend it because of how hard it is. However, if you plan on taking it, take it with her. She does a fabulous job connecting the overall pictures and her lectures really make sense. I can't express it enough on how much I loved her teaching.

As an educator, Dr. Rose is great! I've learned a lot and had fun doing it. However, the majority of her exam questions are trick questions. Even if you study the recommended 20 HOURSWEEK, you can get a horrible grade because of the test phrasing. If you can afford a hit to your GPA, take this class; otherwise it's a gamble

Anth101 wasn't too bad, most of the grade relied on in classwork, he didn't mark me down for skipping class, but if you miss an inclass assignment it matters a lot. Didn't read a single page of the online textbook so it was light. Prof. Sanders is intense, kind of funny, and an interesting guy. personally i thought The lectures were pretty boring

He had his moments where he could be intimidating but he cares about his students. He's straightforward in our lab and pretty funny. One downside is he doesn't really post announcements or grades so you are unsure about your grade throughout the course. Labs were rarely ever 3 hours

I thought the class was going to be cool, unfortunately due to his teaching style it just was not what I wanted out of this class. He'd often go on tangents that had nothing to do with class, most of the assignments had nothing to do with the lectures, and he grades attendance heavily. He seems nice at first but don't be misled.

He's really not that bad of a professor. However, he does have a habit of getting off track. Due to this, you have to spend some time to teach yourself the information, which is simple as he has his lectures and textbook chapters on canvas. Despite him getting off track, I enjoyed the lectures as they always felt like a conversation

Hybrid Course: The course it self wasn't entirely difficult as there are no exams, you don't need to read the textbook & the quizzes are online. However, there are in class assignment's which you only get in class. As other reviews say, the professor is truly rude, arrogant, disorganized, & unhelpful. You won't learn much & basically on your own.

This professor is one of the most mind boggling people i've experienced. most of his lectures are disorganized and go off track. it's obvious he does NOT want to be around those who might know less than him let alone TEACH them. don't take him unless you can out-smartas$ him.

If you are love challenging and difficult professors, if you are the teacher's pet and can kiss ass, if you love professors that drone off subject, if your entire life is about getting the A, this professor is for you! If on the other hand you want to keep your sanity and take classes with kind and approachable instructors, take a hard pass!

He constantly makes micro-aggressions to his black students and he is very passive-aggressive and grades super hard. He is not good at explaining things and expects his students to understand off the bat.

Do not take a class with him. If the course is required, take it online at another CC. Not only are the lectures confusing and he is constantly going on tangents, he is verbally abusive and takes his own issues out on the class, and his assignments are randomly given out. Genuinely the worst professor I've had after 4 years of college classes.

Prof. Sanders is an amazing professor with minor caveats. He is adept at incorporating examples into lectures, so if you're interested in the subject, you'll learn a lot more than what the text provides. He's more caring and patient than he lets on. However, his Canvas is disorganized, and he only grades assignments near the end of the semester.

All of Professor Sanders' classes are a must-take if you have any interest in Anthropology or Archaeology. His lectures are incredibly engaging and energizing, he always has a well-read response to any question you may have, whether it pertains to the lecture or not. Not very tech savvy, but imo that's a fair trade for great in person lectures.

Whoever is giving the good reviews (cough...Professor Sanders?) is full of it, or the rare student this professor takes a shine to. I cannot say enough bad things about this professor. He hates his job and it is really obvious. If you want to keep your experience at SBCC as good as you can then avoid, avoid avoid this tool.

This professor gives SBCC a bad name. He clearly hates teaching, hates his students (unless you are MENSA) and is super scattered in both lectures and instructions. I was really looking forward to this class because the subject is super interesting. He unfortunately made it a horrible environment and boring as hell. Would avoid any of his classes!

he is the worst professor i have ever had. i am convinced he wrote all the positive reviews because there is no way that students actually like him or his class. he doesnt care about students' time and does not grade anything. i havent learned a single thing in this class.

This is quite literally the worst class I have every taken. His class makes me not want to go to UCLA cause he is the only teacher that offers this required course. He does not grade assignments, I have genuinely learned nothing in this class. He goes completely off topic for the whole entire class and has no respect for his students or their time.
DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!! this is the worst class i have ever taken

Professor Sanders, is very engaging and knowledgeable about the course topics. He is hilarious and cares very deeply about his students and their educational success. He maintains regular office hours and is willing to work with his students, who struggle with challenges, as I do. I highly recommend Professor Sanders at SBCC.

Professor Sanders is extremely knowledgeable on archeology and clearly has a lot of passion for it, his lectures can go off-topic but the exams are written around what he got to in class. Grades usually take a long time to come out. Beware the occasional in-class assignment that is unmentioned online.

Mark Sanders is one of the weirdest professors I've had, someone complained and he got pulled from our Sociology 101 class mid-semester and we got a new professor. He goes on and on about random things and doesn't even teach on the subject. Quizzes are random. Has a weird superiority complex too kinda annoying. Easy A but I do not recommend taking.

Do not take this class. Unorganized, unclear and overall frustrating. Professor 'knows" a lot but rarely actually follows the book or powerpoint. He goes on long unrelated tangents about random things. Doesn't grade anything till the end, so it's easy to complete class on your own but you won't know if you're doing it right till the end.Worst class

Professor seems excited to share the material! He is passionate about what he teaches, and helps the students that make it to class by giving insight into which areas are needed to focus on for exams. Attendance is super important as he delivers detail packed lectures, but you must be prepared to read the textbook chapters that are assigned.

He doesn't know how to use canvas so it is hard to know what is due and when. He also doesn't input grades until final grades are due so you never know how you are doing in the class. He goes on long tangents which makes the class run an hour longer than it should. The homework load is very easy but the class isn't enjoyable.

Do not take this, professor! It was the most unorganized college course I have ever taken. He would go on tangents for hours, leaving the course material uncovered. He was not clear with assignments. He would not welcome questions being asked, even though directions were unclear. Not a good environment for learning. Worst Professor I ever had.

If you need to take a Lab class do not take this class with Sanders. He is completely unprofessional, never responded to a single email all semester, and goes on long tangents that dont relate to the course. He didnt grade any material until final grades had to be in. Doesnt know how canvas works and kept half of the assignments locked. BAD

I was excited for this course but Prof Sanders is just so unorganized. Taking his online class it was very hard to navigate what was due and what was part of a past course. He definitely wanted to be "right" when confronted with course homework problems. I NEEDED my book and personal notes for the exams, would not have passed without open book...
Not great for online class and doesn't update assignments from previous semesters. Confusing format and if you can take someone else do it

Gives tricky assignments and quizzes. Need the book if you want to even attempt the class. Never responded to anyones emails and it is the last week of the semester and he has graded only the first weeks work. Not sure how a professor could be so MIA!

He was absent during the course and did not respond to emails. He allows students to submit a weekly "now I understand" paper to get extra credit for assignments and tests you messed up, but since he didn't grade anything until after the class ended, I could not do this. Several students said they reached out and he ghosted. TERRIBLE. AVOID.

If I could give him a negative number I would. He is hands down the worst professor I have ever taken, and I have taken over 100 credits of classes. He is a biased, disrespectful, narcissistic and down right abusive teacher. Was 20 min late to the midterm and promptly screamed at the whole class and tried to get them to tell who called the dean.

I struggled in the beginning and this teacher really had patience where I was able to take his course and pass it in 6 weeks summer course. I had a hard time understanding at first. His test are open book and not timed so it helped me.

Jill Scala is an excellent professor!! I am so happy that I enrolled in her COMM 131 course for the Summer Session. I didn't know what to expect, but the assignments she presented allowed for us students to expand our knowledge of topics and subjects that of our choice & present them. 100% Recommend!! In fact I will take another of hers if I can !!

Professor Scala was AMAZING. She is understanding and took the time to explain assignments. I also really liked how the entire semester was posted in the beginning, so I knew what was expected of me. She also posted announcements in advance, so I had enough time to complete assignments. Finally, I appreciated her fast and helpful replies.
Good teacher, but lecture slides were disorganized and she was over an hour late to the live zoom presentation.

love it! 161 recommend team role-play as hiring managers interviewed students and picked who to hire. Fun in lab w/free food. 121 helped communicate w/family. Office hr flexible and at night. All assignments posted for whole semester but she allowed late work. Great teacher. Wanna pass read announcements.

Thanks to Professor Scala's teaching prowess, this class was the easiest I've taken in college. Her ability to break down challenging topics into manageable segments made learning enjoyable and stress-free. Her lectures were engaging, and she always encouraged active participation, fostering a supportive and inclusive learning environment.

Professor Scala is one of the most exceptional educators I've encountered. Her communication skills are unparalleled; she explains complex concepts with remarkable clarity, ensuring every student grasps the material. What truly sets her apart is the impeccable structure of her course. The syllabus is thoughtfully designed and easy to understand

Homework was easy enough, not much communication from her on the online course I took. Would get a notification for an assignment 2 days before it was due. Though, her class is simple, and it is not hard to get a good grade, but she is honestly just not the nicest. I think some more warmth and support from her would change my rating to 5/5
best professor ever

I really enjoyed the content and value I got from this class, and I think Professor Scala is fantastic teacher. Her curriculum was very clear and I learned a lot from it especially about relationships. The knowledge was very practical!

Love this class and relevant to my life! She genuinely cares about her students' success and called me personally to help me get back on track. No busy work, assignments were relatable to my life. She provides personalized feedback for assignments and also shares other examples to show how to improve. Highly recommend.

Left assignments off the course calendar until the absolute last minute. Abysmal communication skills in her assignments as written. Lied about the methodology of our midterm for some reason. Maybe she would be better in person, but she seems completely incapable of handling an online course. Happy to never need another course with her.

My 2nd class with Prof. Scala. While another student noted communication problems, I had the opposite experience. She is available by email & mobile number, she has been supportive when a personal situation arose gave me extra time. I know she had a family matter that came up last semester. She truly cares about her students success.

While the class itself is not very difficult, the way she was MIA the whole course made it difficult to succeed. she wouldn't post the assignments on time, never graded assignments until way later, canceled live zoom presentations multiple times, had vague instructions, would email abt assignments then never post them, etc. overall very unorganized

Great class, It would have been better live or hybrid because I am a comm and business major. The group assignment can be challenging but all of the assignments are practical and applicable to my life. She provides a lot of feedback and sends weekly updates. If you need to meet, just email and you can zoom.

Thoughtful and understanding. Professor called me at home to make sure I was ok and understood the presentation assignment. Spent 30+min on assignment but also took time to get to know me. Accessible anytime via text. Wish more teachers were like this.. Definitely take this course.

She's nice but her set up was so confusing. The instructions are unclear and messy. There was issues with the live presentations and then the make up sheet was just everywhere. It's not a hard class but you just kind of have to look everywhere for the instructions.

Very nice prof just like most of the other comm profs at sbcc! She is understanding and supportive and wants you to do your best and feel your best. Took the summer course and workload is manageable bc it's asynchronous. Get ready to make speeches, just like comm 131 & 141. Text needed and hw discussions and other assignments.

Exceptional professor real life examples, engaging, interactive discussions, and multimedia resources makes complex theories interesting. Tonight we had a 2 hour discussion on a difficult chapter. She made me feel important, motivated me to excel in my comm rlsps. Caring, genuine and passion for her students. Feedback is constructive.

She's super nice and her class is pretty interesting. She genuinely cares about her students and wants everyone to do well. Her canvas is sometimes confusing to follow and some instructions are unclear. other than that, she's a great professor!

Prof. Scala is such a kind lady and seems like she is very passionate about comm and teaching, but her directions are very unclear. Hard to follow. Her instructions are just so confusing. Like, for one assignment it said: "step one; follow all instructions." Uh okay yeah that's what I am currently trying to do.

Prof. Scala is so kind and it is obvious she cares about her students. She is very passionate about what she teaches and really wants her students to use what they have learned. There were two speeches in this class along with quizzes and discussions. All of the assignments were useful and not just busy work. I definitely recommend this class!

Prof. Scala is so kind and it is obvious she cares about her students. She is very passionate about what she teaches and really wants her students to use what they have learned. There were two speeches in this class along with quizzes and discussions. All of the assignments were useful and not just busy work. I definitely recommend this class!

Loves job and students. Wants students to take away something meaningful with course activities and explains how to implement them. Canvas can be hard to follow at times but instructor sends announcements 2x a week to keep students on track. Quick to respond via email or text Understanding and caring. For overachievers, offers helpful resources.

Loves job and students. Wants students to take away something meaningful and explains how to implement skill into everyday life. Canvas can be hard to follow at times but instructor sends announcements 2x a week to keep students on track. quick in responding via email or text. Understanding and caring. For overachievers, offers helpful resources.

She's a very kind and caring teacher. Very passionate about the subject and easy to reach out to for help. Textbook is very interesting to read. Overall, easy class and important communication skills to learn. The canvas page is a little bit confusing and disorganized but you can ask her anything.

This class surpassed my expectations. I was able to apply what I learned in both professional life and to improve personal relationships. I have told all of my friends to take this course. My instructor was always available if I had questions. She is passionate and caring with lot of business communication experience

I loved her class ! She was very nice and definitely cared about her students. The class was somewhat easy if you read the book you will pass! No homework and not a lot of assignments a few important assignments and presentations. Would 100% recommend Jill! She seems very understanding!
Ms. Scala is the most caring Professor you could have, She offers to help a lot of students and really helped me learn more in the class.

Shes an awesome professor! She cares alot about her students, and is very funny! Can always come to her for questions about assignments and has a clear grading system. Participate and turn in assignments on time and you will pass.

Professor Scala was a great teacher. Always available, always a smile/willing to help. Unfortunately, she doesn't organize well. The modules were all over the place and it was hard because she posted so many different instruction sheets. Hard to keep track. Easy A once you get the hang of her pattern. She is very sweet though and a big help!
