Prof M was amazing I loved him and his class. He cares a lot. He makes the classroom feel like a community. I would highly recommend his class! He is very clear about what he expects and how he grades. You definitely have to try to go above and beyond for his class but he is a fair grader and he won't accept the bare minimum.
Make sure you show up since it is part of your grade. Make sure you go to the writing center for papers and ask him a lot of questions when papers are due.

Really funny, awesome guy. If you like a class with a lot of very open discussion, this is great. He makes a really nice collaborative classroom environment. There is a LOT of reading, but it's still a good class. I really enjoyed it.

Dude this class is dreadful. I would not recommend, especially as a freshman. It took everything I had in me not to fall asleep everytime I went to his lectures. Mark seems like a great guy, don't get me wrong, but the way he delivers the material is not efficient, especially for Gen Z.

Good teacher, definitely go to class, although it can be boring, but he goes over a lot of material/ideas you can use for inspiration on essays. Only 3 or 4 essays, no homework besides that. There is an intro essay to do, but it's easy. If you're taking many other classes with this one, it's very manageable.

Your grade is based on three essays and two other assignments for the whole semester. His essay grading criteria were not clear, and he provided a prompt without specifying what to do with it. You do have a lot of time to work on the essay, though. He also flagged my final essay for AI usage, but I did not use AI at all on it, and my grade dropped.

Genuinely one of the most personable teachers I've had at SBCC. I enjoyed his lectures, and he was always kind and understanding. There were only 3 essays during the semester, with no additional work aside from journal entries that are given in class. Very manageable class, with excellent teaching.

Great professor. I took him for two semesters and I'd take him for another. He's genuinely a great guy, always starts the class off with a funny joke. For assignments, he gives a reasonable amount of time to do and a straight forward rubric to follow.

Super harsh grader and classes are extremely boring. Attendance is graded but his classes are pointless so its really annoying. No one participates and he gets very upset about side any conversation. He is a nice guy but he has unrealistic expectations for his students. Work load is light. Its an okay class just not great.

Professor Scamahorn is a good guy, very nice and caring, however his class is extremely boring. He only assigned 3 papers for English 110 and he often spent the entire class going on about nothing and nobody participated. It's not a horrible class but I'd avoid it if you can. If you show up and complete assignments it's not hard to get an A.

Class consisted of writing a journal for 15 min., discussing it for 30, then analyzing the book no one read for 45ish min. Super boring because no one participates. Very nice and respectful teacher, but hard grader and talks about repetitive topics. Often let our class out early.

I don't think that he could teach at all, all we did every day was write journals, and talk about a book that every time you read, you'd lose half of your brain cells. The book was awful, I can't even read a paragraph without going outside and breathing some air, and he expected us to read 40-60 pages in one day. My advice: don't take him.
Worse professor! He doesn't explain why you got that grade all he does is ask you to go to writing tutors. Also, worse at giving grades. DON'T TAKE IT!!!

A tough grader. He doesn't give good feedback. Only allows revision on the first paper. He says to ask him for help and then sends you to a tutor or look in your book. Do not take his class. The books that we read are horrible

take this prof if you want to hear Berkely stories, deal with arbitrary subjective gradings such as "weird" or "akward" which lead the entire class to get bad grades, and listen to rambling politcal talks made to create tension in the classroom.

Prof. Scamahorn graded extremely harsh & had an unprofessional attitude. The class was worth a total 32 points & a QUIZ was worth 6 points while the final was worth 2. After classes moved online due to COVID, he failed to mention due dates & refused to accept late work when half the class had missed the quiz, which dropped my grade from a B to a D.

Nice guy, but the class kinda sucks if people don't participate, nothing on him, he really tries to get people talking. You need to keep track of your grades bc he doesn't really use canvas, but it's not super hard to calculate. he gives awesome feedback on essays and will help you figure out how to be a better writer

An incredible and hilarious professor. His class discussions were deep and intriguing, and yes, you had to read and actually participate to get a good grade, but overall he's a very easy grader that only makes you write about three papers. Definitely a professor that I wish taught English 111 too!

One of the greatest teachers I've ever had, Mark is incredibly inspirational and everything you do in class and what you read all work together to present a bigger picture towards the end of the semester. So pay attention to what he says because what you learn in this class will stay with you when school's over (and not just your English skills).

Mark is one of those professors who don't put grades up on Canvas, so it makes it hard to keep track of your grade. His grading scale is also kind of confusing like all essays are out of 6 points....? Anyways! He's really funny and as long as you write academic papers and utilize the writing center you'll be fine :)

Mark is a great teacher and really tries to relate to his students. He makes his expectations clear and the work is very manageable. The beginning is slow but the work does start to pile up near the end with papers and reading/reading quizzes. He doesn't give a final, instead a five minute presentation. All in all great guy & will work with you.
Not a big fan of reading but he made it enjoyable and somewhat easy. Couple of papers, really good guy which he goes over what we read and gives good feedback on our work.
Was really nice guy, tended to go off on tangents, but they were always interesting and made you think about other world views.

Best English class Ive ever taken. Mark is challenging and doesnt give the best feedback on papers but if you go to class he tells you exactly what he expects. He made a 8am-11am English class enjoyable, just dont fall asleep and the class is easy.

In my opinion Professor Scamahorn was a great teacher. He made every class entertaining and was very approachable. He wanted students to pass the class and he made the students think to get a good grade. The class is not very challenging. If you apply yourself you will get a good grade.

Mark Scamahorn tries to make his lectures as enjoyable as possible, but he is an unreasonable grader and he barely gives any feedback. If you receive a D or a C, you'd expect more feedback than one sentence. He has no time outside of his office hours, so if you have class or work you can't make appointments. Make sure to get on his good side.

For a 3 hour class, Scamahorn made time fly by at times. Has a funny sense of humor, but can be a hard grader. Big presentation you have to do at the end of the semester. The overall final isn't to bad.

He's a nice guy, and does try to relate to the students. What I didn't like was that he gave us way more work than other professors. Every class, at least one take-home journal is assigned in addition to readings and work that's constantly being done on our papers. This class is incredibly boring, and requires a ton of work. Avoid if you can.

The best! I enjoyed his class so much, he's very funny but he also knows how to keep things moving. It can be difficult at first grading wise, he can be on the tougher side, but he's always willing to help. The only thing I would say is maybe don't take the Friday only class just because it gets crunched time wise because of holidays.

A truly amazing and motivational teacher. He really makes you think and reflect upon yourself. He's also extremely organized and makes class discussions interesting. Essay prompts were clear and filled with detail, so you're never confused. I would highly recommend taking this class if you enjoy getting pushed to reach your highest potential.

I've taken two of his classes and plan on taking a third one. I love his classes so much, he always has good things to say, he's very funny and he'll work with you on anything you need. Highly recommend his classes!!
LOVE THIS CLASS!!! HONESTLY JUST TAKE IT!!! It's so much fun and inspired me to pursue screenwriting as a career.

Shaw is super helpful and caring, he gives great feedback and is also super funny in the online lectures! This class was a really good experience, I definitely recommend, he helps you to succeed. Its an easy class you just have to try, you basically build on your idea for a story to make it a whole and strong. Great experience!!

Best teacher I've ever had in SBCC. John is incredibly funny, his lectures are easy to understand and he is so easy going. It's super easy to get an A if you participate, and that makes it so much fun. I recommend the Screenwriting classes, 10/10 points from me.
Wish I could re-take his classes, I enjoyed them so much. His feedback is spot on. He's generous with his time. Great teacher.
John was very helpful, answers emails quickly and explained everything thoroughly. I'm taking his advanced screenwriting class next semester because I liked the class and him as a professor so much.
LOVED this class. I'm taking his advanced class in the fall because I loved it so much. He gives GREAT feedback and replies very quickly.
Shaw is the man! Super rad class online. Definitely take.

Even though I took his class online I really enjoyed this class. The course work is not difficult, and he is NOT a hard grader. Just make sure that you put in the work that he asks for and it should be smooth sailing.He gives GREAT feedback and makes an online class as interesting as it can be! Definitely take this class.

I took the online class so I did not really meet Professor Shaw but he gives great feedback and his online lectures are hilarious and very helpful. The entire class was based on completing a feature length screenplay so it's hard, but doable. Just commit and you'll get through it.
Very cool dude!! Very fair, smart, funny. I took a online screen writting class. Great.
Great teacher; very helpful and encouraging. Good class structure.
Brilliant writer, brilliant teacher.
Very reasonable and understanding!

She is quite literally one of my favorite Professors ever! So caring and passionate about her field of study, it really adds to a class when your Professor actually likes educating students. She brought in a lot of speakers which I personally loved! Very cool and interesting person, I would highly recommend her class!

Prof. Shean is very passionate about her subject and her students. She makes sure everyone is understanding the chapters and she is not offended if you ask for clarification. She also likes to bring in guest speakers to share their experiences. She is very engaging in her lectures and I looked forward to this class every week. Take this prof!
Very well organized class. Professor Shean is very nice and understanding, not a hard grader but still a very entertaining class. Highly recommend one of my favorite professors at SBCC
I love participating in the lectures the professor had but I would have to be aware of when papers are due. That's about it and she is very open and understands situations and gives you great advice.

She's funny, sweet, caring and genuine. She really cares about her students and super passionate about the material. However there is hella reading. I did all of it. Turns out the quizzes r just straight from the textbook so u can chat gpt it and get a perfect or almost perfect score. I never did but it may be frustrating for ppl who did the readin

I was nervous for this class because I am not a sciencey person but Prof Shellhorn was so incredibly kind and helpful that the class was very enjoyable. Although the zooms are not mandatory to attend I highly recommend going as she is very helpful and will walk you through any problem that you are having trouble with. I am so glad I had her class!

Jennifer Shellhorn was the kindest and most down to earth professor I have ever had. When I first enrolled in the class I was very stressed because Science isn't my best subject and it is hard for me. She was always so kind to me and made me feel like everything was going to workout. She is so helpful, listens, and truly cares about her students.

Everyone in my class loved John. Getting in the ocean at 8 am is hard, he was the best part about it. Tells cool stories and is really knowledgeable about tons of random things. As long as you have reasonable attendance (~75%) you'll get an A, and he's very understanding.
I really enjoyed this class! I would love if they offered more classes and maybe another level!
I would love to take this class again at a higher level. It would be great if there were more ocean kayaking classes. John is a great teacher and always makes the classes so fun and I improved a lot!

Won Conference and Coach got us to the State final this year and final 4 for the 2nd time in 2 yrs. He's great, doesn't scream or shout, and uses his experience to get you through. He's got a great sense of humor! He's very knowlegable and calm and makes good decisions. He really cares for you and his door is always open for you. The best!!

Ocean kayaking is awesome!!! Do not take this class if you don't want to get wet. You learn rescues so you will be in the water and you learn to surf, which is super fun!! You can tell he gets bugged by people who don't want to participate. It's lots of fun and if you have a class right after, there is a shower at the rental place! Bring a towel :)
He drills you! But he is fair and has a great sense of humor. Don't take this class if you're a wimp! He can talk about absolutely anything! AND loves his students!

He works you very hard but is fair and will help you. I hated it at the time as he pushed me so hard, but now I wish I could do it all again. Probably the fittest I've been ever! On reflection he's the best I've had, and has a great sense of humor!

Coach is awesome! If I had known better, I would've taken the class in the Fall instead of Spring (cause the water was still freezing from the winter). The water was so cold and the wetsuit helped only a little bit. Grade is based on participation. Don't just sit there- Ride the waves! And dont be afraid to tip over!

He's a super easy teacher guarantee you'll pass I missed alot of classes and still passed .. For soccer improving skills he's really easy going and just lets you play games I recommend taking this class with a friend since there is a lot of free time and games .. Wish there were more drills but still cool class I recommend him

This class is so fun! I wish it did it include drills, because it's just games, but most people prefer that. This coach is really fun and there's no extra work outside of class. He's a brit with a sense of 'umor.
Great teacher. Lots of fun and great instruction. Wish I was 20 yrs older....what a great guy!
BEST SOCCER COACH EVER! I LOVE YOU JOHNNY BOYYY.
Great Instructor
Awesome ocean kayaking class, great teacher! Tons of fun, and I would recommend this class to any CC student that wants to make the most of their college experience.
Excellent soccer coach, period.

I took her Pathophysiology class this summer semester. She is knowledgeable, nice, and supportive. Her learning materials are detailed and organized. Quizzes are from the textbook and her slides. You should prepare to learn a considerable amount of information fast and effectively if you take HIT 204, especially in summer. I really love her class!
She is the worst. everything is out of the textbook or so she says but the quiz questions never match up. she does not offer help at all and is very cold even through an email.

She is an empty uniform. This is my 2nd time taking her and though class is online, she is not meant to be a teacher. She is absent in every sense of the word. She is not helpful at all and is only harshly critical apart from being rude. If you want a professor that is rude, unresponsive, and never there then take her classes. Terrible person.

This was my second HIT class. I'm not sure if it's a department thing, but they really need to do a complete overhaul. HIT 204 firehoses info at you, and professor is basically absent. Lecture slides/notes lack proofreading and can have wrong info. Luckily each quiz (20 pt each) has 2 attempts. 50 pt essay boosts your grade if you score well.

Don't let the "Dr." fool you. She has absolutely no clinical experience. Small-minded, lazy, and cocky. She lets the course run itself and is not reasonable when it comes to the hardships of struggling/minority students. Savior attitude and ignorance problem. Slow to respond w/ short replies. She shuts down questions if they threaten her ego.

Very difficult class, especially for online and summer; no lectures, so if reading the textbook doesn't cut it, you'll need to find alternate methods (YouTube saved my butt). 2-3 quizzes per week, plus discussion forums. No midterm, but final is cumulative. One paper on a rare disease due towards the end of term.

DON'T take this class with Slade. She's a filler professor and literally doesn't teach. No lecture, you just read textbook and take quizzes. You have to use PROCTORIO. She uses random quizlet questions doesn't even make them herself. Avg 6 hrs studying for one quiz. Honestly you end up studying quizlet more than the material. Complete waste of time

I struggled a little in the beginning because I never took an online class. She was really nice and gave me great pointers on getting the material to sink in. Besides taking notes, I did notecards and really made sure to study the material before exams. The paper was your typical research paper and I had a lot of fun researching Factitious Disorder

Allison is an awesome teacher, we somehow had a very small class this semester, really enjoyed connecting with her and she definitely didn't let me fail, kept motivating me, and kept the class interesting.

It was obvious Allison did not care about teaching this class at all. She was snappy and rude if you asked questions. She rushed through power points, and always seemed stressed. Did not have office hours. Did not let people talk to her after class. Exams are directly out of the textbook, but she grades on "participation"

Very sweet lady and very knowledgable. Flies through powerpoints so fast that you can't take notes, but she puts them online so no worries. READING IS ESSENTIAL. She relies on the textbook heavily, like I'm talking bringing it to class and reading at home too. Very interesting subject however, and she is very clear about what she expects. Recommend
Good glass, eat right and it will really help you out. Fun teacher.

Awesome teacher, so personable & very helpful when asking. Holds students accountable & is very ispiring! Some can take her as blunt, but she has nothing but good intentions & wants the best for her students whom she truly cares about. I've taken bootcamp 3 semesters and hiking for 2 as well & couldn't ask for a better instructor-Rock on Allison!!

If u want to learn techniques for running dont take this class. She only talks to the ppl in the class that are natural athletes. If u ask her about a certain stretch she just ignores u or gives u a rude look. Never gives tips and runs with class instead of analyzing our running and providing feed back. Not worth the trouble.

Easy A if you attend class regularly. Super fun beach volleyball class. One easy 2 page research paper about beach volleyball and a couple other really easy assignments. You do some drills in the beginning of class but she always lets you play doubles/triples for at least the rest of the 2nd half of class. Nice teacher but nothing special. Take it!

Super wicked awesome! Great instructor and cool lady. Her classes are extremely fun and challenging. Not a wimpy play on the beach or walk through the mountains kind of class. I have never seen a person move so fast that wasn't running. Summer class is very fun, you get to hit some cool water holes and go swimming. Volleyball kicked my butt!

I took her hiking class and had a great time. A few of the hikes are pretty challenging, but it's a good workout and you get to meet some cool people while enjoying some beautiful scenery. She requires her students to give one or two very short presentations on a wilderness-related topic, but it's easy. Take this class!

Great Teacher.. Super fun and loves what she does.. I took hiking with her in the summer and had lots of fun and met new people.. the hikes are tougher in the summer and longer.. so be prepared to sweat..must take a class with her!
She is the coolest and most fun teacher. will take any class she offers...twice if possible.

She definitely chooses her favorites. I took her body conditioning boot camp, and if you are looking for a good work out, you will get one. Don't miss her classes or else you'll have to do make up work for participation points. If you are not an early person don't take this class.

Totally awesome. Loves her job and loves getting a good workout. I took her for hiking during the summer and I have never had so much fun in a class before. Some of the hikes were difficult, but so much fun!
Awesome teacher. Highly recommned any sport with her if you want to get a good workout.
Took surfing from her, she has a HUGE ego and is very strict, don't get on her bad side, she is not a nice person whatsoever.

A ton of HW. Busy work is what I call it. You can learn terminology without all the HW she gives out. Tough grader. However, the narrations on her PowerPoints do not work. I found her very annoying. Will not take again!!!!

Lots of homework and quizzes every week. But all the assignments relate directly to the quizzes and exams. Although, there is a lot of work it makes sure you not only memorize the material but also understand it. Study a lot and quizlet is your best friend.
Tough teacher, but very thorough and fair

I took the accelerated summer accounting course and I highly recommend that for any student. If you hunker down, watch the lectures, and really give it your all, you will pass this class in a few weeks with an easy A. Bob breaks it all down simply and is overall great and easy to reach out to.

I absolutely enjoyed Accounting 110 with Bob Smagula. He was attentive, caring, funny, relatable, and incredibly knowledgable, which allows him to bring the language of accounting to life. I highly recommend others to take his class. Thank you, Bob, for taking the time and care to explain the language of accounting.

Avoid this teacher at all costs. She failed to make students comfortable and has a very bad attitude. She tried to give me a zero on my assignment because it was 1 min late due to poor wifi in HER classroom. When I addressed it due to the fact I was there and did the work assigned to me, she tried to argue I was not in class.
it's been a full year since I took this class with her and I'm still thinking about how much I hated it

Professor Smith struggled to explain concepts clearly, making it hard to complete the work. Her attitude toward her students felt childish; she seemed frustrated when students were quiet, and didn't encourage questions. I left feeling less confident, not gaining any extra knowledge. I recommend a different teacher for a better learning experience.
AVOID THIS PROFESSOR AT ALL COSTS

The worst teacher I have ever had in my life. She gets mad at the class if they don't ask questions regarding her assignments, but will make fun of and pick on students when they ask questions about the assignments. Out of the 4 essays during the class, she gave out *one* A. I think she genuinely enjoys seeing students struggle in her class.

I recommend you never take a class with her. So much effort and countless hours spent for a B- because of how unclear her instruction is and how tough she grades every assignment. She accuses students of cheating when they are not, & makes a fool of students in front of the entire class when she doesn't like them for whatever reason. Bad experience

Lectures are often posted on canvas weeks afterwards. full of surprises, less time to prepare. 2 essays (4 pages each) in 2 weeks. average score for essays in the class: 56. Doesn't teach much. If you actually want to pass the course, RUN!
I have never hated a teacher more.

Absolutely awful teacher, never explains assignments clearly then grades you poorly with no explanation. Blames students for EVERYTHING, even mistakes she made. She is unwilling to change her teaching approach, and takes zero responsibility for her student's failure in her class. Extremely rude and inflexible, avoid at all costs.
I wish there was a score lower than one. Don't take this class or anything with this teacher, I can't ever describe how awful she is.
Worst teacher I have ever had. Stupid tough grader, and gives no clear expectations on what she wants. Just don't take the class.

Save yourself from the emotional stress and unpredictably of this Professor's class by choosing another class. Grading on papers was very difficult to understand, unexplained, and not constructive to the writer whatsoever. A good portion of your grade is dependent on reading "Grit" by Angela Duckworth. Be careful signing up for this course.
don't take her class

I genuinely tried to enjoy this class and get out of it as much as I could, but Natsuki made it very difficult. She is not clear on writing instructions and her expectations vary and contradict. Feed back was also little to none and she rolled her eyes and appeared frustrated when students would ask questions.

Harsh grader, gives lots of homework and very little feedback. She would also often be rude to students asking questions. My main issue with Professor Smith was how unreasonable she was - I had to go to the department chair (who took my side) because of one of her policies. Everyone in my class was frustrated and unhappy.

Professor Smith makes you feel stupid. I would ask a question and she would respond in the most mind bogglingly RUDE tone and basically say my question was idiotic. She never gave constructive criticism and she took points off for fun. Do not take this class unless you want to spend hours on assignments worth 10 points or you hate yourself.

She never posts anything on canvas until the day it's due, so you have no idea what to do before class. She assigns so many papers, and when she gives you feedback it's not even helpful. The workload is a LOT too.

THE WORST TEACHER AT SBCC. I honestly don't know how she hasn't gotten fired yet. She is the worst grader of all time and doesn't care about you as an individual. She makes everything that you are learning in class difficult and confusing. Please don't make the mistake and take this teacher.

Natsuki Smith was easily the worst professor I have had at SBCC. She was hard to work with, contradicted herself over feedback, and was overall inconsistent. The only aspect she remained consistent in was making you feel like you were literally insane trying to get meaningful feedback. Honestly questioned her qualifications throughout the semester.

I took this class online and I have never been more miserable, the work load was absolutely stupid, I spent hours on every assignment that were NOT worth the points. No matter what I did, she would mark me off for something, her comments would be "great work, great paper, super well written but conclusion needed some work." then gives me a 80% .
Unfair and unclear when it comes to grading and expectations.

Professor Smith is a super nice and caring teacher. She makes sure that her students are prepared and that they understand the material. She is also very funny, and she has some great anecdotes! Be prepared to write a lot of papers, as that is the majority of the work assigned. Reading is also required for homework. 10/10 would recommend.
For online english 110, this teachers grading was unfair when it comes with reading, even when it comes with TttT. Smith can miss grading online and be a bit rude.

Natsuki Smith is an awesome english teacher. Her expectations are clear, and she makes writing papers a lot easier than most teachers do. I would definitely take her class again and I wish she taught english 111

She is so hilarious. If you do what you are asked to do, then you will be guaranteed a good grade. If you slack, sorry you get what you put in. She may seem harsh at times but mainly its just to push you to do good. But when you turn in your work she isn't harsh, as long as she sees you are putting effort. I wish she taught English 111!
Great Teacher! Do assignments and you will pass. Very helpful and kind. I would take for all my english classes if i could

She is a very good English teacher, but you better do your work and show up to class. She taught me everything I should have learned in High School. No mid-terms or finals just a final department exam she prepares you well for. Would definitely recommend her to writers that need improvement.
Worst English teacher !! She doesn't know what she doing and she doesn't give you back things on time. Hard to approach. You really don't learn and the class just makes you waste time. Avoid!
Do not take this teacher if you want to learn something. Confusing and blames all the students for her fault. This teacher goes to the history of the worst teacher in history.
One of the easiest classes ive taken in my life. Theres a quiz a week and thats literally it, 2 attempts, open note. Cool class too interesting material.

Professor Sneddon is the most kind, caring and understanding teacher I've had. she gives so much extra credit, her lectures are really interesting and her explanations are so funny. I ended the class with a 118%, and I genuinely enjoyed the class so much. highly recommend if your interested in environmental impacts and just how the earth works.
Professor Sneddon is awesome! One of the kindest, most caring professors I've ever had the pleasure of having. Honestly, if you don't take a class with her, you are missing out. She was the best!

So kind & motivating! Welcomes questions & comments with grace. Clear lectures with slides and occasional drawings on the board. Don't buy the textbook! Grade based on in-class math Q's (you go over everything together), short final group presentation, and final project. Wish we had more tests since it would've helped me review my notes.

She is the best professor I've had yet. She explains everything incredibly Clear and is extremely passionate about the lectures. You can always ask for help, and you can really see her aim is for everyone to understand and pass the class. All the exams and quizzes are from the lectures, and you can always just re take them. FOR SURE recommend.

There's weekly quizzes and sometimes discussion posts but everything is all open notes including exams. Lectures are informational and are helpful during quizzes/exams. It's a pretty easy class just make sure to pay attention.

I 100% recommend taking a course by Kristen. One of the best professors I have ever had. She is very caring about you. I never bought the textbook... you don't need it. Class isn't mandatory but she appreciates you showing up and class conversations. She's the best.

Class is fairly easy. Only assignments are discussion posts, weekly quizes, mid term and final. She has a ton of extra credit opportunities available. All assignments are able to be made up for full credit up until the last day of class online. We never use the text book so I wouldn't recommend getting it for her 111 class.

Prof. Sneddon's class was easy but she never responded to my emails all semester. The tutor for the class was great and always responded when I needed help. She says you need the textbook, no you don't. I bought it and never once opened it all semester. Take her class if you need the science requirement it's an easy pass!

Took this course online and it was very confusing. There is a textbook for the class but it will in no way help you. There were quizzes every week and the answers could not be found in the textbook, on the google slides, and sometimes not even online. Grading was confusing I had a 107% that suddenly dropped to a 92% the week of finals for no reason

Sneddon is a very kind lady and knows what she is talking about. Every week consisted of a quiz and either a discussion or a group meeting. The group meetings are on zoom every other week but she works around your schedule. At times she took a while to post the classwork, but she is an easy grader and gives tons of extra credit opportunities.

Sneddon is a gem! Her objective is for you to learn the material and will give you every opportunity to excel. Her extra credit is time consuming but it will insure that you ace every exam. She is never unfair and her exams are straight forward which is awesome because the material is complex. I loved her class so much and I loved her as a person.
Kristen is hands down the best Professor Ive had at SBCC. Amazingly passionate, understanding and caring. She offers field trips and extra credit. Always ready to help!

The best teacher I've ever had! She gives you every opportunity to learn the material and do well. The tests are open book and untimed and you can take them more than once. There are lots of extra credit assignments so if you work hard you're sure to get an A. I found the prerecorded lectures very dry, but I enjoyed her live recordings.

Professor Sneddon provides a very simple class. If you give some effort, you will get an A. I respect her flexibility and she will work with you if you get a bad grade. Interesting class with a very manageable workload.

Sneddon was a good professor to have for geology especially if it's not your major and you're just doing it for credit. Her lectures are short and straightforward without confusion. There are a lot of quizzes and the exams take time and are hard, but they're not proctored so you can use notes/textbook. Not a lot of work, highly recommend! Easy A.

Kristen always has fantastic lectures because she writes and draws everything on the board rather than using slides. She will make random quirky jokes that makes class less stressful. The topics can be difficult sometimes, but there is always a feeling that everyone is in it together.

Kristen is one of the best teachers and I'm extremely glad I chose to take earth science with her. She makes class interesting even though the subject matter may not be. She gives many opportunities for extra credit and clearly wants the best for her students. I high recommend taking this class if you think science is not your strong suit.

Take Sneddon's class if you need an easy A, for your science requirement. She gives so many extra credit opportunities, it's almost impossible to fail her class. She's super understanding with late work, just communicate with her. If you complete the study guide for tests, you get 10+ points on your overall score. I've gotten over 100% each time!

Kristen is a great professor and really caring about her students with great knowledge of her subject. She gives lots of opportunities to turn assignments and make up quizzes if life outside of the classroom happens. Overall, her class is a great introduction to geology and the environment!

Kristen is a good teacher and she really cares about the students getting a good grade. She cares about the subject she is learning and loves the subject. It was an easier class but it was interesting. If you want to get a science requirement out of the way take this class.

Professor Sneddon was definitely a pleasure to have as a professor! She cares about you succeeding in her class and getting the most out of her lectures! She gives a lot of extra credit even though she makes the class as easy as possible to pass.
She loves what she's teaching, answers all questions, respects students, makes it relevant to the wold, multiple learning techniques, my favorite teacher at SBCC
Really very understanding and willing to help if needed

You will literally receive an A if you show up and pay attention. Gives so much extra credit! There is some confusion when it comes to grading, though. 100% of information is found on powerpoints that she posts online.

I love her subject. BUT she is the WORST professor I've ever had at SBCC. She gives only basics and sometimes it feels like she doesn't know much about this class.I wish I could drop the class and save my money. So, if it's your major and you really care about it-do NOT take her class! But if you just want an easy A for your general ed - go for it

Very easy, nice and chill teacher. Wants to see you succeed and will give extra credit or make things easier for the class. Great woman, I learned a lot from her. There is no way you will not get an A.

My fellow Canadian! What an amazing professor, he actually teaches you so much and it made me want to minor in PA so I could become a lawyer in the future:) He's a great person and understands what students go through. Just make sure you listen to the audio lecture and you're set for success!

The best professor I have had during my time in college. You will learn a lot in this course and leave feeling more motivated than ever. I cannot say enough good things. An excellent professor who truly cares about your learning and personal growth. This is the kind of professor who should be teaching at Harvard, MIT, or Stanford.

Incredible professor, would 100% take again. There are a few group projects, lots of fun lectures and interesting things he teaches outside the textbook. For example small claims court and how it works. He is an incredible teacher who will help you if you ask for it. Take his class!!!

Take Prof. Spitz's class if you can. Although there's quite a lot of reading (which was my biggest struggle), the topics are pretty easy to understand for the most part. He also offers extra credit, and he can be pretty funny during his lectures too. The only things he's really strict on is AI usage and in person attendance (unless necessary.)

Professor Spitz hot fire in class I turned into two life-time deals, mid litigation, across states. Before I was just a troll. Now I'm a damn troll with court filings and a calculator, along with legal tech and a payment processor mapped like war plans. If you want fluff, scroll TikTok. If you want game? It's this class. The invoices are proof.

The Daniel Spitz experience is something out of a T.V. show. He is the most charismatic and personable professor I have ever had. His lectures are super engaging and he makes them as easy for you to follow as possible with his handouts. I loved his B-law class so much I came back the next semester for Business of entertainment & loved it even more!

Really one of our best professors ever. Grades fairly, a lot of class participation and industry up-to-date information. A practicing lawyer, he discusses current cases in the news/entertainment business. The assignments are progressively more and more intricate but he scaffolds and you feel accomplished and professional at the end of the semester!

Really funny teacher, he keeps students engaged while lecturing and understands that students have other classes to focus on, besides his. Tests and exams are open note and moderately easy. He had the power to make the class so much fun for me!

Good teacher, really in-tune with current events and exams were open-book, open note. Relies heavily on studying outside of class though. Super accessible outside of class and gives fun bits during his lecture to keep students engaged, being confident and outgoing is the key in acing his class.

Spitz is the man. Never would I have thought that a Wednesday morning lecture would get me fired up. He brings the energy week in and week out. Favorite professor I've had thus far. 100% recommend this course and make sure to take it in person if possible.

Daniel Spitz is an outstanding professor. His lectures are engaging and prepare you for the exams/quizzes, and he'd end each one with some kind of motivational speech or comedy skit. He's a professor that truly cares for the well-being and academic performance of his students. Would highly recommend taking his class!

Professor Spitz is by far the best professor I've ever had. He genuinely cares about his students, allows notes during exams, and eliminates the stress of closed-book tests. His lectures are engaging, and he makes learning enjoyable. He truly sets the standard for what a great professor should be. Highly recommend taking his class!

Professor Spitz is the best!! His coursework is organized super well, and it was the same pattern of work that we'd get assigned every week, which made it a lot less stressful to balance this class with my other classes. He cares about his students and wants them to succeed. His lectures are easy to follow and pretty interesting too

Enjoy the ride. You'll learn a great deal. This instructor really cares about you with a great passion to involve his students in practical applications of studies that will lend themselves to any future career. Bravo!

Best professor in all of SBCC campus. Teaches you what you need to learn and is amazing at it. Such a fun class! He makes you feel seen & inspires you to do more. His curriculum is perfect and makes learning easy. Take his IN PERSON class. Truly never looked forward to going to school until I took this class. Wednesdays became my favorite day. :)

Fantastic professor. He gives funny and exciting lectures, has experience in the industry, and cares a lot about the quality of his teaching, and puts a lot of effort in. It's not difficult to do well in his class, but you will learn a lot and enjoy it.

Mr. Spitz was an incredible teacher! It is clear to see he loves teaching and cares deeply about his students! Bus 101 was a bit lecture-heavy and dense, with a lot of material to cover, but that wasn't really in Spitz's control. The workload is very reasonable with little homework. Spitz is also pretty funny/entertaining. I will take him again.

Professor Spitz is a practicing attorney & incorporates his experience from that into the lectures, which made them super interesting. I found the subject material to be very interesting & I like the way he organized his class. Note sheets were given to us to print & fill out while watching lectures. I found this to be an effective learning method.

If you show up to class and turn everything in you will get an A. He really cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He incorporates a lot of extra topics and several non traditional group projects. As he says the first day "I hope this is BEST class you take in college".

not bad of a class, wouldn't take again. if you take this class I would recommend taking it in online because the in person lectures were pointless. don't miss any assignment 1 discusion post will put you behind a lot. doesn't answer emails.

Professor Spitz provides many detailed audio lectures full of great information and provides note sheets to go with them. His grading criteria is clear and he provides great feedback. Make sure to understand the content of each lecture not just memorize terms before taking quizzes.
One of the best. If you have the chance to take him do it 100% amazing guy never met someone who didnt absolutely love him

HANDS DOWN one of the BEST professors at this school! A practicing lawyer and professor with a ton of experience and knowledge. He knows his stuff. TAKE HIM. He tried to pursue SNL so he is extremely funny and great at standup. Can't rave about him enough. Make sure you are on top of assignments and really study those handouts he gives out.

A surprisingly easy online class! His lectures were very fun and engaging, and he gives you handouts to fill out your notes with, which you're allowed to use on the exams. As long as you pay attention to the lectures and do all the handouts, you shouldn't have much trouble with this class! Just don't pay for the textbook, I never even touched mine.
Spitz really cares about his students, and prioritizes actual learning and experiences over busy work.
fun professor, nice lectures, don't get the textbook, pay attention and you should be ok. he doesn't like when students use their phones!

if you have a chance to take any of his classes DO IT! Prof. Spitz is hands down the best teacher I have ever had in my life. He truly cares about his students and he cares about what hes teaching you and tried his best to make it fun and interesting.
Literally one of the most amazing professors Ive had at sbcc. Such a nice guy and you can tell he genuinely enjoys teaching

I loved this class! This was my first business class and Mr.Spitz was amazing. He is so funny and is really engaged with the students. His lectures are super clear and easy to understand. He made this course really enjoyable and I would strongly recommend this class to anyone who has a slight interest in the business world.

Professor Spitz is an amazing teacher!! I really enjoyed his class and would definitely recommend. His lectures are very interesting and he helps you out with note taking by providing handouts you can fill out. His tests are a bit hard, but if you take notes you will pass and he typically curves his tests to the average scores. Loved this class!!
Great teacher !

This class was accelerated, so you have to stay on top of assignments and log on several times during the week. The information you learn is very valuable especially if you are a marketing major or looking to start your own online business. The material is not hard if your read your chapters and do the required assignments. I truly enjoyed this cou
Online it's always hard to measure anyone, but Prof. Stampe seemed very knowledgeable about e-Commerce. The class was enjoyable despite moving at a face pace since I took the 8 week version.
Great

This is my third marketing class (my first with Mrs. Stampe) and can I just say, every marketing class so far I have taken has been amazing, especially this one. If you're in marketing and this is a class you can take, take it! Like any other class, work for your grade and earn it. Plus, you learn she's a professor that cares about her students.
Great teacher! Some of the due dates were messed up in the system but she gave everyone a fair chance to catch up in case they missed something.
Great class! I would definitely take again.
I loved this class, and Jessica made it super fun! Group projects as midterm and final, and not too hard material. Would absolutely take again!

Realy enjoyed the class. Clear and concise weekly reading, quizzes & discussion. She takes the time to read your papers & offer personalized feedback. A rare treat. The Textbook was my issue. Minuscule print, overpriced, and not engaging. I actually used a book from a similar class. Would love to take another class with this Professor.
I liked that class and learned a lot!! Thank you for a great semester

Prof Stampe is awesome! Great communicator (we were regularly updated with any changes to due dates or assignments, and what to expect in the week ahead) and seems genuinely invested in her students. Would absolutely take another class with her if I could!

I took my first online course with prof. Stampe, it was really clear and easy to follow. Class discussions were always interesting, and I learned a lot from them. The teacher is always available for any doubt or question. I totally recommend this class!

Jessica Stampe is the best teacher I have come across in years. I'm a full time working person and that didn't hurt me in her class. She gives really good feedback and accepted late work which saved me in this class. She expects a lot but it's worth it. Her emphasis in our class were a group midterm and final project which was major cool
Great caring teacher, easy homework. Textbook needed for quizzes but can be done through internet as well. Group projects for midterm and final which are both fun!

Professor Stampe is an inspiring instructor who is clearly an expert in her field. She integrates fun, which make learning an important topic, Personal Finance, not only easy to learn and absorb, but helps students integrate these objectives into our daily lives. A real joy and rewarding experience.
Prof Stampe is a really caring teacher and super nice to her students.
USEFUL MATERIAL NOT JUST IN DAILY LIFE BUT IN WORKFORCE AS WELL, THIS IS NOT A CLASS VERY YOU END UP LOSING THE INFORMATION FROM LECTURE, HOMEWORK OR PROJECTS.

I've had both good and bad experiences with teachers at sbcc, but with jessica it was really great. She made it easy. She really puts in time and dedication to helping her students and i would happily take another one of her classes if I got the chance

I really thought this was a great class, I am so glad that I took it. There was quite a bit of work but also a lot of information to cover and knowledge to gain!! I thought Prof. Stampe did a great job!
Encourages students to do their best and keep up.
Good class! should change the book.

I took this class cause I needed it in my major, but the content is sooo boring. Teacher is nice and has the cutest laugh ever. She gives good feedback, is understanding and really kind. Instead of taking long tests for midterm and final she made it fun and had us do presentations instead. Show up, do the homework/quiz and you will get a good grade
I loved this class. Learned a lot and had fun too. Professor was very nice and always willing to help/answer emails.
OMG, THIS PROFESSOR IS WONDERFUL. SHE TALKS CLEARLY AND SHE IS RESPONSIBLE FOR HER STUDENTS. SHE IS NICE AND EASYGOING PERSON. SHE IS ALSO BEAUTIFUL.
She is excellent teacher. Her class is so easy and clarity. She worries for explain each point to their students get goods grades

She is the nicest and her class is super easy and straight forward. She is well organized and will definitely recommend for personal knowledge and also as an ideal UC elective. She encourages a lot of participation from students on online forums.
great teacher all around highly recomend her

Professor Stampe is a very sweet teacher that is there for you. She made a class about bland spreadsheets and responsibility very exciting!. The work load is not much, but be prepared to work hard on what you do have. Overall, if you are looking for a class to teach you all about money from budgets to the stock market, this is the class for you.

Jessica Stampe is an amazing teacher. She's always so friendly and she arrives to class every Monday with a big smile on your lips. She seems to really care about her students and I think that's a good thing when being a teacher. I would like to thank her so much for this semester and also wishing her all luck in the future. #sweden
Shes great! always there for help and has great knowledge in the finance field
Very enriching class, manageable workload. Would reccomend taking regardless of your major.

Jill's class has a fair workload, with assignments due every 2 weeks. The lectures and textbook provide the info needed to do well. Using Unstuck study alongside the course materials helped me stay on top of the content.

Jill is a great professor! The canvas was really easy to follow and she sent lots of reminders/announcements. the work load was really light and we had 2 weeks to turn assignments in. I really recommend taking this class!
Was a bit of homework but only ever due, every other week. She was an easy grader and assignments were mostly simple to complete. She was very kind the few times i needed to contact her.

Very interesting class, gets you to think about media and society in a way you likely haven't before. The textbook readings are dense but I thought that was kind of alleviated by the fact they cover very interesting topics. Prof herself gives good feedback too. She takes a while to grade things but it's not really her fault. Overall would recommend

Prof Stein's course was very engaging, yet not hard to pass at all. The topics and assignments were very straightforward, but her passion and lesson plans made me interested in a major/subject I had never considered before. Loved, loved her course, would love to take another from her in the future.

Professor Stein's course requires some self-accountability as assignments are due every two weeks. There is a decent amount of textbook reading that will best prepare you for quizzes and exams. Moderate amount of writing, but she doesn't place weight on it being professional writing, which takes some pressure off. Super nice and amazing professor!
Lectures cover a wide range of topics relating to media and sociology. Overall very interesting topics covered.

Great professor. Uses her own book; reading required and almost all test answers are found in just the book so don't need lectures or slides. Lots of different assignments to cushion your grade if tests do not go well. Hw is due every two weeks. Research paper that she gives you lots of time to work on. Overall pretty good and insightful class.

The class was good as long as you put in the effort on the assignments. Half-assing it won't get you an A or B. She is really kind and gives good feedback. She is clear on what she wants from the students.

This class brought me a new perspective to the world around me. Some of the papers and field work were outside of my norm but proved to be very interesting and insightful. Definitely not going to be a SOC major, but I did enjoy this class.

My grade was slipping towards the end of the semester, and I met with her in her office and she was super accommodating!! She let me make up so much of my late work. She is super sweet and knowledgeable.
made class fun!

Love her class. Prof. Davis is more than flexible and wants to help you pass. She isnt too homework heavy, and her exams are straightforward and easy if you read the material. She really gets you thinking like a sociologist in a way that is fun.

Huge pro; All the exams are taken at home, online, so you can use all your notes. Her lectures can be a bit dense, but it is an interesting subject. The textbook is good for taking notes outside of class, but if you take enough IN class, you might pass without. Participation is graded by in class assignments, so make sure you're present!
Literally all her exams and quizzes are online. Same with the lectures. Go to class though because she gives little 10 point assignments almost every class. Super chill class though.

Professor Stein is one of the professors that has made her understanding and care towards her students evident while teaching her passion. There are many resources given to ensure the success of the student. The class is not difficult. Showing up to class, doing the readings, and the assignments are all necessary to at least get a decent grade.
The class is literally graded on the online test and quizzes, so study! Doesnt give homework but you should attend class regularly since she gives a lot of in-class work.

She's such a sweet soul and very passionate about Sociology. All her quizes and tests (even the mf FINAL) are online. She's very flexible with the work and gives a lot of time for each quiz and exam. She clearly wants her students to pass the class, but she has some assignments in class to prove attendance of students.

she is very professional. im not the best at doing assignments but i currently have a 91 and its a reasonably easy class. def get the book cuz the info for the quizzes are on the chapter that the quiz is assigned for

So disorganized! She didnt even upload any information to Canvas until the third day of the semester, doesnt follow the assignment list on her own syllabus or give notice when shes going to change something, takes weeks to grade, and gives almost no notice about exams, including the final! Definitely not how an intro class should be structured.

One of my favorite professors. The class is set up so you can succeed and she offers many extra credit opportunities. The tests are moderately hard but if you study for them you should be fine. Super easy 10 point quizzes on canvas each week, 1 writing assignment, 2 midterms and a final. Never used my textbook and finished with an A- in the class.

Super easy class - just make sure to do the quizzes each week & go to class for the in class activity points. Her lectures aren't that boring and you can tell she really cares about her students getting good grades. Lots of extra credit opportunities. Super nice and genuine person, I highly recommend taking her SOC101 class!
This class is not to difficult. If you show up to all the lectures you dont really need to read the textbook

Technically you don't have to go to class bc her power points are on canvas, but i felt that I learned better while going to class and listening. Textbook is not necessary. not much studying is required just a few read throughs of the notes and you should be fine. quizzes do add up so make sure you have an A-or B+ average. 1 super easy paper!

Professor Stein was a great teacher. Her lectures were interesting and engaging, she really cared about her subjects, and if you went to class and paid attention, you can do really well on all of the assignments and exams. She is really easy to work with and is always there to help.
Professor Stein, knows her subject and delivers it with passion.

Jill is really nice and I like her lectures. She has a cool background in history and wrote the textbook used in class. Her tests are HARD. i've only gotten 70-80% on them. You really have to study the book, and take good notes. She goes over a lot in class that is on the tests. She also gives out extra credit.
Oh well, now I am officially Sociology major. Her class totally converted me into Sociology from Law major.
I really appreciate what she taught me during Soc 101! She is amazing!!!!!
easiest class ive taken at sbcc, most time consuming part was watching the movie (which you get to choose)

Great intro class! The professor is organized and explains material clearly and gives helpful tips for assignments. Easy grader and allows late work. No detailed feedback but gives general advice to the class. Lectures and textbooks are the main tools. Engaging and easy to follow!

Take. This. Class. This class is hands down the most ideal GE you can possibly take. He gives you ample resources if your interested in film, he is so passionate about what he teaches, he is very obviously an educated professor and he provides SO much support under circumstances. This class= 0 stress and he keeps it super interesting. take him.

Easiest A ever. You just need to watch movies (which I love anyway) and write about it. You need to write a paper on the film each week which is slightly tricky because you can't use first person, and you can't just explain the plot it's more about the techniques used in the film and the themes. Just attend class and don't be on your phone :)

Laid back, super fly, and loves his job; Professor Stinson's Introduction to Film Studies class was my easiest class I took, ALL YOU HAVE TO DO IS GO TO CLASS! I promise you it's an easy A as long as you're there! One of the best teachers at SBCC! It's literally watching your favorite movies, and connecting it to class. EASY A! GO TO CLASS!

The workload for each week consists of writing a one-page report on any film. It takes about 20 minutes plus just watching a movie once a week. Half the time, the lecture consists of watching a movie for the class period, which you can write a report on. He also gives all the answers for the final before you take it. Impossible not to get an A.

Easy A for sure. Once a week page-long film analysis, online midterm and he gives the answers if you show up to class. There's a 4-6 page final paper as well. Pretty easy material and you watch a movie about every other class (though they were questionable lol). Having the book is beneficial for the weekly assignments, but not necessarily needed.
i want to die next to a man like him. #needthat (lectures were great also) (changed my life)

Easiest A of my life. Once a week movie reviews that are fun and easy, online midterms, but if you just show up to class he literally gives you the answers to them, and one 5 page paper for the final that was pretty much just a big movie review. Easy material, very cool and funny guy, would take his class again! Everybody in my class loved him.

Grading has been good, not very strict, but the films shown in class are very graphic and uncomfortable. There are so many amazing films out there, yet we mainly (for in-class screenings) watch heavily sexual movies. Very creepy. Also, he is very strict on phones; he will call you out in front of class for having it out.

Basically free credits. Very easy homework, 2 tests (which he gives the answers for in class) & 1 paper. He's a super nice guy that is passionate about film & with extensive experience. A lot of people stop showing up, & it seemed to sadden him. Attendance isn't necessary, but def recommended. The class itself consumes very little time. Take it!

Stinson is a great guy. He is very caring and compassionate about film. He might be the easiest grader of all time and he also super funny. I recommend t the class if you want a chill class with an easy A.

Professor Stinson makes this class engaging and easy. He's passionate about film and his lectures are informative. Assignments are straightforward, and he's generous with late submissions. unstuck AI was a great study tool alongside the textbook.

Great professor and very funny. He's really kind and wants all his students to do well. I had him in spring for 101 and summer class 173 and both very light workload and super manageable. Would retake if I could

Michael is great. In class, all we did is read what we wrote, and he would give very insightful feedback and encourage the class to as well. He recommended us filmmakers, books, and movies to look into based on our style. every assignment I got 100% no matter what Michael really just wants to hear what you write and push you to be great.
Great
He enjoys his job. Very minimal work

Great professor that actually made me interested and engaged in class. Easily graded screening reports that are due every week, but you can submit them as late as you want for FULL credit. Just do not miss lectures, easy A.
He is very understanding and informative. Very engaging, passionate, and takes a big interest in his profession.

Prof. Stinson was amazing. We watch a film every other week and go over the midterms and finals together in class. Overall he makes the class very engaging and is always prepared to put on an awesome movie for the class.

Prof Stinson was awesome. Super easy to talk to and very understanding about assignments. You only have 1 screening report a week which is graded very easily. Goes over every exam before you take it, super fun and easy class. Oh and you also watch a movie every other week, and they are actually good movies.

Super nice and laid back teacher. One page screening report a week , along with a midterm, final, and final paper which he prepares you for extremely well. We watch a movie in class every other class, and he is always asking students to submit questions to a discussion post every so often because he truly wants his students to succeed
Super knowledgeable but amazing at making film simple to understand. His passion for film carries over into his teaching in the best way.
One of the best professors ive had so far in my sbcc experience, very professional and clear instructions.

TAKE THIS CLASS! Prof. Stinson is such an amazing professor that is genuinely passionate about what he does. Once a week there are screen reports which are simple and easy. He encourages you to use Chat GPT to get inspiration on your papers. There is one longer paper due at the end of the semester. No need to buy the textbook if you go to class.
Really interesting and enjoyable. Easy A as long as you do your work which he accepts late.

Michael Stinson is a professor who has a clear passion for teaching film. His lectures were very informative, but also fun and engaging (which is impressive considering the size of my class). The movies he screened were super interesting and not what you would expect. Super easy grader and overall a great teacher who I would definitely recommend.

The professor clearly knows what he is talking about without making the lectures boring. Weekly one-page homework assignments, a midterm/final test that he gives the answers for, and a 13-page essay that is the same as the weekly homework but longer. I have already recommended this class to a friend who is taking it next semester. TAKE THIS CLASS

Really interesting lectures, and made me think about the way movies were made that I never had before. You have to do 1 screening report every week, a midterm, 1 paper, and a final test. You're not graded on much so if you don't do the assignments even if they're not hard, you'll fail. Doesn't give much feedback for writing which is a downside.

very creepy. studied abroad with him. He privately made inappropriate comments towards female students , pried students about personal things, and made several sexual references during his lectures. He is very self absorbed for a community college professor, doesn't allow questions, and attempts to intimidate students. yelled at an autistic kid.
She's amazing, so helpful and so nice too.

She is so amazing. I took this class online. I was so far back with classwork and homeowrk due to my work and personal life and she was very considerate with helping me out. I'm not really good with writing essays but she has always reassured me with how amazing I write and there are very minor changes to my essays.
She's Goated.

I truly enjoyed taking Kathy's class. She is a true sweetheart. There were times that her instruction was unorganized but if you pointed it out, she would always fix it and make it clear. So chill with late work. Do is your work and you will pass. The only reason you should come to class is the free writes which you cant make up. Great teacher!

Professor Stotter is truly an amazing, kind teacher I could ever possibly meet in college. She was my first English professor when I started college- and she was so sweet and caring. She truly cares about her students. I'm in my second semester, and I still think of her and how empathetic she was. Keep up good work, and you will do great!
She's a very sweet teacher who cares about her students. She is always available if you need help and gives great feedback on essays. Keep up with assignments and you'll do great.

You won't regret Kathy's ENG 110E! Don't let the extra lab time dissuade you, it's 100x better than ENG 110. All work can be done in-class w/ endless support. Almost no homework. You will get an A if you just show up & try. 3 papers made up of the weekly assignments copy/pasted together (papers basically write themselves.) ADHD friendly.

Kathy was very sweet, I had her last year and by showing up and doing your work, you will pass the class. She gives feedback, and allows resubmissions (something I dont see much in Eng111). Shes very sweet and truly cares for her students succeeding. Would recommend!!!!!!

An absolutely amazing professor, I highly recommend taking her classes if you get the chance. Communication is key as well as respect. She really wants you to pass the class and will help you through it all! Also very understanding

She makes the class very simple, she gives very good feedback on the essays. There is a total of 3 essays, no tests and one weekly homework assignment. Do the reading, and answer questions in class and she'll like you. She has good office hours, and is always willing to help. She accepts late work and will let you work on your essay for more credit
Kathy is the most amazing English teacher I have ever had.I was so traumatized by past teachers, but she made me regain my confidence in writing.

This teacher helped me so much! She is a very caring teacher that not only wants you to pass and puts a lot of effort into it, she wants you to have a grade you are happy with. She always took time outside of class to help me write the essays and always was there for me. Awesome and helpful teacher!! Especially when you are struggling a bit.

I'm really glad I took this online class with Kathy. She's very empathetic and wants her students to succeed and pass the class. Goes out of her way to contact you if you are missing assignments. If you communicate with her and explain why/where you are struggling, it's very likely that she will help you out as much as she can.

Such an amazing teacher! I learned so much with her and she gives great feedback. She has every student's best interest in mind and really cares. If you want to succeed in this class reach out to her for help and communicate with her if you are having trouble. I wish I could have taken her class in person!

Kathy Stotter has been an absolutely wonderful teacher to work with. She wants all of her students to succeed and continues to share that concept throughout the course. She appreciates communication and will walk you through tough assignments. Her passion for teaching shows. I wish I could take another class from her. Take her if you want to learn.

One of my favorite teachers I've ever had. She has a great understanding for her students and is VERY flexible with deadlines, she is willing to help you through a lot. She also lets you redo any papers for a better grade. She has such a great class and you are really able to improve and learn.

Professor Stotter is a very nice lady. She is a good professor but sometimes she pushes her political views on the class too much which is the only reason I didnt give her 5/5. She gives great feedback though and is very reasonable/ accepts late work and genuinely cares about your personal life. Very nice Professor.

Kathy is a very nice Professor but she is unorganized, and gives a lot of unclear assignments. She gives us directions and the next class she switches it up. She hardly ever put grades into canvas so you aren't aware of your grade. She does give you a lot of chances to fix papers or turn in assignments bu I do not recommend.

The class would be much better if she would be more organized. She keeps make you do things at last minute because she forgets and sometimes she doesn't even give you a feedback and you'll never get back what you wrote. She often gives unclear instructions. She is a nice person but should organize her lectures in a better way

If you're looking for a class to actually receive good feedback on your essays and drafts, do not take this class. Sucks at grading and also makes you do everything last minute because she falls behind.
She is an okay teacher. However, she always fall behind and she makes you catch up at the end of the year. So we are always rushing to finish papers.

Kathy is one of the best teachers at city college. Do your work and you'll get a good grade. She will help you more than you want (Its a good thing, I promise). She wants you to succeed more than anything. Super easy to talk to. Kathy knew my name by the second week of class. She's just awesome all around.

Took Kathy for English 98 and has to be one of the best Professor I have had at Sbcc. Shes very caring and respectful. Also is very easy to meet with her oustide of class. She gives great feedback and makes the class fun. Would recommend taking her class!

Kathy is a great teacher who above all else really cares about her students success. It is widely agreed amongst many of her students that we have noticed she continuously goes out of her more than most teachers I've had. She knows that students have a life outside of school and is very understanding if you talk to her.

Kathy cares a lot about her students and truly seems to want them to pass. If something seems unclear, she will clearify it for you. She spends extra time giving feedback on papers, so the more effort you put into the essays, the more help you will receive. Take her course because she is funny, kind, and will really push for you to pass the class.

Professor Stotter is a fantastic teacher. She cares about her students well being and that can be an obscurity among teachers. She is very clear and gives great feedback on your papers. I highly recommend taking her class. You won't regret it.
Good teacher. Pushes you to do the best you can. Has high expectations for her students. Will work with you to make sure you pass. Use her feedback. Will really improve your papers
Super cool teacher as long as you do your work and show up. Very friendly and funny and tries to help you out as much as possible. Can have a short temper sometimes though

I just took Kathy Stotter's English 98 class Fall 2016. I loved it very much! Kathy is a wonderful person and English professor! She's very clear and her course is solid! She is very smart and helps students with their essays and is always willing to help and guide students anytime! She will help you make sure you have a passing portfolio!
She is an awesome professor, makes you work for your grade, but guides you along the way. Her course is solid.

Professor Strenn was an incredible teacher! He was always available outside of class and genuinely cared about his students. His lectures were excellent and covered most of the topics covered in the lab. He also offered many extra credit opportunities and was easy to reach outside of class for any assistance.

Professor Strenn is awesome, this was my last cs class at sbcc. If I knew how good he was earlier, I would have taken him for every class. He is a great lecturer, he pushes people to participate, he is always encouraging and is willing to help. I learned so much with him. By far the best cs professor at bcc. This was my favorite class at sbcc.
great professor, super nice. enjoyed his lectures. assignments were time consuming, but that was expected. don't take the class if you don't like coding, obviously. he was great!

One of the best professors I've had. The class isn't easy but if you put in the time you should be fine. The only way to truly learn DSA is by working on projects, so I definitely recommend getting involved with the team programming, CS or robotics club to apply the skills you learn, which he can give you extra credit for.

Good professor. Not many assignments, they were kinda hard. Enough time to complete them. He would go over the allotted time for lecture which was lowkey annoying. You would have to stay to get credit. The lab would start really late also. Overall good, him going over the class time was annoying but the inclass assignments did help. Would recommend

Best CS professor at SBCC currently. Amazing lectures, original coding assignments, and extra credit opportunities. Very clear grading criteria. Tests are relatively difficult with averages being 70%, but they are extremely fair with each question simply being a variation of an in class activity. Would 100% take again, learned so much.
Best CS class at CC.

He's pretty good, the only problem is he THINKS he's the coolest ever. Also, every class went 20-60 minutes late, which was very disrespectful, as often class time was taken up with him talking about his own life. It was very hard to meet with him during his office hours, and he wasn't very helpful whenever you could find a slot. But he's ok.

Best CS professor ever. On the first day of lecture I was shocked because he's truly in a league of his own. Dude clearly knows what he's talking about and it shows through his beam of confidence, public speaking skills, and true passion for this subject. Tests are extremally fair I would say. Please take him if you care about learning. I love him

Professor Strenn is an amazing teacher. I really learned a lot in his class. That being said he should be held accountable for making students download spyware, and pressuring them to buy his eBook to be successful. A book HE profits from. 5/5 teacher 2/5 ethics. Mind you the only tab you can have open on tests is a purchasable book from him.
Professor Strenn taught me so much. His lectures on Big(O) and sorting algorithms actually helped me in other CS classes I have taken since then. He is one of the best out there.

He's a garbage professor who isn't very helpful. When you go to him for help, he does a horrible job in giving assistance and barely gives any assistance. His TA's aren't very helpful either as they refuse to meet through zoom which makes it very tough. His assignments are also very tough as well and is hard to get an A in his class. Avoid him.

Never coded before but now I feel really confident with MATLAB and I had a lot of fun learning how to learn in this course. He has the best TA's who are always available and easy to access and will literally walk through assignments with you if needed. I would definitely recommend Strenn to anyone he's been my favorite professor so far.

His programming assignments are way too difficult and are almost impossible to figure out. Also he isn't very helpful during his office hours and expects you to figure it out all on your own. His TA's aren't very helpful at all either. Also he makes you use proctorio as well which isn't ideal and he makes it very tough to get an A in his class.

Strenn is a great (and hilarious) professor who really cares about you. He wrote the textbook for the class so make sure to use it because it contains critically important examples that you can go back to at any time when you are having trouble understanding something. He does breakout rooms after his lecture and you should stick around for those.

Professor Strenn is great! He is always willing to help during office hours and lab. As long as you pay attention in class and do all the assignments, you should do well in this course. I learned a lot from this class and I recommend it to anyone who is interested in computer science!

Professor Strenn is incredibly nice and is very supportive of his students. His in-class assignments and homework assignments will give you a very hands-on approach to learning the material he teaches in his lectures. By going to class and making good use of your lab time and working with him and the tutors, you will learn a lot.

Professor Strenn is in the league of top professors at SBCC! He is beyond helpful and extremely knowledgeable and maybe most important of all he is patient! He will go over your code with you piece by piece and explain what is going on and how all the parts work together with each other. Take his classes you will not regret it!

Professor Strenn was awesome. He is always accepting and welcoming of questions, and will go back and explain something to someone in a different and creative way if they are struggling to understand. Our semester was entirely on zoom, which is a shame because I think his class would have been awesome in person.
