Prof Strenn teaches well. Subject is interesting on how to use matlab software and programming language to solve science/engineering problems. No lecture notes posted, so buy the $20 guidebook which has all the lecture notes and more. Open note exams. In class assignments mix it up. Can be harder for first time programmers, otherwise not.

Prof Strenn is a good teacher. Data Struct is a pretty hard subject. Assignments can be tough. No lecture notes posted, so buy the $20 guidebook which has lecture notes. Exams are open note. In class assignments mix it up.
Certainly a hard class but there is no doubt Strenn is a genius... He will work with you to understand the Computer Science theory as long as you show determination to put in the work.
Grade is primarily based on a few tests and your coding projects throughout the year. His office hours aren't great but he is willing to work with you on any of your questions.
Best professor I've had.. really makes you enjoy class.

Awesome guy; knows his material. His lectures were fine. The real learning comes from the project assignments. Tests are fair if you attend class and go over the textbook. The best comp sci professor I've taken at SBCC, he makes the material less dry with fun analogies and real-world applications.
Best prof in the department, he's half of what makes the department so great

This guy gives some of the best lectures I've ever attended. He has a guidebook for the lecture slides, as well as a textbook, but most of the good info you get out of this class is from what he says on the fly. He's very knowledgeable, and his explanations are clear. Lectures are mandatory because he has a graded activity at the end of each class.

Easily the best CS teacher I have ever had! Hefty assignments, but if you put in the work you will get a good grade. Take as many classes as you can with him. Regarding the past couple reviews: he has been department chair for the past year, he is an awesome guy, but he has been overworked an stressed out since he has become DC.
He is like the nicest guy there is but he is such a horrible teacher. He is mean and is not there for you when you need help. He makes you feel stupid for asking questions. Stay away.

Professor Strenn is a nice guy but his lectures are horrible. He expects you to know everything, and when you don't he makes you feel like garbage. The assignments are doable but only with help or if you are really good already. Final is okay.
homegirl doesn't grade anything until the last minute, grades harshly if it isn't exactly what she's looking for, then never gives any real feedback.

She assigns so much busy work that is very time consuming, and then grades it very harshly. I did not receive a 100% on anything that I turned in the whole semester, even the little assignments. She would always find something to critique and mark you off for it.

I took her INTRO class and it felt like an advanced.She expects you to know advanced film language .She is such a hard grader.There is so much components you have to read online and the quizzes are hard sometimes.I would never take her again. For the final she assigns a 6-8 page essay, forcing you to follow an essay guideline she posts.

I took her late star class and it was alright. Watching movies every week and writing about 3 pages for them could be time consuming for some, but overall the workload is doable. The only complaint I had was sometimes she was not able to provide movies that we had to watch on our own and write about, students may not have any streaming services.
She is very passionate about what she teaches and the Study of Film. Very fast-paced class so make sure you take notes and actually watch the movies which will help you succeed.
His lectures are amazing. Just hard when having to type those essays he assigns.

I liked this class a lot! There was a quiz each week and around 6 discussions throughout the semester. There were 2 essays, both of which took me a couple of days to finish (5 pages minimum, I wrote 8 each time). I disagree with the other reviews; the expectations are clear, and there is plenty of material to write about. Would recommend.
1 midterm and 1 final essay (6 pages). No busy work, every assignment has a purpose and helps understand the lessons. (No more than 2 assignments per week).

Professor Suarez is very passionate about what he teaches, however I wished he had been more organized and clear with what he expects on essays. It is really difficult to understand what he wants when writing essays in his class. He expects 6-10 pages in an essay which is manageable, however there is not enough material to accurately write about.

Professor Suarez is an alright professor. In my opinion, he's not good or bad but his essays are something else. They have to be very specific and you can only use the information given by him. I would recommend taking him if you only want to put in real effort twice in the class the whole semester.

I really enjoyed taking history with Prof. Suarez, the material he teaches and the way he does so makes his classes very interesting and I always look forward to attending. Amid COVID, his class online is very accessible and laid out clearly. I look forward to taking another class with him in the spring.

Prof. Suarez may seem tough at the beginning of the semester but it doesn't take long to warm up to his style. He is very kind and has a unique perspective when it comes to teaching history that most professors don't have. Prof. Suarez succeeds in making history interesting for students who may not be history majors.

Suarez has to be hands down the best teacher I have ever had here at SBCC so far. He truly cares about his students and He lectures on things you actually need to know about the class giving you papers that will help you on the midterm and test. I would take him again in the future.

Suarez is amazing! I took history of mexico with him. He is so passionate about history and it shows in his teaching. He wants you to have fun while learning, and gives straight forward, group assignments. Make sure you keep up with the readings, they do help understand lectures. He is great at communicating and is always available to help you.

Mr. Suarez told us lies about ritual sacrificies in Mexico. It s clear now after finding pyramids of skulls in Mexico of women and childs includes that the horror political system BEFORE the arival od spaniards is ONE (Nnot the only) explanation of the evolution of the democracy in LATAM.

If you're into listening to a guy talk with a slideshow behind him for an hour then stick with him. He's unconventional when teaching 100 so if you just show up to do the (all) in class assignments A for you. I loved all his stories that can help us and he really cares about you. I took in all of his advice for life/college. He's a funny and great!

Although he may refer to El Camino College a lot it really does not matter. He is inspirational and a super chill man. Do not get on his bad side, just put in the effort and make him feel like you do really care about your education. Listen to what he has to say, you will not regret it!
talks about himself way too much, you will learn more about history than the actual class. He refers to his el camino students way too much and will make you feel like that school is better.
He's okay. Talks too much about himself and says the same thing through out the semester. Would recommend you take this course from someone else if you actually want to get something out of it.

Suarez is a good guy. His lectures make sense and he genuinely wants to see you transfer. BUT he repeats himself throughout the semester. He talks a lot but you won't learn more than a few things. Take pd 100 if you want easy credits but keep in mind he WILL talk you down compared to his el camino students. Also, you'll hardly use the books you buy
If you take another PD 100 class your not smart.
Take his class and follow his advice; seriously, just do it. You won't be sorry and you'll guaranteed be extremely successful at SBCC.

Jason is by far the best instructor I've had since being at sbcc. He really cares about his students and you can clearly tell he is passionate about teaching. You have a few easy assignments, and as long as you do them you'll get an A. TAKE THIS CLASS.

One of the best teachers I have ever had. Suarez is extremely helpful and he really cares about his students. Just don't be late or else he will yell at you especially on the first day. I would reccomend anybody to take him.

Suarez is a great teacher. He teaches at el camino college and always talks about how much he loves it there. The class really helps you get settled into college and learn what you need to do to succeed. Take this class....EASY A
Awesome teacher helps with anything
Best teacher ever!!!!!!

Jason is a very great teacher. All of his assignments, projects and topics anyone can use in or out of college. Straight forward, not strict on due dates, strict on attendance and work, a little hard time getting a hold of him, he gets you into your assignments, everything he teaches is online in his website. Helps anyone with anything they need,

Best teacher I have had, very interested in his students and wants all his students to succeed. Is a great help in anything students need. Focuses a lot on having connections and working the system. He has connections with all services at SBCC and can help set up counseling apts. Really helped me figure out what I want to do with my Education.

Best, most caring teacher i have ever had. He always willing to help his students. One of the few teacher ive had that actually seems like he cares about his students suceeding. I definetly recommend this teacher and his class.
He's the most helpful, caring teacher I have had. He is always willing to help his students. It was a pleasure being in his pd 100 class
Great teacher super passionate and understanding. I also took her sociology class intro to women studies she was awesome there too!

Great teacher who deeply understands the material she teaches. Very forgiving with late-work and gives amazing feedback on larger assignments. I took IWGS online and the reading load was decent, but the homework per week was next to none, usually just one reading reflection and one discussion. Take her class!!

I took the online history class and it is very well organized. There is a lot of reading/writing but she is very accommodating. Anytime I had a question or needed help she would respond back in a timely manner. Be sure to keep up with all the assignments and the class should run smoothly, I would definitely recommend her as a History professor.

She is a super sweet teacher, definitely passionate, and cares a lot about her students. The content covered was interesting and watching documentaries and videos were some of the highlights of her course for me. You just have to be on top of the assignments and quizzes she gives and it'll be easy to get an A.
Very sweet and understanding professor. Always checks up on us to make sure we are doing okay outside of class. I would recommend her to any student.

If you can take one of her classes TAKE IT! She is amazing and truly cares about her students and the content she is teaching. She inspires me to vote and share my voice! Literally my favorite professor here at SBCC!
Very sweet professor. Easy grader. If you do the assignments it is an easy A.

An extremely caring and sympathetic professor. There is a bit of reading/videos to watch, but the workload itself is very light. There are no test/quizzes, and the final was a very easy (made to be stress free) essay. The only graded assignments are summaries and discussion boards. The class is structured very nicely, 10/10 professor!

Class was really straight forward. Reading summaries and discussions every week. It's not hard work but it is a lot. You read 2-4 very long articles every week and have to summarize each, so the textbook is a must. No tests and/or quizzes, and the final is a reflection paper that isn't to difficult prompt and requirement-wise. Just so much reading!

This class is a lot of work, but it is manageable. Professor Swiontek is pretty clear about what she is looking for in the discussion posts, and once you have that figured out it is pretty easy to achieve a full score every time. Her lectures can be pretty long, and there is a lot of material to read through. She accepts late if you let her know.

The amount of study work for the class is obscene. We were reading 160 pages a week and watching 2-4 hours of documentary. And that's not even starting on the actual graded assignments. She is a very nice person, and was very good and open to communication. So if the study load won't bother you go for it. But if you struggle with studying, avoid.

I have one thing to say. If you like history and want to actually learn something, DO NOT take this class. I regret not trying to find a good professor, and also not using this site before signing up for a class without doing research on the professor

I am a 4.0 student, one week into the semester and I am about to drop this class for the sole reason she uses INQUISITIVE. She posts clear lectures and canvas quizzes but then ADDITIONALLY makes you take tests on inquisitive. I got 100% on canvas quizzes and 68% on inquisitive. Just be okay with the way inquisitive grades if you take this class.

I took Professor Swiontek's women's studies class and loved it!! She is amazing and taught me so much valuable information. She is one of the best professors I have had an SBCC. She really cares about her students and wants them to succeed.

Professor Swiontek was one of the most clear and organized teachers I have ever had. I took History 102 with her online over the summer and it was very clear that if you followed directions and turned your work in on time, you will get an A. Additionally, she provided extra credit opportunities!

One of the best teachers I've ever had! She cares so much about her students and her lectures are super interesting and engaging. I enjoyed the class so much and would take it with her again without hesitation

Dr. Swiontek was a decent teacher, the usual. Her class had really boring lectures but easy A for sure - a group assignment everyday and lots of opportunities for extra credit. Essays were not bad. The hard part of her class was homework and when class went online because of COVID. Lots of busy work but overall a nice, understanding teacher.
She is amazing, caring and straightforward! You'll pass if you attend class :)

SBCC better be paying Danielle top dollar because she is a real gem!! This class was super fun and she really valued our input about what kind of assignments were helpful and which weren't. Truly believe everyone should take her for this class and all of her other ones!!!

Dr. Swiontek deserves immense respect for two reasons: she is a brilliant Stanford educated historian and she has no ego. She is so chill when it comes to grading. She is an educator. She teaches. She doesnt crack the whip out of some inner need for power. I learned so much. Her rating should be higher. Anyone at CC would be lucky to have her.

She's amazing. She gives so much extra credit--Do it & it is almost hard to not get an A. I'm not the biggest fan of history, but I really enjoyed her class. She is so sweet and understanding. There are no tests- just one paper and a final, the rest is simple classwork.

Class is jus group discussion of the readings assigned from book. At least skim it, get the gist, & ur good. If you didn't read at all, they go over it in the class anyways Homework is a prewrite of the readings assigned. Book was difficult for me personally so I would take a look. She also cancelled class like 10 times Many small ex credit things

Last semester was my first semester of college and this class was such a great place to start. Swiontek really knows what she's talking about and makes the class easy. Make sure you spend time studying for the final and don't start the paper right before its due and you'll definitely earn your A. She's 10/10 teacher.

Swiontek is the best professor i've had at SBCC so far! She's a fair grader and always willing to help you with work if you need it. She's also very relatable and honest which makes class time fly by! As long as you are prepared for the test and have decent writing skills, you will pass this class and leave it having learned something valuable.

Swiontek is a loving mother and knowledgable professor. However, her class structure seems odd and it can easily be seen as an English class. Every assignment is in groups of 4-5. Summed up she lectures one day, shows a video, next day is group work. Repeat next week. Then throw in essays, and essay for final, and the online work (ez) Take for ez A
Took her 2x because she is the best!
Very organized and super sweet!

Danielle is honestly one of the best Professors I've ever had. History isn't even my major (I took this class for a GE requirement) but her dedication to the subject matter made me actually think about switching my major to history. Everyone at SBCC should be honored to have this kind of professor at their school. 100% will be in her class next sem

Danielle has got to be the best professor I have had at CC. Just show up to class on days with assignments and when you write your paper go to the tutor and do all the extra stuff or your grade will go down a bit. Also, do the online stuff a little bit every day and the class will fly by. Get ready to learn and love American History!
She is very easy going and gives the amount of homework a normal professor should give to students who are taking multiple classes. I would recommend taking her class.

This is the best class I have taken at SBCC. I will say that it is easy to get an A in this class, however if you do not work hard you will not improve as an actor. Matt is extremely good at providing helpful feedback, but you need to meet him half way. He tries really hard to make everyone reach their full potential and is very clear about craft.
Again, Matt is absolutely amazing; one review simply didn't give him justice. HEAVILY RECOMMEND BOTH CLASSES. PLEASE SBCC GIVE MATT MORE CLASSES TO TEACH!!!

there is not enough space to truly explain Matt's greatness; truly one of the greatest acting teachers I've ever had the honor of learning from. if I could go higher than a 5/5, I would, and if I could tag more than 3 tags, I would. If you are an actor, HE IS A MUST TAKE!!!!! My one complaint is he only has two courses. SBCC GIVE HIM MORE CLASSES!!

The best!!! Teacher!! Ever!! Seriously, he is amazing in every way. Hilarious and just such a fun guy to be around. Genuinely wants his students to succeed, and will help you with anything you need. You really need to be in class to succeed tho!!

Matt is such a great guy and teacher! I had so much fun in his class and I met so many great friends. I took his acting for the camera class and I learned so many real-life applicable skills about auditioning and scene work. Everything was so interesting. He expects you to work hard and you have to memorize a lot of scenes but its all great stuff!
Incredibly great teacher who genuinely wants his students to succeed.

Matt is awesome. Voice and diction is super fun, you do really weird exercises but they actually help and Matt really works hard with you in the workshops. Take the class if you enjoy acting and find accents interesting. It's super fun, accents can be hard but they are really fun.

Matt's acting for the camera class literally changed my whole perspective on acting. He gives the best feedback during our scenes and monologue that you have a clear idea on what to change, not just some abstract concept like most acting teachers. I adore him and the class, but it is a lot of work. Rehearse a lot and take risks and you'll do well
Matt is an awesome teacher. He is honest, funny, and really knows what he's talking about. He is focusing on every student, and you learn so much!

He is such a amazing professor. He is so funny and he is so genuine. I would totally take his class again if I could. The acting assignments were a lot of work but totally worth it. Would 100/100 take this class again. DO IT!

Best teacher in every way. Would take more classes if i didn't move away. Would recommend this class and Matt every day. He has at least inspired me and helped me get over my fear of seeing myself and being scared of messing up. Best in every way

One of the best teachers I have ever had!! I took his acting for the camera class last semester and loved everything about it. He made the class so enjoyable and a class you would look forward to going to. Matt is amazing and so knowledgable. I wish there was an acting for the camera two. Would definitely recommend any of his classes!!!
Amazing teacher! Learned a lot from him in his acting for the camera class. He is such a kind teacher and cares about his students. He is also HILARIOUS. One of the must fun classes I've taken.

Matt Talbott is, by far, the BEST teacher I have ever had!! He is caring, communicative, respected and respectful, smart, and is truly AMAZING!! Thank you, Matt, for making the class the BEST I have ever attended!!

MATT IS AWESOME. I took his acting for the camera class, and I am pretty sure every single person in that class enjoyed all 2 and 12 hours we were in there everyday we had class. Matt is SO funny, and he knows what he is talking about. He is always open to hear others opinions and up for trying new things to benefit your acting! GREAT teacher!!

I took Matt's Voice and Diction+his acting 4 the camera and both are exceptional. His work sessions force you to better your performance in front of the entire class which is horrifying at first but is the best practice for taking a directors feedback on set. Come to sessions prepared or you'll just waste chances to better yourself as an actor.
Makes it really easy and fun to learn! He makes us feel like equals, "Learns as much from us as he teach". Takes his time on every student and makes us feel unique!

Great teacher. Although it was acting for the camera he gives you techniques to be used on stage as well. there are a lot of opportunities to act in this class. also, the scenes are from movies so their interesting.

AWESOME TEACHER! learned more about acting in his class than in my acting class. Young and fresh, treats you like a buddy. Takes time for internationals for diction. Take his class, you will not regret it!
Best acting teacher I have ever had. Takes a clean simple approach to the craft
Matt!!! Such a good teacher! I am able to communicate well with others & I am more outgoing since the "acting in front of a camera class". Very nice teacher! :) fun fun fun

I took this class as part of my GE. PD 100 is an easy A, simply show up for your participation points and don't forget to complete the weekly online hw! Ms. Taylor is extremely nice, caring and she even drops your lowest grade (if you miss class or do poorly). The final paper is a piece of cake and you'll learn lots of new helpful studying habits.

Kimberly is so nice and helpful. She is very straight to the point and really got me thinking about what I want to do in my life as far as academics. Now I'm well on my way to becoming a Chemical Engineer! I don't know much about the other PD teachers but I would definentally recommend her.
I loved her ceramics class! Super informative.

You must spend time outside of class in the lab if you want a decent grade. Missing a singular quiz (out of the three) will take you down a letter grade, and she won't accept any excuses, but they're easy. She won't budge when it comes to grading, at all. Fun class, but you might start to resent ceramics if you don't already have that commitment.

critique sessions were unhelpful and uninspiring; entirely based on student comments.Grading + rubrics inconsistent.Any creativity put into a project must fit into the professors own idea of what your project shouldve been.Never got helpful feedback,only vague suggestions.Only had real feedback when projects were graded.Grades were not debatable.

It was incredibly hard to express yourself creatively in this class, ideas that differ slightly from the rubric will be shot down. You have a small amount of assignments that make up your grade, and a lot of the grading points are subjective. Conversations with her about assignments were unclear, and I was often graded poorly with no feedback

Professor Karen was so encouraging of challenging oneself creatively. I spent many hours outside of class in the studio and had so many great conversations with this professor. If you have the opportunity to take this class you will leave encouraged and empowered!

Professor Terry was great! She gives wonderful feedback and is always willing to walk students through any issue they may be having. I will say though there was some serious disconnect between the textbook/online assignments/exams and the assignments she would post. Be prepared to work hard, but its worth it.

Professor Terry is very caring and passionate about her class. She has multiple office hours spread throughout the week and is willing to work around them to answer any questions you may have. On her online course, she assigns a discussion, hand written problem, and 2 online assignments through McGraw for each chapter. You will definitely learn!

Absolutely loved Carolyn, not only is she an amazing professor, but an overall just really amazing person. Shes so fun to talk to! She really cares for her students and is willing to help in any way possible. Lots of homework and reading,but its accounting and a 5 unit class, so its expected in any class. Work hard for your grade and you'll do well
I rate Terry for 5/10. In Terry class, the only thing you need to do is go back home and read the book and that all.
Honestly one of the nicest and most caring teachers. Gives a good amount of extra credit but you have to earn it. 100% would recommend!

shes is an amazing professor. she cares about her students and is always willing to help. If you need help go to her office hours or ask her in class shes is great at helping. this course is considered hard but it is not impossible to achieve a good grade. there are mandatory notes and quizzes every week. most hate it, but it forces you to learn

Loved her!! She loves accounting, good at explaining concepts so you can grasp them easy. Unlike other classes where you can get by without reading the textbook, you have to read and take good notes on each chapter. She lets you have a full page of cheat sheet on tests. Hard class but if you pt in the work, you'll get an A.

I had the pleasure of taking Carolyn's Accounting w/ Quickbooks class. I really like her. She is beyond kind, loves accounting, and comes in everyday with a goofy story or two, she's funny. Great class, great person.

Terry knows her stuff, she's very friendly and sympathetic. Accounting is hard for a lot of people, but she does her best to help you understand the material. You have to read a lot, people who aced the class re-read each chapter 3 times. She stresses that often. She holds office hours also. The online portion of the course can be annoying.

Professor Terry is an amazing professor. She is very knowledgeable and knows how to teach it. Her class is really hard and there is a lot of reading to do, she recommends 20-25 hours a week. But if you're ready to put in the work youll get a solid A or B.

Professor Terry is the best thing that ever happened to SBCC. Great lectures, great person, my favorite college professor by far. Don't take accounting with anyone else and pray to God that you get into her class if you aren't already enrolled. Never felt so thankful in my whole life to have such a wonderful professor teach such a hard subject.

If you need to take accounting and you don't take Terry you are SERIOUSLY missing out. She is the best. She is will to go out of her way to help you learn the material and is super nice and relatable. Also a lot of ec which makes it easy to get a good grade, especially cause there is no +- This class is a lot of work but she really wants to help.

She is the best accounting teacher. If you have to take accounting 230, take her class. She requires students to spend 20 hours per week. It is necessary, or you won't pass. You have to read the textbook and take notes. There is a quiz in every meeting. No + - grades. Go to her office hours, or tutoring hours, you will be fine.

Carolyn is a great teacher. Material is hard, but she helps you as much as she can. A little improvement on the in class assignments, but she knows her material. I had fun in the class this last semester and suggest you take it with her. Good luck!

Not only is this class beneficial but it is easy if you apply yourself. It is super helpful and you can go at your own pace if you wish. I seriously recommend taking this class while it is still available.

Carolyn is absolutely AMAZING! You have to put the work into in order to succeed in the class and she motivates you a lot to study. She explains the accounting concepts her own way, answer to any question,always here for you, fast response in email( about 1 hour). The class ia well structured which make it easier to learn. TAKE HER!

Miss Carolyn is the best ACCT ever! She is really helpful, offer extra credit! This course is not easy but she explain all the material so clear. Lots and tons of homework, 2 quiz and a chapter summary EVERY WEEK ; but I think taking with the G or Corn (you know who) would be more worse than that. I'm gonna miss Miss Carolyn voice, makes me sleepy.
If you want to love accounting and learn, you have take it with her she is excellent .
she is awesome, she is really helpful and care about her students. take accounting class just with her

If you're taking ACCT 230 I definitely recommend taking this teacher. She is really passionate about her subject and is one of the most dedicated teachers I've had at SBCC. It's a fast-paced class so there is a lot of material to cover, just make sure to put effort into studying and doing homework and you'll be fine.

Professor Terry is by far one of the most helpful professors at SBCC. She holds extra tutoring hours and will do anything she can to help you understand the material. Take advantage of her tutoring! For those who say it's hard, yes ACCT 230 is hard in general. Just read, do HW, and go to tutoring and you will pass! It takes dedication.

Professor Terry is by far the best Accounting professor at SBCC. She is always available and even tutors twice a week for free if you need additional help or have questions. She really cares about her students doing well and understanding the material. Accounting 230 is a really tough class, expect to study a lot but if you do it will pay off.

Although I took Mrs. Terry for acct 110 and enjoyed it, I definitely believe she should stick to basic accounting classes. This was her first time ever teaching this class and it showed. Students taught themselves for practically the entire year. I suggest taking acct 230 with graziano, you will definitely learn more.
Mrs. Terry definitely knows how to teach beginning accounting. She is great at teaching the basics of accounting. Take 110 with her!

I've got to say out of all the accounting teachers in SBCC Professor Terry is the best. Why? Because she is the most helpful. She won't leave you behind and she is very clear on her teachings. Have a question? She will not leave you confused. Why can't all accounting teachers be like her?

In my top two best professors I've had at SBCC. Takes student feedback into consideration and that itself is amazing ( I don't know how many professors I've had that only see things their way). She takes the time to explain things (over and over again if she has to) until all the students understand. She is highly respected by her students.

Professor Terry’s many years’ experience in QuickBooks at professional level made managing this course with ease. Her teaching style, and timely tips provided in this subject matter skillfully helped hands-on-experience with QuickBooks. Above all, I enjoyed her patience she passionately shows for all student learners in computer assisted course.

Nicest person I have ever met in my life she is a true teacher all the way through. Helpful, knowledgeable, and caring. She is what a teacher needs to strive to be. Work hard and study, you will get an A.

READ TEXTBOOK, do homework (use my accounting lab), attend class, and take tests. You can get A. Without reading book before class, you can't understand what she talks. She is nice and helpful, loves questions. Even though using scantron for exam, you should write G/L etc for test. Exam time is very short. You can enter before normal class time.
she's a really good teacher!! highly recommend

She's very caring, reasonable and flexible. It's important to attend her lectures if you want to actually understand her slides, she explains everything in depth and makes sure if everyone is following along. If you actually pay attention it's very interesting information and very good to knows.

She is not clear and omits a lot of important information. Her tests are sadistically difficult, you have less than a minute to answer each question which require writing paragraphs. The highest grade for the midterm was a C! There was literally not enough time to finish. I am so in love with climatology and its such a shame she tried to ruin it.

I really loved Dr. T. I admit I was nervous to take this class, but it was great. She was so helpful and caring (given that you care about your grade and learning the material). Her office hours are so helpful. Learning about the weather and climate of this atmosphere was rad too. Care about the class and you will do well!

Her quiz and test questions weren't easy, but if you watched her lectures and studied, they were very doable considering they're multiple choice. She offered roughly 20% in extra credit through optional exam questions and assignments. There's no excuse for not getting an A if you actually try.

The worst professor at Sbcc. Shes super negative and sarcastic. If you have the option to take geo with anyone else please do so. The material itself is simple, but she is so difficult. She responds so rudely in her emails. Isnt clear with her homework instructions. Horrible.
Online takers she uses Proctorio a online exam watcher that video tapes you while you do exams.

So I took this class and I absolutely hated it. Very unclear and didn't understand the subject. Took this as a distance learner and mostly every quiz and test had a question regarding to the location of the school. I don't live anywhere near SB so I always got those ?s wrong. The PPT is basically the book being read aloud. She doesn't explain .

Her and the TA would make sure we all had all the right answers before leaving so we could all get 100% on every lab but that did mean we ended up staying for the whole lab time most of the time. She's really fun and cool but not the best at explaining hard to grasp concepts, but that doesn't matter if you aren't pursuing geology. Prelabs are hard.

I loved Doctor T. She has great powerpoints and makes it clear what you need to know for the class. She gives you a study guide for every exam and she won't put something on the exam that you haven't seen before. I would recommend doing the extra credit scrapbook at the end of the semester, it's worth 4 percentage points.
Sit in the front of the class, Dr. Thomas tends to speak softly. And go to review sessions.

She was very unclear about what we should know for tests and did not provide any kind of study guide. Of the "Review" that was available it was unclear what would be on the test and I studied for hours only to find none of the material was on the tests. She offers a lot of extra credit but I would not recommend this class to anyone.
Long PowerPoint lectures

Clearly very knowledgable and enthusiastic about the subject, but if you take her class, sit close because she speaks very softly. Always ask for clarification if her lecture is unclear, because generally with further explanation, she gets the point across. Tests are VERY tough.
Very nice teacher. She's is very enthusiastic about the subject. Loved her energy and spunk. Would take again

I had zero interest in the subject. Lectures were boring and unclear to me. Attendance is mandatory (clicker questions). Weekly quizzes through Moodle (answers online). Team work on midterms is allowed. Not too much work outside of the classroom. Not a hard class, but extremely boring. Doesn't seem like she explains things in simple, basic terms.

She is so sweet. Feels more like a friend than professor. Makes everyone in class feel comfortable and I've never had a more down to earth and understanding professor. She is young and can relate to student life a lot. Attendance is manadtory so make sure to show up and you'll easily pass the class

Aimei's nergy is contagious even early mornings. She is passionate about COMMS and uses her own personal examples along with having us come up with our own examples and actively engage with the content. Super fun and sweet professor

Ai Mei is a great professor. She said it was her first semester teaching 101 so she's going to change some things for next semester. But it's impossible to fail if you turn everything in. She's very understanding if anything comes up and her lectures are fun- she uses a lot of her personal experiences in examples so you get to know her too

Professor Thompson's 101 class is really easy if you turn everything in. Attendance is a big part of the grade so make sure you don't miss. 2 open note exams, chapter quizzes and a final paper. She grades nicely and gives good feedback on written assignments. Thompson is super understanding and warm so reach out if you are having a hard time

Professor Thompson is young and can relate to struggles that come iwth being a student. She give slots of extra credit opportunities and takes our feedback into consideration when it comes to assignments. Easy class if you attend and turn things in. She genuinely wants everyone to succeed and I even felt comfortable sharing a personal situation

AiMei is the most kind teacher ive ever had. unfortunately i had to withdraw due to personal circumstances but she even offered me support outside of the classroom and to work 1 on 1 with me to complete the course at a later time. I deeply appreciate her understanding and how much she cares about us beyond as her students
Professor Thompson is kind. our class is pretty quiet but she makes lectures engaging and provides peronal examples for concepts which help with my learning.

Dude, she is the absolute goat - I love her so much! She is so so understanding, especially if you communicate. There are about 4 essays you'll have to write, but they're all doable, and the feedback she provided made me a better writer. I could not recommend her enough. Super super manageable class, but it also helps you improve as a thinker.
An incredible professor, very funny and a good grader. Though there is a good bit of reading.

She taught my ENG-111 class last semester, and she was truly exceptional. Her kindness, patience, and clarity made writing far less intimidating. Her office hours were incredibly helpful, her grading fair, and her support genuine. She is an outstanding instructor and an even better human being.

Shes one of the nicest professors ive had. I had her for eng 110 and taking her 111 class. She explains & goes over her expectation really well and even though her essays can be more lengthy you definitely develop more skills within her class. Homework is also interactive & easy maybe like 1 or 2 assignments at most with a few chapters from a book.

I took Trujillo for ENG-111; she is the most amazing professor and person I have ever met. She is so kind and she will help a lot with essays. She is super nice and easy to talk to. See always has office hours, and. you can chat with her. I hate English when it comes to writing because I am bad, but she brought my interest. She's truly the best!

I took this class as my first College English course. She did very well in letting everyone adjust to college classes and provided clear instructions about what she wanted from us. As long as you are fine with a bit of reading before class (like 30-45 mins), you will be absolutely fine. The essays weren't that hard and she gave really good feedback

Christina is a kind and dedicated teacher, but I didn't really enjoy her class. The course is heavily centered around horror stories and films, which was interesting at first, but over time it started got boring. She assigns a lot of homework and is a fairly tough grader. Get ready to read and write a lot, the workload can definitely be intense.

Pros: She's VERY generous w/ grading & due dates if u communicate w/ her. She's also very understanding if u need to leave class early & is always available thru emails Cons: Her class is based entirely on Horror so if ur not a fan I would reconsider, since we watched a bunch of horror related things & the essays (there are 3) are based on that.

Chris is pretty chill. However, if he doesn't like your idea or what you're painting, he will make it known. He wants things done his way, but it's easy to figure out what he finds good art to be. If you don't handle any form critique well, do not take his class. And finally, be prepared to spend LOTS of time in the studio outside of class.
Awful, this is not an art class it Is a judging class. He is not clear on instructions and grades so harsh it demotivates your creativity.

132 is a HUGE time commitment. Would not recommend taking with 4+ other classes. Chris is a great guy, very funny and helps you learn necessary art skills even if he is a little harsh about it. He's never mean, only constructive. 132 is a beginner class but you'll have experienced people in the class - don't let it discourage you though!
Wasn't easily accessible and never answered emails. He is very witty though and made the class interesting. Assignments were a bit confusing, but I still enjoyed the time in class and in the studio.

I took what was supposed to be a beginner-level art class but I feel it should be more of a middle-level art class. Very tough grader, wasn't clear on most assignments, had unclear quizzes, & took forever to respond to emails or just wouldn't answer. If you are a beginner or just taking this class for general education look for a different teacher.
No recommend these teacher he is very bad he never like anything please used your value time in another class where you effort counts.

Didn't like his class because he was unclear in his online assignment instructions, refused to answer my questions about it in class, told me I was a bad student for not understanding, and gave me a bad grade on the assignments because it "didn't meet his guidelines." Views good art as something that looks good to him, not hard work or creativity

Wouldn't recommend. I dropped the class because (at least online) he wasn't individually present with students. It was hard to get help. Easy to fail assignments, even over things out of your control.

Terrible teacher, extremely immature in meetings and in his announcements. If he doesn't like your work he'll give no helpful feedback, even if you're a beginner. Looked up his paintings and they're no different than what the beginners in class were making.

Nice guy but a bad teacher. Very condescending in feedback. Took the class online because of the COVID pandemic, he doesn't seem to consider an individual student's circumstances and skill level when coming up with assignments, like we had to do a "Larger than Life" project which I struggled with because I had literally nowhere to put it at home.

Just not a good human being. At all. Overdramatizes. Unempathetic. Not approachable. Sexist. Emily emily emily you need to calm down.-only ever saying to girls, btw. Not approachable. And if you dont see art like he does, well good luck.

Chris gives good feedback, and is very willing to work individually with ppl regardless of skill level. He'll call you on it if you don't do the work. You get out what you put in, and he's also one of the few art faculty that gives good insight for art majors. It's not like most classes, but well worth it if taken on its own terms.

Not a good professor. Didn't care at all about actually teaching you anything about painting. Very opinionated when grading and was clearly bias towards those with less experience. Don't take this class if you actually care about painting or want to keep a good grade point average. Terrible professor.
Not a very fair grader. Praises best painters and is not very understanding about beginning skill level. Lectures are very long and boring. Gets upset when students arent outgoing.

Google his paintings before enrollling: he's good on composition, color, but you won't learn perspective, portaiture, or fine finishing bec. after painting for many years, he still paints like a kid. I would try a different school for painting.

Disappointed into to painting, more than half the time you will not be painting. Very strict with attendance. Lectures last forever, BORING still lifes that make up majority of assignments. Very unresponsive in emails and good luck trying to figure out your grade as he doesnt properly use canvas. Youre expected to work quickly, not my style.
Really funny and knows what he's talking about. Always gives fantastic feedback.

Very fast pace class. Attendance is mandatory if you do not have time outside of class to finish paintings. He is funny and gives clear instructions and hands-on help, but does not give class instruction on painting at all, even though it is an intro class. Grades on improvement and participation not on how well you paint.

Attend class and be on time, or make him laugh to make up for it. Pretty chill, he's had a lot of experience painting. He doesn't like to give lots of direction on where to take your paintings besides broad instruction because he wants you to experience and make mistakes and learn from them. He knows krav maga. He takes his kids horseback riding!
Great professor overall I guess... He's sorta funny and let's you do whatever you want. - not a very serious guy. A little touchy with the ladies though ... :(
He'll let you dive in , respects your art level. Don't miss class attendance is important and don't bring attitude.

He does not give good feedbacks -He is a tough critic. He is very knowledgable on his field - graphic design. Make sure you show up to class on time - there will be quizzes. There will also be many projects await you. They are assigned every week. I'd recommend this class if you are not a full-time student, and not working as well. So you have time
simply the best.
best prof. ever
i loved ulivo's 2D class. A lot of work,but fun.
He's very helpful. He usually would go through everyone in the class to discuss ideas and solve your problems about the assignment. He's also really funny. Take his class, you won't regret.

Such a funny teacher! He sees and notices both good and improvable things about students work and is willing to help us working. You need to attend the class and turn all assignments in by due date if you want an A.

He was great teacher and would reach out if you missed an assignment. He would check in on the students to ensure they were on track. This is a professor that actually cared about his students. There is a 10 page paper at the end of semester. I did need more time to work on it and he was very understanding, if communicated with properly! Winter2023

I loved this professor. He was very passionate about the subject and he works in the field so he brought real cases sometimes to discuss. There is aa quiz and sometimes a discussion due every Sunday and he gives the whole week to complete the assignments. One big research paper at the end and two midterms. 10/10

Professor Ullemeyer insured the success of every student in his class. The lectures were on the longer side but were extremely detailed and not once did I catch myself losing interest. I especially liked his stories of his first hand accounts of working and breaking down a crime scene. So far my favorite professor I have ever had.

I came into with an interest for these aspects of criminology and walked out craving to learn more.The lectures are cool to watch and he is straight to the point,has character,and the material is really fascinating itself.I would just put his lecture on and go around.With the online situation homework is so fun and extremely light in work.
Class was very interesting but kinda sucked it was only at night once a week.
Outstanding professional �� ������ �� The best Prof... He is an actual CSI INVESTIGATOR who activity solves crimes and teaching at the college.

Probably one of my favorite teachers ever. His class is always interesting, he is clear on assignments and never assigns impossible tasks. He understands students have a life outside of school, and makes each class entertaining as well as education. Id even recommend taking his class for fun!!

Online class is just reading the textbook, watching the lecture, taking the quiz. Straight forward and easy. The lectures are longer than I think are necessary. You get 2 attempts at the quiz, but you don't know what questions you missed when you take the quiz again. 10 page term paper. Very nice guy!
Mr. Ullemeyer has been a wonderful professor, especially during COVID-19. Hes understanding and helpful and TRULY cares about his students and is passionate about his career.

Hes a wonderful person. I loved taking this class and I learned so much. Even if youre not interested in forensics, Michael definitely makes it worth it and might even spark an interest in his line of work. (He did for me!) He cares about his teaching, his job, and his students, and it shows. Id take a class of his any day!

I love professor ullemeyer! He gives such a good insight on the CSI aspect of criminology and shows us real crime scenes. Hes funny and makes the 3 hour class seem like 45 minutes. He has made me want to go into forensic sciences and I would recommend this class to everyone!

His lectures are long. They are accessible online but its impossible to show up to class and take in class notes during lecture because it's too much information. Recommend to print them out and highlight important stuff during lecture. He is a really good teacher overall and the class isn't hard! (You have a research paper/presentation to do)

Mr. Ullemeyer is an extremely knowledgeable forensics professor. The final project was a unique and fun way to engage the class and I greatly enjoyed it. I would highly recommend taking his class if you can. He is very funny and very good at explaining certain aspects of forensic science.

Professor Ullemeyer is funny, interesting, and a great teacher overall. There aren't that many assignments in the class, but as long as you do them it's an easy A. The tests aren't hard, and you learn a lot. I've been trying to get in his forensics class for a year, and now that I've completed it, totally worth the wait!

If Vince Vaughn and Johnny Knoxville had a baby, it would look and talk like Professor Ullemeyer. Jokes aside, he is an awesome teacher. He is down to earth, informative, and always makes an effort to make class fun, relevant and entertaining. Beware though, he will crush ur CSI dreams. He tells forensics how it is, and crime tv will never be same.

Great teacher, he really knows what he is talking about. Three hour classes, so it is a lot to take in, but he switches it up between theory and examples from local cases. A very fun class, take it if you are interested in Criminal Investigation. Be in class, sometimes he shares some hard test questions early.
The coolest class with the coolest teacher he makes it almost impossible to fail if you come to class and take notes like you should then ur golden! Would take again!
It was a cool class but if you are looking for an easy A don't take it. Really interesting though you'll never look at CSI shows the same way
BEST. PROFESSOR. EVER.

Mr. Ullemeyer is one of the most amazing teachers I have ever had. He's very funny and interesting. He's very clear on what it takes to pass the class. The assignments aren't your typical assignments, they're always shortfun activities. He's always there for you if you have any questions or concerns. Also, GO TO CLASS.

I took this class and it was pretty easy but I was not interested in the class at all so it made things harder. As for the teacher, he is a very nice and funny guy and class is pretty easy. He does fun projects but does give out a lot of homework as well.

Professor Ullemeyer was one of the most supportive teachers I have ever encountered. I ended up having to drop his class but he was there every step of the way and provided great feedback. His class was long and the lectures were sometimes tough to sit through since it was late at night. Overall interesting and great professor.

Awesome, easy, fun, interesting teacher! He's very nice, the teaching method is powerpoint and are clear. You go over real cases and it's pretty eye opening to how much crime is even going on around SB! Good teacher and you also do experiments in class and have guest speakers :]

The forensics class is freakin awesome. It totally changed my life, I'm serious. you look at crime shows differently, you look at the justic system differently. I mean it's really eye opening. And I have never had more fun in a class in my life. And it is an easy A if you go to class or even study a little. Mike is a really cool guy!!

This is for the forensics class. It is soooo cool! It's super fun and we do cool stuff like lifting fingerprints from his van and looking at fake blood spatter, etc. It's really interactive and pretty easy too. The final is easy. And the Jeopardy game is a riot! I am SOOO glad I took this class. I learned so much b/c it was FUN! P.S. Go to class!!

He is an awesome teacher with a passion for passing knowledge. He bends over backwards to make sure that people are getting the material. A very down to earth guy and a master in the field. I highly reccommend :)
Great guy, passionate about the subject, open to helping students wherever possible and even lets students explore the police station!

James is an Amazing professor. He is very patient and caring. I had taken him in the past for other Graphic Design classes and you just learn so much from his classes. If you do the work you will do well in class. The class was not very difficult but there is a lot of work that goes into the projects. Very fun and creative class.

I have had an amazing experience taking every one of James' classes. As the chair of the graphic design department, I think he has done an excellent job catering the individual classes and creating various large scale projects which are engaging, fun, and great practice for what it's like to work as a designer outside of an academic setting.
loved the projects, James is so engaging and thoughtful, definitely one of my favorite prof I've ever had, learned a lot

One of the best experiences in school I've had. Prof Van Arsdale structures his classes well, offers extra resources, and provides genuine feedback on projects. The amount of work will keep you busy, but every assignment is useful, not just busy work. Especially appreciated the balance of direction and room for your own creativity. Highly recommend

During this pandemic, there have been teachers who have struggled to adapt their classes online. His class was the complete opposite. He gives good feedback and is always there to answer questions (he answers emails quickly!). His lectures are always interesting and relevant to our assignments. I would love to take his class again!

James is the greatest, most caring professor I've ever had. He cares so much for his students and he goes out of his way to help you. Even if you're not an art/graphic design student I urge you to take his class, because every time you're in class you can collect your thoughts and really calm yourself down. He always plays the best music too.

James is a fantastic graphic design teacher. Rarely do you get teachers with the patience to personally check on the projects of the ENTIRE class every period. His inputs are always useful, he is always respectful and is a very cool, relaxed guy. You would be a fool to want any other teacher, it wouldn't make sense. Had him twice now, awesome guy

I love James. He is the reason why I made graphic design my major. I wanted to take an online gdp class but because he is not the instructor of that one I will wait another semester and take it with him then. It's really easy to get an A, and he inspires me a lot. Really good lectures too!

I've been to an Art School and all of those teachers FAIL to compete with James, he's very attentive, a great teacher, and super cool and relaxed. With that being said, he does not allow you to slack off, he still makes sure you're on top of everything.

James is the best instructor I've ever had. He really inspires me and his exercises in class are super interesting. I took Media Design and every project I did felt like something I could actually do if I worked as a Graphic Designer. He doesn't really care if you don't show up all the time, and as long as you submit everything, you'll get an A.
I wish she taught more in-person classes because I genuinely enjoyed this class so much because of her, 10/10.
easy class cool teacher and shes really tall, could be kind of boring to some but if you're interested in the subject i dont think you'll find it boring

Professor Vilander is an amazing professor. Class is very easy and very few graded assignments. Midterms and finals are easy as well, only hard assignment was an article analysis. That assignment was not explained well but once you get through that the class is a breeze. She also is very funny and keeps class interesting!
Very friendly and knowledgeable. Class was manageable and straightforward, it set you up to be ready for the tests.

Prof. Vilander is super approachable & flexible. While the lectures could be more interactive, she makes it incredibly clear what you need to know for the exam. As long as you take notes & study, this class is a breeze. As someone who hates essays, the writing assignments are broken down in class & very manageable, + she gives good feedback.

Caring & sets clear reasonable expectations, but lectures are very dull despite taking the class out of sheer interest. Too much detail & no sense of context make it easy to get lost. Said we'd have group projects & discussions, but we never did. Graded on vocab quizzes, image tests, a presentation, & 2,900w final paper that makes up half of grade.

Vilander is awful at throughly explaining instructions. Myself and other people in the class were always confused. She is also a tough grader. We were given surprise in class writing assignments as well. I took art hoping for something fun and a bit easier than my other classes however I think this class has caused me more stress than pre calc.

Such an amazing person! Cares about every student & really wants you to succeed. Lectures weren't mandatory so nobody ever came but I did & they were interesting and fun. So much extra credit, easy quizzes & midterms. Send her your notes what you are studying & she will tell you if thats what you need to know or not. Take it! Easy A - I did nothing

This was probably one of my least favorite classes taken so far at SBCC. Maybe it's because of the online platform, but she is not a very thoughtful teacher. She pretty much just copy and paste's information into her lectures from the textbook, and it seems like she reuses non-applicable material from other classes. I will avoid her in the future.

Absolutely one of the best professors at sbcc (which is saying a lot cuz there is not many). She is deeply kind and caring and made the class work for me. I have a condition where I get sick a lot and she was always understanding and gave grace often. Her assignments were always straight to the point and easy to follow. Love this professor!!!!
Can't go wrong with any class from Viliander. She's amazing and super caring and all you have to do is listen to lecture and do the homework so easy.
The best professor I have ever taken . Vilander is defiantly a gem. You will not regret taking any class with her. She actually cares about her students very much.

I'm taking her Asian Art History class during quarantine (so online) and it's really nice! Although there are no zoom lectures, they are prerecorded and I can tell she is a very nice person who cares for everyone and their grades. You HAVE to listen to all the lectures because they have all the information you need to past the exams. Love the class

Really enjoyed History of American Art. Course was very interesting. Professor Vilander is very knowledgeable and a great professor. Good sense of humor and respectful of students. The exams are taken directly from the lecture notes. I would definitely take another course with her.

i took vilanders history of photography class and loved it. ive always been interested in photography but she helped me see it in even better ways. shes super kind and genuinely cares about her students and making sure they do well. practically no homework at all and super clear testing expectations. overall, an awesome prof who truly cares!!!!!
Amazing professor, you can really tell she is passionate about the subject.

Lots of opportunities for extra credit. Barbara is very passionate about what she teaches. Don't be scared to approach her with questions. She doesn't like when people are late- do NOT be late. Study for her tests!!! Lots of memorization. A lot on the tests are based off of what she discusses in lectures. Pay attention. There's 3 exams and 1 essay

She's a truly amazing professor. Her passion for the topic is clear and absolutely radiating, I honestly do not believe it is possible for a class to be more well taught than this one. One of the very best professors I've had the pleasure of meeting.

She is very nice, and educated about the topic, couldn't ask for a better professor. Class was clear and she was willing to help if needed. I would take her again. I learned a lot about art. BTW I didn't open the book once. It is not needed.

Best Professor I've ever had! She is such an excellent speaker, and intelligent! She really knows what she is talking about. I've grown an appreciation for art after completing this class. I highly suggest taking this class!

This was a fantastic class! The lectures were really engaging and thoroughly covered everything on the tests (I used the book minimally to study before the tests). Dr. Vilander was always available outside of class to go over questions (relating to the essay or the class in general) and really welcomed different interpretations of the art.
A very passionate teacher, that is helpful and explains things well. I highly recommend her.

It's too bad this is a night class. Apart from that, it was one of the most interesting classes I've taken. As long as you attend lectures the class is easy. At the end of every lecture she goes through the images that will appear on the quizzes. The lectures were never boring either, you could tell she enjoys teaching this class!
Very great teacher overall! She is very clear on what she wants you must do to pass the class & is always willing to help you with any class work. Excellent teacher

I had her for two art history classes and she's the best! She always emphasizes what you'll need to know for the exams and explains everything really well. She's very well educated but is also open to people's opinions. She's really engaging and makes the material interesting. This is how all teachers should teach!

Such a chill teacher! Very easy to understand, very informative and sweet. Easiest class of ALL TIME while learning valuable information about art. Didn't even bother buying the $200 book and got an A in the class. Take this class!

Barbara is GREAT! She made me really think more about art, did not rely heavily on the textbook and made everything super interesting! Her tests are a piece of cake, just know the dates. I wish I could have her for another class. Strongly recommend her!

Great person, she is fun and tells you exactly what is going to be on the test. All you have to do is read a couple sentences in the book, memorize your notes and you will get an A. She also gives out 2 extra credit assignments that are worth a letter grade just in case.

She's nice and means well but not the brightest bulb in the tool shed. She basically takes 2 sentences from the text you should be reading, adds a lot obvious details you can observe without being a teacher and jumbles them up into a loose jibber jabber rant on repeat. It's really boring and if you really pay attention, not very effective teaching.
I'm taking history of photography, and it is a great class! very interesting!

She was a good professor, but if you miss a quiz you will get a zero on it, even if you are sick. I wish English 110 and 111 included classic literature, I'm not a fan of the modern study we read at this school. This professor sometimes talked a lot during class which would make it feel long, but I still learned a lot.

Walker is a good teacher but a very passive aggressive person. She likes to make comments at students and belittle them in front of others. She rarely gives A papers even tho she will only leave 3 comments on an essay. Her class is fairly easy, 4 essays and multiple small quizzes on readings. You just have to show up because she counts absences.

She's a really nice professor and in English 111 she was an easygoing grader and gave good feedback on my essays. There is light reading and reading comprehension quizzes that she prepares you well for.

Professor Walker honestly just seeks meaningful conversations and seeing her students care about literature and the subjects of her curriculum. She can be a harsh grader at times but she offers a great deal of support to her students and even on the day of due date of assignments will help tweak essays to help you achieve the best grade.

Walker is a fantasic Professor! Her class gave me a foundation of writing/reading skills, while also being able to keep me engaged and interested. Her grading/ feedback was reasonable and fair! Please keep in mind Eng110 is a 4 unit class, so the workload is understandable. This course will help you will every other class moving forward!!

Professor Walker provides excellent feedback on essays, and if you incorporate the writing techniques she teaches, it's easy to receive good grades. Basically, follow her instructions, and you'll do great.

She is nice as a person, but she isn't really understanding of absence. She will email you saying that you are at risk for getting dropped if you miss 2 classes. She is a tough essay grader, but there are only 4 essays and the last one is the final.

I am currently still enrolled in her class, and I find that she is a tough grader who is honestly just looking to pick apart my essays more and more. She gives me no feedback, which results in me being lost even with the help of the writing center. Her directions change a lot. Also, she has no grace when you are sick which is so rude. not happy.

Professor Walker is an encouraging teacher who expects her students to meet her very doable standards. As long as you are willing to put in the time and effort to meet her deadlines, and participate in her class you will do well! The one bummer about her class is she puts a maximum of 3 absences in her course, but just communicate if necessary!

Kind teacher who puts a lot of weight into how hard you show you're trying. You are guaranteed to get a better grade if you go to office hours and ask for feedback/implement it. Extra credit and revision opportunities gave a chance to boost our grades before the final, which was graded relatively easily.

Amazing professor! All the assignments are very straightforward and as long as you complete them all on time, you are guaranteed an A. She gives good feedback with essays as well and if you ever have any questions, she is really easy to contact and reach. 11/10 recommend.

Simple. The assignments come down to basic comprehension and the essay topics are very surface level stuff. The answers are discussed in class and by the teacher, so there's really no excuse for failing unless you just don't show up. She does grade essays a bit tough though.

I got A+ on all my papers except the last one. when a spoke to her about why my writing was all of a sudden not up to her standard, I came to the conclusion that it was bc she wanted me to participate in the revision essay assignment which I would not have needed to do otherwise. also offered no make up work when i had bronchitis for a week.
such a tough grader

AMAZING professor. She has taught me so much and has brought me back to my love for reading and writing. She gives good feedback and ideas which benefited my writing ability. There is a lot to read but that's usually the case with Eng110 and the readings were very interesting! Trust me and take her class!

Professor Walker was extremely egocentric. I am strong in my writing capability but she made me feel as if I was incompetent and insufficient. I recall from her class believing I had poor writing skills based on her opinion-oriented grading style. She was also rude. I was going through a rough point in my life and she did not care at all. Big nope.

In the beginning of class she was grading my things well, as I was trying my best but mid semester, she began grading me for things harshly. She gives way too much papers, essays, review essays, and lots of HW. Even with the help of the writing center she literally grades dumb. She's not caring, she hardly responded to my emails as well! Not happy.

If you're ideas don't match her, good luck getting a good grade. She had a lot of busywork assignments for the online class. If you don't turn in on time, you can't turn it in at all. She had a lot of somewhat political topics to write about but I felt uncomfortable expressing my opinion knowing she would grade me down. AVOID IF YOU CAN.

Walker was the perfect English teacher. Clear and organized from day one. She has to give out 4 essays but the 4th is where you rewrite essay 1 or 2 so you can change and improve your grade! She truly cares and is willing to help you through every step of essays and readings. In the end, we read a novel, I hate reading but this one was great!
Pretty rude and gives the impression that she's too good or something. Doesn't really seem invested in helping people outside class and even then has a really linear idea of what makes a paper good.
Rude

Prof Walker is just down the middle of the road. You have to do a lot of reading and writing, but she doesn't really push you hard. Pretty easy grader as long as you hit the major points she wants you to do.
I took writing 2 at ucsb. She is not too helpful but just follow the rubric and make your essay organized in the five paragraph essay style then you are good to go.

I think another person said, if your ideas don't match hers, then she'll probably grade your papers according to how well she likes you. I've seen students with poor writing skills get "A" papers, students with great writing abilities get Bs. She's boring and people don't participate. Its usually silent. I had high hopes but she let me down. :(

Super repetitive. If you took AP English Literature in high school, it will be the exact same thing. Professor Walker is super nice and the topics are interesting. As long as you show up and write your essays, you'll be good to go.

Don't get me wrong I'm sure she is a good person, just not a good professor. Many in our class complained about her inability to stay organized and lack of care. You will be graded down if your ideas don't match hers. I went to class every day and still struggled to understand what she wanted. her grading standards changed constantly, did not help.

An average english teacher considering it's the last english class you have to take. She is reasonable but you have to work with her. She does care about her students but you have to prove it to her by wanting to improve your writing skills. Hard grader even on students who do well on essays. But overall, she is willing to work with you.

I took a pass/no pass class with her, and it is really easy. If you do your assignment, she always gives your full points. So, it is easy to pass the class. She is really helpful during her office hour. If you want to take English 100, choose her class.

Overall, she is a pretty easy grader. If you go see her in her office hours before your paper is due she will help you out a lot. Its not mandatory to show up to class, although she does take attendance. She cares about her students and wants you to succeed. I liked her as a teacher, although the class was time consuming.

Although she wasn't the most helpful or most approachable teacher, she forced me to become a better writer and reader. Emphasis on FORCED. Attendance is mandatory or you will be dropped or fail the course. There's a LOT of work, but if you make a serious effort it'll feel great when you pass her difficult course.

Just show up to the hikes at least ten minutes early and you'll be fine. Showed up nine minutes early once and he left me behind. Easiest hikes of your life, nothing hard, and you'll be blasted by vape smoke every few minutes. Enjoy secondhand smoke inhalation.
Rude, unorganised, standoff ish,

He marks people absent even when they are there, doesn't respond to emails, doesn't actually teach you how to play, is on his phone the whole time, and calls students profanities. He doesn't like his job. The only good thing about his class was that I could play volleyball with friends. But I could do that without him there.

Hikes don't start to get good until the end of the semester, and when suggested different hikes he gets very defensive. He makes very back handed comments and is usually very rude. Although I love hiking I wouldn't take this class again, you don't really make friends, the hikes are very far, and boring.

Beach volleyball: he sits on his phone the entire class. I almost passed out one day and had to ask a student to get him as he ignores students and has earbuds in 99% of the time. He is not respectful and his attendance grading is horrid- miss one class and you get dropped a full letter grade; only accepts Dr's notes.

As long as you show up to the hike, you will pass. Not hard at all. Just attend. Good luck on trying to reach him outside of class to find where the hike is though... that's the hardest journey you'll ever deal with

Easy A, need to be able to drive to and from the hikes which can be inconvenient. He chooses hikes closer to where he lives and makes the students drive farther. He can be a little weird but overall it's hiking so not much to complain about! You can only miss 3 hikes, but he canceled 3 anyways so it's an easy 1.5 credits if you need it!
Canceled the hikes half the time, and did not even show up to one of the hikes when the rest of the class did which was super annoying. The class was easy but it was not fun.

Don't take the beach volleyball class if you actually want to learn how to play the sport. He just sits there and does nothing while the class occurs. Waste of my time, this guy is not a nice person and he does not care about his students whatsoever.

Took his Drafting 120 class and it was fairly easy. The worst part about it is that HE IS NOT SPECIFIC about any of his assignments. You have to get help from his tutor (Fadi) during office hours or you're clueless. He will grade it and take points off of your work even though he did not specify anything he critiqued. You can def still pass though.

Great professor. Classes are generally laid back and he's usually available to give in-person feedback and assistance if you have any questions while working on assignments in class. Almost sure to get a good grade if you collaborate for feedback and put quality/effort into your work. Otherwise, great character overall, would recommend.
I love Professor Warner. He really made me want to become an architect. He gives amazing feedback, and gives you a lot of chances to get an A in his class.
She is really great. Just do the work. Her class is a little time consuming, but easy.

Professor Angela Warren is an excellent online professor. She is very caring and encouraging to her students, easy to reach in and outside of class, and overall an amazing professor. Her online PD100 class was great. There was quite a bit of weekly reading and some chapter response assignments. Take her class! It was really interesting and fun!

Not if you're looking to learn how to draw. I didn't enjoy the assignment prompts and I felt there wasn't enough instruction. Probably better in person. The materials seemed out of order, drawing was critiqued on stuff we'd learn later :/ $80 kit to start, was not listed on anything until the first day of class.

You look at so many artists. The assignments are very different each week. Some were harder than others for me but you have time to figure it out even if you don't have a lot of experience. Clear online work and the drawing portfolios are the most important. You get a lot of help in lab and can work in groups too. Interesting lectures.

She had reputation of having favorite students back in ucsb. Now I believe it. She gives good grades to certain students. Once she decides she is not a fan of you there's no coming back. She grades very tough. But the reason for lower grades doesn't make sense. Effort doesn't matter to her. I would not recommend taking her classes.
Go to class, make sure your portfolio is done well. One main assignment per week, nothing is actually due until you turn in portfolio. Loved this class and learned so much. Highly recommend!
I learned a lot. You try out so many different media. You just really have to do the work each week. Make sure you make drawings better for the portfolios.
Interesting class. I think its more work than people expect but you have to practice to improve.

Extremely tough grader for ART of all things which is highly personal and people want to be inspired. She is harsh and not really a great teacher for people who want to do art as therapy. It's been a tough couple of years and you would think teachers would be compassionate to all skill levels but not this professor.

She's a harsh grader, but very understanding and gives great feedback. She grades based on whether she can see you trying/improving. Don't take for an easy A, she won't take BS. I took her online, so I don't know what she's like in person, but she seems to want to bring out the best in all her students.

Very strict on when assignments are due. Gives good feedback. Sometimes instructions are unclear but most of the time is good about giving examples. As long as you put effort into the art and follow the criteria, you'll do good. it is very easy to tell when someone doesn't try. She is a little difficult to please, but everyone has dif art styles
