Online drawing class because of Covid- I was surprised I really learned a lot. You need to practice and do the work. Lots of media and different approaches.

Way too specific and hard at grading. Never gave a 100% to anyone in the class for any assignment. I would get Cs and low Bs on assignments for super small "mistakes" and it made me really hate this class. Way too critical for an art course.

It is hard to grade art. I feel that Professor Washburn looks for a specific political and /or societal outlook in pieces. It is hard to understand a person's mindset in art, and that makes it difficult to achieve what she is looking for. She gives good critiques, though. I would take her again.
Smart professor. Helpful and challenging. Really got me into art.

Do not hesitate. Take her class. Im hundred percent sure youll get what you want. Youll have classmates who you want to meet, and youll be inspired, knowledgable, and skillful. Moreover, after you finish this class, youll definitely know what you want to do next. Take her class.

Very informative class. You'll get skills and also be challenged to think about what you're doing and what art is. Shows a range of art to get things across. Relaxed, generous, really smart teacher. Makes you work and focus.

Completely unengagng, walks away while you speak to her. Makes it see like she's doing you a favor just being there but doesn't show you how to do anything. If she doesn't like t then it's reflected in your grade. Choose a less vindictive teacher and you will learn more.
Stephanie Washburn is super calm and kind. She definitely isn't playing around but overall she is a great teacher and when she lectures it's super interesting.

If you're an artist who is looking to learn the basic skills this is the class and teacher is for you! Stephanie is great, she lets you use your time your way, she every class day talks, one on one with you to grow and she challenges you to always do your best! Her grading System is a little funky for a beginners art class but you'll understand why
Prof. Watson is kind, helpful and truly cares for his students. One of my favorite teachers in the music department!

Homework instructions are incredibly clear, and demonstrated in class. He's quick to answer any questions, and he's always in the building (doing one job or another) so you can always pop by to ask him a question if you're having an issue. He answers emails and canvas messages quickly and besides that, it was a fascinating course on music making.
Bring a pillow to class. You will get more out of it if you just sleep through it.

Watson is dynamically the best professor for introduction to music, to music theory, and to sound recording and audio engineering. Very knowledgeable on the subject and definitely knows what he is doing! plays every instrument known to man and rips! Watson is the man

Took MUSIC110 online & if you put in the work it's very easy. Lots of reading keeping track of deadlines but super straightforward. Fair, passionate about music & wants students to succeed. Answered e-mails quickly & his personality showed through online. Reading and giving feedback, quizzes, attend music concert and final project. Easy peasy
Prof. Watson is awesome. He drops the lowest grade on one quiz so that helps with the the grade overall. I'd definitely recommend him!

Mr. Watson is a very fair instructor. Helpful responses and gives extra credit for extra effort. Enjoyed attending classical concerts (required). Identifying music audio on quizzes was difficult but not impossible. Two writing assignments weekly.
He trys to act so chill and cool and then grades you low on everything. I would nthink recommend this unique profession studio teacher

If you really are into music and want to pursue that in life then pay attention to what he says cause it is definitely necessary in the Music Major. Watson can be funny and explains the material clearly, just show up and participate because this class is an easy A. Just follow his directions and do what he says. The final is very easy as well.

Such a great class, He makes his own handouts that explain reading sheet music and writing sheet music very well. Attendance is important as he doesn't give out but maybe 3 in class worksheets a semester. No midterm and your class will all vote on what material goes on the Final and you're pretty much guaranteed a 100% if you go to class every day.
Really awesome teacher. He explains everything in class, if you don't get it, handouts explain everything else so no textbook required, otherwise he is there to help you.

I learned sooo much in this class! James is an awesome professor. I came into this class never having even touched an instrument and he explained everything clearly and wouldn't hesitate to help you if you did not understand something. I highly recommend taking this class!

James is fantastic. He is very smart and teaches with great clarity. He is always there to help you and is very good at what he does. There is no text required and he prints out all the work for you, plus he knows how to make it fun! A fantastic class to take if you're interested in becoming a Music Major, you'll learn tons. Def take his classes!

Nan was amazing for public speaking. She was clear on what was expected but also lenient if something personal comes up. She is passionate about encouraging her students to grow and become better at public speaking. It's a class you could do online but showing up to class really makes the difference and she appreciates it a lot.

I barley passed her class..... The quizzes are not based off the readings and she doesn't teach any lessons. She depends on McGraw Hill and gives you 2-4 40 question tests A WEEK. Very disappointing, I did not learn any thing.

If you want a teacher who doesn't teach a single lesson and creates very hard quizzes (not following the book), Choose Mary! I have never received lower than a B+ in college, and my first C is in this class... Medical terminology... No matter how much you study, the quizzes (every week) will have material outside of the textbook.

DO NOT TAKE HER IF YOURE TAKING MORE THAN 3 OTHER CLASSES! This class is so time consuming it's ridiculous! I fell behind in my other classes when I took her and I'm writing this because I know registration is about to open . 2-3 Quizzes EVERY WEEK all 20 points each. This class is all McGraw hill. I dont feel like I learned much.

Her HIT 135 online class is literally all Mcgraw-Hill. I really dont think she has anything of her own works in that class, well maybe the tests, she may create her own tests but that im not even sure about.
okay

This professor is condescendng, unhelpful and rude. Does not give quality feedback. She has prepared students for their certification testing. Her lecture are less than helpful and confusing. Concerned that she is the only choice in regards to coding going forward. It is clear that the department does not listen to student input, may leave SBCC.

Took the fast track course (8 weeks). Due to COVID this class was online. Professor was absent - pretty much a grader and thats it. Grade was based on weekly quizzes, one paper and a final exam. No extra credit. Exams and quizzes are proctored using Proctorio. Reading is essential - expect at least 10 hours for each lesson.

This Professor seems to focus on the things that are not critical to learn; class is Advanced Coding Applications, and more than half of this class has focused on guidelines, procedures, writing papers (citation strict) anything but actual coding. I don't feel like i have gotten much out of this class in terms of prep for my certification exam.

Prof Williamson is a GREAT professor. Responsive, caring and gives several ways to the learn the material. This is not an easy class nor it should be. You must practice and do the work- there is no winging it. She offers, videos, links, paper lectures. She answer promptly and thoroughly. TAKE her class. She is smart, knows her stuff, I respect her!

Very organized and clear. The on line class was so well set up I never had to connect with the professor for questions. Be sure to read all the assigned materials and to participate. The quizzes are easy. The midterm and final are easy. Coding assignments are a little hard. Read instructions carefully and pay attention to details. And practice!

WORST PROFESSOR EVER. Unwilling to help even when it was clearly a technical error that you had no control over. Failed me by ten points because of these technical errors. I received an A on her final and B on her midterm and still will not be willing to work it out. Her online class layout is also completely unorganized and hard to follow. WORST!
Does NOT respond to questions. Worse "professor" I've had yet!

Professor Williamson was very clear and helpful. She was very responsive to questions, and always has helpful input without being pushy. She knows howto gently guide you in the right direction. I got more than I ever expected out of this class!

I just finished my advanced coding class. Professor Williamson knows her stuff, she usually response to questions with-in 24-hours with FULL detailed explanation which is awesome! she does take awhile to grade papers but is extremely helpful!
She teaches a lot of HIT classes, very knowledgable and helpful. If you participate youll pass with an A

I am in my final classes here and have to say this woman is extremely rude and totally unprofessional in her reponses. The worst professor yet. I have never been so frustrated as I am in her class. I'm passing with a B but she never fails to make me feel bad when responding to my work! She needs a course in communication because she is lacking.

Advanced coding-a hard class that requires lots of study. I didn't have the time and that's why my grade was a C. Lynette is always willing to take the time to respond to emails and try and make you understand the subject. She REALLY KNOWS her subject. She is one of the few instructors who really care that you learn. I just wish I had more time.
Nice person and qualified but the class is boring. Lots of work for no credit and a site visit that is difficult to arrange.

Very organized. She takes weekends off, and resides back east, so her hours are based on EST. She has a lot of knowledge in this subject and is very fair. Tests are challenging, but you learn so much from Lynette.

This class is an easy A. If you go online and do the homework you will get an A. The course is out of 100 point total and she gives 20 points of extra credit for doing the orientation assignments. Super easy class.
Kendra turns a seemingly uninteresting topic into an immerse experience with her personal stories that she shares about being a restaurant inspector. She's awesome at using Canvas as well!
Kendra is an amazing teacher and knows what she's doing. I highly suggest taking her class! The homework is easy and lectures are engaging.
Kendra is a great professor! Hospitality Sanitation doesn't sound like a great fun time, but Kendra has amazing knowledge of the field and makes it a really great learning experience.
She really wanted you to learn and like accounting. The class cefinitely wasn't easy but you can get a good grade as long as you submit classwork and get help when needed.

Dr. Woyach and Dr. Alsheimer changed my life. I've been wondering what words to use to help people understand the gravity of the respect I have for the program they've put together. I've just finished all four bookkeeping certificates and I keep bugging about the ethics class. I still keep in touch and bother her whenever I'm near her.
She is amazing!

Laura Woyach is by far the best professor I have had at SBCC. If you want a teacher who cares about you and your future, take her. She is hilarious, genuine, and very intelligent. She knows what she's talking about and will help you with anything. She's a quick responder and always there to help when needed. Her class is hard but worth taking.

The most down-to-earth, compassionate professor at SBCC. You will be spending hours on end for this course. MUST attend lecture. Extremely difficult but interesting and engaging content. Provides resources to succeed and genuinely cares about you. Teaches essential life skills beyond accounting. Felt like my friend, I am forever grateful for that.

I've taken Professor Woyach for accounting 230, 240 and intro to business. She is one of the best professors I've ever had! She is extremely hilarious, compassionate and uses real-life comparisons for each topic learned in the class which is very helpful. I suggest going to every class, reading the textbook, and reviewing class notes before exams.

I love this teacher! She has such a great personality and makes class enjoyable. Don't get me wrong it is a touch class. Just show up to every class & do the homework! She offers a ton of extra credit! It's up to you if you want to pass this class!

Makes her class fun but lots of homework. Test questions seem more complicated and different than homework. From all the SBCC teachers I've had, I'd never seen a tough and unfair one like this professor. You either need to be too smart and quick as her on test; or, who knows if sympathy counts for grading. Don't go beyond required extra credit.
Very tough grader. She doesn't grade on a curve so if this isn't your best subject, skip this teacher. The assignments are the same every week.

I took BUS101 with Laura Woyach and had a fantastic experience. It was completely online and a half-semester course with no zoom or in person quizzes. I have since gotten to meet and interact with her and no dissapointements so far. Will be taking her accounting 230 class in the future.
Took her for an 8 week online course and it was the hardest class of my life, but decided to take her again for an in person 16 week class and she is very helpful and open to teach.

Mrs. Woyach is a great professor who really understands the concepts well. My favorite professor at SBCC. With that being said, there is a significant amount of homework in this class, but she makes up for it by having the tests be super similar to the homework that as long as you complete every assignment, you put yourself in a place to succeed.

Overall amazing professor. Definitely be ready to put in the work outside of class when it comes to homework and studying. The exams are relatively difficult but she offers tons of extra credit opportunities and gives you ways to easily contact her out of the classroom. You can tell she wants you to succeed and get a good grade.

The class is difficult, but she makes it so much better, I have ADHD and I would not have done well in this class with another prof. She makes the lectures fun and she is very personable. I enjoyed coming to class even though it was a 3+ hr lecture. She misses class sometimes and is sometimes late, but she is super fair and helpful.

I can't express enough how awesome Professor Woyach is. She cares about her students and will help you whenever she can. She makes it easy to understand and take notes You can easily pass if you participate, take notes, and turn in your assignment on time. This was my favorite class last semester and I recommend Professor Wayach for ACCT 230.

Having her in an in-person class was a pleasure since I loved how she talked about accounting. I enjoyed asking questions and she is often available to speak after class. I would recommend it to people who like accounting or are ready to work because she does a lot of extra credit and she will always reward hard work. Would definitively take 240.

I highly recommend Professor Laura as one of the best professors I have ever had. Her teaching style is clear, and easy to follow. She is approachable, helpful, and always willing to go the extra mile to ensure that her students fully comprehend the material. In addition, her class is an easy A if you do all your homework.
Had laura as professor for 3 semesters. Was very much open to help at all times, cares about your development. Learns you how to actualy learn instead of just teaching the textbook.

For me, a positive learning environment is the best, and Laura provided that, every lecture. There's in-class, collaborative practice that actually makes you think, and lectures are almost never boring and tie in really well with the homework. The course itself is put together very well. Laura makes it all very fun and incredibly digestible.

Professor Woyach provides great feedback and support for her students. She is super accessible and easy to reach out to. The class was well structured and was not too difficult. Be sure to also take advantage of her many extra credit opportunities as they are an easy way to pad your grade.
She cares about her students and provides lots of extra credit opportunities, do the homework and go to class and you should be fine. Listens to student feedback.

Professor Woyach is one of my favorites, if not my favorite teacher that I have had at City College. She makes her lectures very interesting, she cares about her students, and she is very accessible outside of class. If you show up to class and participate in lectures, you will do very well.

I highly recommend taking this professor for accounting. You get out as much as you put in so I would prioritize participating in class as much as possible. The material can be difficult at times, but Professor Woyach will make sure you understand the material as long as you show interest. Regardless of the courses you take, take this professor.
great teacher, makes lectures fun. Take notes and you will do fine and make sure you are in class for engagement points. Really nice teacher. Im taking her again next year.

Great professor, one of my favorites at city college. Not only does she give reasonable amounts of homework she actually teaches the material. As long as you keep up with the homework this class should be a breeze. Take it with Woyach though!
This was my third class with her, and I would recommend her. Her exams are online to avoid test anxiety and she cares about her students.

I hated this class so much. I did all of my homework and tried my hardest but I had to miss two exams because of urgent family stuff. She would not let me take the exam the next day or at all and she accused me of lying to her. I don't understand why she cant let people take the exam on a later date. Selfish. Doesn't care about her students at all.

I think Dr. Woyach was one of the best professors I have ever had. She incorporated business into accounting well. I think this was extremely important to me as a finance major, it allowed me to have a deeper understanding of the concept as a whole. If you think you can keep up with the pace of the class you should look no further than Dr. Woyach.

I had Professor Woyach for both financial and managerial accounting and she was awesome. If you show up and participate in lectures you will have a great experience. Overall, one of my favorite professors at city college and would definitely recommend.

Accounting is something that is required and most people dont want to take. Its a hard class regardless of who you take it with. That being said, she does her best to make it interesting and engage with students as much as she can in an online format. Would have like if she didnt rely so much on the publishers material but I still learned a lot.

I took Dynamic Earth. Very enthusiastic lectures with lots of information, and has no problem with going over topics again when asked. Took the time to know everybody's name, and held exam practice the class before a midterm or final. Quizzes and tests can be difficult, but curved grades when necessary. Overall a very enjoyable experience.

ERTH 171/172 Is the GIS class. You do all the assignments in class, just follow the directs he gives you on the GIS software and you will get 100%. If you finish early you can leave early. If you miss a day you can still submit it via going to the computer lab another time as long it's before the deadline. The program is only on school computers.
Wrobel is an understanding teacher and he is easy to talk to. He also teaches GIS at City College and helps with the second-year geology field courses.

One group project, labs are mandatory but super fun and go by really fast + 3 field trips. Teacher is great and actually seems like he enjoys what he teaches. Tests are 40 questions and always have a review lectures beforehand. Textbook isn't really necessary and its online, lab manual is very necessary.

Honestly one of the best professors I've came across while learning here at SBCC. He genuinely cares about his students, is always willing to help, & creates an environment where learning feels less stressful & more enjoyable. He stays true to himself, makes lectures fun, & offers plenty of extra credit there's no reason to fail his class.
The most enthusiastic and caring professor at SBCC. If you need a science credit, take DR. D!

I cannot stress enough, how amazing Daniel Zarate is for Concepts of Biology. He cares so much about each individual student's success. He is more than passionate about the subject, and you get a sense of that during his lectures and labs. Dr. Zarate is accommodating, hilarious, and just himself. Couldn't ask for more from a professor.

I loved this class. The professor's teaching style made the material easy to understand, and even the three-hour labs felt fun and lighthearted. I became so fascinated by the topics that I changed my major to Biology. Couldn't recommend it enough—also pretty easy if you're worried about taking a science class.

Best professor I have ever taken, probably one of the best at SBCC. I was very worried about failing at the start of class and now am ending with an A. He cares about each and every student and is willing to provide immense amounts of help. Also really understands mental health!!!! Every class was super funny while still being informative, 100/10!

One of the best professors you can take for bio, he teaches complex concepts in simple ways as if we were kindergarteners. He inspired me to change my major to Biology. He follows the philosophy of no dumb questions, as long as you are a half decent student you will pass his class. 10/10 Teacher, I would give a 6 if I could cute dog too
Hot and an amazing teacher- made me so passionate about biology that I changed to be a stem major

Daniel is genuinely the best professor ever. His tests are so easy as long as you pay attention in class. The labs are super fun and he has such a bubbly personality that makes everything better. He never assigns homework and is always there to make everything make sense.
Hot guy with a cool easy class, fairly lax with rules and such, able to make labs and lectures chill and fun.

It was one of the most fun courses I've had at SBCC. Labs are really fun, and there are a couple of field trips toward the end of the semester. He has a great teaching style and is helpful if presented with questions. Does not give a lot of homework, and if you go to lectures and pay attention, this class is not difficult.
Great teacher. No homework except for study exams. Great lectures as well, very interesting. Would take again.

A very kind and supportive teacher and that cares for his students. There is very minimal homework and always make sure to take the time to study for exams. Information was fun and engaging to learn and I really enjoyed his class, definitely recommend.
Dan is an incredible teacher who is both informative and funny. His labs were always fun and interactive. It is clear he is very passionate about his profession, and wants his students to succeed.

Dan is the definition of GOAT. He really wants you to learn the curriculum and will be happy to answer questions during and after class, even outside of class too. Very funny person and makes class and labs not feel like a bore. Very flexible class and 100% would take again.

If I could give Mr. Zarate and even higher rating I would. He's been one of my favorite professors here at SBCC and understands bio 100 is not meant for bio majors. He doesn't assign any homework other than recommending to study the review sheets for exams and even offers test corrections for 50% of the questions you missed. He's incredible!

Mr. Zarate was one of my favorite professors last semester. He made all the information fun to learn while also being easy to process. All of his lecture slides were very straightforward with the "need to know" information and his labs were definitely eventful to say the least lol.

Prof. Zarate is great. He's understanding & flexible with grading/deadlines, as long as you put true effort in. Very approachable & motivating, genuinely cares about every student's success. You have to pay attention in lecture & show up to lab to do well. If you do, the class is easily manageable. Textbook very helpful but not necessary (hybrid).

He really wants everyone to succeed, if you do bad in this class its kind of on you. He wants everyone to learn a lot and nothing is that hard, just do your homework and come to class and you will succeed.

Such an interesting class! You get to see through the lense of a real cop and what happens in the city! Not to mention everything is super easy. Everything you need to know to pass is on the slides and can reference them when taking quizzes. Plus lots of extra credit points ! I think I had a 105%
He's not good at explaining

Ok yes his teaching style is pretty bad if your not a note taking person and also he uses the whiteboard a lot which he also covers a lot. However his grading is really easy and simple. Also you won't know your grade until each exam you take so if you stress about your grade throughout the semester just know that. Overall easiest math class ever.

He blocks the board when writing but it's not too bad and it's still easy to take notes. There's no homework but there are quizzes every day besides the days after exams which are pretty easy. His lectures are quite long and he gives out worksheets. The material itself isn't too hard and he teaches pretty straightforward.

Lecture consists of tons of examples, so there's no surprises on tests and quizzes. He allows some notes and calculators for tests. And most impressively he returns all work with feedback and a grade the very next day. Honestly one of my favorite classes and I feel way more confident with math after taking his class.

The lectures are not the best. Quiz at the end of every lecture. I just studied during lecture and then caught up later with the material. He erases the board really quickly . No homework required, 3 exams and a final. Final was worth 200 points. He is generous with partial credit. Lets you use a ti84 for exams and a notesheet. Not too bad tbh
He doesn't teach anything

Worst teacher I've ever had. Gave no explanations for functions/equations in class, expected students to understand everything, practically refused to answer questions, literally just stood with his back to the class the whole 2 hour lecture writing on the board barely speaking. Quizzes every class. Take literally any other teacher than him.

Worst teacher I ever had. Doesn't teach you anything, just solves problems on the board without explaining or teaching you how to do it, blocks what he's writing with his body and then erases it while you are trying to copy it down. Quiz every class and a few exams. Doesn't use canvas. You will be teaching yourself 100% of calc. DO NOT TAKE IT!!
He had the worst lectures. This old man doesn't know how to teach.

Horrible. Students were walking out on the first day. He doesn't know how to teach and speaks in a monotone voice. He also covers the board while writing and then erases the work 30 seconds later. If you get stuck with this teacher just take the class at a different school.

This prof does not care about his students. If you don't solve a problem the way he "taught", he will mark it wrong. If you ask him a question, he just looks at you like he has no clue what you asked, or gives a very dry response that doesn't even help you in the slightest. Does not engage with the class at all, honestly don't waste your time.

If you care about your grade, do not take his class. You're only graded on quizzes and exams, and you are left in the dark about your grade until he passes back exams. The problems on the exams are made much more difficult than need be. His lectures are good for nothing. I genuinely do not understand how people are passing his class.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. Unless you are completely fine with having to teach yourself calc. His lectures are terrible, it's just him mumbling to the board and blocking everything he's writing. You will have a quiz everyday, and you're only graded on those and exams. The only good thing about out his class is he allows you ONE page of notes for exams.

terrible, seemed to have zero care for teaching the least enthusiastic person in the room, seems more dreadful than the students, blocks the board then erases it immediately not giving anyone a chance to copy it down, terrible teacher doesn't explain anything just puts it on the board and expects understanding doesn't give homework quiz every class

professor is not fit to teach it's almost as if he has never cared for the thought of it or seems to even enjoy teaching whatsoever. Zero enthusiasm about the topic looks more dreaded to be here than most of the students, constantly blocks the board and doesn't know how to move and erases the material immediately after he's finished. Bad, avoid!
This class requires a lot of self-study. The professor is not very engaging, but the open-book, 24-hour exams make it manageable if you put in the work. unstuck study was a huge help for exam prep.

I wish this class was more of a human interaction based, rather than a professor and K numbers. Professor is not willing to communicate with his students, dry and grades harshly, also not willing to understand personal matters concerning a student's absence and could easily fail you. Do not sign up for his class no matter the circumstances. 0/10
RUN. He mumbles to the board and is not willing to explain anything further. Cant understand anything that he is saying
He is very direct and to the point. As long as you attend every class and complete all the homework, you will pass the course.

Tbh a very chill class. Was expecting a lot worse reading the other reviews but was pleasantly surprised. The professor mainly just lectures for around 2 hours or so without interruption and then gives you worksheets to work on for the rest of class. Quizzes every day, but they're short and pretty easy (nothing unexpected).
literally didn't learn anything.

Do not take this professor. He is not clear, hard to understand, and does not provide much help. I am now in Calculus and both my Trigonometry and Calculus classes are easier than taking Alarcon's class. Very difficult. Please avoid.
Avoid at all costs. If you have no other choice but to sign up for his class then good luck.

Professor Alarcon doesn't really do anything aside from posting recycled content on canvas and grading daily quizzes. No interaction, unforgiving grader. If you aren't comfortable self-teaching, avoid this class.

The only thing he does as a teacher is give lectures, he does not help you in the class and if you ask questions about the lecture he is condescending and expects us to already know all the material. This class is incredibly difficult because the grade depends on your test scores, which you have to teach yourself because of his terrible teaching.

Besides barely being able to understand him or his lectures, being online made this class a breeze, yes it is still calculus but using his recorded lectures and material notes on top of really understanding the material by just finding videos online made this class pretty easy.
RUN.

He is awful. Not understanding to emergencies . Concise and blunt emails . I would NOT take him if you have other options . Self teaching is a MUST , and expect tests every day of the week . 10/10 would not recommend.
Worst teacher I've ever had, does not respond to emails and grades incredibly harsh. Don't expect to be taught anything by him, if you can teach yourself then you can succeed.

You're going to have to teach yourself but his class is really straightforward which I liked since it was a summer course. The textbook is great, he drops your two lowest quizzes, and your final can replace one of the three exams or the cumulative quiz score. If you read the textbook and do the exercises he recommends from the textbook you're set.

Took COMM101 during the summer as my first class at MiraCosta. Aj Anderson was my professor and her feedback was great and helped me work to improve on my speeches. I enjoyed the topics we had to write on quite a lot. The topics were quite thought-provoking and inspiring. Overall, great class and professor.

I took 121. It is communication so you do have to present on zoom if taking online. Last presentation is a simple video of you talking about the class. She understood how anxious I was to present and I felt like I did so bad. But she gave me a B! Amazing of her. TAKE HER!!!

Took public speaking with AJ at Mira Costa and she was great! A bit of a "pick me" teacher but in a good way because she was very friendly and chill! She made her classroom a safe space for everyone to practice speaking, gave good feedback and was super genuine and nice. No quizzes or finals, but participation and actually public speaking matters

Harsh and inconsistent grading. But when there's a mistake with his instruction/incorrect deadline on his behalf, it's no big deal. Provides incorrect examples of projects, making things more confusing. Very knowledgeable but frequently unavailable/unwilling to answer questions. Long/redundant lectures when time could be spent practicing Autocad.

He either will like you or not care for you at all. If you don't turn your work in right at the start of class it will be worth nothing. If you have a medical emergency and can't turn something in because of it don't expect any leniency. Couldn't care less about seeing anyone succeed unless he knows you personally or you are in architecture.

I learned a lot because it is a hands-on class. If you do the work and ask questions you will learn the basics of AutoCAD. Lectures are not very long and leave a lot of questions, helps to get you engaged. You must be present and participate, like any other compute program, you just can't learn from a book or lecture. Very helpful teacher.

Armando is extremely uncaring and makes you feel like he wants you to fail. Even the best students will not do well in his class. Do not turn in anything late, you get zero credit. You cant do enough to make him happy. He also is the only SBCC professor for many interior design classes, my advice would be to take them online through another school.

Armando is very knowledgeable in AutoCAD. His lectures will not alone leave you an AutoCAD wiz. You need to put in the work and come to him with questions. He is very happy to help anyone that really wants to succeed. Simply going to class for me is not enough. I use the lab time as well and it makes a world of difference.
Lectures were very repetitive, and he wasn't very available for students. Class was fairly simple, and assignments are not too hard.
Besides his uncaring attitude, he is pretty unclear about the expectations on assignments. If you have computer issues he'll literally just say "thats too bad"

Honestly he was an alright teacher in the beginning but I didn't really like his attitude. It was pretty dry and mostly looks like does not care about you. knew the program and was pretty good till we went all online and my computer wasnt corresponding with me and didnt let me continue with my project. He was carless, could've gotten an A but got C

Armando is a reluctant teacher. He intimidates and insults students who dare to ask him questions. He creates a hostile, unforgiving environment. His lectures are hard to understand. He makes no effort to help people who are confused. He's just not invested in teaching... he makes it very obvious his class (and you) are a chore/burden to him. F

Armando seems to be getting lazier with his teaching each semester leaving it up to his students to learn autocad on their own. Answers questions extremely vaguely or not at all. Works on personal work during class time and just seems to not really care about teaching at all. Would not recommend if you want to learn how to use design programs.

Armando is the best. He knows the Autocad pretty well and loves to share his knowledge. The projects are fun, because you get to pick what would be your project. This class fits for Architects, Engineers or any one that wants to do 2d design. Classes are fun and the lab and printers are almost always availabe. A few easy test, open book.

I have taken different classes with this teacher. If you really what to learn just do your project and ask questions. He is very supportive and knows a lot about the programs he teaches. If you actually want to learn a CAD program, he is the best! Grades are fare and you will work only one project during the semester.

Had this teacher for a bunch of CAD classes, doesn't seem to want to answer your questions, bad attitude, just overall bad experience. Not clear during his lectures, can't really understand what he's saying. Doesn't stick to the syllabus, gave the whole class an assignment he never talked about for the whole semester. BOOOO Don't take him!

Armando is very smart and enjoys sharing it. He helps his students when they need it, and makes sure they understand. He will help to make sure that your design is close to flawless and help you with his knowledge of design but will work with you to incorporate your own design. It is very easy to want to go to his class, you walk out with a new lev
Armando, please get rid of this class!! Revit is not used in the Architectural business world for a reason!
Good teacher, very chill, gives plenty of time for assignments. Is a great teacher and helps the student understand the programs. Ive taken him 2 times now and I think he is a very cool guy
nice and good

Geordie is wonderful! Her class is so amazing and she keeps it engaging and interesting! She has tons of extra credit opportunities and she is very understanding if you are taking a difficult major and communicate about your situation -- Definitely go to her office hours. Participation is also super important during lectures.

Took over summer. Watch ~20 TED/YouTube vids + her 1.5-2hr lecture vids + discussion post & quiz PER section/week. Some weeks, you had to watch a movie and take a quiz on it. Offered EC where you watch a specific movie/show and write an analysis about it that referenced the class material. Only graded by: discussion post, section quiz + movie quiz.

The work load is light which is nice, but I had a few issues with her class, for one I think we had to watch too much media, especially considering her own lectures were often around 3 hours each week. Also, I felt like a lot of the content was off-topic from the economic side of Economic Geography and more so about the cultural and social side.
She's awesome. Took this as a gen ed and did not disappoint. wasn't just an easy class but i actually really liked it. take this!!

Geordie is the best teacher you will ever have. She makes lectures fun and entertaining, while learning things that you wouldn't think of learning in a geography class. Attendance matters but its not mandatory. If you don't go to class you will miss stuff that is on the exam but you will want to go to class!
Geordie is a fantastic professor! She is very understanding, gives a ton of extra credit and the class itself is pretty easy. I'd recommend anyone to take her class.

She called a POC student “white-washed,” which is completely inappropriate, ESPECIALLY coming from a WHITE professor. No educator should make assumptions about a student's identity or speak about them in that manner. It creates an unwelcoming environment and shows a lack of professionalism. If you want to be respected, I don't recommend her.
Engaging professor with a unique teaching style. Lots of interesting videos, but the workload can be heavy. Unstuck ai was a big help for preparing for tests and making the most of the course.

The class was very interesting and fun to learn but there's A LOT of videos to watch. Quizzes are fairly easy as long as you pay attention to the videos and figure out a way to manage your time with this class. Overall very cool class just be aware of the amount of videos/lectures you'll need to watch.

She only has two things that make up the grade book. Quizzes and Tests, quizzes are very easy but the test are 70 question multiple choice questions, take forever to study for and if you do bad on a test good luck

One of the best professors I've had during my college experience! I needed this class for a GE and was dreading taking it, but it ended up being my favorite class so far. I learned so much, the content is really interesting and engaging. Her passion for the subject is infectious. Class wraps this week and I am genuinely sad. I will miss this class.
Awesome! Super accessible via email and very understanding. Her online video lectures are great!

Professor Armstrong is a unique individual that genuinely cares about her students and always goes the extra mile to help any student that may be having difficulties keeping up with required classwork.

I LOVED this class! Geordie is so knowledgable and clearly loves her job. Each section has a lot of videos to watch and then a discussion, quiz, and often a documentary+quiz for it. You have two weeks for each section, so plenty of time and she accepts late work with little markdown. I learned SO much from this class and recommend it 100%.

Super interesting topics and I feel like I learned a lot! Tests are completely based on what she says you need to know at the beginning of the module, so take notes on that because tests are open note! Prof Armstrong is fun to listen to and I loved that the modules are in 2.5 week chunks so you can work wayy ahead. Great class!
Great professor, she is always available to help. She responds quickly to emails. I would take Professor Armstrong again.

Cares about students academically and personally. Tells funny stories that may seem irrelevant, but connect to course marital in a meaningful way. Tests are easy but structured to ensure you grok the material, and is very willing to answer questions inside and outside of class. Very passionate about her topics and her students!
Funny teacher. Not the best but not the worst. Definitely has a bias towards some students

Professor Armstrong reached out to me several times during the semester after she realized that I was struggling. Her attitude and careful concern for my progress in the class was unbelievable. I believe that she truly cares about her students and wants all of them to do well in her class. It was such a pleasure having her for an instructor.
Great and passionate professor! Lots of videos to watch, but the information is very interesting!

Geordie is by far the best professor at SBCC, but more seen as a friend. She is passionate about what she teaches and wants to see you succeed. You must attend lectures to succeed in the class, but trust me they are entertaining with her sense of humor. Offers tons of extra credit which will ensure an A if you show up to class as well. 12/10

Other reviews below me are right... she is cool. She's a good professor and the class gives you different perspectives of marginalized people. There isn't a lot in the grade book, so do your assignments. She gives lots of extra credit, so there really shouldn't be any reason you get lower than a B in the class.
Geordie is the coolest professor ever, I love her. She is so awesome. Take her class!!

She's witty, lively, & uses maps to explain info. Lectures are interesting & well-structured. Grade based on: 6 documentary quizzes, video (ex: Ted Talks) quizzes, & 3 exams based on in-class lecture content. Provides a study guide for exams. Offers lots of extra credit. No textbook.

Geordie is an incredibly hard working teacher and it shows. She goes against the teacher norms and teaches how she wants. Make sure you take advantage of the extra credit because you are only graded on tests and quizzes. Easy class if you show up to class and study. Get ready for some funny stories and raunchy jokes. Best prof at SBCC hands down.

The best teacher ive EVER had. Finding myself taking another geo class next semester even tho im a marketing major just because i loved taking her class so much. Doesnt matter what yout ake just trust me and take it with geordie.

Geordie is really funny and goes on random tangents sometimes. Offers a bunch of extra credit opportunities which I recommend doing. Lectures were okay, just try your best to pay attention and take notes. Offers a lot of online resources to help you better understand the lectures and what's going on. Cares about students and again, really funny.

My favorite out of all the classes I've taken at SBCC. I've taken GEOG 101 and 102 with her because she is so amazing! Her subject material is so entertaining. She's extremely well travelled and is so interesting to learn from.If you're looking for a fun easy elective where you'll learn so much, this is the class to take.

I loved this class and professor! There is so much incredible information and thought provoking content! I am a returning student after serving in the military and convalescing after an injury. I was scared to come back in the classroom, but she was so welcoming and helpful, gave me the confidence to keep going!

Though there is txtbook, I never opened the shrink wrap and did fine. She was clear from the beginning of the class what the expectations were each week, allowed for planning ahead. The grade is based on few assignments but she offers extra credit every week. She is straight to the point on uncomfortable topics, makes it accessible and refreshing.

Sam was my anatomy lab teacher. He really puts in the time and effort for his students. The class is challenging but he makes it fun and helps make hard concepts easy to understand. If you go to his open labs and put in the effort you will easily be able to succeed.

I had Sam for my anatomy lab and he was fantastic .He is very knowledgeable and you can tell he enjoys teaching. He cares a lot about his students and truly wants you to succeed. Showing up to this class does matter and you will have to put a lot of work into the class however if you attend open lab and stay on top of studying you will do fine.

I took this class online as an "easy A class". Turns out its a lot of work per week, some weeks are even two weeks worth at a time. It's online but her class is so unorganized you can't submit things on your own time throughout the week because she won't have the submit button active or she will forget to change a form to edit instead of view only.
Took her online class. Wants very thorough and detailed responses to weekly discussions and weekly assignments. Class was organized but a lot to go through. Very long midterms and final.

Professor Ayoub is one of the best professors I've ever had. There's extra credit opportunities all semester, and most of your grade comes from quizzes. Discussions and notes are graded but doable. He genuinely loves what he does, we didn't have a final he cares about his students mental health and wants everyone to be as stress free as possible.

So kind and enthusiastic. Talks about important issues in the world in his lessons. He really cares about the individual needs of his students. Attendance wasn't totally mandatory but there were frequent in-class discussions that would also get you some easy points. All homework was submitted online and was easy. No tests or final at all either! ;)

George Ayoub was an amazing professor who cared about his students. He considered students who had busy outside lives and made assignments easy. There were no midterms or finals. The in-person discussions were very interesting, and you learned so much from this class. If you need Psych 100, take it with George Ayoub!!
Great teacher, super nice and easy class. Show up to class for discussions if you want an A.

Professor Ayoub is an incredibly professor&very passionate about psych. The kindest professor I have met and even though most students stopped attending class, he continued to speak enthusiastically to those who showed up. Assignments are completed online. I recommend attending group discussions, as they are a great way to secure a good grade!10/10

Dr. Ayoub is a chill professor. He answers emails and answers any questions during class. We did a lot of group discussions. This class consisted mostly of class discussions. It was a very relaxing class! Also, there were no exams!

This is my second class with Dr. Ayoub and I absolutely love him as a teacher. He really cares about his students and making sure they understand the contents covered in class. He usually has a weekly quiz and discussion that is accessible online but he doesn't seem to do midterms or exams in any of his classes.

The sweetest and most caring professor and human ever. I love the dynamic of his class, we had weekly group discussions, which allowed me to engage with so many new people. He cares a lot about his students and what they have to say. He is a wonderful, very knowledgeable teacher. If you have the chance, take his class. It's amazing.

He's a very sweet man and is cares very much about his students and their success. The class schedule was easy and predictable and there wasn't much work. He made sure we had weekly discussions and made friends. His class was super inviting and caring I loved it.

He is a very nice professor and wants students to do well. All assignments, quizzes, and tests were online to take at your own pace and we often had in-class discussions. There were online tests due every Friday on the chapter we were learning about but no final exam.

Prof Ayoub made this class so engaging and fun. He is beyond qualified to be teaching and has such valuable experience. His genuine care for his students is palpable and does his best to help you succeed. ANyone who takes a class with Dr. Ayoub is so so lucky. 10/10 recommend.

I absolutely loved learning from Dr.Ayoub. Dr.Ayoub is incredibly knowledgeable and can teach the class without using his slides. He creates a caring, judgment-free environment for all of his students.Dr.Ayoub is easily the best professor I have ever had at CC. If anyone is taking PSY100 for their major or as an extra class, take it with Dr.Ayoub.

I took this class as a pre-reqs for a health program. I have a busy schedule but this class was really stress free, nothing is proctored and workload is very manageable. He tends to digress during lectures. He is very caring and once gave me a free pastry because I was there early. I was actually super hungry and very thankful for his generosity.

One of the best teacher I ever had! He is such a nice professor, cares a lot about his students, and wants you to succeed! His lectures are very interesting, super easy to understand, but it is a lottt, kind of heavy! But don't worry I bet you are going to get a very good grade with professor George!

Online class. Recorded lectures were good. The tests can be a little text specific and are looking for a fill-in-the-blank answer that feels a little contrived but a great class. Caring and helpful professor, be sure to use him for additional help.

I extremely enjoyed the class I took with professor Ayoub. He really cares for his students and the material we learned in class was super interesting. He provides super interesting articles for discussions which I loved the most.

This professor was AMAZING! He gives good lectures that are interesting and engaging (doesn't just read off the slides). But most of all, he cares. I got really depressed during the semester and I started failing his class. I emailed, explained my situation, and he helped me and was so understanding and helpful with outside resources too. LOVE HIM!

I have no words to express the love and admiration I have for Professor Ayoub. His lectures were fun and easy to understand, his quizzes were difficult but he always offered EC for meeting with a tutor! He brought us snacks every single day to make sure we were fed and is genuinely the most incredible human I know.

I took his summer class and even though it was a lot of work, he was an amazing prof and really made me love the course. We went through three chapters every week so it was kind of intense but manageable. I would've loved it even more as a regular 16-week course. Great prof!

Great Class. Easy to pass if you study a little bit. Each week theres a chapter so 14 chapters total. Theres an easy discussion, and a quiz and test each week. He provides lectures and lecture slides but I find reading the chapter is best. He uses canvas and grades quickly.

Professor Ayoub is a very good professor and really cares that his students not only do well but also understand the material. He is very accommodating about being late or missing class, but almost all of the material is covered in lecture so you will fall behind if you skip. He managed to make a three hour lecture engaging and interesting.

Dr. Ayoub is a super sweet and honest guy, this class is very easy, do the homework, and online quizzes. There is a midterm and a final, both you take online. He offers extra credit for going to a tutor. You can pass w/o ever going to class, but I enjoy his lectures even tho they are slightly monotone. Easy A if you attend class.

Dr. Ayoub was a great professor and the class was extremely easy. Just do the Homework and study the material and you'll get an A. I never went to class but did the homework on time and used the study guides and got an easy A

Profesor Ayoub passion on this subject made this class fun and interesting. His teaching method are through power points so you will need a notebook for notes. Half of your grade will depend on your homework assignments which are helpful to get you ready for the test. Overall he was great and the material was not difficult to understand.

Professor Ayoub started teaching our class halfway through the semester. Getting used to his teaching wasn't easy, nonetheless, you learn to really like him. He is extremely respectful and clearly passionate about this subject. Super accommodating, and overall very intelligent man.

Long lectures covering some pretty dry material, but it's all presented clearly and with good humor. Paying attention will be the hardest battle here. One major research paper. Frequent tests demand some rote memorization, but if you do well on the first few he lets you skip the final!

Professor Ayoub is a very kindhearted man. He is extremely passionate about what he teaches. Neuroscience isn't exactly a walk in the park, but he does his best to simplify the material. Just pay attention, take notes, and be sure to read the chapters he assigns. The material is dense(brain anatomy, neurotransmitters, chem.) but super interesting!

Prof. Ayoub was a relatively interesting lecturer. I found the class super easy, but I think I was an exception...? Regardless, there were a lot of helpful resources available and he answered all my questions.

Ayoub was okay. He flew through the powerpoints so quickly that most of the class gave up on trying to take notes. To be fair, he is super knowledgable, and the class can be passed- but don't think you can put minimal effort into it and get a passing grade. My major problem was that he NEVER answered any question I had. I was pretty much on my own.

He is really interested in helping every student out. You can tell that he loves what he is teaching. He shows up everyday with a smile and that is something I really value in a professor. The tests are hard, so studying is necessary.
Mr. Barron was a fantastic and very entertaining teacher. Quizzes every lecture and half the time with sources. Late policies are lenient. He really wants you to succeed!
My favorite class I've taken at CC is his bms 136 intro to human sexuality. I'm not a science major but he made all the material so understandable. Would highly recommend any of his classes!

Would definitely recommend his class. My friends can testify since I'm always raving about said class. The way he teaches makes learning fun and understandable. He cares about his students. The course is a lot of information, so be prepared to spend hours of studying, attending lecture & tutoring. Wish I could have him as a teacher for Cell Biology

Awesome professor, and bio102 was a great class. Blake is very passionate and knowledgable about biology and he can be a straight up comedian sometimes. The lecture exams require a lot of studying so be prepared for that, going to the pal tutor sessions is extremely helpful, if you put in the work theres plenty of resources to succeed.

Blake was a truly inspiring professor. The material in this course, I felt, was primarily memorization; you have to be willing to commit time to learn your taxonomy. Once you know your taxonomy, everything else follows. This class was my favorite, and Blake is always willing to assist you and is very understanding. Also, PAL tutoring is a MUST!!

He is kind and funny but goes on tangents often. Attendance is required in lab but not lecture. He tells you everything you need for the tests and quizzes, and the grading is straightforward. There is usually one quiz per week on animal taxonomy, but being quizzed more often made me do better on the exams.The tutors for this class are AMAZING.
literally my favorite professor I have ever had
Absolutely fantastic prof, one of my favorite at SBCC. Knows his stuff, funny, super caring about his students success. More profs need to be like Blake

you have to study for tests but he gives a study guide that is easy to answer. overall, definitely take his as a teacher if you get the opportunity. super inspirational and caring. he is a great teacher and would recommend to anyone who gets the opportunity to take him. 1000000000/10 teacher.

Honestly, one of the best professors. i took his human bio of sexuality and he was so hysterical. outstanding lectures and very engaging. his is beyond kind and truly passionate about what he teaches. make sure to ask him questions in class when you are confused. he can answer any question anyone has about sex.
amazing
took bio of human sexuality online over the summer, Blakes recorded lectures were great I can't imagine how awesome he is in person, interesting class good to fill a GE
Really great professor. Has very clear lectures and informative labs that make you actually want to participate. There is a lot of studying needed to do well in his class but he makes it fun.

Blake will for ever be my favorite teacher. His lectures are super captivating and force you to learn. He makes everyone get involved. However the course is soooo taxonomy heavy and spent all my time memorizing stuff I would forget a week later. But this class changed my perception of everything in this world. I recommend him to everyone.

This class was asynchronous.This class was one of the easiest As I've ever gotten. The book wasn't totally needed but highly recommended. GET READY TO READ. Tests weren't proctored but don't assume you'll pass if you're not doing the homework. The discussion boards were fun too and make it feel more like a real class when responding to others.

Blake is by far the best professor I've ever had. He's hilarious, passionate, and very approachable. I've never met someone so well educated on a topic, and he provides tons of resources to ensure that everyone in his class is getting the most out of it. Super fun field trips and awesome labs. cant recommend him enough.

Blake is the best teacher you will ever have. He teaches bio in a way that changes your whole perception of it. He is available outside to talk to for other things like volunteering and career options. Animal bio is the hardest classes i've taken so far but it helps you get ready for upper division bio classes. lots of work, but he is amazing.

I've known Blake for two years now and he really is my mentor. His class is a lot but so worth it. He really cares and takes the time to get to know his students. When we were all stressed with COVID he pushed back our midterm to give us a weekend to relax and catch our breath. Very approachable. Form a relationship with him early on.

Blake will forever be my favorite professor I'll ever have! He is the literal best. He's very understanding and cares about all his students. His class is a lot of work but totally worth it because it's very interesting. His passion for the subject makes learning a lot better too.

Blake is by far the best professor I've ever had. He's is beyond caring and inspirational. If you put effort into his class he will return the favor. If you get the chance to take his class DO IT! Animal Bio was one of my favorite experiences at the CC. You need to study for an hour or two every day to be prepared for the class as it is difficult.

Blake is awesome but his Animal Bio class is super hard! Does a great job at providing extra resources for you, accepts late work, and will email back. Study hard for this class and if you miss lecture, you need to be sent the notes. Blake is approachable and happy to help/answer questions. When we switched online bc COVID, lectures were recorded.

Blakely is an amazing person! He really cares about his students and really tries to help you pass his classes. The textbook really isnt needed, study your notes, read and study ALL the labs, learn the practicum asap and answer ALL review questions and you will do well. Enjoy the class and Blakely, it was really so much fun!
Not sure why everyone loves him. He thinks hes funny, but hes really not. His lectures are not at all helpful, I never showed up except to take tests and was better off teaching myself.
BLAKE!!! yea YUP .. absolute pog champ of a class. Just be interested in learning and the good grade will follow right along. smiles across the room aye

OMG this man changed my life. Honestly wasn't sure if I was going to go through with being a BIO major, but his love for teaching and for the subject changed everything. BIO 102 is definitely a hard class, but he provides a lot of homework and other opportunities so your grade doesn't just deppend on tests. LUV
She's so fun and encouraging! I definitely will recommend her! some of her workouts are tough, but that's the point!
Just show up to class or work out at the local gym and tell her what you did and you'll pass. Awesome boxing class.

I really enjoyed taking Kristina's spin class! She was always very enthusiastic and kind and provided great workouts each class. She always pushes you to be your best self and is very understanding. Once you attend one class you will be motivated to come back. Such a great teacher!

I loved this class! It was just the right amount of challenge and it was really good to see my progress over the weeks. Kristina is super understanding and one of the nicest people I have met. She is really inspirational and such a strong woman! I would love to take another one of her classes.

Took her beginning boxing class last summer, and I loved it! She helps modify workouts when needed, and lets you do lots of fun extra credit, like making playlists for the class to workout to (which I did!). Very encouraging, and you see great results by the end of it all if you put the effort in.

I took beginning spinning with her and it was such a fun class I always came out happy because she knows how to make you sweat and work on every muscle in the body. I felt happy leaving her class because I got the workout I needed. Highly recommend she always checks in with her students before class to see how their doing and puts up beat songs.

Kristina is the nicest lady ever! She checks in with all of her students before class and asks what you want to work on that day. Be prepared for a hard workout every day. She grades by adding points so once you get the grade you want you don't have to go. Lots of extra credit. If you show up or miss and do the extra credit its an easy A.

Kristina is amazing! I took 3 of her classes (beginning & intermediate spin, & beginning kick boxing) she really motivates you to workout and the results are amazing at the end. The connection she has w/ her students is unbelievable, she really gets you involved & makes the class fun. If you're looking for a great workout and a challenge, DO TAKE!!

This course was straight forward, but I am also knowledgeable on Word, Excel, Access, and Power Point. They provide plenty of practice assignments before an exam. I taught myself everything, but there are tutors available if you need help.

It is an automated as so many online course. So many announcements, and the course is with some other company - Cengage? Professor Beletti helped me with the course, but she was not able to teach the actual material. She does not know Access or Excel beyond the instruction of the textbook, which is very basic. To learn Office, take another course.

Professor Belletti was so helpful and encouraging throughout the course considering how time-consuming the assignments are. This isn't an easy class, however, Lorrie and her assistant Jennifer are so kind and always willing to assist with questions. Take advantage of extra credit and be willing to put in the time and you'll pass. ASK QUESTIONS.

Prof Beletti is AMAZING. I struggle as a student and she helps me every single time I ask for help and that is a lot. She answers questions all day and night. I asked a question at midnight and she answerd me 20 mins later. That doesn't happen every time but she's helpful even on the weekends. If you need to take a computer class this is it!

She threatened to drop me for not turning in a 10 point assignment even though I have an A+ in the class even after the fact. Said, and I quote, "refusal to turn in the assignment will be detrimental at best, at worse you will be dropped from the course". Weird scare tactic with sucky use of cengage. 3/10
This was an easy A for sure. There was lots of work but as long as you turned it in your golden. She's super nice. I will definitely be taking other classes she teaches.
Professor Belletti is awesome! Just do the work. She helps you with everything you need and shes actually really nice too. Just ask questions and she'll answer them real quick.

The material is kinda hard but if you read and listen to her directions you will be fine. Don't skip around. No late work accepted but extra credit given at the beginning of the semester in case you run into any problems during the semester. Always there when you need help.
Listen to what she says and follow directions. She's great about contacting students. Answered all my questions. She's great. Couldn't have done it without her help.

She listened and answered all my questions in record time. It's a hard class so it's important that the teacher is there for you. The class is hard, but if you read her emails with helpful hints, the class is so much easier. FOLLOW DIRECTIONS and you will do great.
If you do the work, you do great. She tells you exactly what to do to get an A.
She's very clear about her expectations. She's always there for you whenever you need her. Great Instructor.
She is awesome. Great instructor. Cares about her students.
Nice
Super!
Awesome!
No Comments

Took her for RT 100. She is amazing and understanding if you can't make it to her online class. Says it's a 2 hour class but literally under an hour. She gives a lot of powerpoint and just take notes because all the answers are in there.
Very liberal teacher. If your essays are not liberal you will get a C with a bs excuse as to why. Also you will have a few modules on sex.

This class was super easy! All you have to do is attend 5/6 lectures and complete a final project. The project is pretty vague but not too hard. The lectures are informational and quick. The only downside is that the class is on a Friday morning!

As a straight-A student, do not take him. He is probably the toughest grader I have ever had. He's nice, but unless you do it specifically how he wants it, even if it's not asked in a prompt, you won't get an A. Lots of reading, but you do get two weeks to do it.
Class is just very particular in your response to questions. Tough grader and must read the textbook and have a good understanding of it in order to do well on assignments

Taking this as an online summer class, the workload is managable. 3 assignments a week, one quiz, one discussion and a 600 word reflection. a lot of reading required to fully understand the material, if you dont it will show in the reflection and he will take points off. Effort is not enough it has to be exactly what hes looking for to get an A.

Overall, he is a great logic professor. The subject is not easy, but he is always willing to answer questions and has great patience. Attending class really matters because he is very lecture-heavy, grades fairly, and is clear about what's on exams.

Professor Bobro's course is challenging but rewarding. The workload is heavy, with quizzes, discussions, and reflections, but he wants students to succeed. unstuck ai was a lifesaver for exam prep alongside the textbook.

He is such a tough grader. Would get marked down for answers being too "short" or too long. No way of winning with him. He's not understanding of students life and will get marked down on everything if not perfect. Ruined my GPA and it's hard to make up points.

We honestly did not have a lot of class work, class was really a large discussion on philosophy. As long as you show up and participate getting a good grade is light work. He was a really nice teacher as well.

Incapable of grading fairly with his expectations. One of his assignments, the guidelines are: answer the prompt, and provide *less* than 600 words. Despite not having a word minimum, and only a word maximum (for whatever reason) you will lose points if you turn in a response that is 400-500 words, even though his prompt is only 1-2 questions.

I now hate this subject. I was excited to take this class, but the grading on the reflections is too harsh. Ruined my 4.0 gpa streak by a couple points. Boring reading, easy to drift into a mental oblivion. I wish I could change the speed of the podcast he does to go faster, but that is more a me issue.

Took him fall semester, phenomenal class. Very engaging lectures and well-challenged content, yet mellow/relaxed in his demeanor. Very knowledgeable in philosophy (especially in this era). So well-versed in philosophy, he even wrote a paper on RMP!

Professor is a super cool guy with super cool opinions and he's definitely very funny and nice. Super good class environment, and asking questions definitely makes this class more entertaining, it can get a little dull at times. Make sure to do the readings and respond to the discussions but other than that there is really not much work.

Philosophy is usually not a subject that I know a lot about or am interested much in, but I definitely learned something from this class! Professor Bobro is a cool dude and gives you so many opportunities to earn a good grade. This semester we had 5 quizzes + discussions and 3 papers, which were all pretty easy to do as long as you show up to class

Class is very methodical, he engages in good discussions. At the end there is an Honors Project, make sure you work on it early so you don't cram on it during finals week. Overall great professor, he does grade the reflections very critically, but after all, this is an ethics class.

Doctor Bobro is super cool. His online lectures are in the form of podcasts, which I genuinely enjoyed and he is super personable. He might be a little tough on grading his reflections, but the comments are critically helpful. Super engaging and all you really have to do is care and put in the work. Take his class!!

Really wants students to succeed and get the most out of the class. No tests, drops lowest scores in each assignment section. The material/lectures are interesting and the workload is not too heavy. I wasn't interested in philosophy, just took this to fill a GE but I looked forward to every class. Cool guy and very knowledgeable.

Bobro definitely knows what he is talking about. This class consists of weekly discussions, quizzes, and reflections. The discussions and quizzes are easy, but he grades the reflections toughly leaving no room for error. Despite the reflections this class is pretty easy. Lots of reading, but no midterms or final.

Professor Bobro is omniscient in his discipline. I took ethics, logic, ancient, and modern philosophy with him. All of them are superb. You will learn much more if you do all the readings and participate in class. It is fun to write reflections for the ethics class, and I think he is not a tough grader. If you like maths, logic will be easy.

Professor Bobro has quizzes, discussions, and reflections. The quizzes aren't that bad and he is an easy grader when it comes to discussions. However, he makes you work for the reflections. When it comes to his feedbacks they can almost sound a bit rude but honestly, he is just straightforward. No finals and drops the lowest score in each section.

I hate Philosophy of Art. The entire class is full of questions with no answers like "what is art?" etc. It's an easy A and the professor is nice but the class itself is brutal. All about the aesthetics of art and morality of different art pieces. Massive waste of my time but it's easy enough, just a headache to read philosophers' writing.
Worst professor I have ever had at Sbcc. Sucks because I was actually looking forward to taking this class but he ruined it for me. He also doesnt care about his students

Professor Bobro is super knowledgeable. I learned a lot from his class, but you definitely have to put in a lot of work to get an A. There are a lot of quizzes and exams. Each class makes up of lectures, discussions, and quiz every class.

This class separates itself from the rest of my elective courses. It challenges you to think critically in ways you've most likely never had before. The online lectures are easy to follow and coupled with helpful zoom calls make for an easy learning process. Mr. Bobro cares about his subject and goes above and beyond to help his students learn.

I loved this class so much! I liked to listen to the lectures while cleaning my room or something! His lectures are in podcast form so it doesn't really feel like a lecture and the topics he discusses are SUPER interesting. Also every topic is separated by two weeks instead of a new topic every week. Found the textbook online for free, TAKE HIM!!

Pretty easy class. Weekly quizzes, discussions and reflections. You get two attempts at the quizzes so its pretty easy to get an A on all of them. The only hard part about this class are the reflections.

I took Bobro for deductive logic and am very happy with the outcome. He gives a few pages of homework and a few tests throughout the entire semester, so barely any work. HOWEVER, it took me 4 hours to complete each graded homework that was assigned. Logic is difficult and takes a lot of time to figure out, but Bobro explains the subject very well.

Marc was by far the best teacher I've had this semester. He is organized, goes into depth in his lectures and always has a quick answer that is logical when people try to challenge him. He is also really funny, very laid back, and is understanding if you have confusion. He gives out study guides for tests. Only thing is pop quizzes! Go to class.

I really liked this professor. He taught very well. Very nice. Class was enjoyable. Had some time for questions. I learned a lot. Tests were not easy, but not extremely hard. Homework was good for practicing the material from lecture.

He marks down your opinions, which makes absolutely no sense. You have a discussion post, a 20 question quiz, and a reflection due every week. It's not hard, but he marks you down for extremely confusing reasons, and it seems like he makes them up just to keep your grade around the same. Might not be true, but it happened to me.

I loved this class, I felt like I learned the most about American history and culture. Her lectures are amazing, she organizes them into case studies that align with the period you read about for homework. Attendance isn't mandatory, but there are daily quizzes, and she goes over information about topics that will be on the tests. you read alot

Prof. Buller is a total sweetheart. She cares so much about her students and is very approachable if you have any questions. She creates a great vibe in class and motivates me to do the work, so I wasn't mad about the reading quizzes. I learned more in this class than in my other ones because of her teaching style.

Although it seemed (to me) like she assigns a lot to read, it's all for good reasons. She cares about what she is teaching, so the more you put in, the more you'll get out of this class. Participate in class and do the assignments, it'll prep you for the exams. Critical thinkers join here.

Professor Buller is really smart and knows what she's talking about, however the reading is insane, and unnecessary. Everyday you walk into class met with a reading quiz, so not only be in class, but be on time everyday. She talks the whole class period with only a couple minutes left for class discussion. This simple class made the sem. very hard.

If you want a good grade in this class then you must show up every day. There is a quiz as soon as you walk in the door. Not many extra credit opportunities are given. The midterm and final aren't too bad and the study guides are pretty good. You do need to memorize dates. You end up reading the whole text book so you definitely need it.

I absolutely LOVED this class! I've never liked history much before taking this course. You must take it! The material is so eye-opening and interesting. Gets you to really think outside of the box. Justina is amazing! Super kind, supportive, passionate, inspirational, and she explains/teaches so naturally! She wants everyone to learn and do well!

Super cool teacher. Very enlightening class, very eye-opening. People were pretty sick in the class, too. Lots of reading assignments, but the majority were decently interesting. Two papers total, quizzes are easy, the midterm and final were not the worst, I studied a bit. They sound harder than they actually end up being. Take this class!

Great class, but felt like 5 units. Dense reading, hard midterm/final, and a research paper, but if you're interested in the topic, the material is really eye-opening. Prof is enthusiastic and passionate, grades kinda easy, and offered quite a few extra credit opportunities. I put a lot of work in for an A, but otherwise, it wouldn't be that bad.

Shes the best. Professor Buller is challenging, yet caring of the student and gives you all the tools you need to be successful in the class. There is a lot of work, but it is well worth it and a real history eye opener.
