I think, every American freshman should be obligated to take her class, especially for the earlier time in the U.S. history. Her approach, method, and attitude for learning history are necessary for a student, I would say.

Professor uses InQuizitive for a test every week that randomly generates questions that are often confusingly worded. Makes discussion posts meant to start social and political conversations but often lacks contextual information in order to make every discussion "two sided." Little flexibility with self guided projects. Don't go above and beyond.

I would rate her a 5 if it wasn't for her midterm and final because they were weirdly hard compared to the rest of her assignments (2 weekly discussions and 2 big projects). Overall though she's a genuinely caring and friendly person and wants her students to learn. As long as you read the textbook/articles and watch her lectures you'll be fine!

Justina Buller is an amazing professor and I am so happy I was able to take her class. She had amazing lectures and always provided feedback and was super accessible outside of class. She truly is a wonderful professor and her lectures were super engaging for me. Make sure you finish readings- expect a 25+ page article alongside weekly chapters.

She is amazing. I have gotten more out of her class than any class in my life before. She really dives deep into each of the topics that she discusses- every reading & discussion is very intentional. She is awesome to talk to about personal/ class related things. She puts all her effort into every conversation & all feedback. She is the best ever.

Overall, you can tell that she cares a lot about the subject and her students. She's willing to help and talk in office hours. However, there is a LOT of work, so be prepared to put in a lot of time in this class. Lots of reading, course readers, textbook. An interesting class, but you need to put in a lot of time.

I have no problem with Mrs. Buller herself, she's an amazing teacher and person. But I was expected 9 hours per week which in my opinion is a bit ridicules for a GE class. Im taking 4 classes right now, took this one for credit, and I feel like I'm more focused on this course than I am on my actual major related classes.

Professor Buller has very good insights and doesnt pull punches when it comes to a hard look at the history of early Colonial America, and the U.S. Not only did she teach history, but she also knew that a lot of her students would be freshmen, and the first week was on learning how to learn. This has helped me in all my other classes as well.

Tough Grader, Absolutely no room for late work. Her use of online program "Inquisitive" sucks, I hated the low grades it gave me even though I know I was right. The course Load I had, I would say was harder than any Engineering or Physics course I took so far. Chem 101 was easier. The chapters are 60+ pages long each, Dyslexics beware.

Justina's awesome! Her style of teaching was a little off-putting at first--felt kind of high school-like--but then we got into it and the class didn't feel like that at all and we all learned so much. I put 4/5 on the difficulty because I intentionally put a lot of work in. I wouldn't be too worried about the difficulty, but be ready to engage.

I loved taking Bullers HIS102, she was my first history prof. at SBCC and has been one of the best. I love her enthusiasm and her willingness to see her students succeed. The material is interesting and the workload is not small but normal, she gives you the tools to be successful. I took this class hybrid and enjoyed it a lot!

Justina is no doubt my favorite professor I've had at Sbcc. Her teaching style is amazing. She wants you to understand the material more than anything. Its a good amount of work. The readings take up to an hour every class but it's not boring reading at all. Pay attention in class, take notes and PARTICIPATE! Changed my view on history forever

Professor Buller is passionate, kind and caring, which made this class even better. Ive never been the biggest history fan, however because of Professor Buller this class was great and I learned a lot. There is a lot of required reading and assignments every week but if you do the readings and pay attention you should be fine!

She is without doubt VERY passionate about what she teaches. It's a unique class with a lot of class discussions/debates. Her office hours are open and she is very welcoming to talk with. Be prepared for lots of reading, some busy work, and her pushing a political agenda.

Justina is very organized and passionate. She changed my view of US history & inspired me to major in ES. That being said, I found it very difficult to balance on top of a full load of classes and work, as she assigns extensive readings/a quiz per class. It is no where near an easy A, but you'll have an in-depth understanding of the material!

Very textbook heavy class. Lots of reading like a college history class should be so it's not unbearable. Makes a lot of corny jokes in which you learn to appreciate over time. Being vocal in class is KEY! She notices who participates more than others. Overall, really enjoyed her class and am excited to take more with her again.

ENVS 112 has been tough. You should not take this class if it does not seem very interesting to you or if your major does not require it. That said, this class has changed my view of the world; I now see the ways our deeply rooted systems--capitalism, science, a societal fixation on efficiency--has influenced all of our lives. Phenomenal class.

I would take this class 1000000 times over! Justina is so passionate and provides such interesting material that will change your view of American history. It is a lot of reading but it is all fascinating and completely changed my mindset towards history classes and class in general. This class inspired me to major in ENVS! I LOVED this class!!

The teacher is SO enthusiastic & inspiring & you learn a ton. BUT you have about 40-60 pages of reading a week, a quiz each class period which makes attendance mandatory bc you can't make them up. The midterm you gotta study HARD for & research project was one of the hardest things ever. Dont take it bc you think its an easy A for your GEs. Its not

MsBuller was by far one of the best professors I have ever had in my life. She genuinely cares about her students and their success. She is very sweet but also very moving about getting your work done efficiently. Would most definitely take her class again

She is a good professor and is clearly passionate about her subject. There is quite a lot of reading and homework but the grading criteria is very clear and if you do the work you will get a good grade. The case studies/lectures were often off topic and were clearly pushing a political agenda but still sometimes interesting.

Took the online Bio 100 and it wasn't a difficult class. You do need to study the material but the lectures were helpful and the study guides helped if you completed them and out them into quizlet to study. I asked to take an exam past the due date and she was very nice about it and gave me a plenty of time to take the exam and not rush it.

The instructor uses a free, online, & easily accessible textbook, which was nice of her to provide. Still, it was wordy and explained concepts in a complicated manner that often left me more confused. The average score on the first exam was ~50%, which most of the class felt majorly unprepared for & tanked grades at the beginning of the semester.

Professor Calkins was my online lab professor for this summer course, and the labs were absolutely ridiculous. I understand that it is a hefty course for 6 weeks, but 45-page labs are you kidding me? There is also 0 guidance on what to study for the quizzes. This class, especially the labs, just felt like a waste of my time.

I dropped her class mid-way through the semester because I asked for help from her and she flat out asked me if I had even turned in any of the labs or homework. She wouldn't even give me the time of day to meet with me over Zoom to try and help me achieve a better grade. Do not take her class if you struggle with test because it is all proctored.

Erin is a nice professor and wishes the best for her students. Although she may go too fast on her lectures so pay close attention and ask questions if you have any. Assignments are pretty easy and based off lectures/readings. Exams are kind of long and there are quizzes once a week, so study!

I had Erin for the lab portion of BIO100 and the first few classes I didn't like her because she seemed to have an attitude. She has come from teaching middle school so this actually makes sense. She is always willing to answer and explain things, and shes pretty funny too. I also have heard that she lets us get off easy compared to Jens lab

class averages of 30-40%, everyone drops, impossible to prepare for his test, egotistical control freak who genuinely does NOT want students to succeed, lectures are confusing, pedantic, and useless to learning the material. Highest grades on exams were C's and D's (which I had), drop math for the semester if you have to take it with him, AVOID PLZ

This class was probably the hardest math class I have taken. Every class has lengthy lectures that are always rushed, and the tests are very difficult with witheringly low averages (most people did not pass). Jamie assigns copious homework that is graded on accuracy, and though he is a cool guy, it's super hard to do well or even pass the class.

Professor Campbell is a good guy, though I wouldn't recommend his class. Very harsh tests with questions you wouldn't come across on his practice exams or homework's. And in lecture speaks pretty fast. He definitely knows his stuff and is great in math, but the class seems to have been made excessively hard.

Unless you have exceptional learning ability and mental endurance, don't expect an A. The professor doesn't care if no one gets an A. Attendance is mandatory but only deducts points. Homework is heavy yet worth just 15%. Exams are extremely hard, cover unseen problems, and count for 85%. Take it next term if possible.

Unfairly specific about notation you use in homework and exams... I put a small, rightward arrow indicating where my work was continuing and got points marked off? There is nowhere he specifies his preferences except handed back work. If you use algebraic methods from previous classes he hasn't pre-approved, you will also get marked off.
This is without a doubt the hardest math professor at SBCC he excepts nothing but perfection. He's not a good dude and his grading and exams are insanely hard and flat out unfair. Don't take him.

The way Jamie handles homework and feedback for it is grossly tedious and unhelpful... I think it's to make grading easier? Don't really feel confident in the content despite being very on top of the work so far. otherwise, he's very responsive to class questions and emails, albeit a bit rude... willing to help our class pass but isnt nice about it

Condescending replies, stops contact if you're behind. Prioritizes rigid pacing over clarity. Heavy use of a clunky, outdated online system for homework. Random exam changes mid-semester. Grade boosters don't offset rushed content. Sub-70% pass rate—avoid if you actually want to learn Differential Equations. Sign up early for someone else.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!! He is very condescending toward students, and his lectures are impossible to learn from. He is very pedantic with his notation, and doesn't answer questions. I took a precalc class before his and I had an A+, but is his class I ended up with a C. This man singlehandedly destroyed my mental health in one semester.

Tests every 3 weeks. You only have half of a class period to complete a test that should take the full period. Very condescending towards students. Does not set you up for success. DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH THIS PROFESSOR IF POSSIBLE

This class is challenging, but the professor is helpful if you put in the work. Attendance is mandatory, and the grading can be strict. Unstuckstudy was a lifesaver for preparing for tests alongside the course materials.

Genuinely one of the worst professors I've ever had the misfortune of having. My class's average lay in the 50% range for nearly every test. His tests are absurdly difficult, and he failed the majority of my class. There is nothing you can possibly do to please him unless you are perfect. I don't understand why he was hired. Avoid at all costs.

Never seems to cover what will be on the homework/exams in the lecture packets as the way he describes concepts are in an extremely broad manner. He frustrates me, and the math tutors when I have to ask them a question. I would not apply for any classes taught by Mr. Campbell, however if I had met him in a non-school setting I would like him a lot.
If all the other math classes are full and this is your only option left during registration just take math next semester. ABSOLUTELY DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.

DONT TAKE THIS CLASS . He cannot teach and when he tries to explain how to solve a question he explains it in the most impossible way ever. I had an A+ in precalc and still failed some tests in this class. IF YOU DO NOT HAVE A STRONG BACKGROUND IN CALCULUS YOU WILL FAIL

ABSOLUTELY DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. Listen to me carefully when I say this, it is better to take the class next semester with ANOTHER professor than to take this class. He took 15 points of one of my assignments just for scribbling an answer off to correct it. He chooses the hardest most difficult calc questions and doesn't teach you how to do it .

Definitely the hardest math teacher in the department. He's condescending towards students, pedantic w/notation, and not very accommodating. Work feels unnecessary and long and didn't learn much at the end of the class, especially when compared to "easier" classes like 210 w/Pretel. I'd recommend taking this class with another teacher if possible.
He is a very tough grader. Take this class with a different professor if possible.
He is very strict with everything. You will receive markdowns if you use another teacher's methods. Very tough grader, take this class with a different professor if you can.
Worst math professor out there. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.

Awful professor. Hybrid class meetings only once a week. And for like 3 weeks he wasn't there. Does not actually teach in-person. All self-taught via his videos that skip essential steps. Exams areworth 50% and he's a tough grader with no cheat sheets. WOULD NOT EVER TAKE AGAIN. RUN FAR AWAY.. unless you're super comfortable teaching yourself math.

Strict class rules, strict math rules, hierarchical relationship, teaches you how to obey his nitpicky criteria, you will lose points for tiny things, red marks on homework for messy work or too much work, avoid this professor. On the good side, he is incredibly talented at math, often available to help, wants you to understand the material well
This class is very hard I do not like mr campbell:( he is tough grader on tests

Jamie's explanation is very unclear, in other words, not helpful to you. He also likes to skip a lot of key steps, leaving you confused. Then there's the fact that he has very little practice and never gives you a solution, so you can't quickly figure out where you're wrong. Anyway, I'm not taking his class anymore.

Lectures all recorded and not always helpful, but made you have tests in person. Homework was reasonably but putting in your answers was difficult. Put in hours of work and studying, but would still get a C on the tests. Was mostly because he is very anal about notation which he didn't mention. Rarely gives partial credit.

If you have a basic understanding of Algebra you will do fine. He has online video lectures and you can just follow along. While this works for me, it might not work for others. Although his class is online, his exams are in person, and quite difficult if you do not have an understanding of the material. Expect to put work in yourself!
avoid this class!

The worst professor I have had at SBCC so far. Is not flexible with his students in helping them. Doesn't teach the material and says to watch his lecture videos to learn the material. I dont know how he is the department head...

Prof. Campbell is a nice guy but a bad teacher. Even though its an online class he makes you take tests in person. The lectures do not even relate to the homework. Probably going to have to retake this class because he does not teach what he quizzes on.

Jamie Campbell's rating overall rating should be so much lower. I've never seen more people fail so many midterms and the overall class. He forces his online asynchronous classes to take exams in person. He is arrogant and doesn't recognize that his methods may not work for everyone (the majority of his classes!). He should not be a math teacher.

This here professor comes in clutch in a time when learning is integrating to a mostly online environment. The cool thing about Angel is that he makes the coursework almost intuitive, and his experience in the field makes the online transition almost seamless.

Professor Cardenas very good professor. 30 years experience in networking. He share with you his experience. He makes positive learning environment. He treats all students fairly and equally. If you want to increase your Networking skills, take his class/classes. I promise, you will learn very much from him.

Professor Cardenas has been teaching at SBCC for more than 20 years, and 30 years experience in networking. He makes positive learning environment. If you want to increase your Networking skills, take his class/classes. Follow his instructors, show up. I promise, you will learn very much and you will reach your goal. Good luck.

Professor Cardenas makes very positive learning environment. If you want to increase your Networking skills, take his class/classes. Follow his instructors, show up. I promise, you will learn very much and you will reach your goal. Good luck.

Angel is an amazing professor. He gives great lectures and his knowledge knows no bounds in the field of computer network engineering. His lectures can be very long but he tries his best to make them enjoyable. Get ready to read a lot. Don't be afraid to ask him questions. He is always happy to help if you need it. Lab Assignment heavy classes.

Angel Cardenas is an outstanding teacher. His courses are extremely well organized with a clear path of required work. He a fantastic teacher his years of experience in the field is evident in the expertise he brings to his classes. I have taken several of his classes and enjoyed them all immensely. I cannot say enough good things about him. Thanks
Very helpful teacher and very knowledgeable about the subject. Really chill guy, I recommend this class for an easy A and those interested in learning about computer networking.
Angel is a cool teacher and the cnee program is really good here. 101 is an intro into the IT world. answered all of our questions. I recommend him

HE is a pretty cool teacher! HE is always willing to help you and to make you work when he knows you can do it! I'd being taking a couple clases with him and so far i have nothing bad to say about him.

Awsome Teacher the classes he teaches are easy as long as you come to class prepared to learn. He is extremely Knowledgable abut all subjects of computers and is awsome at making complex subjects easy to understand

I thought I would take a basic spreadsheet class, but was surprised how much I learned about Excel. I am an accounting major, and this course is a big help for me. I also liked how quickly the professor responded to my questions. The course does not take a lot of time, but there a lot of small to medium activities to complete each week.

I took some Excel in COMP 101 at SBCC, but in this course Excel is really good for students in Finance or Business. A lot of homework, but I did not have to spend too much time on it. What surprised me how quick he responded each time to my email. Tip: If you have a MAC, use the sbcc remote desktop (vpn?) to do all the activities.

I took an excel course via an internet course. I decided to take this course for credit , and was surprised how this course was integrated with business principles. It was not easy at times, but it was worth taking the course. Also, surprised on the quick responses I received on my IMs. He helped me several times via zoom.

Professor Carter did a great job with this class online. The class was organized, the content was meaningful and helped improve my skills in Microsoft Excel a lot. I would have liked to get to know the professor a bit more. He seems like a knowledgable guy, who really enjoys Excel.
Course good - Class would be better face to face, phil met with me a few times on campus which was very helpful. tutors in the lab when available were great

I enjoyed the class overall. The textbook was alright, but I am not a big fan of SAM. Professor Carter gives us a lot of direction, the course was very organized, and his feedback was always quick - always same day. Without his input though I think the class is too hard. Computer lab access was great too! The tutors were a great help.

A crazy amount of homework is expected, poor reading material for the level expexted, the book was all over the place. Has you working weekends. Help is minimal. The online tests didnt work during important exams and you would lose important points and he seemed to not care that much.

I took this class last semester. The way the class is structured is horrible. The grading criteria is poor for what is expected, and he issues way too much work for the duration. I've been here a long time and never encountered this!

Took this class online in 2014. He was okay; not a 'real' teacher though. I would recommend someone else if that's an option though. I did not get a lot out of the class and his instruction did not remedy that. It's a low level class so don't waste time on assignments that don't get you points and you should be fine.

Tara is a fantastic professor! She shows unmatched dedication to her field and the welfare of her students. Unit tests are replaced by ~3-page essays, which she gives clear feedback on. Take notes on in-class presentations, they can be used on the essays. Definitely take Tara's course if possible, it makes for an enjoyable, valuable experience.

Take this class! Tara is a relatively laid-back professor, and her lectures are always engaging, often covering current and relevant topics that everyone should be aware of. She accepts late work and allows you to revise assignments. While she can be a tough grader, her feedback is fair and helpful. Overall, I'd definitely recommend her class.

MAKE SURE TO WATCH LECTURE Professor Carter is such an amazing Professor. While teaching you important concepts that help in life, she incorporates motivation and good feedback to ensure you do good in school. Her lectures are funny, quick, and needed to answer assignment Q's. Overall Amazing class. Going to miss the several dogs cameoed in lecture

GO TO LECTURE. Tara is the sweetest, most down to earth person and is very understanding of students' personal matters. I usually hate science-based classes but I learned so much from this class and had a great time participating in lecture. Moderate amount of hw and a few short essays but easily manageable and again, she's very understanding.

I absolutely loved Dr. Carter! Her class may seem hard at first, but it is not. Attendance is not really mandatory unless you want to do well in class. She takes the information from the book and explains it in a way that makes sense. Her lectures are very entertaining, never boring. Her assignments are extremely easy, too.

Tara Carter is a passionate professor. Late work and revisions are allowed. There is a pretty big load of assignments and lecture videos each week. The quizzes and essays are manageable, it was the weekly assignments that were difficult to keep up with and were graded pretty strictly. This class is good if you're willing to put in the effort.
Tara is an amazing professor. Her lectures are very interesting and she gives amazing feedback on assignments. Definitely take her class if you have the option to.

Ah Dr. Carter was so so amazing! I took a shorter class with her, and there was a chunk of work to do but if you keep on top watching lectures and turning things in on time you're set! I'm a high school student, she made everything understandable and digestible in her videos. She's so sweet, genuine,and gives really helpful feedback on assignments!

I was super nervous I wasn't gonna pass this class but she kept her gradebook open till the very last night (please don't do what I did) she's so great. If your new to college PLEASE TAKE HER COURSE!!! She gives great feedback on essays and has a great growth mindset for her students, it'll really help out with other classes you'll take!

Tara Carter is the best professor I have ever had. I learned more from her class than from the entirety of my life experience. She is genuinely exited about the material and can explain it well. I would recommend taking her class to anyone who wants to learn.

Very kind professor who is passionate about her subject matter. All work can be resubmitted for a better grade after revision and she is very understanding of problems that may occur in a student's life and is always willing to work with them and compromise

There are 3 bigger writing assignments. The class is majority far left liberal ideas in it. You don't have to show up every day most of the in-class assignments you can still submit online. She does allow late work and corrections up until the last day which is helpful. There is a free online textbook.

Dr. Carter gives you all the leeway you could possibly ask for as a student; she accepts late work, lets you revise your work to improve your grades, and isn't strict on attendance. She's a fair grader, but she also gives you amazing feedback on your work.

Good class overall. Carter will do almost anything to help students grasp the topics they don't understand. Work can always be revised, and in-depth explanations about how you can improve are always provided. The lectures were also pretty interesting, especially the last unit.

Amazing! Lecture starts off slow, but gets very interesting! Super genuine, kind, professor. She's an absolute gem! Information is presented clearly and weekly readings are from interesting articles that are relevant to class. Grade based on 3 essays, occasional worksheets, and short online quizzes. Accepts late work! Very willing to help.

She's very fun and interesting to listen to. Very engaging, and puts genuine effort in her lectures. She makes resources available, and is very understanding of her students, and the world as a whole. At the end of our semester she got a standing ovation :)

Everyone needs to take a class of hers. Not only is she an incredibly engaging and passionate professor, but I've deeply retained the info of my entire semester with her because she connects the content to our present in a very informed and captivating way. I've regurgitated what I've learned in my lectures to my friends bc it's that interesting.

Super knowledgeable professor, and she gives really interesting lectures. She is very lenient about late work and allows you to resubmit, however she is a bit particular on how she wants things written. She also doesn't give exams either which is amazing. She's super caring and definitely knows her stuff!!

DEFINITELY TAKE HER CLASS!!! SHE IS SO KIND AND CARING. SHE ACCEPTS LATE WORK AND DOESN'T HAVE TESTS. I GOT SICK AND COULDN'T TURN IN A FEW ASSIGNMENTS FOR A WEEK AND SHE GAVE ME AN EXTENSION. I WOULD TAKE HER AGAIN. 1000000/10

Tara's class is the best class I have ever taken. I switched majors to anthropology after taking it, as she made everything so interesting. There is a good amount of reading, videos, and lectures, but they are genuinely so interesting that it's not too bad. 10/10 recommend taking her class.
We are only four weeks into the semester and she is already my favorite professor. Her lectures are super interesting and she loves what she teaches. She is delightful and cares about your success.
really caring prof, kinda nerdy but cool lectures, learned a lot of real word applicable knowledge

I want to love Professor Carter's class. But honestly, it was okay. Some of the homework prompts were extremely confusing and I tapped out during the final weeks. She truly cares about her students and their success. I just find ANTH very boring. Her lectures were interesting but very long. Listen at 1.5-1.75 speed. Trust me.

Very sweet and understanding professor. She really cares for her students, and is always willing to help them out. I highly recommend taking any of her classes for either your major or even just for fun. She also allows resubmissions if you got a low score on a discussion :)

Professor Carter teaches great info that should to be taught and learned. However, the assignments she assigns can be written so confusingly that I have no idea what the questions are even asking. There is no final in this class but there were six different home work assignments including an essay due on finals week and it was definately a lot.

I took cultural anthropology with Professor Carter and the class discussed super interesting topics that I think are very nessacary to learn in todays environment. Although the topics are interesting I think the lectures can get kind of boring and more in class activities would be fun.
Absolutely never makes any sense when grading assignments. Asks for "evidence" constantly, even if you include 40 quotes. Total joke of a class.

You can tell that this professor truly loves her job -- she speaks with passion and really makes you understand the context in a clear way. I greatly enjoyed watching her lectures as they were never monotone or boring -- she always found a way to engage her students. I highly recommend this class!

Dr. Carter is my absolute favorite professor at Sbcc. She doesn't have a final but she is a wonderful professor who really takes the time to make sure you understand the material. I really could go on an on about her, to be honest. The only thing is you need to make sure to do the essays with full effort, you will get a bad grade if you don't.

Mrs. Carter couldn't have been a better teacher. The class has 3 main units and there are lectures to go with each unit. You have to write three essays, three quizzes but lots of fun discussion assignments. Mrs. Carter really cares about the subject she teaches which really makes the class much more enjoyable. Take this class if you need the credit
avoid at any and all costs. any good review on here has to be coming from a teachers pet who is already good at the subject but no hate to those people just watch out for this prof

Before attending class I wouldn't look both ways before crossing the street because of how badly I wanted to be saved from this insufferable course. He is the problem, he is the reason a majority of his students drop the class or are failing by the end of the first month. I am not one to see the bad in people, but this guy made me miserable.

Seriously THE worst teacher I have ever had in all years of my education. I have never met someone so rude, ignorant, and proud of being a complete douche bag. He doesn't give a shit about his students and demoralizes you for asking him a question. I truly hope he reads this so he will be inspired to quit his job. I could say so much more about him

literally the rudest professer i've ever met. almost made me cry on multiple occasions. makes you feel stupid and praises his favorite students in front of you. he is a nice person but not a nice professer. reminds me of a strict asian parent (if ykyk). would never take his class ever again.

This guy is so mean. Genuinely the worst teacher i've ever had. He made me cry multiple times. Genuinely awful. Please don't waste your time please. His lectures are repetitive and if you ask questions he will not slow down or take the time to help. If you go ask him questions he will begrudgingly answer them for you.
rude man who does not treat people correctly. the workload was easy and subjects were easy if you paid attention but he needs an attitude adjustment and some more love and compassion to share wow.

Difficult, but fair. He's very good at explaining the course content, however, he can be really sassy, especially if you incorrectly answer one of his questions during the lecture. If you pay attention in class and do the practice problems, you should be fine.

Needs a reality check on how he wants to treat people if he's gonna treat his students without caring about questions. Only ever cares to answer about his trips and where he goes. Taking 15 min out of class just to talk about his trips and be happy and then go back to being insufferable
Just don't take this class. Makes you feel dumb for asking questions. Insufferable person to be around everything is black and white for him.

Rude, doesn't explain what you did wrong on a test when you ask, just annoyingly shrugs you off as if he doesn't have the time of day to help you. Talks about himself a lot and is only willing to answer a question if it is about himself or a trip he has gone on. Doesn't let you make up anything, even if you had to miss class for an emergency. HATE
Only nice ever talking about himself and his trips. Dude is self absorbed does not care about others.

He knew what he was talking about but didn't know how to express it to students. He didn't know how to speak with students in a constructive way. He comes off as rude, and while that could have been offset by good teaching, he didn't offer that either.
do not take his class. thumbs down.

Do not take this, professor, if you value your time, energy, or mental stability!!!! Plenty of office hours are available, but he's great at making you feel stupid if you don't understand a concept, as he is not great at answering questions! DO NOT TAKE WITH HIM IF MATH IS YOUR WEAK SUBJECT, AS HE IS NOT MUCH HELP!

Don't take this professor unless you enjoy being degraded and want to leave every class feeling worthless. Extremely tough grader, absolutely no lenience, and downright rude. Said numerous times that if we can't do things his way then we should “go enjoy our lives”… that says enough.
Very rude professor. Doesn't care about the students. Gloats about using taxpayer dollars to travel around the world.

just so unnecessarily rude to people for no reason, picks favorites and makes fun of others in the class. clearly knows what he's teaching but his lectures are very confusing and there's an overwhelming amount of work he assigns.

He was very condescending and not a great teacher. He makes jokes that aren't funny and at others' expense. I didn't like him. He knows his subject but the lectures are extremely boring and there is a lot of work required. Most of the class is just calculator language and you don't learn anything useful for your future except probability.

Strict. Has favorites. May be a good teacher for a very specific learning style. Discusses equations in depth. Places an understanding of the topic over how to do the actual math. Confusing and sometimes rude.

Do not take this professor if you value your time, energy, or mental stability. This professor is talented at making you feel stupid for every action/thing he deems wrong. Don't even try being nice, being cordial will not save you from his attitude. He will embarrass and/or berate you in front of the class for things as common as being distracted.

This guy is tough, I'm not the best at math, but he is not forgiving. Never missed one class, but he insisted I did and didn't give me extra credit because of that. Screwed up on one test at the beginning of the year, and spent the whole time playing catch-up. Regularly yelled at the class too. That being said, I did learn a lot just a lil rude
He isn't very understanding or helpful and his classes are extremely boring. However he does know his subject well

Yes professor Chang can be brutal with his deliveries but he is honest and there is nothing unexpected in his class which I respect. Show up on time, do your work, and ask questions you will succeed. His brutal upfronted-ness honestly pushed me to be better. Definitely intimidating on the first day but he is really a great professor.

Professor Chang has set up his curriculum to be extremely smooth and expected. There are no surprises in his class and he is extremely upfront. If you put the work and do the necessary homework which is extremely reasonable, you will succeed in his class. Great teaching style and organization, quizzes are often open-note or partnered at choice.

Professor Chang is not soft and does not soften any blows, he is upfront, honest, and good at what he does. Although he can be blunt, he is an incredible teacher and I wish he taught every section of math. If you cannot handle brut honesty or uncomfortable confrontation, he might not be the professor for you. He is very talented at his job.

Mr. Chang does not make me feel comfortable asking for help. He alway seems irritated or annoyed by something. My other teachers are much more approachable. Since his salary is close to $300k per year, you would think he would work harder at being nice.
I really enjoyed Mr.Changs class and thought he did a great job of explaining everything. As long as you did the homework assigned and listened to his lectures, I felt it was very easy to do well.
My 137 teacher taught only one class and had lots of time to help outside the classroom. Mr. Chang taught six classes and had no time, or desire, to help us. He always seemed irritated.

The material is straight forward if you pay attention but its his attitude that's the problem. Very strict and expresses his dislike to my class during lectures. He is frankly intimidating and would not like to ask questions in fear of him getting upset. Students shouldn't have to feel like this when seeking help.

Tough grader. Takes off points for useless things. Way too much homework even for a compact summer class. Spent 5+ hours daily and weekends. Slow grader. Although, '23 is first time offering a 5wk course, executed poorly. Lectures/videos are very bland. Very vague and difficult to contact. Gets mad if you email instead of joining office hours.

Yen clearly is passionate about math but not his students. He is a tough grader and be prepared to study a lot and go to tutors if you can. When you ask him questions, he usually circles around the topic without giving a clear answer. The class is test heavy (80%) and he doesn't do curves, extra credit, or any dropped tests.

It's not like I was expecting 130 to be easy, but Yen grades way too harshly for a class intended for people who probably won't use calculus again. He's a cool guy, nothing personal against him, I'm just not sure it's worth your grade.

Grades harshly for no real reason. Most people taking 130 probably aren't math or STEM majors. Tests worth 80% of the grade. Cool guy, class is set up super well, but take someone else if you're not the best test taker.

He is upbeat and encourages questions in class and has office hours. Grade based on 4 exams making up 80% of final grade, take-home review quizzes making up 15%, and homework problem reports making up 5%. Class moves very quickly in the summer and it's easy to get behind.

Yen explains your questions thoroughly and teaches very professionally, he is an all around great professor. If you understand the homework, you can easily get an A. His class proves that working hard will lead to success, I loved his class and hope to learn from him later on. He cares for his students and is very easy to reach outside of class.

Professor is kind, caring, and wants you to succeed. I did not do well in the class but that was no fault of his. He responds quickly to emails and is understanding. Will email you reminders on due dates and is happy to help. He truly cares and is also a funny guy.

In Professor Chou's hybrid MATH 117 course, he gives students practice materials that always cover all the questions you'll see in the exams. I was never good at math, but his math course is very accessible since he encourages using calculator, learn how to push buttons and you can walk away with an A without grasping any of the math concepts.

Professor Chou is one of the best profs I have had in my entire college experience. He is super accessible and kind and makes the material so understandable. His tests have basic questions, and no surprises. He helps his students to succeed. Do the homework and use all the resources he offers, and you will have a great time in this class!

If you aim for an easier A, avoid Yen. His tests are tough, making up 80% of the grade. He makes the class really tough to uphold SBCC's engineering program's integrity (like what?). Switched professors next semester and it was so much easier. He makes Calc 150 hard for no reason. Really cool dude but its not worth your grade.

Yen is great. I had him online for M150-160 and his online lectures are structured very well and aren't a daunting task because he makes them interesting. I loved calculus and having yen helped me understand the subject immensely. I am now a tutor for those classes because of how much i liked them.

I respect him for being passionate in Calc and for having drip in the classroom. However I felt like I had to work so much harder in this class than any other class, not because of the material but because of the amount of pop quizzes and excessive homework. Its nice that he gives you a week to complete it however.

Best professor I've, he really cares about his students wether you are in-person or online. Very understanding and wants to help out as much as he can. For online classes there is a lot of homework that is time consuming, but he does drop some of the lowest scored assignments. Don't lag in his classes and you will pass. 10/10

One of my favorite professors of all time. Incredibly caring, understanding, willing to take hours out of his day to explain concepts and walk you through problems you don't fully grasp. Legend in all regards.

One of the best professors I've ever had in my life... Extremely understanding, willing to explain concepts to you with parallels and similes to make them more palatable, willing to accommodate you for any shortcomings you might have, happy to discuss mathematics even if they lie outside of the topics covered by the class. 10/10.
Super easy tests. Better to take in person. I would not pick another professor. He is so caring and explains very well.

The quality of his video lectures makes it difficult to understand what he is saying. If you keep up with the notes, the material is easier to learn. He has all the assignments, quizzes, and lectures organized very well on canvas. But overall, I would not recommend taking his course online.

(M130) Yen's great at simplifying and explaining abstract concepts! He never puts people down for asking for further explanation, and is generally a nice dude. Participation is key and Yen loves to put problems from the practice quizzes/classwork on the exams, so make sure to attend each lecture & take good notes; you'll be fine!

EASY TESTS. In lecture, he really emphasizes the intuition of what he is teaching making sure that if you don't remember a formula you at least understand where it comes from so you can derive it. His homework is very hard, but you will get an A if you do it. Also, the man is drippin with style point out his basketball shoes he will blush.

Math 137 is a tough course, as it is worth 5 credits. There's quite a bit of homework as well as 3 midterms, but I believe Yen set up the course to help you succeed. Each midterm had a review quiz before it, which really helped. His lectures were pretty straightforward as well. If you dedicate time to the course, you'll do well.

I had to teach myself EVERYTHING. Chou didn't teach us at all. He just posted youtube videos of different professors teaching a class the material we were learning. It was very hard to learn from those. I submitted the first assignment a week early and he never told me I did it wrong. Just graded it, didn't explain and didn't let me redo it.

The way that this class is set up is amazing. Yes there can be a lot of hw but its worth it. He gives short quizzes and long review quizzes. When it comes to tests, he gives you 10 questions and you get to pick 5 questions that you want to do and submit. Test and Quiz questions are straight from hw. He is a fast grader. Yen makes it easy to succeed

This was my worst experience in college so far. His videos are extremely low quality. I couldn't understand 50% of what he was saying, like the actual words, let alone the material. I had to teach myself everything. His grading was arbitrary. He would pick two or three random problems from the exams and grade everything based on them. Avoid Yen.

I took Professor Chou for 150 over the summer and his class was a LOT. Maybe because it was over the summer, but the homework took 4 hours every day. I recommend taking some introductory class before or not taking this class over the summer.

Tests are tough but you can only do well if you do the hw. Hw went from mandatory to optional, but your test grade will replace your hw grade so you have to do well. There are WAY too many lectures. It's overwhelming. Amount of hw is crazy. It's like he doesn't care about what his students do outside his class. He can get rude in office hours too.

PROFESSOR YEN IS AMAZING! I strongly recommend taking your maths with him. Yes, there was a lot of homework, but I was taking it in six weeks, so that was expected. He really wants the students to understand and is very passionate and enthusiastic when he is teaching. I was super nervous to take this class (MATH 150), but now Im glad I did!

If you know you struggle with math take the IN PERSON class or make sure a tutor is available to you. I cannot stress that enough. He may be a great teacher, but beware that the online courses have you watch old Algebra videos (I'm talking 90's or early 2000's) and he does not actually teach you at all, but rather supervises the in person exams.

This professors online class is garbage. Had us watch 2 hours of khan academy videos on our own and expect students to dedicate 8-10 HOURS PER DAY during the summer session. No way I can actually understand math through a Khan Academy video. Dropped the class because I know there is no way I can learn through this class.

Yen is simply the best. Yen's lectures and notes are crystal clear. Exams are reasonably challenging and it's doable if you truly earn the materials. If he teaches MATH200, I will be the first one signing up.

The class is based on homework, quizzes with notes, and closed-note, four hour (maximum) exams where we get to drop 2/5 problems. I got an A and worked from 9am-6pm. There are lecture videos we watch to learn the material. Professor Chou is super responsive with emails (even at 11 at night) and he has two hour Zoom sessions each day for questions.

Yen is a wonderful teacher. I really like that he's extremely accessible and always willing to work with his students, going way beyond his office hours! I could say so many positive things. My personal cons stem from his difficulty. He's very lecture heavy and is a tough grader on tests. Very important to do the homework early and ask questions!
always let you resubmit the assignments with correction and gives useful comments.
This class is harder than you thought.
Toughest grader I have ever seen in my life.

For a 1 credit class you think this class would be pretty easy and laid back. However, this class had more work than some of my 3 credit courses. Its an easy class but she is a tough grader and give a lot of homework.
She is a very critical grader and will Doc you for any little mistake. Most of her feed back was unreasonable. This was my least favorite class of all time.

Course lectures were long and boring; all were in google document form with many grammatical errors, making it more confusing than need be.Material could have been presented more concise and efficiently in another format.Instructor was over the top for an easy subject and gave little help for poor delivery of her material.
she is very passionate
to much speak spanish, but teach english
No Comments

Worst professor I've had. He never responded to my emails, which made the class especially challenging since it was online. Criteria for assignments is super confusing, often puts important things in the modules without adding due dates, so it's really easy to miss stuff. Overall, I felt really unsupported and frustrated throughout the course.

The other ratings commenting on his heritage are honestly super weird and you guys need to stop -- so disrespectful and inappropriate. There's one major project due at the end of the semester, based on reading four biographies on Asian-American experiences. Stay on schedule! Short essay response assignments on four films. Good class overall!

Would Highly recommend this class. He's a fair grader and you will learn a lot. There is a large amount of reading but it is all very interesting. He is very funny and even online I laughed at a lot of the videos in his lectures. Work on your final project all semester.

Great Professor. Very knowledgeable about the subject and you will learn a lot from his class. Assignments are rare and he is a fair grader. Make sure you work on your final project throughout the whole semester. A lot of reading but the books/articles are very interesting. He is a funny guy and this is conveyed even on an online class.

Professor Cook is knowledgeable but his classes are not engaging. He lectures but leaves no time in class for conversation, it's run like an online class but you go in person. He is rude over email an assumes the worst of his students. If this class is required for you to take I would recommend fining a different teacher.
bad experience overall

This class is a bit hit-or-miss. While the content is interesting, the professor can be unclear on expectations and grading. The workload is heavy, but using Unstuckstudy for study prep really helped me stay on top of it.
Class information not laid out very well, extra unnecessary steps to access curriculum. Material itself is very interesting
Easiest class I have ever taken. North of 20% extra credit.

His online Asian-American Studies class was pretty good. Homework assignments were infrequent, but required a good amount of writing, and he seemed to just hand out A's on everything I turned in. The final project is utterly massive and worth 50% of your grade, so make sure you work on it throughout the semester. Prof. Cook is a chill guy.

I am not aware of how Craig Cook teaches in person, but because of the experience from when I have taken one course online with his instruction, I do not intend to. This is because of his unclear manner of scheduling which is harmful to students' ability to derive meaning from his instruction. (This instruction consists of reading a textbook)
Seems to be a lot of busy work, but not that hard. Just do the assignments and you'll be fine. Pretty minimal, seems like a chill guy

I have taken other history classes and Professor Cook has many assignments that are also very time consuming if you are taking an online be prepared to submit a 5-7 paper on slave narrative, also watching over an 1 hour long video and responding to non clear questions as well as discussions also doesn't ACCEPT LATE WORK at all! DONT TAKE THIS CLASS

A stick no late work policy for an all online class seems pretty extreme to me. Hes really a professor that doesn't give you a break. If you plan on taking this class. MAKE SURE YOU TURN IN EVERY ASSIMENT ON TIME. Also make sure to fully understand what is due when. Some assignments have funky due dates, like the dissuasions.

No real care for students or the work. Every piece of feedback copy and pasted for all students. FIRM NO LATE WORK POLICY, doesn't matter what happened he wont accept it basically. Besides that, super easy class if you just read the chapters and take the quizzes. Beef up your writing assignments w/ more words and you'll be all good.

Immigration is such an important topic area and I was super excited to take this course. Unfortunately, I chose to drop it because the teacher was not clear on course expectations and there are three chapters of reading and three quizzes a week that don't count for anything, which is odd. I was afraid his unclear grading system would impact my gpa

Immigration is such an important topic in America today that I was really excited to take this course. The teacher is absent or aggressive and arrogant. His instruction was not helpful. I dropped the class for fear it would bring down my perfect GPA. Too bad because the topic is interesting, but the teacher is not.

I urge you not to take his class. No late work policy, large amount of reading, 700+ for largest assignment (25% of grade) The class only cover slavery. Craig Cook seems to be one of those Professors that believe his class is the most important class to every student he has, this was simply not the case for myself and many other.

I would not recommend taking the BLST-101 course from Mr. Cook under any circumstance, especially if you intend to transfer and require a strong GPA. His online course is organized in such a way that the class is both convoluted and unforgiving, and this combination along with his uninvolved use of his policies ruins this class .

Genuinely the worst most horrible class of my life:(. The assignments would require a 2hour documentary viewing, then a 3-4 page response (10 paragraphs) several times a week. Emails and feedback was all auto response and if you emailed him for help or concern he would be super rude. Also instructions made no sense. Please DO NOT take this class.

He assigns video analyses that are like 45 minutes each and expects you to give a scholarly run down of it even though they are worth no points. He isn't understanding. He's careless towards students and honestly very slow when it comes to using tech - he always misspells stuff and words prompts weirdly so you interpret them wrong.

assignments were 3-10 pts for so much work. assigned 1hr videos, then had to write a 6-8 paragraph response to the videos for only 3 points and same with discussion/reading posts. low amount of points for assignments would lower your grade if you got anything below a 100% and not change your grade if you 100%. hard to get get good grade in class
Absolutely the worst teacher I've had at sbcc. Very unhelpful when responding to emails. Copies and pastes everything, and nothing is thoroughly explained. Highly recommend not taking this class.

Didn't even feel like he was the professor of the class, had automated announcements, one discussion post a week with two replies, doesn't seem to care at all about his students, one midterm and a final that he grades super harshly and expects way too much work from a two hour limit. Only reason he gets a 2 is because it's an easy class, but STRESS

Asian American History 101 gave more reading than any class I have ever taken. There were 5 books, around 200-300 pages in length. The responses for grades are copy/pasted and there is no personal feedback to improve your scores. The material is not difficult, but is massive. There are few assignments for the course so you need to ace everything.

This review is for ASAM101 (not on list). Professor Cook presents great material that everyone should read. There's only a lot of reading if you're not used to reading regularly, but I found it to be more than manageable. All of his feedback is copy/pasted, and grading criteria are extremely unclear. He doesn't seem to engage with students.

Made a seemingly-fun class incredibly stressful, is so hands off from the class it doesnt even feel like hes the professor as he has automated announcements and doesnt seem to read submissions, doesnt take kindly to emails about questions for assignments or general class questions, COVID seems to have made him care less which is difficult

First off, he begins with an article about "not asking professors questions" -- I'm sorry, but if you do not understand-- ask!! He references his syllabus a lot, but it's a not well written and very confusing. When you ask a question he is unclear, and rudely sarcastic. He's not a professor who teaches for his students, not sure why he became a pro

Probably my least favorite professor- his online class was like 200 pages a week from the textbook and supplemental readings and a powerpoint lecture that didn't make sense and had videos that didn't work. Whenever you emailed him a question his responses would be super rude and his grading made no sense. Avoid if you can

Honestly after reading the reviews I thought I was going to regret taking this class, but it's not as it's depicted in the negative reviews. As long as you participate in the discussion board, cite others work (super important), fulfill word counts, and read the text book you should be fine. The 1st exam I took was multiple choice/ essay prompt.
A teacher with a great sense of humor who knows how to relate to his students, but doesn't let that get in the way of his teaching.
He has plenty of relevant experience and is open to new ideas or solutions to problems. Pay attention and you should be fine.

Ben is an awesome teacher. You really learn a lot!! Its great because he has tons of experience and defiantly knows what he's talking about. Great sense of humor, is willing to talklisten to you and really works with you to help you succeed. He's young so he can relate to you and he is super fun. Each class had something new to learn!!!

Great teacher, he's young so he can relate to you. Quizzes are super easy and homework isn't too bad. It gets confusing at times but Ben really helps out a lot. He also has a great sense of humor so that makes the class pretty fun.

I took Professor Erin Cross's online AJ101 class and it was wonderful! She always left comments and made an effort to engage with each of us even though we were fully online for this class. There were weekly quizzes and discussion posts, and clear requirements and expectations. Excited to see that she has other AJ classes so that I can take those!

I loved the course and would choose it again. It was well-structured, with many guest speakers who made everything more interesting. The field trips, like the ride-along with the sheriff's department, jail tour and court proceedings, were amazing. The professor was passionate about teaching, which made the class even more engaging and enjoyable.

Very interesting and interactive class. You get many guest speakers and cool assignments such as a jail visit and cop ride along. You can tell she cares about her students learning. She is entertaining to listen to as well. The workload is very easy and she's a very light grader meaning as long as you do the assignments you'll get an A.

Took Erin's 101 summer course and very much enjoyed it. Assignments were very hands on and simulate situations that happen in the actual criminal justice world, such as playing the role of district attorney and determining which case to prosecute and why. Workload is very manageable, even for a summer class. She gives amazing feedback as well.

Loved taking 104 with her. She knows so much in her field and is constantly sharing current news articles that coincide with the current lectures. Overall a fair grader, gives clear feedback and improvement suggestions. Loved this class and highly recommend taking it.

Prof. Cross was great! I liked receiving her feedback on the assignments every week. Her tests and quizzes are nowhere to be found online so make sure you actually study or have notes and the textbook. I did not buy the textbook and was perfectly find without it al semester. Highly recommend her class if you need an elective!

Professor Erin Cross's online class was great! She is a great professor and super caring and supportive of her online students. Her class is very well-structured with tons of information to cover. You have weekly quizzes on the chapter slides and some assignments/discussions. Take this course! It is great!!!!
Easily one of my favorite professors, she puts effort into making the lectures not so dreadful. She really cares about what shes teaching and it makes the class really easy to understand.

I took this class as an online class and it was really well structured! Professor Cross makes an effort to read all of my homework answers and responds in the comments to each one. Her lectures are well organized and she includes slide shows that are great to study from. She also includes articles that are interesting to read but still give lots of

Very organized and clear grading criteria which makes it easy to know where you stand in the class. I learned a lot that I would not have known otherwise, and Professor Cross was always easy to reach. She is really funny and truly cares for her students, while also having a passion for her field!

Very organized and you know what you are suppose to do for each assignment. I would 100% take this class again. Not too much work to do and can easily be taken if you had some other serious classes without stressing you out. The professor is also very kind!

I took Erins AJ101 class online in the summer and I got an A, but I found the class boring. It basically consisted of reading the textbook and long assignments after. However she does give great feedback! Do the work, exceed the minimum, and you should pass easily.

I can only assume shes nice in person but her online class SUCKS. Lecture PowerPoints are completely useless, zero information there, yet she asks very specific questions on the homework which I had to google.. and still got points off because she wanted more detailed papers but she wont give us more detailed PowerPoints? you wont learn a thing!

The reading schedule was little off, just had to read a chapter or two in advance, but super straightforward. Read the textbook, answer the questions, get an A. We had 2 papers, both 3-5 pages, super easy. One midterm, one final, both worth 50 points.

I took the online class with Erin and I really liked how organised the class was. It's clear what you have to do and when you have to do it. It's a little bit hard to know what is expected from the homework and tests but if you just put time into the assignments you'll get a good grade. She always gives good feedback as well.

Absolutely one of the best teachers at SBCC. Took her to fulfill a GE and has been the most fun class Ive taken. Shes a VERY easy grader and brings in real judges, attorneys, and law enforcement into the class for Q and A. Ive learned so much in this class. If youre thinking about taking her, do it!!!

Online course was very organized, and even though it was a late-start course it did not feel rushed at all. I would skim the readings and go through her power point lectures, write a 500-1000 word response to the weekly questions, and got an A! She emails you what to study for the midterm and final, and there aren't any surprises. Loved this class

I took this class just because it sounded interesting. Don't have any regrets! Ms. Cross is hilarious, she keeps the class extremely entertaining, and it's easy to tell she's passionate about what she's teaching. Easy A, go to the majority of classes and just make sure to study for the midterm and final!

Took AJ 101 online this past summer session 2. Erin is a tough grader but it's all in good intention. She wants her students to learn and understand what they are learning. Very organized and quick to grade and gives amazing feed back! Do your readings and study her slides, you'll pass her class with no problem!

Took her online class, Very well organized, quick to grade and comment. Never once met her but even behind a screen it is clear that she desires for the student to learn and understand the subject, and is also just helpful.

Her Introduction to corrections class is awful. I tried so hard on all assignments and would never get anything above average. She was unhelpful as well. Maybe she would be better in an on-campus situation. Seriously, beware. It was a treacherous semester!
Amazing professor!
Amazing professor! Even if you're not majoring in criminal justice, take her class! It's fun & you learn a lot! She is the best!

Erin Cross is a very funny person and a great teacher. We did a lot of group work which was very nice. She doesn't give out tests expect Midterm and Final. Final was 10 questions. You don't need to be there everyday but you won't really want to miss. She was a very funny and sweet teacher and I really enjoyed her class.
Great class very interesting you learn a lot. All homework assignments are interesting. Straight forward questions and you simply respond.

Erin is one of the best professors I've ever had at SBCC. She has a dry (but hilarious) sense of humor, but knows how to engage people in a lecture. There are 2 papers, a midterm, and final. Definitely take her corrections class, it's super interesting and she's a genuinely caring and great person to know.
Easiest class ever?

Awesome teacher. She's SUPER funny:) She makes class fun. I look forward going to class,her class was my favorite. I never missed it. She's very clear. She's very helpful. Her class is pretty easy to pass if you do the work and participate in class. Take her class, you wont regret it:)

She was great and funny!!!! She always keeps you entertained. I loved this class.The only thing is she does the notes really fast but its straight out of the book. I would recommend highlighting the book and doing the notes later.

Prof Cross is awesome! I took her Prison Society class when it was offered. She is knowledgeable, approachable and fair. Show up to class & do the work. Easy A. Truly an asset to SBCC and the Probation Dept. Take her class!
Very kind woman, reach out to her and form a connection. Super understanding and easy to talk to. Easy class, but you still have to try and make sure you stay on top of things.

I had the pleasure of taking Anita Cruse for English 103. She responds to emails fast and provides exceptional feedback. While she assigns a substantial amount of reading, the books were genuinely interesting. Professor Cruse allows late work but deducts 5 points for each day of delay. Doing the work genuinely and on time will give you 100%.

Great teacher and class. I was literally "trying too hard," which impacted the grading on my assignments. Dr. Cruse let me correct them and still gave full/updated credit. Great professor who loves teaching. Show up to zoom meetings and make an effort when it is your turn to talk.
Ms Cruse is amazing and super sweet. I took her for ed101 and the workload is not bad! Gives great feedback. Do your best and youll pass.

I really liked taking this course online. The instructions were pretty clear, and most of the assignments were about analyzing videos, chapters from the book, or articles. Ms Cruse loves being a teacher and she is always there if you need support or if you have questions, and she appreciates your participation.

She has been doing her thing for a bit and from what I can tell she is quite good. Little to no heavy work at all, she is very sweet. She let us know, if we ever need anything to get in contact with her. Idk how many professors do that so anyways awesome

She is a great teacher, tries to help as many possible ways, but if you are not ready to take advice from her, then obviously you will fail. Attendance is essential to get a good grade, and also do all your homework. If you like to have an A+, you need good essay grade.

She contradicts herself far to much, doesnt explain herself clearly. She should note her other peoples opinions before she gives her negative feedback. If she likes you, you pass. If she doesnt like you, youre going to fail. Takes everything too personal. The Negative reviews below are not wrong at all.

Anita Cruse was all around a very great teacher. She was very caring and made sure everyone reached the grade they wanted to receive. There was 4-5 papers in the semester but every paper was worked on in class for 70% of the time and she would go over our corrections in class as well if you messed up. Also do not miss class and you will be solid.
Just do all your homework! She is so sweet and caring, you will enjoy it. Show your respect a lot and do not come late

cares about her students success and loves her job! Do not miss class because you will get far behind. She gives great feedback on essays and is a very friendly person. One of the best english teachers i have ever had. Be prepared to read a lot and write essays on the articles read. Highly recommend her class.
Easily one of the best teachers. Truly crew for her students and their success in her class. I recommend her 100%. I enjoyed being in her class and learning from her .

Anita is a wonderful professor. She's very understanding and willing to help. To the person who had just recently rated her, maybe you're saying this because you didn't pass. Work hard & you'll be fine! The material is amazing and it helps in the long run!! Highly suggest her!

Your grade depended on her opinion of you. Was very rude to some students (me) and others could do no wrong. She gave contradictory feedback on my essays, so I didn't know how to fix them. Very bad at clarifying. Didn't enjoy her class at all
Highly recommend Anita to any student interested in English. She is very helpful. Homework is every night and can get overwhelming as well as essays but it is a great class.

She was a great teacher! You do a lot of annotating in books. She was part of the learning community so she made sure we got to know each other. We had about 4 to 5 essays. As long as you go to class and do most of the assignments you should pass.
This class is very time consuming for an online class. The teacher is not very specific with what she wants you to do and she is a harsh grader. Overall I would not recommend this class.
Dr. Cruse is an amazing woman! She is very helpful and loves her students. She responds quickly to e-mails, not a very challenging course, and dont forget to ANNOTATE!
Bad Teacher!

Dr Cruse is honestly one of the best teachers I've ever had. She is kind, smart, and caring towards her students. Do the assignments and show up to every class and she will most likely love you and you'll get an A. Take her if you have the opportunity!

She is not helpful at all! she doesnt teach anything and i think thats why people say they like her bc she doesnt challenege her students! im going into eng 110 not knowing anything...she can also be extremely rude to students and she pushes her personal believes way too much(buddist)...she should be teaching 5th grade english not college level!
Good teacher

I took Anita for learning community English 100 and 102. This was my first semester in the fall and I LOVED HER! My favorite teacher at SBCC. She is a great teacher, she pushes you but isn't that why we are in college? I would recommend her to anyone. Homework is pretty much everyday. She loves annotating, essays are hard but she will always help!
She is awesome.

Best professor I have ever had. Very passionate and loves her students as if they were her own. Makes her students want to come to class. Variety of teaching styles. I wish she taught all the rest of my classes. LOVE HER! I suggest everyone takes her!. Makes you think outside of the box.
She is extremely nice! There were a few kids that really mad her angry but overall she was happy and nice. Very easy and easy to talk to. I highly recommend her!
SHE IS AMAZING!!!!Best english teacher ever!!! Do your work, communicate with her and go to class and your golden! She truely cares about her students..TAKE HER!! you will learn a lot
She is such an amazing teacher, i enjoyed every minute of her class. I highly recommend it :)

An easy class and she's a sweet teacher. Extremely easy A if you attend class and read and annotate your books. Only bad thing is if you miss class you get marked down A BUNCH of points which sucks. i got A's on everything and had 3 excused absences and that got me a B because of "in class" work missed. its like a high school class posters, videos

Anita Cruse is a very nice teacher. DO NOT GET ON HER BAD SIDE..I was on her good side so I never had problems! She helps students out a LOT and if you just need someone to talk to, go to her. She is very patient and knows what she is doing. You read 2 books for the entire semester. DON'T FORGET TO ANNOTATE YOUR BOOKS!!!!

The class navigation is confusing since it's on the Professor's website. Does not grade or post work through Canv@s, which makes it difficult to navigate through the assignments and check grades. Expect to calculate your own grade since he will not do it for you. Expect to waste a lot of money on labs & plant variation where you live is a must.

not a very good teacher for botany the labs are difficutlt your supposed to go and waste money on labs i wasted like 60 bucks on his labs for him to tell the i did them wrong on some of the labs are just too much some students dont have access to specific plants around their era my mans wanted a specific plant and i didn't even grow where i live.

Among the best organized online classes offered through SBCC. Stay ahead of the labs, and get a cheap microscope for your phone. I went in to the class without taking biology 101 and had to hit khan academy hard the first month but was able to successfully complete the class. Great class with clear instructions and fun assignments
Mr. Cummings was absolutely the best teacher I have ever had! - and I have four college degrees.

Online class. 10 quizzes, 10 labs, 2 essays, midterm, and final. Final and midterm are essay questions with a min word count of 1500 total. You have to buy things for the labs (seeds, fruits, seeds, etc.) Overall, it was in interesting class. However, taking it all online for a 6 week summer session was difficult. Amazing professor, always helpful

Great guy, great attitude, great love for the subject. He's passionate, and helpful. However, if science isn't your thing, probably not going to be the best experience. Take it for knowledge not for units. If you're looking for that last science or extra units, take like biology 100.

Took Plant Diversity Online with Dr. Bob. While I got an A, it wasn't great. Many assignment prompts were vague, grading was slow, and there were mistakes in grading and on the course website. Zero direction or interaction from the professor and grading was inconsistent and seemed subjective. Correct answers/key was not provided for weekly quizzes.

I had Dr Cummings for biology over 4 years ago. I am now currently graduating from UC and have never had a professor come close to caring about his student's success like Bob does. Truly a great professor and a great community college! Because of Bob, I will remember the integumented mega sporangia for the rest of my life

Grades on a curve, super understanding if everyone does poorly. Lab quizzes are 30% of your grade and many of the answers are on your handout, so KEEP IT! The beginning is molecular and hard, but after the first midterm the class is fun and very informative. I'm very glad I took this class and you will be, too.

Great guy! I had a hard time following some of his lectures, even having had a pretty demanding high school bio class but making use of his office hours and the tutor helped me get an A in this class. I would highly recommend taking him if you can since Spring 2012 is his last semester teaching at sbcc! Tough course but well worth the work.

I took a class with Bob 13 years ago...I have since graduated with a BS in Marine Biology and I am working toward a MS in Science Ed...he remains to this day one of the best instructors I have ever had. He is intelligent, funny, charismatic, personable, and a lovely human being. Get him while you still can, he IS the BEST...hands down.
Great teacher. The kind you wish they all were like.
Bob took a class that would otherwise seem a bit dull and made it interesting and exciting. He is hilarious and very down to earth. Everyone should take a class with Bob before leaving cc.

By far my favorite teacher. Sets the tone for bio majors. He really is a funny guy with interesting material. It's still a hard class with LOTS of vocab, and you defiantly need to come to all lectures and especially labs.
Bob is the man! He is so into what he teachers which makes it easy to want to do well. I wouldn't recommend buying the book though. I received an A in the class and I didn't open the book once.
Bob is the single best instructor I've ever had. He teaches his subject well and connects with living now. Live long and prosper Bob! jd
One of the best teachers I have ever had. He was really nice and understanding. I really learned a lot in his class. On regret I have is not being able to take this class in person.

Bob remains one of the greatest teachers I have ever had and I took his class 11 years ago! He is so dedicated to his job and his students. Students line up at his door and he always has time for everyone. People who probably never really contemplated mycology would bring him in the latest shroom they found. He truly inspires...I heart Bob!

Botany 121 is online and a complete introduction to plants, algae and fungus. Exams are tough but open notes since online, grading easy but I learned a LOT. Beautiful photographs and an easy to understand website helped.

My favorite teacher of all time. I really do miss his class. It was so fun attending. He honestly inspiring. TAKE HIS CLASS NOW! just be sure to take good notes and ATTEND TUTORING SESSIONS and you will be more than fine

Bob is the man. He loves bio so much it is incredible. His love for bio transfers to all students some how. He is hilarious. Every lecture/lab is a performance. Labs were the best. He is so helpful in all ways. It is important that you attend every class. the tests is all on the notes. lab practicals are difficult though. go to tutoring for A

One of the best teachers i have ever had. I was not at all interested in plant biology but had to take it for my major. He is extremely clear-go to class and copy down EVERYTHING he writes on the board. He basically tells you what is going to be on each midterm, DO NOT MISS CLASS THE DAY BEFORE THE MIDTERM OR FINAL!
Bob is a great guy. Always has time. Really enthusiastic and interesting.

Bob is the greatest guy ever!!! An amazing teacher, he loves what he does and makes you love it too. The information is a bit hard but the way he teaches it makes it impossible not to have fun. Especially in the labs, we made sushi! Take it no matter what your major, its a life changer class for sure.

amazing really knows what hes talking about, captivating speaker,will do everything he can to help you, will also help if you need more time to turn in assignments or don't understand something, vocab is a big thing. take this class it will change your outlook on life!
