Really nice guy. Attendence imperative if you plan on understaning the material. He has a PHD in MUSHROOMS! hes still a kid in adult form. its apparent he really loves botany!

Great professor! took online botany class, was easy. A lot of information. His midterm, final, and projects seemed hard, but he's actually an easy grader. quizzes were open notes. Really gets you involved in the class. Highly recommended!
First of all he's a TOTAL honey! Dr. Cummings also happens to be a mighty fine instructor - Do yourself a favor and take his class, you won't regret it!
First of all he's a TOTAL honey! Dr. Cummings also happens to be a mighty fine instructor - Do yourself a favor and take his class, you won't regret it!
good

Luke is super chill. He gives you the assignments, guidelines, and does an in-class tutorial for how to make whatever the project is, and then pretty much sets you free. Especially in the second half of the semester, you can basically do whatever you want as long as you meet the guidelines. No judgment and encourages creativity.

If you love music, take this course. Ms. Dechaine was 1)a fun, kind, and sweet teacher, and 2)had a very easy/mellow course. Grades are based on short discussions and quizzes, thats it. The quizzes are easy and answers are clearly in the book and on quizlet. Discusions were so fun- about beatles songs and how Bob Dylan's music evolved. Best class.
Would recommend.

Rosabeth is very professional, helpful and caring. You are being taught by someone who wants you to succeed. Her class is fun, relaxing, physical, motivational and informative. You will get a work out as well as learn to decompress. If you are having any difficulties, she is more than happy to help. I highly recommend taking this class.

I took the online class and I have never experienced a PE class this confusing, all the modules were unorganized, nothing made any sense and there were too many unnecessary workout videos. Also, you had to take a written Exams and I don't think that is needed. This class took more of my time than my Chemistry class did.

An amazing professor. A kind and patient person. An easy A if you show up. Fitness Evaluation of your sit-ups, push ups, etc. in the beginning/end of the semester. Only assignment is a group presentation of 4-5 stretches to present to the class, no written papers at all. Take it if you need to relax and need an A. She also gives extra credit.

She's a really lovely person and probably a great yoga teacher but this is not the class for you if you have been practicing yoga for any extended period of time before taking this class. As someone who regularly practices outside of the school I was a little bored. But she knows what she's doing and is super nice and caring.

She was injured so she couldn't do yoga with us, she changed up the style of yoga each class so that was nice and we didn't get bored. There is a group final and you have to instruct yoga for the class, a stupid assignment but my only complaint with the class.

She is so amazing and her stretching and relaxation class was great. One group project at the end of the semester but nothing big. Just show up to class but if you're not gonna be there then email her.

Rosabeth is great and this class is super relaxing. She lets you go at your own pace whether you're sick or even just tired. Only thing is participation DEFINITELY matters- you get 30 points off every class you miss, no matter the reason. Easy A.

Rosabeth is such a sweetheart and I really recommend her stretching and relaxation class to anyone who has a great amount of stress in their life. The class is an easy A as long as you show up. There are two group presentations, but nothing too drastic and she even takes you to have class on the beach and on the campus's gardens! Would take again!

I've had this professor last semester for "Stretching and Relaxation" & now I enrolled in her class "Yoga 1" for the second semester. I really do enjoy this prof, she's a little weird but that's just who she is. She is a very professional yoga teacher, 84% of your grade is just showing up everyday, which is a plus in my book. She doesn't do + & -

Showing up to class is a big part of your grade. The tests are just group practicals where you lead the class through a sun salutation, and don't need much practice since you only have to memorize 3-5 lines. I improved a lot more with my flexibility than I thought I would! Good easy class that helps you stay in shape.

Rosabeth is a good yoga instructor, but if you are already a yogy the class might be slow as it depends on the overall level. There are only a pre-post evaluation and a presentation for midterm. Otherwise Rosabeth doesn't know how to use moodle and a computer in general, so make sure you are on time to turn in the assignments.

Attendance is 84% of grade. She's helpful especially if you try to make an effort to talk to her. She seems to be old fashioned and get easily frustrated with the average student. If you're respectful, the class is an easy A only 2 outside assignments: a pre and post fitness evaluation.

Kinda cocky in that she likes to brag about the people she's worked with, but hey, she has the qualifications and experience to back it up. All in all, awesome yoga teacher. She made me look forward to attending class. Try hard and show up and you will see improvement. I plan on taking the intermediate class with her in the fall. Loved the class.
This was my first time taking a yoga class. She explains and demonstrates all the postures. There is a midterm and final, but they're really no big deal. It's an easy class and a lot of fun.
Don't listen to the bad ratings. If you go to class and write a journal, you'll pass. She's the best Yoga teacher ever. If you want to try Yoga, take it from her and you won't be disappointed.

I have taken Rosabeth's yoga (indoors & outdoors) and stretching & relaxation classes. I agree with the comments that if you go in with a good attitude and willingness to learn, it can change your life! Rosabeth is a knowledgeable and caring instructor who gives 100%. Apply yourself and she will help you reach new levels of awareness & fitness!

This class was definitely one of my favorite classes I have taken at City College. Before this class I had little knowledge on yoga. I am now able to do yoga on my own. She encompasses different types of yoga into her class to give a variety of yoga practices. If you go to class and try to learn with a good attitude this class can change your life!

I took this class two times. I joined her class to improve my flexibility for baseball. Rosabeth not only helped me improve my flexibility through her class she made me want to join her class again. Rosabeth even with over 140 kids will give you the attention you need to achieve the goal you are looking for.

This is a class for people who really look for solutions for daily stress in their life.If you are a dedicated student that really cares about becoming more productive she will be more than willing to extend her hand. She's strict, but that's just because there is much to learn,it can be bad if done incorrectly. She cares for her students. Namaste.

Rosabeth is a FANTASTIC yoga instructor. I have taken her class multiple times and learn something new each semester. She is extremely caring, encouraging, and helpful. I am sure I had class with the people who made the recent negative comments and I can assure you they are only ticked b/c they were called out on EXTREMELY disrespectful behavior.

What a waste of a perfectly good morning. This class was supposed to be stretching and relaxing, and it was anything but. I wound up doing more paper work and assignments for this 1 unit PE class then for my 3 unit History class. She is never on time, and then you have to listen to her story about why she is late. I recommend sleeping in!!!

She is an awful yoga teacher. She teaches as if you are weight training and not in a yoga class! Yoga can be RELAXING and A WORKOUT at the same time, but she fails terribly at doing so! Not a zen attitude at all! Very abrupt aswell! I loved yoga and hated this class!
very good teacher. got very interested in yoga. doesn't stick to the same old routine. you get a lot of new experiences in the class. very good definitely recommend it to anyone interested in yoga.

rosabeth is a great yoga instructor. she's good for both introductory and experienced yoga. she's chill and as long as you go to class and do the little that she'll ask of you, you'll be fine. for the final, know what muscles are used in differnent poses. good luck

She's a really easy teacher. she excuses your absences if you're sick as long as you bring a doctor's note. easy A. she's has a lot of fun during class. can be spontaneous at times. likes to try new things a lot.

Super nice professor and down to earth! Always kind. All work is paper, attendance to lectures is a must because she'll give you in lecture work. Late work can only be physically submitted at her office, but after 1 week, a 10% deduction to your grade is given every day (or week, idk). Overall, an easy but interesting class if you do the work.

Took this Anthro 104 (language and culture) as an online summer course, very interesting class and fun teacher! She gives extra credit on almost every assignment if you put effort in, just meet requirements and word count. No final!

Phyllisa is a great professor who makes this anthro class pretty easy, especially if you take it in-person. Attendance matters, but you can get a solid A just by showing up and participating. I also found unstuck study super helpful for quiz prep!

Phyllisa herself is a nice person, and I can only assume her fall and spring semester classes are better, but do not take her summer class unless you really have to or you aren't working. Way too much work and readings assigned given how little time there is compared to fall/spring semester.

You do have to pay 70 bucks for a program to take online quizes (its Inquizitive) and view the textbook. But look for a free PDF of it online its easier to search through than the paid subscription. The class was pretty easy and straight forward a couple bigger assignments but nothing to crazy. No tests or real quizes just discussion posts.

Super chill class, especially if you're considering taking the honors course! I had a friend who took her online class and it looked much more difficult in comparison to hybrid. No final or midterm, just a paper that's relatively easy if u follow the rules. She hands out assignments that are printed so make sure you're at class. Take her for free A

I took her honors course and it was pretty easy. The only honors requirement was you had to make a presentation based on a specific topic with a partner. Attendance does matter percentage wise for your overall grade! I heard the online class is much harder so I would recommend taking her in person if possible.

Phyllisa is a really great professor. I took the class in-person and it was so easy. Most of the graded work is just based on your attendance and participation. If she assigns homework to be turned in in-person, she means it. Even if you're out of town, sick, etc. your paper needs to be physically turned in. I really recommend taking her!

Dr. Eisentraut's 101 lab is very engaging. She has students break into consistent groups, so your experience in this class may highly depend on who your lab partners are. She usually sticks to the lab activities in the textbook, but sometimes she also comes up with her own exercises. It's very easy to earn extra credit from her if you show effort.

Dr. Eisentraut cares about her students, and she is highly approachable. If you have any questions, whether they are course-related or not, she will be there to help you. Her in-person ANTH 101 course is easy to get an A in if you attend the lectures; a significant portion of the grades comes from in-lecture assignments, so attendance is required.

I would day there is a big difference between online and in-person class. I think its much easier to take in-person because you don't have to deal with waiting 24 hours to post a comment which sounds annoying. In substitute In-person class requires you to engage with classmates for class credit. Besides that this class is fairly easy!

She would make us start our discussion posts a whole five days before the deadline then make us wait 24 hours before responding to someone else. Real tough grader and the work is really hard and extensive. Wasn't very understanding when I had a personal problem and needed an extension also took too long to respond to emails.

Phyllisa is a good professor. I took this class during the summer so obviously it was in a time crunch. My one major complaint about her is the discussion posts. You are required to make 3 posts 24 hours apart. If they are even 23 hours apart you will get points taken off of your post. Mistakes happen and I forgot and it dramatically hurt my grade

Such a great teacher and gives awesome feedback. Grades were based on textbook quizzes and large assignments with no exams. Very passionate for an online course and always made sure to be available for her students.

Gives extra credit for extra work. No exams if students participate in discussions (which have rules RE post time), but you won't be able to see your grade until final grades are posted, unless you calculate it yourself. Theres one assignment where you have to go to a zoo, or view an zoo live feed. Theres only ~ 7 assignments per section so do it
In person lectures were good and many times we played games. Lots of worksheets so if you miss class you have to print them yourself. There were no exams so this class is so easy

LOVE HER - Her style is immaculate, very reasonable assignments and know about all of them ahead of time. She is so cute and funny. Quizzes can take some time but they are not really quizzes, more like study assignments, and can keep going until you get a good grade on them. Absolutely suggest you take this class. Fun and thought provoking!

If you show up to every class and do every assignment you will get an A. She seems to mostly grade on completion and accepts late work. There are a lot of worksheets but they are pretty easy to complete. She is very understanding, easy to talk to, and accepts a lot of extra credit work.

The material is interesting but she grades very harshly. It's spring break right now and she assigned us the same amount of work as a normal week. I find this very disrespectful to students since the whole point of spring break is to *have a BREAK* so that we can rest and come back to school refreshed. I typically spend 5-7 hrs/wk on this class.

Dr. Eisentraut seems to be really passionate about what she does! I took her summer 6 week course, and it wasn't too much work for how short of a class it is. She is really kind and will respond to emails outside class hours!

She is not a good prof. The quizzes are graded unfairly as the answer will be directly in front of you in the textbook, and youll write that answer and shell mark it wrong. When you contact her she And show her, she is very rude.

Took P's class during the COVID transition, she was very understanding and allowed a smooth transition into online from in-person. Passed the class using the textbook alone. All exams open book/note, in-person class has weekly assignments for grade, and she's clearly dedicated/connected to her subject. Definitely recommend, but it is book-heavy

I was honestly shocked to see ratings lower than 5/5! Expectations are EXTREMELY clear and you WILL get what put into this course. 6 week class is split into 5 parts with due dates at least a week apart, plenty of time to post multiple times in discussions (not hard). LOTS of reading, but it pays off - EC for exceeding expectations in assignments!!

I had professor Phyllisa Eisentraut for Anthro 101 Summer I 2020. She did a great job on laying everything out in the syllabus on what to expect in the following 6weeks so nothing was a surprise. There was a lot of reading, note taking, quiz's, and two major test, zoo observation, article reading, and some videos assignments. Smooth course!

There was so much I could say about this professor. I am glad I chose to stick with her. I struggled in the beginning, but she was encouraging and helpful. Her work was not too heavy and it was possible to do in 6 week summer course. I rater her a plus.

I'm taking her online class right now and DO NOT TAKE HER. I hate her discussion post cause she literally makes you post your discussion and post the second comments the next day or else she'll mark you down which is annoying as hell and doesn't make sense. I get that she wants her questions to be more unique but make it more relevant than be vague

Took Cultural Anthro during the year, taking Summer I Biological Anthro now. Great teacher. Very on top of grading. My best advice is to write a lot for the assignments. I wrote an extra 300 words, she gave me 10 extra points on an assignment. Want to improve your grade? Work harder. She can tell. Tests are hard, read the questions carefully!

Phyllisa is a great professor, she is very laid back and understanding. Her lectures can often get a little boring, but she does her best to keep them lively. Outside work is easy, and the quizzes/exams are all online. While you can get away with skipping a few classes, the in-class work really does stack up. Be consistent and you'll pass!
Very esat to get A, midterm is online, the answer can be found on quizlet. There is only one assignment in a semester. You can skip normal classes.

worst teacher. assigns busy work that makes absolutely no sense for the online class. She is a tough grader and all of her directions come off as overly picky/snotty and rude. Do not take her, take a different teacher. Also forces inquisitive every week. MORE BUSY WORK
amazing, would recommend.

He's a very kind man and he is willing to help anyone even on weekends, he might be a little intimidating but he's really nice and he also has a tutor to help on homework and testes. I took this class during the fall of my senior year because my school wasn't offering AP Calculus.

He was very slow during lectures. He was also very hard to reach through email. he made multiple mistakes when explaining math problems and often did problems wrong and other students had to call him out. We did not get very far in the book and the homework he assigns do not aline with what we learned in class.
He was very concise and very good at explaining intricate topics
Mohammed, a great professor, goes very slowly so the class understands, and takes moments for everyone to ask questions and catch up. Make sure to attend class and take notes.
Very nice give plenty of note taking material. He gives plenty of time to ask questions in class. Plenty of quiet moments (very boring if you're not doing anything)
Mohammed is a good person, but an okay professor. I often had to teach myself the lessons and spent a lot more time then I should have had to to get an A.

He is a good teacher and will try to help you the best he can. If the way he teaches is confusing and you cant figure it out, he has a tutor you can see. skipping class isnt an option you NEED to be there to hear lectures or you will fall behind. he assigns homework on hawkes system and he assigns a good amount. as long as you keep up ur fine.

This was the easiest math class ever, easier than any math class i took in high school. Theres a lot of homework but if you stay on top of, its easy. He offers bonus points if people do bad on the midterm. His classes are interesting and he waits for everyone to understand. He will help you anytime tell 8 pm at night and has a separate tutor also

This class without a doubt was the hardest class I have taken so far. He works with a program called Hawkes and there is a ton of homework and tests every week. I had no clue what my grade would be until the very last week because he only submitted a few homework/test grades to Canvas. He is accessible and you can make use of the tutor if you want.

I usually struggle with math classes, but Mohammed really helped me understand the material! He encourages students to meet with him during office hours so that he can answer anyone's questions and concerns. The majority of the grading is based off of your quizzes and exams in a separate website, and he lets you take each of the quizzes twice.

This was my first math class online so I was pretty nervous considering that I find maths hard. However, Professor Mohammed is the BEST and he was super HELPFUL during the semester. He was also very understanding that it´s hard to have classes online and he CARES a lot about his students. I would absolutely recommend him to others.

It was my first online Math class. I work full time during this epidemic and I have two kids. Needless to say that i had a busy schedule and NEEDED A LOT of help. Professor Muhamad was extremely helpful. His availability was Monday-Friday from 8AM to 9PM. That was extremely generous of him and he was always there to help when I needed his help.

If you want to be stressed out and crying over 500 unnecessary homework, he is the teacher for you. This man runs his class as if stats is the only class you have. He does not set you up for success and makes statistics 100 times harder than it needs to be. All he does is hard to follow lectures and uses an impossible program for homework. BAD IDEA
So boring. Do not take this class for stats he wont teach you anything and there are way better teacher options. Worst math class ever.

I had my apprehensions about choosing him after seeing the comments on this website. I ended up taking his summer class anyway and was surprised to learn that he's actually pretty chill. He understands that many students work and does his best to accommodate everyone. I went to 2 lectures out of 23 and still got an A. Easy material, nice teacher.

Mohammed is not a bad guy but he is just not a good teacher. His lectures are SO hard to follow along with and if you fall behind, you will not be able to recover. The class is extremely boring and nobody had any idea what was going on 90% the time. If you want a life, do not take this class because he also gives like 100 homework assignments.

Mohammed is a very smart man and is passionate about math. This class isn't for everyone, but he does care about his students success and will help you tremendously in order to pass the class. Just use the resources and pay attention in class and you'll be fine.

I passed this class fairly easily, but it required extra help from the tutor. He does not explain things that clearly, and doesn't respond quickly to emails. There are 3 exams and 1 final. Also chapter quizzes once a week. However, you are allowed a 1/2 page of notes on the quizzes and a full page of notes on the exams.

I honestly hate math because it is not my strong suit but I took this class thinking I was for sure going to fail and surprisingly I didn't . I received a C which isn't super good either but I'll settle. Mohammed provides a lot of homework and lectures to go over to really understand the material and pass his exams.

I missed a significant amount of classes cause it was at 8am and i would sleep through but I definitely knew all of the material thanks to his help. He was an amazing teacher and person! He made it so easy to understand stats and was always approachable and accommodating.

Professor Elhakimi goes through a lot of material during a short period. Pay attention or you will fall behind. If you fall behind, you will not be able to recover unless you put in a lot of effort. He is not the best at teaching, and there is a lot of students failing / dropping his class. Takes a long time returning tests and quizes.

Mohammed makes this class a pain. As others have stated, it is a self taught course and even though he stresses he has office hours, he shuts you down and redirects you. I also barely scraped by in this course, and trust me I put my time and soul in learning this. Takes forever to grade your stuff too. He was not inspiring in way or another.

Good teacher, a good amount of online homework. Use math lab and you should be good. Recommend to take the late start class only if you are a fast learner. Be respectful and show up to class. Be ready to study and you will do fine!

do not take this course with this professor. worst class ever. I had to teach myself everything, and just barely got by. I also didn't know what my grade was until the last week of the semester, so I had no time to improve. DO NOT TAKE!

If you are not a math person, don't take this class. Explains things once on board and moves on. Hard to approach. 50+ online homework assignments all due at end of semester...I just took my final and am still doing HW. He lost my third exam and inputted it as a zero so I had to visit office hours so he could relocate my test. Self-taught course.

Mohammed is one of the best math teachers I have ever had. His teaching style is hard to understand at the beginning of the semester but once you get familiar he is straight to the point and the material is easy to understand. A lot of homework but as long as you do app of it you will pass! I wish he was respected more because he cares!

Prof E. was a very good teacher. He was nice and personable and I really appreciated how he was able to connect with everyone and make it a fun environment to learn. He knows his stuff too. You could tell he has been doing this for a long time.

He was my STAT professor and he made it really easy for me. Everyone, please listen, YOU HAVE TO STUDY for this class, otherwise you will not be able to catch up in this class. His lecture style is pretty different than other professors, however, once you get hang of it, it is piece of cake. If you are the busy person, take his class.
Let"s just say, I have taken many math classes and they have all been very HARD for me. I have always done very average in math until I took stats 117 w/ this prof. Thanks Mohamad.

MGMT 101, tough grader. Always felt like I did good but got a bad grade with no feedback. Have to go back to 1 assignment for 3 or 4 times for credit, but you forget its there because it's not a 'to do' anymore in Canvas. Every class's deadline is 11.59pm but this class is 8pm, annoying. Lectures were good. Overall would not recommend.

I took Business 101 over the summer and I got a little behind on the first week (I got covid) and she basically told me to drop the class cause she didn't think I was capable of catching up... not super nice and not super helpful with anything. Basically forced me to drop for no reason. Overall rude and discouraging.

She's a great choice for your business class, and it's definitely an easy A. She really knows what she's talking about and makes lectures interesting because there's many small group discussion breaks. Also, I'm taking her 5:30-8:30pm class, which sounds like a drag but after the first few weeks she let's us out over an hour early EVERY time. Easy!

I took her class and we met once a week and really enjoyed the class the three hours didnt drag on like you would think. We took quizzes every week and she would grade them during the break so we would find out our score that day, super nice! The project seems intimidating but a larger group makes it easier!

I really enjoyed her Business class and didn't have trouble with the three-hour class. She offered a lot of opportunities for extra credit which is nice. Make sure you're in a solid group for the final project and you'll be fine, more group members means less work, just make sure your group members have been showing up.

Valerie's is an OK teacher, she is not hard but her grading is highly subjective even if she tells you it's not. Her class is boring and somewhat depressing; special when she starts talking about her life. It's good that she gives extra credit for just about everything even showing up early. If you want the easy A just show up and do the project.

Valerie Ellis is one of my favourite professors from SBCC! She is engaging and gives us clear instructions and examples before every homework assignment. The workbook is great and the whole class is great, even though it is a lot of work.

Claims she likes class participation but she doesn't really. Doesn't like to go off script, definitely not willing to go the extra mile to clear up questions. She is an easy grader which is nice but beware of the final project which is essentially more important than final exam. If you actually want an engaged professor don't take her.

Professor Ellis was extremely fair. The weekly quizzes were fairly simple, and her lectures were very clear. She even provided an optional final review session prior to the exam, which was an absolute gift. I enjoyed the class. Some advice: you are required to take part in very important group project. Be sure to pick team members that contribute.
Did not enjoy this class at all. She makes it impossible to focus and her tests are extremely difficult. She will also be very tough on you. Definitely not taking any class of hers again.

Great teacher! Unfortunately, due to health issues I had to drop out, but I would have easily gotten an A if I would have stayed. She is nice, funny and extremely helpful. If you do the reading and the homework you will get an A!
Great and inspirational teacher. Tough but fair. Gives good explanations on different levels. She is very professional but still manage to keep the class entertaining.

Great teacher! She is funny, explains clearly, gives real-life examples. Quiz every week instead of midterm, and a big final exam. Study and learn all important terms and you will get good grades on the tests. Playing games in class for credit and extra credit is fun and you learn a lot in doing so. Would love to have her as a teacher again!
She have it her way and she have even wrote the textbook. I felt like I did the same assignment thru the whole semester, but it is easy.
Her class is pretty tough, but she is a really good teacher. She makes you work for your grade. There are quizzes every week.
If you are serious about business do not take this course. Unorganized, not helpful and not professional.

Start Up- Entrepreneurship with Valerie was a decent class. She has a pretty casual teaching style and the class was fun. Her self written textbook needs a little work- as the assignments were repetitive and/or vague. Sometimes directions can be confusing or disorganized- she gets flustered. However, she is a fair grader and helpful when approached

She's very helpful but strict with her requirements.Very fair as long as you follow the schedule & do the work. Doesn't curve,but she gives extra credits(though you still have to know the materials to get it)Her class is extremely fun,informative,you'll walk away w/skills when you complete her course. She's one of the best teachers I've had!
She's a great teacher and a nice lady. Her classes are fun (for school) and she's totally fair. I would recommend her to anybody.
Very Good Class!!!
Poor communicator. Doesn't give answers. Not accommodating.
This class was enjoyable I learned many self-defense mechanisms and this Professor was great!
He is such a great teacher and I loved learning from him. He is very clear about grading materials and is an easy-A with your participation.
He is just a really cool guy. Life happens and if you skip a class or two no problem, just don't skip them all. He is really nice and I found karate a great way to relax and learn.
Professor Erdman is the best! Material was straight-forward! All you have to do throughout the semester is post in forums, turn in two papers, and make two videos (midterm & final).. Extremely easy!

I took this class over summer and it was super easy. You have two responses the whole summer and a video to make at the end. Make sure you start the video early because it's about two hours long and it takes forevverrr to watch the moves, then record yourself doing them. It's a really easy A online class. Take it!
Great guy. Take the class, and take it as many times as possible. He is just an all around great guy and amazing teacher. Make sure you go to the sushi night as well! its the last day of class
The guy's hilarious. He tried to keep it fun for all levels and has a ridiculous amount of patience for stupid things. Furthermore, if he likes you he'll tell you some really funny stories...
Easy A (online PE class) write two papers and make two videos... its cake!
Really cool guy, teaches you alot of really tight stuff. Doesn't care at all if you are late, and hardly cares if you dont show up. Very helpfull, nice guy. Makes the class fun.

An intense course that requires dedication. While the grading can be harsh, the material is engaging. Unstuckstudy AI proved helpful for studying alongside the textbook. Overall, a challenging but rewarding class if you're willing to put in the work.
A legend in his own mind. Demands to be called Dr. as opposed to Professor. Really attached to his "royal" lineage. Teaching methods very outdated, just monologues, the star of the show.

If you actually want to learn, DO NOT take a class with this professor. As a PoliSci Major, I had no choice but to take his classes and he never fails to disappoint each time. He's not helpful at all and will literally drop your grade for the most unreasonable things. It has been the worst few semesters in my life and it's all because of him.

He made me resent political science. I'd get 70/100 marked down only due to "improper" submission. No 2nd chances or accept any work after submission etc. It's a really hard, time consuming course and I really tried my best, submitted my best work, but his unnecessary, copious markdowns failed me. Understanding's completely alien to him.

Didn't really learn anything. Had to write discussion posts and papers every other week. Definitely assigns a lot to read but you could honestly write anything because I doubt he reads it anyways. Basically just read the readings of philosophers and had to write 2 page papers every other week along discussions. 4 page midterm and 12 page final

Took POLS101 as a political science major. The first week was pretty tough but then it was fairly easy the rest of the semester. Basically the same two assignments every week with an occasional quiz or paper. Lectures can be kind of boring since they are long, typed out chunks of text but that's expected with an online course. Overall a good course

I have heard that he is a good professor in person. However, online he absolutely sucks. He has ruined any passion I had for politics. The same assignments each week, and it feels like busy work. His online class is a waste of my time. And his lectures suck too, they're just typed out which is weird. He seems to put zero effort into the class.

I hate this prof. He was the worst teacher ever because he didn't even teach; he just sent out an index card worth of lecture notes to us every week and we taught ourselves essentially. There was so much reading and it was just an awful class overall. Just try to avoid him altogether.

Prof doesn't have a creative or out of the box bone in his body. Tests are copy/pasted from teachers manual & you will get little to no feedback from prof. It's an easy A class but not worth it if you think Polysci is something you're interested in. Prof puts in minimal effort, uninspiring is the word I'd use to characterize both course & prof.

Worst professor Ive had and Ive taken over 80 credits. He sends out aggressive emails threatening to fail students. He routinely gives failing grades on quality posts for not meeting format requirements etc. Very condescending. Doesnt abide by his own due dates. Penalizes last min submissions even if on time. Easy test bank curriculum. Bad prof

Discussions every week due on Sunday but if we do it on Saturday you receive half credit immediately because "your waiting till the last minute" Makes no sense, a due date is a due date. Don't even bother to reach out to him because hes doing to tell you that he cant help, to reach out to student tech, etc.

I would not take this professor again Im not sure if he believes the city college is Harvard or what but he is setting an expectation for all students that is not near reality. He is extremely picky with grading and if you do one thing wrong on the discussions he will give you a 0 and will discourage you by a ton. Take any other professor.

I would not recommend the professor. The amount of work expected from us was huge(dozens of pages submitted), which would be one thing if the work he showed us in return matched. Instead he gave little to no feedback and his lectures were a few typed word pages every other week. I worked way harder and longer in this class than my 5 unit courses.

I dropped this class in hopes to take it with another professor who is a better fit. I didn't care for the structure of the class and while I could tell that he cared about the subject matter (not the students) and was an invested professor, his overall attitude wasn't for me. Too preoccupied with complaining about online teaching.

Reached out to this professor multiple times with questions about assignments and how they should be shaped. The best response I got from him was read more. Set up meetings with counselors and tutors and still failed this class, while having received good grades from nearly every other teacher in this department.

Essentially, I taught myself everything throughout this course. His grading criteria are vague but will grade you harshly if you don't meet his ambiguous standards. He gives zero feedback. There is not a ton of homework, but the midterm is a 4-page paper and the final is a 10-page paper so prepare for that. I do NOT recommend this teacher at all.

I took his Pols 101 class and LOVED this prof, clearly cares about the subject and makes you want to as well. He can be a harsh grader and is very nit-picky about formatting. Not a lot of work, just big projects. If you reach out for help though and show you care, he will notice. If you are interested in politics I would definitely recommend.

Pretty unclear on expectations you have to search through the modules to find what you need to do. Doesn't forgive late work. If you figure out what you need to do and follow requirements youll be fine. Two big essays, group project, biweekly discussions. Better in person. Online he struggles.

It's been a year since I took this class and I still think about it all the time. Dr. Eskandari made a massive impact on my life just through one class. The readings were interesting and made all the more meaningful by his lectures. Not to mention that he's hilarious. He will demand your participation but it's worth it. I would take it again.

Web and paper assignments are easy, but MAKE SURE YOU GET FULL CREDIT on those because you'll need it to make up for the insanely difficult tests. I studied for hours and took the pre-quiz tests, wrote down the answers, studied that material, wrote down definitions, and the 50 question 50 minute quiz is almost impossible to ace

It is true that he is not very engaged w/ students as a professor. Most of the quiz questions fall outside of textbook readings & written lectures. Be prepared to do your best for the sake of passing, even if you find it hard to get the most of the class.

The worst experience I have ever had with a professor. He was uncompromising, aloof, and showed little or no regard for his students success. He is probably a great lecturer, but unfortunately I was never directly taught by him and was instead directed entirely by textbook reading.

I mean you don't really have any other options for this class so he'll do. Lectures were a bit dry so you could probably could make do with skipping some of them. Strict grader because I think my final deserved a little more than a 77.

I took his online summer class. The class was a lot of reading from the textbook & his written lectures. Theres a lot of quizzes: pre-tests every week & 50 point quizzes every week as well which are timed. I found the class boring & even though I got an A I feel like I didnt learn much. Hes a tough grader on papers so exceed the min!

Professor Eskandari is the worst professor Ive had at SBCC. I got a B because I submitted a paper in the wrong format. He gave me a zero for the assignment. When I emailed him, he was rude and never answered my question. Its an easy class to pass online because he is simply not present. This guy is a joke!

Course relied heavily on use of textbook and the lectures were not my favorite but still had lots of valuable information and are worth paying attention to. The Quizzes and tests are straightforward and the paper assignments are equally easy. The class is an easy A as long as you do not put off assignments until the last minute.

Worst professor I have ever had. He is very unprofessional and rude. I would not recommend this professor. His lectures and assignments are very unclear, even if you do as the prompt says he will still find a way to fail you.

so you may be wondering what a chem major is doing taking this class but i needed a humanities for my igetc requirement. anyways, as much as i wanted to keep this class, walking in all there was was political jargon and if you weren't a polisci major, you were pretty much going to have a hard time. also this class felt like it lacked diversity.

He gave me a zero on a complete, well written, and thorough paper for turning it in in the wrong format. (google docs, not microsoft word) paper was worth 1/5th of my grade and the zero set me up for failure for the whole semester and even though I got A's on everything else, I only got a C in the class. was rude and unprofessional when i emailed

Grading policy is strict and unclear. You get one minute per question on the "quizes" that are basically exams because of their length. The mandatory pre-quizes have nothing to do with what is on the actual ones. The prompts for the papers are unclear and you are likely to loose points for not doing something that you didn't even know you had to.

Mrs. Eurman is a great teacher, she is dedicated in helping all of her student. She makes time to meet with each student to discuss ways to help you find a career path. This class is one of those classes that you get what you give. I truly enjoyed it, and was able to get clarity on my life goals and purpose.

it's a really easy class to pass but she is so unorganized it's not even worth it. she constantly interrupts herself and others and will call people out in front of the entire class if they are late or mess up. the class is pretty much a joke but an easy A as long as you do all the assignments.

Val is a cool teacher! Make sure you show up because participation is a huge part of your grade. Class is really easy and she is a really easy grader. She's very passionate about her job and wants to help her students succeed. The class has helped a little to determine my majorcareer but not a huge amount.

Val is really kind, easy going & a good professor. Class was easy. The bad part was the other students, lots of students placed in her class because they don't know what they want to do. While this class is meant to help you with that, it also attracts a lot of slackers. But, it makes it easier for you to stand out!
Great teacher!

Prof. Fack is an amazing teacher, and you really learn a lot in her class. She is very helpful not only with class but with career advice. I would definitely recommend anyone looking to do any accounting in the future to take her course. She was also very friendly and caring.
One of the best professors I have had. Very helpful. Too bad she only teaches payroll class. The final project is a little difficult so attend class it helps.
Literally gives out extra credit for rating him on Rate My Professor. Does not care about students at all and is not an easy grader. Will give you poor grades without watching your presenations.
A lot of work for an elective class, and he was very slow to grade anything.

Does not care about class, and does not care about your grade. I got an A in every other class at City College the past two years, but struggled to get a B in this class. He also will not let you make up something if you miss something.
Super cool teacher, such a nice class
Laid back guy, sometimes slow to update grades
Super chill guy, loves Tupac lol
Very discussion post oriented. Not that bad of a class

Get professor it is very manageable class. Homeworks are just online discussions responding to the text book. He doest grade harshly great class if you don't want a heavy work load. He's an amazing professor. Participation was selected because it matters that you do the homework not because you needed to talk everyday in class. He gives discussion post every week but was relatively easy. One presentation in front of class but wasn't hard. Textbook was selected as yes because you do need use it but he provides an online version

It was an easy chill class. He would only do a small lecture in the beginning of class that was sometimes boring but otherwise ok. He helped us a lot on the midterm and gave us advice on how to write and get a good grade on it. Would recommend this class if you want a stress free semester.

Solid prodigy prof w/ clear expectations from day 1, concepts r straightforward if u show up & stay engaged. He connects ancient Mexican cultures to CHST while tying in golden age rap & Brown & Black history (wrote a fat dope thesis). His class sharpens true writing & critical thinking & prepares u 4 grad level coursework, he will NOT bbysit. -Luna
Super cool teacher, Classes weren't too bad, a lot of movies. Some presentations and papers, nothing too crazy.
Cool person, not very knowledgeable for the class, nor cared much about the students.

He is a great professor and his class is very straight forward. You should not have a problem getting a good grade if you show up and do the work :) I ended with a low grade because circumstances caused me to miss a lot of classes Including the last few where I was ment to do a presentation (a hefty part of your grade).
Honestly, a cool person, but didn't show up 50 percent of the time, didn't care about much, and will not respond to emails.

If you go to every class you'll get an A pretty much. I did not but he's an absolute chiller and gave me a good grade anyway. You pretty much watch the Tupac hulu show all semester until the end where you have to do a presentation. I did not show up to class for a month when we were doing presentations and still got a B on the assignment. twinn fr
Super easy prof! The class was mandatory and consisted of only watching films in class. Hes a very easy grader as well.

The professor demonstrates strong professional competence in their deep understanding of Chicanx history, culture, social movements, and contemporary issues, which is evident in the way they contextualize course material and connect it to broader themes of identity, resistance, and social justice.
He's a great professor also he is very understanding about grades. Class time is very informational and the work is straight forward !
His class was terrifi

This class was a breeze and any missing work was slacking on my part because professor Flores is very encouraging, helpful, and has clear expectations. He also just seems like a decent human being, he makes sure to include everybody, answers questions thoroughly, and he is super understanding. Would take again if he taught more courses!
Easy class to pass and even get an A. Work is easy, and you get a lot of time to do it.
The professor was okay. He didn't really respond to emails. But it was a fairly easy class.

This class is very straight forward, interesting, and engaging. Professor Mejia is very caring and gives good feedback when you ask him questions. Overall I would recommend taking his class it is worth it! Class is mandatory so pull up.

Overall amazing class, even better teacher. Professor Flores cares about you learning, so make sure to show up to class, most, if not all of your assignments are based off the lectures. Pay close attention to the video's shown as they help you towards your assignments. Class is smooth sailing if you just show up!

This class is easy to pass since it's only one discussion a week plus one midterm, one presentation, and a final. The professor is also very understanding and clarifies any questions about the assignments. Would definitely take his class again!
This class was very easy to pass as long as you keep up with the work the professor is very kind and cares about the subject he teaches.
This class is very easy to pass as long as you keep up with the work and the professor is passionate about the subject and makes it easy to understand.

I took his 6-9 lecture on Wednesdays where attendance was mandatory however we were only there till 9 two times the whole year which was due to the one presentation that you have to complete. Other than that we were let out at 7:30 everyday. Lectures were very quick and then you will watch a video for the last hour of the class. Homework was easy.

I liked taking this class. It was an easy chill class. He would only do a small lecture in the beginning of class that was sometimes boring but otherwise ok. He helped us a lot on the midterm and gave us advice on how to write and get a good grade on it. Would recommend this class if you want a stress free semester.
Professor Frankel was a delight. An A is attainable if you do all the assignments and turn them on time. She gives lots of extra credit opportunities. Recommend her to the moon and back!
Shes a very unfair and though grader. Worst mistake was taking her class.

Professor Frankel is a very genuine person that enthusiastic about what she teaches and it is infectious. She gives lots of extra credit that it is nearly impossible to not get an A. Modules are open on thursdays and you have 10 full days to complete. Exams are open note and multiple choice. One of the best profs at Sbcc and I highly recommend her

Esther is such a computer geek. She teaches the content well, it's just super difficult and there's so much to remember. She offers a lot of extra credit, but it's also super difficult. Granted if I were to actually do all of it, I probably would've gotten an A instead of a B. I recommend her for this course, just be prepared to work for your grade

Professor Frankel is an amazing professor. If I were to have taken CIS 101 with any other professor, I would have most definitely not have received an A grade nor would I have even passed the class. Computer Integrated Science is a VERY tough topic to understand and Professor Frankel is amazing at helping each student understand the topics.

Prof Frankel is one of the BEST professors I've ever had. She is always available over email and during office hours if you have any questions. If you want an A make sure take LOTS of notes and try not to miss any classes. Exams are open book/notes, but they're timed and difficult so studying is still necessary. Make sure to do all extra credit!!

This is a professor who really cares about what she teaches. She dresses according to what the days lesson is on and even baked cookies for the class. This class felt pretty easy until I took the final exam. The average grade on the final exam was a 56%. She offers plenty of extra credit that can save your grade though.

Esther is a great teacher, gives good lectures based on the textbook. She is always open to answering any questions during her lectures and presentations if you don't understand or want another example. Always shows really good TED talks, that are both fun and interesting. Be prepared to read every page in the book to prepare for midterms etc.
Esther is a great teacher, fair grader, and runs her class in a very interesting manner. Extremely kind, and very communicative and ready to help. Would take again!

Esther is on it! She responds very quickly to students and posts homework and test results soon after you complete it. She cares very much about her students and goes above and beyond to answer your questions in depth. She makes the online course "geeky" fun for all. What a brilliant and hilarious woman; I aspire to be like her one day.

Absolutely great professor. She wants you to succeed in the class, gives a ton of extra credit, lets you have open notes/book for ALL exams, only 1 quiz, and she curves your grade. Grades are in literally hours after taking exams or doing assignments. She makes the class fun and inspiring! I switched my major to CIS after taking this course.
Gives little to no help to her online students. She isn't very clear on her grading of her assignments. Tip: cite anything you put into your assignments or she will write you up for plagiarism.

Literally the BEST CIS teacher ever! Miss Esther is really nice and helpful teacher! REALLY HELPFUL. Like REALLY. Miss Esther offers a lot of extra credit which help you get an easy A! One Quiz, Midterm and Final are all OPEN BOOKS and NOTES! I'm not geeky but she makes me geeky. LOVE HER CLASS! RECOMMENDED!

BEST TEACHER EVER!!! I took her online class and it was the best class ever. Just do the online assignments on time, study a little bit for the exams and you will get an A. She also gives so many extra credit points that it makes it impossible not to get at LEAST a B !! HIGHLY RECOMMEND
Her personality is worth a bushel of hot peppers!
Great teacher - very helpful and very involved.

Professor Frankel is easily the best online professor I've had. She is a very active participant in the online discussions; she is more involved than many "in-person" professors I've had. Her assignments are challenging yet thought provoking; there is no busy work in her class. Definitely challenges you and makes learning rewarding. A+!
Esther is awesome, she is super helpful and you'll really learn something. She's gotten to where she is now because of how great she is!

Professor Frankel is an amazing and fun professor. I thought this class was hard but she makes it really interesting. You will have to read a lot, the textbook is about 700 pages and YES you will read every single page. Show up to class and you will pass. She gives a lot of extra credit opportunities, take them!

Very nice and friendly teacher, offers a decent amount of extra credit, gives you all the tools to succeed, only negative is her tests are hard even though they are open booknotes you should still study as if it's not because there isn't enough time to look up all the answers, overall great teacher and would take her again if needed.
speaks loudly and clearly, connects with students, lectures are understandable
Best teacher I ever had, clear, nice, funny etc. But mostly, inspirational! A real motivator for college!

Ester Frankel is one of the best teachers at SBCC I really enjoyed taking her classes. I have had a couple of classes with her. I regret I am out of classes to take with her. She is dedicated teacher who gives greatly of her experiences. SBCC is lucky to have her. The baked goods are also awesome. Thanks Ester.

Not only was Esther the only teacher I had this semester that cares about the class, she put her own personal matters aside to help a flustered student out. No matter what grade you get in the class you will walk away with a life lessons and a mentor that for the rest of your time here. In my opinion, this teacher excels at every aspect of teaching

Esther is the best instructor at SBCC out of many fine ones. She's highly intelligent, extremely knowledgeable, and always right there to help you in any way she can. She did a great job of developing the class and she's fully engaged with replies that are very detailed and full. She's a wonderful person, loves technology, and loves to share.

As always, awesome instructor. No one cares or helps more than this woman does. Even if you're not into computers much, take a class from her for your gen requirements. The knowledge you'll learn in invaluable and she's such a fun person!

This is the sweetest lady ever. She baked our class cookies...twice. She loves being geeky and wants everyone to be geeky as well. Class is staight forward and interesting. You take ALOT of notes in class but its good cause the book can get a little dry. Shes very knowledgeable and is more than willing to help. Great and fun class :)

For those you want an easy A this is not the class for you! Esther is a great teacher, who is helpful, clear, and nice. She is very knowledgeable. Expect to read two full books in this class. Microsoft 70-680 and 70-685. If you want a taste of system configuration in windows this is the class for you.

She is a very good teacher and very knowledgeable. I love her lecture notes, they are clear, concise and very comprehensive. I love the way she grades assignments too, one gets an accurate feedback on them. And the assignment she gives encourage students to go through text books at least once. I took CIS 245 IT Project Mgmt with her and am glad.
If there is a person with stronger experience and qualifications to teach this class, I would be surprised.

Took her for Work Exp. in Business and while I never met her in person, she was very clear with the grading expectations and fair with the grading. Basically you just complete a couple assignments about your goals through the semester and fill out a time card. If you're working in business or doing an internship, sign up here and boost your GPA.

He's a good guy, yes, the class is more than averagely work heavy, expect to write a lot for the discussions and projects but the answers are all in the textbook provided online or film that you're watching, if you're of chicano heritage I recommend taking this class because you'll have a sense of every learning goal in this course.

From the start of this class I got a clear indication this guy was not a good professor. His constant threats of dropping students if they don't do a unit or the unnecessary final project got draining, also constant essays on every assignment. He also doesn't show your current grade on canvas. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. Stray far away from him.

I'm taking a course during the summertime so I understand there's a big workload. But this man blows it out of the water with the work he assigns. Every week its 2 400-word discussions and then 2 300-word media analyses. I wake up and my first thought is how do I finish the work? Topped with other courses this class becomes a burden on my life.

Every assignment was an essay. Even if it was just a discussion post it was basically still just another essay. In all honesty I know that this is a college class and it was gonna require a lot of work but at some point it just becomes excessive. His grading is also completely messed up it makes no sense. I would not recommend him to my worst enemy

It's a lot of reading. His class isn't like other classes you only have 3 days to read an entire chapter (up to 40 pages+) and write 400-word discussion questions. and another 2 days to respond to 2 other classmates. There were also 2 essays one for midterm and one for the final. The midterm also had another long writing assigment to go with it.

Professor Fuentes was really nice and emphasized that he was open to help any students at any time. He gives out extra credit throughout the semester so look out for that. Overall, he was really understanding and a great professor.

Dr. Fuentes was my Chicano Studies professor online and he was extremely unorganized. He stopped responding to emails months before the semester ended, graded every assignment late, and gave no feedback. He was nearly impossible to reach outside of class and the entire semester felt pointless. The subject matter was interesting and engaging though.

I took Dr. Fuentes' online course and all we did was read from assigned texts and write about it. He gives no feedback on anything that you write and doesn't justify grades. He is very disorganized because he "teaches" at so many schools. Easy A, but not much of a class. It felt like I was writing into an abyss every week. Gives extensions btw

Dr. Fuentes was an AMAZING Chicano Studies professor. He really cares, the content he teaches is highly relevant, and he is always there to help you succeed. I highly, highly recommend taking his class!!!

Best teacher at SBCC! He is extremely clear about when and what you need to take notes on which makes tests really easy. If you can I would take his lab at the same time. He was always playing music and and made the environment super fun. He cares about all his students and goes above and beyond for us. He threw a pizza party for us. 10/10 teacher!

hisaya's animal diversity lab is not very hands-on and feels more like a reading class with long weekly packets and required drawings. you can't leave until all your work is done and there is a test every week with the most obscure questions.

hisaya's animal diversity class is very test-heavy with weekly quizzes and memorization only exams. quizzes are open-note, but exams require intense studying. instead of learning animal differences, the focus is on niche cell functions. he's also very chatty, even during tests.

Syi has been one of my favorite professors. He truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. His work was easy to not only understand but also do. I took his lab as well which is probably the easiest lab you'll take. His lectures as also very clear and easy to understand.

Best teacher I've taken at SBCC. Cares deeply about his students, makes himself supper accessible and is always more than willing to help make sure you're doing well in his class. Super fun environment. Lets us talk and plays music on the speakers. Also is a very interesting class. Lots of live animals!

Sye is a legend. Cannot recommend him enough. He is extremely clear about when and what you need to take notes on which makes tests a breeze. I took the lab as well and had a great time. He was always playing music and made the environment super fun. He also obviously cares deeply about his students and goes above and beyond for us. 10/10 teacher!

Easily my best class and professor I've ever had. Great guy, relatively easy material, easy tests too. Study with Quizlet and you'll get 100% on all 3. Super fun personality and he cares about the class deeply as well as his students. If you can, take his lab (ZOOL123) at the same time if you can. Had it right after lecture and it was great.

Sye is awesome. My favorite prof at sbcc so far. He is extremely clear about what you need to take notes on for exams and quizzes and the study guides are the exact same as the exams. I took the lab as well and it was very interesting. It is somewhat time consuming but the content is fun to learn. I would recommend him over and over!

I attended both Zoology lecture and lab and enjoyed both, the professor is very straight forward with the information expected to be memorized, providing very clear study guides and notes. Clearly passionate not just about the subject but teaching itself. Truly a class that motivates effort and attention

Sye is a straightforward professor. Pay attention to lecture, take notes, do the study guide, and you get an A. He's funny, passionate about the subject, and cares about students and wants you to succeed. Didn't take the lab, but that is probably a great way to get to know him on a deeper level.

this teacher is awesome I took his lecture and lab and I have the best experience. he is so caring and will help you out if you don't understand or need help. he wants the best for his students. I would 100% recommend his class.

This is an easy A if you need credits. Sye is so nice and funny. You do 6 taxonomy drawings per class and the lab manual which is homework but he gives us time in class to do them. The two big finals are identifying the live examples from your drawings youve done in class. Do the drawings with the taxonomy info and youll pass.

He is one of the best professors Ive ever had. He really cares about the class and his students. You need to attend lecture for the notes because the tests and quizzes are all on them. Hes very understanding especially if you attend lecture. Study from the study guide when he gives it to you and youll do fine on the tests.

Took zoology this year and absolutely loved this class! Sye is a dedicated and understanding teacher and while you might not interact with him one on one much, he constantly encourages students to reach out if they have any difficulty. If you need to get some Natural Sciences credits I highly recommend taking his Zoology class!

The best teacher I've ever had. If you're considering taking zoology for gen ed please take Sye! He is the most understanding and caring teacher, there is not enough characters for me to write about how awesome he is, just please take him! I'm an awful student but he makes me want to show up and do the work!
LOVE HISAYA!! Though his lectures can get a little tedious at times, he is very devoted to making sure you successfully pass the class. Very funny with the quips and overall a nice solid guy!

Sye is a seriously cool guy. He loves what he does and cares about his students' success. He knows a ton about animals and is very passionate about them. His tests are hard, but if you really study and grind for them, you'll see the reward. There are four (?) online quizzes and 3 tests, that's all.

Professor Hisaya Fukui (Sye) is the best teacher I have ever had. His passion for animals and clear notes on what the exams entail made this class not only interesting but fun! He is thorough and kind, if you put effort into this class, that means making and memorizing flashcards on the study guides he posts, you will get an A. I loved this class.

Great teacher who cares about his labs and his students. His passion made the course one of my best and I enjoyed having a professor that treats everyone with the same value. Recommend this class if you need a lab!

TAKE HIS CLASS! He is funny, passionate about animals, cares about all his students passing, and makes lectures fun/easy to learn. There is an online book with approx. 7 quizzes throughout the semester(open book), and 3 tests including the midterm and final. Just show up, take the notes, memorize the notes, and you'll pass. You won't regret it!!

Fun zoology lecture, I took it with the lab as well and enjoyed both a lot. Not a lot of homework, main work is showing uo and memorizing exam material. Overall a really interesting course with a fun and not very difficult (lots of drawing) lab, and a outgoing and funny teacher who is very straight forward, and grades 85% and up is an A. Loved it!

Zoo lab has a mandatory attendance because if you skip class, you won't be able to make the missing lab up for the exam. Class is very easy to follow, and is the definition of an easy A. Sye is always trying to help so don't be afraid to ask any questions!

Sye brings so much passion and energy to every lecture he gives. Very good professor and he keeps students engaged during lectures through his passion for the subject. Basically show up every Thursday for the quiz, don't forget about the online homework and exams are a piece of cake because it's all memorization.

Sye is one of the gems at SBCC. Professors/instructors like him make students want to keep coming to class and learn. Most (if not all) of Sye's students want him to teach more subjects, if the school would let him. Goes out of his way to help students. He has lots of life-experience in the field that permeates into his teachings
Sye is undoubtedly one of my favorite professors I've ever had. He's hilarious, makes the class very interesting, and it's pretty simple as long as you're showing up to class. Really cool guy

Sye cares about his students, and he is very specific on what he wants us to take notes on so we don't have to scramble and write down everything he says. He gives a study guide that is in the same order of the exam and he doesn't twist words around. As long as you study the study guide and write down the notes he puts on the board, you'll get an A

His Biology 142 class is pretty easy if you do all the weekly readings. I was not interested in marine biology at all before I took this class. But I am now! If you take this class online, he does offer proctored exams. I took them at my local library so that helped me out a lot!

He is honestly one of the best teachers I've had at SBCC. He truly cares about all his students and wants them to succeed. He makes every lecture very interesting and every study guide is basically the exams. Very helpful and very funny. Super chill, you can talk to him about anything. As long as you do your work you'll get an A.
This class is basically a ton of reading and a few assignmentstests. You aren't going to interact with the professor too much, but he is there for you if you need him.

This class is unnecessarily hard and he grades things based on how much he likes you. He thinks his class should be put above any other priorities or things you may have going on in your life. He doesn't give clear grading instructions and he made us read a book that he wrote that has absolutely nothing to do with philosophy.
Dennis is an amazing professor. Be ready to read everything he asks and be curious. You'll do just fine if you keep an open mind to learning.

i've had previous bad experiences with philosophy classes & was a bit worried about this course, but prof was so genuine in his lectures & was happy to answer and explain anything, no matter how long it had been since first covered. attendance is important and in my experience was no issue for me, his lectures were enjoyable :)

Class is formatted where A is achievable if you show up and put in the work. Allows you to redo/send in for review every assignment, including the midterm & final. Pretty unclear with feedback and if you want to redo assignments well you WILL have to ask him about it. Go to class every day. Mostly categorical logic, not actual philosophy.

This course was quite challenging, but Professor Gagnon breaks down course material in lecture. He sets up the course so that it's possible for everyone to get an "A" as long as they are willing to put in the work. This course requires a lot of critical thinking. Turning in drafts and getting feedback for exams is highly recommended.

For me, this was a challenging class. It requires a lot of reading, but there is a lot of interesting material. This class involves a lot of critical thinking about abstract ideas, but Professor Gagnon breaks down the material in his lectures and study guide. If you want a good grade, you must put in your effort.

The study guide is all you need to read(super easy), he allows retakes for ALL of the in-class quizzes and also replaces the lowest exam grade with the highest (3 take-home exams). He allows you to send him drafts before grading and gives very clear feedback, so pretty much impossible to not get an A unless you don't put any effort in.Very cool guy

If you're trying to fill a req for English or even just interested in what I guess normal people assume philosophy to be, it is a mistake to take this class. This is a logic course not philosophy. for the pretentious, stop, no one who isn't a philosophy major will enjoy logic let alone have a preconception of what it is.MISTAKE(would type a essay

Participation is mandatory and everything you will be tested on is learned in the lectures. The textbook readings help, but aren't necessary. All homework can be redone and he will preview your essays (one per quarter) and give you feedback if you ask.
This class is very lecture based. Lots of student participation and harsh grader. Not a easy A at all.

I was originally going to drop this class but my curiosity got the better of me after the first week, and I am very happy I stayed. His personal stories that he connects to the topics we learn in class is really interesting and inspiring. Make sure you go to class and never miss the in class quiz, it affects your grade a lot. Send drafts for finals

If you don't like being called on randomly I wouldn't recommend his class because he makes sure every student speaks. This class definitely pushed me and I learned a lot. Don't take this class if you want an easy A. He is a pretty tough grader. He also does a one question quiz at the beginning of each class so attendance is super important.

This was a tough class for me because the topic is inherently subjective. It's really hard to get a good grade if you don't submit essay drafts early enough for him to give you feedback. Discussions can be really interesting and he definitely knows his stuff.

Gagnon was awesome! Kind man who wants you to succeed. He makes it very easy to do well, you just have to use the resources he gives you. Phil 101 did require more reading than expected, but if you follow his lectures and study guide you can do well if you miss the reading. Would reccomend taking phil 101 with Gagnon.
Enjoyed the class more than I anticipated. Professor Gagnon was extremely knowledgeable, fair, and gives you everything you need to succeed in the class.

This is not a super easy A philosophy class. You have to be willing to work for it. You can earn a 100% or more IF you do the work. Mr. Gagnon lets you email him your drafts of the midterm and final exams, multiple times, and he gives you feedback on them before you turn them in. In essence, I would email him 5 drafts and get 100% . It takes time

His grading is very harsh. He's boring & not clear. He doesn't 'break down' the difficult stuff. If you have to take this, see if there's a different teacher. I would never take another class from him.

I took Professor Gagnon's class during COVID so everything was online. He records every lecture for you to review if needed. If you turn in your homework on time and don't like the score you got, he lets you make it up for full credit. He also lets you send in rough drafts of the midterms and final. Super helpful and wants his students to succeed.

I really enjoyed this class because I was able to actually learn online. In the beginning I was having some trouble understanding the material and Gagnon was quick to set up a meeting with me to really explain it to me in detail. After that meeting it all became more clear and as long as you do homework and turn the 3 tests in. YOU will be good.

I was pleasantly surprised by having Gagnon. He is a very knowledgable guy and loves philosophy. The class was relatively easy but still engaging. He gives you everything you need to get an A and will even give you feedback on your exam answers before taking the exam. If you don't do well on his exams, it's because you didn't follow directions.

There's a quiz every class period but it is open-notes/book. You can retake the quiz as many times as you want within 2 weeks of first taking it He provides the exam questions ahead of time so you can prepare your essay response ahead of time. His lectures are heavy but he provides them all on his canvas as well as a "study guide" for each module.

Gagnon is definitely passionate about Philosophy. We had several good arguments/discussions between the students and him and it honestly helped with learning the content because it made it more memorable. There's definitely a lot of content and the PowerPoints are word heavy and seem intimidating, but the content itself isn't too hard to understand

Dennis is a really sweet guy who cares a lot about his students. His class can get a little dry at times, but everything that gets covered in lecture can always be found on canvas (which is extremely helpful). He gives great and specific feed back, but is a hard grader, and he hands grades back the day after you turn your work in.

This class is incredibly difficult and conceptual but if you really apply yourself you can get through it. Dennis always lets you submit drafts of papers before the deadline to be checked and gives good feedback, and you can redo hw assignments as many times as you want. I would highly recommend this class if you like logic and arguing a point.

Straight forward professor. Speaks in concise, borderline dull language. But it's philosophy and it's always best kept simple in his opinion. Everything is necessary in this class or you will get behind, believe me- book, HW, etc. So refresh yourself every day. Two papers, two exams, and homework every lecture. Easy as long as you can keep up.

Worst class imaginable that half the class ended up dropping. Not a clear grading criteria and harsh grader for someone who explains the material in a much more difficult manner than need be. Not helpful, when you ask questions you get a condescending response. Long, long, boring lectures and reads right from his lecture notes. Do not take.

This was one of the most interesting classes I've taken at sbcc, and it was probably one of the most helpful in terms of learning how to writes and and analyze arguments. Professor Gagnon is a very interesting lecturer, and challenges students to think critically about pretty interesting philosophical problems.

I'm an international student with no background in physics or philosophy, and I wouldn't say I'm a geek either, still, I could follow the content of the class and really enjoy it. It's mostly a conceptual class and it provoked me to start questioning reality through approaching how past and current theories are made from observations. TAKE IT!!!
First rate. I am a GEEK and he made philosophy fun and useful. I actually learning something about Physics!

I took the "Great Ideas of Physics"-class. Professor Gagnon is a really smart man. His lecture notes are very well written, and he gives clear feedback on the paper(s). He is an asset to SBCC. As a foreign student with limited background in physics, the class was hard, but managable. It opens up a new world for the student. Recommended!

Professor Galicia is amazing, he's one of the most respectful and understanding professors I've had. he's very knowledgeable and invested in the topics, particularly about racial inequality, he connects the topics to his own life and experiences often. I will say there are very few assignments, and it's definitely an easy A.

took soc101 with dr. galicia, extremely kind and interested in the material, but preoccupied with things other than the class. cancelled class repeatedly, told us he was going to post lectures and never did. hardly any assignments, and lectures were replaced by group discussions. extremely easy but felt kind of like a waste of time

Dr. Galicia was great. His class was super easy and one of the most intriguing soc classes I have taken. I would highly recommend him to anyone that wants to learn about racial inequality or soc in general. Super cool teacher and very respectable too.

Dr G was awesome, he shares about his former life and is such like a real person who had been through crazy times but turned it around. No homework, he even showed a documentary I recommended in class, never had that happen. Show up to every class and just participate in the lecture and you'll get an A.
