I have had her for a few classes now and she is a very chill and flexible professor. There were times where I didn't finish an assignment or even midterm on time and she was extremely understanding after I emailed. She is very sweet and knowledgable about Chicano Studies. Highly recommend.
Love this class and I think she's a passionate teacher and pretty chill! Highly recommend.
Loved how she treated her online class as if it was in person. Dressed up, sat in her library, and taught the art history class with passion. Which was contagious.

Professor Gandara's teachings and content explore much more than the durable and potent Chicana. Melinda empowers the student to go deep enough to find their own revolutionary attitude. I have never experienced such compelling learning until I took her class. She also, gives wonderful feedback.

Professor Gandara is the reason I have continued my education. She breathes inspiration and honesty into the studies of the contemporary and past lives of the Chicana/o. I have taken all of her classes that are offered. Melinda Gandara is a secure stepping stone for anyone who wants an interesting learning journey. Take any of her classes!

Melinda Gandara is a professor that genuinely cares about her students in many ways: she offers feedback that is very helpful and kind. It is also clear that Professor Gandara cares about what she is teaching, her content is so interesting and often hits the heart strings. Take her for any classes she offers, she is the best!
Melinda is an amazing teacher she helps a lot and she gives you time if you need it.

I found my inspiration for Chicano History from her. She is inspirational and cares about learning. If you are having trouble she guides you and helps you really understand what the topic is all about. Best teacher I have had and at my age really to find someone so willing and able to help. Definitely an amazing instructor.

I loved Melinda! She doesn't grade too intensely so you are free to make mistakes and she will guide you in the right direction. I recommend flashcards or taking notes cause it's a lot of reading and lecture material. Very tech savvy, the online class was easy to navigate and she was always up for a Zoom call if needed.

SUCH A CUTE LADY. She is so passionate about what she teaches. Her class isn't hard but you will have to spend a lot of time on the lectures and your assignments. She's honestly the best professor I have ever had. She is just so sweet and kind. She really makes you learn the material. Love her!! Would recommend if you actually want to learn haha

This class was life-changing. It was completely outside my typical courses, and I felt like I grew so much as a human and a student from taking this class. It gets really political and shows you history from new perspectives. She is the sweetest, and I cannot recommend this class enough! Take this class!

Other than being caring and being very helpful, Prof. Gandara makes the class and subject enjoyable. This was an asynchronus course, we had online lecture videos, and we also had to read the textbook which was about Chicano artwork. Assignments are mainly online discussions which were not difficult. We did have a midterm and final exam.

Professor Melina is so awesome. It is clear from day 1 that she is extremely passionate about the subject, and she is extremely accessible via email (even late at night she will respond within minutes) She is patient and really just wants you to learn. To be fair she assigns a lot of reading but you can get by with minimal reading.

Prof Gandara was nothing short of incredible! Such a great, supportive professor - really cares about what you write, who you are, the material. Quite a bit of reading every week but it's 100% do-able. Loved her!!!

Interesting class and very passionate prof. The structure is not well defined. Lectures are great. She posts a TON of reading and videos in canvas with little instruction on how to approach it (60-100+ pages per day). Difficult because it is too much to reasonably study and read. I ended up only watching lectures/reading few articles and did well

Its always a gamble taking some professors as some of them can be difficult to work with, not the case with Mrs Gandara,this is my first time taking her class and its been a great experience so far! She provides great feedback and is always on top of getting back to you, she is flexible and will work with you! I would definitely take her again!

Most understanding professor. Offers plenty of extra credit. Allows all students to have a full page of notes for exams. Her goal is for her students to leave her class having learned something, not having memorized the material. Very eager and enthusiastic about everything she teaches. Passed by taking good notes during lectures and skim reading.

Took her 8 week course and it was extremely lecture heavy. Not sure if it was due to the time crunch, but youre left in the dark about your grade in the class. She does more exams/pop quizzes than in class assignments or homework which is not balanced imo. She is really passionate about the subject, and loves what she does and Overall shes nice.

Melinda is one of the best professors at City College. She truly cares about you and will definitely work with you if you reach out. Lectures are heavy but if you have a love for Chicano culture than there is no problem with sitting through the entire lecture.

Great professor, only thing that I dont like is that she shows a lot of favoritism to former students which makes other students feel like their opinions arent as valued since she mainly goes for students who think just like her.

A wonderful lady who truly cares about her students and their lives and educational success! Everyone in the class had had her before and took her again because she's awesome. Easy class, show up because a lot of the learning is visual. Taking anther one of her classes for sure.

Profe. Gandara is amazing, she is a great professor. She definitely looks out for her students, don't take her kindness for weakness. Any class in the Ethnic Studies field taught by her are awesome, she is passionate about what she teaches and not bias at all.

Professor Gandara is an exceptional scholar. She is exceedingly well-versed in her focus of study, has an unparalleled level of enthusiasm for Mexican culture, art, the importance of the principles of involvement in our community andand for the methodology of a democratic process of educating. I would not hesitate to recommend this professor!

SHe's AMAZING. sweetest lady ever. you can tell she loves what she does and is always willing to help you. She makes lectures interesting and offers a ton of extra credit opportunities. i would highly recommend her!

I love Melinda she is super sweet and genuine you can tell she loves her students and job, she is willing to help any students in and out of class. Easy to approach, she is great at giving feedback and responds to emails quickly. Melinda is an inspiration and opens so many doors for students. Lots of extra credit opportunities. Great reads in class

Professor Garard is amazing! I took his 289 and 288 classes in person over the past year. Do NOT take both courses in the same semester. There is a long research paper in each class (around 10-15 pages), but he gives you more than enough time to get it done. The textbook practice quizes r gonna be ur best friend for the midterm and final. Excellent

Such a great professor and a great class, you can tell he really cares. As long as you attend class regularly and stay on top of assignments you'll pass easily. There is one long paper that you should definitely look at starting early, other than that there is only a midterm and a final, both can be passed as long as you study. Best professor ever.

10/10 professor and class. It is a manageable work load with an extra credit discussion every week. One big paper but plenty of time to get it done. 3 exams that are just like the quizzes. Professor Gerard is so funny and you can tell he cares about his students and the content he is teaching.
I am a straight A student and this is the only class I did not pass. He was very rude to me and is not flexible AT ALL.

Prof Gerrard is absolutely hilarious! Funniest professor I've had. He cares for you and wants to help you succeed. His class is highly respected and the students are outgoing (comm class duhhh)! If u don't need this class don't take it tho, it is so so much work. 289 is easier than 288. In person is better than online too!

Hands down my fav professor I've ever had, genuinely the most charismatic man ever I loved coming to class and was so sad I had to take 288 online. TAKE 289 BEFORE 288. Both classes have very long research papers that you're prepared for throughout the semester, but I promise doing 289 first helps significantly with the big 288 paper.

Professor Garard is the best professor I have had at SBCC and teaches the content in a way that makes lectures enjoyable. He is super organized and will answer questions you ask very quickly. He loves teaching and it is clear through his knowledge on the subject. He is also hilarious and very cheeky, overall highly recommended!!

I am a visual learner and his lectures are great for retaining information. The notes in class work as a resource for the weekly 10 point open note quizzes. He gives lots of feedback on papers. He also gives extra credit opportunities every week for both classes. As long as you show up to class and take notes there should be no problem getting an A

Professor Garard is great! He is engaging and is really knowledgeable in his craft.The structure of the class is more traditional, there are 2 heavy papers and a scantron mid-term/final. If you plan on transferring to UCSB for comm, these classes will properly prepare you. You will get out what you put in.

He is such a nice professor! I took both his 289 and 288 classes and really enjoyed them. He makes lectures very interactive which is fun and seems pretty passionate about what he is teaching. Fair amount of reading and work for both classes but he is super organized and helpful along the way. If you put in the work, you will succeed!

Although it was a hard class, Professor Garard aims to prepare his students for UC classes. The class is mainly just writing 3 essays, and a 15 page term paper towards the end, so get ready to write a lot. He was an amazing professor and cared about his students learning the material, I suggest his class for those wanting to transfer to a UC.
Learned so much! well organized class

He knows his stuff. He has two exams, both pretty difficult and are my only complaint as some of the questions aren't worded very well and the answers are pretty similar. Besides that, you write 3 2 page logs about a comm theory as well as a long final paper that takes a while.

Professor Garad is one of the best teachers at SBCC. He truly cares about his students and wants them to do well. He is a tough grader but gives lots of feedback in how to improve. He also offers extra credit every week. His lectures are great he's really funny which make them enjoyable. The class is very manageable and is set up for you success!

Professor Garard was amazing this semester!!! Lots of lectures but made them enjoyable and something I actually looked forward to. The class may seem intimidating at first but he does a great job explaining and organizing all of the topics. He also offers extra credit each week which is pretty easy. Would highly recommend his class!

Garard is funny, caring, and smart. He is a great teacher and gives great lectures. He is a tough grader on papers and his tests are hard but as long as you study and don't procrastinate, you will succeed. He is also very understanding about absences and offers a live zoom every class. Also extra credit every week

Professor Darin is hilarious and truly cares about student success. His specific teaching style takes some adjusting, but using resources like the free textbook, study sessions, and tools like Unstuck AI can go a long way. Overall, a rewarding class if you put in the effort.
Great professor, I like his lectures. He makes them pretty interesting to listen to. Only thing, if you're online, everything is heavily proctored so you really have to study for the big exams.
BEST TEACHER EVER!!!!! My favorite SBCC teacher, so funny, kind, and actually puts effort into grading.

Absolutely amazing professor! Not only amazing academically, but also as a human. Super personable and friendly. Willing to work with his students in order to help them succeed. Overall, one of if not my favorite professor of all time. TAKE HIS CLASSES. I took both his comm 289 and comm 288 and they were both amazing!

A great professor. Not only as a human being but as a academic as well. I didnt use enough enough effort in his class. But that is completely on me. If you try hard and put in the effort you will succeed
If you can find an alternative elsewhere please save yourself.

Professor Garard was a tough grader, but I like that in teachers. He gave great feedback, made it really clear what he wanted and how my essays and theory logs could be improved. If you can take his feedback and adapt, your writing and analysis of communication theories will improve. Great class, smart teacher, and he's funny. 10/10 recommend

This was one of the more challenging classes i took at sbcc. However he was one of my favorite teachers the hard work made this class most rewarding. He is a great teacher if your not doing well it prob is because your not attending office hours and getting the help you need. The office hours he had made a ton of difference. He gives good feedback

I dont understand how people are giving him such good ratings, he is one of the worst professors that I have ever had. Expects you to understand everything from his lectures right away and doesn't give good feedback to help you succeed. 300-point papers with little to no help? No thanks, avoid this class if you can.

I'm taking Professor Garard's COMM288 and COMM289 courses at the same time and he is an excellent professor. He offers extra credit every week, is very clear with his grading criteria and genuinely wants his students to succeed. Although attendance isn't required, there is a sign-in sheet and the lectures are better in person. Also records lecture

Great professor, I understand why people loved his class but there was clear favoritism and it was very apparent. If you are looking for an easy A, I would look for a different class. This class will take up a significant amount of your time and there were 300-point papers, not including the midterm or final.

Professor Garard is an excellent professor in the Comm. department. His lectures are engaging and informational. Grading is based on quizzes, logs (like mini-essays), a term paper, and two exams. He gives excellent feedback on all papers and assignments. There is also extra credit available.

Although I had to take this course to transfer, it was not an easy course. A lot of studying is necessary to do well on exams, so 100/10 recommend the self-help quizzes!!! For the papers, please visit the tutors. It seemed as though he expected us to have top of the line essays, but he is a tough grader as is, so you have to really work.

Dr. Garard is one of the best. Even though I did end up getting a C mainly because my log assignments and theory analysis paper were not developed enough, I learned so much. All I can say is do every extra credit discussion, start your theory analysis paper early, and use the self help quizzes to study for the midterm and final FOR SURE!
Awesome teacher! He really cares about his students engagement with the class. He also provides meditation guides which help calm anxious feelings.
Professor Garcia is caring guy and he knows how to make lectures engaging. I highly recommend him!

She is not a good teacher. I am still waiting for my assignments to be graded, which I've emailed her multiple times about. She is extremely unorganized, and her canvas/assignments page was a mess and confusing. If u value your GPA, do not take this class.

The bottom line is she isn't a good teacher, but her class isn't impossible. She slacks on grading and gives very unclear instructions. The work we had to do wasn't necessarily hard; it was her lack of organization and overall teaching skills that made it difficult. Don't take her class if im keeping it a buck.

She is just very confused. She forgets assignments, instructions, and to grade things all the time. I have talked to her AT LEAST 6 times about the same assignment and it still hasn't been graded. In class you learn basically nothing and get confusing and unclear instructions on an assignment that may be due that night or not due at all.

Extremely unorganized, had to send her multiple emails to grade assignments from 3 months ago. tanked my GPA, I get GOOD grades, she harshly graded my work and refused to let me redo my essays because it was 'past the make-up do date" but that's because she took THREE MONTHS TO GRADE MY WORK. Twice she posted assignments 2 days before they were due

Absolutely baffling experience... a masterclass in confusion. Instructions were either nonexistent or written in such broken English that deciphering them felt like a second assignment. Genuinely don't understand how she's employed. If you value your GPA or your sanity, avoid at all costs.

Professor has absolutely no structure in the class and attending her lectures are useless. I always leave the class confused and she grades harshly if you don't meet her nonexistent requirements. Class felt like I was back in high school, she puts a big emphasis on bonding with another students. Big group project and public speaking involved.

I took English 111 with her at SBCC. After going to every class I was leaving more confused about what assignment we are doing then I would be if I didn't go to class. Assignments would be introduced on top of one another with no clear due date.There is absolutely NO structure, rubric, or instructions but there is VERY harsh grading. Don't take!!!!

She is such a sweetheart! I love her personality and she is a really empathetic and kind person. That said, I am not the type of person that does well with absolutely no structure. I was confused every single assignment and she will give low grades if the assignments do not meet her nonexistent descriptions. If you need any structure, just don't.

Super sweet lady- not organized at all. I thought I'd finished the final three times and then she assigned another one. Lots of writing but she was generous with her grades so I'm happy with how the class ended! Overall not my first choice because of how disorganized the class was but at least I'm done with english haha :)

ENG111 with Professor Gavaskar was easily my favorite class at SBCC. This class was fun, and by taking advantage of class time I found a good balance with a full course load. After this class, I have used Professor Gavaskar's lessons in my other classes and have become very well-rounded and have a different outlook on learning. Thank you Gavaskar!

Dr. Gavaskar is understanding and not a tough grader, even if you don't understand the instructions 100%. If you have questions, just ask; she replies quickly. If you want to improve your writing, I suggest taking her course, as the feedback, writing, and reading analysis will prepare you. (Tip: Don't overthink or doubt yourself; you can do it.)

Professor Gavaskar is a nice and caring person don't even get me wrong. However, I felt like she didn't have structure in the class. we'd be working on one assignment and then she'd introduce a new assignment with only a few instructions on what to do. Besides that, she is a good instructor and will give good feedback on improving.

Vandana claims to be easy which she can be to an extent. Horrible instruction. Doesn't give much guidance then grades so harsh. Attending class is so pointless because she gives very little instruction as to what we are even working on. Is a sweet person not much of a teacher. Would warn other to never take her class.

She is very sympathetic to late work and willing to work through it if instructions are unclear. However, the course is VERY disorganized. There were a lot of times I had to message her for clarification or had to redo assignments because they didn't fall within her unwritten expectations.

Instructions for assignments were very vague/not clear, yet she'll grade you harshly for not responding exactly how she wants you to. Taking the online class was also a mistake, as there are group projects where it's extremely hard to get ahold of other classmates, yet you'll receive a low grade for not successfully working together with them.

I took her ENG111. Her assignments can have confusing guidelines but she is extremely flexible with deadlines and you can redo anything. The classes are a little bit useless at times but participation definitely matters to her.

took her ENGL 110/111 course this year. The work load is the same difficulty as a Highschool course but with much more guidance. Her wide selection of work topics help you choose to work in areas you are stronger in which takes the stress of the workload off. She truly cares about all our well being and i 100% recommend taking her courses.

Vandana is very confusing and unclear about her assignments. I took her thinking it would be an easy A but it's not! Her classes are pointless, only talk in small groups the whole time (reminds me of high school) and if you don't go she will form a vendetta towards you. Don't listen to the rate my professor reviews she is not an easy A!

She is so sweet and validating and shows true interested in you and your views. Every morning, professor Gavaskar enters with a smile on her face and loves to chat / engage in friendly banter. If you are just entering college and will be taking english110, I highly recommend her class, coursework is light and much of it is completed during class.

Professor Vandana Gavaskar is one of the best professors at SBCC. She is very understanding and inspiring towards her students and about English 110. I am taking her English 111 class next semester and am super excited. Please take her, she is the besr!!!!

Look I think Sally is overrated. I took both her Gen Chem classes & passed. But overall there's nothing special for her. Her lectures don't give to much contents to understand. There's no graded hw. She's kind of mean. FWY doing the problems 5 times will not give you an A. It doesn't help by memorizing them every time. Very disappointed teacher.
I'm writing this review more than 10 years since I took the class. She is the one professor that I will always remember and cherish.

I've already graduated from sbcc. I've took chemistry right up there to o chem. I took Sally's Chemistry class. Honestly Sally isn't that great. Just because you do the problems five times doesn't mean that you're going to get an A. You're basically memorizing the problems. Her lectures are too simplistic and you can easily fail the class.

Sally is very charismatic, she manages to make the class interesting and enjoyable, it's clear she understands and loves chemistry. She tells you day one, do the problems 5 times... seriously though, do it. Chemistry is complicated and it's physically impossible to get all the info from lecture. But as far as chemistry goes, these classes are easy.

Honestly, Sally doesnt live up to the hype if youre not one of her favorites. She only pays attention to her top 4 favorite students, and the rest of the students she is passive aggressive and hostile towards. Also, I dont think shes a great teacher. She throws concepts at you without thoroughly explaining them.

The most amazing professor that I had the opportunity to learn from. She is hilarious, kind, caring, and unbelievably smart. Put in the effort and you'll excel. I had no idea how much I enjoyed chemistry until I took this sequence with her. I am so sad that I won't have her for orgo.

If you have to take chem I would really recommend her for 155. She makes it interested and has some good humor. Chem is hard but she tries to make it doable for most. definitely have to do the problems at least 3 times

Do the homework problems five times. Literally. As you go along, cross off the ones that are super easy and just do repeats of the hard ones. If you start to memorize the problems, do other ones in the book. READ THE BOOK TOO. Actually understand what you are doing at a basic level. DONT SCREW YOURSELF OVER EARLY ON!!

I took genchem series with Sally & both classes were absolutely amazing 5/5stars. However, when I took ochem with Sally, I felt so lost. I think half to blame was the material, but the other half may have been bc she last minute picked up the class and wasn't as prepped for it as she was for genchem. I failed the class, but plan to retake it w her.

Sally's tests are tough and she really expects a high standard out of her students. I worked harder in this class than any other I've taken and still barely made the A. She is however extremely fair and always stays late after class to address questions about the material and is very welcoming during office hours. I understand the material now.

Dr. Ghizzoni is truly an amazing professor. There is plenty of material to cover and LOTS of reading, but if one follows her directions on how to prepare for her tests, the results will speak for themselves. Her lectures are very profound, yet still very interesting. As long as you dedicate enough time to the work, you'll get AT LEAST a B.

Heard great things from her 155/156 students. This was her 1st year teaching ochem and it was horrible. Average grades on exams were 60-65% (tests are your grade) & she does not curve. Touches on a few concepts in class, but you're required to know everything from the text. She extended the amount of B's in her class by curving C's 5%.

College Chemistry is no joke! I am a 3.5 gpa student who works hard. Sally is a great person and teacher. The class itself requires a lot of work if you dont have a great background in chem. This class took over my life bc I was playing catch up, don't be me. Do the problems @ least 3 times! Read! Make study guides! attend lect as review&offic hrs.

Sally is amazing. I would not have been able to pass these two courses if it weren't for her. Don't get me wrong, these were damn hard classes, but she made it easIER to comprehend! She expects you to do a TON of problems from the book (not graded) but if you don't do them, you won't pass. She also gives extra credit right on the exams.

Coming from 155 with Horowitz, Sally was comparable but a bit more clear in her lecturing. She's quite friendly and is very willing to help her students. At times I would lose points on exams for questionable reasons but most of the time she grades fairly and includes many extra credit questions. I enjoyed the class overall, and would recommend it!

This class basically took over my life but I must say Sally is the way to go if you need this course. I learned a lot and she is there for you if you need the help. Get ready to read and do the problems. Read ahead and DO NOT FALL BEHIND. She is teaching the subject yet she even earned a B in this course so get ready to work.

Over all, I learned most/all of the material in this class from reading the book and studying on my own. I would suggest reading the chapter before you go into lecture because if you don't you may get lost in class quickly. Do not expect to be taught the entire chapter from lecture, you have to put a lot of your own work into passing this class!

Sally is amazing I never even knew i had an interest in chemistry until I took her class. She is really helpful as well. In order to pass the class you HAVE to study doing the recommend problems 3-5 times. She really cares about her students success not just in her class but in general. Inviting to all questions.

Sally can defiantly be harsh sometimes, but get on her good side and she will love you forever. If you work hard, and don't ask stupid questions she will love you. Some days are good some days are bad. Overall I really liked her and we had some good conversations. Seems a little too relaxed at times during lecture, but over all is pretty good.

its overall a good class, lectures are kinda useless, she just explains some concepts, but for the most part you have to read the book. also try to find a friend who took this class before as she recycles 95% of the question from last year(usually two semesters ago). some them same exact numbers too.

Sally is very nice and really wants you to do well and get a good grade. However, I felt her lectures were kind of pointless, everything is based off the book. The problems are in the book and exams are based off the problems. Exams are multiple choice! She doesn't really have office hours or post anything grade wise online.

Honestly, I didn't learn much from lecture, I learned it all from the book or Khan Academy. The exams are based off of the hw, so just make sure you have a thorough understanding of everything in the hw and you'll be fine.

she gives us lectures which don't really apply to the exams she gives. Also in some chapters the only way to learn the steps for the problem or ANY of the formulas is by looking in the solutions manual (which she says not to do at first). And this material is nowhere in the book. Good luck she is the worst professor I have had in college.

Sally is great. Makes everything easy to understand and is always available for help outside class. She says do the homework multiple times and she means it. If you do the homework and put effort into the labs/prelabs then it is very possible to get an A.

Sally is an amazing Chem prof. She delivers the material in a very clear straight forward way, and gives you every possible advantage eg, examples of test questions, office hours, strategies for success. That being said it is a difficult class and doing the homework multiple times is required to do well.

Sally is the best chemistry professor at this school. She is absolutely the reason why I changed my major to chemistry. Obviously the material is hard, and the course requires a ridiculous amount of dedication but she is one of the hardest working, intelligent, organized, and helpful woman probably in the entire school.

I took both 155 and 156 with Sally. Sally is a great teacher who is very clear about what she expects on her exams. I personally did all problem sets twice and got a high A. I personally didn't enjoy the lab portion of the class (except from making soap...), but if you do the work you will do fine. Sally gives a lot of extra credit on the exams.

First time I took this class I received a D, but it was completely my fault. The tests are written from the homework she gives you and are very fair. She even admits that she didn't do well her first chemistry class. Her rule is do the homework 5 times to get an A. Lots of work, but an A is not out of reach at all! She offers free tutoring as well.

Great professor. Genuinely cares about students and their success and is very helpful in office hours. While lecture is helpful in solidifying material, tests are based off of homework from the textbook. Highly recommended, very passionate about the subject.

Great teacher. Sally loves teaching and is always there to help, even with other classes. Just take good notes, including what she says..they are the conceptual questions on the test. challenging but A is there if you take good notes.
Please take his class. He truly is an inspiration. You will definitely intake his lecture. 10/10 recommended!
Amazing human being! Noel is truly a saint doing Gods work. If you need a professor who truly cares about your success and future, Noel is that guy
Good. Does not make anything more difficult than it needs to be. Easy A. Just do the work for the most part.
Great professor! Really cares about his students and their well-being. Also, lovely to look at!

He is a great inspiration. Since taking his class he has always been available to me for guidance and encouragement. Even after succeeding in his class, professor Gomez has remained concerned for my educational well being.

Not only is he a great professor, he's an amazing human being. He genuinely cares about your success as a student. He is very approachable and will go above and beyond to help any of his students. I will definitely take his class again!
Professor Gomez was very helpful and clear about homework. Do the work and you will pass, easy as that.
Great professor IS ALWAYS on TIME!! Need help just ask and you got it. As long as you do your homework you should pass. Tests are easy again if you do homework!
Always late to teach his class. Would not recommend if you are trying to actually learn college success skills.

I had her for Data Science "for all". I was told to take this because the stats class was full because its "similar" but its not. Doesn't go over any basic coding information I was lost on day 1. She works with another teacher, they can't agree on grading and testing, harsh graders + assign way too much work for no reason. Do not take this class.

Amazing. I've taken her for a few classes and she's always been great. Her work with other groups around campus has also been great, and she's been helpful for me helping me think about other classes to take as well. She seems to really care and also has relevant material (esp. in data science) to real life.

Nathalie Guebels is incredibly understanding and a gifted professor. The projects are fun and relevant, and the exams test your ability to write code rather than your ability to study technical questions from a book. She is a fantastic professor to get to know and is your biggest ally in going on to be a future success at a 4-year university.

Nathalie is a very good and kind professor who teaches intro to programming very well. It is a comfortable class for beginners and lots of help and advice is available. The lectures can be quite long but are valuable, and it pays off if you pay attention.

The prof is really nice, she gave us the option to use up to 5 pages of notes during the exams (which is way more than you need), she does post a lot of extra credits, and could get your average pass 100% easily. Her lecture is pretty fun I'd say.

I've had Professor Guebles for two CS classes and had the exact same experience. She responds to emails late most of the time despite the syllabus saying she will reply in around 24 hours. She uses a grader to automatically give you points, so no partial credit. The last 2 assignments are worth lots of points and are extremely difficult.
She a great professor so as long as you pay attention and do the work your set to pass.

Nathalie is probably the nicest person at SBCC. She is a great teacher and a great mentor. I dropped because of medical reasons, but I wish I could have stayed for the whole CS137 with Nathalie because I was really enjoying her class and her teaching style.

Prof Guebels is a very nice lady and teaches well and covers a good amount of lecture material in good time. Class is also fairly enjoyable to go to, has in class activities. Assignments can be pretty hard for first time programmers. Otherwise not too bad. Lectures can be boring in any subject, but you have to cover the material.

If you're taking CS 104, take Nathalie's class. The class consists of a power point and some examples uses of the code you're learning that week. Then you move onto a lab. Then HW consists of an online quiz and either one or two small projects that are easier than the labs. The HW sometimes comes with an extra-credit option. Don't skip class.

Easy A, but the lectures were very dry. I came from a strong background of programming, having taken Java, Data Structures, C++ and plenty of other classes. My recommendation to all of you is to use her example programs on Canvas and you shouldn't struggle with any of the assignments. Show up to class for the in-class assignments.

It's an easy class, the lectures can sometimes be confusing, but that's because C can be confusing. You have a lot of time to work on your projects and they're not bad at all. She gives a lot of extra credit and is always sharing opportunities with students. She's pretty available. You can tell Natalie really cares for her students' success.

I wish there was more than a 350 character limit because I could go on for days about how great she is. From great lectures to tons of extra credit opportunities, to telling me about internship opportunities and hackathons specific to my skill level, she really does the most to help her students succeed. Take her class! She's amazing!

The final exam was so tricky that I almost failed it. She does not understand the average level of student knowledge. The lab work was very difficult and time consuming. She was working for the commercial company before and her teaching is similar to PowerPoint presentation at work meetings.

Nathalie clearly knows what she's talking about. It's just the whole powerpoint lecture format doesn't seem to suit the students very well and is rather dull. Otherwise good teacher, straightforward tests and assignments.

The class was boring. She just read the power point and sometimes not clear what she was talking. The tests were tricky. I have taken C++ before so I still got an A+. If you don't know any programming language (JAVA/C++/OO programming) before, do not take her class.
I really enjoyed this class. The projects and tests were difficult but I learned much.

Not a bad professor. Lectures are pretty dull, she just goes over slides. Her tests are completely open-book/note, and not too hard. Expect to spend a lot of time on lab assignments. You'll feel overwhelmed at first if you don't have much CS experience, but stick to it and the course becomes manageable.

One of the worst professors I have ever had at SBCC. She literally offered zero help to us last semester. I had some serious health issues so luckily I dropped the class. Her tests are brutal and are designed to purposely trick you. I wouldn't take her unless you have a solid foundation for C Programming, so if you are beginner, stay away!
So sweet and really great at explaining math, especially to people like me for whom it does not come naturally! Very fair and understanding. If you need your math credit, take her class!

Really great professor. I have taken stats before and had a horrible time, so I was dreading this class, but she is great at teaching it. She explains everything well. There are a lot of low-stakes quizzes, and like 6 big tests, but it actually really helped me understand all the material. She is super nice, and clearly cares a LOT about students.
What the actual f#@$

Do NOT take this class if you are planning to take 4+ classes at the same time! Wonderful content, easy to learn, but soooo much homework. She expects a 12-14 hour weekly commitment (including class), so DO NOT TAKE IF YOU WILL BE BUSY!!!! She's very kind and understanding, but don't expect leniency with deadlines. Very high-quality content.
Horrific
Seriously the WORST teacher I have ever had

This class was a huge time commitment but it was worth it. Lots of easy homework that really helps you understand the class and makes the tests super easy. The sweetest woman ever and will help you with anything you need. I suck at math and she explained stats in such a fantastic way that it finally clicked for me.
Notes can be a little confusing but overall her class was great for statistics you just need to put in effort and you will get a good grade.

She's a really good professor and her work is easy but I highly recommend you set time to do the notes and really focus on every little thing she says and ask her for help and then for wiely first buy it and then ask her for help she has notes how to access it but just go to her for help and then for wiely just have you notes out while you do it

Pam is a good teacher with an interesting class structure. I spent a lot of time on homework and remote note-taking for this course but it made me learn that material well, statistics is not super intuitive! She is very quick at responding to emails and is genuinely a kind person.

1/2 online 1/2 in person class, in class we mostly only did tests which was a little annoying as learning math IN PERSON is why I took this class. Quizzes each week, sometimes group quizzes. Written homework isn't checked but some question are on quizzes so.. Online Homework too, lets you use a notecard on test/quiz 10 points EC on finalopportunity
Professor was fair and the class was pretty easy to follow.

Not bad as long as you keep up with the weekly quizzes. Zoom office hours helped me to understand some of the tougher financial concepts in the course. I was not good with excel but the Prof worked through it with me.
Best instructor! Helpful when you have questions and workload isn't bad. Coursework is easy to follow and as long as you do your discussions, assignments and quizzes, you'll get a good grade.

The professor is great. Homework (e.g., discussions, readings, chapter tests, and one assignment each week) are not hard. The course is straight forward just do what it asks, and you'll have an A in the class. One con about the professor is that he takes forever to respond to emails. Overall, an easy course with lots of good information.
Would 100% take him again - easy A.
Helped point me on the right real estate career path and very thankful for that…. Easy grade as long as you turn in your homework.
Online format is nice- work at your own pace. But only graded on a handful of things. Quizzes and discussions are worth half of the grade. Both only took me 30 minutes a week to complete cake walk.

So much writing and reading. I can understand the reading but he has you write so much to the point where I feel I did not learn as much as I could have because I was so stressed out trying to fill in my discussions and papers with nonsense filler words because of his ridiculous word count. Also, I email him and he just ignored me.
Helpful but you need to know excel. I didn't know how to type formulas which made it tough. The course was easy other then that aspect. Multiple choice tests.

The professor was very knowledgeable about the field and is willing to give extra advice to get ahead in the real estate profession. I recommend taking him and ask good questions. Very helpful professor!
Would take again. Class was ok.
Easy Grade.
Good class, good professor, easy units.
Very fair professor. Gives opportunity to make up assignments. Online class, but his picture was hott. Wish he was on campus teaching other classes!

NO LECTURES! You basically teach yourself & wait for someone who didn't teach you a single thing to grade it. WORST Real Estate Professor at SBCC!!! Unfair grading & doesn't know how to phrase questions. Not accessible! Very close minded! Strict & not lenient. Expects so much when people have jobs or other classes. This school needs to start firing

Faye Hampton is a very unfair teacher and for just a little mistake you will lose the whole point of your assignment. I remember that for one assignment she thought that I did not put the address right, but I did, and I explained to her where was the address in the assignment through an email, but she ignored my email and gave me zero out of ten.
She was really nice and caring I Liked taking this class with this teacher

I took this class in the summer. The professor gave a lot of good videos, offered extra credit, and always gave great advice and feedback on assignments. There were discussions, and chapter quizzes, and the terminology test was a little challenging. Overall, the class was an awesome environment to be in.

I have to tell you that this professor is hard on assignments! Strict guidelines to follow, and she does not give good feedback. I felt discouraged every time my assignments were graded with the comments she has made. And I'm not the only one, the class average is failing. If you upload your assignments/work and she has to download it? Nope, 0 pts.

Syllabus can be changed on the fly, assignments are graded too harsh, assignments are hard to turn in as the professor requires their own way of turning assignments in. Overall, I would not take this course with this professor again.

As far as the class goes you pretty much run the whole thing yourself and you have to be on top of grades because she doesn't proof read any of her tests and you will often get marked wrong when your answer was correct. Ironically she is a very tough grader when I comes to writing assignments and she will mark you down for a simple mistake.

An unforgettable learning experience & exceptionally engaging, despite it being online due to covid. Professor Hampton has a great deal of professional experience in real estate and related fields, illustrated by the vast knowledge she shares within the modules. Shes thorough in explaining complex concepts and is fair in grading. Highly recommend!
Take her!! She's so amazing! Helps you out a lot and is very good at getting back to you. She gives extra credit if you need it.
Lynda cares about her students and their success. Do the online homework read the chapters and study and you will get an A. She is a great teacher.
Solid professor, well organized class, can't go wrong. Professor Hampton's expectations are clear, reasonable, and she gives good feedback. Great class if you want to go into medicine.

If you are not good at online classes DO NOT take medical term at SBCC. Lynda is not helpful at all either. I have to retake this class because I will not pass. there is a quiz every week. The chapters range from 10-40 also, and sometime you have to do two chapters each week. IF you teaching yourself works for you, take the class but If not do not

Mrs. Hampton was great! Any question you have, she answers. She is EXTREMELY sweet and she cares about her students. Grades are earned over a variety of assignments (easy) and tests (you should study). So if you bomb a test, it won't KILL your grade. She is fair and gives you a second chance (in some cases) if you make a mistake. Great professor!
Very nice teacher. I never had any problems in her class. Graded fairly and accurately. Great teacher!
Very easy class online. You use the book and the quizes are easy and are based off the chapters. She is a very nice teacher and she gets back to you asap..

Don is amazing. He knows so much and his teaching style is great. he is laid back and funny, and he will help you as much as possible. He will answer all your questions and is not condescending at all. If you get the chance to take his class, do it. He will also take you on great field trips. He's the type of guy who will just chill with his class.

If you have a chance to take Don and Karen's class, do it! You will leave feeling so inspired, and also so much more prepared, to go work in the field of ecological restoration. Once-in-a-lifetime weekend field trips to the Channel Islands, anyone? Some students are skating by but most are super passionate, you'll get a lot if you're one of them.
Super chill dude who likes to get dirty. Brings his students to his ranch for field work. Really fun guy who loves plants and grow food and who cares about the earth. TAKE HIM!!

Literally the best class ever. Learned so much and the workload was completely manageable. I couldn't make the zoom meetings and met with the tutor instead. There is the asynchronous option to get credit, but meeting with the tutor is so much more helpful. Hashimoto is super accessible and accommodating. Plus she accepts basically all late work.

Hashimoto-sensei is one of the best professors of any topic. Her class is the best language course I've ever taken. Her Zoom speaking practices are the best, and she is clearly interested in you learning Japanese in a supportive, immersive environment above all else. She is willing to put in the time to make sure you really understand Japanese.

Hashimoto-sensei is amazing! She really cares and as long as you do the homework/speaking practices you will receive credit. There is a lot to learn but doing the speaking practices makes a huge difference for remembering everything. If you want to learn Japanese take this class! She's the best.
She is amazing and show great care and attention to everyone. My wife and I took all 4 JPN100 courses and was sad to not see her after. Best professor I have ever had.

Hashimoto-sensei is one of the best professors that I've had at any college. She truly cares about her students and helps everyone succeed at Japanese. Her biggest strength is in her Zoom speaking practice where everyone gets a chance to practice speaking Japanese with her. She has the patience of a saint! I have zero complaints.

I had Hashimoto Sensei for Jpn 101-102 and she was wonderful!!!! She's super sweet and patient/will give great help if you're struggling with anything. My only advice if you're taking one of her classes is to manage your time well!! Work can stack up really fast if you fall behind...so try your best to keep on top of it!! Epic teacher!!!!!!!

Hashimoto sensei is an amazing and sweet teacher. You definitely have to study outside of class, but that's the case for every language class in life. She is willing to stay after class to go over things that confuse you- and also offers tutors outside of class! I highly recommend her, and you won't be disappointed with her class :)

Had Hashimoto Sensee Japanese 102-104. If you take her class for more than one semester, you will see that the class becomes sort of a family. If you are shy, get ready for breakout rooms. This class has allowed me to come out of my shell and sharpen my thinking skills. I will miss this class dearly. My best experience at SBCC.

Hashimoto is super nice and considerate. If you run into online problems she's very understanding and does her best to help. JAP 102 is a big jump from JAP 101, but she provides a lot of helpful resources and is always willing to answer questions. Very funny and easy to talk to. Would take again! But don't sign up if you don't feel ready/prepared!

I've completed Jap101 with her and I felt so comfortable studying with Ms. Hashimoto, hence I joined Jap102. She was extremely helpful to the students, totally devoted and would never judge her students' speaking ability. Every quizz/HW is graded with feedback. Provides lecture videos before Zoom, but always welcome to ask questions on Zoom.

(took for 103 and 104 but RMP only gives options for 101 and 102) Wants to propel you into a better understanding of Japanese above all things. If you can't make the lectures, she lets you do it with a tutor during different hours, or even upload an mp3 of yourself doing the speaking exercises, though doing it with a tutor/classmate is preferred.

I normally don't bother to rate my professor but Professor Hashimoto is one of the kindest, most understanding and without a doubt the overall best professor I've ever had. She's just excited to help you learn above all else, and though the course requires effort it's not grueling at all. I would 100% suggest you take her.

Hashimoto Sensei is one of my favorite professors I've ever had! It is very important to attend class because she covers so much, but her lectures are very clear and easy to understand. The subject itself is very hard, but if you participate in class and pay attention you will be fine. She is very kind, funny, and understanding!

Kumi is very kind and does a great job structuring her classes to ensure a good comprehensive review of all the important material. You must attend class to earn credit, very important! Commit to the learning process and homework and you will have a decent time getting an A. The class moves fast, so don't slack!
She's very kind and very patient -- helps you with everything and explains thoroughly. Definitely go to office hours if you want that extra practice and study the vocab and grammar!

Hashimoto sensei is the most fun and engaging teacher in the Japanese department. Her PowerPoint slides are always organized and easy to understand. Don't be afraid to say the wrong thing because participation is the key to doing well in this class. Japanese isn't just an easy A , so study and do all the homework! Take this class with a friend!

She is the most adorable human being in the world. Teaching a language is hard and she has figured out how to do it super easy. Also helps that she cares so much about her students doing well in the class, and she always comes in with a smile on her face and a good story to tell. Her tutors are also always great. Please take her class. She is awsom

Even though in JAPN102 we have to learn a lot of new grammar, Hashimoto sensei always makes sure that everyone in the class understand before moving onto other sections. Class is a bit boring if you're not into Japanese, but we have a lot of speaking practice with partner. Everything is the same as the syllabus, so if you follow it you should pass.

Kumi (Hashimoto sensei) is great. There are a billion assignments throughout the semester but honestly it's the only way to learn a language. She's a very fair grader (do your work, show up and you'll pass), doesn't ask anything more than what's expected (great review sheets) and is very helpful. I would take a class with her again.

Class assignments are due at random times (ex 6pm). Missing one assignment will tank your grade. Assignments aren't graded for a couple weeks after the due date. Very few assignments. NO LATE WORK accepted, even with the weird due time. Read the description of all assignments, sometimes assignment will close before due date. Work closed till weekof

This is such a simple class but I have learned a lot throughout the way. All she really cares about is meeting the requirements for assignments so she is an easy graded. She is very easy to talk to and I would recommend this class to anyone who wants a good professor and an easy course.

I really like ms dee. She is one of my favorite professors out of the whole college. She is very kind, funny and cares for her students. When I am with her, I get this good vibe out of her and it makes me feel more better about myself from being with her. I highly recommend taking her unless you want to deal with a rude person like Kathy King..
Shes great!! Easy class and super interesting. Few projects and the ones she does assign, are very fun. I wish I had her for every class!

Super easy class. You put in the work, make a solid effort and youll pass. The tests/assignments are what impact your grade so really make an effort. Add in quotes and elaborate to elongate your work and youll be fine (as long as you include credible evidence). None of my assignments were graded until a little over halfway through the course.

She doesn't respond to emails. No late work accepted, no matter what. Assignments are worth a lot of points. Very lengthy writing assignments. Assignments aren't due at 11:59 she just chooses random times. She can see very time you log in and participate.

Ms. D is AMAZING. Her class is really interesting, yet also easy if you show up and listen. We need more professors like Ms. D. This can be a very personal class, as we discuss childhood a lot. She made her class a safe place for everyone who wanted to open up about themselves (as this is a class on childhood). I love her so much!

She was great for the online course!There weren't really lectures, just things to read that she wrote about concepts. Textbook reading was a little heavy b/c the textbook font is small, but start early so it's manageable. Assignments consisted of weekly discussions,a couple of essays,and a few projects. Assessments were written and straightforward.
This class consists of essays given throughout the course. Materials for these essays come directly from the textbook. Very informative course if you read assigned chapters of the textbook.

I love her, best teacher I have had at SBCC. I was unsure about the route I would be taking my second year and decided to take her ECE 120 class kind of for fun and kind of for my major and was the best choice. She is very accessible through email, since my class was online and I am sure she is just as accessible on campus as well.

Dee is an overall great educator. She takes the time to truly engage with her students but she also does a great job building connections with her students. That by far, to me as a future educator, is by far the most important aspect a teacher can have. I really like her. Her assignments are easy.

I love Ms. D!!!! She's been my favorite professor in my four years of taking SBCC classes. She is hilarious and cares a lot about every student. She is an easy grader, as long as she sees you at least tried your best. I would love to take more classes with her, however it doesn't look like she has many this year. Take Child, Family and Community.

my very first professor of college and I am so grateful to have taken 3 classes with her so far. She is the best. I really recommend ECE 102 to anyone even if you are not interested in early childhood education. She makes it interesting and fun and you learn a lot about yourself and the others around you. Overall she is great and such a young soul

Took this class during summer session. Super easy A as long as you do all the work! If you miss one assignment it will hurt your score. Assignments were not difficult. You could finish all the weeks assignments in less than 5 hours if you really wanted to. Definitely take if you are trying to fill up credits.

Ms D is such an awesome and inspiring teacher. You can tell that she loves each of her students and what she teaches. She took time to talk to each person and jokes around but you definitely learn in each class. I took her Monday only class which was 3 hr long and it felt like 30 min because that's how fun and interesting she made it. LOVE YOU MS D
Super fun and easy class
Loved this class. Super easy
My only complain is that she was not clear enough about the content of the essays. She was vey kind and the overall class wasn't very hard at all. No test! which is amazing.
Just all around amazing. An angel on earth, really lucked out with this one. Definitely recommend taking her class.

She is a fantastic teacher and is very loving, caring, and easy to talk to. Her 3 hour classes go by so quickly because she is so interactive, her stories are hilarious, and the classroom is so tight knit. It's just an overall great class I highly recommend.

Professor Devona is a truly amazing professor who genuinely cares about her students. She is funny and sheds light on real world problems. The class I took from her was three hours long at night but it only felt like an hour and it was my favorite class. Just be sure to keep up on your grade and that it's up to date and you're not missing anything

Devona is my FAVORITE professor I've ever had. I'm not even an ECE major & Im considering taking another course of hers that I don't even need. She's absolutely hilarious and really wants to be friends with her students. This class is an easy A and it's super fun. I cannot recommend taking Devona enough- take her class!!!! you will not regret it.
Over all I enjoyed music appreciation. His class was test heavy. Make sure to study good for all the vocabulary words and listening pieces everyday. The music concerts were also fun too.

Professor Heidner has a wonderful passion for what he teaches. I have to admit some of his humor is cringe-worthy and awkward. Having taken this class and receiving a C, here is my advice if you want a C or better. Go to class, every class. Start the concert reports early. READ THE TEXTBOOK AND CROSSWORDS. This class is graded on very few things.

Although an initially unintriguing course, I ended up learning a lot. Professor Heidner made the class a somehow awkward experience, but I liked his spirit for the topic. He is extremely knowledgeable on the subject and makes the contents easy to understand. As long as you do the crossword, go to class, and do concert reports you will do well.

Eric comes off as a bit of a dweeb but so what. Corny jokes near insufferable are made up by the fact that he is an excellent educator. His class was organized and easy to engage with. He was always prepared to teach and teach well. I look forward to continue learning from him.

Professor Heidner is an amazing professor. Took him back in 2021 and 2022 when he first started doing the Musicianship Track for Year 1 Music Majors. He gives you enough prep for the singing tests and music dictations. He wants everyone to succeed and has accessible office hours.

Take this class if you want to learn. I know probably 150 terms about music that I NEVER knew before this class. Also, if you take this class (pro tip) when you are assigned concert reports go to them the first month! He offers another one for extra credit - which should be a must. Study the crossword - check quizlet and thats all. NO HOMEWORK

"A Timeline of Western Music" would be a fitting name. The first 4 weeks of class cover music theory terms which set the stage for going through musical styles from the Middle Ages to today. Grade consists of: 4, 50 point multiple choice exams & a 100 point final, attendance, & 3 video reviews, 3 short written concert reports. Fun & fascinating!

I really enjoyed Professor Heidner's class. It was online due to covid, but he was still very accessable. There were 5 exams this semester and 3 concert reports to submit; a fourth for extra credit. Great professor; I looked forward to his lectures each class!

I can't say I was expecting an A from Mr. Heidner's class, but here's what I did: - I got a c average on tests (study crosswords and memorize some of the songs) - I came to every class (dozed off the whole time) - Did 4 concert reports (1 extra credit) - Did the packets he assigned. Expect a pretty dull class, but the final grade can be worth it.

Took both his Fundamentals Of Music & Beginning Winds/Percussion class and this guy is an absolute sweetheart who loves his job. He is super kind and will go above and beyond to make sure you succeed. If you need extra help, all you have to do is ask! He is an absolute expert in brass music btw and is so involved in SBCC's bands. He is SBCC Music.

Prof Heidner is one of the best teachers I've ever had and I have a masters degree so have dealt w may profs. Learned a lot. Clear lectures, extremely well prepared, good time management, open to all questions, and he has a good sense of humor. You better show up & do homework. Highly recommend Music 100 - a great overall music theory course.

Heidner is nice and I can tell he does his best but omg this class is boring. If you want an A, just show up, it gets harder throughout the semester to do so because he is all lecture but just do it, go. Tests are pretty easy, except for listening. Doesn't post on canvas. It is an easy class, but just so boring. There is one extra credit for 25 pts

Homework every night. As a lifelong musician, this has been my least favorite class here. He makes a lot of dad jokes, but some can be a bit too much. Pretty hard if you doze off during the lecture for a minute. I don't recommend. I dropped his class lol.

I played an instrument for years and yet I almost failed his class. Not the best teacher, but he makes a lot of funny jokes tbh. I wouldn't take him again... Lectures are hard to grasp especially if you aren't musically inclined.. AND I AM, yet it was a hard class

Prof. Heidner is everything you want in a college professor. His lectures are straightforward and interesting, he's clearly very passionate about music, and his homework is easy and minimal. Memorize the review crosswords he gives out and you'll do well on tests. He has a pretty lax attendance policy and I never had to use the textbook to get an A.
Solid class. Great for beginners. Study with others to get a really good grade.

Heidner is my favorite professor! He's very funny and likes to make jokes. This class is an easy A so long as you show up to class and listen. He says you need the textbook he wrote but I haven't read it once and I'm getting an A. He doesn't like it when people are on their phones/laptops whatever so he's stern about that. Tests are few and easy.

Show up, don't pay attention memorize the crossword and you do well on tests. There are 4 tests and a final and 3 required concert reports however he allows you to do a 4th one for 25 extra credit point. Tests are 50 points concert reports are 25 points attendance is 100 points and final is 100 points. One of the easiest classes SBCC offers

Easy A if you memorize the terms. He gives out crosswords as study guides before each test which help a lot. The only hard part is memorizing songs names/who composed them but other than that it's an easy A. There's never any homework. He's super goofy and makes lots of silly jokes. Nice guy. Definitely recommend if you need some extra GE's .

He's really cool. I love music and have tried to study it on my own before but I figured I would take this class. At first it seemed daunting and like I'd get lost but he is very helpful. He's super passionate and if you're passionate about music it should be an easy class. He's there to help you. He cracks good jokes to but no one ever laughs lol

He's pretty clear on expectations; attendance is 100 points, requires you to go to 3 concerts and write a paper on each and a lab packet. Lectures can be very boring, but he is definitely extremely passionate about music. If you can memorize terms and show up to class, it's an easy A.

Heidner is a cool guy. He helps you out as much as he can and tries to make everything clear. The crosswords he gives are the keys to the test and if you pay attention in class and do the crosswords you will be fine. The essays and other things you have to do for the class are annoying and feel like your wasting your time but they are equally easy.

Heidner is a fun professor. You will hear the same jokes 5 times, but you'll learn to love them. He is very knowledgable and has tons of factoids about music history and other things. Tests are easy if you do the crosswords. Important to attend lectures, which can be dry without visual aids, but still interesting and info-packed. Take this class!

Really knows his stuff and enjoys the subject. Gives you a easy packet & 3 short concert reports that aren't due until the day of the final. Easiest tests I have ever taken in a class. Grades you on attendance, but the other work is so easy that attendance shouldn't drop your grade too much. Recommend him for Music Appreciation!
he is awesome, the class is easy to understand and fun :-)
Very clearly loves his subject. The lectures are very clear and he tries very hard to work with students to help them understand the subject. He's very helpful if you come to him with any questions.
definition of a good teacher
He is too cool. Great at making concepts clear and understandable. Didn't use the textbook once. Easy A. A fun class. Sausage fest though. That comb-over is too much.
Very helpful, will help during office hours, offers tutors outside of office hours. No penalty for late homework, turn in any time.
Eric is a truly delightful guy! Humerous and easy going.All week can't wait for his next class. Outstanding conductor- I'm not the only who thinks so!! Joanie Fogel

He was a great Spanish teacher! I took his class as a dual enrollment student, and during finals weeks near May, I had a lot of work to do because I was taking 8 courses, and I asked him if it would be possible to get an extension. He was very accommodating and willing to help. He genuinely wants you to understand the content and course material.
Great personality! Shows up and puts in his all every class time even when barely anyone shows up! Awesome teacher overall just tough to follow the ciriculim.
Awesome and charismatic teacher

He was really funny and made class fun! There was never a dull moment in class. He is a really easy grader. He would give us pop quizzes but he would let us partner up. You HAVE to go to class in order to pass. There isn't a lot of homework either! He is overall an amazing teacher and when you really connect with him it makes the class better.

Professor Hernandez is the homie. He's always coming in with outfits on fleek. Always rocking his huaraches! He's super nice and as long as you try your best to learn, he's not gonna be hard on you. You have to engage, though. He will call on you, as it's a Spanish class; do your best to answer. If you don't return his greetings, he will be upset!

This professor is probably the nicest i've ever had. 2 hours goes so fast! He is fun, very nice and helpful, quick and we did a lot of fun exercises in group every class. The classroom was a nice place where we could all talk and make mistakes. Book is on canvas and the exercises is being done together in class. Very easy!

Professor Hernandez is exactly what a Spanish teacher needs to be. His class is fun, not boring, and he really cares about his students. I have to agree with a previous comment, he is the best dressed teacher!

Overall great professor! Since I am not a native speaker, I was challenging myself taking this class but he made it so comfortable. He makes the class very fun and does activities each class. He puts a lot of time into carefully planning each class. If I could take him again I would, Highly recommend!

Mr Hernandez is one of the best professors I have ever had. He finds a way to make every class fun and makes me want to actually learn. He is also 100% the best dressed professor on campus. VIVA PERU CARAJO
Mr. Hernandez is literally the best professor I've ever had and thats ON JAH

He's hilarious and caring, and his class is extremely easy. If you keep up on the minimal homework and the online classes/exams, then you're going to pass with flying colors. We had a potluck, watched movies, funny videos, and participating in class is so much fun! Have Google translate ready, and you're good to go!

Luis Hernandez is one of the best teachers I have ever had. He is HILARIOUS and wants all of his students to pass. He makes it incredibly easy to get an A, really all you need to do is show up. There's little homework and the tests are online, with two chances each. One super easy presentation and Spanish interview and no hw for the last month.

Luis is probably one of the best professors I've had at city college. He's hilarious and you will actually learn in his class if you put in the effort. Even if you don't learn, you can likely get a pretty good grade. He gives out a ton of extra credit and really wants you to succeed. Participation is really important in his class.
He is the best teacher. I recommend everyone to take him even though you don't know any Spanish like me. His class is very fun. He cares and supports his students. Good teacher.

Luis is so funny and really wants his students to succeed! He speaks mainly in spanish and quickly which was hard for me since spanish comes a little more difficult to me, but he was there for me when i needed help. He really prepares you for the midterm and final. The book, exams and mainly all assignments are all online.

He is the best teacher ever. Real stuff. He was super funny and silly but he also knew how to get students to do stuff. He is really nice until you try him then he gets a lil sarcastic and mad. Stay on his good side even tho he doesnt rlly have a bad side. The work was super easy but there was a lot of it especially in the summer. Luv him
Very knowledgable, helpful and he really wants his students to succeed. He makes the class so much fun. Great teacher

I absolutely loved this class. I haven't liked Spanish in the past but Luis is such an amazing teacher. I really feel I learned a lot from him. He is the best spanish teacher I've ever had. So helpful and wants his students to succeed. Take his class!
explained the material really well

Took over summer. Idk why he has so many neg. reviews but he's a good professor. Exams are exactly like the homework. Super easy class. Highly recommend him for an easy A. If you're not good at math, obvi you're going to struggle but this class is easy and you can always watch other youtube videos if you don't understand his lecture vids.

do not take this professor!!!! so much homework, boring teaching style, and doesn't understand that his students have a life outside of his class. selfish and does everything in his best interest rather than his students. not sure why he has such a high rating, avoid at all costs!!!

Calc 150 is definitely not easy and Hersh's class is complete lecture style. Grading is based solely off tests with maybe like 10% homework grade. He was not a bad professor nor was the class bad, but the lectures are very boring and it is a lot of sitting and listening. That being said, study, and work very hard, and it's possible to get an A+
Don't take this teacher. He expects you to know everything. Did not help when i asked.

I took this class for summer school. It wasn't the easiest class, but if you pay attention to the lectures the tests are easier than the homework in my opinion. He was accommodating over zoom if you needed help. One of the best teachers I have had at SBCC.
the best professor for math. his notes are amazing and if you pay attention you will learn so much.
Terrible teacher. Reads straight out of the textbook for lectures. Gives awful practice exams - (gives you types if problems that are not on the exam)

Jared Hersh was a great professor, perfect for Math 149, which is the combo course. There are a lot of exams and quizzes. The homework is not graded, and neither are the quizzes; the only grades that matter are the exams. The quizzes are used to boost exam grades. There is nothing allowed for quizzes and exams besides a calculator.

I'm very surprised at all the negative remote reviews. I attended his class in person. Hersh was the best math teacher I've ever had. Yes there is a lot of homework. Yes, it's a hard class. The handout notes are like gold. Everything led to another in ways that made sense. I'm not sure I could have learned as much with someone else. Recommended.

If you are planning on taking this course with this professor, I would advise not to. My experience in his online class was very stressful. There were many lectures that went along with several units of classwork that was due within a few days after being assigned. If you don't do the work his way, you will be accused of academic dishonesty...

Don't. I was shocked when I took him and realized how bad of a professor he is because his reviews said otherwise. Assigns 2 packets of hw with 20 pages in each, You only have a day or two to complete. He claimed I was academically dishonest on two of my tests which was not the case. A horrible professor. He's the reason I had to switch my major

lot's of homework. his online video lectures are long and difficult to pay attention to imo. textbook was not mandatory. he gives multiple packets for every unit, due dates are kind of weird imo, assignments were not due on a weekly basis, sometimes it was a Tuesday, Friday, Saturday, etc. also went to tutoring for this class but it was still hard

Lectures are on YouTube which make it easy. Get ready to print! I bought the textbook but honestly didn't need to. He's a nice guy who clearly loves math but was reasonable with his expectations about our love (or lack-there-of) of math. It's easy to get in touch with him, meet with him on zoom if you have questions throughout the semester.

Alongside 137C, this was a relatively easy course! There's a decent amount of classwork, nothing too crazy. Plenty of prep for every exam (all unproctored), but the final is deceptively hard lol. Prof seems really sweet, and provided plenty of note sheets + video explanations. Put in your fair share of hard work and you'll do great!

He is a good teacher and does try his best to help. He is a fair grader and I am terrible at math. Me passing is nothing short a miracle between him and my tutor. However, it was a lot of homework. Although it is not mandatory, you will find that it is helpful. Don't wait until the last minute to do things, you will be overwhelmed.
overall worst teacher I had this semester. little to no help whatsoever. Him being hard on us wasn't that bad, but the fact that he made it hard to understand and follow his class was frustrating.

A really great teacher. He ALWAYS responds to emails and is always willing to go further in depth on the concepts in this lectures and on the homework. He responds to emails in a timely manner but plan on getting a response in the middle of the night. Overall 11/10 experience.

Jared Hersh is a really amazing professor! Math is not my strong suit but his class spelled everything out for you. He has fill-in notes for his students and goes in-depth on every topic. He is always there to answer your questions. His class taught me so much even though it was review for me. (you don't have to do the homework,testsreplacelowgrade

This professor gives a great deal of homework, which is easy enough to do since he will provide the odd number problems, meaning, the book will provide the answers. The problem is the homework is extremely time consuming and none of them will help you when you go to take the test. Beware!

This professor gives a great deal of homework, which is easy enough to do since he will provide the odd number problems. Meaning, the book will provide the answer so you can figure them out on your own. The problem is none of them are extremely time consuming and none of them on the test, therefore, they will not provide you with for tests. Beware!

Makes a great deal of mistakes (handouts/during class) and doesn't fix until a student points them out. He shows favoritism to students who have taken the course previously. Basically "he's that math nerd" who is doing his homework, in front of you, on a projector. He could care less if you are succeed, that's not a teacher, that is arrogance.

Jared is a great professor. He makes it clear what to expect in his class. All of his note packets are great and the classwork/homework given is very helpful. He teaches the material well and will gladly help you if you're stuck. Overall, you should pass the class easily as everything is laid out for you.

MATH107/137/138 Jared Hersh is by far the best professor/teacher I have had. He cares about his students, he gives clear grading criteria, he gives great feedback, and his lectures are well set up. Students in my class only have positive things to say about him, even when we are outside of class. I would recommend his class for any of my friends.

If you are looking for an easy A Jared is not your guy. You must attend class, pay attention, and do ALL the homework and class work. With that being said he is an awesome professor. The way he teaches is simple and to the point. He is also always willing to help during office hours and takes questions during lectures.

Taking the combo class was difficult, but extremely doable. He teaches in a very straight forward manner, which is how I learn. He prints out the notes and goes through them with you.
