He is very smart and sometimes jokes can go over his head, but he is a great teacher and I honestly wouldn't have taken the combo class with another professor.

Makes the class run smoothly. Gives you printed notes to follow along with, and clear dates set for quizzes and tests. He also gives you a chance to replace a bad quiz grade with the same percentage you received on the test, if you did better. This goes for most of the work you turn in. Clear with the students about what he expects. I enjoyed him!

Professor Hersh is very clear about what he wants you to learn. He gives you lots of helpful packets that contain the info that's on the test. He is very matter of fact, and not that sympathetic if you miss class. He conveys the information very well though. 10/10 would recommend.

Jared Hersh is a rare gem on this campus. His lectures are enjoyable and clear. He is very organized and all of his handouts (including notes) are posted online. I would totally take him again, no questions asked.

amazing professor if you get the chance to take him drop everything and TAKE HIM!!!!!!!! He is understanding, cute, funny, SMART. He really knows what he is talking about and is a really nice grader! LOVE YOU JARED!!!!!!!! also do your hw thoroughly because he actually grades it and gives you feedback!

I took this class thinking that it would be relaxed as its only 101. Professor Holdman is very kind and understanding about late work, and she is very enthusiastic in class, but the amount of work each week is REALLY hard to keep up with. Be aware that this class is a huge time commitment, and you have to plan ahead to manage the huge course load

I took Spanish 104 with Sra. Holdman and she was the best! If I had any questions I would email her and she always replied super quickly. Each week you do some activities, and every few week you do a written assignment (alone) and an oral assignment (with a partner). The partner work is pretty important so I'd recommend taking this with a friend.
Be ready to actually do work…. She’s not easy… is VERY specific with all assignments. All my other Spanish professors were never as tough as her….
Cool class! Her goal really is to teach you and/or improve your Spanish. Great class and professor.
She was great. Actually cared about teaching material, and would talk with you if you were struggling. Super cool professor.

By far the best spanish professor i've ever had. She really cares about students making the effort to learn and pushes you to get outside of your comfort zone, as learning a new language requires. She is very understanding and a great teacher. The class is taught in almost 100% spanish which is a great way to learn. Loved her! She's funny and cool.

I really enjoyed this class. Participation matters, though! The professor has a very clear outline of the class.. If you follow the professor's teaching method and attend the lectures, as well as do the homework, you will do great! Very fun class, very caring teacher, and VERY understanding! Well respected, my favorite professor so far.

Best Spanish teacher I ever had! She teaches so much in a fun way and is very patient and helpful! She is a tough grader and all, but she gives great feedback and is always very patient and willing to help whenever needed! She's an amazing person and is very smart! I love her teaching methods and I always will respect her!

She is great! Made me love learning spanish. I would take her class again and again if I wasn't transferring. She talkes a lot about spanish culture not just how to learn the language. I never spent more than 15 minutes on homework and got an A on every test. Ask for help or clarification and she will give it to you.

Most fun lang. class I've ever taken at SBCC. Wide variety of activities planned for classes with lots of student to student interaction. Is super cool,has a great sense of humor and lets u know what u need to study in order to do well on exams. Speaks lots of Spanish,so u get listening practice but will explain if u r clueless. I want her for 102!
Great class. Professora Holdman is super helpful and makes learning Spanish fun. The class is tough but totally worth it if you have to take a Spanish class.
Professora Holdman kept me engaged and ready to work in the course. She pushed me while always making me feel like I was able to succeed. I learned so much and hope to work with her again!

If you've never taken Spanish this is a good place to start, although for me she wasn't the greatest, didn't like how I would use an actual Spanish dialect (my tutor is from Spain and it stuck) but if you can get passed the Professor drop deadline then you can get more comfortable, get a tutor, do online Quia, she may give extra credit

Very helpful, she does expect you to work though. But as long as you work and actually try you will be fine. She's pretty funny too and will call out student's if you're slacking or being dumb. Really enjoyed her class.

Holdman is the Best Teacher Ever. I passed this class with an A+. She is extremely helpful and very clear on assignment. We do have to talk in spanish though, but its not that bad. We also have a couple presentations, but its a very fun class. I highly recommend it!

Holdman is a very good professor. She only speaks Spanish in class, but will drop many hints throughout the class period. She also gives hints on the test, and if you pay attention, getting an A is a very attainable grade.
Ms. Holdman is among the best teachers I have ever encountered. She is endlessly helpful, has quick wit, and will do almost anything to help you out. She is an amazing, amazing teacher.
Very clear teacher. Very helpfull and if you take spanish I recommend taking her.
Really good fun teacher. Very clear and if you pay attention she can be really nice.
I had Ms Holdman for 3 years of spanish in highschool. she's super nice, really chill, and helpful
Great teacher! She has other assignments so if you fail test you can still get an A

Annette Holdman is awesome. Really teaches you the language and focuses on Spanish from Mexico. Only speaks spanish the first hour so it really helps to get in the groove of speaking fluently. Brings in props and good examples and gets the entire class envolved. Best language teacher I have ever had. I will always recommend her class to anyone!!!!!
I had this teacher in high school and she was super nice. She is very patient and willing to help you out.
She was the best language teacher I have had. Ms. Holdman is very funny and helpful in all respects. I strongly reccomend taking her classes.

Katie was the lab instructor for chem 101 lecture and it is clear that she is so passionate about chem. Please take her, she gets to know her students personally and it's clear she cares. She makes sure everyone understands the lessons and is happy to help with anything.
If you ever have the oppertunity to take her. do it. She is amazing, sweet, caring, inspirational. AMAZING, DO IT.
Super understanding of your situations. Made learning Chemistry 104 enjoyable and easy to understand. 10/10 would recommend taking this class with her.

Katie did a really amazing job of putting together a practical no-nonsense curriculum, even providing us a clear schedule of assignments and tasks that ultimately helped make things more manageable for an accelerated class. She was wonderful at explaining things in a way that we all understood whilst maintaining her enthusiasm for chemistry.

she is one of the sweetest people you will ever meet. she truley wants her students to understand the content and is always willing to help people out when they are struggling. Her labs are straightforward and there is no reason you should fail the class unless you don't show up. I wish she taught my normal chem class and not just the labs :(

she is so caring and understanding. She really wants students to learn the material and not just memorize for the test, and her lecturers reflect that. She is your biggest supporter and you can feel that. She is a teacher that wants you to do well, she offers extra credit. But put in your own effort it will be a hard class if you don't study!

I had Prof. Katie as a lab instructor for Intro Chem---she's funny, caring, and knowledgeable of the course content. Her instructions for the labs were always clear, and she was very forgiving with late work. If you have the option to select her as your lab instructor, do so! You'll succeed in the class.

take chem 104 with Katie! She's an amazing and caring professor. Only 3 exams & you're allowed a page of notes. Homework and Lecture quizzes every week but are easy (10 questions). The Labs are easy and help you with your grade, and she's very understanding if you talk to her! Please attend the tutoring and study for the exams!!!

Katie was my lab instructor for Chem110. She cares deeply about the learning and wellbeing of her students, and it very good at demonstrating this. Katie is also amazing at explaining complex ideas in simple ways. I highly recommend this teacher and am so grateful to of met her!

Katie truly cares about you and your well-being, as well as how you're doing in the course. I took her over the summer and even with the shorter time constraint, she could effectively teach the material and was always open to answer any questions. She held review sessions the day before exams and gave out tons of extra credit!

Professor Hughes makes a tough class tolerable and easy! She explains the material very well, is super sweet and caring! She gives ton of extra credit, has study guides that are actually helpful and allows you a page of notes for exams.

Professor Hughes is the best Chemistry professor I have ever had. She makes her class very fair, and is happy to answer questions or explain any concepts if you are confused. If you have the option to take her, you will not regret it. I have struggled in Chemistry in the past but she makes it very digestible and provides resources for help as well.
She is Caring and understanding ! Offers extra credit and goes over all the material that will be covered in the exams. Explains well the material.

Unfortunately she's the only typography teacher for my required art degree or I wouldn't have taken her. Was rude to me in person even though I didn't do anything wrong. She also doesn't like when you ask questions so you better get everything correctly the first time or she'll embarrass you in front of the whole class.

Easy class if you alr have creative mind. Pretty young teacher, which gives a great view on current work of graphic designers. Explains everything very thoroughly which is why she can get frustrated if you ask a question - bc it prob means you didn't listen. She praises good work a lot. Gives advice on how to become a Graphic Designer like herself.

Madeleine was one of my favorite professors I have had the privilege to take a class with. I've heard some students say she is too tough and critical with her feedback, but I think this is what makes her great. I appreciate the honesty and great advice she has given me, and I truly believe it has made me grow tremendously as a designer.

Madeleine is still one of my most favorite professors at SBCC. She is incredibly smart, well spoken and keeps everyone informed. Whether about art in culture, the importance of checking your privileges, or all the Creative Cloud programs. She's fantastic, I would take her for so many classes if she taught more. If you can, take her classes!

Ignon is fast paced and gets frustrated when you ask her for help. She's like a robot shooting out words. I would suggest she stick with strictly online teaching because her lack of social skills can be improved. And, also grades your work with a fine tooth comb.

Although it is an online course, Jacquin's teaching methods and long boring lectures makes it difficult for students to learn. The weekly quizzes are tough and have such a short time limit. Would not take again!

One of the best professors I know. It is very interesting material & her lectures are engaging. She clearly presents the material & deals with difficult topics with compassion. She has great experience as a respected professional in the field that is invaluable. She's there for her students and wants you to learn about anything.

She was very kind and helped me find ways to get into the field I want. She has pretty entertaining lectures even though they are online. I bet I would have loved her in person. Makes information easy to understand and the projects are fun yet challenging. Discussion posts started off easier and got harder towards the end. Overall great teacher.

Absolutely love her! Her online Intro to Criminology class was structured and organized. Her lectures were all YouTube videos, so you can pause and write your notes, which I found to be really nice. She's highly qualified, has a nice voice, and all the info. is interesting. All tests/quizzes are easy and straight from lecture. Miss this class!

Dr. Jacquin is really sweet and she is teaching something that she is passionate about. You could tell which areas that she is more passionate about, but she also wants you to end the class with a high grade and tries, therefore, to help you with easy guides before exams. There's not necessary to read the literature.

The content of this class was interesting, but the professor did not present it very nicely. You have to watch really long, voiceover, boring lecture videos each week. You HAVE to take lots of notes or you will not pass. The tests are long and each one is cumulative. I would take this class with a different professor.

Dr. Jacquin is so great! I took Crime and human behavior as a class I was just interested in and I have learned so much all throughout her class! There are 4 exams and she drops one your lowest grade. Attendance is not mandatory but if you go to all classes you get extra credit, and there are in person quiz and assignments. Easy if you go to class!

She's an amazing professor. She really knows what she's talking about. I took the Crime & Human behavior class, it was so interesting and I've learned a lot. If you have a question you can just raise your hand and she will explain. Amazing professor, I recommend this class!

Dr. Jacquin is an amazing professor and really enjoys her job. I took her for Crime & Human Behavior and it was one of the best classes I've taken at CC. She respects students and her lectures come with outlines that you take notes in (print them yourself). She's got a lot of cool stories about her job, She's a very inspirational teacher!!

I took Professor Jacquin Intro to Criminology class and it was my favorite class. She is a great teacher and you truly do learn a lot from her. She makes you work for your grade but her class is so interesting that it is fun. Tests aren't that bad and she drops your lowest one at the end of the semester. Would def recommend her!!!

Alejandra is very inspiring if this is the field you want to go into, she teaches in the MAT lab and is very knowledgeable in the field. I feel like I was able to learn a lot and if I had any questions I could go to her about them.

There is hardly real lectures, it's mostly youtube tutorials that she didn't make herself, and very often she will "lecture" about completely irrelevant topics. She also constantly belittled me in front of the class for my choice in major and career path. Multiple times she told me I was going to end up poor or living out of my car. wtf?

I took MAT 118 taught by Alejandra, and I learned a lot! She gives great feedback and has high expectations of her students (In a good way). Be warned that this is a class that you have to put significant effort into, if you want to earn a good grade. Alejandra is also very funny in my opinion, but I don't think she means to be funny.

Alejandra is one professor that honestly cares about her students well-being and their classwork. Her instructions are very clear and detailed. She is also extremely knowledgable in the media field! She is accessible and fair to students that need extra help, so don't hesitate to ask her. She definitely wants to see all of her students succeed!

she is really willing to help you improve your work in multimedia, especially animation. she can sometimes be a hard critique but ultimately she does this to help you improve your skill and no to put you down and her advice is always really solid.

If you are serious about the multimedia field, you shouldn't be annoyed that she assigns a lot of homework. She is trying to make her students successful! If you put a lot of effort into this class, you will gain a good basic understanding of theory and practical application. I'm really looking forward to taking more classes with her.

I was weary about taking the class because of reviews but I appreciated having her as a professor. SHE DOES TALK A LOT, but only if you aren't chiming in, so if you want to have a break in long tangents, try and participate. The reading material is dense & requires your attention. If you participate, read & put an effort on projects, you'll pass.

Professor Jarabo definitely knows her field and is very passionate about what she teaches. Her lectures can be hard to follow, and you will fall behind if you miss a class. However, if you reach out to her for feedback she will go above and beyond to help you succeed. She cares a lot and wants you to do well.

The subject itself is interesting and very broad. The course covers a lot. The lectures however quite often became spacey and often discusses irrelevant topics like bull fight and single parents. If you want feedback you have to ask and it's important to not miss class cause then you will fall behind.

Okey so here's the deal, during the lectures she always spaces out and starts talking about very irrelevant stuff like bull-fighting, drugs or how life will be if you're single. You never really know what the assignment are about or what you're supposed to do, she doesn't really help either. You always get a late notice about them too. Good luck!
She really knows her subject well and like's to talk about it. It's a lot of discussions to do so be prepared. If you do all the discussions and assignments you have easy A.

She's a really good instructor. She always gives you good feedback on your projects and she always let you fix it and resubmit it or submit it later. You can always email her and she'll respond. Interactive design class was super interesting, I learned so many new things.

In this class, you'll learn some light action script coding and make fun stuff in Flash. If you take it online, she offers you to sit in her classes on campus if you have time. She's very helpful and forgiving with her grading, you'll need it. If you get behind, let her know right away.

Alejandra's a dedicated teacher and I'm sad to be done with MAT 103. She's funny, engaging and will make you think about how media has played a big role in the development of humanity. She's a lenient grader, if you keep up with the assignments and participate in class you'll do great! Get the reader, you'll need it!

I'm taking 2 classes with her (MAT 103 & 116) - her 116 class is a bit more thorough, but i'm finding it hard to figure out what she wants in MAT 103 from her run-on sentences. Also, animation class is hard enough (online class too), but she expects you to be able to piece together the work from her disorganized "lecture notes" - Feeling frustrated

I really liked "Introduction to Multimedia". Alejandra has chosen selected reading material for us to get acquainted about the evolution of media and it's schools of thought. It helped me understand the "hows & whys" of multimedia. Yes, you have to read a lot for this class. Great teacher who is in touch with the latest technology and trends.

Towards the end of the semester, she commented about how the class should be called "Theory on Multimedia" instead of "Introduction to Multimedia". You'll have to buy a "book" for this class which is just an overpriced mishmash of copy pastes from the web and other books from other authors. Tons of notes and scribbles inside makes it illegible.

Alejandra Jarabo is a truly great and inspirational teacher. She's very passionate about the field, and what I like most about the lectures is that she's always reflecting on what you can use the class and knowledge for in the future, which is so helpful. I advise to go to all the classes, because you learn so much from her that's not in the text.

Some students might say Alejandra is rough, unclear, etc. Here's the thing: if you don't care to learn anything, you're not going to have a good time. Alejandra will push you to do your best; she isn't an easy-A teacher. If you take the time to communicate, you'll find she's a fantastic teacher who genuinely cares about her students and her work.

For some reason she keeps pushing Animation all the time. It gets annoying at times and makes you want to think that her expertise is only animation. I did not have a great experience in her Interface Design class. Some of the assignments were completely irrelevant. I think I wasted my money and time on this one.
Alejandra is a great and unique teacher. You have to put effort into the class, but it's not a struggle. She will make sure you understand the subject!
Great teacher and very helpful! Her lectures are clear and interesting and she gives good feedback on every assignment you submit. Expect assignments each week and a few projects.

Alejandra works very hard to give her students the most they can get. She's very clear with explanations; if you don't understand, she will explain it again. If you work hard she will try to help you as much as possible. She's the most feisty teacher I've had. She gives the class a fun atmosphere; it's not a boring experience, but an exciting one!!

Her classes are always fun. She will help you figure out what you are good for in the game business or someplace else once she gets to know you. She will help you and extend turn in times as long as you let her know what is going on, but she will know if you are using it as an excuse. Will give you her honest opinion.

Took Introduction To Multimedia (MAT103) online. Teacher directions are unclear, class isnt hard but YOU HAVE TO READ! If not you will fail her class. You have to keep up in her class so be online and check every week for work. 3 quizzes, no tests, 1 midterm and 1 final. midterm and final is a project but pretty easy just know powerpoint and read!

Took Flash I: Interactive Fundamentals (MAT116). Except hard work because each assignments are time consuming. My final wasnt clear some directions are hard to understand, she suggests the book but i didnt use it because theres videos on youtube and she has some one moodle. The TA is very helpful when your stuck. No tests but 3 quizzes.
Really engages students in lectures and likes to get to know them. Will call you out if you're not participating in discussions or seem like you're not paying attention.

She was really funny, the lectures were long but she really helped open my mind to the possibilities of multimedia in a fun expressive way. She gave great feedback on assignments. I would learning from them and could see improvement throughout the semester.
This class opened up my eye to things that I have been blind too.

Alejandra is understanding to the needs of her students and will go out of her way to help you if you ask for it. You get as much out of the class as you put into it though - you CAN take the easy way out with the assignments, but you will prosper much more if you put the time into it. Made me love learning again after wanting to drop out.

Took this class online. Discussions were always easy points. Quizzes were not hard if you had the right kind of mindset. Really nice professor who actually added to discussion (never seen that before). Super easy A. Don't make more work than it's worth for yourself like some other students did.

Professor gives good feedback. She assigns a lot of reading but makes a real effort to engage students with the material. There are a lot of discussions of varying degrees of work that you have to complete. The tests are surprisingly challenging. I learned a lot from her and spent a lot of time doing so.

This instructor does not really instruct. She is not a good communicator. She does not grade timely. This instructor does not effectively teach the material. She lacks clear communication and is not helpful when students have questions. She does not grade timely. Exams have wrong answers.. I will have to get a tutor before ODS exam.

I have taken this professor for several courses as she is the one and only professor to teach all CIM courses. If there was a choice, I would pick another professor. My classmates seem to dislike her teaching methods and I don't blame them but honestly, for me, the thing I don't like is how disorganized the classes are.

Ms. Kalbag is an amazing teacher, she grades quickly, is understanding, and most of all teaches calculus in a way that makes it make sense. All of her grading is very fair and in my opinion quite generous. She also has an excellent sense of humor that makes her class incredibly enjoyable to take and overall just a pleasure.

Ms. Kalbag's class helps you understand the material, and as someone who had shaky calculus foundations before, she helped me catch up. She's a very fair grader and is happy to answer questions. There is a lot of homework for this class, but I promise it's worth it! Just be punctual with homework (and taking notes in class is mostly unnecessary).
Very helpful and tried to help you do well
Kalbag is a great teacher who takes a lot of time and effort to help students through the difficult material taught in MATH 160.
She's a great teacher who really enjoys the subject of Calculus and takes pride in teaching.

She really shines in 160. It's a harder subject and all the practice is super necessary. After this class, you will be able to do integrals really well and quickly, which is really important for later math classes. Integrals require more clever thinking than a normal math problem, but her lectures are clear and helpful. Great class

Mrs. Kalbag is a great teacher. You will really know calculus and be able to execute it if you take her class. The only thing is that her lectures are a bit dry, but my classmates were cool people and she interacts with her students which made it really fun. She's also very enthusiastic about the math and it really makes the class fun.

I will start off by saying that Kalbag is the WORST teacher I have ever had. She is unreasonable, mean and a bully. She makes fun of students who don't know Calculus coming into her class. I can't say enough horrible things about her. Stay clear of this teacher at all costs. Worst mistake to take a class from her!

The class wasn't terrible, but his lectures could literally be summed up in 15 minutes instead of like an hour and a half. He does give extra credit and his quizzes and exams are easy. Definitely only pushes the democratic viewpoint so just be aware of that.

get where the hate is coming from. he always uploads slides, explains the chapters really well, and clearly knows his stuff. he's actually pretty funny, grades fairly, and always gives opportunities to boost your grade. midterm questions are super close to the study guide too. amazing guy!

I dont know where all this hate is coming from, his exams are VERY easy, his lectures are solid and he leaves time at the beginning of class to just have open forum about current politics. The only bad thing I could note is sometimes he has grammatical errors on exams. I highly recommend taking his course.
I cant say I learned anything can't but i'm pretty sure he just gives everyone an A because I literally did nothing in this class.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!! Look I think he is a good guy, he clearly knows about politics. That being said he may be the worst professor I've ever had. I walk out of each lecture feeling more confused than I did when I walked in. The readings have nothing to do with the assignments and it is just so confusing. save yourself the stress, please.

Many in-class group assignments, but none ended up being graded. A LOT of offered extra credit, my grade went from a 95% to above 100% after completing it. Experienced and respected although he doesn't do much more than read off the slides, which is basically the textbook. Also doesn't accept late assignments under any circumstance.

I actually took POLS 104. Basically his class is really easy and were just graded by discussion posts that didn't relate to the lectures at all as well as a midterm with open notes and final group project. He is a funny guy and definitely knows what he's talking about but doesn't apply it well in class. Did not care about attendance at all!
Interesting class, interesting Prof., fair grader.

Prof. Kasdin knows what he is talking about, which is not surprising because he is actually in politics. He's easy going and funny and enjoys talking to students after class. Complete the work and you'll do fine. There are chapter quizzes, short write-ups, a midterm and a final. He allows a cheat sheet and extra credit

I enjoyed the class all around, even though I am not majoring in Political Science. Really interesting and relevant stuff, and Kasdin's dry humor is entertaining. Moderate amount of work. Fair grader.

I am currently in this class right now. I have taken a government class before and its really not that complicated if you have a good professor. Everything in the lectures and in the book does not correlate with the tests. I am literally fighting for my life and debating switching my major because of this class.

don't take this professor for any classes unless you want to hate your life. He Didn't grade a single assignment until the last month of class and wasn't clear about any assignments given. Also he was so unorganized I never knew a professor could be like this. Absolutely horrible. Hope this helps :)

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS AT ALL !!!!! He is the most unorganized and worst teacher I have ever had, his assignments do not align with his lectures and he will give you study guides for the tests, and nothing he has on there is on the tests. He's horrible online to. He rights all exams 2 days before it is going to be taken. Horrible all around.

Not sure why he has a lot of bad reviews, his class is really straight forward and doable. In class group assignments, takes attendance, one exam w page of notes, 4 page easy essay, and a final. I took the class P/NP so I wasn't really interested in poly sci so he was a good option. Hes a good professor and seems to care about students.

I walked into that class not knowing much about politics and I felt lost and unwanted due to my lack of knowledge. He speaks at his students as if they are up to date with the news and like we are actually there for our political science majors, which none of us in the class were. This is POLS 101, an intro course, although it doesn't seem like it

POLS is a pretty boring subject but the lectures were easy to follow, assignments were easily accessible with lecture material, and he brought in a couple cool guest speakers which I though was pretty cool!

Do not take this class. During lecture, he either reads directly from the power points or talks about something completely unrelated. I leave his lectures not understanding anything that was discussed. When you email him questions he doesnt give a direct answer or replies with a response that is not related to the question at all.

He seems really disorganized and often doesn't know why he put something in his lectures. I emailed him multiple times for assistance and he either did not respond or he did not know the answer. The study guides are vague compared to the questions on the exams and I often had to teach myself material. His lectures are not engaging.

This teacher barely explains any of the class content. You have to teach yourself most of the material, and when students asked questions in class he barely knew how to answer them. He overcomplicates everything and was really disorganized the whole semester. Do not take his class.

Weren't that many projects or tests so the majority of you grade is made up by quizzes and discussions. Gave a good general overview of the TV industry. The end of the semester project is fairly easy and you have the whole semester to prep for it.

What to do and assignments are pretty straightforward. There are a few papers for the class but they aren't very long or hard. If you watch the lectures and screenings you should do fine on assignments and quizzes. Aleah's lectures are interesting she gives really good feedback on your assignments so if you get points off, you know what it's for.

Professor Kiley is great! She's super cool and passionate about TV. Her class is definitely not hard, but she will be disappointed if you don't do the work (and obviously give you a bad grade). I suggest doing the work because it's usually really fun. Awesome class! I would love to take another class with Professor Kiley.
She was always very kind and willing to help/give extra time when necessary. She was also very into the class and enjoyed teaching, even online, which showed throughout her class!

I really liked this professor because she did everything she could to be accessible through email and help you out with quick responses when you needed it. However the book we used was extremely boring and most of the grading was only based on movie reflections and most of the movies were definitely never any I would want to watch again.

We wrote only five papers, but every time you write one you also have to revise. We read one book and there was a reading quiz after each assigned reading, but they were very easy 3 question quizzes. Attendance is mandatory and you have to sign in for class, but she's also pretty accommodating if something comes up.

Julia is amazing! I'm an international student, she helped me a lot. inclass quiz is very easy, about 5 questions, multipule choices. We always do revision after inclass writing. She may seem mean at first, but she's really nice, and I'd like to get on her eng111, I don't know if she'll turn it in or not.

One of the best professors I've had. She genuinely cares about her students and wants everyone to succeed in her class. She makes sure concepts are clear. She is approachable and always willing to answer questions. She's an easy grader, which makes the class less stressful. I would highly recommend taking her class.

she's nice and all but has made comments about the 2020 riots and made a comment saying 'Why would anyone be scared of an immigrant" As a black person let alone immigrant I found these comments very offensive. She doesn't seem to watch what she says or how it could offend her students other than all that she's okay and is kind.

I was in this class for one week and have since transferred out. I felt ignored and overlooked. Kilgore was consistently at least 5 minutes late to her classes, mis-graded my assignment, and completely lost mine and another students essay (as well as spilled water on all others making some illegible). She did not seem to care about my learning.

I had just finished taking this class and can say that it was beyond doubt one of the best classes I had taken this semester. She really intends to help her students reach their fullest potential in this class. It is very well organized and the instructions are clear. Most empathetic and kindest professor out there, 10/10 would recommend.

I am currently in Professor Kilgore's class and can say without a doubt she has been my favorite teacher ever. She is so kind and knowledgeable especially when it comes to essays and giving feedback. She makes you want to participate in lectures even during COVID. She makes me wanna take time on my assignments and do them right. TAKE THE CLASS

Don't take her class if you really want to be skilled at writing. She always relies on Writing Center or her tutor. She didn't finish to grade some students' essays before spring break so they had to wait or a student was returned it after break. She comments on your essay a few or check just about the number of words. I passed but worry about 110.

Great and Enthusiastic Professor, but very unorganized and late to class sessions towards the end of the semester. I really enjoyed the class discussions, but the class kind of disapatied towards the end. The tutor was ok, and helpful also. Would recommend taking. But make sure ok with the comments on here first. Late but good teacher jst not orgn.
Wouldn't recommend. You will be writing a ton. It's a very easy class, but this instructor is not very good.

She is extremely funny. But that doesn't make her a great teacher. she is always late, always looses someones paper and hates to go over things. if you go to class, read and participate you'll do fine, just not the best Eng teach at sbcc

ok she is alwaysss late but she is really laid back and easy to talk to..she has children and understand ppl have lives outside of school. she does assign 5 essays a semester but she gives multiple essay topics to chose from. overall she is a pretty chill teacher

Just an average teacher. She's super nice and understanding. The writing assignments almost always involve a topic which is impossible to write about (5 total assignments). Don't get me wrong, I love to write, but I wish there was more freedom in this class. Just do the reading, put on a smile, participate, and you'll do fine

Love her! Best teacher i've ever met!always kind and hot and helpful.she gives enough freedom and space to write.i do not understand why there is someone does not like her. if you do not take her class, you will be regret

Terrible class, not interesting at all.She plays some ramdom videos, leaves class eraly, is always late. Takes weeks to get anything back to you (papers or midterms) BUT expects everyone to have a 5 page essay ready every 2 weeks...a paini relaly hate this class..do NOT recommend, even though i am gettying A's

LOVE HER! Wish i still went to SBCC so i could take her again. She is the best teacher I have ever had. After the last class she gave me her card and said I could call her anytime, even if I haven't had her in a while, to get help with transfer essays. She is honestly the best! Interesting class, use the book so you def need it. Not too much h
Very good teacher
Loved her, she knows her stuff and is really helpful. Just do the work it is an easy A. She is an adorable person but is usually tardy.
She is really helpful and she relates to her students very well.
Caring, Humorous, Knows her stuff

i lovedddd her! she was the chillest and most down-to-earth teacher i've ever had. she can relate to her students and has a genuine interest in every single one of them. she has a great amount of respect for her students and she keeps everyone awake by being a little outrageous at times. she is exrtremely lenient and understanding. i had her class
always late and you get into the habit of coming late too. she does have some bad days like everyone. she is a good teacher yet there are times when i was bord out of my mind.
really nice teacher, doesn't make a big deal if you miss her class. You'll pass if you do what she tells you to do, which are really simple handouts
very good teacher. helpful and easygoing. also very nice
No Comments

She's a really good teacher! Kinda late sometimes if u take the 8am class, she's always hot so if u get cold easily bring a sweater. You have homework pretty much everyclass but it's easy. Dont slack off either because then the class gets hard so just make sure u always do ur hw
No Comments
she is so nice!! Great teacher!
Coolest teacher ever, and she plays volleyball.

Awesome. Can't recommend highly enough. Allowed me to curb my essay to a related area which followed my interests. Engages students in stimulating discussions and encourages classroom participation. Definitely one of the best teachers I have ever had.

I took regular concert choir and it was a great experience. Taking the class with a friend or getting to know the people around you will make the class significantly more enjoyable. It's a decent sized choir with people of all ages. Attend class regularly and all concerts and rehearsals and you'll get an A.
He is excellent for you to get comfortable with singing. It's a gradual process to performing alone and he makes constructive feedback and never makes anyone feel bad.
He's so funny and caring. He makes everyone feel comfortable and gives great feedback. I wouldn't have picked any other introduction to singing.
Kreitzer is an incredible teacher, and he gives great constructive critiques. Will never put anyone down!!
Kreitzer is an incredible educator, he is funny and kind and he is great at what he does. He makes everyone feel like they are special!

This professor and class were so great I am taking the next one! Nathan was so sweet and always so caring and encouraging about singing in front of others and learning how to do it better every time. The material to learn isn't hard at all, and as long as you are in class you should get an A. He's really funny as well and an amazing teacher.

Encouraging, nice, funny, and extremely passionate about the music and what he does. Grade is based entirely on attendance (class is weekly for 3 hours & there is a final performance at the end of the course). We usually got out early, and there are snacks during the 15 minute break. This class is choral music only, so don't expect otherwise.

Nathan is an amazing musician and he really just wants his students to have a good time and learn how to improve their singing abilities. He would never put a student down or make them feel bad. His criticism is always constructive and he praises more than he criticizes. Great sense of humor as well. Highly recommend!
Krietzer is awesome. Be expected to sing in front of everyone. He's very nice and very helpful and I'm super excited to take another class with him.

I took Mr. Kreitzer in the first vocal tech class. I have been singing in my room by myself for years but never knew if I was any good. The first time I sung in front of people was in this class and you have to be ready to perform at least twice every semester. Nervous as I was Kreitzer was more than nice, giving me amazing constructive criticism3

He's great guy, good sense of humor and is extremely supportive and understanding when i would butcher songs. Keep in mind I'm a dude who never sang before this class, and after 1 semester I'm fairly confident to sing around whoever, and i didn't even buy the book. Thumbs up for Prof. Kreitzer!
This class is amazing. Kreitzer is a great professor and knows what he is talking about. Everyone in my class improved tenfold with singing. Chill class with an pretty chill professor.

Kreitzer was really a great teacher for vocal tech. It was crazy to watch everyone in the class improve since day 1. His class has a very fun and comfortable atmosphere. I swear, he's like a voice whisperer. If you do exacltly whatever advice he gives you, you will definitely be on your way to becoming a better singer. He's very chill. Enjoy!!!

Kreitzer is amazing for vocal classes because he makes the class comfortable and easy going.he gives you good tips for improving your voice.his choir on the other hand is kind of disorganized and the music choice is kind of boring.i wish it were more up beat and exciting.hes a great guy and definitely worth taking a class with him!

Kreitzer is a great teacher, he gives you advice what you need to work on the next performance. I took his class like three times and each time, I got better at performing. I recommend taking his vocal techniques class

Very good teacher - very encouraging & supportive. Really wants to help everyone be able to sing well. One of the nicest guys too. Recommend his class for anyone who wants to learn how to sing. Only criticism is that there is very little structure to the class & he allows people to talk (w/o any consequences) during people's performances :O(
Great professor and great easy class that is fun. Take it!
omg. he is kinda old but super hot in a weird way. LOVED his class and want to take more if I can next sememster.
Wonderful teacher and decent human being. I have loads of respect for this guy and he can teach anyone how to sing. His class is a pleasure.

Awesome teacher! Really helps people who are tone deaf learn how to sing like champs. If you want to learn how to sing but feel inhibited for whatever reason, take his vocal technique classes. He's really nice and will definitely help you!

He is a great teacher. He makes class enjoyable and is funny. He really tries to help you sing better and only wants the best for you. He is a very down to earth guy. Honestly one of the best classes ever.
Wow. It looks like everyone here wants a piece of Kreitzer! Dibs on his buttocks!

Been singing for Nate for the last 8 years. Works well on vocal techniques as well as diction. He's groups are the reason I'm still singing in Santa Barbara today. Not only is he my professor, but I can call him a great personal friend.
Great director.... and I'd do him.
HOT AND AWESOME! very helpful, patient, and funny. always ends class early. SUper laid back and makes you LOVE SINGING!
He is the best teacher, ever had! He is awesome. He has a super nice personality!

Hooooo my goodness is he hottt. Yes but that has nothing to do with his teaching skills. So... I had him for choir, and in that regard I think he does well with bringing us together and creating a wonderful sound. If he wasn't so attractive and if the guy singers weren't so motivated this year I don't know what we would have sounded like though.
Kreitzer is the best. End of story.
Prof. Kreitzer is a very clear, helpful and fun teacher. I highly recommend his classes.
Kreitzer is awesome! He is a great person and an even better professor.

By far one of the best English teachers I've had to date. Even though the meeting times were short, he really tried to get to know his students. You could tell he cared and wanted to see them do their best. The coursework was very manageable, and all essays were in person, which took some of the stress off. Would HIGHLY recommend.

amazing prof. he is very lenient on things. all you have to do is read and participate (he notices and appreciates it). i go to his office hours a lot and he is truly a joy to be around especially if you enjoy writing + english as a whole. would take his class a million times if i could.

There was alot of religious undertone the whole semester which was horrible. He is extremely passive aggressive and has a major attitude when people don't show up to his 8 am class. One of those teachers who thinks his class is the most important one youll ever take. Pretty easy grader tho. ALL essays and assignments written by hand in person!

He was so nice. He would call on you randomly though so be ready but I really enjoyed his class. we did meditations sometimes too which was nice because I took it at 8am. Overall great but sometimes he would get mad at the class.

I love Professor Land. He is a great teacher and truly loves his students and wants them to succeed. He lets you prepare well for in person essays and is willing to answer any questions or just talk about life. Take him.
The most influential professor I've had in my academic career. Take his class, change ya life.

Brett is a super interesting and engaging professor, would definitely take his class again! If u do all the readings and participate, u will get an A. Not a super tough grader, 1 in person essay and a couple online, final was 50 MCQ based off readings from the sem (super easy). DONT be late or he will not like u, show up to class as much as u can.

Best english teacher ever!!! We dont have textbooks, but we have to buy about four books, and they are super interesting for someone who doesnt like to read. I would totally take his class again, he really teaches us and shows us whats important. Hes a great guy. Class consists of essays mostly, freewrites, lectures, and a final exam. Dont be late!

Professor Land is an amazing teacher who inspired me in almost every lecture. You can't help but cling to every word he says in class. He is kind, caring, and humorous. You have to attend class and you can't be late or he will not like you.

Brett is awesome. If you need to take English 111 to transfer but don't like English, take Brett. I was in this same situation, and Brett's class was great. 90% of your grade is on the papers you write, but they aren't that hard, and he is ready to help if you need it. He is also just a cool dude with many interesting stories. Lots of writing tho
He truly cares about his students and material. I would love to take him again if I could!

He's a very nice professor and wants to see you succeed, but your essay scores are your grade and the final it worth a lot. Only 10% of your grade is actual assignments. Wish he could've made the essays and final worth less. Even if you get 90+/100 on everything theres still a chance you will pass with a B rather than an A

good professor, answers emails within a day. has good business hours and gives feedback on all your essays, also offers to talk in person if you want to go over it more. a lot of reading( don't skip anything you'll regret it at the end), only 4 essays so not to much writing. overall would recommend taking this class

Professor Land is an amazing professor. I learned so much from his class and he is able to teach in a way you want to learn and really inspires you. He is a great professor and a very personable professor at that. Very understanding as long as he sees that you are putting in effort. His breathing exercises really helped me.
Brett is a good guy, he cares about you and loves you. love ya brett!

Brett Land is easily my favorite college professor I've had! I've taken his class twice now because I enjoyed it so much. I'm not a big reader but I really enjoyed most of the books in the class. He is a super chill surfer/yoga teacher guy and a fun fact that he probably won't tell you is that he was played by Nick Jonas in a movie called Goat.

If you want to take a yoga class this is perfect for you! Not so much if you want to learn anything English related. Spends 20-30 minutes of class breathing in silence, when I requested to come in late because I didn't want to do that he had a huge attitude. Do not recommend if you like to learn.

Never in my life would I have expected to like an English class. Brett Land changed my mind, and made me think if things I would have never thought about in terms of english. Super cool professor if you show up, care, and ultimately put in the work. Honestly not a lot of homework. About 4-5 essays through the term. Super cool person!

Professor Land was fine, seemed pointless to take the class in person since he would only go over assignments listed in Canvas. No hard remarks, he cared a lot and it was not a difficult class. I would recommend the course online instead of in person

By far my favorite professor at SBCC. I strongly recommend you take his class. He cares about his students and offers plenty of help. The workload is pretty easy as long as you keep up with the reading, which wasn't too bad. Im not a huge fan of reading but the books he used were relatable and interesting. I can't say enough good things about him!

Participation and keeping up with the reading are the most important things in this class!!! As long as you show up and speak during class conversations you should be good. He is a great professor and is super understanding and helpful when it comes to classwork and homework.

Professor Land's class literally changed my life. I still remember the books we read vividly and continue to read some of the poetry selections we went over in class. I can't say you'll have the same experience but he's a quality instructor no doubt. Highly recommended.
Cant say anything bad about him. He was a really good teacher and you learn a lot. Get ready to write a lot of essays.
Great teacher. Really cares about his students. Also amazing person! He just had a baby mid semester in the Fall when I had him.

Dr. Land is a great teacher and English 111 was my favorite class this semester. It's easy to get a good grade if you pay attention to the in-class discussions. He values those who go to class and participate. There is a lot of reading but he assigns interesting books that I actually wanted to read. He offers good feedback and advice on essays.

Professor Land was one of the best teachers I have ever had. His class was truly enjoyable and the readings were some of best books I have ever read. I transferred out of SBCC but if Brett was teaching at the university I am at now I would definitely take his class.

Brett is an intelligent and supportive guy. He wants students to succeed, but you have to show him that youre trying and that you care! He wrote some of the most inspirational and constructive feedback Ive ever received on my essays. His teaching focuses on unlearning the typical frustrating elements of reading and writing. 100% recommend!

Brett was cool but I feel like I deserved at least an A. He wasn't clear on what he wanted. He wasn't really interested in helping his students because after we went into quarantine I emailed him a bunch of times he never emailed me back for help. So now Im angry that I received a B+ so that's why I'm giving him a 3/5 lol. He's chill tho.

Incredibly Professor! SO understanding and has the best book choices for the semester. One of the first classes I have had where the reading assigned was something I not only did with ease but looked forward to! Highly recommend this professor!!!

He is a good guy, but he really floods you with assignments (about 7/week) even on weeks when a big essay is due. I would recommend this class but don't expect grades back on time. If you take the class online, make sure you reach out individually (email/zoom) because if he likes you he loosens up and is way more helpful.
She is the worst professor I have ever taken at CC I would never take her again

she is super nice but there isn't any homework. you are graded on tests and participation only. if you need homework to hold yourself accountable then this isn't the class for you. if you study and use tutoring then you should be fine in the class. she posts all her notes online, which is more helpful than her lectures... which are mandatory.

Monika is a caring math teacher whose best interest is for her students to do well. Her lectures are long, but she provides clear examples. I would take this class with Monica again. Your grade is based off exams and a research essay, which helps boost your grade. She does board extra credit every class. She is a great teacher
Very easy to follow. Theres not too much work. She is very positive and always gives feedback. Her moodle was organized and flowed well

The most confusing teacher I'd never encounter for a 2-unit class. First, I was told that there would be no assignments whatsoever at beginning of the course. Turn around, there are so many assignments unannounced, more than you could imagine than the 3-unit classes. Class schedule is very inconsistency. I wouldn't recommend taking her class.

I'm honestly a pessimist but it's tough when people project their own opinions so heavily onto others. He usually has class favorites and likes to call people out, especially for being late. It's a glorified songwriting circle. He definitely needs more structure and like someone else said on here, he could bring in local talent/songwriters.

The course was good but could be improved. The lectures were incomplete, and I didn't learn much. The main focus was preparing two final songs, presenting drafts, and receiving feedback from the group and professor. The textbook didn't help much, but the feedback was useful. More structure and depth would make the course better.

Ralph wasn't friendly in his teaching. He is very pessimistic and can be tough with students. Previous teachers of this class would bring in celebrities from the local area- there is so much room for this class to grow in the curriculum. Instead, it's really just a songwriting circle where people share songs. Except with Ralph it's a rough environ.

This dude is the most negative professor I've ever had. He's always taking a pessimistic tone towards music, the class, the industry as a whole. It's hard to not let that impact my own passions towards music. Highly do not recommend.

Ralph is very blunt. Perhaps too much so, often his critique of the class or individuals can be seen as hurtful or personal. The class isn't very concise, more so just showing up with individuals sharing songs they've worked on and receiving feedback. You need to play an instrument for this class to work out successfully. Ralph gives little guidanc

I was expecting this to be my easy and fun course but it was not. I only made it through a few weeks of this course before I had to drop it. There was more work for this class than my other three courses combined. The teacher seemed nice and knowledgeable. If you have the extra time I would recommend this course, unfortunately I just didnt.

I took this class online and I really enjoyed it. I learned a lot about music and the textbook was interesting. Get ready to read as you'll have about 4 assignments per week. You pretty much get full credit if you do the assignments. Quizzes are open book and they aren't too bad. Overall a great class.

He knows his material very well. The book is easy to read and understand. Make sure you read because the quizzes are based on material directly from the book. His exercises make you actually engage with and think about the material. I really learned a lot about the history of popular music. Would take a course with him again!

really interesting, gives like no homework, isn't really legalistic with text content. not a tough grader, funny, great feedback, adds interesting info in lectures that isnt in the book, just asks for the important info for tests

I took Ralph Lowi's songwriting class and it made me realize a talent I never knew. He is very passionate about what he is teaching and the class is in a style where you present your song assignment and he gives you feedback. There are also group projects that are actually fun. Don't act like you don't care and waste his time and you will pass.

He knows a lot about music and I really appreciate that but he can be really confusing in his lectures. He jumps back and forth, so it's hard to follow. He talks about the content from the book in his lectures but doesn't go in order. It's hard to know what really to study.
I took this class as an easy summer class to get a GE done over the summer and it was an easy A. There were a lot of short essays you have to do during the week but other than that it was easy.

Professor Lowi does a great job with telling his students exactly what they need to do to do well on the tests and papers. Open to every students opinions and freely shares his without expecting you to agree. Would take another class with him anyday.

Interesting and easy going class. You will need to complete 1 weekly assignment almost every week but they only take around 1-2 hours to complete. The midterm and final are fairly easy if you have read the chapters and studied the songs on the playlists given. Ralph knows a lot about the subject and is a easy going teacher. Recommended class!
Very intersting class. You just have to read the text book and do the assignments and you'll get an A.

By far the most cultured and conscious professor I have ever had. He never ceases to surprise me with his progressive mindset - the equality he seems to give to all people regardless of any differences. He does an amazing job at being unbiased, and acknowledging his opinion as only opinion. He focuses on the cultures, less on pure memorization.
Pretty unapproachable and intimidating. He was pretty rude to some students in my class. Knows a lot about music, but is really really boring in his presentation.

He pretty much just facilitates the course by assigning short assignments based on the chapters from the textbook, so there's not much actual instruction or back-and-forth going on. Online courses are sometimes like that. Anyway, just get the assignments in and you'll do fine. I consider the guys who wrote the textbook the real teachers.

A good teacher. This class is very easy. Assignments to do every week almost, but it is very easy. 20% of the grade is based on midterm and final together. The rest is based on assignments, lab and attendancy.

I love music history, but this class was a total drag.I got 100 on all assignments, but hated writing a paper every week. Lectures were very hard to stay for. Need 10 hrs of lab work. He gave us no clue on what to study besides a list of music. Memorized artists/songs, exam only on genre and the names of those genres changed from the study list.

This class was a complete piece of cake & satisfied a GE Requirement. You have to do all of the reading, but the assignments are simple and clear. The teacher seems nice and was responsive. If you want a relatively stress-free experience, go for this class.
Good teacher. Always answer on time. But the exam is little bit hard.
Wanted us to do a TON of assignments on fourth of July Weekend no less. I understand it's a summer class, but why have so much stuff on fourth of July weekend?

I took his summer online course. You MUST read the textbook in order to do well on the quizzes/exams (not alot of time to look up answers during quiz/exam), but I found the reading to be very interesting. Responds to emails quickly and is very flexible with students. Weekly discussion and listening assignments due, but don't take long. RECOMMEND.

this class was VERY boring but honestly VERY easy if you attend class and pay attention to what he says and listen to the music he plays. I barely read the book and listened to him every lecture and ended up with an A and im not the brightest student lol. RECOMMEND! :D

He really likes talking about music, all kinds of. Whatever the topic is, he knows lots of musical common knowledge.The exams are not hard if you make notes all the time. If you listen the lists what he provides, review the notes about youtube clips, and read the textbook, everything is easy.

I would strongly recommend taking music 115 with John Clark instead. Ralph is definitely not the best teacher...class is very boring, he's not enthusiastic, he doesn't have office hours, his midterm and final have multiple essay questions, etc. He also spends way too much time on the blues so we only made it to the early '70s for rock and roll.

It was like pulling my teeth going to his class. Has no concept of how to teach. Thinks he's cool because he's in a crap band. He's obviously depressed he never became a rockstar. I rather jump out of a burning building then sit through another 3 hr lecture with monotone chump!
Great teacher who clearly has a passion for music. As long as you attend the classes, there's a good chance you'll do well.
totally easyyy.. totally fun.. specially when u start learning bout the beatles and the doors. etc

Each assignment should basically be 0 or 1 because if you don't have everything correct he gives you a zero. No grading fairly just a zero or 100 percent. Even if you put in hours into an assignment he will still give a zero if it isn't perfect.

Expect lots of technical errors in both your own projects and his lecture videos. Maya Autodesk is a complicated & stressful program to work with, so be prepared. This class forces you to rely on time management & tutoring. I wish he would edit his instruction videos to cut out parts where he made mistakes, it is easy to get confused when watching.

Professor Mahapatra was by far the most difficult, and unorganized professor I've had. His Canvas was a mess, his grading was unfair, and gave unprofessional attitude towards students that simply questioned the specifications for turning in assignments.

Mihir can be tough if you don't put in the effort. But if you do your work and keep in contact with him via email, he will help you every step of the way. He is stern but you can tell that it comes for a good place. He wants to see his students succeed. He also gives a ton of advice regarding the 3D industry! I'd gladly take another class with him!

Mihir is highly respected. As long as you attend the meetings as well as just put in the effort, youll get an easy A. Hes so caring and just wants you to pass the class. He has extra credit and hours outside of class. No due dates for assignments but if you dont stay on top of it, youre gonna drown in work at the end of the semester. Love him!

He gave me an F for my final project even though I met the minimum requirements, when was to have a still life model and 3 point lights from Arnold. Honestly the worst teacher I've ever had here at CC which is a shame because I've always had great experiences with the teachers here. Avoid if possible.

I learned more watching tutorials on Youtube than in the entirety of his class. His Canvas organization is a nightmare. He only grades the Final Still Life and Animation project. There were about 16 people in the class but by the final week only like 5 people actively participated in discussions, so take that as you will.

I really wanted to enjoy this class but it's such a bore and Mihir doesn't help with that. His videos are hard to understand because he speaks with a thick accent, which I have no problem with but they're so quiet and he murmurs a lot. Even if you ignore his accent, a lot of his videos are low quality making hard to see.

His online organization is a nightmare. He has you download videos that are 5+ years old and half the time the video formats don't even work. His written English is hard to understand, and when he gives you corrections on work he is non-specific. I was really excited for the content of this class but this professor absolutely killed it for me.

So the first few weeks were fine. but at the 3rd week it became very obvious that he is not well organized in the slightest. His Canvas page is an absolute pig sty. His communication skills are awful, he uses technical jargon without defining it to you and he take's the better part of a week to respond to any questions posted. Avoid if possible.

Mihir is a respected teacher and all around good guy! It's not a course for lazy people, but those who really want to learn it and put in the effort. If you hand in all the assignments and really attempt the final, you'll walk away with at least a B, if not an A.

He's very helpful and willing to help anyone who asks for it. He gives good feedback and makes it simple to learn. His videos are also very helpful and step by step so you can see how he does everything. I never felt in the dark so it was nice to have the simplicity in a 3D program like Maya.

About the most disorganized online class I've taken. His videos are extremely quiet. Most of videos are either from Lynda or from Youtube. Varying quality, but mostly hard to follow. He needs to edit his own videos. Frequently makes mistakes during videos that students must correct. Barely leaves feedback. Only final project is graded.

I've taken many classes with Mihir, he may be a little hard to understand at times, but he is one of my favorite teachers at SBCC. As long as your serious about his class he will go out of his way to help you succeed. The guy really knows his stuff and any problems you face hes sure to help you fix them and teach you what you did wrong. Awesome guy
Very willing to give help and feedback on your projects, also has some good experience and insight into the industry so if you want to know more, he's good to talk to.

Make sure you go to all of his classes. If you miss more than a week, he has all the right to drop you. His class is easy and he teaches it really well, IF you pay attention. It says ART136 but it really is MAT136. He'll give you a break in class and you can do your work in class as well with not only his help but with the help of the TA as well.

Mihir's judgment is very trustworthy as he has extensive history in his field. He is also incredibly helpful toward those who care enough to ask for guidance. His classes aren't difficult if you show up and make an honest effort. Work hard, have fun, communicate, and you'll learn a lot from him while getting an easy but well-earned A.

Mihir is a very nice guy and is always there to help you out if you need it, just ask. I've had the pleasure to take 4 different classes with him and I learned a lot. Attendance is not crucial to passing the class as long as you do all your work and turn it in on time. There is no homework in any of his classes, just a few projects.
Best teacher I ever had. Nice guy, but gets the job done

Kimberly is THE teacher to pass psych stats with!! Her lectures are amazing and explains in detail with easy to understand wording. Need to pass a stats class? take her!! she cares and want you to succeed and is available on zoom for extra help!!! I was even failing towards mid semester and her zooming with me once a week helped me pass, 48% to 76%

I am horrible at math and started out this semester terrified of her class. It turns out the book is super helpful, and Professor Mangel is one of the best teachers I've ever had. She goes over everything over zoom, and is informative and helpful. Do not hesitate to reach out to her if you did poorly on a test (or she may reach out to you first!

I am terrible at math but enjoyed this class. The first time I did bad on an assignment, Kim reached out and asked if I wanted to set up a zoom. That turned into meeting weekly on zoom, she ensured that I understood everything and never let me feel inferior for not getting a topic. A great teacher and wonderful human, she cares so much. Love her!!

Mangel was one of the best math teachers I've had at SBCC! She genuinely cares about her students. If you need to take Statistics or a STEM major planning on doing any kind of research, TAKE HER STATS CLASS!! She's teaches the subject in applicable ways. She is helpful and straightforward.

Well informed teacher. Lectures were kinda long plus two discussions per week that were 40% of the grade. If you try and put thought into this class you'll be fine. There were 5 extra credit opportunities each worth 100 points. Lightens the work load the last two weeks of the semester. Tests aren't proctored. Some low scores are dropped

The assignments were easy and the discussions were rather fun to complete, but the reason Prof. Mangel is getting a 2 is due to her grading. She grades everything super late, and uses invalid AI checker tools to grade, resulting in unfair accusations of using AI on students who care about learning and completing the work thoroughly.

Despite being an online course Professor Mangel is the sweetest and most hands on math teacher I have came across. She offers a lot of extra credit and reaches out if she notices you struggled on a practice quiz! She responds quickly to emails and always offers to help. She cares about all her students, I would totally recommend!

I want everyone to know how AMAZING professor Mangel is. She is so incredibly caring for her students and her lectures are so so so clear. She honestly made me LOVE statistics. I took AP stats in high school and I literally hated all of it but now I love stats. She is also super gracious with extensions. I love her so much. Take this class.

Stats was honestly hard, but if you meet with her during office hours she explains the concepts great! There is lots of opportunity for extra credit and as long as you do the work and stay on top of the assignments you should get an A.

Kim is an absolute amazing professor. She is kind and caring and goes out of her way (like meeting way after office or even working hours) to meet you halfway as long as you put in the work and show you also care. There is definitely a heavier workload; but it truly does benefit you to keep up with it and do it. She loves her job and it shows.

This class is very difficult because of the type of math it is, however, you are properly prepared for these difficulties. Professor Mangel is a great teacher and there is a heavier work load, but it is necessary to properly understand the math. Only downside is she gets behind on grading work so you may not know final grade till too late.

She is an amazing teacher and cares a lot about her students. She dedicates a lot of time outside of class to help you and is very available. The only thing I will say is that it is a very heavy workload and you definitely cannot fall behind. But her lectures are super helpful and well-structured, overall great class!

She was very sweet and kind, but this course was unnecessarily frustrating. The math itself was easy because she taught it very well. However, the statistical program we used was horrible. It requires you to (on a Mac) open a Windows desktop to use it. It made my computer crash countless times and I had to restart assignments 10 times to finish.

Prof. Mangel is one of the best professors I have had at SBCC. There are detailed lectures and slides, and the textbook is a must, plus it gives you math refreshers at the back. There are groups for problems and also extra credit problems as well as kahoot quizzes. I recommend checking over your work A LOT. She will help you succeed.

I had her for my stats class. Kim is literally the best. The class is moderately difficult (such is math), if you have a hard time GO INTO OFFICE HOURS, she is extremely helpful, patient and understanding.

I was scared to take a stats class and I was very discouraged due to struggling in high school. I swear she is hands down the best teacher I have ever ever had in my entire schooling. Her lectures are so helpful and she is so sweet and understanding felt like a second mom. She made me feel confident in math again. You have to put in the work!
She cares about her students and will do her best to help you out! One of the best teachers I've had!

Professor Mangel wants you to pass but you have to be willing to put in the work. You have multiple things do every week but don't let that scare you. Her directions and videos help incredibly. She is very well organized and has the modules setup perfectly. Can't slack and must have the time to stay on top it.

Mrs. Mangel is such a truly caring and helpful professor. You can tell that she loves her job and all the students that come with it. She is so upbeat and kind everyday while also smart and respectful. She is clear about assignments and she makes the material easy to learn by covering it repeatedly in class with videos and kahoots :)
Three tests, all done on multiple-choice scantrons ("recognition memory"). She really wants you to understand her class, your brain, and "learn to learn."

Kim is awesome!!! She is very intelligent and cares about what she teaches. Her lectures are very interesting, and take good notes and it will help you out a lot before the tests. She also provides a lot of study resources! Highly recommend!

Kim is the whole package! Funny, sweet, caring, insightful, knowledgeable, and excellent at explaining things. Don't let this fool you, however, her tests can be a bit dense and challenging. Luckily she provides a plethora of resources and materials to help you become successful in her class. Go Kim! Highly recommend her!

I LOOOOVED Kim's class. I've never seen a teacher work so hard just to make sure her students succeed. She held more office hours than any of my other teaches combined- and will tailor them towards what you need too! She is incredibly caring, smart, sweet, and kind- there is no way you will not do well in this class if you put in the effort.

Kim cares about her students personally more than any other professor I've met at City College. She's always available to help and sets up extra office hours with either herself or the tutor every week. She wants everyone to succeed and does her best to help, but it's on you to put in the work and learn the material.
She actually made me like stats. One of the best teachers I've ever had! Caring, understanding, offers tons of resources and incredibly chill. If you're taking stats, take her!

Kim was amazing! She wants all of her students to succeed so she works overtime to make sure she can accommodate everyone needs (extra office hours). She also provides a lot of opportunity for extra credit. This class has two midterms, a group research proposal and an essay, all pretty straightforward if you read the directions. She's a sweetheart!
I loved taking PSY100 with her, her lectures were interesting and insightful. All the homework and tests were online and were super easy.

Hands down the best Professor I have ever had! The class was engaging and if your prepared to put the work in you can get an A. She took time to answer the classes questions individually, and made it her job to make sure students had every opportunity to get the help they needed.

Amazing teacher! Very caring, funny and understandable. She posts the textbook HW pages, the class notes and class activities online. Attending class is very important as the content is complicated and you are likely to have quite a few questions. She is extremely helpful so attend her office hours whenever you can. Tough grader but consistent.
She is great and this class is interesting. Study before midterms!!!

I think the class was good but I really hate his pop quiz format. The quizzes and exams are worth most of your grade, there is homework,plenty of time to do. The homework is graded off of correctness. Some of the lectures seemed to drag on, there are no lecture slides or videos, there is also no practice exams. Overall it was interesting.

He might be better in-person, but his online class, although not terribly difficult and well-organized, felt kind of dry. I shouldn't be saying that about a CS class. Little to no actual engagement with the class, no feedback on assignments. I didn't think he was terrible but I also probably wouldn't take a class with him again.

He doesn't really engage with the class much and doesn't give feedback on assignments. CS Circles is also a frustrating platform with an outdated UI that looks like it's from the 90's. His class isn't terribly difficult and is well structured and organized, I just wish he engaged with the class more, especially considering it's an online class.

He's not that interesting or unique of a professor, but thinking back on everything, he explained things pretty well and was pretty organized. I did all the work in the lab but that ended up being okay. I did 104 but that isn't listed here anyone for some reason.
One of the easiest classes i've ever taken. I probably only dedicated 30 mins a week to this class and still got an A.

Professor Sam is one of the best I have encountered in my educational career. His lectures are always clear to understand and he is straightforward on what it takes to do well on exams. His friendly demeanor, light sense of humor, and ability to connect to his students are a bonus. I feel fortunate to have been exposed to his teaching.
