One of the best teachers on campus. He explains everything so clearly and goes through examples to make sure we understand. He does have the occasional pop quiz, but will definitely drop hints that one is coming up. I loved the programming assignments that went along with the class - I ended up changing my major to Computer Science because of him!

Sam is a unique breed of teacher. He knows how to be your teacher and your friend, which is refreshing. He knows how to communicate with students and saved us money on a book with cohesive slideshows. I love what he is doing with the CS 101 courses lately as well, teaching python as an intro course is a great idea. I need to take Sam again soon :)

He loves teaching and loves the subject. He's well prepared with PDFs we could download for every topic. He creates examples, and when you say AHA! that's it, he says but let's test this differently and shows you what the answer really is- it's easy to remember his examples. He's top notch; I learned so much. I loved his energy and his style.

Prof. Sam has a love of the subject that is evident in his demeanor. He asks lots of questions, and then demonstrates the correct choice through code, and his examples make it easy to remember. He is well prepared, unfailingly encouraging, and a bright light in the SBCC Computer Science Dept.
It was really a fun and entertaining class. Professor Sam knows a lot about digital design, and you can tell he enjoys it because he gets really into it too.

Awesome fun class, but he read out of his notebook all the time and didn't really have answers to questions that were not immediately the example he was using. Very confusing explainer of more complex problems, as if he didn't really know the answer himself, but would continually look stuff up in his notebook.

Genuinely wants you/sets you up to succeed.Content heavy class.Structures + organizes the class well.Lecture videos are great.Extra credit is built into the class. Math Lab is good for building study groups and finding great tutors to help you.Very doable—just requires a good amount of time outside of class.Take it with her.Great teacher overall.

Great professor - clear course structure, excellent lectures, fair exams. Lots and lots of homework, so be warned and have good time management (in person meetings are mandatory). I thought she was a sweet lady, definitely wanted you to succeed in this course. Have a good fundamentals foundation and adequate time to complete work, you'll be fine.

Worst Professor I have ever had. Please save yourself. I have always been very good with Calculus, however she makes it impossible. She teaches horribly. Nearly my whole class dropped. Her tests are impossible and half of the information you learn will just not be on the test. Run while you can. Your mental health will be ruined. TRUST ME..

She's gives very hard exams! The homework work would always take me like 2 hours to do and that's rushing it indeed treats this class like a full time job have to be very ! Dedicated to the class even though I was it wasn't enough

Please do not take her class. I am telling you because it was traumatic for me. She does not care about the students and is not helpful. As a person, she is not nice either. I had withdrawn her class for the sake of my mental health.
Non-captivating and is just rude. I would try to take someone else you can actually learn from.

Easy class if you have strong algebraic fundamentals and are disciplined enough to do the homeworks (there is a lot of homework). Pre-recorded lectures were high quality and well-structured. I noticed my classmates that struggled and complained about the class/material were lacking in fundamental math skills.

I really liked the structure of her class, but the homeworks were unnecessarily long and repetitive. I was spending at least 2 hours a day on this class including the weekends, and up to four-five hours a day during test weeks. If you go to her office hours she'll like you more and will be more lenient with partial points on tests.

Professor Sharareh Masoonman will assign more homework than any teacher you've ever had. She will treat math 150 like a full time job, and will be passive aggressive if you don't know how to do something. Do not take this class for your own mental health, even my math savvy friends had a miserable time.

Tough class with frequent homework and exams. Lectures can be dry, but using resources like unstuck ai and the free textbook helped me stay on top of the material. Overall, a challenging but manageable course.
Masooman is not approachable and has poor teaching skills. The college should consider hiring other teachers who actually care. She is passive aggressive and makes math harder than it needs to be.

This teacher was awful! I felt I could't ask questions during class because she humiliated me a couple of times. Keep in mind this is a flipped class. Meaning you watch the lectures on your own at home. Then go to school for 2 and half hours to complete 1 PAGE of work. The teacher is just rude, would not take this class ever again.

Really good professor but challenging! The class fast paced and the exams are difficult. Keep on top of homework assignments! Utilize the ask for help on Webassign she is pretty quick with her responses and they are very helpful. Overall I think she's very good there is a lot of group learning so find reliable people to study with.
Be prepared for fast-paced learning. You HAVE to stay on top of your work, but she is accommodating regarding classwork. Lots of group learning, try not to be dependent on your peers.
Awful
Great professor
good luck on the discussion category

Understanding and patient. Treats you like an adult and overall respects you. Online lectures let you go at your own pace, and gives you plenty of time to study for exams. Staying on top of work can be hard, but the penalties aren't bad.
I don't think she is fit to teach an online course. That's all I have to say.

Great teacher. I'm confused what the other reviews here are about. But I can say there's a lot of people who don't want to put in the work and want an easy A. If you are willing to put effort in and want to really learn Math, take it with her. Otherwise don't take the class and then complain for being the lazy one yourself.
You most likely not going to have an A with her. She might be the reason you won't transfer into your top school if you take her. Awful teaching skills and a non captivating personality.

No just no don't do the mistake of your life and take a class with her. Trust me it's not even funny how a professor can be so harsh and dull. These professors are very dangerous , it's very very hard to get an A with her. The 5 credit course will feel like a 10 credit course . I really can't believe she still teaches, I'm shocked.

Easiest way to fail Math150. 5 homework assignments per week that take up a lot of time. Hardest grader I've probably ever had, very picky about problem format. and she gives very short amount of time for the exams with questions that can take up to 10 minutes to complete. Also cuts into your exam time by talking a lot before the test starts.

literally the worst professor I have ever had. If theres any other professors teaching a course that shes also teaching choose a different one. Least helpful person ive ever met and if you have a question dont bother asking it. She explains everything in the most difficult and confusing so good luck understanding anything shes trying to teach.

On her final exam she spoke through a portion of our test time, and only allowed us 3 minutes per question; she does this for all of her exams. Your only saving grace is the Web Quest because unless you've taken this class before or are extremely familiar with the formulas, it'll be difficult. Study domains. Last you're on your own, get a tutor.

An absurd amount of homework. She is one of those teachers who thinks her class is the only class someone takes. Her teaching itself isn't very great either as she just does problems and doesn't actually explain how to do any of it. Not a good teacher and would not take again.

She is awful. She only does office hours for less than an hour while she gives more than hour long lecture videos with 2 hour homework. I literally have been using the school provided tutors for everything because she cannot teach. Dont choose her ever. She is not a good person giving homework for everyday. Even giving homework on exam days.

Professor Masooman is a terrible teacher to take for a topic as difficult as calculus. I have been to her office hours numerous times but still, have to teach myself everything. Her notes are bareboned and don't even go with what is found in the homework. She doesn't allow notes or even a cheat sheet on the exam. Honestly the worst teacher at SBCC

I hated this class. She assigns a ton of homework and she is not a very warm person. I would not take this class ever again and I would not recommend her to anyone. She also doesn't assign extra credit unless you get every question on a test or quiz right. If you don't test well good luck keeping your grade up in this class.

If you really care about the class and go to office hours/class she will help you a lot, but if you slack off she won't have sympathy for you. There is a lot of homework throughout the week, but she does have some extra credit opportunities if you watch the videos. All in all, difficult but rewarding class. Don't take if you want an easy A.

Brooke Was one of those teachers who sticks with you after your class Brooke is such a kind and compassionate woman who cares deeply about what she does and she passes on her knowledge of ballet and jazz to each of her students including myself I need more than 350 words but what I will say is you should absolutely take the class!!!
I would definitely recommend Brooke! Her dance classes are fun and she makes an effort to get to know each student individually.
She is a really really nice teacher and she cares about her students. Beginning jazz is not a hard class, Why not enrich yourself.

Brooke is one of the most caring instructors I ever had at a university/college. She makes you feel special even though you don't feel like you belong or doesn't develop as fast as the other students. She shows how important warm-up and stretching and muscles is for dance.
DO NOT TAKE she means well but will you won't learn anything
She talks about herself A LOT and I didn't learn anything from this class. She is also really bad at using technology, you won't be able to see your grade until the end of the semester.

she is a super sweet lady and means well. however, she is unorganized, doesn't use canvas, doesn't make instructions for assignments clear, and assigns 7-8 page papers with very short notice. not clear with communicating with students about deadlines or their own grades. i would definitely not recommend

Super sweet lady, but so confusing. Barely uses canvas and doesn't let you know anything about your grade until the semester is over. Also assigns a 7-8 page essay the week before finals. Very confusing about deadlines and I didn't get my syllabus until halfway through the semester. Changes deadlines randomly and doesn't announce it on canvas.

Her instructions are completely unclear and she doesn't use Canvas at all. She didn't give out our syllabus til week 6. She's completely unorganized, unclear, and never stops talking about things that literally aren't important. Only wants you to write how she wants you to write. Doesn't give due dates but is always fitted to the max.

Jody is extremely passionate about her work and includes amazing and helpful works of literature in her class. Jody does NOT use canvas almost at all which can be challenging. It is very important to go to class if you want to succeed. You won't know your grade till end of semester and she really likes when students write a specific way.
she's not good with using canvas and posting the assignment. and she take a long time to response to your emails, so there is a lack of communication

I put in so much effort and she only teaches you how to please HER specific writing style/ I did not become a better writer out of this class, I just learned how to kiss up. She is a sweet person. but a TERRIBLE teacher. So much work to learn NOTHING. Felt like I was in middle school again. Unless you can afford a therapist transfer schools.

Lovely person BUT: disorganized. Unclear bout hw. No syllabus until over a month in. Assigned a 4 page paper ON FINALS WEEK ON TOP OF the FINAL and revisions to an 8 PAGE PAPER. Doesn't use canvas. Won't know grade until the end. Wants everything printed out and wants you to keep EVERY paper to be submitted at the end manually.

Jody is my favorite teacher so far! She does not really use canvas, but the whole semester's due dates are in the syllabus/packet she gives you. She is a little disorganized but that is because of how passionate she is. She really helped me with my essays during office hours, and is very funny.

If I had known that we wouldnt be reading usual novels and mostly plays and short stories, and that we would be graded by a few paper wed written throughout the semester, I probably would not have taken this class. She has a passion for her what she teaches, but it can get lost in how unorganized she is as well as unclear instructions.

I love Prof. Millward! She's so intelligent and an amazing person and very passionate about what she teaches. She does not use Canvas so it takes a minute to adapt to the format of the class, but definitely worth it!

Worst english class ive taken. She doesnt use canvas & is extremely disorganized. The storys we wrote essays on werent very interesting besides Trifles & Medea. Dont recommend her class at all. You will not know your grade until the end of the semester which doesnt really seem fair to me but whatever.

She is very disorganized. I have no idea what she was talking about half of the time because she likes to going back and forth with the topics and expects you to somehow take notes in the correct order, which is extremely difficult. She only gives oral announcements about assignments most of the time. No directions and unclear about due dates.

Jody is cute and funny, however she is EXTREMELY unorganized. She doesn't clearly state when assignments are due, and gets mad if you don't have them. She also is not always in her office during office hours which can be frustrating. She has never answered my emails, I always go to the TA for help. No Canvas, so you don't know your grade.

She is a nice person, just super disorganized and doesn't use canvas so you don't know your grade till the end of the semester. She assigns journals and reading and what not and gives you points but you just never know where you stand in that class. Put in the EFFORT and do what she asks of you and SHOW UP TO CLASS, you'll probably get an A

Jody didn't give us a syllabus until after a few weeks of class, and then she didn't even make an attempt to follow it at all. Within the last couple weeks of class she assigned an 8 page research paper on a book she didn't spend enough time on, a 4-5 page essay for self-reflection, and a final that was unrelated to these things. Do not take her.

She's obviously an incredibly intelligent woman, but her organization for the class is lacking. We never were handed a syllabus and she would often give assignments verbally as class ends, so they often were forgotten. Then, after a lax semester we suddenly had two huge essays due. If you like actually knowing what to do in class, don't take her.

Dr. Jody Millward is a dedicated teacher, she gives you helpful feedback on papers for revisions and she is always in her office talking to students. Yes, she can seem unorganized, but that is mainly because she teaches more than one class with about 25-35 students in each class. She wants you to succeed but she will also fail you with a smile.

She assigns a lot of essays and you can do revisions. She's very helpful but you have to go to her to get the help. Writes comments on your papers to make them better and you just have to put in the work to get a good grade. I honestly think it's a lot of work and I hate writing essays but she makes me think and it helps a lot. She's the best at CC

She's amazing, you have to read in her class so that you'll understand her lectures. She's very helpful and will tell you what she wants you to write about. Very passionate about teaching and very nice. She's also funny and a busy person. Make sure to go to meet with her during her office hours to get help. Loved her class but you can't slack off.
Best English professor on campus. She's really funny! She snorts when she laughs and tells amazing stories about her life. She makes things interesting. :)

How anyone can not love Jody is beyond me. She is one of the best teachers I've ever had. She is funny, engaging, and extremely smart. I love when she laughs and snorts, so hilarious. She pushes her students to be great writers and she is very helpful. She is SLIGHTLY unorganized but she is constantly working to help. Take her!

Jody Millward is by far the best English teacher at SBCC. Although she is very unorganized, she is extremely passionate on making her students better writers. If you do the work and ask her questions then you will do well in her class. she also allows revisions on essays!! great teacher!!

Millward is SO unorganized. she has no idea what she's saying half the time let alone anyone else understanding her. due dates change constantly and she never specifies what she wants you to do for an assignment or when it is due. nobody ever has any idea what is going on in her class. would not recommend.
VERY unorganized. Due dates would change everyday. Homework would be assigned then not even discussed the following class. Received a "CALENDAR" which was to be our syllabus at the beg of November.

Jody is an awesome teacher! i would definitely recommend her class i wish i could take it again next year. class can be tedious but she's pretty funny and very approachable. she makes essays seem easy to write and due dates are flexible. love her!
All I have to say is take her class. !!!!!!

Jody is such a great teacher!! She really knows her stuff and will give anyone who asks much individual attention. Her humorous personality makes it fun to go to class! Readings are great. She can be a bit confusing sometimes so it takes for the students to read through the reader and ask her if something is unclear. Her office hours are helpful.

Dr.Jody Millward is the best!!! Like the last comment said she teaches english in a way that you understand. She is Funny and outgoing. Also she has an amazing outline for essays. take her class believe me you wont regret it. thank you Jody i know your reading this. ** chesse** hint remember the first day of class?
Shes super chill and sends mid-week reminders.

Rating for AJ105. She copies and pastes from chatgpt for lectures. Vague feedback on assignments. Snarky responses to emails. Powerpoints are messy and obviously lifted from uncredited sources. Super boring class.
Great structure to the class and clear on the PowerPoints, only thing I'll say is that sometimes she gives us too much material to read in the modules
(AJ105) Professor Minero is a great professor, the only issue I have is that her grading criteria is a little confusing.
Nice professor and very interesting class!

Professor Silvina Minero is one of the best professors at SBCC. She is extremely caring of her students and her subject. I took her Introduction to Criminal Justice class and it was amazing. I am going to miss her soo much!! Please take her class, you will not regret it!!!

I had a 79.5% and emailed her for extra credit opportunities as I was going through a tough time and wanted her to hopefully round it so I could finish with a B. She responded days later saying she cannot change the grade. I have had many professors, way tougher than her automatically round up because that's just ridiculous.
Didnt care about anyone, left nasty comments on my canvas

Professor Minero is super knowledgeable about criminal justice. Her presentations are clear and full of facts (she doesn't waste your time with fluff). She gives really good feedback on posts to let you know what to improve. The textbook is not dense and its actually interesting and a pretty easy read. Watch the presentation and use terminology
Good teacher. Not many assignments, so make sure you do them all! Take notes on the slides in class. Very caring and does want everyone to pass and learn the material.
I wouldn't recommend this instructor for any courses. I had a bad experience for Fall 2021 online. my writing was solid. she looked to leave ugly comments in my canvas feedback.
Super nice teacher, 100% would recommend.

I took COMP 232 - Programming Languages with Professor Minkova. She is an amazing teacher. I learned a lot from her course and truly appreciate her teaching style. She's always approachable, supportive, and understanding. she genuinely wants all her students to succeed.

I took this class online and it was not good, no videos just powerpoint slides to read. There was a lot of extra credit but she was extremely unclear as to what was on the final. And she changed the percentage of the final after we took it. She seems like a nice person but don't take this class online!!!!

Best instructor eva.Taken 3 courses w her-loved all.Material is digestible hw is fun+interesting.1 of the kindest most caring ppl Ik and will do anything she can for students to succeed.During hard times in life she was always understanding.Truly cares about students in a way Ive never seen b4.Could not recommend more regardless of ur skill level.

CS 107 is very hands-on -- you can see the effects of your code on a real-life object while working with the boards. Professor Minkova creates a fun, inclusive atmosphere. She and her team will help you with the labs, and you will pick up some good debugging techniques from them instead of only relying on the board's blinking lights. Great class!

Kira is a great teacher, her lectures are pretty well put together, and the lecture notes are available to review if needed. Might need to google a few things, but that's to be expected in a CS Class (imo). She is completely understanding and puts a lot of trust in us. If you work with her, she will work with you. Kind and funny! Would recommend!

She's a good teacher overall. The biggest thing to note is she gives A LOT of extra credit. As for the workload, I definitely didn't find myself being bogged down by the amount of work she assigned or anything and the difficulty wasn't too bad. The lectures can be long and a little boring, but she makes up for it with her personality.

Took her for CS-262. She'll ramble in broken english for an hour, then give you an assignment. Her notes are un-usable and are literally just arrows with words pointing at code (it doesn't even explain what the code is, it just tells you it's there). It's an ARM assembly class, so good luck finding outside resources (not even GPT can save you). RUN
Kira is fun and very helpful outside of class, but lectures could be presented better.

she literally the best, plenty of opportunities to get your grade up, lab time was really helpful. lectures were not the best imo, a little hard to follow, but the examples she gave were helpful. If you have to take 105 take it w kira she on top

Kira is by far the best teacher I've ever had- I cannot express how much I recommend her classes! She is an amazing teacher, I knew nothing about coding going into my first semester with her and she made me love it so much that I've decided to focus on CS in my future studies. She is beyond kind and truly cares about her students. Take her class!

Kira is LITERALLY THE GOAT! Love her shes so amazing. Shes an amazing professor. Be sure to listen since the course is a little fast. She will literally make sure you succeed. I took cs-104 in highschool with her too and loved it!! And i had to choose her again for this course! Choose Kira shes the best.
Really hard to get a less than an A in the class. She made programming so fun

Probably the best professor I've ever had, and that means a lot coming from me because I use ratemyprofessor religiously and have never had a bad professor. I was so scared to take a programming class, but she really is a great teacher I learned so much and somehow actually like programming now. Very kind and understanding, could not recommend more

Amazing cs professor which I definitely recommend taking! Her class is always fun and she does really good job explaining Java. Even if you have never coded before, this course is good foundation and you will learn a ton! 10/10

Kira is a very kind and humorous computer science professor. It is clear she is an expert in the subject and has much real world experience. She will give you unlimited help/additional instruction if you come to her after class. Cares about all of her students.

Kira is an outstanding professor, her classes are interesting and enjoyable! There are options for Extra Credit and if you put enough work in-- you will do well in her class! I definitely recommend this professor.

Professor Minkova is an enthusiastic, supportive professor and gives plenty of extra credit opportunities, but do not expect the class to be easy. Assembly Language is a difficult topic, and many concepts aren't easily communicated over lectures. My advice to new students is show up and do the work.

I have been Kira's student for 3 different CS classes at this point and it has been a real privilege to learn from her during my time at city college. Lectures are clear, very communicative and cares for students' understanding of material and success. On top of that, highly collaborative with writing recommendation letters for intern/scholarships

Great lectures, responds great to questions and tries her best to make sure you understand. Very understanding of technical issues, homework was interesting and fun assignments, extremely responsive to email and ready to meet online that day or next day. Overall, the class was a little easy, but the test were challenging.

She is one of the easiest professors I've had so far. Lots of classwork and homework, but prepares you so well for the exams (which are a piece of cake). Always available to help students and is very responsive via email. She wants everyone to succeed and would try and support as much as she possibly can. Highly recommend.

Prof. Minkova is just excellent: clear, no nonsense, always accessible to help, very well structured course with great homework problems and explanations. You will love taking her classes - she is an expert in the subjects she teaches and a genuine person. Really lucky to have had her teach that course (intro to python).

Kira did an amazing job making sure everyone succeeded in her Java class. Very gentle and patient with new students, and made sure to challenge more advanced students. She did a great job being available outside of class, to make sure everyone got the attention and grade they desired. Would definitely take again!

The skills you learn here are skills you will actually use. This class was designed in partner with a Google employee. The learning curve ramps up quickly then levels out after assignment 3. She is a really great and caring professor. She adjust due dates according to how far a long the majority of the class is. Plenty of EC if you work for it.

CS107 is a hard class. You want to show up to lecture. She always go over the tricky stuff you will need for the assignment. It will save you hours of headaches. Go to lab/office hours, she is always there to help. If you aren't able to be there when she is there, you can always email her for help. She is the professor to take for this course.

Kira is a nice lady. Linux is a hard subject. Lectures were not too helpful. Homework was very hard. Tests were easier relative to HW. Prof was willing to change due dates and grading to accommodate class's difficulties. If you have the time to spend on this class, you can get a lot out of it.

Is a lot different from other CS classes, but Kira is a great teacher. She gives a lot of extra credit points, so as long as you are completing all of your work, you should be able to easily get an A. Took a while to get lab grades in, but if you should be able to know what grade you got on it based on how much of the program you got to work.

Kira is a great professor for kicking off your journey into CS.I took her intro to programming class and it is an easy A. She gives so much EC its impossible to get a bad grade. She cares more about teaching you the basics of python and computer programming. The class is intended to smoothly move you into Object Oriented Programming.

Be prepared to teach yourself and do EC. She teaches you in a way that you feel like you understand the concept, but once you head to the lab the work is almost nothing like she taught. She even puts "google is your friend" in the assignments! I don't understand how she makes us use Google to finish assignments but couldn't just teach us it.
Go to class, do the homework and go to the lab. Any problems and she will explain it very clearly.

BEST TEACHER IVE EVER HAD! Maggie made us all feel so welcomed and loved! Not a single person in that class would have anything bad to say about her! She is very fun and plays lots of games! She really seems to care deeply for each student and so open to help anyone out. Even after it's over she still gives me a hug every time she sees me! love her
Maggie is a great teacher that gives everyone a fair chance and wants all of her students to succeed.

Maggie's class was amazing! You really learn some good basic acting skills as well as having a really fun class. It made my semester fun and made me happy to get up in the morning. You make good friends and learn a lot. Would highly recommend!

This was one of the most fun classes I've ever had. The group and team exercises make you close; Every single person in this class are now good friends of mine. All you have to do is memorize and give it your best, and Maggie is an easy grader, all about participation. Would recommend for all majors, just as an experience.
Wonderful person, very approachable. Class is fairly easy until you have to memorize stuff but just stay on top of it and you're good.

Maggie was just going through the motions of teaching ... life is tough for these folks with their massive teaching load and weak students. I suppose they do a great job given all they are up against. Many easy solutions were available to avoid some big logistical performance problems that happened later on. Make a fuss if you have a good idea.
Good

Maggie is the sweetest teacher I've ever had. She really cares about each of her students and their work. I was really sick during the final and she let us schedule it the following week which she showed up in a sunhat and brought her dog along for the show. She's the best teacher i've ever had and rekindled my love for theatre. TAKE HER CLASS! :)

Maggie is a very sweet woman who is willing to help support people who are passionate about theatre. Even students who are unprepared in their outside memorization of monologues, she continues to assist them. She gives great feedback and allows for many opportunities of growth.

I had Maggie for Beginning Acting and she was awesome! Great approach for beginning acting. Practice and preparation necessary to pass but that should be obvious. Attendance is mandatory and missing important days is hard to make up. Class is pretty easy if you make the effort.
She is an awesome teacher. You just have to actually listen to her. She gives great acting direction and can really be helpful!

Professor Mook is very sweet. No busy work, your grade is only made up of three exams and a presentation. Gives many opportunities for extra credit that are as simple as answering a question in front of the class. Participation matters (you get up to 50 participation points) and getting those extra credit points can make or break your grade.

Prof Mook is SUCH an angel. It's so easy to succeed in this class, there's not much outside of class work other than two speeches. Study guides for exams are EXACTLY what's on the exams so as long as you read the textbook there's no reason to do poorly. She is so kind and connects class to the textbook with fun interactive activities. LOVED her!

Maia is super nice and understands presentations are nerve racking. You'll have 2 presentations that you have to prepare, one you do research for. There's other casual presentations she encourages participation. She teaches all about interpersonal relationships and engages the class. There's 3 non cumulative exams that are a little challenging.

My favorite class this semester. She's so engaging, cares about every student, and always has fun activities that make the material more relevant/understandable. The exams are on terminology from the textbook, so be sure to study those. Easy A, very interesting material, and she's one of the sweetest and most helpful people!

Prof. Mook communicates effectively on what's expected. If you follow her clear instructions, you will do well. This includes reading, studying, and completing projects at deadlines. She cares about your success and will give multiple reminders of things that are due.

One of my favorite professors I have had at SBCC. She makes the class very fun and exciting. I learned a lot from this class and implemented a lot of the material into my own life. She doesn't have a lot of assignments in canvas, so the exams and final paper have a big impact on your grade. Easy class if you attend and do the work and study!

HIGHLY RECOMMEND. She cares about your concerns and wants you to succeed. You only have EASY 10 quizzes and 3 EASY tests. You have one 3-5min presentation that is very easy. The only challenge in her class is the reflection paper. She is accessible outside of class. She wants you to be involved in class and that helps your overall grade.

Mrs. Mook cared a ton about her class. she wanted everyone to know Communication and how everyone can improve on it. She cares about your participation and wants you to get comfortable talking to other people. she does have quizzes EVERY WEEK so be prepared. her tests happen every third chapter (1-3, 4-6, 7-9 and maybe 10). HIGHLY RECOMMEND

Ms. Mook is a very nice teacher who wants her students to succeed. She is always available and tries to help her students as much as possible. Moreover, she is very understanding and tries her hardest to make every student comfortable.

She wants you to learn and provides a free textbook. She has a quiz due every week that is 10 minutes for 10 questions which is a bit challenging. She is passionate about communications and wants you to succeed. Lots of enthusiasm and she explains challenging terms. Participation does matter and you self evaluate at the end of the course.

She is so sweet and genuinely cares about her students! Her class is set up in a way to make sure you succeed, she is very clear of her criteria/expectations and provides lots of examples and help during class. Also very accessible outside of class. Your grade is based off weekly (easy) chapter quizzes and 2 presentations. Easy A, great teacher.

Professor Mook really wants all of her students to succeed and makes that very clear at the beginning of the course. Her class is very organized on Canvas, which makes it easy to complete all assignments. There are a few discussion posts, weekly quizzes on the textbook readings, and two virtual presentations. Provides great feedback on assignments.

Maia is so incredibly nice and her assignments are not busy work and are intentional. She has two presentations but they arent hard because she gives a word for word outline of how to write each presentation. She also often comments on your work with positive feedback and she really appreciates responses back and have individual discussions.

Professor Mook is one of the best professors I have had! I have her online because of Covid but she has been so kind and understanding with every circumstance! She is so great about responding right away. If you need to take comm121 she is the best option! There are not too many assignments but I still have learned so much from this class!

Professor Mook is really kind and does a great job of creating a supportive atmosphere, which is pretty impressive in an online asynchronous class. The textbook she assigns is actually really interesting, and I ended up reading the whole thing. She is easily accessible when you need help and I wish I could have taken her class in person instead.

Professor Mook is one of the nicest teachers at SBCC. I took her class during the pandemic, so everything was online. We only had 2 zoom meetings, which were for the speeches. There are about 5 discussion posts, quizzes every week (open note), and 2 speeches. She responds to emails very quickly. She gives great feedback on your assignments. 10/10

Professor Mook was so awesome! I took comm 121 for IGETC and I really enjoyed it. She is such a kind professor and the class actually truly helps you with communicating with small groups. Being in her class was extremely pleasant. Learned a lot and enjoyed it all!

I was dreading taking this class. In fact, I even dropped COMM121 with another professor in order to take it with Maia because I heard such great things about her. She did not disappoint. You learn, but you're not forced to complete busy work. She's very caring and wants each student to succeed. Very positive and encouraging towards her students.
My favorite professor at SBCC. Her class was really interesting, and she made sure it stayed fun with lots of class exercises. She truly cares about her students and wants the best for them.

Maia works very hard so that her students don't have to. By saying that, I mean that if you go to class, she'll give you all of the information that you need to succeed. She is very accessible in and out of class. Graded by 3 tests, 2 presentations, and participation. This means that you can't slack, but Maia will make sure to keep you entertained

Mook is a great professor and seems to care about her students, but she comes off as very fake and the class was very slow paced which caused me to lose interest. This was definitely not a class I looked forward to being in. It was always a drag to go.

I absolutely loved this professor. Professor Mook gave interesting lectures and showed many enriching examples to engage the class. Homework was non existent aside from studying for exams. Which she provided detailed study guides listing all material that would be covered on the exams. The book is necessary but there are also some in the COMM lab.

Great professor who gives great feedback. Ask for help and you'll get it. 2 easy presentations. 3 exams rather than 1 midterm and 1 final, which makes it easier. She gives study guides, just use them and you'll pass. Take her class, you won't regret it!

Really recommend this teacher! Loves what she teaches and will help you if you ask for it. Have to do two or three speeches in front of class, but rest of class is just group/interpersonal (between two people) conversations. On top of speeches, you have two or three tests. Very easy class and it will help you grow.

She's an AMAZING teacher! Always in a great mood and always willing to help. Took her 3 hr class on fridays and time would always fly by. Only 2 presentations , 3 midterms and the final, which she gives you a study guides for. The practice exams given at the comm lab before every midterm really help! Totally would recommend her class!
Good teacher.. Doesn't give back quizzes. Tests are non-cumulative. Use many resources and you will pass

She is super nice and jumps at any chance to help you learn better, if you ask she is there for you no matter what and tries to alter the course to your learning abilities. Truly an outstanding professor!
Professor Maia Mook is without a doubt one of the best professors at cc. Please, please consider taking her class, whether you are a comm major or not.
If you need take 121 take hers. Best ever

Maia Mook is a great professor! Lectures are straight-forward, nothing surprising on the tests, and she's an all-around friendly person. I felt like I did terribly on our first presentation and she still scored me well. I wouldn't recommend missing the days other people present because she has you give feedback and gives participation points for it

This class is easy if you pay attention in lecture and take notes! She sometimes is confusing but she teaches slow and gives you time to understand the concepts. She also tells you what's going to be on the test. If you don't go to lecture you won't do good. Other than that, pop an Addy and rip the library a day before the test. Worked for me.

WORST CLASS IVE EVER TAKEN! She's not good at teaching and very bad at responding to emails. She lags a lot or doesn't respond. I took her summer class.The lectures don't make sense you basically have to self teach yourself. Tutors were no help they would never show up to meetings. The quizzes were on lock down browser and didn't make sense

It's a heavy class although Professor morales does a great job at giving engaging informational lectures. This is the second class I've taken from her and it's apparent that she's incredibly knowledgeable on the subject and wants her students to do well. I have a lot of respect for her and if you pay attention in this class you'll do fine.
If Econ is required for your major then take it at another school. The Econ department at SBCC in general doesn't seem to be optimal.

I enjoyed the class and found it super interesting and relevant overall, prof. Morales was kind and personable but lagged on responding to emails and wasn't much support for an online class. Took at least 4/5 hours a week for homework which is worth a lot and her exams were so so so difficult. would take again though just really prioritize it.
She's a strict teacher because the quizzes don't really pertain to the chapter. and the proctor, which took me an hour to complete just before the test. I had to withdraw from this online course.

Take this class if you want to be tortured. Don't take her class if you care about privacy. She makes you show your id and force you to upload your profile picture even you don't want to or you will be losing 100pts from total credit.

Exams are worth a huge portion of grade but she does reviews before each exam. MindTap the program she uses for online hw is BS, so frustrating. Exam material is pretty much the things she harps on the most during lecture so if you attend and pay attention to lectures and exam reviews you'll know what to look for in exams. She drops the lowest exam

She's a caring person when she see's that you are trying. The homework was irritating but I think reading the textbook and taking lecture notes is what really helped me pull through. She goes through what will be on the tests if you're there. She's very keen on people showing up. She is also very strict and whatever is in the syllabus is like law.

Attendance for getting a good grade in this class is a must. Professor Morales uses analogies that help her students grasp the material. Homework every week, but it is very helpful for exams. She gives her students all the resources to succeed. If you keep up with the work, lectures, and attend the exam review sessions, YOU WILL GET AN A.

I took her class for both 101 and 102, and she has been great for both. There's at most an hour of homework a week, and though MindTap is annoying, it's not that bad. She has fun stories at her lectures and makes learning economics much more interesting than most professors could ever.

Shes nice but is the worst teacher ive had. Ive never had a class where the homework takes that much effort and time away from studying when its unrelated to the exams that are 80% of your grade. The exam drop policy would be nice if we knew what we were being tested on. She needs to change how she teaches

why. just why. Either make the homework easier to learn or make it related to the exams. you dont have enough time to learn how to do both. Everyone i know has taken her and says the exact same thing yet she still doesnt change it. Also mind tap is horrible

homework is unrelated to the exams and you have to put a lot of effort you have to understand the homework just for none of it to matter when it comes to the exam. You will have a false idea of what youre being tested on the chapter objectives and study guide will all be leading you to something but then the test is different. Why does she do thi

The lectures, chapters, and slides will all have the same learning objectives. The study guide will be based off those and then will only cover about half the exam. You dont really know what youre going to be tested on. If you just want to get a B or C the class is fine but if you want an A I would never take this class.

actually horrible. she doesn't even really teach. you have to pay for mintap to do homework. hw also way harder than actual exams. also when you finsih an exam, you can't see what you got wrong and grades aren't posted for weeks so you don't know your grades until almost the end of the semester.
She sucksssss

Challenging class, but engaging lectures. Textbook is a must-read. Exams are tough - focus on studying with unstuck AI and other resources to excel. Overall, a rewarding course if you're willing to put in the work.

She doesn't really teach. You also have to pay and sign up for Cengage in order to do the class. The homework on there is way more confusing than anything we actually learn in her online lectures. Also all of her quizzes and tests are heavily proctored

The homework was probably the toughest part, as the questions were never like the ones on the exam. Her lectures helped a lot but I do recommend using youtube videos before the exam as that's what helped me! She's a tough grader, even if you missed something so little, she'll take points. She's a great teacher and you can ask her so many questions

Be prepared for weekly homeworks, quizzes, and discussion posts. There are five exams, but only four are graded. While the workload is heavy, the exams are fair if you study the practice sheets, as half the questions are multiple-choice. The professor is helpful, passionate, and answers questions clearly.

I found this economics class to be unnecessarily challenging due to the teaching style. The instructor relied heavily on assigning personal reading, often from dense and complex texts, without sufficient in-class discussion or explanation to ensure students understood the material.

The HW is all on cengage and canvas discussions, My main issue is that on the homework you frequently may only plot points and move lines on graphs for the assignment but those exercises will never appear on the proctored exams, but even if you took a non-college credit Econ class in high school you'll do just fine.

To be clear, your lowest three homework grades get dropped and your lowest exam gets dropped as well. The lectures are very engaging and they provide most of what you need to succeed on exams and homework but yes you'll have to be proactive for some of it. She does not deserve below a 4.0 IMO and def not for the reasons mentioned in other reviews.
Bad teacher

Be ready to spend a long time trying to succeed in the class. Her lectures made sense & they helped understand the concepts in the book but they were totally unrelated to the exams. The weekly hw assignments were so time consuming bc you were expected to be an expert on that chapter before the hw. I def learned in this class but it was stressfulll

This course was challenging if you do not have time. Lectures are 1 hour, and the information is not on exams or homework. You must have time to be tutored or read the textbook to succeed. Would not offer extra credit or anything to help grade even within 0.25% from passing.
Don't take this class if you want to learn, unreasonably hard for no reason. Gives no extra help and does not care about her students. Find another teacher

worst teacher I ever hard, you wont learn anything in her lectures and she talks about the same thing over and over, if you dont have time to get extra help outside of class hours you will not pass. Her exams and homework are completely different information than what she teaches in class, does not prepare you at all. Don't take this class

Absolute worst teacher I have ever had, do not take this class unless it is going to be your #1 priority. Her lectures are boring and the notes wont help you with the homework. You grade is based off exams and if you work or don't have time outside of class to go to tutoring or get extra help you will not learn anything. Do not take this class!

Very very easy class and A. Would not recommend to any Psych major because there's no tests so you're not gonna wanna pay attention. But really kind professor and caring. Not very organized but definitely worth it! I would take this class over and over bc it was so easy.
class was a mess.

Extremely easy, boring A. If you want to learn about Psych or it's your major I would not recommend this class. It is great if you are interested in Pysch and need GE. No tests or quizzes, easy but slightly time-consuming homework. Was disorganized at times. Easiest A if you do all the assignments.

Prof Morello is a solid teacher. Not particularly interesting but such an easy A. Homework is usually only a few questions, but tests are dense and study guides kinda suck so study thoroughly. She'll drop your lowest test score, but takes forever to grade anything. Work is super straightforward.
Julie is really nice and is very willing to help with anything you need. However, the class itself is quite boring. She is also very quiet so it helps to sit closer to the front.

Professor Morello really encourages students to engage in class and group discussions. The class was mostly students sharing their own experiences about various topics related to child development. The tests were kind of tough, but you can drop your lowest grade, and there is no final! Overall, easy class, and I would take her again.

She is very sweet, but I don't think she is a teacher by nature. All of the assignments, lectures, and tests are directly out of the textbook, so there's no creativity or excitement to look forward to. She's big on small-group discussions. She lags so hard on grading homework so I was never totally sure where I stood as far as grades were concerned

Interesting class but Julie is an unreliable professor, although very sweet. Many times I had emails that were never responded to. This class was once a week and the homework/reading is very manageable. Discussion in class is valuable too but again her lack of communication made it difficult for me and other students in the course.
Very Fair. She expects the class to participate in discussion and show up. If you do those two things, you will learn a lot and earn a good grade.
Morello is a saint. Very kind, and will take time to help you. Take this class.
I took her class on campus on found it very boring. I found it unnecessary to go to any of the lectures. It's essentially an online class, so if you can take it online, do.

She is a very sweet, kind, woman. Shes a new mommy so sometimes she treats you like you are little ones, also she isnt always on top of the curriculm and basically just reads from the book. All my learning came from the book..

This class will really give you a workout and get you in shape if you truly try. You have the ability to go at your own pace so there is no need to worry. He makes a lot of jokes that sometimes can come off as rude but he means well I think.
Seriously the best teacher on campus, his Teaching and Coaching class is incredibly inspirational and a must take if you're involved in any sports related major. Overall an 11/10!

Really good class and I can truly feel a difference. Coach is a really funny teacher but he makes you work for it. A really good workout and a teamspirit in the group that I have not got in any other of my classes. The class can be adjusted after your own ability so both well trained and non well trained can take it.

First of all Coach Moropoulos, is an amazing person. He is very passionate about coaching/teaching. I was so eager to attend every class. I loved this class because it explored the option of becoming a coach. He had several wonderful speakers. The class is extremely easy. He aspires to help every student. TAKE THIS FUN CLASS.

Don't take this class unless you want to just do the SAME WORKOUT every day. It's super boring and you would assume that since he's the football coach that this bootcamp class would be hard but it's not. It's so boring and I hated going to class. Don't waist your time.
Great teacher an even better person and motivational speaker. His class is very easy all you have to do is pay attention and he will guide you through all the material

Super cool teacher. Super easy quizzes, even if you don't read the material, he basically tells you what is on the quizzes. 1 "skill presentation" that is on some kind of sports related skill. Super easy. Show up for the quizzes and your presentation and your golden.
super nice, and fun!Gives you a work out!!Boot camp made my butt hard!

Cares very deeply for her students and is willing to help them as long as they put in the effort to do so. She does give quizzes every week but they're easy as long as you read the text book and do the study guide. Your final grade is heavily based on a presentation so if you're not comfortable with that then consider a different professor!

This class was very interesting and relatable! The online quizzes are very textbook and lecture heavy, therefore it is important to not miss class. Mueller wants her students to succeed and was willing to help if needed.

This class was really interesting and I think it's very relevant. She's a really nice professor and genuinely loves hearing your opinions, there is a lot of unnecessary steps in completing simple tasks, but as long as you turn in everything on time you'll do fine in the class. Take advantage of extra credit opportunities when she mentions them.
Just a boring class. Don't take her course save your money. She has not care for her students

The quizzes and exams are essentially a breeze when completed online, but the two in person presentations are everything and can be difficult. Not only is showing up REQUIRED to pass the class, but the presentations are difficult, annoying, and will hurt your grade unless you are extremely prepared.

I started this semester thinking she would be awful since she came off pretty harsh first class, but she grew a lot on me and I will miss her lectures! The class is though with several quizzes, 3 hard exams, and 2 presentations, but she is clear with what she expects from you! She cares about her students and you will learn a lot from this class!
An incredibly hard grader, the tests are also jammed with as much information as she can fit into them. 2 presentations that will very likely sink your grade.

DO NOT TAKE! She expects you to be REALLY out of your comfort zone. Two oral presentations required or you can't pass the class. Will drop you if you miss 2 classes. No excuses for absences, whatsoever. No makeup work. Super hard grader, get ready to be there every second of the 3 hrs as she never stops talking, often redundant. Class Avg. 75%

She genuinely cares and wants her students to succeed. She will spend time and work with you at her office hours in preparation for the oral presentations. I honestly looked forward to every class and was sad when the semester ended. I wanted to buy Mueller a Christmas gift for being such a sweet human being. highly recommend!

The information in the class is genuinely interesting but the way it is taught is not. Be prepared for her to interrupt you while you are presenting. She tends to embarrass students in front of the class and I honestly felt she thought I was stupid by the way she talked to me in front of the class. Not my favorite class or teacher.
This is a wonderfull class and professor Mueller is a really big reason why! She is really attentive and inspiring. She is over all an incredible professor, I would highly recommend this class.

Professor Mueller was absolutely exceptional. She sets a model every teacher should follow. She was kind and caring (she wrote me a thank you note at the end of class), and she also was very respectable. So many professors feel the need to reduce their status to be "relatable", but not her. She (and the students) know she is already fantastic.

Prof. Mueller's course was by far the best class I've ever taken. I learned SO much from it and I will forever use the skills she's taught me. The material is difficult and there's 2 oral presentations, but it helps get out of your comfort zone. She is very caring and is easily accessible. Very kind lady and helps with the hard material.
Awesome Professor. Great personality and wonderful teacher. Definitely enjoyed this class!

I had her from 6pm-9pm and she made every hour worth sitting. She turns a complicated lecture into an interesting conversation. The projects and classwork are nothing out of the ordinary like 100 question scantrons and basic chapter reading. Seems to genuinely care about each of her students. Pretty irrelevant but she has a good sense of style.

The instructor is a good person, she likes to help students, but her lectures are so dry. Very tough grader and the test are very confusing and hard. If your plan is to have an easy A then her calss is not a good fit for you.

Shirley is a great teacher I really enjoyed her,Projects are fine, she's very clear in her criteria and I learned alot. However, the only con I would provide is that; her tests are nastily challenging,and it seemed to me that every question on the test, she's trying to throw a curve ball almost every question. Give us tests that you give guides to.

Great teacher and her lectures aren't boring at all. As long as you read all the assigned chapters, you'll most likely pass the class. Gives a 5 question quiz at the start of every class, which is when the reading comes in handy. Her exams are relatively easy since she gives study guides for them. But be prepared for 3 in-class presentations.

Shirley is so nice! She is considerate of all her students and offers help in any way that she can. In my opinion she is an easy grader and you only have three presentations (which turned out to be way easier than I thought) and one paper which was also easy (only 2 pages). She keeps things interesting and brings food to class :) would recommend!

Her lectures are really uninteresting she reads mostly from power points. It sounds very scripted and she goes at a very fast pace. She does not prepare students for presentations as much as I would have wished. She's too busy focusing on the text and power points. she also makes you do 3 presentations, I have heard other instructors only give 2.

I NEVER wanted 2 take Comm! I get so nervous speaking in front of a class but I HAD 2 take it. What I can say is, Prof Mueller made this class painless 4 me and everyone in it. She put us all at ease and was soo nice. U have to do 2 presentations and 1 big paper, it's so doable tho if u just plan it all out. if u hav 2 take comm, TAKE HER!
She knows what she's talking about, but she lectures the entire class and gives out so much information sometimes it's hard to keep up. She's really nice but tests are pretty hard!

I didn't mind her. She was quirky, but very defensive and scripted in her lectures. I really loved the kids in my class, which is probably why I can't remember being miserable in the class. The work load is large, but easy busy work sorta stuff. Weird assignments and lectures. Take it is ya gotta, what can I say?

I expected my Communications teacher to at least be an effective communicator--not the snappy, defensive, skull-numbingly uncreative and dry lecturer I was presented with after spending my hard-earned money and time on her 121 class. I got a B or a C, but if you care about learning or maintaining your GPA, avoid her like the plague.
very hard grader don't expect to get an A or B. pass or no pass would be a good option if u have already taken her. usually less than or maybe only one person in the entire class. good luck!^^

Ms.miller is a good person. Make sure you know the layout,directions,and execution of labs, skills demo.'s, and assigments to the the T. i had to ask for more specific directions because she just didnt know how to clearly explain the directions. She also "disagreed" with the text book and decided to start making up her own theories. Overall OKAY.

This class is very rigid and scheduled. I found it a bit dry and not enough actual usage tequiniques we learned. There was little interpersonal communication. Shirley is very good hearted and willing to help you. Make sure you follow the guidelines for the presentation to the T I know it's dry just do it. Also take her in the morn. She was often ti

I love love Interpersonal Communications especially with Ms. Mueller teaching! I love this class! It is soo interesting and amaz-zing! Take this class if your interested in this type of material . Ms. Mueller really cares about students, teaches really well, and loves what she does. Not hard at all! Just right!

Most definitely my favorite teacher. Not only was she extremely personable and so cool to talk to and share stories with, but was a great teacher too. She always made sure everything she said in class was understood by every single one of her students and didn't mind taking out extra time in and out of class to help anyone out.

She was my favorite teacher! I really enjoyed her class, the material was interesting and the class was usually fun. She really cares about her students and is very understanding. I wish she was teaching more Communication classes!
I recommend this class. I learned very much from professor Musacchio . She is a really great professor & helpful. Thoughtful, knowledgeable. She want you to learn, and she wants you to succeed!

The class is lecture heavy but you can also just teach yourself from the textbook. She knows what she is taking about she just doesn't know how to teach it. She tells nerdy jokes and gets sidetracked a lot in her lessons. At the end your final won't negatively affect your grade it can only boost it.

Very unprofessional and passive aggressive. Disrespectful of students time. In class we would work through problems which she didn't know how to solve then got frustrated at students for asking questions trying to get clarification. Lacked communication. Promised resources that was never provided. Be ready to teach yourself.

One of the worst classes Ive taken. She wasn't prepared for class, arrived late. Most of the class was struggling b/c half the time she had to ask students for help. 3 exams were in the final weeks of the semester. It felt like a waste of my time, money, & effort in paying someone who wasn't even really teaching me. DONT recommend her class!

She would mess up equations, but then do the correct one without explaining. Wouldn't respond to emails or if she did, she took forever. Got mad when students emailed her constantly over valid concerns. Wasn't clear on what to study for exams. Schedule exams close together end of semester. Had to teach myself. Majority of students felt the same.

Very disrespectful of our time, she is at least 10 minutes late to almost every single class and arrives not prepared. She is the most unorganized professor I've ever had, she's very scatterbrained, gets sidetracked, and gets the problems in class wrong. The material and tests aren't too difficult but overall she makes this class almost unbearable

She is very nice, BUT this is one of the worst classes ive ever taken. She is very unorganized and it effects the class.When she goes over problems in class, she 9 time out of 10 gets them wrong and has to refer to students in class to help her. I basically have taught myself all the material. And she scheduled 3 exams in the final weeks of school!

Genuinely one of the most frustrating classes I have ever taken. It took up until 3 weeks before the semester is over for any of my lab report grades to be entered, meaning no feedback on the reports and no idea how to get an A on them. She is extremely unorganized and seems to be always confused. I taught myself everything before each test.

She's nice, but her teaching skills aren't great. She missed two weeks of class due to personal issues. When she came back, she missed or ended lecture early. She a scheduled 3 exams within weeks of the end of the semester. Had to teach myself much of it or go to tutors. Many felt lost like we were running around like headless chickens.

She was nice, but her teaching techniques were subpar. She took too long to respond to emails. She missed two weeks for personal reasons. However, this left the class in disarray b/c we didn't know what we were doing. When she did come back many of our lectures were cut short. We were behind in lectures so they didn't match up with the labs.

Dr. Napoleon is one of the best professors I've ever had! She was hilarious in lectures so I never skipped a class, and she explains every topic very thoroughly. I recommend taking as many notes as you can during lectures, as I was grateful for them when preparing for exams! Pay attention, go to class, ask for help during labs if needed.

I have loved taking this class. The lectures are very informative and as long as you take one or two practice tests before each exam you will do well. I didn't read the textbook and had to watch 3-4 videos on topics I didn't quite understand but overall the class was structured well and easy for a summer course.
She's great! Can be a little scattered (often late or slow grading) but she offers so much help and clear study guides. She cares a lot and makes it easy to pass!

Dr. Napoleon is one of the best professors I've had. She makes the class engaging, entertaining, and provides you with everything you need to be successful in the class. Be prepared for class, practice all problems, and keep a consistent study schedule and you'll do great.

Dr. Napoleon is an excellent chemistry professor at SBCC. Her approachable lectures and supportive nature make her a standout. She allows for the dropping of the lowest lab grade, exam grade, and two homework assignments. Had to withdraw from a chem101 at diff inst. because of how rude the professor was, and now I have an A in this chem101 class!

So much fun!!!!! She was my favorite professor here so far. She is very funny and relatable. Love her lots, and wish she could teach all my classes. I will admit that sometimes she can be slightly disorganized, but she will always help you out if you talk to her about it. Love you Napolean!!

Her class is very easy, but I don't like her personality at all. She tries to relate to her students by talking about drinking/drugs. Very unprofessional and honestly uncomfortable at times. Late to every lab/lecture and has to rush because of it. Lets her anger show very clearly, and gets upset when students don't understand her teaching.

I have loved every class at cc except this one. I have good grades but rlly struggled and she could care less & made it very known. she was rude, wouldn't respond to emails, & was hard to talk to w/o feeling bad about myself. some people had good relationships w/ her but I just couldn't stand the toxic environment and decided to withdrawal.

Not good at explaining things and does not go in depth into explaining her lectures. She does drop some scores at the end and offer extra credit, but there was no study guides for the exams. When we had a study guide she gave a study guide with answers but didn't explain how she got them. She also said we can find answers of "sketchy websites"

She is not very good at explaining things and tends to just read off slides and get distracted by talking about random things like her zodiac sign. She is intimidating to ask questions to and makes it known she has office hours but then acts mad when you ask her something. She seems like a nice person but wasn't my favorite teacher.

Dr. Napoleon is a great teacher when it comes to delivering information in her lectures, but if you fall behind she doesn't show much mercy. I have received many passive aggressive email from her and all I have done is show her kindness. So its pretty 50/50 for me!

Absolutely an awful and unprofessional person. She does not know how to listen to other peoples opinions and continually shuts down ideas that are not her own. A very typical SJW who does not know how to show respect and compassion for other people or their opinions.

Her lectures are great and to the point with a touch of humor added every now and then. Tests are the biggest part of your grade. Biggest downside is if you're an athlete. She is merciless when it comes to balancing sports with school work. I was forced to sit out 3 of my matches. Highly do not recommend this teacher if you are a student-athlete.

I found Napoleon to be very disorganized. Shes constantly playing catch up and is never ahead with grading assignments. The labs are 30 percent of your grade so make sure you do well on those! Make sure to double check your sig figs because she knocks off -1/2 point for each incorrect sig fig and hardly gives partial credit on exams.

Napolean is an amazing professor who wants her students to excel. Chem intimidating and one of the hardest classes, but if you do the work and come prepared for classes you'll be fine. She is very understanding and always happy to meet to walk you through anything. Make sure to see the tutors. She drops a few grades at the end as well!

She makes the class interesting and entertaining for a Chem class. I took her class for my summer session and didn't regret it. She drops your lowest test, lab, quiz scores and curves up your grade. Make sure to do the practice problems she assigns every chapter and you will definitely get an A+!
Loved Dr Napoleon! She is absolutely great, made lectures interesting and labs were great too. Always available and willing to help, she really cares about her students

Dr. RzaRae is Ah-MAZING!!! I wish she taught more chem sections, I'd take them all! I used many tutors & OH's, bc Chem is hard, even the intro class. Dr Napoleon makes lecture interesting & funny while explaining difficult topics; she even made quantum mechanics as easily digestible as it can be. Do practice problems, they're key!! #chilipepper<3

If you are willing to put in effort, you will easily pass this class. If you do the practice problems and know your equations you will do well on the exams. She also drops your lowest test score at the end of the semester.

Dr. Napoleon is an amazing professor. I took Chem 101 with her over the summer, and having never taken a chemistry class before, I was nervous about what to expect. As long as you're willing to put in the work, like reading the assigned chapters before lecture and doing the homework problems (ungraded), she will recognize that.

Raeanne is really dedicated to making sure you understand the material during lecture. Definitely do not be shy to raise your hand and ask questions during lecture. She might sound like there is some rudeness/conceitedness in her tone but it isn't real, don't let it get to ya. Also USE. HER. OFFICE. HOURS. and email!! She is really quick to respond

Dr. Newman is absolutely incredible at what he does. He is incredibly caring, and emphasizes that he wants his students to be prepared for life outside of college, and encourages using the assignments he gives as learning opportunities about whatever topic you want to know more about. He is funny, smart, and just a great person.

Professor Newman was amazing! He really cares about his students and genuinely wants the best for them. He's passionate about public speaking and makes it way less intimidating. The material is very doable and easy to comprehend. There are typically 2-3 weekly quizzes but it takes 15 minutes max to do. Would definitely take this class again!

Donte Newman was the best online public speaking professor. He gives you light light light homework so you can focus on the preparation for your speeches. If you show up for your speech times and memorize your writing you will easily have an A in the class. He truly wants all of his students to succeed and cares about the wellbeing of his students.
Really encouraging and great professor.

Online, Zoom optional(for extra credit). Write weekly discussion + post video discussion every 2-3w + 2 reply to peers every time, no HW. Topics are controversy but also interesting to challenge myself. Instruction is clearly displayed+explained. Prof is very dedicating in giving feedback & I improved so much from that. Easy A if you put in effort.

If you try in class and are confident, you'll get a good grade in the class, and it's a nice way to practice public speaking without too much risk. He can tell when you tried with a speech, and that's all he really asks. Super funny too, so showing up to class was something to look forward to!

He's a funny teacher, makes class interesting. Public speaking can be a hard topic for some but he makes the class very interesting. But he's not very helpful when helping with outlines, and he's a tough grader.

The grade feedback seemed generic each time but the class was not hard and the speeches were relatively short at around 5 minutes. Two mandatory zoom speeches required, final was a recorded speech. Really liked his enthusiasm and would recommend him to anyone. Not too much work each week really, just practicing.
Super great guy! As long as you do the weekly discussion you will get an A. Did the hybrid class, highly recommend!
TAKE HIS PUBLIC SPEAKING COURSE!!!! I loved this class! Dr. Newman is probably one of the most inspiring, funny, and amazing public speaking professors at SBCC. You won't regret it!!
This most real professor I've had in my entire live. Truly based. Super understanding. Honestly just a cool guy.

Professor Newman is a great teacher and person. He genuinely cares for his students and wants to best for them during and after the course. The class was low-stress but I still walked away with fundamental public speaking skills. overall, Donte is hilarious. Please take!

This guy is funny, caring, and a very well-rounded instructor. Does not grade harsh, yet he still grades thoroughly giving amazing feedback. He accommodates even small needs and goes the extra mile as an instructor, and he simply teaches well. I definitely enjoyed this class.
Excellent Professor. Effort is all you need. The best way to show it is to join Zoom Meetings.
Professor Newman is one of the best professors I've had thus far!

Dr. Newman truly cares about his students and WANTS YOU TO PASS! Each assignment is straightforward and relatively easy as long as you truly put in effort. Reading the textbook is not 100% necessary, you succeed with a skim of the reading. He is inspirational and very positive and encouraging. I will be taking another one of his classes.

This course was straight-forward. Complete your assignments and you will earn the grade you want. Dr. Newman cares about your success--just as much as you do! If there is any chance to participate in his optional Zoom meetings, attend! You'll gain as much as you put in. Greatly recommend this professor to everyone, especially Communication Majors.

Amazing professor, Class was fun with a light workload. Speeches on Zoom were nerve-wracking, but Professor Newman's humor and personal approach made it enjoyable and easily my favorite class. The textbook was a free online ebook, and quizzes were easy read the short chapter, and you'd have all the answers. Could not recommend this class more.

There wasn't one class that I didn't leave without my cheeks hurting from laughing so much. Dr. Newman is amazing at what he does. His course is engaging and inspiring. Public speaking was hard for me because of my anxiety and often spent a few hours drafting each speech, but listening to other's speeches was an awesome learning experience.

Dr. Newman is very encouraging professor who goes out of his way to support his students. He is available to listen and help with course material or otherwise. The assignments in this class are fun and thought-provoking. By far my favorite class I've taken here and I enjoyed it so much that I would recommend any course taught by him.

Dr. Newman is a super kind person, and he's a lot of fun to speak to if you go to his zoom meetings. However, the assignments were not very interesting. Each week we would have a discussion, which often felt like I was writing the same thing over and over again in my responses. Overall, the class is easy, if a little boring.

he's funny and generous w/ grading, u just gotta follow instructions. as an introvert, public speaking is tough for me & i was very worried, but was able to pass this class easily. highly recommend taking it online if ur like me! there are 4 speeches & some simple non-proctored quizzes on the textbook. i personally didnt care for the textbook tho
Its super easy if you follow the guidelines and dont use chatgpt
He's great! If you do the work and actually try, you will succeed. He has office hours, is there for you, and will help you. I can honestly say, he cares about his students.
Solid teacher.

Professor Newman is an extremely personable professor who puts you at ease in the first meeting and never intends to make class goal unreachable. He has an open door policy that is really reassuring. You can learn a lot about yourself in his public speaking class.
Probably the easiest A I've ever gotten, especially in a summer course. Not as challenging as other communications professor but for a grade he's great!

Dr. Newman truly cares about your success. His constant reminders through Canvas portal and emails keep you on track for your assignments. There's no excuse to missing your projects! His approach to teaching is "workshop" like and if you participate, you will receive the grade you want.
For 235, take it in Summer. Less work. His course is really easy. As long as you put in effort, you will succeed.

He was really supportive and great but also thinks he is really funny ... no comment. I think he really enjoyed stirring the pot because of course it was a debate class so I liked that. Overall he only had a few assignments but they were large and tedious. Good Prof. Better than Medel.

Never was a big sociology fan until this class. Highly recommend this professor. Do the work, the reading, and show up. Fascinating lectures and he's happy to talk to you about even weirder sociology subjects. Awesome.

Easily the best professor I've ever had. He is an absolutely amazing lecturer. I have ADHD and often struggle to pay attention in lectures, but this course was no issue whatsoever because he made the class so interesting. I wish he taught more different courses so I could take them! Grades are based on long-ish discussion posts and quizzes.
