Prof O'Brien's class had a huge impact on me. Sociology has became my favorite subject. Everything we learned fascinated me and I had know idea how much I would be into sociology when I was choosing my classes. His enthusiasm and passion for the subject has been an inspiration to me and motivates me to do well in all of my classes. Excellent Prof!

Prof O'Brien was the best ever. He is so funny, cares about his students, and always makes class fun and interesting. The class is lecture heavy but he makes the material easy to understand. The workload is very manageable as well and he gives good feedback. 100% recommend this class and would take again!!!
SEXY ASFFFF TAKE ANY CLASS U CAN FROM HIM
Took online over the summer, 1 long discussion post + 3 replies a week as well as 2 quizzes. Interesting material and easy grader.

Took 101 and 109 over summer and loved it so much. his content is relevant and interesting but there was tons of reading and writing 1200+ word discussion posts weekly. the lectures were great and i learned a lot. hope to take more of his classes 10/10

i've taken two classes by prof. o'brien, he is very charismatic and passionate! his classes seem to have the same formula of weekly forum responses of 700 wds, they're not difficult but slightly time consuming. he does assign a lot of reading and online quizzes each week but its so worth it for no tests or final/midterm!!! love him!

Prof O'Brien is in a class of his own. He makes class interesting and fun and I appreciated all his current resources and his thoughtful comments. He made us feel heard and valued our input. Not all teachers can teach, he does it beautifully. Homework every week can be challenging, but totally fair. Take his classes!

I took two classes with Dr. O'Brien and it's what inspired me to change my major and sparked my love for sociology. Follow his writing directions and cite the book and lecture. Fair grader and workload. Always super interesting lectures. His classes taught me exponentially more than I could have imagined and I thank him for that.

Firstly, 70% of his class is based on the discussions which isn't bad until he is really picky about what he wants in those posts. Personally, this was more a "how to research" instead of a "intro to soc" class which is not what I signed up for. This felt more of a "boss to coworker" vs a "teacher to student" type of class. Don't recommend.

Makes lectures interesting. I've taken a couple of classes with him and he's an amazing and chill professor. From what I've taken, he doesn't do midterms/ finals, just discussions with somewhat strict guidelines. Overall, pretty easy class. Pretty handsome professor. Overall, highly recommend taking classes with him.

Lectures were interesting and fun. The work consists of weekly discussions, a weekly Canvas quiz, and another quiz on an external website. It took me about two hours a week to do this. If you dedicate time and attention to the class and assignments, you will learn a lot and achieve success. But be prepared to put in a little extra effort.
THE BEST THE BEST. He is so understanding and chill. Lectures are fun and there isn't too much work. He makes sure to keep class interesting and fun, it was by far my favorite class I've ever taken.

Hes such a good teacher. You can tell he loves what he is teaching. This class is super easy too. Attendance isnt mandatory but he does in class activities for points some times. Hes super funny especially if you get a talkative class. Very easy to engage and grades pretty easily. Try to get him for your classes.

Professor O'Brien's SOC101 class is a must-take. The lectures are engaging, and the weekly assignments, while challenging, really solidify the course material. unstuckstudy was a lifesaver for preparing for quizzes and discussions. Overall, a rewarding class that I'd highly recommend.

Amazing Professor.His lectures were very interesting and always involved good communication. Class is mandatory because you need to take in class notes for the every other week 700 word discussion post. No mid term or final in O'Brien class.

Dr. O'Brien is well spoken and his lectures were well thought out. I took the hybrid class and the online portion was very well organized and structured. Workload is fair and he leaves kind feedback. The class challenged me but I genuinely enjoyed learning every week. I highly recommend!

Professor O'Brien's class was amazing! 7 interesting papers, 14 quizzes, follow directions (cite) and you will do great. I really enjoyed his class and I learned so much. This was my first introduction to sociology and his class allowed me to look at our world through a whole new lens. This prof makes a difference in the world of education.

I took Soc 101 and 109 from Dr. O'Brien online and I enjoyed them both equally as a sociology major. I have so much gratitude for Dr. O'Brien for his feedback and the interesting class material he provides. I highly recommend his classes to everyone.

Professor O'Brien's class is the reason I developed a passion for sociology. His class was so well done and his passion was infectious. He fostered a great learning environment and his lectures were so interesting. I highly recommend his classes for anyone interested in thinking critically about our society. Thank you Prof O'Brien!

Loved Dr. O'Brien. His class was easy enough. A 800 word discussion post every week was the only really assignments we had. His lectures are engaging and easy to follow. Honestly, show up, do the word, and you'll get an A.
Great class. Love the man. Sexy, hot and great lectures ;) I love being apart of a society that Daddy P is also in.
I loved this class and it was not too hard. Daddy Obrien was a amazing professor and kept the class interesting. I would highly recommend this class.

Professor O'Brien is very knowledgeable and passionate about sociology. Awesome lecturer and pretty to look at. The work load is very manageable with only 2 essays and a quiz every other week. While attendance is not required, activities will be completed in class for credit. Highly recommend this class.

Prof O'Brien is a great professor! I always appreciated his feedback and I loved attending his lectures. This has been one of my favorite classes I have ever taken. If you do the work and readings and come to class, you will do good. I recommend his classes to everyone.
HOT!!!!
Awesome lectures and professor, easy weekly homework just follow the directions and you will get a good grade. good GE class, everyone should take this class

Prof. O'Brien is probably the best prof. I have ever had, he is an inspiration to me. I major in Soc now and actually became excited to do the homework. Patrick O'Brien was amazing! He got brought in to take over my Sociology 101 course and his lectures were great and got me excited about the subject! Coursework wasn't too difficult and he's a really cool guy. Definitely take him if you can!!!

class is difficult and includes a ton of reading but if you show up and do the work you will be fine. definitely show up to all the zoom meetings. he's very nice tho and constructive and points out things he likes about your work then gives helpful constructive criticism also not a tough grader you just need to make sure you turn stuff in correctly

This is probably the worst professor I have had to date. His class is rather annoying, and grades are based on zoom meetings and discussions. No real lectures; everything is big blocks of text that deter your interest in learning. Do not recommend.

Honestly, this professor seems to care little about anyone. I told him how I had an emergency and basically said whatever, like hello?? Honestly, there is so many Zoom meetings required, and the exams are not so great, they make no sense. There are many lectures and readings, and personally, his material is not exactly clear.

Death in the family? he doesn't care. no excuse you'd better be there. Enough said, good luck with whoever has him.

Yes, there is a lot of reading. Yes, there are proctored quizzes. But, I learned sooooo much from this teacher. If you are actually interested in pursuing a career in psychology, this teacher knows a lot and he is more than willing to share with you during his weekly office hours.
It is to my understanding from information provided by the psychology department that this teacher has been barred from teaching on Campus. Enough said.

There is a ton of reading from textbooks and lecture, that you can't catch up with that correlates with hard graded proctored exams. There are required zoom discussions that are also graded hard and it's a large part of your grade even though you can't control how other people partake.

I have never experienced disrespect by a teacher until this class. He does not care for his students and is not understanding of things coming up. He was rude to both myself and one of my classmates. I do not reccommend any of his classes to anyone.

Awful. The professor gave no instruction, no course materials, no lectures, and the only assignments we really had were weekly quizzes and exams. He is a harsh grader, and the exams were remotely proctored. Take this class with Gina Bell instead.
Get ready for read, he is a very tough grader in group projects, there are 5 zoom meetings that are mandatory for group work and it weighs heavily on your grade

So far this professor has been the rudest teacher I have encountered in college thus far. He is not helpful as his comments on assignments do not help to gain constructive criticism, rather they shut down any personal ideas on material. Do not recommend.
Honestly do not take this section, it is more politics than it is psychology. Professor also sent numerous backhanded emails to me and other emails, was very rude.

He is not very available or tech savvy. You're basically on your own. His lectures are written so there is a lot of reading... read the text book, read the lectures. He sends a video each week from his backyard which is not very helpful.

Horribly rude when communicating, not understanding, he does not listen and replies horribly. His whole class is lectures, and not much work, other than group projects, so every assignment is highly weighted

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!! He will not let you makeup an exam if you're sick you will simply be given a zero, end of discussion. He's the worst professor I've ever had, nothing engaging, only material is the textbook, and all exams are HEAVILY proctored online.

I have a hard time understanding why his ratings are so low. I just finished his online accelerated course this summer, and it was a solid experience. I know about abnormal Psych to this day because of this professor...he is very hard, but if you stick with it, you will learn the material for life. don't bother with this class, the professor doesn't put any effort into teaching the course, you need to read the book and take quizzes every week, but the exams are proctored and has very specific indepth questions that we never went over, this is an amazing subject to learn, but in this course, you don't learn anything

Uses proctorio and gets low test score averages. Lots of reading especially in the 8 week version. Could be better in person but barely makes an appearance in online course. Blunt. Dr. Oropeza is an amazing professor. She really cares about what she teaches and makes sure you feel cared for too. She always responded to questions and made it clear what she expects from you. Overall, amazing teacher and person! Would take again

So amazing!! I had to retake this class because my last class was made so difficult, but Dr.O has made me find my love for learning again. Dr. O is a really good professor and has taught me to think outside the box with the reading assignments. Manageable workload and an easy class if you put in the work and effort. Would take her class again.

Worst ENGLISH professor ever. I did not feel welcome in her class.

She's a great teacher! Don't be afraid to participate. I promise it helps to dwell on your ideas and thoughts. As long as you budget your time, it's extremely manageable.

You have to show her that you care about the class, then she will go out of her way helping you. She cares about her students. She is talking about topics that are relevant today. She is the sweetest teacher every but always talks in such an unnecessary way, like she could be using regular words but uses more academic words that nobody understands. Professor Oropeza is such a sweet lady, she truly cares about her students, especially those who take the time to meet with her and talk to her. My writing skills definitely improved after her class. grinding clash during clash and a was still chill

Amazing class, she's very caring and wants her students to succeed. She gives detailed helpful feedback on the stages of writing your essays which I found very useful. Abby was the best professor I have had. I had her for two classes and she is such a caring, understanding professor. She is very lenient about late work and will accept it with no penalty. professor pasley is one of the best teachers i have ever had, she is understanding, sensitive, and flexible. her class has a lot of homework which was hard to keep up with at times, but she accepts late work with no penalty and will listen to your concerns.

Abby Pasley is the best professor for English 110. She is super caring and gives us breaks in class and lots of time to work. She also is super chill about late assignments and homework. Not a lot of work for homework and is super clear with instructions and assignments.

Professor Pasley is by far the best Professor I've ever had in my life and I would suggest everyone who wants/needs to take an English class to take her class. She's outgoing, friendly, caring, fair, and gives fantastic feedback. She offers redo assignments and extra credit. I look forward to attending her classes.

She is the best English teacher I've ever had! Writing wasn't my strength but now I'm more comfortable with it and I feel ready to write various essays. Professor Pasley is amazing! Her assignments are clear, organized, and thoughtfully constructed. She offers a lot support and guidance with essays, and is easy to communicate with outside of class as well. One of the best professors I've ever had! I highly recommend taking her class

I put "easy" above not because the class isn't challenging, but because Prof Pasley breaks up all assignments into small, manageable chunks. It never felt overwhelming or difficult, she gives thoughtful feedback and will help you grow as a writer without the pressure of a usual grading scale. She is the best English professor I have ever had. She is amazing and gives great feedback. Definitely recommend taking her class!

I failed English 110 two times before taking her class and passing with an A. You can just tell how much she wants her students to succeed and her way of grading gives every student the best opportunity to do well. She was a really great english teacher, honestly the best one I've had. She gives great feedback and is really there for her students and wants them all to succeed. Take this class. I mean it. She's so forgiving and honestly cares about students so much. Need to write 4 papers, one research over 6.5 pages, and some discussion boards. Also need to read a book called "there there." Really great feedback, super nice person. Take the class!!

TAKE HER CLASS! Answers every email and makes herself available. Her expectations are clear and you can tell she really cares. Gives excellent feedback and offers chances to revise on essays. Extra credit and interesting articles and discussions. If I could have her for my next english class I would.I really learned a lot!Work load is manageable.
gives great, positive feedback and I learned everything I had missed from years of bad high school English teachers.

Professor Pasley is exceptional! She genuinely cares about her students and will go to great lengths to make sure we pass the class. Always willing to explain and re-explain things if you are ever unsure. She gives excellent feedbacks; this is where she truly shines as a teacher. Given a chance, I would like to be in her classroom again. Tip: I found it very helpful to re-read her feedbacks from my previous essays before writing another draft for her class.
Love love love her! Amazing and so sweet, absolute character. Also great at teaching and making students who are hesitant about participating get involved and learn.

She's always available and is eager to help as long as you care about the subject. She has little patience for slackers. You have weekly meetings to speak in Italian with a tutor that grades you and if you aren't practicing it can become embarrassing. Join her zoom office hours, she is there to help. Participating means an A!

As long as you do the homework and participate in class you'll be fine. Exams are take home and if you can correct them with the tutor she'll give you extra points. She expects you to be prepared when you come to class, but is also very understanding when you can't. I enjoyed her teaching style and took her for 101 and 103.

Dont take this teacher if you are a beginner in Spanish. Her Spanish might be good but her way of teaching is soo confusing. She never knows how her online book works and she is extremely rude. But its an easy A since you can do your test over and over.

Carla is a nice professor who draws all lectures directly from the textbook. Although she seems to have little patience for unmotivated students, she genuinely enjoys learning about us during exercises in conversation.

Idk what is with all of the hate on here... she is an amazing teacher, literally the easiest A I have ever gotten, and if you pay attention you learn a lot. It would literally be hard to not get an A in her class. She is a sweetheart and will let you take as long as you need to turn in HW

I wouldn't recommend taking the class with her. She is extremely rude and doesn't have a good teaching style. I am fluent in Spanish and was confused a majority of the time. It's an easy A but I recommend you take it with a different professor.

Carla clearly knows how to speak Spanish fluently, but she doesn't teach it well. She speaks in Spanish most of the time so as a beginning level class we are always confused. She uses an online site for tests and homework that is very easy to get an A on assignments on tests, but she has a hard time working it so sometimes the due dates are wrong.

She is extremely nice but won't take kindly if you did not do the homework. Homework is not turned in, but it is expected that you do it so you can correct it in class. Tests are take home and they are pretty easy. Study guides to the midterm and final are very helpful.

This Professor is really knowledgable in languages, but cannot teach them. She just reads from the book and never gives us notes or asks us if we have questions. She expects us to teach ourselves. Being a 101 class, I did not appreciate that. You know it's bad when the fluent kids in the class are confused by the teacher. Do not take this professor

Mr. Perez is an excellent and compassionate instructor! He is most interested in his students well being and truly cares about them. Normal amount of chapter reading and writing to quick prompts (online) each week but easy if you keep up. Mid term paper, oral presentation and final paper are WELL lined out and not too challenging.

Very nice guy, cares a lot about you doing well. Weekly homework assignment was due every Sunday night and just taught you about how to better yourself through study skills and understanding what you want your career to be. Helpful class.

I LOVED Sergio and his PD 100 class. If you do your homework and show up to class, you will get an A! He is very kind and easy to reach by email. You also learn a lot about yourself and life. Take this class if you can.

He is a great teacher and very caring of his students. He wants everyone to pass his class and will help you in any way he can. He is very inspirational and believes everyone can accomplish anything. He's a great easy going teacher and his projects really help you put your future in perspective

Nice guy i guess. He uses every minute of class time if not more. Hw everyday before class. Class was pretty easy but the final was such a drag!!!!! totally unexpected and very vague on instructions. Final is 5 pages long!!! You will get dropped if you miss class!.

He genuinely cares about his students and if we make the effort he gives us the grade we deserve. He keeps the class entertained and tries getting us involved with everyone around us. I've grown a lot for the better and I'm sure he'll be happy to know that part of it was because of him :)
Very easy going. Cares about his students success.

amazing class! great for freshmen, helps research a possible major and a great class to meet people. NEED the book, you actually have to look through the book to do well on the quiz's. Amazing professor and counselor

HANDS DOWN THE BEST CLASS AND EASIEST A YOU WILL GET A SBCC!HE IS AN AWESOME TEACHER AND I RECOMMEND HIM TO ANYONE PLUS IT LOOKS GOOD ON YOUR TRANSCRIPT. EASY HW ASSIGNMENTS NO TEST OR QUIZZES JUST JOURNALS AND FEW PAPERS! TAKE HIM!!!!!!

Such an easy class! I LOVED his class! Very chill teacher and funny too...Interesting subjects. He gives lots of extra credit opportunities! Participation points for being in class. Homework consists of journals from the book which are easy! I recommend his class!! Easy A!

actually terrifying. she gives VERY LITTLE homework & is an easy grader. Says class is mandatory but there is quite literally no point in going unless you have an in class thing due, or want to rip your ears out. i have learned more overhearing conversations in public. she's not hard just psychotic. keep your head down and smile you'll be fine.

The actual spawn of something cruel and terrible. Good luck adjusting to college with her in your way, the instructions for her assignments were so unclear and when I confronted/asked her about it via email. The next day in class I was completely targeted, berated, and bullied. if you get a good grade, then she probably has a crush on you.
absolute worst teacher ever and could care less if you have life emergencies and wont let u make up any work awful

Easy A, very little work. One assignment a week usually and work is explained starting with the very basics. Not the nicest lady and definitely will call you out in front of the class, also kind of ran like an elementary classroom.

Extremely picky. Not clear with what she expects on assignments beforehand, then will give you a 💩 grade despite not specifying what she expects beforehand. No wonder so many bad reviews. If you need an understanding professor, she isn't the one. Just an irritating pain in the neck overall.

I took Meryls class due to a requirement to transfer. But I dont know how this woman got a job here or how she maintains it. . She's rude, impatient, and assumes that you can adjust to her class and teachings asap. She bullied me out of her class and embarrassed me in front of all of her students. if youre not a favorite you. will. not. pass.

If you are interested in English and generally enjoy the subject do not take her class. This professor only uses condescending language during her "lectures" and likes to ask questions that she expects everyone to know the answer to. No one participates in class because its just not worth it.

All u have to do is stay on top of ur work, attend class, and ask for help. She makes instructions clear + and has high expectations for her students bc she wants to see growth. She also has an assistant who I highly recommend meeting with if you're stuck on something. A major reason why I did well is bc I scheduled frequent meetings for my essay.

The lower ratings are correct about her personality, but as long as she likes you, you'll do fine in her class. Definitely has favorites, but she's funny and encourages her students to participate. I like her but understand why others don't, course is easy with 1 assignment a week. DON'T PROCRASTINATE ON THE RESEARCH PAPER.

There was one assignment a week that was very easily done. One research paper that you can retry.But the class I was in had very little participation in it so, I did not put my 100 percent but enough to stand out just a little bit and she seemed to take a liking to me. Moral of the story show that you are putting effort and it is a great class.
I like this lady. There were only like 5 students in class one day and she just bought us all coffee and we did our work outside the cafeteria. She weird af but I like her.
Doesnt use canvas/barely knows how to give you accessible materials. Her assignment setup is horrible to read making you not know what is required and what is not. DONT TAKE ONLINE COURSE

I would not take her again. I do not think her class is difficult if you do the assignments on time. She is very picky on attendance yet makes showing up to class feel pointless. It will be her talking most of the time and then calling on students who do not want to talk, making them feel uncomfortable. Her comments are sometimes awkward and mean.
WORST TEACHER EVER DO NOT TAKE EVEN IF TRANSFERRING DEPENDS ON IT!!!!!! UNFAIR & UNCLEAR

Pretty good professor. I really enjoyed her class structure and choices of literature. However, her instructions can be unclear like our final, she wanted a research paper & then called it a literary analysis but still wanted a research paper. Also, she used Spanish poems in her class which can be frustrating & inconvenient to non-Spanish speakers.

There is only one assignment a week and your entire grade basically revolves around that. Assignments are so unclear and confusing to find instructions. Insanely hard grader, if you mess up in the beginning there is so fixing it.

If your first essay/draft short story is not good. Forget about passing the class, she will already tell you that it is impossible for you and will recommend you to drop her class even though it is your primer paper.

Honestly, this class was pretty easy. There is only one assignment a week, if that, and we only had 2 big essays. Lectures weren't mandatory and were recorded, all lectures are easy to understand with some tangents. Prof Peters was a tough grader for the essays, but if you do the weekly assignments you should get an A easily! Lots of extra credit.

I really enjoyed Meryl's class, and if I'm being honest, with her teaching style I kind of felt like I was in high school again, Which is not a bad thing! She's a fun and funny teacher who keeps class interesting with making us perform plays and such. The workload is very manageable and I never found myself complaining about her class.

Such a good English class. Was my first class English class of college and was a breeze. 4 essays over the semester along with a well defined Syllabus. She explains things clearly and her tests are easy as long as you pay attention. Make sure to read for the book for reading quizzes. Loved her and would take again.

I am taking her this semester and by far she has taught me the most in any English course I've ever taken. The people who are commenting harsh things are kids who 1) never show up 2) expect a grade to be handed to them 3) didn't try to understand the material. She's very understanding and will help you if you help yourself.

She is not very good when it comes to teaching and also tends to get off topic a lot which takes away from what we are learning. Does not give very good explanations for assignments that are given to us either.

loved her, You either get her or you don't at first, but trust me she makes life easy and she is by far the best English teacher I've had. She is NOT harsh and relates/understands her students. TAKE HER CLASS (English 111)

one of the nicest, best professors at sbcc in the english department! took her for eng 110 and eng 111 and got an A in both. most of these comments are clearly from students that didnt do any work and failed. from a student that actually tried, she is great and cares about all her students!

she is literally the worst. rude to students, not understanding of why some stuff cannot be turned in on time, homework and what is due is usually very unclear, classes are boring and she reteaches things we learned in high school- i hated showing up and it ruined my entire day. save yourself

she's a lunatic, whatever you do, DONT take her class. She's more uptight than a high school teacher. Counts 2 tardies as an absence and 2 absences and she will drop you from the course. Also, she is the LEAST understanding professor I have ever met, she went over on class time every class and made multiple students late to their next class

Participation definitely matters in this class but if you simply talk once or twice, she'll give you full credit. Peters is a tough grader but is very reasonable at the same time. She follows the syllabus very closely.

Was not considerate when i was out sick Even though it says redos on papers gets one week to complete she told me that I needed to have the final draft due that night as I needed to redo it. It says one week on syllabus and she said that's only after i grade it. she was making me redo it instantly and didn't follow syllabus she made so i dropped it

There seems to be a lot of negative thoughts on Meryl and her class, but those of you considering taking her class SHOULD. I signed up for her class last minute b/c she was the only one with room left and it was the best thing I ever did. If you do the work and show up, you'll get an A. Shes understanding, just communicate with her. Shes the best.

Really awful. Extremely rigid on deadlines, I had a family emergency and she called me a liar and I had to miss a big assignment instead of it being a little bit late. She also tried to drop me after I missed two classes, it's school policy but she's the only teacher who follows it. Don't take her class. Terrible teacher. Waste of time.
Super easy, like really easy. Final/midterm is collaborative if you do it in person. Nice person, you just have to talk to her.

Prof. Petrosian is awesome. The class itself was really easy but definitely try not to miss class a lot because the lectures are packed with lots of info. The only downside about this class was the final because it was way too challenging compared to the work we did during the semester. She gives semi good feedback and is an easy grader.

Disappointing class. The instructor gives unclear and unfair feedback, taking off points for answers she personally disagrees with, even when they're correct. She's unfriendly, unaccommodating, and not engaging to students. I recommend choosing a different instructor.

Disappointing class. The instructor gives unclear and unfair feedback, taking off points for answers she personally disagrees with, even when they're correct. She's unfriendly, unaccommodating, and not engaging. I recommend choosing a different instructor and asking around before enrolling in this class.
the class is easy but painfully boring. i'd rather have harder coursework with a teacher who makes it engaging. she goes on tangents, talks in circles, and isn't very friendly.

Great Professor took her with my sister and friends last spring and had a blast! Very kind and helpful! It was sad to finish the class, definitely recommend taking it with friends. Take notes during class and you will do great!

I honestly wish I could take take a course with this professor again. Kana is one of the most caring professors on SBCC's campus and genuinely wants to see you succeed in the classroom and in the world. Participation matters, you do need to put in effort but it will be greatly appreciated and reflected in your grade. This professor is amazing!

Kana is the easiest professor at SBCC. Attendance isn't mandatory and I lowkey never showed up but ended with an A. Like 2 assignments were assigned the whole year but the final was a lot of work. Super chill I even got to turn in my final days late without any points lost.

Amazing, easy class. Lectures are long but when I took her class she only gave one assignment a month. Super easy to pass unless you actually avoid doing absolutely anything. Discussion posts and the midterm were easy enough though the final project was a bit heavy to do. She's a very understanding teacher.

Took 2 (101 and 141) of her classes and she is truly one of the best professors I've ever had. If you hate lectures/notes probably not the best option for you, but seriously I learned so much in her class. Relatively easy grader and is super flexible!

Be sure to get definitions in her words; they're sometimes a little ambiguous or dissonant. Lectures were unengaging, exams were like 80% of our grade but a pretty generous grader. Very sweet woman, definitely has more sympathy if she can see you care about the class. Definitely read the textbook and clarify everything you can!!!

Loved this class and Dr. Kay. She can be hard to read but is actually very sweet and funny. Her lectures are a little long and monotone but attending and participating really helped with the midterm (gives extra credit for participation). Very few assignments with plenty of time to complete them and easy grading. She wants everyone to succeed.

K. Petrosian is an overall good professor, if you participate and show up to class you will be looked upon more favorably. She offers lots of opportunities for you to do your work after the due date, but don't wait till later. Overall good class, but definitely study for the midterm and final!

Dr. Kay gives clear instructions on assignments and homework. If you participate and complete the work, you will get the grade you want. Very easy to talk to if you ever need any assistance. She cares about your success.

Kana Petrosian is a very nice person and cares about her students success and well-being. Her lectures can feel long and repetitive sometimes. This class is super easy if you do the work and put in the effort.

Horrible service for her students. Its a easy class to pass without even showing up much. But other than that she's has a monotone voice which is hard to keep up with and describes course concepts in the most confusing way possible. I hated this class if you have to take comm take it with a professor who cares for the success of their students.

This class is definitely boring but a super easy A. As long as you go to class and write whatever she has on the board it'll be easy to memorize the terms. Textbook is used maybe a couple of times but it's free online. She also accepts late work and is a very easy grader.

Super sweet woman, not the most engaging, lectures can get a little dull, but the class is super easy. You don't have to buy the textbook. She does tell some funny stories, and you will get the best out of class when you participate and answer her questions. Really chill, good, and basic intro class. Final is just writing a long paper.

Professor K's lectures are sometimes long and repetitive, but her class is easy as long as you pay attention, take the occasional notes, and keep up with the online class discussions. Sometimes she is a little unclear about her grading criteria on specific assignments, but if you ask her clarifying questions she is very helpful.

I will forever be grateful for Dr. Kay. She is always willing to help, cares deeply for her students, & made personal relationships w/ each student. I screwed up a few times & owned up to it & she gave me a second chance. I have never met a wiser or more incredible professor in my life. LOVE YOU KANA!!!!
She offers plenty of extra credit for participating in class, so make sure to attend lectures!

A teacher who genuinely cares about her students. Little things such as bringing the class candy for Halloween and donuts for finals day really showed her compassion. Very understanding about missing class/assignments during the first semester back in person from COVID. Just a very friendly person overall.

Professor Petrosian is a very easy going teacher. She is very understanding and accommodating to college students. I highly recommend taking this class. The lectures often drag on but other than that I don't have anything negative to say! Take the class! I enjoyed this class because it was a really nice introduction to Communication.

Professor Petrosian is a super easy-going and laxed teacher. As long as you show up to class and put in some participation, you'll pass this class no doubt. She is easy on grading and funny sometimes! She can drag out her lectures, so I did find my mind wondering off sometimes.

This is a great class to take if you are shy. You will improve your public speaking skills and confidence. Kana is a very kind teacher and doesn't get annoyed with students. She is phenomenal at her job.
She is an awesome teacher, she has good feedback and cares about her students. The class is helpful for your GPA, while still being informative, and enjoyable.

Ms. Petrosian is very informed about what she's teaching. Her grading criteria are very clear and it is an easy A if you do what she expects. It is a pity that she's also quite boring and her lecture and slides too. I was honestly pretty happy to go online because of COVID.

Professor Petrosian is a caring and kind professor. She is very straight-forward, thus for a class such as Public Speaking - it was beneficial. Her syllabus is crystal clear, so as long as you complete what is asked of you (a few speeches, some online assignments) then there is no reason you should not receive an A. Interesting, would recommend!

Professor Phillips is a great French professor but you have to show up to her Zoom sessions to really feel prepared for the Midterm and Exam. There is definitely a lot of homework. Midterm was hard and the final requires a lot more memorization and help via Zoom since its worth 20% of your grade. If it's an option, take her class in person!!
Marion is such a pleasure to have. She is very animated and enthusiastic about teaching. She cares about all of her students, and makes the classroom a safe place for everyone.

If you want a solid base of French grammar and make it a lifetime, Prof. Phillips is such a solid choice. I took her class during 6 weeks of Summer II and I learnt a lot. You're not required to attend any of her meeting but it is always helpful to see her if you have any questions about French grammar. She will explain it a crystal clear way.
By far one of the best professors I have had. Makes learning French fun.

One of my fav professors so far! He's an easy grader who gives amazing feedback on all submissions. He also has a ton of extra credit opportunities so in my opinion it's impossible to fail his class. However if you do take his class it's important to understand and respect deaf culture especially if you're taking the hybrid class.

ignacio is by far the best professor i've had in community college. interactive, caring, funny. i was in the same class at the 1 rating person below and they were incredibly disrespectful. part of a DEAF classroom is no voices bc it can be taking advantage of deaf prof. if you respect deaf culture there shouldn't be a problem in his class. love him

He has been a great prof. I just wanna say that the one bad review of him has lies within it. In my class many students were disrespectful and talked in class. Those ppl didn't stop no matter how many convos he had with the class about how disrespectful it is. As long as you don't talk and show up, you will have a great time connecting with ppl.

You have to understand the rules of each class, just like any other ones, and the deaf culture. Participation matters, and since asl101 he wants us to get in the habit of signing, because its a sign lang class, and not to speak because their form of communication is sign lang Great class, effort and showing up matters.

Dont know why he has been getting bad reviews. Show up to class, you'll learn,the environment is interactive. This is the second class of his I have taken.If you are lazy and skip, you will not do good, like any other class. He cares, hes a good teacher, and hes funny. You have to put in effort its a language, but overall always loved his classes.

OMG! Mr Ponce is an amazing teacher. His signing videos are all very clear and doesn't give a lot of work. He gives so much extra credit in this class which was amazing. He gave great feedback on videos and graded assignments right away. Sadly I couldn't take ASL 102 this semester but if I could I would have!

I took Ponce for 102 and 103. I definitely like his method for breaking up weeks and assignments so you don't get overwhelmed. If you read and follow up on announcements that he sends out weekly. Def recommend either chatting with other students or using the on campus tutor, immensely helpful, and you get EC for seeing the tutor as well.

took this class for gen ed but it was very fun to learn asl, this professor makes the class easy to pass. in person lectures were very fun and very engaging. 10/10 would recommend if you were taking this class to do it in person.

He was the best professor Ive ever taken! Lecture slides are easy to follow through & he gives fun hw assignments like kahoot games. He also offers an immense amount of extra credit & has grace periods for assignments. You have to do online biweekly study groups with two other classmates but it was a good way to socialize with other classmates!

I can not stress this enough...TAKE THIS CLASS! he is the best professor i have ever come across. he is so hilarious and keeps you engaged at all times. he makes it so easy to learn a new language and has the best lectures. his class was my favorite. He made me want to learn more. He is very humble and helps you with whatever you need. TAKE IT!
Amazing professor

If you need this class for your language requirement or just credit in general TAKE THIS CLASS. His class is literally so easy and he offers TONS OF EXTRA CREDIT. I could not pass Spanish for the life of me but got an A in his class. This class is such an easy A, all you need to do is turn in your work. He is a very lenient grader. Great professor!

Such an amazing teacher!! Ignacio makes the content very interesting and structures the class very well. Do not be daunted by it being taught all in ASL, you'd be amazed how well it works. He was very friendly, kind, easy to reach, and offered tons of extra credit opportunities. Class was always fun. Taking 102 next semester and I'm stoked.

Best teacher I've ever had. Very organized and has a clear structure to his class. Lectures are very helpful and he gives lots of practice. He grades easily but gives good feedback. Super friendly. Best part is he lets you retake most quizzes so you can get more practice and not stress so much about the grade. Had him for 3 classes and loved it!

He's a good professor who had a lot of variety in terms of learning methods. I took beginning ASL over the summer, and there were lecture/quizzes, Kahoot games, normal quizzes, and helpful learning videos for everything. Many of the assignments had retakes and the lectures would provide the correct answer if you didn't understand the first time.

Best ASL class I have ever taken! He makes the material so easy to learn and wants you to succeed. He responds quickly to emails and his class is very organized. He also has a great sense of humor. I definitely would recommend him for anyone who is interested in taking ASL.

Ignacio really cares about his students and wants them to succeed. I took ASL 101 in fall 2020. Everything was online and he gave plenty of extra credit chances. He gives meaningful and helpful feedback on videos. I always look forward to his lecture presentations bc he makes them funny and exciting. I definitely recommend Ignacio to learn ASL!!
Genuinely an amazing professor. He made pandemic-style learning actually worthwhile and effective. The introduction into deaf empowerment and deaf culture was super interesting!

He gives a short break in the middle of class. His homework can be done in 1-1:30 hours, one is usually a quiz and the other will be a video of you signing. He offers 5 points extra credit every time you go to a tutor. If you miss the deadline for anything you're out of luck. Gives good feedback. Really nice guy, fun to sign with outside of class.

Ig is a really cool guy and funny... but try to not be inconsiderate. This class requires you to be involved in the deaf community. You get points for going to deaf events and club meetings so if you cant do that this isnt the class for you. There is about 140 points of extra credit.. you have to try to fail
AMAzingggg teacher!! so Funny! He grades really easily and gives candy, lots of extra credit opp, but has favorites. He also is very good teacher, and you get to understand and learn a lot of ASL.
Ig is amazing! As long as you do the assignments on time and put in some effort it's an easy class to pass.
Ig is the best professor you will ever have. At first he may seem a bit harsh but that is just his kind of humor. He requires attendance so you need to show up and be present.
Awesome ASL teacher! He is caring and wants all of his students to succeed! Highly recommend!!
Best teacher I've had! Show up to class, practice, and participate!

A real tough teacher if youre not on his good side but also easily buttered up, though neither will effect your grade. A real funny guy. but doenst take any B.S. so dont even try. Makes you hate him a few times throughout the semester but its most likely your own fault, because theres a lot of work. But overall, hes awesome and Im gonna miss him :(

Super cool teacher to be learning asl from because he is actually a part of the deaf community. Really funny and nice guy, the class is a lot of getting up and walking around and signing with each other. He will get mad if you talk during class so try not to and stay on his good side.
Amazing teacher! I've been at SBCC for 4 years and he is the best teacher I've had! Easy if you show up to class and PRACTICE!!
This was one of the greatest classes I have ever taken. If you want to learn sign, this is the guy.

Ig is such an awesome teacher! Really kind and understanding, as long as you seem like you're really there to learn (just don't speak in class--not only is it just super disrespectful, you'll definitely get on his bad side). Just do all the assignments, and you're more than likely to get an A. Probably the most fun language class at SBCC.

I took her real estate class and there were a few assignments and reading due every week. It was manageable and not too hard. Assignments were not too difficult, just read the chapters and do the work so you know what to do for the assignments. No lectures from her are given. She has you read the textbook and you learn from that.

Took Prof. Powers accelerated RE 102. The class was very informative was done online so it worked with my schedule. 10/10 would take classes with her again and I am planning to do so. I loved this class and it helped me understand what to expect pursuing a career in real estate. Thank you prof. Powers! See you in march :)

Carina is an awesome instructor! I was worried at first when I took ENT247 ; not being a digital native, but, she made it palatable. Doing the material in small chunks in combination with her patience and expertise made it doable. I learned so much and understand more about the IT needs of a business; it's very practical information.

Professor Powers is very knowledgeable, Patient, and cares about her students. She responds quickly when you have a question and encourages her students to learn. I have taken two classes with her and have learned so much. I highly recommend her!

I took her last semester, she is so sweet and understanding, she is one of the few professors that gave me extra time when I had a family emergency, take her you wouldn't regret it, easy class and you learn a lot.

Carina is such a role model for students being a young teacher. The reason I decided to take more action about Carina is because she is such a great teacher, in fact my most favorite teacher, and I would say my second best teacher. She gives motivation and pushes students to full potential. She has helped me in and out of the class which most don't

FIN 100 was easily the most real-world applicable class I have taken at the CC. In addition to learning a variety of financial material that is necessary knowledge in society today, you will practice applying this knowledge to your own life doing fun analysis projects related to your current and future! A very clear route to success for anyone!

Took this as a late add, so I don't think she read through my work in full detail, because I got 100% on everything. By the textbook online, it's easier for the "assignments". The Final was a 4 part project so make sure you spend more time on it than the day it's due (trust me, I waited and then asked my mom to help). Do the work and you'll be fine

The first thing that struck me about Professor Powers was her terrific sense of humor. That gave me an idea of what I was in for with her class. So glad I took Mktg 205! Prof Powers epitomizes "cool" and for me, the class was SO worth my time! Enjoyed the assignments, which were a lot of fun. (Yes, fun!)

This class have been my first online class ever, but the experience has been very good. Great book and the topics for this class has been very interesting and I have learned a lot about the subject this past 4 months. I can highly recommend taking this class with professor Powers!
Great professor

I have really enjoyed being a student in Professor Powers class and have learned so much! The book is wonderful, the material is interesting and most of all practical! I haven't learned so much in a class for a long time and unlike other classes, I think I will be able to retain this information. I highly recommend Professor Powers as an instructor
Carina is very enthusiastic and dedicated to teaching the material.
Excellent Teacher.
She is the best!

Professor Carina Powers is an excellent teacher, and her marketing class is very informative and enjoyable. She's one of the most helpful teachers I've ever had, and is very responsive to any questions. Her curriculum is very engaging and helps you understand marketing skills that you can really apply. I definitely recommend taking her classes.

Carina is one of the best teachers I have had. Her marketing classes are the ones I have learned the most from. The projects we did gave me real life experience which is of great value. Carina Powers classes are well organized, she is very helpful and she had interesting lectures and guest speakers.

Carina was very helpful and would be willing to set aside time after class or during her office hours to assist you if there was something you didn't understand. For the most part, just attend class and use the textbook and turn in assignments. I recommend taking this class.

Ms. Powers would always be there to assist you after class if you had questions about an assignment or just needed to talk to her. The material was not hard to understand. she stuck to the syllabus and I would recommend this class.
Ms. Powers was very accessible. She responds to e-mails within seconds. Her class was very inspiring and the project gave real world experience that has helped me with my own business.
Quick & clear. Final projects were helpful & gave practical experience in the field.

Carina is very well-prepared and organized as a teacher. Carina has had the most valuable and interesting classes at SBCC. She is very helpful and she is the best teacher in the marketing department at SBCC. Her experience is very valuable and you will learn a lot by taking this class!
Carina was very thorough and helpful. Her information and experience is very valuable. I would take another class from her. She treated every student as worthy of her attention.

Carina is one of the best teachers in the marketing department at SBCC. She is very organized, always prepared, good at explaining and have fun and challenging classes and exercises. In this class you will learn a lot!
She teachers very well. Great guest speakers, at least 5 thought the semester and all that are very successful. Projects where great. Books is hard but she does a good job of explaining better do us.
Amazing teacher. Cares so much about her students. You will not regret taking anything she teaches.
Great teacher. I learned a lot, she is very nice and helpful and shows interest.
BEST CLASS at SBCC!!! The projects were so awesome! It gave me real life experience that made me change my major!Take this class you wont regret it
Miss powers, was by far the best teacher in the Marketing department. She is engaging and exciting to listen to!Class is easy and I learned alot! I would definately recommend this class to anyone.

Carina Powers is one of the most prepared and organized teachers from the marketing department of SBCC. She will turn every boring topic into something better and always has fun stories to tell. Besides from that, she is extremely helpful outside of class time and takes a personal interest in every student.
The best math teacher in Sbcc

Fine as a person. Doesn't use Canvas, which I think really affects his grading. Assigns homework and quizzes that aren't ever graded. Received exam 1 score two days before exam 2. And received exam 2 scores the day before our final. Claims that he would send our personal grade spreadsheet when there was something to put in the grade book.never did.

Yes, you won't know your grade until the end of the semester. Annoying, but a small price to pay to have such a great teacher. I just transferred and my Math foundations are so solid, thanks to him. His lectures are fantastics, he prepares you well for his exams and for your future math classes. Best Math teacher at sbcc, by far.

Had him for 200, 210, and 220. Incredible Professor. One of the few who makes Santa Barbara City College special. His courses prepared me well beyond in my educational career. If you can, take him. Study well and study often. Do homework well ahead of time. If you do this, you will be more prepared in math than many of your peers.

One of the best lecturers I've ever had. Gabe is a character unlike any I've had the lecture of studying under. His lectures are long and arduous, but mastering his unique curriculum is it's own reward. I've never seen a curriculum that was so fundamentally shaped by it's creator. Extremely challenging, get ready to work. Good luck.

This class was not necessarily hard, it was just a bunch of work. If you pay attention on the note packets and the stuff that is discussed in class and then spend about 1 hour everyday outside of class on homework, you can easily pass with a good grade. If you can't make it to class, don't worry reading his note packet is not hard either.

Gabe is a great professor and explains things well, for this reason he's probably the best math professor at SBCC. But he skips topics that are lowkey important or at least part of the course description (row reduction in 210, Cauchy Euler equations in 220). And you will literally never know your grade in the class unless you ask him.

Best math professor I've had at SBCC and I would take every math class from him if possible, his only downside is you don't know your grade till the end of the class unless you reach out to ask. 10/10 would recommend.
it was hard
Genuinely one of the best professors I have ever had. He teaches you in a way that starts from the ground up and builds on that knowledge so that you have a complete understanding of the material.

Had him for 150 and knew I had to take him again for 160. He makes it seem like a hard class and explains everything extremely well. Go to class, do the homework and you get a B if you don't study and an A if you do. You won't regret taking him
good vibes only! very fun class

Another banger of a semester by Gabe. He changed up the class structure, so instead of 4 exams there were only 3, along with a short quiz (1-2 questions) almost every week. His lectures are engaging and his patience is limitless. Once again, my only qualm is his grading habits. Still would highly recommend, though.

Genuinely the best math professor there is. Just go to lectures and do your homework, and you'll breeze through this class. Not in a 'easy A' way, but Gabe is so good at teaching the material that calculus becomes easy to learn. There's a reason why every single person that's ever taken math at SBCC will tell you to take his class.

Gabe is a great guy but not a good professor. I started 220 in late January and I only know what I got on ONE of the exams I took in February. Mind you we had 3 exams, 9 quizzes, and 9 hw assignments. If you take Gabe know that your final grade will be a total surprise and you will have zero feedback. very frustrating being one of his students tbh

Took it last semester. He teaches in a very intuitive manner, often explaining things with geometry and more physical explanations rather than raw computation. Goes through lectures in a very exploratory, step-by-step manner which I thought helped with understanding. Very cool person during and after class as well.

I genuinely enjoyed this class and Gabe's enthusiasm for the subject is contagious. He does assign a bit of homework, but it's worth doing as all of it directly applies to the tests and quizzes. If you want to learn the subject well or are a math major, this is the class for you.

Gabe is wonderful. His enthusiasm for the subject is contagious. His lectures are engaging and insightful, but they are also crucial for passing the class. If you miss the lectures you will fall behind fast. He is also a lenient test grader. Understand how to do the calc and basic algebra you will get good grades. He is VERY SLOW to return tests.

Gabe is an articulate lecturer and a cool person. However, he assigns excessive homework that he never grades. He provides little to no grade updates and doesn't uphold his syllabus. Gabe is taking on too much math department work to the students' detriment. I appreciated his enthusiasm for math, but he expects everyone to be a math prodigy.

Gabe is great. His teaching style makes his lectures really easy to understand and he makes sure to clear any confusion students might have. He holds a lot of office hours. The only negative about him is that he doesn't use canvas and takes forever to grade. He gives us plenty of time to turn in homework and they reflect what will be on the test.

Gabe did a really good job at explaining calc to me, first time taking calc. He has good lecture notes, well thoughtout examples, homework was spread out enough so that you had enough time to absorb and practice. HE IS NOT A QUICK GRADER. Doesnt use canvas, didnt know my grade or if ill pass until after the final grades were posted. he cool tho.

Overall a really great math teacher. Crafts his lecture notes and lectures, and truly enjoys teaching his topics. Perfect amount of homework to allow you to be successful on the tests, and not too much to bog you down. Tests are difficult but fair. The only sucky part, you will not know your grade until the end. Don't know why...

Pretel has clearly put a lot of work into creating his system of notes, lectures, and homework, and it shows. The class is structured in a way that makes it easy to go back and review if you fall behind or to study for exams. Clear understanding of the material as well as how to teach it. Great at answering questions, can't recommend enough.

Gabe is the best. I took 200,210 and 220 with him and I'm very glad that I did. His courses are structured in a way that just makes sense. He's able to distill the essence of tough material and make it easy. He's very accessible outside of class. You don't really get many grades back. I never had a problem with it, but some people don't like that.

Gabe is an amazing lecturer! Don't settle for the other math teachers, Gabe is the man. Although exams may be tough he gives lots of credit for attempting problems. If you complete and turn in all homework on time you will probably pass. Although you wont really know your grade till the end (doesn't use canvas), Gabe's classes are worth it!
Overall great professor but takes a long time to grade. Hw is based on completion and posts answers online. Test somewhat difficult because you can't use notes.

Gabe is amazing. He puts a serious amount of time and effort into his lecture materials and it shows. He and his material are very thorough and are designed to help you succeed and really learn the material. You wont breeze through his classes and get an A without working for it, but if you put in the work you will get an A no problem.

the WORST professor ive ever seen at CC, even my whole life. DOESNT HELP YOU PASS AT ALL!! no hw solutions, no exam practice, no study guides, no canvas page, no resources, and no formula sheets. and when he uploads something its at 10pm the night before exams. hw and tests are unnecessarily hard and go beyond course material. DO NOT TAKE GABE!!

Best math professor I have ever had! I took him for math 200, 210, and 220. Lectures are very thorough, great prep for test, which are no walk in the park but he prepares you very well if you do the HW. I would 100% recommend Gabe if you want to really understand math. He never gave back a graded exam which did add extra stress to a hard subject...

I took 2 of Gabe's classes online, he had required lecture notes, problems sets, quizzes, and exams. He is an amazing instructor in terms of how he teaches the material, extremely thorough and his website has plenty of information needed to pass his class. Gabe was not quick to grade exams and I didn't know my grade until after I took the final.

She is an incredibly kind, supportive, and inspiring professor who truly cares about her students. Her encouragement and thoughtful feedback made a huge difference in my confidence and writing. She creates a welcoming, uplifting classroom environment and is easily one of the best professors I've had.
I love her

Grades everything at the end of the semester and often assigns unclear assignments. Lectures and course material cover very interesting topics and if doing the work good grade. I would, however, not recommend her class, because she doesn't give feedback on your work, which makes it is hard to know what to improve and how you are doing in class.
Just dont say things that you can't promise yourself..
she's great period.
This teacher is super nice but not the most helpful. She is always in a good mood but isn't completely clear about her assignments and was a push-over about them. other than that she was okay.

i took the hybrid class with her so i attended once a week then had an online class via Moodle. she is the best teacher i had at C.C. yet. you would have to really try to fail this class because the work isnt like work since it is so fun and interesting!
She's the best teacher. She is helpful, devoted and clearly knows how to teach. She helped me develop my english skills to a new level. Definitely recommended.

She is extremely helpful, I took an online ENG 70 class with her, it was the first time, that it was offered online. She helped us deal with all the system errors and everything. I definitely recommend her!
Shes a Great teacher, I highly recommend taking her class. She is always positive and helpful, she makes class as fun as possible. She just loves teaching!

I wasn't happy going into this class, although Margaret changed my attitude. She is extremly nice and outgoing with a smile on her face every day. She has a great way of making your writing better and is very helpful. If you have to take this class I would recommend her.

Margret prothero is amazing! She's extremely out-going, friendly and is always lending a helpful hand out. She always has a smile on her face and always is positive. Her class is fun and she teaches helpful study habits! Take her class!
GREAT!!! teacher. if you r looking forward to take english 70 and don't know which teacher to take take this class. wonderful teacher. u will learn lot. fun class 2.
The best english teacher i have ever had. She keaps her classes interesting and gets everyone involved with activities. I learned a lot and really improved I definetely recomend her class.

She has an interesting class, mundane at times, you can miss one or two classes and she doesnt care, wouldn't make it a point to just miss class. Email her and ask which books you will need prior to class (some marked mandatory are not needed) fun teacher and final presentations were great. Ask around though, she might not be for you (wasnt for me)
MY FAVORITE ESL TEACHER! She is very funny, class is super-easy and there is no way you can fail. Ok, maybe the hot guy sitting in front of me helped the whole experience :)
This is my favorite teacher ever, very easy class because she picks such enjoyable curriculum and wonderful teacher, a lot of fun, not a lot of homework. Very fufilling! the best teacher ever!
Best English teacher!

She's a control freak, always sending threatening emails telling us to turn things in on time, which I don't get, just fail those who don't? They're adults and can handle their own grades. It's annoying for those of us who turn things in on time.She also complains we don't follow directions,yet she messed up our presentation slides, just so ironic.

took history of graphic design. easiest class i've ever taken! lectures are short and informative, very few assignments/homework, and many fun and easy class activities. didn't have to do any reading or notes and midterm/final was open book/internet

Her feedback was largely unhelpful and led to more time being spent on simple problems. Her feedback is harsh and sometimes irrelevant. My final was criticized for its file size and layer structure which are both unrelated the finished work and rubric. I wouldn't have taken the class knowing what I know now about the structure and teaching style.

I took Graphic Design History and it was a great class. Irene is very inspiring and her lectures are very interesting. It is not like a typical History course. There is a lot of fun hands on activities. If you attend class and do the work you will succeed. Do not fall behind on the reading and vocab.

Irene is the best. Her lectures are very thorough and inspiring. Her in person class was very fun and we did a lot of hands on activities. It is not an easy class but she makes it fun. If you attend class, do the work and do NOT fall behind on reading and vocabulary you will do great.
Take this class! She is an amazing professor and the class is interesting, especially for someone like me who didn't have any previous knowledge of graphic design.

HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS CLASS. The only HW we had was weekly vocab. The final is a compilation of the vocab words and one 5 min presentation with 3 other team members. She is accessible outside of class. I learned a plethora of things about graphic design in her class. SHE WANTS YOU TO SUCCEED.

Mrs. Ramirez was very caring about her students and wants her students to succeed. Her only homework is weekly vocab and one or two major assignments during the semester. she does lectures in class so be ready to take notes. she always released us from class early which was nice. HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS CLASS.
Great professor. Had her first and second semester of my first year at SBCC. Would recommend for any graphic design majors.
Irene is an amazing teacher and definitely takes the time to get know her students. Take her classes!

Chef Rapp is the meanest and most blatantly disrespectful and u aware teacher I have ever had. He insults the students and ridicules them daily for not reading his mind or over a small simple mistake. He is very punctual with time and attendance and if you miss classes he treats you worse. It is shocking how he allowed to teach in the year 2023!

If you think Chef Rapp is a bad teacher, then you clearly couldn't handle the pressure in a kitchen. Chef Rapp is very tough on you because he wants to teach you the right way of doing things. He is also tough on you because he knows the expectations of a fine dining restaurant. Don't be soft and drop his class because you can't get over your ego.

Yes, Chef Rapp can be really really difficult from the start of your semester until your last. You might not like him when you first start out, but eventually you're going to realize that he truly cares about each of his students. I almost dropped out, I'm so glad I didn't. Choosing this culinary department was one of the best decisions ever.

First day of class he really impress you with his talking, but later? I got into one of his classes and what a shame. I waste my time with him You tube have better tutorials. He's not passionate not standards to teach professionally. He compares his cooking skills with students to humiliate them. Request another instructor if you want to learn.

He is passionate, hard working and unbelievably talented Chef. Older man coming from France, his training is tough but rightfully so. Without him, I feel you will fall short in knowledge. He truly does care and if you show him you do too,he will help you in class and getting a good working job. His ways have soften since I 1st joined. Great overall

This guy is a disgrace to the Culinary Dept. here at SBCC. Way too tough of a grader. He brings in the harshness the French typically act and bring it into our culture,(i know bc i worked in france)expecting only perfect. He gave me a C grade in practicums, saying that I could have done better. I did FAR better than anyone. He favors entitled kids
Don't. Waste your money. My grandma taught me more about cooking than this lop ever could.

He is absolutely the worst teacher in the culinary department!!! Such a shame when we pay to learn. Never stop yelling and breaking the students. No mistake is allowed, as if students were employees. Good luck if you enroll in his classes. Very hard to get a good grade.
He is a great teacher, if you don't get it you in the wrong place!!!!!

Chef Rapp has a reputation for being difficult, but he is really trying to create a high standard of skill for the students. He is an excellent instructor and many students do not appreciate his incredible attention to detail, depth of knowledge, and tremendous care he puts into his work.

I agree, show up, know your stuff and don't talk back and you will be ok. I don't agree with the way he treats his students, the anger thing needs to be managed. Also, he does almost all the cooking. He should demo, then allow us to cook. He also needs to understand we are students not employees. We pay him.

Stephane is passionate about food, sustainability, waste, and set-up. Follow procedures, take notes, pay close attention (French accent can be interesting and even humorous), and show enthusiasm and it will be a great learning experience. Loafers, late-comers, and laziness is not tolerated. Show up to learn and you'll be fine.
Chef Rapp is quality...you just need to know ur sh*t and you will pass fine...show up and write things down...

He is a great teacher. Yes he does have a slight anger problem but as long as you have your**** together you'll do fine. He has always been willing to help me. He does try and break you down but it only makes you stronger in the long run. If you give him respect he will give you respect back. He is knowledgeable and has a lot of good information.

he isnt nice to his students. He tries to break you down, he tries to make you quit cooking once you step into kitchen. Make sure that you dont talk back if you want to get a good grade. I talked back giving my opinion on the awful food, but that made my grade go down. Make sure you dont get on his bad side. Be prepared, know ur recipes by heart.

says to go out of the book, then, when the product turns out poorly, says the book is crap. he will yell at you. he will tell you that he would not hire you. the funny part is... if you know him, you know this man could never work on a line. he and his classes are useless.
I liked chef rapp...he yells at some people, but was always nice to me. I think he's kinda sexy too.

Rapp is by far the worst teacher in the culinary department. I do not know why he shows up at all. Wait..yes I do! So that he can yell and belittle his students, make the most innefficient use of class time, while serving up some of the worst food I have ever tasted as our "model" of food preperation. He is a shame to the culinary arts department!

This guy loves to yell. He needs to take his aggression out in therapy instead of on his students. He is difficult to understand with his accent and does not explain himself well. He just wants you to watch him cook so you will get very little experience cooking in this class. Too bad this class is required.

This teacher is great but you can't understand a damn word with his French accent. He has toned it way down since I started two years ago. He used to rage on students but now he is in a mindset to teach.

Javier is generally a funny professor and I do enjoy the labs. Although, he doesn't explain the material in class super well so when you take the quizzes they are confusing. The other classes get a study guide but we don't. He will mark you late, is strict with how he wants hw assignments done.

Participation is absolutely required since he makes it difficult for you to make it up. You do have to stay for the full 3 hrs of lab. He makes no exceptions for any excuses & you will lose points. He is also very strict about phone use & will kick you out. Overall he is rude, unreasonable, & inconsiderate. The course material it self isn't so bad.

I regret to say that my experience with Professor Javier in Earth 102 was extremely disappointing. Javier seemed unapproachable and dismissive when students asked questions or sought clarification. His responses were often curt and unhelpful, leaving many of us feeling frustrated and unsupported. The assignments and exams were poorly structured.

The quizzes cover some material that isnt covered in the lectures or supplementals and are pretty difficult. The homework is not necessarily difficult but hes very specific and picky about the way he wants it done. If you make one single particular mistake youll get points off. All way too much work and frustration for a one credit class.
I did not enjoy this class too much
awful class waste of time. should've dropped in the beginning. bad teacher

This is the worst teacher of my life, the emails I've sent for extra clarification are responded with rudeness and telling me to look at the instructions when I ask very specific questions or one word replies. She is unprofessional and unqualified, honestly I could teach the course better than her. If you think about having this teacher think again
Definitely a waste of time! She just throws so much unnecessary busy work at you!! The final is a huge drag as well. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!
Carmen Riveros class is fun. Average pace, and a good start at any level. Accommodating to those that need help. A memorable class, and good learning atmosphere.
Easy class, 2 assignments each week + a 8 page final. If you do the assignments you will get an A . Recommended!

I took this class online and thought it would be a great class to further my education. I didn't realize that I would actually be discouraged from school by taking this class. Prof Rivero doesn't grade based off of her syllabus she grades based of of what the other students say. VERY DISCOURAGING CLASS

I don't know what some of these people are talking about, but I really learned a lot and I took this class online! This class helped me progress as a student. The one time I contacted Her, she was very nice. She is clear in describing what she wants in assignments and it's not hard to receive an A as long as you do your work.

this class was a pian. i thought it was cool at first but after a couple weeks it felt like a waste of time. Mrs. rivero is a very nice person but i was at a loss as to what was going on half the time.
this class is an easy A but she does keep track of tardies,absences and participation. If you cannot pass her class you shouldn't be in college.

Like High School all over again... she counts tardies and absences...if you dont show up a couple of times and don't particpate in class.. you will deserve a C instead of an A even if you complete all the asignments
No Comments
Great communication and really cares about her students. This class was super easy and educational! There were always 3-4 assignments per week that could be completed easily!

LOVE Sandrine. Classes were basically just motivational talks, we always got out early and I was always able to study for other classes during lectures! Sandrine really cares about her students and wants to see them succeed. Highly recommend this class if you're at all interested in kinesiology!!

Loved this professor and her class! Enticing lectures and clear class structure. Really caring and helpful if you step up and ask for the help, she has a lot of connections and knowledge. Miss it already!

Sandrine is the best teacher I had this summer. Having had the experience in two classes this summer, I can note that the other class really sucked, and this class was the one I looked forward too. Her lectures are amazing, not a "normal" lecture, and they are very interesting. She is very encouraging and will open doors to many opportunities. :)

So encouraging! Sandrine will push you to be your best in the best way! She motivates you and talks you up, and helps you work up a great sweat! I always felt amazing after her classes (if a little tired) and would recommend to anyone that wants to get motivated to start moving! MTXE!!!

Sandrine will give 100 and 10% to make sure you succeed and is a great teacher. However, her kinesiology class is more of a career search class than on body movement. Easy A just turn in your assignments and do the extra credit (I forgot a few things).
Shes great! I've taken every class she teaches, & look forward to seeing her every semester!!

I LOVE Sandrine!! She is amazing, and really cares about your success, provided you do too. Open book online quizzes, crib sheet for MTs, she sets you up for success. Turn in your assignments, you'll pass.

Sandrine is very inspiring, and she knows a lot about the subject! However, you have to earn and work for your grade in this class. She gives a lot of extra credit which really helped me, but the material can be kind of heavy. If you want to take Kinesiology in the ART aspect, take Sandrine, if you want to take it in the SCIENCE aspect, don't.

Great Teacher, Very Inspiring, she always says give 100% or leave no judgement. Always find ways to inspire her students and give it all,most students want to come back and tell their friends to take her class. If your interested in changing your body, lack confidence, want to get fit, love fitness, want to push yourself this is the class for you.

If you don't know any Spanish, then you will have a tough time. I'm a native Spanish speaker & some of it was even a bit tough for me. Be prepared for the crazy amount of homework.
