It's not hard, they're technically "quizzes" but you can do them as many times you want to get a perfect score. Pretty easy A again if you know some Spanish.
I think the class is heavy, it doesn't help that much and he doesn't give you the opportunity to get extra credits to raise your grades.

This professor and this class were very challenging, but he is fair and willing to work with you if you put in the effort. The class was weirdly structured in Canvas, but I figured it out as I continued with the class. I would recommend going to the office hours, which I didn't do but can be extremely beneficial if you are struggling with the class
He was not available for any help outside of class, and he kept changing due dates so much that it was hard to keep track of when homework was due! I would not recommend him.

I feel like I never learned much in Professor Rodriguezs online class at all. There is no in class speaking interaction over grammar. Youre just expected to memorize material out of the textbook. Such a horrible way to learn Spanish! I transferred to UCSB and took a Spanish class, but I was greatly not prepared for it due to this class. Avoid!

This teacher did not have the online format set up correctly and this made the class extremely confusing. I believe he would be a good teacher in person, but online the class setup is a disaster and finding where your assignments are is close to impossible sometimes. Also, course material from other teachers classes is randomly included.

Since Spanish is 5 units you have the usual crazy amount of work load that any Spanish professor will give. Professor Rodriguez was easy to get ahold of with any questions or concerns, and very understanding. He gives good options for extra credit and the tests are all based off of the homework.

Professor Rodriguez doesn't teach lectures and only assigns homework which you are magically expected to know how to do. There is no textbook. The exams are pretty difficult. Even though I ended up with an A-, definitely make sure that you did all the assignments because I accidentally skipped a few which brought my grade down.

The online format sucks. Not learning anything and constantly confused. If you want to learn spanish take it in person. Every assignment is a quiz but those are easy and can retake as much as you want.

Took Spanish 102 & 104 online with Professor Rodriguez and it was great. The textbook he uses for both classes (written by him) is the same, which came to be money-saving. If you have questions or problems, whenever I emailed him, he responded super fast too. I recommend taking him because you also know what to expect every week with his due dates.

I took his online class. There was a lot of work however you were able to take the quizzes as many times as you'd like to achieve an A. There was 4 midterms/test and a final which was super easy. the midterms or tests consisted of filming a video and explaining things you learned in English and a little bit in Spanish. It was not difficult.

Here's the deal... I loved Prof. Rodriguez but HATED the online format that he uses. It doesn't teach you spanish at all and has so many issues. Rodriguez cares so much and is so helpful, but that doesnt make up for the awful program. WIth that being said, I got a very easy A+, but it will take some time. If you wanna learn spanish, take in-person

I took Span 102 online thinking it would be an Easy A since I am pretty fluent in Spanish. However, he dosen't really know how to operate an online class, every week there would be another problem with the assignments. He would also be late giving the assignments each week, only giving you four days to complete 12 assignments. Very easy grader
Required textbook access code and has alot of work
Teacher is amazing. Class is super easy. If you're looking for an easy grade in a language take this class. Just make sure you keep up with all of the work that's due weekly.

He's very good at responding to emails - but I was constantly having to remind him to open assignments because his canvas got messed up at the beginning of the semester. Other than that it's definitely a passable class because you can redo all the homeworks to get the right answers but definitely a lot of homework.

Took online during summer. I did not learn anything from this class. It is assumed that students already know some Spanish, the book has very little English explanations. If you know little Spanish it will be very difficult unless you study all day, everyday. Final was very hard. Only take if needed for credits or you already Spanish.

I took this class online. Boy was it tough! I learned a lot though. The teacher is incredibly helpful. If you spend a few hours a day interacting with the material and make a study notebook and take advantage of his tutoring hours you'll do fine! Just take the class seriously and don't procrastinate and there's no reason to not get an A

I took his class online its set up so if you spend 2 hrs/wk copy/pasting you'll get a 75% plus anything you get correct on the final (which was really hard, but he gave us a practice midterm that was very close to the final, some questions were identical I ) If you do the work you will pass but maybe not learn much unless you take very good notes

I took this class in Summer Session. 6 weeks of intense work for a 5 credit class. You will spend 25 - 35 hours a week on this class. The online modules let you take them as many times as you need and give you the answers to what is wrong. No reason to not get an A. This will not teach you spanish in 6 weeks but will satisfy your UC requirements.

Rodriguez's online class was easy in the fact that homework was simple. If you can't teach yourself or take the time to, don't take this online. I really enjoyed him as a professor and he will almost immediately respond to any email which is totally awesome! Taking him again in fall for SPAN102 online :)

Wonderful Professor. I took two classes from him and would definitely take more if available. Very helpful and available to answer questions and assist when needed. Very reasonable grader. Overall outstanding teacher.

This was an online class. I so much appreciate he has an online class since I am an athlete and only this way I can take Spanish which is mandatory. He does it in a way that I have to work a lot, but it is all doable. I don't mind working, but it allows me to work at my own time and he is very clear about deadlines . Gracias

Dr. Rodriguez is the perennial altruist. He is a kind souls who legitimately cares about the success of his students and was tremendously helpful throughout the semester. There is no reason not to do well.
Online classes are the best.

He answered e-mail cordially and also quickly. The material is tough although there are an army of online and on-campus tutors available and we regularly got e-mail about online tutoring hours. It isn't always fun, although no excuse not to do well.

BEST PROFESSOR EVER! If you have the opportunity, take one of his courses. He is not only super col and easy going, but he is an excellent teacher. He gives great feedback and suggest really helpful resources if you need additional help with homework! Awesome seasoned professor!

Prof Rodriguez constructed a regimen of exercises and tests that were cumulative and easy to follow. Through diligently completing all assignments, I learned a great deal. There are many little quizzes and other exercises you will have to complete, but you can do it! It was a wonderful class!

The professor follows the syllabus well and offers online screen-to-screen office hours every week. The book work is more difficult that the special exercises in a workbook online,but hard work and using a spanish/english dictionary while reading the book, along with taking notes helps so much. The class isn't very difficult if you study enough.
Great class and very interesting textbook. I was surprised by how much I could learn in an online course. Professor was very understanding.

So laid back very understanding if assignments need more time. 2 zoom speech's the rest are videos n discussion questions you can't fail unless you don't do ur speech's super easy n awesome professor.
She was so nice. I did it for summer and she gave such good feedback defiently take her class.
Nothing bad to say, easy class and really kind teacher!!
Very kind and approachable professor. I had her for a couple COMM courses and I'd take her again if I needed to.

She was a very understanding professor. I had situations that made it hard for me to complete certain assignments, and she was always understanding and fair. The coursework was very simple, and didn't grade to critically on certain weird niche details that other professors do all the time. Keep up with the coursework and her classes are a breeze.
SUPER SWEET PROFESSOR!!! She was very understanding, helpful, and just overall kind. I wish I could take all of my coms courses with her! MUST TAKE WITH HER!!!

She's awesome! She really makes public speaking as easy as it can be and is super understanding about everything! It seems impossible to get a bad grade as long as you try. I had some family stuff over spring break and forgot to turn something in, and she let me turn it in for full credit still. Super sweet and cares about her students!

Love her so much, she is a gem. All of the assignments are very clearly written out and her grading is also very clear. She is so sweet and truly cares about her students! I was super anxious about public speaking and she was so understanding of that and I learned so much from this class.

Professor Rodriguez was really great for this class! She's very organized online, and will give helpful constructive feedback on your speech drafts ahead of time. If you do all the assignments and make use of the feedback on drafts, there's basically no way to get a poor grade. Just show up and do the work

I love Rebekah. I've taken 3 of her classes and have not been disappointed. I was scared to public speak but she made it 10x easier. She understands that life exists outside the classroom and she has a very clear grading criteria which makes assignments super easy. HIGHLY RECOMMEND any class that she offers :)
Highly recommend Rebekah for any comm class! Very informational, very kind, very easy homework and speeches. The only possible con about her is that she only teaches online classes. 10/10
I highly recommend this class. Rebekah was very directive, kind and understood the problems of public speaking. Her grading was very clear and made the class easy to understand and learn from. 10/10

I LOVE REBEKAH. I took her comm141 class and was very directive and understanding. Would highly recommend. I am currently taking her comm131 class and it seems very simple and easy. My friends say the same things about her.

Amazing professor. I am glad she's for the core curriculum of communication. I took the summer course and it is manageable. Very clear grading just make sure it's on time and you get full credit which make for an easy good grade. Speeches were fun and she let us be as creative we could get within the guidelines of the instructions. Love her!
An amazing prof and co

Professor Rodriquez is so awesome and chill. I took her group communications class, and while I did have some difficulties working with the other members in my group, she helped resolve every issue in a timely manner. She genuinely wants to see you succeed and you can easily get an A if you put in effort.

Ms. Rodriguez was a great teacher. Pretty easy to get a good grade all you need to do is actually listen to her feedback. I totally forgot about a speech I had to do and she let me take it another time. Great personality I definitely recommend her.

Professor Rodriguez was such a great professor! I loved her course, and found her feedback on my speeches to be kind and encouraging. This type of course can be nerve racking, however Professor Rodriguez gives all her students the space to feel comfortable. As long as you meet the grading criteria, and turn in your work on time you will do well!

Professor Rodriguez was truly a great teacher! I was nervous about public speaking but she was very open about her own fears in regards to public speaking and made sure to have very clear rubrics for assignments. I can truly say I gained knowledge and learnt important life skills from this class that I will carry with me through my career.

l absolutely loved this class with Prof. Rodriguez!! I didn't know what to expect but it has been one of my favorites so far! The professor is knowledgeable and helpful & she is enthusiastic. She is quick in responding and it is very clear that she wants you to succeed. I recommend this class to everyone and I wish she taught more classes here!!!
Such an amazing professor. Would take her class 100x more just so she could be my professor. Shes such and amazing and caring human! Her workload is not much, just participate. I loved this class.
She is an amazing teacher and very understanding.

She is AMAZING! She is so sweet and understanding as long as you are a good student! I can't watch lectures for too long, but her lectures are super easy to understand and they are not time consuming, so WATCH THEM for better understanding! Textbook is not enforced since she explains the main stuff, but it does help, and usually it is a free pdf!
Take it! She is very nice and responds to emails quick. She is very encouraging and open minded. Quizzes are very easy and we did like 4 speeches. Assigned a textbook, but I didn't use it.

She is a GEM. This was my first communications class, and it exceeded my expectations. She is SO NICE, incredibly organized, and provided many resources as well as detailed instructions. Plus she always responds very quick which I appreciated. Do the hw, practice your 4 speeches: easy/doable A.

I was so scared of taking public speaking but I had the best experience in Rebekah's summer class. She goes out of her way to help her students and she is very understanding. You can tell she loves her job and cares about her students and that really makes a big difference!!!
I don't think she always graded fairly but she was nice. Short lectures are always nice.

I took Rebekahs class for public speaking! I was so nervous about public speaking before I took this class but by the end she made me feel so comfortable. She is a tough grader, but do the best you can and it is an easy A!
Sooooo organized! A great class, it is fast pace at times but she makes it manageable.
I took the hybrid version of this class Spring 2020 and absolutely loved it. I came into the class very nervous but this class taught me a lot of confidence. Rebekah is very caring and interesting.
Beware of her tests. They are intensely tricky and very little guidance leading up to the exam. Study guides are ambiguous and lacking- nothing you study for will be on the test.

She will email you reminders about due dates and tests and explains what is expected in regards to assignments or content. Her tests were not tricky at all, just do the reading Her Forum Questions were an easy way to earn points, some questions were way easy. Would def take again.

Teacher had weird assignment due dates, everyone's final paper had to be pushed back because she said the due date was a Tuesday but did not mention it was at 1am. Kind of hard to reach, did not offer extra credit but was lenient on late work if you're a good student. Did awful in this class just because I couldnt remember the weird due dates.

Very dry class. Lots of reading, weekly quizzes. This class has 1 project, a group project. You are grouped with people in your region, and you have to visit a hospital. If you are grouped with someone who works at a hospital, and they can arrange a visit, do it! These visits are almost impossible to arrange and it is a lot of points.

Wonderfully diverse classes. Positive dynamic. Uplifting environment. Excellent instructor. Caring, patient and kind. Reasonable expectations for each student. Adapts activities to individual capabilities. All ages succeed. Senior friendly. Disability friendly. Good to know: Even in bad weather, the water is always about 80 degrees.

Super nice and cares about you! I was an okay swimmer when I came in but then she helped me with form and such and I became and felt faster in swimming! Would take it again just to hang out with her to talk about swimming or surfing!

Take this class. It's good for destressing about school. I missed 1 quiz so I was glad I took it for credit and not a grade. Sally is a nice teacher and I learned some cool stretches for my back, which always seems to hurt.

I loved this class! I took stretch and relax from another teacher at SBCC and I didn't learn much from her, but it was relaxing. Ilike Sally b/c she explained things. I definitely was more flexible after ths class and I miss the relaxation part of it!
Good teacher, Cares about you. I wasn't a great swimmer but I feel good about my ability now. It's a good workout.
Good coach, knows swimming and fitness. Take it pass/no pass so you don't have to worry about missing too much.Bring sunscreen!
easy going, just have to show up to pass.
Excellent class but not for beginner swimmers. Good workout! Take it credit/no credit then if you miss no big deal. I took it twice and I'm trying again this year!
Awesome class very good workouts if you like swimming as a sport not a free swim around thing.
No Comments
Pros: Very informative Lots of study material Cons: Long lectures

A really fun and engaging professor. She cares very deeply about the success of her students and offers good resources for studying. This course is VERY lecture heavy, even though she records her lectures it's definitely not a good idea to skip the in person lecture since she might give out a lecture quiz which can not be made up.

As a returning student after a 20-year gap, I found this professor to be amazing! Patty is personable and teaches you to understand, not memorize. She teaches in a way that makes lecture and lab not only understandable, but fun. I appreciated the ability to take practice quizzes, the tutor's helpfulness, and the extra credit.

I have been a stuent of Patty's throughout BMS 100 and 107 and all I can say is she has solidified my inspiration to pursuing a career in healthcare. Her professionalism, guidance, and thoroughness has shaped my success in the classroom, labs, and exams. Patty is an excellent role model for students pursuing nursing or any BMS majors.

Patty is awesome! Super enthusiastic about teaching and cares about her students! Exams are pretty lengthy, but have everything to do with what was spoken about during lectures. Attending tutoring helps a ton with outcomes of exams!!

I took the 6 week summer anatomy class. Very hard and fast paced but she gave you everything you needed for the exams word for word. Studied only the study guides and got close to 100% on everything! Good amount of extra credit. Labs were hard but she had practice quizzes you could do as many times as you wanted. Patty is great!

prof. saito is awesome! class is well organized and structured, lots of work but not super difficult! she posts lectures that can replace reading the textbook, quizzes every week that are mostly memorization, and exams are long + proctored but build off of the weekly quizzes. she offers a lot of good resources and practice work if you need help!
I took this online Summer class for BMS 100. Very helpful, has tutoring sessions available. Challenging class but rewarding. Would take her class again.

Patty is an amazing teacher with a fun, bubbly personality. Her class can be tough, so make sure to study a lot, especially for the weekly lab quizzes. One of the best parts is that she offers plenty of extra credit opportunities. The only downside is that she writes notes a bit fast, but her labs are super fun! MAKE SURE YOU ATTEND CLASS EVERYDAY!

I can't say enough good things about Patty! She's amazing! It's a lot of studying outside class but if you're going into the healthcare field, it's important. She's very clear about what is going to be on the tests and gives you everything you need to pass. Make sure to do the practice tests and practice the drawings! Wish she taught BMS 108 ):
I love patty

I honestly don't understand the good reviews for this teacher....not only is she asking you to learn FAR more than other instructors in the same course, her "practice" quizzes and study guides definitely do not align with what the actual quizzes and exams cover. She also likes to send rude messages frequently! Awful teacher/person.

Patty is a straightforward professor who demands a lot of hard work, but it's worth it. The content is challenging, but using resources like Unstuck ai, along with the provided study guides and tutors, can really help you succeed in this class.

Patty is very intelligent and speaks very well about the material we cover. She definitely cares about her students and wants us to succeed. Her labs are educational and entertaining, and she wants us to learn hands-on. She is also very enthusiastic in lectures and always covers all that we need to know for exams and quizzes.

Patty is a caring professor than genuinely wants her students to do well. However, YOU have to do your part. Get ready to study many hours outside of class. If you do the test study guides, study for lab & take the practice quizzes, and do the (few) extra credit assignments you will do well in the class. If you don't, you will not do well.

Patty was a great Professor! She provided everything a student needs to pass. I 100% would recommend to a new coming student. She always knows how to keep a lecture or lab entertaining. She is the best professor I had this semester!

Patty was a great professor! I would 100% recommend her to anybody going into health sciences. She provided everything you need to pass the class. She knows how to make long lab/lecture days fun. 10/10

Patty is definitely the best professor i've ever had! She's always so bubbly and really cares abt her students. Anatomy is definitely one of the hardest classes you'll have to take, be prepared for A LOT of information. Don't get behind on studying bc exams will sneak up on you, ALWAYS take her practice tests (learn drawings), do the extra credit!

Patty is truly a prodigy in her field of teaching. She is extremely welcoming, transparent, but most importantly, she teaches very well. You can expect to work many hours outside of class studying, but homework is practically non-existent, this class rides on you taking good notes, and studying well for the weekly lab and/or lecture quiz.

She is an excellent instructor; she wants the students to succeed in BIO 100. 6/2024 Be prepared to study, complete the study guides for each chapter, and visit the tutor. I recommend this class and plan to enroll in BIO 107

Patty is a very straightforward teacher. The test content is exactly what she teaches but you have to pay attention and not miss any lectures! Just focus when you're in class and take good notes and you will be okay. Yes, you will have to spend a lot of time studying outside of class if you want to do well, but it is worth it.

The class wasn't too hard, but there was a lot of homework to stay on top of. Average week is two graded lectures plus a study guide to fill out, a lab, and a lab quiz. Patty is very nice and clearly cares about seeing her students in succeed class, as well as learn things that can be useful in everyday life.

Patty is the best professor I've ever had! She is very straight forward in her lectures and does written notes on the projector with you, which is amazing if your someone to learns by writing things down. She provides so many resources and wants you to succeed in this class. Her tutor's are amazing and so helpful!

I highly suggest that you are ready to work hard and be serious to learn about Anatomy. I was just confused on what to study. I did not get Patty's learning style.(which is okay)She is a great professor, she takes her classes very seriously. I highly suggest taking another professor if you want an easy A. I learned a lot from Patty in a short bit.

Professor Saito definitely wants students to be prepared for their medical career. I already have a B.A. but doing a pre-req. I was studying 20-40 hours a week. I know anatomy is tough, and I was already seeing the tutors and was getting all different kinds of info. The quizzes and tests were very different from what she explained.
all I can say is she is an amazing professor.

I took Patty for BMS 100, and the lectures were a lot to memorize but she takes notes for you during the lectures! She's super caring and understanding about your success in her class. Make sure to study for the lab quizzes, it's only 10 questions and she gives you a very helpful lab manual! Patty's study guide and practice exams are very helpful!

I didn't do well in this class. That being said, all of it was entirely my fault and none on Patty. She is easily the best professor I've ever had by far. When I asked questions after class she gave her full attention. Lecture and tests EXTREMELY heavy but worth it. Study for everything a week ahead. Take her over anyone else. Little extra credit.

Patty is extremely thorough. There will not be a stone, or in this case bone/muscle left unturned. Her class is a lot of work, but if you put in the time and dedication you can pass. She is not tricky which I appreciate in science courses. Her study guides are long, but if you master them you will pass her tests.

FAVORITE TEACHER/PROF EVER Patty is amazing!!! I learned so much in this course and she kept it so interesting the whole time. I feel extremely prepared going into anatomy and felt on top of the work the whole time. Take advantage of the extra credit and study for lab and chapter quizzes and you'll get an A!! :)

I loved his class! I took the class to learn how to take and edit photos better. I learned a lot from him. He is always in a good mood and you can tell that he enjoys teaching people. I highly recommend his classes.

Really good class! A lot of work, but Mr. Sanders was there to walk us through it. I learned a lot about light and am more confident in what I am going to do when I go on shoots. I wish we covered more Photoshop though.
Great class! Mr. Sanders loves photography and he made me fall in love with it too. I can't wait to take my next class with him.

He's a really nice guy and super encouraging, but he didn't grade our assignments until the last month of class. He only gave me written feedback on three assignments. I'm a visual learner, so I didn't like that 95% of the material was reading. He means well but is too lax considering we spend money on these classes.

Amazing experience in this class. He is very understanding, grades fairly and really wants his students to succeed. He deserves his 100% on this website. I took online and everything is laid out so well. He encourages students to look up things they don't know during tests. Sent in my portfolio, he sent it back with a hand-written note- so sweet.

Seantel has been one of the most inspirational teacher I've had so for. He's is absolutely fantastic and all of his assignments were great. He is very passionate about what he's teaching and even if my major isn't photography I'd definitely take this class again!

TAKE HIS CLASS EVEN IF YOU'RE NOT A PHOTOGRAPHY MAJOR. He is one of the most positive and inspirational people I've ever met, and I'm so so happy he decided to be a teacher because he motivates his student so much. His exercises and lectures make you see the world in a different way, it sounds cliché but there's no other way to describe it.
Amazing class! Really liked the assignments! Always had a good time during class.
Awesome teacher! He creates an awesome learning environment for his students and will answer any question you have in as much detail as you need. would absolutely recommend any of his classes.
He is amazing. I would take all of his classes.

It was my first photography class & after being instructed by him, I am now minoring in Photography. He truly cares about everyone & how far they come from the beginning of the class to the end. I would say that attendance is not mandatory , but if I were you I would show up, its fun. Also we almost always got our of class an hour or 30 mins early.

I really like Sean as a teacher, my only critique is that in photo 109 he says everyone's work is brilliant and in photo209 nobody's is. The photos that he always goes absolutely nuts for are the most boring, like a close up of a wrinkled sheet of paper (no joke). Stay away from complex compositions if your looking to specifically please him.
Great

If you want to learn how to light and take good pictures, take this class! The prof is cool and you do lots of shooting during class so there's lots of time to practice. I hardly read the textbook cause Mr. Sanders explained everything so well, but I bought it at the end of the semester to review what we learned. A+!!!
Very good professor and amazing class! I learned so much about my camera, that I never use my phone anymore... my photography has improved so much!

He's a good teacher but he's becoming a bit slack. The class always starts about 20 minutes after it's supposed to because he's always of doing something else. There's a lot of just sitting, waiting for him to get things going and it's frustrating since you pay a lot of money for the class. Things you learn in 5 hours could be taught in 3 hours.
Photo109 Sanders was a good teacher! I looked forward to his classes!

AWESOME Professor! I learned so much in this class. You can tell he really loves teaching and his passion rubs off on the students. I feel like my photography has improved by leaps and bounds. Highly recommended.

A raw of sunshine, that is what he is, a great photographer, inspiring, helpful, friendly. He is one of the best teachers I ever had and I have taken two classes with him - I hope they will invent more so I can have him again! Funny assignments, mind-thrilling inspiration and great In-class-assignments. I learned so much!
Great course! Fun, entertaining and you'l learn how to use your camera! Great elective class, highly recommend this professor :)

Sean really makes the class fun. He is very charming, open and positive. I wish it actually was a little harder to get a good grade, because he is an easy A, but it still is a lot of good assignments so you can be creative. And of course he is super cute. Last semester the class stayed even though we were allowed to go home. He is awesome.
I entered the class with a nonchalant attitude hoping I could just take all my pictures with my iPhone 4. By the end I loved photography enough to by a $600 Canon. Sean's an amazing teacher!

Was hesitant to take the class but am so glad I took it because of what an absolutely fantastic teacher he is. Always smiling always a positive attitude. Really grateful for the experience I had and for what I learned in that class, and I was pretty closed-minded at the beginning.

Sanders is my favorite teacher I have ever had. His classes are perfect for learning technical photography and he even incorporates the artistic, conceptual side of photography into his classes. YOU WILL LOVE PHOTOGRAPHY AFTER TAKING HIS CLASSES GUARANTEED!!
He is the best teacher of the photography dpt.His classes are interesting and fun. He really knows how to give a five hour lesson without getting you bored.

Seantel is so cool! He could probably teach anything with that great attitude and smile. He is passionate and really easy to talk to. Cares about students and knows so much about the industry. He is the best professor in the whole Photo Dept. Class can be long but he is a pleasure to listen to. Grading is really fair and he is inspiring!

Seantel subbed in my class for a day and it was so much better than my regular teacher, Linda Lowell. He was able to teach the class concepts in a matter of hours what Linda couldn't for months. He always has a smile, remembers you after meeting once, and helps students that aren't even his own. Smart, handsome, and very talented!!! Take him!
awesome guy and great teacher.

Seantel is lovely! Despite classes of 40 people, he knows all of our names and remembers details of our previous photography. This memory of his truly helps us see and understand the growth within our work, in a relatively short time period of 4 months. Also, he makes you laugh, which really makes the class a pleasure.

Sean is great! He always has a smile on his face, loves what he does, and finds humor in everything. He brings photography to life and really captures the best way to teach it. Although the class is long, it doesn't feel it. Class included an amazing field trip! I highly recommend this class to anyone who is committed to learning about photography!

I have taken two of her classes, and she is always available to help, grades fairly, and explains the course clearly. I always know what's going on and what I need to do in her class to be successful.

I liked Professor Shmitd; she is a great professor, but the only complaint is that she uses a frustrating website for assignments and tests. This website is called WileyPlus. When I explained the glitches, Professor Shmidt basically just said, "That sucks." I've lost so many points over this, I would not recommend taking this class.
Amazing professor!

A very hands-off fully online course, but a reasonable amount of work! As long as you keep up with assignments each week and read the textbook, you'll be fine. Weekly assessments rather than midterms/final exams, and everything is open-book.
She has little interests in students' learning.

Prof Schmidt made a not-so-fun course very approachable and the grading scale made it great, because there are so many additive factors besides the exams to determine the outcome. It was a lot of work, but so worth it !!

I enjoyed her Hybrid class. Every other Monday we met. We did not have to go to class as long as we did out work or had any questions. She does great lectures and is willing to help anyone out - flexible for students working full time. Does offer some extra credit. Pay attention to the reading. The quizzes/ test/homework are based off of it.

Eva made Accounting fun and easy. I've never done any accounting and even though it is a long class she managed to make it fun. She was always ready to explain in different ways and help you! Her syllabus was straightforward and it was clear from day one what was due for each class throughout the semester. I can't wait to take 230 and 240 with her.
Excellent teacher! This class actually help me get a better job position!! So glad I signed up!

Great class! Invest the effort & it will be well worth it. This class was my first accounting class & it was not easy. However, Eva is a great learning facilitator. She doesnt overload you with useless assignments. Since it was an evening class many in the clas work all day & are limited on time. She was always helpful when someone was struggling
Be prepared to be self taught.
Good class, very helpful, kind/caring, easy to approach/easygoing, always willing to explain something if you don't get it the first time around, flexible with assignments if you're really trying
Eva was very helpful with the in-class computer assignments. If you're not sure just ask her and she'll talk you through the problem.

I highly recommend this teacher if you want to learn the real world application of accounting basics. She loves for students to ask questions. And her explanations make sense. Its so obvious that she cares about her student!

Eva is a great instructor especially for new students in the accounting field; although, she does a great job with students of all levels of accounting and business knowledge. Eva is honest and open to answer questions from a non-judgmental perspective. I would highly recommend taking her courses.

This isn't a difficult class if you understand money and math. Your grade is out of 500 points and the 4 tests total 400 points. Every week there is a lot of homework out of the book, then you have online work, THEN there are in class assignments. And that's all only worth 100 points . . .lame. Tests are all out of the book and multiple choice.

Should have taken it for credit or no credit. When you ask for help you get more confused. Her use of Powerpoints, homework, class work are very confusing. Plan on reading the entire book word for word and using the tutors available at school.

She is the most unclear teacher I have ever had the misfortune of encountering. There is no direction in the class, everyone seems confused ALL THE TIME. She is a super nice lady, but I do not recommend taking a class from her.

She isn't clear at all. It sometime feels like she isn't even speaking English and she doesn't even bother to ask if we get the material. There is way too much homework for the actual course material. Do not take this class from her.

Took Soc 115 online for a requirment and I think the class was overall easy. You have to actually put effort into discussion posts and you will recieve full credit. All the class requires is a discussion post weekly and all the answers are straight foward in the textbook. Love her class, super interesting.

It's almost like this professor wants to make this class harder for us. I don't think her intention is for us to learn, and she likes to be very particular on assignments and tests. The grading is just flat out unfair and I have not had a professor that grades so harshly. She needs to be more clear on her expectations in the class and more flexible

Clear expectations when it comes to AI use, but the grading feels overly harsh. Feedback doesn't explain how to improve, which makes it hard to learn or grow. Seems more focused on being tough than actually helping students understand the material. I wish there were more emphasis on learning rather than nitpicking.

I had her during the summer for SOC 113. There are video clips/films and quite a bit of reading, but she provides a slide show with key points from the textbook. We had weekly discussion posts, a midterm, and final. She is really clear in her syllabus and strict on any AI use. Overall, if you do the work and enjoy the material, you'll do fine.

Professor Schmidt is a great and engaging Professor! There are "reading groups", every week you are required to read a chapter from Pearson (online textbook) and take a short quiz. Class just goes over the chapter you needed to have read the days before. It's very easy to follow what is being asked to be done in this class. I recommend!

Shes good but very fast. You can't take notes so its all based on just being involved in the class. you have ALOT of reading for chapters and two quizzes after all of them and you have to be a part of a reading group for every class. shes pretty easy if you do the work. its an easy class and pretty intresting but most common sense.

Ingrid taught me how to swim correctly in her beginning pool class. The following semester, she taught me to love swimming out in the open ocean. She is passionate, generous, and flexible. She wants you to succeed! She is a great coach! She's very supportive and always shows you how you can do better. Would love to take ocean swimming again!

In my personal opinion, she is the best ... At the beginning of class I regretted having taken her class because I could not walk well in the first week of class, but in the end I became very strong and able to exercise a lot and without get tired.

Ovean swimming is not for the feint of heart. You need to go for it and test your limits, and that is how Ingrid teaches this class. She is incredibly knowledgeable and supportive, enthusiastic and challenging.

Have taken many PE classes and Ingrid teaches 2 of the toughest, but most rewarding - Ocean Swim and Boot Camp. She has style, wit and grace, and her classes are more fun than a day @Disneyland!! FYI, in PE you must show up and participate in order to pass the class!

Ingrid is hands down the most caring teacher at SBCC she is an amazing swim instructor and truly cares about every single student in her class. I conquered a life long fear of mine this semester and I could not have done it without her. I am looking forward to taking other swim courses with her and I highly recommend you take any course she teaches

Ingrid is so great! Her ocean swimming class was by far the coolest class I've ever had the opportunity to take! She was always very enthusiastic and supportive. She just wanted people to get out in the water and have a good swim. She made me love the ocean even more. It's a great environment! Highly recommend both the teacher and the class!

Ingrid Schmitz is a precise but fun and joyful coach and teacher. My swimming has improved greatly. She uses video, lots of demonstrations & even gets in the water herself at times to demonstrate. She knows to inspire without being demanding. It was the class I most looked forward to and always put me in a good mood for the rest of the day.

I had Ingrid for Skiing, Ocean Swimming and now Bootcamp. In all three classes she inspired me to do my best and achieve things I never thought I could. She's kicking my butt in Bootcamp with high-intensity, interval training work-outs. She's super positive, always smiling, extremely patient and really cares about her students. You'll love her!

Amazing class, improved so much during this class. Started out with a really bad freestyle stroke (not sure you can call what I did freestyle swimming) but Ingrid really helped me with feedback and tips and now I can swim for more than 1h 30min without stopping. I really recommend this class, swimming with dolphins happened more than once.
Very nice teacher. She loves what she teaches and care about you improving in her class. I got an A

Ocean Swimming(oc) changed my life and how I view the power of the mind. I've taken oc 2x and the 1st time I took it I won't lie, I was scared. Hwvr, the more consistent I was, the easier it became to push the scary thoughts out; enjoy the ride. Ingrid is amazin/supportive in becoming familiar with swimming in the ocean. Oc made me stronger swimmer

Ingrid is a great person and teacher! I was very excited to take ocean swimming. I love swimming in pools and I like the ocean. But, I didn't know how different or scary it would be to me because we didn't go in the ocean until after the drop date. Make sure you like open-water swim and are willing to buy a wetsuit before you commit!

Ingird is really nice! I was really excited about this class, but as time went on I lost interest. If you aren't afraid of sharks, seaweed, or the ocean, then take this class! If you are afraid of any of those things, think twice before taking ocean swim. It made it very difficult to motivate me to go to the class.
She is an incredible teacher. So helpful and so kind
Summer tennis class with Ingrid will go down as one of my all time favorite experiences in college! She is about as nice and caring a person as I've ever met. If you get the chance TAKE THIS CLASS!!!
Super fun and really cares about her students and you definetly improve in swim strok
Very enthusiastic, knowledgeable and fun!
I really loved this teacher. I came to class about half the time and was so behind on work but I came to her for help and she extended my due dates so I could pass! Really sweet.
No Comments
She is so nice. Will help anyone pass her class. I suggest taking her.
Makes class interesting by bringing in people from different careers> helps with preparing for exams talks about current sport news good discussions career project takes time but good>

Professor Jose Segundo understands the difficulty of learning a new language so he makes an effort to help out students and cares for their success in the class. Pretty heavy workload, but all activities are designed to practice Spanish grammar. Great professor!

Jose has some refining to do on his course. The experience was frankly a mess. Jose is reasonable, and has good character, but the workload is insane. The highlights are his group activities, he makes them fun and engaging. Unfortunately I recommend you avoid this course. There was 3x the number of assignments than my next most demanding class.
To give you perspective, I just redid Spanish 102 with a different professor and got an A, so. I think that's all the explaining you need.
Very understanding and accessible

I was really dreading the course as I was only taking more language for a requirement but loved it. He structured the class well especially for it only being 6 weeks. During the final, I got logged off because my internet was shut down. He was super understanding for make-ups as I had a medical procedure the next day.

I was a bit nervous to be taking a language online but Profe Segundo pleasantly surprised me! With little time to improvise in the corona madness, he did an amazing job creating material and making fun video assignments to keep it interactive. Hes also super helpful in answering any questions in Canvas or zoom. Do the work & you can get an A!

Initially, I was nervous to take a 5 unit class in a 6-week summer session but Profe Gil was always willing to elaborate on instructions (zoom office hours at your convenience). There are a lot of assignments but they are manageable if you stay on top of the course material and due dates. I would definitely take a Spanish course with him again

Jose is an accessible, accommodating, and encouraging professor. He uses a variety of resources and platforms so students can practice speaking in low stress environments. My favorite part was talking in a small group (4-5 students) with a professor from Spain on zoom once a week. The class is very wholistic and not very memorization heavy.

I want to give him 4-5 stars because he was the best Spanish teacher I've had but because he's fairly new to teaching it was tough. Even for a summer course the amount of work he gave was overwhelming and when we confronted him about it he gave us that 'too bad' attitude. Assignment requirements were ridiculous and many more things that were unfair

Such an amazing professor. He goes out of his way to help his students during his own free time. He is genuinely passionate to spread his knowledge of the Spanish language and cultures. I strongly encourage taking the class with him. The activities that he planned were super fun and engaging as well. :) A thousand percent recommend.

Professor Gil was a really great Professor! Always there to help and always answers emails. There is a lot of homework to do in this class but then again it is learning a language and is for your best! The homework is very easy. Would definitely recommend Professor Gil! He is very caring towards his students and truly wants you to achieve.

One of the greatest teachers that I've had. He is there for his students who really want help and are willing to put in effort. All you have to do is ask and he is there to answer your questions .He teaches you everything you need to pass the course and genuinely cares for his students success. Highly recommend his class. Thank you professor!!
Worst professor I have ever had. Takes forever to grade assignments and doesnt even teach the material in class just plays games. Dont take if you actually want to learn Spanish.

He is the absolute best! He genuinely cares for our well being and acknowledges the heavy workload which comes with a foreign language and tries his best to accommodate our needs. Also he is hilarious.. I actually looked forward to his classes each day.
Best professor I've ever had. Really in it for the students. Highly recommend to anyone but especially anyone who's really interested in actually learning the language.

Ive never met a professor so kind and attentive as Jose. He was the most understanding and patient professor thus far. I recommend him to EVERYONE. Do your part and he will sure show you that he acknowledges your efforts.
Prof Jose was a great instructor. Very friendly and had fun lectures. Class was very easy as long as you showed up. Would definitely recommend taking this class.

Jose is one of the best teachers I've had at SBCC. He truly cares about his students, while keeping the class interesting and full of information. He is approachable and willing to teach until you fully understand. It felt great walking away from class everyday knowing I really got my money's worth of education. Attendance is crucial/respectful.

During class, he interacts a lot with the students. We improved our Spanish by talking a lot and playing games. The class was very useful to take. He's also a very nice teacher who cares about his students.

Hands down the best Spanish teacher I've ever had. He's passionate and excited to show up to class everyday. Everything is based on participation and lecture. If you pay attention and participate you will get good grades and learn the language. this is how a foreign language should be taught!

Prof Jose is a great instructor- he clearly loves teaching and his enthusiasm and energy are contagious. Attendance is important as he stresses interaction with classmates. He tries to make class fun by using games and popular culture. He wants students to succeed. Work hard: your Spanish will improve and you'll have fun along the way.

Jose is the best Spanish professor I have ever had. It's important to go to class in order to learn the material and better practice and understand the lectures, but honestly going to his class is so fun and easy it didn't even matter. He really cares about his students so as long as you work hard and show up it's easy to earn an A.
Really great professor! He likes to interact with his students a lot, so if your someone who is shy and likes to sit in the back of the class. He will kind of make you intersect with others.

One of the best professors I've ever had. The Positive Psychology course was so enjoyable! I learned so much in this class that has already benefitted my life, and that I'm sure will give me a better life going forward. He offers so much wisdom, really cares about students, presents fascinating material, and is a kind teacher.

Kind, approachable, & knowledgable in the field but class time spent rehashing readings, showing Ted talks, & briefly discussing our biweekly journaling assignment. Grade: weekly reading notes ("Authentic Happiness" Seligman & "Positive Psychology" Hart), short take-home final. Probably a good class if you're a fast reader. Provides a snack break.

Honestly, this is the most life changing class I have ever taken. Get ready for a professor that cares about his material and about your absorbing for the betterment of your own life. The way he has styled his class is so that you can apply what he teaches to your life. Lots of reading but super good stuff.Take hime if you get the chance.wont regr.
The material is super interesting and important. I highly recommend this class and professor. He's very experienced and easy to talk to. Only downside is that it's a night class for 3 hours.

If you are a Psych major, PLEASE take him! He not only follows the textbook, but also talks about his opinions and experience on the subjects discussed. The only homework are quizzes but you get two tries on them and all the info is in the book. He will make sure you get nothing beneath an A.
Most amazing educator I have ever had. If you have the ability to take him, YOU SHOULD!

Take the positive psychology class, you wont be disappointed!!! VERY easy grader, he makes it hard to earn anything lower than an A. I've learned so much important advice about life and myself. The only thing is that the classes are pretty long and can get tiring, but they're always full of interesting topics and discussions.
He has an amazing curriculum and is super humble. Easy class. Amazing human being.
Easily one of my top professors ever. Spence really wants all his students to do well and is super flexible for your needs. You really can't ask for a better professor!!!!!!

Mr Sherman has a love to teach and inspire which makes him such a wonderful teacher. He has his class set up to self pace which left me so much more less stressed and able to learn as much as I could. He really cares about his students and doesn't ask much at all. Just do your best and you'll pass with flying colors. You will not regret taking him.

You could tell that he has a love for teaching and for psychology. There was a quiz every week but if you read the textbook is was easy. For intro to psych, his lectures were interesting and he made the classes enjoyable overall.

If you ever get the chance to take one of his classes do not hesitate! He really is a gem! He clearly cares about all of his students and loves his job. He literally inspired my career path by his amazing detailed lectures. This is a class that you will look forward to and grow from.

Spence is a very caring teacher, he really wants you to succeed in the class. You just need to read two chapters per week and take notes down. Really useful information in positive psychology. Take his class, or you will be regretful.
He's such an amazing teacher. He's really passionate about psych and makes class fun.

Prof. Sherman is incredibly caring, and he always wants his students to succeed. We took notes for each reading, watched TED talks in class, & the only test we took was the final (which was very easy if you paid attention in class). He facilities interesting discussions for the class and his insight is eye-opening. I learned SO much about life!
Spence is a very very very good teacher. He is mild and respectful. Just take his class, easy A! I love his class so much. You can learn a lot in his class.

Took his positive psych class and was one my favorite classes ever. Hw is light notes on books we read in class and 1 forum/week. U will learn different ways to increase positive emotion, why happiness is so important and new things about yourself u didnt know. Great class and amazing professor highly recommend whether youre a psych major or not.

Spencer Sherman is a really nice professor. You can tell that he loves psychology and tries to motivate you as a student to do so as well. Getting an A is very easy in this class. The lectures were okay, not too informative to be honest, he just talks a lot about what the book already teaches you.
He is an amazing teacher, very easy class, trust me. You will love him as you take his class. Easy to get an A+ without struggling hard.

Spence has been my absolute favorite teacher so far @sbcc. I was nervous taking a class from 6-9 but have never seen someone so happy and dedicated to being there which made me look forward to going. As long as you show up, do the journal entry and engage; easy A. Spence cares about each student so much. just finished this class and miss it already

I really enjoyed having professor Sherman. He has a very clear syllabus that explains how you can get an A. You can make it super easy for yourself by completing each homework assignment on time and showing up.

I love Sherman as a person and I very much respect him but his classes can get quite boring and since attendance is not required it is sometimes hard to force myself to go. He teaches directly out of the book and attendance can definitely help you out on the tests but definitely not necessary

Spencer Sherman's a great person, he seems really fascinated with the subject but unfortunately doesn't teach very well. The thing is, all of our studying is in the textbook and actually teaching us new information in the class doesn't happen. Although he says his tests are really easy, it is still a good idea to study a lot. Great teacher!!

I liked Professor Sherman overall, he's a good teacher. One thing I didn't like is that his lectures basically consists of him telling us what's in the book. There's barely any homework aside from studying for exams and no attendance is required but he gives you extra credit for coming to class.

Professor Sherman is an amazing professor. He is passionate about psychology and really just wants his students to love and understand it too. He is very clear and fair when it comes to grading. In addition, if you have any questions he loves to answer them, give you reading material on the topic and will make sure you under stand it.

Spencer is nice and likes teaching but everything he lectures on and what's on the tests are all in the text book. So going to the class is rather pointless but he does offer some extra credit for attendance. I would take hime again.

The material in the class was absolutely fascinating and changed my life for the better - I'm so much happier. He does play extremely long (20-40 minute) videos. His lectures, however, are absolutely fascinating. You can only miss two classes while getting an A. Homework and journal is easy to do. But you really need to show up.
Spence is amazing. Seriously, if you have the chance to take this class- take it. EASY A.
need an easy A then take this class. the class is interesting, helpful and not hard.
Spencer Sherman made a hard class easy, while still teaching just as much as any other psych class. Super passionate and I feel like I've learned the material, not just memorized it.

Ausgezeichnet! Frau Spandau is an amazing professor. Makes learning German fun and easy. There are a few assignments due at the end of every week, mostly worksheets and fill in the blanks. The midterm is a small writing assignment and the final is essentially a massive cumulative worksheet. Take her class for a fun, easy, and rewarding experience.

Professor Spandau ist ein fantastischer Dozent! Sie liebt ihr deutsches Erbe wirklich und das merkt man in jeder Vorlesung. Professor Spandau is a fantastic instructor! She really loves her German heritage and that comes through with each lecture.

Professor Spandau offers an accelerated class, in which you move through material relatively quickly. You must be interested and invested in learning German to do well. Definitely not a class to slack off or not show up to.
She is one of the best professors I've ever had! She's genuine, kind and seriously cares about her students. There are weekly vocabulary quizzes, but if you study they are easy points.
German is a very confusing language but she did her best to teach us

This was an extremely demanding class. You have to be really want to learn German because you will be studying for vocabulary quizzes every week. Online work also takes up a lot of your free time. She is one of the best teachers though because of her humor, her ability to work with the student and u can hand in late assignments and corrected tests
Great teacher! Final was hard though, so study well.

If you have never taken german you have to study often! None of this show up to class everyday and pass. She is a nice teacher but moves through the material way to quick for a language class. The text book is decent. If you have taken german before, 101 will be breeze. 102 is a crap ton of grammar though :(...

i have never felt more talked down to in a class before.she asked our class if we knew that multiplication and division weren't just buttons on a calculator and instead of helping my lab group she walked away telling us that maybe science just wasn't our strong suit.shes incredibly rude to her students and consistently starts class 20+ minutes late

I took her physics 103 class last semester and I got an A. I am not a stem person at all and just told her throughout the semester that I wanted to finish with as strong of a grade as I could and made sure to turn everything in. Best way to get a good grade is to just show up to each lab as a lot of people miss them. She was so kind and helpful!

Professor Stark was one of the best teachers I've had in my life. While the classes were long and the labs were usually pretty time consuming, Stark did everything she could to make sure everyone left the classroom understanding what we learned. All she wanted was for everyone to be successful, and she would do anything she could to help.

If you take the class, listen to what she says before the lab starts! She answers most of the questions while she talks. She talks down on students if they don't understand or ask a 'dumb' question, and the take home assignment directions are insanely confusing. However, the labs aren't hard, the homework isn't hard, and it is an easy A.

Professor Stark was a really great teacher. The lectures and the labs were often really fun and engaging. There are a few extra credit assignments throughout the semester. There are group quizzes and individuals quizzes every week, but they're not too hard. I'm not the best at physics, but I really enjoyed the class. Show up & participate for an A.

terrible teacher. if you dont understand something she makes fun of you for it. has terrible preparations for exams and finals and none of the quiz practices on canvas are the same as the quiz's so studying them is pointless. Because of this class my gpa is going to go down a ton. would not take her again....

everything assigned has some sort of trick to it so you fail almost everything, example is on the first test, half the grade is tricking you by this little arrow you must draw that is only assigned by extra credit which she does not say it is the only way to study for it, if you have the time to take the class twice than you will get an easy A

She tries her hardest to cover a ton of information and the book isn't mandatory but does help. With tons of material comes tons of information! Pay attention in the class and take as much notes as you can! Easy quizzes with extra credit potential every week. Mandatory attendance (90% Two or fewer Extra 10 pts!). Very knowledgeable and fun!
Lynn is very nice, she is really into the subject. Class is fun, quizz every week,two midterms and a final and little projects here and there. she is really nice and class is very interesting!

interesting class, lots of info. i studied alot for the tests and still didnt do that well. utilize the textbook. class really is not that hard, i didnt try that hard and still passed! i didnt miss one class though, quiz every week. at the beginning there was alot of trick questions but you just get used to them

I am not a science person but thoroughly enjoyed this class. Multiple extra credit opportunities. If you pay attention, attend class, and complete the assignments, it's an easy A. We always got out at least an hour early for lab. Lynne is very approachable...go to her office hours if you need extra help!!

Devoted teacher. Her classroom style is a little fast and unclear, same for lab. Grades hard, and expects independent thinking & ability. Not an easy class - tough with regard to analytics. You will learn multitudes of info that are useful, but be prepared to work for your grade. If you are not into science, give this a miss. Liked her as a person.

great science class for non science majors. work moderately hard for a B. really hard and an A is attainable. u basically skim the basics of a lot of different stuff. lots of interesting stuff. highly reccomend!

Lynne is awesome! I'm not the science type at all, but Lynne's class was totally manageable. I took this class to complete my lab science and I'm glad I did. The subject matter is all surface level. She's so passionate about her class and has been doing it for years. Very nice and approachable too. I highly recommend her!

The class is an easy A if you genuinely try hard, attend all lectures and lab, and do the guided reading. There's no room for half-assing if you want an A. She's REALLY nice and this class is DEF for non-science majors. It's probably the easiest science class SBCC has to offer. (It's not ALL equations and stuff)

He actions toward her students are very uncalled for. She does not treat her students with respect like most teachers do. She is also not the most positive person. She told me that I was not going to do well and that this class was maybe not for me after the first two weeks. But I will admit that it made me want to try harder to prove her wrong

she is the best teacher I've ever had. she is a very good grader and good at explaining. she also gives lots of extra credits. you will be probably given "B" or A if you study cuz I got A grade even though I'm an international students and not that good at english.

i thought this was going to be an easy class... i was told its a science for non science majors. if you are not a science person DO NOT take this class! I had a good GPA and this is going to drop it... think twice and find another option if you want a good grade

The class touches on a variety of topics, including physics, geology, global warming, and astronomy. The lectures are interesting and she makes them fun by demonstrating various experiments. Homework consists of reading (nothing lengthy) and the exams are multiple choice. The labs are fairly easy and she provides weekly extra credit opportunities.

She is an annoying hippi, but her class is simple. Never opened my book, don't buy it. Just listen to her boring lectures and do good on the labs, that of course may be hard if your group sucks, but me and my friends were in a group together.

Prof Stark is the best professor I have ever had. She explains everything so well that you don't even have to read the book. Just take notes in case you forget. Class is actually not that bad because she has cool science experiments that she shows us and most of the material in that class is really interesting.

EASIEST CLASS I'VE TAKEN AT CC. The lectures are boring as hell but go to them and pay attention and you won't have to study. Go to the labs and do the extra credit. 3 projects, but if you ask her to look at them before they are due you guarantee yourself 100%. Nice lady, too into science but nice.

very good teacher, genuinly cares for her students and wants to see them succeed. easy class, tons of extra credit. she clearly teaches and explains everything, makes the class as interesting as she can..shes just a nice hippy who really loves science and her students! if you have to take a science with a lab i highly reccomend her!

One of the best teachers @ SBCC. ALOT of EC oppurtunities and if you fail a few quizes there are plenty more to rasie your grade!! The finals are somewhat hard but you get to have notecards, so that makes it easy!! You learn ALOT from this teacher and the class...VERY HELPFUL....VERY INFORMATIVE...TAKE HER!!

Comments are correct. However, she doesn't make it feel comfortable to ask questions. She does not give U much of a chance after she is done with a concept and moves on. She also never looks U in the eye while lecturing its always above your head and she is very negative about her students succeeding and tells you she sets your mind up for failure

This is one of THE BEST teachers @ SBCC. Very easy + you learn ALOT. She goes out of her way to help out every1, plus gives A TON of extra credit (almost once twice every week, one on weekly quiz and a take home EC -> The answers for take home EC r online). Take her, you wont be sorry, you'll not only learn, but get an A/B on your report card.
good class easy take notes extra credit easy just ask tutor also all answers to extra credit on Google.
great class-good demos, clear instruction. Easy and fun.

Bob is a great professor, he can be tough on the students that act like children but if you're mature and professional he is an incredible instructor. Expect to work and learn a ton when you take his class.

HORRIBLE school and teachers are harassing students! I took classes in automotive services in 2008 with an old grouchy teacher Robert Stockero. Mr. Stockero, is really helpful, and he has a hot body!
Great teacher and knows engine performance very well.
Rosette is one of a kind! You will love her class and learn so much!! She makes it so easy to stay engaged in a 4 hour lecture!! I recommend her over and over AGAIN!!!
One of the greatest teachers I've had, she's very knowledgeable and helpful.

Shes legit. Rosette has the best stories and there is so much to learn from her. Her powerpoints are helpful and reliable - she doesnt give study guides but all material on the slides are testable so pay attention to those. I enjoyed her class and ability to make a 4 hour lecture not just bearable, but pleasant.
She's an amazing instructor and any nursing student is lucky have the chance to learn from her!

Rosette was the bomb-diggaty! He lectures were clear, concise, interesting and fun. She's a no nonsense teacher though so you have to come to class ready to learn and have your assignmnets in on time. In my 150 units of college Ive only had 1 other teacher as fantabulous as her!
She is very intelligent, and the BEST instructor i have ever had. She is Amazing, and very knowledgeable, and always available for help.

She is probably the BEST instructor I have EVER had. She's such an inspiration to future nurses. She knows her stuff and seems a little intimidating at first, but once you get to know her, you'll be glad you have her as your instructor. She's awesome! :)

This instructor is amazing. She really wants you to succeed, and believes in all of her students. She is truly kind and compassionate and full of knowledge. I feel honored to have been able to learn from her and will miss her spirit for nursing and education after I graduate. She is a person anyone would be lucky to call a friend.

She is an amazing instructor. Extremely intelligent and has high standards for her students. She is tough beucase as nurses we are dealing with people's lives, therefore, we have to be well prepared. She prepares you to be the best nurse you can be!

Extremely intelligent and talented instructor. Give her 100% and you will have no problems. You will be the best nurse thanks to her teaching (same goes for the other LVN instructors, but especially this one).
No Comments
Nice to your face. Just keep your head down and no matter what morals you have to sacrifice, stay on her good side!!!!

Professor Sweeney's class was very straightforward and informative. There were weekly assignments and quizzes, with a project at the end of the semester. You have enough assignments to make up for your grade, and she is very helpful and responsive. I would take her class again!

Profession Shawna is awesome. She immediately responds to module questions and emails. Even though, she did not have many lecture videos, her power points and chapter reading was enough to get As on the quizzes and tests. This class was great! I would definitely take her class again.

She either wants to make her course as inconvenient as possible by making you complete assignments on 3 different platforms or she gets commission for forcing you to buy Connect access on top of an expensive textbook. Going to take medical terminology at another college. Not worth breaking the bank for an optional class.

She wants you to buy a textbook AND Connect access which costs over $100 instead of having you do quizzes/tests/assignments on Canvas like every other professor I've had since they stopped using Moodle. Speaking of Moodle, she ALSO wants you to do certain things on there as well. & doesn't let you see your course grade, just specific assignments.

Assignments due weekly along with discussion and quizzes. Assignments and quizzes aren't difficult and if you study the material that week, you will pass. She resuses her syllabus over and over and many dates are wrong. I was having to look at her schedule daily in Canvas because I missed a couple of assignments due to wrong dates on the syllabus.

Professor Sweeney cares about the learning experience of her students. She was always very when questions were asked. She has high expectations of her students though her assignment load will push you to do your best. She is talented with online tools and teaches her students how to be their best. She is awesome!

Took this as an online class during summer. You are LITERALLY teaching yourself. The 'lecture' is just a list of things to look up in the textbook. The slides to look at are taken directly from the textbook with no talking over them, no notes, nothing that she added herself. The discussion has nothing helpful from her. You're your own professor.

No compassion for us hard-working students. Does not cut off load in the Summer making it an unrealistic class to take if you work even part-time. And doesn't even warn you about what is expected of you next. Do not recommend her. ESPECIALLY FOR ONLY 3 UNITS!! I give her a 0! DO NOT TAKE IT IN THE SUMMER!!!

The class was very repetitive and the book that we used never worked for me. After reaching out to her for help about this problem, her email was unhelpful and basically suggested if I had WiFi. Too focused on small details and not about the big picture.

This teacher is too focused on discussion forums. They are due every week, you can't see others responses until you post one yourself . Forums like this aren't helpful, I would rather have an actual coding assignment or paper even - not constant posting to other students - i want more from the teacher, not the students.
Very sweet, nice lady. Always there for you whenever you need help. I definitely recommend her.

The HIT program at SBCC is tougher than one might think and the reason for that is the lack of good instructors. Shawna is exceptional. As an older student I've come to understand that several Profs set students up to fail when it should be the opposite. Shawna is challenging but helpful. I hope other instructors will take a queue from her.

I strongly recommend her classes if you are serious about HIT and planning for credentials like RHIT. The forum topics, homework (even though it feels like a lot) helped me to understand what are the important(most know) things in this field. If you work hard, you will get good grades.

HIT 240 was ok. She gives decent lectures and the quizes are mostly clear. She might reword things as to confuse you in tests. Worst part was the group project (5+ page essay) that is worth a lot of points. Overall shes ok. She does respond fairly quick to emails and is an overall nice lady.

Shawna provided great tips in her lectures.Almost all assignments are thru the textbook and she gives good support when needed (follow the text and you should do well).Instructions for the Project Analysis could use some re-editing to make it easier to follow or understand (was somewhat confusing).

Her discharge analysis Project was extremely confusing/unclear She sent out an email to everyone upset that we were all emailing her because we were confused about the instructions! It would of helped if we would have worked on the sections through-out the class b4 giving us a foreign assignment.

She's a good professor all in all, she's extremely detail-oriented and gave good feedback. Assignments are relatively doable as long as you have strong writing skills. I don't think it was the prof, but this class was very stressful and taxing for me (wouldn't take it again).

This professor made the class unreasonably hard and it's only worth 1 credit. My 5 credit class is less stressful and less work than this. I have no idea how she will decide to grade my next assignment. She does not give a clear rubric on how she grades her assignments and when asked, she says that is what I deserved. She's extremely unhelpful.
really good professor i don't understand the bad reviews
so much todo just to receive one credit boooo

I had Prof. Taibjee for my Intro to Library Research course. It was a simple course based on Pass or Not Pass. I passed with an A but it would be the same for a B or C. She was nice and helpful and teaches how to do better research in preparation for harder, more intensive courses. Assignments are straightforward, just get it done for easy credit

An extremely tough grader, but doesn't communicate how she is going to grade very well. She will fail you on a last minute technicality she made up... at the last minute. For example she will write instructions that don't match the sample, and she will create a rubric, but then grade you based off of how she feels at the moment. I do not recommend.

There is next to no instruction from her for this class, she just posts videos and tutorials for you to do. When you do an assignment wrong she doesnt explain how you can fix it, just tells you what you did wrong. Very harsh and honestly rude to students. I learned nothing in this class that will benefit me in any way. Worst teacher I have had.

She doesn't know what she's doing. Super harsh grader and when you ask her to explain what you did wrong so you can fix it she refuses to explain. Sucks at getting back to you when you have questions or just need to reach out. This class could have actually been helpful but i learned nothing because of her style of "teaching". VERY disappointing.

Expect work of 3 credit class (6.75hrs x 3) per week. The pedagogy of this class needs a lot of work. The videos are no substitute for a lecture. Know MLA this class won't teach it. May get negative grade reprisal from asking process questions about class configuration. Trick quiz questions, never learn expected answer. Don't procrastinate.
do not take her, that's all I have to say

She is the best teacher I've ever had. Deeply cares about her subject and making sure that you learn in her class. She let's you re-submit the assignment after she gives her feedback to you, without penalizing you. i WISH ALL OF MY PROFESSORS WERE LIKE HER. It's a real wow experience. R.E.S.P.E.C.T. Thank you!

Online was easier than in person. I have taken him in person first and it seemed harder. I didn't pass so I took him online and much easier. He literally does videos and basically gives the answers for the lab work book.

I wasn't expecting this class to be easy by any means but I feel as though the professor makes it harder than it needs to be. He is a tough grader and there's A LOT of work. Expect to complete assignments, quizzes and "pop" quizzes same week as midterms. Sometimes "pop" quizzes assigned and due the same day as the midterm.

This was the hardest course I've ever taken, but the professor and tutor were incredibly helpful. Joining a study group is highly recommended—it helps with learning the material. Despite its difficulty, it was one of my favorite classes. The professor is a tough grader but always available, eager to help, and truly wants students to succeed.

The professor deserves a higher rating. Labs were fun, and he's very knowledgeable. He's a tough grader and expects a lot— course requires serious effort, both in and outside of class (expected for a healthcare career). He might be a bit old-school, but it was a great course. He's dedicated, always available, genuinely cares about student success.

DON'T TAKE W/ BARRY. Lab quizzes every single week, every single thing from each week prior is fair game; spelling counts. Lecture super detailed and exams pull out small details. Barely passed and I've had a 4.0 for 6 yrs. You need to calculate your own grade he doesn't even log it in canvas; its based on points. Nice guy, horrible class.

Lectures gave no insight as to what would be on the test. all fend for yourself. No study guide. No useful open lab or study hours.
