Prof would not answer any of your questions would be told to figure it out yourself. Started class with approx 30 students, ended with 10. Majority of students dropped.

Professor Barry's class is no cakewalk, but the resources he provides can be a lifesaver. Lectures are dense, so staying engaged and using tools like Unstuck study are key. The workload is heavy, but put in the effort and you'll learn a ton.
Professor Tanowitz is excellent. He makes memorizing all those bones, organs and systems enjoyable.
Impossible to learn successfully in this class. Lack of organization, motivation, and talent. As someone with a learning disability this is an impossible class to thrive in.

Oh, how much I tortured Prof. Tanowitz. Tons of questions, and he loved answering those. This is a medical field, so you like it or not, you have to study. He offers everything that he knows and then some, and it's our job to absorb the info. I personally loved his slow, handwritten lectures, better than others that don't allow taking pictures...;)

I don't know how he has such a high rating. I have never had a professor be so rude and grade so difficult. His lectures make no sense and the tests are so hard. Half of my class dropped because of his teaching. Take patty or peter instead.

Love Barry as a person! He really knows his stuff and it is clear he loves his job. I always had a fun time in lab and it was so interesting. But… if you want to spend countless hours studying and barely hang on to a good grade then this class is for you. This class is NOT for the weak. Take with ease.

I love Barry as a person! But the way his class is structured is very difficult to not to fall back. Lab is the easiest part of the class. But lectures are heavy with so much information. Everything is cumulative which makes everything worse. Take advantage of tutors and review sessions!!

He is heavy on homework and tests on many of the most obsolete information some of which he never lectures on and is barely mentioned in the book. Also he does things like makes an online quiz due on THANKSGIVING! (a Thursday) when canvas quizes are ALWAYS due on Sundays. Purposely tricky! He does not grade on a curve he is completely point based.

Please listen to these reviews. You might do well if you have no job and it's your only class. The class is so disorganized, tutors are barely there to help, study materiel does not match exams/quizzes, a lot of it is self taught. I've spent HOURS a day studying and watching YouTube just to hopefully pass at all.

You can definitely do well in this class but it'll only be if you put the work in, not because of any help from the professor. In general, the lectures are bad, he is not very helpful if you email, and his quizzes will randomly be on information from weeks ago so there is no way to effectively study for them. If you can, take Peter instead.

I took the course of the summer and didn't do as well as I wanted, but the professor helped me get to where I needed to be to pass the class. He was easy to reach and responded to questions within a short time frame. His lectures are also well organized and easy to follow, so are the study guides for exams. Great professor!

If u r serious about the medical field take Barry. He is not a bad teacher, but it is a challenging course + information overload. U will want to dedicate at least 4-6 hours a day to study. He promptly responds to emails &has sunday review sessions. The tutor was extremely helpful. Plenty of extra credit offered

I found Barry's teaching style to be perfect for physio. He'd always take time to draw every schema/structure which was super helpful. The nature of the class is tough & he doesn't hold your hand but he gives A LOT of resources that you need to take advantage of. If you plan to transfer to a UC, be ready bc many profs run their courses like him imo
He is very passionate about physiology and anatomy, which is good because you are able to learn a lot. Tough grader, you will spend your life on this class. STUDY STUDY STUDY!!!

Ditto to all others. In addition to lots of memorizing word for word, I had to teach myself concepts due to ineffective lectures. Test are cumulative, course is poorly organized. I oftentimes wasn't studying optimally for the upcoming quiz/test. Not a bad guy, but he's a bad teacher, and it is a challenging course.

He has unclear course expectations, some material in the course is misleading/false, the syllabus is bad, his material is scattered across resources, wastes your time, tests don't reflect assigned material, rather than making clear expectations just offers a lot of extra credit.Worse than professors I've had who can't speak English. Seems lazy.

In a nutshell, prepare to not have a life. The amount of material is too much, not including time to study it. Reviewing it, THEN studying it was approx. 3-5 hours per day. 3/5 stars because at least Barry is organized and gives study material. It's a shame that he overloads because then students can't actually retain and learn the information.

Over 1/2 of the students dropped this class because it is ridiculously hard. Unfair Grading. He fails to upload accurate grades to Canvas then turns in a completely unexpected final grade so you cant protest. You can try to study but you will still fail the tests. No matter how much work you put in, you won't pass.

One of the hardest classes I have taken at the city college, no preparation for exams, no study guides, his lectures are not helpful at all, and has multiple quizzes on top of pop quizzes in the same day and they are a guessing game of what they will be on. Not to mention he puts pop quizzes on the same day as your midterms. Would not recommend.

Do not take BMS classes online with him! He makes them very confusing and I had to go to the tutor every week to understand. Tons of hw (almost 6 hrs some days). Lab, Lab Quiz, and 2 lecture assignments weekly + notes. He is a little rude over zoom when you need help. Health science majors take Peter or Patty!

I had to drop this class. His syllabus is so confusing. The structure of this course in canvas is confusing too. Honestly, everything was all over the place. His class is, a do in your pace with deadlines. His lectures are on YouTube and they have a digital book for the lab part. Also, I wish he open the canvas a week and half before class starts.

Took BMS 107 with Barry and it was one of the hardest A's I have ever gotten. However, he prepared me for classes I had at University. Back taking physio again with Barry and it is the same story as BMS 107. Read your lecture notes, any information Barry gives you must learn, and do all the extra credit assigned. Treat it like a job, you will pass.

An example of how absurd his quizzes and exams are: he tests you on whether you've memorized his mnemonics for certain processes. I've laughed out loud on multiple exams at how disorganized and poorly written his questions are. Doesn't matter if you know the material well.

Hes absolutely horrible & doesn't teach you a single thing. I think in his own head he believes hes a good teacher, but lemme tell you He Sucks! Online class, cant use paper for proctorio tests and quizzes, even though it's basically all chemistry. Does not give periodic table, so memorize all the atomic masses. Just save yourself money, and time.

I took her before Covid and let me tell you she's so kind and does an amazing job keeping everything fun and interactive. She probably works very hard and loves having you use your artistic abilities and partner with others. Unlike with many teachers who make you read boring books, Marit will always choose an interesting one. Thanks )
Great teacher! Would definitely take her again!

I had teacher Marit for ESL130 and ESL 134. She is the best teacher in my life!!! The book about Morrie was so great! I had to write too much but Marit made every class interesting and exCiting. I now like to write essaYs because my teacher helped me.
Best teacher!

It's a really nice teacher. She try to make the class as interesting as possible by game, quiz or group activities. There is a lot of reading and homework but it's easy. Really good teacher I recommended!

She makes sure students understand the course. If we don't understand, she allows us to revise and resubmit. She pays attention to detail, whether it's grading or in-person body language. She loves what she does! She may have a resting face, but don't let it intimidate you; she's very caring!!

Leida Tolentino struts like Harvard-tenured royalty at a community college. Delusional much? Drowns you in busywork hell: weekly essays, endless revisions, daily posts graded like she's personally offended. Real assignment: 400+ words guessing her kid's Halloween costume. Spoiler alert: it was freaking Rumi from KPop Demon Hunters. Peak pointless!
Leída is very passionate about her job, she allows students to participate in open discussions if they are willing. She notices small details and tries to make a connection.
Arrogant. Rude.

I absolutely love her teaching and personality. Im so confused about these ratings , not true at all . Very easy class and very passionate about her job. Please take this professor . This is one of those times where the negative reviews are written by students that just don't do their work and don't participate at all!!

took this class last semester and im still not over how rude and disrespectful this teacher is.when i had an issue with a grade(it was put in completely wrong and i gave evidence)she ignored emails and made herself unavailable.when we finally met she told me she wouldn't change it because i didnt build report with her in the beginning of the class.

Leida is the most passionate and inspiring professor I have ever had in my life. I genuinely look forward to waking up in the morning to attend her classes. The assignments are extremely easy as well, so I am very shocked to see these reviews. Take her class, you will not regret it at all!

I took Tolentino's research methods class last fall and I have to say it was by far the worst class I have taken at sbcc. Not only is she dismissive, passive aggressive and downright rude towards students when asked questions. Her lectures are very difficult to follow and she does not pause even when the entire class seems to be completely lost.
PSY200 is an unfair class because of the harsh and group grading. The professor's expectations are unclear, and the grading doesn't reflect individual effort.

If I didn't have to take this course, trust me I would've dropped it when she told us to in the first week. This teacher is passive aggressive towards certain students especially after groups are assigned. She isn't very available outside of class either so if you want to talk about a grade or anything serious you'll never get the time of day.

One of the best teachers I've had ever. She is so passionate about her job and cares so much about all of her students. She is also so knowledgable and you can tell she's extremely versed in neuroscience and psychology in general. She's also so engaging! Took both bio psych and research. I love her!!! Wish I could take her again :D

Tolentino is my favorite professor I've had at SBCC. Her lectures are engaging, educational, and fun. I'd genuinely be sad if I couldn't make it to class. If she knows her students can reach a higher potential she gives them an appropriate grade & 3 DAYS to revise for a better grade. I liked her so much I am taking her class again this semester.

Do not be led astray by the negative reviews. Professor Tolentino has high standards precisely because she has confidence in your abilities. For assignments, she emphasizes learning as an iterative process and gives many opportunities for R&R (revising and resubmission). She is a passionate and warm person who appreciates / rewards genuine effort.

Overall, I thought this class and Professor T were average. I feel like most of our assignments were group-related work, so as long as you find a good group, you'll do fine in the course. Leida isn't the worst teacher, but she also wasn't the best. She drags her lectures and can be passive (especially when you ask questions), but is a fair grader.

She seems nice at first but is very passive-aggressive toward certain students. I think she wants her students to succeed, but is such a hard grader and will find any little thing to take points off for, which can get frustrating. There is definitely a big emphasis on group projects in her classes, along with discussions and weekly quizzes

Prof T makes a hard class doable. She takes stress out of the equation by giving you the ability to resubmit wrong answers. Her testing is untimed & open book- yet you're not going to do well in the class unless you put the effort in & learn. She also goes above & beyond to be there for students. Definitely one of the best profs at city college.

Her lectures are more like rambling sessions. But worst of all, she was unable to make a clear point at all. She told our group towards the end of the project that we were doing it wrong, but for weeks she said we were doing great. When asked what was wrong, she wasn't able to articulate it and said she 'gives up' trying to explain it.

This was the best Psych class I've taken at SBCC because Prof. T! She's easygoing, encouraging and listens to you if you have any issues. You can easily score well if you read the given material for that week! She gives precise feedback and there are group projects to do, so, communicate with your members!

Lots of group projects but if you want to be on your own, you have that option. She lets you choose what you would like to research about which is great! She also gives helpful feedback. Utilize her office hours and go to tutoring. It's a fun class but you do have to read the text and work for it. Could not recommend her enough.

She's a great professor! I took her during the summer and loved the class. It's reading-based and she doesn't have a lot of lectures so if you're an independent learner, this class is for you. Do the reading and follow her discussion policies and you'll be fine!

Surprised to see these negative reviews of Professor T. She truly wants you to learn. The lectures and assignments are highly engaging. Discussions require critical thinking but the topics are super interesting. Quizzes are easy, and as long as you are attentive in class the lab report is not too bad. She does "review/resubmit" which is awesome!

Fantastic professor and willing to help in any way if you reach out to her. There is a discussion due every week which requires a great deal of critical thinking. This is not an easy slack off class, if you're looking for a quick A. If you keep up with the material it's easy, and if you put in the work it's very rewarding.

DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE. I took summer 1 class with this professor and she is BY FAR the hardest professor you will ever have. She expects perfection on every assignment. I had a lot of extracurricular activities and jobs when I took her course but worked extremely hard to get the grade I deserved. No one can meet her level of perfection.

I took Professor Tolentino's Human Development class this summer, and it has helped me grow as a person and an intellectual thinker. Professor Tolentino makes you work hard for your grade, no doubt, but the lessons you take away from her insightful feedback are priceless. If your dedicated and willing to put in the work, you won't regret it!

Could've gotten an A in the class but because of some sh*tty group project members giving me a poor evaluation while I was already struggling with my mental health & work, she gave me a 77% & them a 91% even though I did almost the same amount of work as them give or take some spacing quotes, but she still didn't think I "deserved" any more points.

By far the worst professor I ever had, shes the reason why so many people give up on education. She has no sympathy for your personal life and thinks the world revolves around her class. I hope to god she gets fired one day.

I gave this my class my all but apparently nothing is "good enough" for this professor . Sometimes, I think she forgets that she teaches at a community college and not a UC cause she really has a reputation for being a tough grader. Just do yourself a favor and take a different professor.

she gives good feedback on papers but she's a HORRIBLE professor. She won't record any online lectures so good luck teaching everything yourself and yet she also has the audacity to be a tough grader LOL. Seriously the worst professor I ever had, I would give her a 0 if I could

Tolentino's Human Development course was well-structured. The discussions were thought-provoking. The exam was tough, papers were challenging, but Tolentino gives constructive feedback and is encouraging. A reasonable work load for this amount of credits; I would take one of Tolentino's classes again.
this professor is extremely inconsiderate, she doesn't deserve to teach the future of this country. to bad i can give her a 0.

I just got my grades, and I'm stoked. The whole semester, I was falling behind, and she kept hyping me up to stay motivated. At the end, she rounded everything up, and I finished with a B. I actually learned a lot too, which TBH wasn't even my goal.

She is an amazing teacher! Their passion for the subject is EXTREMELY noticeable, and they are always understanding and supportive. Plus, everything is well-organized, making it easy to learn and stay on track. I'm so thankful I took this class AND with the professor!

Dr. Tyner is a good instructor. Her summer History 101 class is fast-paced with a lot of work, but she offers extra credit and accepts late assignments with small penalties. The course leans left, but I shared conservative views without issues. Overall, an easy class and I learned a lot.
Such a sweetheart, she clearly cares about the material and her students. I would highly recommend taking a course from her.

Super kind and understanding, she wants her students to succeed and does everything in her power to do so. Attendance isn't mandatory but I would highly recommend attending her lectures. There are lots of assignments but they are super manageable and she is always willing to help.

Tyner is the best. I'm learning a lot. The course is hybrid, so attendance isn't technically required but she helps us complete the assignments for each week while we're in class, so it's good to sit there and plug the answers into Canvas IRL. She wants to hear students' opinions and stir class debate. It's not hard. Plus, she brings snacks.

Finished the 6-week course today with an A. The class is well-organized, and flexible enough if you'll be traveling all summer. If you tackle things once a week, it's not hard. The prof has interesting hw, so it's not just online trivia quizzes and boring "dead white guy" takes. I will take 102 next year and get HS American history over with.

This is the second online summer sesh I've had with Tyner and I got my history reqs outta the way now. The last part of 101 and 102 overlaps and she lets you reuse homework. She's smart, but doesn't flex. She's chill with late work and there's no tests. We had E-book quizzes and she explains current events, which was cool. Def 10/10 recommend.

I struggled with too many units last semester and so I am retaking the class to make up for dropping. Not gonna lie, summer school is a lot in 6 weeks. Mrs. Tyner has helped a lot, letting me use old hw and hacks for getting it done. She sends reminders when stuff is late and it's super motivating.

There's no final, and she drops the lowest assignment score, so that's a relief at the end of the semester. I learned a lot in this class. There's a lot of interesting details that she explains in videos and over Zoom every week. As long as you don't procastinate and cite everything from the slides, vids, and readings, it's not hard.

Ms. Tyner is the sweetest. At the end of the semester, she gave us cards to write inspo to our future selfs about what we learned about gender equality and how to stay positive and motivated. That was a year ago and I just got mine in the mail. It made me cry. In these crazy times, caring teachers like her really make a difference.

Fact-checking what the hater below said – attendance is optional. No tests or papers. only Discussion questions. The teacher is great and respects all ideas. Every single reading has a video with slides plus she gives us a sample A answer for everything. I'm ADHD and I love this class.

She is passionate about women's history and social justice issues. The course load is HEAVY, with about three times as much work each week as any of my other classes. The material is interesting, but there is just so much of it that it is hard to get through it all. I also get the sense that she is looking for a specific answer too narrowly.

I've never had an online class where the professor tries so hard to get to know the students. She's very hands-on and makes us really think about everything which makes me want to try harder. There's no tests or long papers, and she's chill about late work. So if you do your best, and use inline citations she will notice you and give you kudos.

I work full-time and am trying to get GEs done to transfer. Flexible class style makes it easy to watch video lectures, do online quizzes, use the slides, skim the reading and still get an A. She was nice about it when I told her I hate to read. Lol. Anyway, the units are interesting, and I am learning a lot.

Thanks for writing a letter of rec for me, Ms. Tyner. I feel very prepared to transfer now, and I was a little intimidated before your class. You gave me a lot of confidence in my writing. The encouragement and support while also setting high expectations was the perfect recipe for a student like me to motivate and love learning. Never change!

I love that the lack of tests left room for sincere participation and a more mellow class setup, which I like for a subject like this. Plus, her energy was great! Although I couldn't attend any lectures, I always enjoyed listening to the videos later. She is super passionate and makes a lot of jokes, which makes her the best prof I've had at CC.

I just got my grade, and I'm stoked. She rounded it up a lot! I was in the D zone and thohgt I might fail. She told me what things to do to pass and was nice about it. It's strict about citations, but the assignments are pretty cool. Overall, it wasn't hard. There's no final and no big papers. Mostly discussion boards and easy online quizzes.

I have appreciated this course so much! It should be mandatory for everyone to graduate. I'm so glad I became more educated on women's rights and American politics. I feel that a professor's passion for a subject completely dictates the attitude of a class, and Tyner's passion is contagious which made everything unforgettable. Take this class!

Best professor EVER! She gives amazing personal feedback on everything, which makes me never slack because I know she is reading every word. She's very passionate about women's history and the law and hypes up students for their wins. She makes it make sense, gives hope in these rough times. Plus, she brings snacks for us in class. Love her.

I'm shocked about what I've learned in this women's history class that no one ever taught me before. She goes over the readings in lecture so even if you don't read them, there's a video for everything. She is great at explaining complicated things.I can tell she cares about students. She's helped me a lot. Everyone should take this class.
Fairly easy class and it's generally lots of reading and some discussion/assignments. The way she grades is extremely clear and I learned a lot from this class it was very interesting.

Prof. Tyner is the GOAT. Her class is balance of being flexible and making the learning legit. She kept emailing when I split for a minute, and helped me not fail. Lectures are lol funny. She explains why everythings a mess but don't lose hope (vote!) and how civil rights movements work. I wish she taught bio and math too cuz that would be fire.

Professor Tyner is not only passionate about her course and her students. She's extremely clear on workload, gives a ton of extra credit, is relatable, it makes the class extremely interesting. I wish all of my professors were like her.

US women's history is the best online class ever. She explains everything, so it's gut. She's smart and good vibes funny. It's crazy she gives so much time to students hw and ideas. No other teacher does like this. I'm pumped for it. I feel lucky she gave me an add code bc the class was already full. This class should be required to transfer.

Grateful I took her class before transferring to an Ivy! Her honors course + teaching style prepared me for the rigorous road ahead. She is one of the few professors at SBCC that genuinely cares and is interested in her student's work. If you want to become a better writer and improve your historical analysis skills, while having fun, take her!

The format for the discussions is very good and really encourages smart conversation with factual proof with classmates. Something that gets overlooked these days is the ability to have a civil conversation with someone who has opposing views. I totally liked how she encouraged disagreement while reminding us to disagree civilly.

Thank you for a great US history course this summer. Your kindness and modern lectures made it fun. I liked the interactive quizzes and was grateful for no final exam. The midterm reflection was nice. I'm a better writer because you taught me about proof! Also thanks for rounding up the grades. I want to take your women's history course now!

I'm in HS and summer is brutal cuz it's so fast, but she got me a free tutor so I'm good. She allows late work and emails back fast. We use vids, podcasts, cartoons, online quizzes and debates so it's not just textbook reading. We can reuse papers and she gives nice comments. She showed me how to use AI without cheating. Has cool mom vibes.

- Free online textbook - She makes you use 100% effort on ALL assignments - Lots of extra credit opportunities, but they are hard. - She focuses a lot on plagiarism and AI, which I get, but she gets too obsessive on how EVERY single sentence I write must be cited, even on an obvious fact or even if I already had a works cited section. - Opinionated

she is always there to help especially with the labs as they can be pretty meticulous and time consuming. homework is mostly crash courses then a quiz which you can get 100% if you just follow along with the video. AI is a big no so watch for that

A lot of listening to unnecessary stuff. If you don't slack on the work, it's an easy A, but if you get lost kinda hard to make up. She can also get a bit upset about people not showing up, but showing up isn't mandatory. An interesting teacher, definitely a character.

Come to class, do the assignments and you're guaranteed an A. Sometimes the work can be tedious and she might make the class work homework if she looses track of time. But if you're someone who's awful with deadlines, she's very understanding if you talk to her ahead of time. Very easy going and sweet. Emails back in a timely manner.

Dr. V assigns assignments almost every day. The work is "busy work" for the most part and sometimes seems pointless. Attendance is mandatory, and every day there is group work. She somehow makes a fascinating subject like astronomy a bit dreadful. She is overall a very nice lady. Show up to class and do the assignments, and you'll be fine.

Dr. Van Der Veen was an amazing professor! I never leave reviews but she is so underrated I had to. If you follow deadlines and participate you are guaranteed a good grade. She doesn't assign much homework and is very lenient with you if you reach out ahead of time. My advice if you take this course is simply just to show up!
She was great and she is very open to giving extra credit
Professor is very fun and engaging. Likes to round up the overall grade to help her students. Lectures are always fun and interesting.

I loved taking Dr. V's astronomy class, you can tell she cares about her students a lot. The midterms and final are all online, open note, and you get two attempts which makes them a lot less stressful than a traditional exam. The homework was mainly watching videos and taking quizzes on them so that wasn't anything too difficult either.

Although I didn't take her regular ERTH101 class, the honors section was alright. Expect discussion posts every week and lectures that are kinda all over the place, but everything is doable. You will have a final presentation on the topic she teaches you about during the semester and it's actually pretty interesting! Be creative, it will help you.

Dr. Van der Veen is very passionate about what she teaches in this class, and the topic of lunar colonization was very interesting. There are homework assignments every week but it is very manageable as long as you pay attention and participate in class. Show her the final project before actually presenting in class to get pointers.

She defintely has a lot fo lecture slides but it shows her dedication, and group think questions and showing up to class is big part of ur grade. All online tests and quizzes weren't too hard and you can use notes and get two attempts. I think it was a little much fro it being an intro class but love her! she cares a lot!
Lots of quizzes and labs

Dr. Van der Veen's course is pretty intense and LOTS of reading in the lectures. Her class is very time-consuming and had an unnecessary amount of assignments each week. Her lab assignments are also very confusing due to the unclear instructions. Her 101 class did not feel like an entry-level course at all. She offers extra credit, however.

I enjoyed learning the material, there's just lots and LOTS of reading. Lots of labs as well, those take a lot of time so watch out for those. Assignments basically every day of the week and I wasn't able to complete a few due to other commitments but I managed to walk away with a C+.

DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS! - Professor doesn't know what INTRO to a subject means. Insane amount of work, assignments every day of the week. There are LABs that takes hours even if you're taking LAB separate. IT MAKES NO SENSE. Infinite boring slides. The professor isn't didactic at all. THE WORST. My GPA is 3.9 and I asked for a P/NP for this class.

Dont know how she is in person but online makes me want to cry. The workload is so heavy she has us doing assignments every day even weekends. The labs are so hard for no reason and she does not help if you email her about it. For an online class it is extremely confusing and hard to navigate. She also does not take criticism or feedback well.

So I took this class thinking astronomy would be cool and the professor seemed nice, she does not give grace whatsoever I have a condition that all of my teachers have been really nice about accommodating me. I got sick and missed a midterm and she wouldnt let me make it up. Also she gives you assignments every day of the week including breaks.

Dr Van Der Veen was always available to explain and help me understand the material. It's a bit heavy, but I took a 6 week course so it was a lot of work. The only reason I didn't give her 5 is because I was a bit upset at some of the content she used, some of which had some anti-religious sentiment. Fun class overall, caring teacher.

She stayed with me way past office to help me understand content for A DIFFERENT class (102) while I was taking her 101. She cares if her students understand and she reads EVERY post and gives detailed feedback. Never met a teacher who is as caring and thorough as hers, I am actually learning a lot. Sad its not my major cuz I would love to have he
Thought it was going to be a hard class but as long as you keep up with homework you should get an A. Nothing crazy quizzes and testa are really easy and she let us work with a partner on the final.

I want to try to like her but I can't. She makes lectures extremely boring and lacks the ability to explain things clearly. She publically shames you when she catches you using the phone, makes HW more complicated than it should be, gets triggered when you call her ms or mrs and makes you feel wronged when you assert your opinion.
Easy class to take.

I took this class last summer, and the work was definitely a bit more fast-paced due to it being only 6 weeks. But those 6 weeks were definitely memorable and impactful on my reading and writing. The Professor answers emails pretty quickly and gives very informative feedback. She is also very kind and clear about any clarification needed on work.

Professor Scamahorn was one of the nicest professors. She responds quickly to emails within a few hours. Her feedback on writing is great and very informative. Her class does make you read quite a bit, but I feel her book selections are quite relevant and possibly interesting to the student. Make sure to watch those videos she posts very helpful.
One of the best teachers I've ever had. She was very clear about assignments and also a bit lenient on due dates for big papers. Highly recommend.

Some people are saying that she assigns lots of papers but it is an English course so papers should be expected. I took ENG 110 in fall 2020 and I rly enjoyed this class! I had a few emergencies come up and she was completely understanding. She gives a clear rubric for each paper which is super helpful. & she gives plenty of time for each essay.

Apparently she does not really read all of work that you do. In an email she said that she glanced at my final and does not even have a rubric for how its graded. I take that as she did not even read my whole final and it can be graded on a number of factors no one would know. She is not very accommodating even during Covid-19.
If you want to waste you time and money take her, she is unclear and unfair she failed me for 1%. I wasted my time reading the novels so she would give me Ds and Fs. Please do not take this teacher.

She is a tough grader but a really good professor. She is so kind and she really wants her students to succeed. You have to really put effort into the class but you will really improve your writing skills by the end of the semester.

I took Vlcek-Scamahorn for a summer session and even though the class was more intense I really enjoyed having her as my teacher. She gave you clear instructions, had interesting discussions in class, gave you good feedback on your essays and was overall a caring teacher. If I could take her for English 111 as well I definitely would have!

She is the best English teacher Sbcc can have tbh. I feel that I've been improving my writing with her. In case you have any questions you can go to her office hours and she will gladly help you. Make sure to annotate the articles or books and you will easily get an A.

The absolute sweetest lady ever. Her class if super easy and she practically sets you up for success. I have missed multiple classes and left during and I still have a good grade. barely any work just read a bit and I only had 1 essay but easy quizzes every week.
Helpful
She was an amazing professor who cared about her students, she was very helpful and caring.
Reading alot

Not the funnest nor the hardest class. 2 novels, simple reading quizzes after each reading assignment. 3rd novel that you mainly use during class. Not a tough grader, but very specific on certain details. Essay on both of novels that you take home to read (midterm and final). Helped my english skills a lot, don't miss too many classes or any at all

You will be doing work for this teacher reading and assignments and quizzes! You can't slack off or else you'll fall behind quick! Good for serious students, you won't waste her time and she won't waste yours. Teaches great skills and talks about relevant topics so you won't snooze off. If you're a student who slacks off don't bother signing up.

Was given a D on a paper simply because I was missing one citation, and when I asked why I got that grade she "couldn't tell me." About half way through the semester, the classroom was empty every class day and time. No email saying they moved or whatever. And she never emailed me back after I would email her every week. Frustrating.
she is an amazing teacher! highly recommend her!

A good teacher, even I had a hard time to read 2 novels, prepare the quiz and finish long paper, I was confused if is was a writing class and not for reading. BUT, I learned more from the class. TAKE her class, only ENG 70, not ENG 80
she is a bad teacher, and she is really unkind, unclear teaching and no helpful.
unkind and less helpful
A great teacher, wish I could take her in all my English classes. Do your essays in time and you will have no problem passing this class.
My reading level wasn't good and now it's way better. Eileen is a great teacher and I strongly recommend to take classes she teaches!

She gave many useful comments on my essays. All of the essay topics were very interesting and socially significant. She offered plenty of time to work on the essays. There was one journal at the beginning of each class.
She really is a great teacher, super nice and I learnt a lot in her class. She gives prompt feedback on your essays and so much time to write them. The class is easy to pass. I would recommend her.
I really liked having her as a teacher, she is really nice and her class is fairly easy. I learned a lot with her.
I really felt that I connected with her. We not only talked about social issues but about books that make you think. The books were intersting and enjoyable to read, which made class easy

Even if her classes are very boring she is a nice teacher. There is almost no homework during the semester and it's pretty easy to pass this class. Don't buy the textbook I used it only once and it's expensive.8778
I love her. She's awesome and the topics picked for her class are super interesting. We always got out at least half hour early and maybe had homework twice over the semester. Definitely recommend

Obviously, whatever amount of Spanish you come into the class matters but she does a job meeting people's needs and whatever. A bit strict on the grading of some of the busywork but overall decent experience. Managed to pass despite the interruption of the pandemic.

Danya is hands down the best professor Ive taken at SBCC so far. She goes above and beyond to ensure her students success. She really cares about attendance, but I never wanted to skip bc of how enjoyable and fun her class was to go to. She made the material interesting and relatable to real life. I wish that I could have taken her for SPAN 2!

SPAN 101: DO THE HOMEWORK, modules seem overwhelming but they are actually really easy just tedious and time consuming. Stay on top of the module homework and the portfolio, participate in class she loves the effort. Tests are easy, class is chill. Two presentations, but both very easy. She is so kind and understanding, sweetest teacher ever.

Danya was great, really caring, wise and generally a good teacher. I learned a lot during her class. Not mandatory to go to class but you get points if you do. Tedious but easy homework assignments, but they really add up and affect your grade so do them! Gave extra credit opportunities but I never got to attend due to work.
She is a very nice professor! I start to miss her.
Danya is a good teacher, help every student to pass the class

Danya is a good teacher, and the textbook and her lecture really influenced me. Now I pay more attention on recycle and environment protection. She gives a lot of homework, but all these homework is useful. Nice teache
She is really great! I'm an international student and she explains everything very well. Be aware of group projects and get ready to read. She gives extra credit for your extra work.
I really like her teaching style I took more than one class with her this year.

Having been away from school for a number of years Ms. Wahlberg made coming back enjoyable. She is clear in her directions and goes above and beyond to help you succeed. I recommend her to anyone.She shows her passion for teaching in every subject that is discussed. Great Job!
Shes very nice & kind. Extremely helpful and clear. I loved her class and would take her again.

In the end I really did like Mrs Wahlberg and would recommend her to anyone. She's extremely nice and laid back, and fuullll of the most random facts. It's kinda weird actually. The thing I don't like is that it seems like she constantly tries to prove herself...
one of my favorite teachers!!

She was very helpful and encouraged everyone to pass! Very concerned with her students and knew us all by name. Her teaching style was somewhat high school by making sure all assignments are turned in. Very nice lady and will be more than willing to help you out!
He knows what he is talking about and is always willing to stay after lecture and help. Easy going and funny.
It seems he doesn't understand what he is talking about. The slides used in the lecture seem outdated because he cannot explain his own examples in his slides.

Really chill guy. Goes pretty in-depth about the material in his lectures. He knows his s**t. Experienced in the field. He's funny and his lectures are generally a pretty good time, although they're 2 hours long which can be a drag. Tests are open note. Assigns textbook reading which he supplements with his own knowledge in class. Has a nice beard.

Had Professor Zdanowicz for POLS131 online, and it was great! His recorded lectures were understandable and convey the course material very well. There are weekly open-note quizzes and two large exams---all open note and book---so take good notes during the video lectures. The two tests are weighted heavily, so respond carefully to each question.

Mr. Francois looks at all opposing cultures with hostility and prejudice, and it is very shocking to compare terrorist attacks to the Irish independence movement. He said that he would read the news for at least two hours a day, but unfortunately it did not broaden his mind. Reading Kissinger's world order is more useful than taking his class；）

The class as a whole had great content, and Professor Zdanowicz is very understanding. Much of your grade comes from answering questions he asks in class, so be prepared to speak a lot in lectures. Assignments are low-key, and he grades forgivingly-- each one mostly followed content from the slideshow presentations. His feedback is concise.

Oh, Global Studies 102! Prof. Zdanowicz is a gem! His lectures are so interesting and the course is a great survey of issues. His accent takes a little getting used to, but that is really no big deal. Totally encourages students' participation. Relaxed atmosphere, and not a difficult class, either. If you are at all on the fence, go for it.

Painful course, unpleasant instructor. Ziemba has a law degree, expects community college students to perform at academic level of law students. Workload rivals uni course, tons of work for 3 comm college units. Abrupt/rude professor. Dropped after 1 encounter. Consider Health Law online w/differnet college. Only instructor this course at SBCC.

Professor Wanda Ziemba is very understanding and kind. Monthly Zoom meetings were helpful, and she's always available for questions. She makes learning easier and I would definitely recommend her to other students.

Professor truly cares about her students' success. She's approachable, offers extra credit, and is always available for support. I appreciate her teaching style so much that I've enrolled in another class with her this summer. Exams are proctored.

Professor Ziemba was the most unorganized professor I've ever had. Her assignments are heavily outdated, most of them have no clear instructions, and she takes weeks to grade most assignments. If you have no choice but to take her class then I recommend you keep in touch with all of your classmates because she won't answer you in time if at all.

Professor Wanda Ziemba has been one of the most inflexible and unsympathetic professors I have come across. She has a strict no-makeup work policy, even in cases of emergencies beyond one's control. Be prepared to study a lot, as she has high expectations for her students to know the material. Based on my experience, I would not recommend her.

Professor Wanda is not the best. She does not respond to emails or to questions posted under the current module. I pointed out that there was a mistake on her instructions to one project and she has not responded to my emails since then. Her organization is terrible. I still have things not graded from 30 days ago. Beware if you take her class!

HATED this class. Incredibly hard to study effectively for. Be prepared to study A LOT via your own supplemental materials. Online text is a poorly edited information dump and riddled with errors. Lectures are professor reading slides to you. No study guide w firehose quantity of info. Quizzes/Exams include info never covered. Poor teaching.

There are not a ton of assignments in this class, but expects about 4 hours of reading per week. The quiz and assignment schedule is frustrating. We ended the semester with 2 quizzes and the final due on the same day. She has 2 large group projects, so you have to start on those right away. I did not enjoy this class and it was extremely hard.

Besides being a very interesting person, she is a fantastic instructor. She is constantly trying to improve the delivery method & is always available if you have questions or need help & guidance. She ends each session w/"be kind to yourself" & she means it. The key to success is to keep up w/the reading and watch her lectures. She is fair but hard

She gets so offensive when you point out mistakes she made. You have to read more than the average class for little points. It is very unorganized. If its not exact how she expects you won't ever get full credit even for 5 points. The discussions initial post are due a week prior to the actual due date so no one sees it in their to do list.

Dr. Ziemba is very involved with her online course. It is not one of those online courses that are set up to run themselves. Instead, she joins in on discussions and is interactive and communicative. No one grading category is heavily-weighted; this means if you put in the effort, you will do well. She is very knowledgable; use her as a resource!

Took this class online over the summer along with Pathophysiology, which was a huge mistake because of how much material there is to read and all the concepts to understand. Lectures were non-existent, so if you learn by watching/listening, be ready to find alternative methods on your own. Quizzes after every couple chapters, 1 midterm, 1 final.
I wish there was another professor available to take this class

Prof Ziemba was amazing! This was my first semester back in school after 30+ years and she was encouraging and helpful. At first, the course seemed hard as there was a lot of reading, but in the subsequent lessons you re-read a lot of the material so that you really understand and remember it. I loved her ROI assignments and crosswords!

Professor is caring when you reach out to her BUT only checks in to check the class every three weeks or so. Extra credit offered that DOES NOT count towards your grade. Papers are graded a month after submission. Gets defensive when you point out if something is wrong. She should be more involved or should give someone else chance to teach.

Study and dedicated work required. Prof wants everyone to learn and reads all forum entries and responds! First prof to do this. The class is difficult- the amount of memorization required was beyond me but I tried my hardest and came out OK. Yes there is a research paper. This is advanced level class. Prof is fair and caring so reach out if u need

This class by nature is v. difficult bec of the vol of info and level of detail. Summer classes are condensed so esp intensive -16 wks collapsed into 6 . Many students on nursing track. Need to work hard & show up to pass . Intensive study and research paper req. Prof understanding if you need extension but this class isnot for the faint hearted.

This class is way harder than it should be. I made the mistake of taking this class during the summer where the entire curriculum is condensed into 6 weeks. There was way too much reading and the quizzes asked highly specific questions. The professor doesn't seem to understand/care that the students have other obligations besides this class.

Very unhelpful; not much direction given for strange case study quizzes, crazy amt of reading weekly, discussion posts need way too much time spent for few measly points. If you MUST ask a q, do it individually and not in a discussion/question forum - she will humiliate you on the forum to peers and not answer the question. Glad this class is over.

She is clear and helpful. The projects are easy. Just print out the articles and read it carefully. The tests are a little harder. The forums are super easy and fun way to make these extra points. Of course, you have to study. There is a lot of reading assigned. I wasn't on top of my readings and I am still getting a B. So don't worry. Not so bad.

ABSOLUTELY DESPISED THIS CLASS. 20 Q's in 20 min for quizzes , nearly impossible. Forums are Q&A style, meaning you can't see what anyone writes until after you post, wtf does it matter? Does not give an example of an A+ paper for the research paper bc "every paper is different." Again, wtf? Stay clear of this teacher or prepare to do work.

I love this Professor. She responds quickly to all messages and she really reads everything on forums and emails. Post first before you can see others' answers. You will learn a lot from her. Tests are hard. It was not an easy "A" but you can do it. I had fun working on the extra credit and project assignments. This Professor deserves an "A."

Be prepare to work, only teacher you can choose. You're required to participate in forum discussions. Lots of reading! Be prepared to take quiz every two weeks with, "projects" which are quizes.. No, final or midterm. Tests are very tricky. There was a ridiculously tough extra credit project worth as much as a test.

The main bad part was the professor. She wasn't very understanding that I needed an A to be able to transfer and accused me of cheating on my final. Don't take this course with this professor unless you want to be harshly graded or accused for no reason.

This professor after initially passing my final exam, the professor reopened it a month later and failed me, accusing me of academic dishonesty without providing any evidence. The matter was escalated to the dean, who never contacted me for my side of the story. No concerns about grading and accusations were not addressed directly.

The whole class is self taught, there are only dsicussion posts each week and no study guide for the exams so you have no idea what the long answer questions will be, the only thing you can study is past discussions, prof Andersons class feels lazy and seems like she wants students to struggle so she doesn't have to do her job and actually teach.

Her class is really low quality, I've taken many psych and soc classes with different professors and Anderson is by far the worst I've encountered. She couldn't care less about her students, she reuses old lectures. She had a random person, not even a professor, grade our midterm papers!! I could write so much more...insanity.

Terri Anderson seems lazy and it feels like she hates what she does, she does not care to help you get better, really inaccessible through email or Canvas. Doesn't have a good grading system, it's an easy class but it's designed so you struggle. I have a 4.0 and this class is threatening that for literally no reason, she just doesn't care.

Take literally any other class, the midterm and final essay are worth 20% of your grade each, so even if you ace every other assignment you can only really afford to lose a couple points on the essays. Also she doesn't offer extra credit, she is not the most caring professor. She will often ignore comments/emails and refer you to office hours. 3/10

Worst teacher I've taken at SBCC. I am in the honors program, so I can confidently say that I am capable of getting an A but I am barely scraping by. She is not understanding at all. Offers no extra credit. Quizzes are 4 questions, worth two points, 5 minute limit. So 1 missed question = 75%. Quizzes are 30% of your grade. Lazy email responses.

Terri Anderson was by far the least understanding professor I have ever had. She does not seem to care about her students. I am an above average student but struggled to barely receive an A-. I would not recommend her class at all.
She is very specific on what she is looking for, not to helpful with questions always refers you to her open zoom hours online

I do not know why Professor Anderson's ratings are so low. I didn't think that the workload was any heavier than the average class and she never striked me as being rude/uncaring. She has very clear grading criteria and the only assignment each week was a discussion post + replies. We had 2 essays throughout the semester & watched 2 movies!

Professor Anderson is very difficult to communicate with and is not understanding in the slightest. HW can take hours, and she doesn't seem to care about any personal issues that could be affecting your school work. I would not recommend at all, as she is very rude and unprofessional when it comes to emailing her about a concern that you may have.

This is a pass/no pass 8 week course. It was really easy & straight forward. Whenever I had a question, the professor replied to my messages within the hour. She provides plenty of resources & I didn't struggle at all. No tests, but there were 2 quizzes that have unlimited time/unlimited attempts.
Super easy class! Great Professor.

I had to take this class for credit. Its a 1 unit class but for the amount of work I put in I felt it should have been worth more. If you watch the videos and read its super easy. Corrie is very caring and willing to work with students. Shes very responsive to email. This class is not that bad you just have to stay on top of your work.

Absolutely terrible teacher!!! She is the most ridiculous grader and seems to forget it is only a freaking library class. Grades extremely hard no matter how much effort is put in. I didn't pass for no reason and failed my final even though I followed instructions exactly. WAY too much work for a 1 credit class!!! DO NOT TAKE
Watch out, has no consideration for student maximum effort or extenuating circumstances.

His weekly slide videos often go off topic and discriminate against community college students. His essay requirements were often vague, and he wouldn't understand proper MLA citations. Also, he would nearly never give higher than a B for the essays and would not grade them until they were under his unclear guidelines. Do not take his class!

First time rating a professor. He's terrible, At first I thought he was chill but then he started sending me emails for every assignment I submitted accusing me of AI. He does not have this rule on the syllabus but basically he gives you a zero until you re submit the assignment the way he wants it.

Gives unclear feedback, doesn't know his own assignments. Gave a bunch of people bad grades for not quoting more, assignment said only one was necessary. Lectures rambling and unhelpful, use outdated txt. Paranoid about ai, I never used but accuses everyone of it. Rude in emails. I'm usually an A student but withdrawing so he doesn't ruin my GPA.
Ridiculously harsh on grading and gives unclear feedback. Super particular in what he wants.

A ridiculously harsh grader. Wants you to take hours out of your day to watch endless lectures of him yapping. So particular you forget everything he talks about, and spend most of your time not writing, or even thinking for crying out loud, but making sure you got every single one of his little nitpicking requirements. DONT TAKE

He makes many video lectures that don't feel necessary to watch. The ones that are good to watch he goes off topic sometimes and the videos don't always explain things that often. He's very particular about how essays are written with MLA formatting and will take points off if incorrect. He is very good with essay feedback and replying to emails.

I have never written a review on any teacher before having this mf. This guys is simply the worst. He is not only a hard grader, but genuinely he is a mean person. Every essay that I had turned in he sent me an email back saying I need to fix it, yet not telling me how. He is rude over email and has a power trip, do not take a class with him.

He gives you 2 extensions for the entire semester, and won't budge even when a sick note is sent to him. Even if the assignment is only an hour late, you're getting a zero. Why take a class taught by a difficult person like him when you could just get a nicer, easier teacher?
He has no remorse for anything if you have a valid excuse for missing any assignment or class. Also teaches very confusingly and is never clear on directions

He is very rude and discouraging. He went on for 40 minutes of his pre-recorded lectures talking about how no one watches them and that it shows with the disappointing essay's he receives. I can't blame my grade entirely on him, however, all I can say is that I'm taking Eng-110 again and now I have an A. Having a good teacher changes everything!

While he does sometimes talk off-topic, it is never about completely unrelated things. If you listen, and take the time to do everything that he tells you to do, the class is easy, and that itself is probably the hardest part. Overall, I feel the other reviews are overly harsh and I would definitely take this class again. Caring and ethical teacher
The most boring calss I've taken in my life. I've caught myself falling asleep countless times. He absolutely forbids to use computers even for note-taking.
Professor James Brausch seems to have absorbed the previous evaluation of his students, and now it is much easier. He gives a lot of A's on the grade.

Although his classes are tough, it is possible to get an A. Go to his office hours, ask for a lot of feedbacks. He may be bad at teaching but he is not a bad guy. If he sees that you are genuinely putting in the effort and, of course, continuously improving the quality of your essays, you will get an A.
Lot's of homework, boring class

This guys is a really bad teacher and bad person. Rude , unpolite and unorganized too. You will be lost throughout his course and his syllabus it's worthless since is so confusing and poorly made. He doesn't care about teaching to his students which becomes obvious since his teaching style is him rambling about his life

This guy was absolutely terrible. Marks 10 points off your grade on each paper if you have only one or two lines short and very strict on electronic policy (marks you absent if you check your phone or laptop any time during class, even if everyone is packing up or turning in one of HIS assignments online). I hate this teacher, don't take him bro.

James is a good professor if you are willing to put in the work. He may go off topic sometimes but only when he believes the topic is relevant to what we are either writing or reading about. He provides some feedback on your papers and if you need more you can find him during his office hours. He is clear about what to include in your essays.

Even though I think James gives great feedback on papers and is a pretty funny guy theres simply to much to do and he doesnt understand that college students have multiple classes to work with. He also goes of topic a LOT and classes are rarely helpful.
He is a really tough grader, his classes are not helpful on anyway. His assignments are really hard to understand and you end up having to find out what to do by yourself.
It is not even worthy to go to class because his lectures sucks. Although you won't pass if you don't attend. Rude and tough grader. Don't take him.
Instead of letting us work on the infinite papers he assigns us, he makes us listen to him while he talks about politics. Calls people who don't agree with him stupid and honestly grades unfairly.
Tough Grader, Does not teach anything in class, Really strict, Does not post your grades, Does not give feedbacks to your papers

I was very interested in this class and the material it contained; I was not a fan of the professor. She did not teach us, its you teaching yourself completely. You are drowned in lecture videos, hw, labs, and quizzes/midterms. Her midterms are difficult because of all the chapters and info she packs into them. Would not recommend this class.

Absolutely adore Professor Bretz! Personally, I found the lectures and workload to be very reasonable. It usually took me around 2-3 days of working 1-3 hours to complete the lectures, homework, and lab. The midterms were a bit difficult but she is very understanding and curved it every single time.
i was not a fan 0/10

I struggled w/this class. U teach urself &the lectures are prerecorded&confusing. There were 4 group discussions& 4exams in total(no study guides). 2exams had essay questions,the rest is multiple choice.The reading/homework assignments were a bit heavy& not enough time to learn the material. There are office hours but I didn't have time for them

I took Physics online before, but didn't really understand it until her class (phys105). Ik ppl taking this @UCSB, and their prof was absolutely horrendous, to the point where I was tutoring(ridiculous, but shows I was taught well), so I think physics is hard anywhere. Wished all q's are short answer for partial credit, but I enjoyed her teaching!
She gives a lot of notes and homework. A lot of quizzes, she using proctorio. She isn't understanding. I took it for spring semester and it felt like a summer semester. Her lab class was easy.

I get that online teaching is hard but for a PHYSICS class, she expects us to teach ourselves, the class is literally just reading 3 chapters in the book a week then doing homework for them which is like 20 questions each. There are 3-4 midterms and then the final which is CUMULATIVE and you get no study guide, no cheat sheet, and it used proctorio

I do not have complaints about Dr. Bullock, but he does tend to make mistakes when teaching formulas or concepts. He is a good guy, but I've taken other Chemistry professors at SBCC and i felt as if they did more of a complete job as CHEM teachers. I did enjoy his demos he would present in the beginning of his lectures. Exams are not like the final

He's a nice guy and definitely knows chemistry but his lectures don't teach you all that you need to know for the exams and if you want to do good in his class there's a lot more to learn from the actual textbook. His exams are graded very harshly so be extra prepared even if you think you know the material.

The other reviews are a little harsh, or maybe he just changed the tests, but they're almost exact like the study guides now. The material is hard and was difficult for me to understand from his lectures but he does offer office hours and tutoring. Test averages were pretty low (70s) every time.

Dr. Bullock made sense and actually based his test questions off the material we learned in class. Attendance is super necessary because he has in-class exercises almost every day. Overall, it was very clear how to get an A in the class (which is more than I can say for some UCSB chem classes). Thank you Dr. Bullock!

The final review was nothing like the actual final. Doesn't prepare you at all for ANYTHING. Take the class somewhere else if you have the chance, he gets mad when you ask him question and complains that its your fault for not understanding his weird teaching methods.

Worse professor ive ever taken. Should've listen to the reviews and took the other professor instead. Exams are nothing like his reviews, and he lacks on grading assignments so you have no clue what your grade is

Dr. Bullock is one of my favorite professors. He is kind, understanding, and readily available during office hours. He is strict but reasonable, with clear expectations. CHEM155 is a dense class, but he has made it an enjoyable experience!

He sent out a mass email saying the its not his fault that many students are failing the class. If literally everyone is failing your class, it IS your fault. Exams are your grade, sometimes the PAL turtors can't even understand him

Worse professor ive taken so far. I recommend taking the class over the summer to avoid him teaching it. Exam reviews are nothing like the actual exams. Good trying to ask for any help, he'll get offended you didn't understand the first time

Very kind and thoughtful individual, this class is good for independent learners and the resources given by him are amazing, that being said the lectures and course content feel near impossible for many students, he goes very fast and i find myself having a hard time following no matter how much attention i pay due to lecture style.

Constantly messed up on his lectures, practice exams, really anything, and would get offended when you would ask questions or for help. What you get on your test is what your grade will be but good luck because he doesn't know how to teach.

For me, his lectures weren't very interesting and were often confusing. He has a lot of experience in chemistry but maybe forgets to consider the student's perspective. I thought the HW and labs were fairly easy points and exams were similar to his practice exams, but I know I spent more time studying than most and average exam scores were ~65%.

He messes up on practice exams and lectures very often where students are the ones correcting him. He also has you do problems in an odd way, where sometimes tutors can't understand what he is trying to do. Take anyone else, even if it means you'll have a bad class schedule it'll be better then taking his class.

Bullock knows the material and will give useful answers when the questions pertain to the class material. He struggles with communicating topics, enthusiasm, and lecture mistakes, but gives you what you need to get an A on the test if you pay attention + ask questions. weekly HW and biweekly prelecture and prelab tedious but easy.

He is not a good lecturer nor a decent one and his lecture slides are a mess. The textbook, exercises, and hw were the things I used to learn and prep for the exams. His communication skills are poor as well. Doesnt realize that he gets defensive a lot (i.e. exams scores are low because students aren't trying enough, not because of my lectures).

Decent professor, but the online lectures were not engaging and he didn't seem to like teaching very much over zoom. The class was a bit boring and repetitive, and I never felt like I truly grasped each concept, but I could have tried harder. Not the worst experience of my life, but not a class that I would recommend if there was another prof.

Not a very good teacher, if possible take Tro's classes. He doesn't respond to emails, doesn't listen to student concerns or input, and doesn't seem to enjoy teaching very much. Granted, this was an online class and he was vocal about hating online school, so i'm sure this year's circumstances weren't helpful. All in all, not AWFUL but beware.

Prof. Bullock is a nice guy but this is one of the hardest classes I have taken. The exams are nothing like the homework or the practice quizes. I got a 98% on the homework, 97% on the quizes and a 70% on the exams yet I still ended with a B-. Your grade is basically determined on how you do on the exams. I do not recommend taking this class!!!!!

His class is passable but it's really not worth it. He's rude if he responds to email and questions at all. His tests are nothing like the homework or practice tests. Required lecture attendance with less than a week warning and with only 2 lecture weeks left. Doesn't curve tests. I cannot stress this enough, dont take his class if you can avoid it

Would I have rather taken Tro? Yes! Is he as horrible as everyone says? I don't think so. He wants you to understand the material in-depth and not only learn things by heart. Go to his office hours and try to get the things you don't understand explained from him. He is honestly a nice guy. Use the book! It explains almost everything you need.

Just don't take this class. His tests are nothing like the practice he gives, he never responds to emails (an I mean I only got one response to the 7 I have sent over the corse), and everything I learned was from the textbook. Do yourself a favor and if you want a good GPA do not take this class.

He almost never responds to emails, and if he does it's always a very rude, unhelpful response like "look at syllabus or announcements." He never actually answers your question. His lectures are extremely boring and difficult to sit through. If you go to office hour to ask questions he often talks over you & wont let you finish your question

He just reads over slides for a lecture and responds super rudely, if he responds, when asked questions. It really seems like he doesn't like his job. If you have him, the most important thing was the PAL sessions, they care and are good teachers- at least better than him. If you get him, you can get an A, but you'l feel alone and stressed.

Eric has a bit of a bad rep but is a good teacher if you get to know his style. I recommend going to his office hours and PAL tutors if you want to get a good grade because inky going to the lectures most likely wont be enough.
I learned more from reading the textbook than his lectures.

I have had Professor Bullock in person before the pandemic for 155, and now distanced learning for 156. He tries hard, but his lectures are not helpful and most of the learning must be done on your own. He gets frazzled easily, is not very patient, and it makes it difficult to learn the material. If you have the option to take another prof, do it.

Bullock is a good person, but his lectures are just not engaging. He also goes really fast so you need to write quickly. Hw is rare but when there is, it's really hard, so review your notes and use the textbook/resources. Switched to online due to COVID, and he had a lot of trouble with the tech. I would rather not take him again.

Bullock doesnt care for his students. Super unorganized lectures & slow on grading. Would advise to not take him if you can avoid. He never emails students back, and honestly even my lab instructor find it difficult to communicate with him. Read the text book and go to the tutors, those are the only things that taught me during the semester.

I thought Dr. Bullock was a nice guy but not a great teacher. He is very disorganized plus his exams have nothing to do with his lectures. Take this class if you absolutely have to but dont expect youll do good. I recommend going to the tutors and finding time to read the book and you'll probably have a good chance of passing.

Dr. Bullock is the best definitely take him over Sally. Hes organized&the textbook he uses is the best, straight to the point. Go to lectures, take notes and study them for tests(recommend reading the chapters as assigned also). He lets you know all you need and does very similar problems you just gotta practice and study them. Juan for lab��

One quiz a week online and you get two attempts. Very rarely has homework and if he does it's usually extra credit. 3 exams and the final only covers the last units not the whole semester. He usually adds extra credit to the tests. Exams are hard but if you pay attention and use the textbook it's an easy A. Super nice caring guy!!!

I absolutely loved this class. I felt it was very little demand but understood the information at all times. Each class period was a lecture with very little tests and no homework. One 10 question quiz a week with two attempts. Lots of extra credit offered. He cares! Class was the best psychology class I could've imagined. 100% take this class.

Dr. Stan Bursten's class is pretty chill and doesn't require much effort. He spends most of class time reading off a powerpoint with an occasional group activity. He gives 3 non cumulative exams which are challenging, but only worth 25 points each. Do the extra credit and you'll be fine. He's funny, easy to talk to, and cares about his students.

Professor Bursten makes the material engaging. The lectures are interesting, but the tests are challenging. Using unstuckstudy and the free textbook helped me stay on top of the content and do well in the class.
Professor Bürsten is so sweet and caring!! Just one quiz every week on canvas and only 2 test and the final is just like a regular test. Easy A

Prof. Bursten is a great guy, he's passionate about psychology and his assignments help you learn via situations and juxtapositions of the past vs todays world. As long as you do your work in this class you'll do great.

Professor Bursten is a great professor and tries to make the material interesting. He is kind and funny and wants to help his students succeed. It is vital to read the textbook and study the lectures to prepare for the tests because I found them pretty difficult. Also, make sure to follow the study guides on Canvas.

I enjoyed Stan Bursten's lectures. They were really interesting, the first two-three weeks were a little slow but after that, it got really interesting. He knows a lot about his subject and is enthusiastic about his subject. There is not a lot of work, only a quiz a week, and three exams during the semester.

You're going to need to put in more effort than on the rubric to get full points. It was somewhat difficult and time-consuming, but not impossible. The textbook is free so that's good I guess. He's old, so I'm not going to complain. Also to survive this class don't leave things at the last minute. Some of the material is actually interesting.
I enjoyed my time in professor Bursten's class, his lectures were easy to understand and interesting. He was very enthusiastic and kind and there was not a lot of work assigned in his class.

He finds mistakes where there are not mistakes, so you better check your own work to see if he is right. He includes info from future classes on the quizzes and exams. The class is definitely very interesting but he makes it very difficult and stressful because he acts like a mistakes hunter. If on ch.1 read also ch.2 he loves surprises.

Burnstein is very smart. However, he is not a good teacher. He speeds through lessons. Before our class started he sent out an email that suggesting we drop our Thursday lecture. He holds students to very high standards while sending out announcements with typos. His grading is not clear. I will say that he gives a ton of extra credit.

The tests are easy, the videos are really short, but discussion posts are where it matters. He doesn't grade easy, but he will allow you to send him a rough draft so he can point you in the right direction before you post for grading. His speciality is animals so we only learned about actual human psychology in the last 4 weeks. Disappointing class
Overall this professor definitely expects for you togo above and beyond the requirements for assignments. if you don't you will not pass.

Mr Burtsen overall was a good profesor. The only stuff that were difficult was the discussions and he was a tough grader on those. You need to put a lot of effort in those and research about the topic. If you did write something that doesnt make sense he will deduct points. Another thing he gives extra credit too which helps boost your grade!!!!!

If youre taking this class online during the pandemic its super easy if all you want to do is pass. If you want to actually learn you could probably do that too theres a lot of info just it isnt necessary to do work in order to pass

He's knowledgeable about the topics, although most of the work needs the textbook. Discussions were easy and the grading criteria are clear, you just need to do your research and answer the prompts correctly. Be detailed! This class was a breeze for me, although others may think otherwise. You just need to put in the work and reading into it.

Because he wants us to really learn the material and think critically, I find his discussion questions and papers most challenging. You have to use external resources and do research because the textbook will not be sufficient. He does care about your learning so go to his office hours. I find him very nitpicky and strict with grading.

This man has no compassion for students especially during the pandemic. Do not take his class. I am taking BASIC psychology and yet he seems to make it more complicated than it is and expects perfection to a T from everyone. His directions compared to how strict he is with everything does not add. Was rude over email.

Professor Bursten's online class wasn't bad at all! I'm not the smartest in psych, but I definitely feel like he was a fair grader and gave opportunities for extra credit too. All in all, put in the effort and you'll be fine in his class.

Extremely specific and nitpicking grader. No compassion for personal issues, very dry and black and white. Textbook does not have nearly enough detail to answer his discussions, use the internet to teach yourself the material. Doesn't except late work. USE QUIZLET for quizzes, midterms, final. Discussion answers are most important.

This course was a challenge but resources provided by Professor Bursten helped with this along the way. He offers extra credit. Lots of reading to understand the material in preparation for weekly the discussion posts and quizzes and the midterm and final.
Would not take him again or recommend anyone else to. Had a 69.25% and he refused to raise it to a 70% even during a pandemic.

this class will make you work for your grade. the quizzes are online and super easy, but the tests do not compare to the online quizzes. there have been maybe 2 instances where the quiz questions transfer over onto the exams, but for the most part, study the study guide and read the chapter on your own and practically teach yourself the material.
Good Professor... will make you work for an A but he makes it well worth it. Study the study guide and show up to class and you will have a good time.
Stanley is a wonderful professor who has a real passion for psychology and biology. If you attend each lecture, take notes, and keep up with the online quizzes, you'll almost certainly get an A.

Dr. Bursten was an excellent professor and I'd highly recommend the class. That is, if you're a good student who's willing to put in the very little effort required for getting an A in psych 100. I was in a large lecture and a lot of kids complained about him, but they were just that... kids.

Stanley is a very polite profesor. His quizzes are online but exams in class. The exams are very difficult and he did warn my class about that at the beginning of the semester. There is a couple of group assignments throughout the semester.
