He is a really nice guy just stay on track with the work like any other class. It's not an incredibly large amount of work but you do have to earn your grade. He gives a study guide for each exam. There are 3 exams and a final. All of which are based off of the study guides AND the lectures.

I reached put to him to see what I could do to improve my grade was willing to do what I could, and he was not very understanding or willing to give me anything to get a few extra points. Do not seem caring at all.

Very passionate & enthusiastic teaching such a technical subject! It was super cool and refreshing how he focused on the importance of the individuals creativity while teaching the basic techniques. Each project is different from the last so you learn alot. Attendance is key, and he gives great, constructive feedback and is always willing to help.

Osiris gives great insight into the film industry and what it takes to be a successful film maker. He also puts a lot of effort into his teaching, he has one of the best canvas set ups that I have encountered, and everyday in class he brought with him positive energy which helped motivate the class, and make the students excited to learn.

Osiris is a very inspirational teacher who really gives his all to his students. He is very helpful and accessible outside of class. If you have any question or issue he will always be there for feedback about your projects or anything else for that matter. He is very passionate about his work and everything that he is doing.

Absolutely loved this guy. Not only teaches you everything you need to know, but also learns you to achieve the mindset to make it. Cares about every single student in the classroom and helps individually. The lectures were inspirational and interesting. Went to this class with a smile each day. This teacher made my semester.

Osiris is not just a good film teacher but a good teacher all around. He will teach you how to respect people no matter their gender, skin color etc. And if you show respect he will respect you back. I put the difficulty in the middle because he will teach you to get out of your comfort zone and put you in the forefront, which is needed in film.
Cares about his students, is willing to work with students outside of class and is also always willing to help. He sees the good in every student and is patient, as well as uplifting!

Loved this class! I learned the techniques of editing and Osiris even taught some basics in photoshop. This class made me a better editor and understand the art of storytelling. He cares about his students and will take the extra mile to make you successful, like offering class time on Fridays. He is always there to help & happy to answer questions

I give Professor Osiris Castaneda five E's (like 5 Stars) Eclectic.... Electric... Enthusiastic... Engaged... Entertaining Prof. Osiris gives enthusiasm, dedication and his eclectic knowledge to his class and all you have to do is show u, do the work, "Be Present" and you will learn from a documentary PRO. One of my favorite classes!

Osiris is a great guy, but very liberal. He raises a lot of his political views during class, and you should prepare to do a lot of meditation. He takes a lot of class time to explain simple things, which might make it hard to work during class. He gives nothing lower than B's as long as you hand in all of the projects. Not strict about deadlines.

Without a doubt the best teacher Ive ever had! He is incredibly passionate about film and you can truly see that he loves what hes doing. Hes always helping students with their work and he challenges you whilst at the same time inspiring you. If you have a passion for film, Osiris is definitely the right man for the job. Overall incredible class

Osiris is a wild man and there's a good chance you will think he is over the top loud and energetic but he truly cares about his students. If you have any interest in film or media you have to take this class because he will not only teach you how to be a filmmaker you will learn how to tell stories and what it takes to create inspiring content!

I HIGHLY recommend both courses that Osiris teaches. He is a talented instructor and takes the time during class to check in with each student about the assignment and will always help you improve and hone your skills. He always makes himself available 4 U and even seeks out opportunities for his students. Take his classes. You will not regret it!

Osiris taught me everything important about being a film maker. He may have a method of teaching that is different from most professors. But Osiris is a wealth of knowledge, he embraces the technical aspects of film and offers his knowledge to the benifit of his students who try. But he also emphasizes the morality aspect of being a filmmaker.

If youre looking to throughly learn about storytelling, getting real inspired and developing a great idea for a documentary film while having fun and connecting to a class, Osiris is the best choice you can have! Besides all that fundamental and extremely important part, you will learn and practice about sound, camera, lighting and editing!

Very inspirational professor. Really cares about the world-and YOU. On the other hand- you're sure you'll get and A or B in this class. Doesn't require a lot of work. He rates you and your heart more that your skills. Probably 99 % loves it, but I would rather more actual knowledge- classes are not cheap and I need to learn this stuff somehow.

Osiris is one of the best teachers I had ever had. He cares so deeply about his work, his influence, and his students. He's always there to talk. He only grades your assignments at the end of the semester, so you have the ability to redo them before then once you learn Premiere Pro better. He is amazing and so supportive in all that we do.

Cons: Not great with giving clear deadlines. This made time-management hard, and work felt like it piled up at the end. I recommend creating your own deadlines ahead of time. He also talks too loud. Pros: SUPER helpful if you ask for it. Will be honest and help you take your work to the next level. Invests time in his students. Very inspiring!

Osiris was one of the best professors I've had at SBCC. He is super liberal which can be a bit off-putting if you don't share his values but regardless he only cares about the well being of his students. There isn't much he grades on other than the final documentary you need to turn in at the end of the semester but he does a great job teaching doc

I love Osiris. He is such a caring, good professor. I didn't show up to class that much, as long as you turn in your assignemnts on time, you will get a good grade. He is super chill and willing to help all the time. He will help you in any way for you to get an A in the class. Def take him!

One of the best professors I have had. He rekindled my love for film-making. He cares for every one of his students and will make sure that they can succeed in their own ways. The class has a very different and lenient style of learning than other film classes, but it's a nice break from the harsh industry-based classes you have to take.

Awesome class! Perfect intro to editing. You learn final cut, premiere, and a little photoshop. Not so technical that it's overwhelming for beginners, but enough to do amazing editing. Osiris is respectful and patient with students who fall behind. Also, you learn to edit different types of material. Osiris makes editing fun, which is priceless!

Osiris does a great job with this class. He teaches the fundamental skills to make documentaries, then shows how we can take those skills out into the field and make things happen. Some students think he's too laid back, but he creates a very comfortable environment to kick ideas around before execution. He inspires students in a way others don't.
He will let students do meditation in class, he will dance in class with jazz, he will sage you, lol. The coolest teacher. But I hope he could teach us more...
To someone how doesn't care about learning anything this class is perfect. You don't even have to show up.. and you'll pass.

Osiris is Rad, I had him for FP 114, the editing class, osiris is one of the chillest teachers you can have. Though sometimes he goes through lectures fast, and you will have to tell him to slow down. class itself is easy if you pay attention and know how to edit in premiere prior to his class, even if you dont know how to edit he goes over basics.

Osiris is the best professor i have ever had. He really takes his time with each student to make sure they grasp the concepts. I have taken the skills i learned in his class to get jobs in the film industry. Overall This Is a MUST take class with Osiris!

Really outgoing and positive person, will always try to help you out and treats you with respect. Some of the assignments can appear vague I guess, but if you just ask he will help you out. Great person and teacher!

Osiris really cares about his students and will seriously bend over backwards to try to help and accommodate them, which is kind of amazing. He's maybe a bit scattered with his curriculum, but definitely makes up for it in the time he puts in individually with each student. Hard not to love his positive attitude and passion.

If you're into documentary film making you're going to love Castaneda. He is so passionate about making docs and it inspired me a lot. Super helpfull and kind. He wants you to succeed. Something bad about him? Because he's so passionate he can sometimes come up with ideas for projects that are difficult to pull through.

Great class. There wasn't a textbook, and there was a good mix of video lectures and readings. His lectures were good! Assignments were fun and his feedback was quite detailed and specific. A really nice guy!
he was so fine and his lectures were interesting :b

the class is very interesting if you care about art to any capacity. it was easy to understand and i do feel like i learned a lot. there were two exams (midterm and final) and the questions are exactly what was talked about in class. if you take notes you will have no problem. professor cummings is understanding, smart, and passionate. 10/10

Super chill dude. Gives good feedback and wants everyone to get a good grade and do well. Attendance is important and will impact your grade but if you show up and participate you will do really well. 10/10 would take again.

His lectures were interesting and engaging. There were very few homework assignments it was more about in-class assignments, which, as long as you show up are easy to complete. He's really nice and understanding. I would definitely recommend taking this class, and I learned a good amount.

Initially coming into this class I was thinking Art History was going to be boring. But professor Cummings made it really interesting with his lectures. He really engages with his students to the best of his ability. Really laid back, as long as you pay attention and do the hand written assignments you'll pass with ease.
Super great professor, really went in-depth about the course content. Fun assignments, interesting class!

Mr. Cummings is by far my favorite teacher this semester. He was super understanding of absences and always found a way for me to make up missed assignments. He's super relatable and you can tell he loves his job and cares about his students.
He is so fine

Really good lecturer! Lectures can go for a long time on one subject, but all of the subjects focused on in class were interesting and thought provoking, notes are important for this class and concepts are easily communicated through them. As long as you attend and are involved with the conversation, you are sure to do well.
He had interesting
Really good teacher with great lectures!

This class is great! Attending lectures is important for understanding the material. There's a short handwritten response at the end of each lecture, and the exams are also handwritten. The instructor is laid back, offers helpful advice for transferring to a different school, and was a former student at SBCC who transferred to UC Berkeley.

Highly recommend this class! Attendance is mandatory, but then there's very little to no homework, so as long as you show up and are present you will pass. Very low stress course and Mason is super chill and approachable, also very accommodating and there to help. The overall low stress experience made me personally absorb the info much deeper.
Very nice and smart :)

Professor Cummings will be a memorable teacher who made class enjoyable to learn and truly learn something you can use in your everyday life. He wasn't just "easy" but he really challenged your mind to think differently. If you participate in class, presently, you will be very successful. Would definetley recommend.

Professor Cummings has been one of the best professors/teachers l've ever had. His class is low stress and I rly enjoyed going to every lecture as they are all very fascinating and relevant. (This was my class where the most people would attend). Definitely take this class! Even if you aren't interested in art history you will enjoy it.

She tries her best to make sense while lecturing which mostly ends up not making sense, so it was hard to understand her. I also felt like it wasn't very organized, leading me to feel confuse most of the time. I wouldn't really recommend
Good teacher!
not worf

Maybe she is a better teacher in person, but online she is one of the hardest professors I have had to work with. She puts in grades WEEKS late, and you hardly know what your grade is going to be up until the final week. She takes days to respond to you, and doesn't give clear homework assignments. Your better off with another online teacher!

Monica is a very sweet person and does care about her students learning experience, however, I don't think she is meant to be an online teacher. The entire semester basically nothing was graded, only when final grades went in did she grade all the old assignments. Pretty frustrating, and also you will not be able to reach her outside of class.

Had her for the last fall semester. All I can say is wow, you better learn how to teach yourself amd the person next to you. She clearly states this at the beggining of the semester and is simply the worst way of teaching. Be aware she also isnt very fluent in English types her own tests, which have problems that often make no sense. Run

Monica is a great person, but as a professor she's one of the worst. Gives out unclear homework assignments and then spends 90 min class going over them yelling at students for not doing their part. If 4 people did the assignment out of a 30 person COLLEGE class, maybe there's an issue with the assignment? Unresponsive to emails for clarification.

She doesn't give flashcards for her finals because she legit makes the study guide identical to her tests. Do the canvas assignments because she offers extra credit. Participate in her class and ask her questions. Legit she cares about her students & wants everyone to understand. She is sometimes late. Also she gives the class a small break midway!

She can be hard to understand because of her accent. Don't miss class, she goes through a lot in one session. Her studyguides are almost identical to her tests. She doesn't give hw or any for the most part but don't slack off. Take advantage of her office hours. Study for the test also the material and concepts will be repeated so get it down.

Monica is the best math professor ! I took stats in high school and I struggled and I took it again in college and passed with an A. She doesn't use the book at all, she does everything her own way but she focuses on one thing for a while so that everyone can understand it. She cares for her students and doesn't want anyone to struggle. Easy Final
she is a really good stats teacher but at times she can be unorganized but overall great class

I would really recommend not taking this class. So unorganized and you will find yourself being confused so often. By the end of the year there was like 15 people left because everyone dropped. She cares a lot about her students but I feel like she just confuses them way too much.

Monica is an awesome math professor if you have trouble understanding the topic. You really do need to go to class and actively participate in order to get good grades. If you do not take her class seriously she won't take you seriously. She can be unorganized so if you stay on top of it and are patient she will make sure you know enough to do well

Monica is different than other stats teachers because she doesn't focus on the math of stats but rather the critical thinking aspect. She challenges students to think for themselves. If you struggle with math or are only taking this class to transfer definitely take it with her, she cares about her students and makes it easy to pass if you try
good understanding person, but, frazzled and disorganized, late to class 99.99% of the time

Dabos is a great teacher who truly cares about her students. She is passionate about stats which makes her class interesting. She is an easy grader who accepts late work and lets her students have a flashcard on all her tests, including the final. I would recommend her class to anyone. She is GREAT :)

Dabos is like no other teacher you will ever have. She truly cares about all of her students, she is a sweetheart. I recommend her to anyone. Easy grader, late homework acceptable, flashcards on all tests, even finals are allowed :)

My transcript: 19 As and 1 F and this is the F. "Doesn't have a schedule" is the understatement of the century... I spent 10 times as much time trying to figure out what the assignments WERE than actually doing the assignments. Half way through the semester I just gave up. Retaking with a different professor in Spring.

Monica is a great professor if you understand that she isn't going to be like other teachers you have. She doesn't have a set schedule for classwork, which is hard if you want to get ahead. The book we were assigned was also almost never used. She really is passionate about having her students learn how to apply the material in real life.

I have a feeling a lot of people are going soft on her because she has a nice energetic personality. As far as actually teaching you statistics, it simply will not happen. She is all over the place, doing an equation on her own and talking about her life for 30 minutes then turning around and asking "SO DOES EVERYONE GET IT?. No, Monica. we don't.

No schedule. Assignments are very unclear and assigned by mentioning them in class. I've been there for every lectured but learned absolutely nothing. I've learned everything directly from the book. My classmates seem to be as lost as I am.
Monica is a great teacher. Very caring and helpful. I recommend her but unfortunately she may have moved to a college in LA. Big loss for SBCC.

One of the best teachers I've had at SBCC. If you get the chance, I highly recommend taking her! Relates material to real world issues and thus makes it super approachable. Not a brutal work load but challenging. She really wants you to succeed. Take the class, you probably won't find a better stats teacher.

Monica is by far the best math teacher I have ever had ! She is very enthusiastic and actually cares about student success. I hated statistics before I took her class and now I am considering it as a major. She makes the material easy to learn, but you must always do your homework and put in effort. If you do you will be guaranteed an A.

She most likely understand statistics better than any teacher at SBCC and probably a lot of other schools. She is extremely caring about her students and will not move forward unless the entire class understands the material she has taught. She is a very fair and forgiving teacher who has great teaching methods.
She is really nice, but she has a bit of an attitude, and doesn't really explain the material quite well
Great teacher, really cares about her students. Made math fun, if that's possible.
She is a great and funny teacher! Helps her students a lot!! If you don't have a problem with understanding an accent I highly recommend you to take this class!
She was difficult to understand and never at her tutoring hours on time. It was very hard to understand her as well. I would not recommend taking this class...

I'm not a math person, but I managed by in her class. Pretty challenging material, it's almost something else altogether than other math classes I've had. Make sure to go to every class, ask lots of questions, and DO all the homework. Group quiz's are weekly, so make sure to sit next to the smart people.

You can teach yourself OCHEM by watching Chad's prep and reading OCHEM as a second language by David Klein, so it's okay that most of Jess's lectures are pretty mid. One day, he let a hoodie-wearing skater kid attempt a kickflip for our whole class to get extra credit, and he also gives extra credit for writing chemistry puns on exams.
He's the freaking goat. Lower division and upper div chemistry requires you to study asynchronously. Try reading ahead prior to each subject. Openstax is free :D

Great Professor, he's super funny and made sure we were all on the same page before moving topics. I thought this was very considerate given it was a summer course and was pretty fast paced. He does take a long time to grade but will get it done!

Jess is a very nice and chill guy but his class is very disorganized and he lectures way to fast but he does allow test corrections for up to half a point back on each question which is really nice If you take him you will have to teach yourself most of the material but his grading policy is pretty lenient

Jess is super funny but too chill and it makes it hard to stay engaged. He usually teaches ochem which is prob why his chem 101 class is a bit disorganized. If u do good on the labs but bad on the tests u can still pass - chem is a hard subject but if u put in effort, u will be fine!

Professor Estrada is very funny and understanding but very unorganized. I believe he usually teaches organic chem only not intro to chem so he was just following what the previous teacher's agenda. He depended heavily on his TA to grade our assignments and labs but she never did. So I was super unsure what my grade was till the end Lab is critical

Very kind passionate professor. I just wish he was more hands-on during his labs and had a TA for lecs. The lecs were hard to understand. Wish he had taken more time to teach problems and perhaps given more in-class assignments to ensure understanding. He gives you a lot of assignments though so that way you can have that to balance your grade.

One of the worst professors I've ever had the misfortune of having. His tests are absurdly difficult and nothing like the homework. Test averages were almost always a C or lower and he had to insanely curve the final to avoid having more than half the class fail (says a lot about his way of "teaching"). I genuinely don't know how he was hired.

I took Jess is ochem class all the way from fall of 2018 to spring of 2019. It's sad to hear how worse Jess has got over the years.There chem department has a lot of problems teaching students. Jess was a great guy to be around with anytime I need help. Am disappointed how his recent students see him.

Jess's 101 class was his first time teaching it. Lectures are bland, confusing and taught with no flow. Assigns hw you can only do if you've read the entire chapter from the book (which he never mentioned) Don't expect to learn anything from the lectures. Horrible at using Canvas. Forget about knowing when the due dates are. Terrible test material.

This man is AWFUL at his job. His lectures don't have a beginning or end (no background info) and everyday is a struggle trying to make sense of it. Exams are hard because he literally lectures up until the day before the exam and expects you to know how to do it + more complicated stuff on the exam. Avoid, avoid, avoid!

Unprepared, unprofessional and lazy! Horrible teaching style, passive aggressive, would probably like to be anywhere but here. I hated this class with every fiber of my being. There's 2 exams, 1 final, few quizzed and HW assignments. Prepare to teach yourself the whole semester. Idek why he holds class. Even as a person I dislike him. Good luck.

Absolutely horrendous teacher. Doesn't explain anything and just writes numbers on board. Constantly forgetting resources for class such as a formula sheet for our midterm! The canvas website is always messed up, with assignments from months ago still ungraded and practice tests that deny access to everyone. For Chem110. Mean grade on exam was 54%!

Ochem classes are often soulless and impenetrable, but Jess' are the opposite. Best ochem prof one could ask for. Go to office hours. Tests can be hard, requiring many full mechanisms, but grading structure (ec, test corrections, hw) balances it out. Work hard to understand basics, or suffer. Book problems + office hours = success, simple as that!

Jess was about as good of an O chem professor you can get. I can't imagine taking this class and passing with anyone else. The lectures may be called "unstructured" but it's more the fact that students don't understand that you need to actually read the textbook before coming to class to be able to understand what's going on.

Gem of a human being. As someone who struggles academically, Jess inspires me to pursue a PhD. Lab is 2x a week. He works with you so lab reports are not as tedious. 222 is a bit more independent, so you will have to think harder to answer your own questions- jess still helps tho. KEEP THE LAB CLEAN!! You'll be fine- take jess! Trust.

Lab with Jess was amazing. I had the worst anxiety about lab from past experiences, but by the end of the semester, I would look forward to going to lab (and it was an 8am - I'm not a morning person). Very understanding and helpful. Do your part and you will succeed.

Jess is a very understanding and caring professor who listens to his students. You have to do your part and communicate- reaching out first and letting him know. He cares so much about the success of his students and if you have honest feedback, he will listen. Ochem is a difficult subject, but he does his best to make it fun.

Jess is very kind and at the same time stern professor. He expects you to do your part in the class. The way he set up the class grading system is very clear and lenient, if you do the work you get rewarded. His exams are a bit hard, but if you take the necessary time to practice the mechanisms he covers in lecture you should be fine.

The negative reviews come from people not prepared to take this course. If you do problems in the book you will do well. If you can't memorize the mechanisms by doing problems, then make flashcards. There is nothing hard in this course, don't be lazy. Jess is very approachable and understanding, he even bought us pizza in lab.

I believe the entire Chem department is WEAK. The biggest problem I have is that their lectures give basic details on the material and they don't have enough chem professors to effectively teach the material so students feel confident and prepared. Unfortunately, most of the time students have to rely on themselves to teach the material.

Jess isn't good at his job. His class is set up in a very unproductive manner and doesn't help you actively learn. So outside of the videos you HAVE to watch, you end up teaching yourself a lot of the material. Unfortunately he is the only ochem professor here, so unless you can take this class at a 4-year, get ready to put a lot of effort.
You'd be hard pressed to find a more genuine professor than Jess. I feel lucky to have taken the beast that is ochem with him. Much respect.

Jess's Lab was insanely fun and fairly easy if you put time into the lab write ups. He usually had you watch the lab and write up the procedure/info beforehand and then take the data down in class. Super easy and super fun.

Jess is a really funny and nice guy even though his lectures were decently disorganized he made me really enjoy taking OChem. The Lectures were online fully and you had to dedicate a decent amount of time to this class but that is expected. As long as you have time and care you should do well. Just an FYI its slightly hard to get him via email.

Had lecture online & half of the labs in person. Lecture was disorganized & fast. Office hrs only once a week & often cancelled, almost never answered emails the entire semester to any of us -so good luck if you need clarification on anything. Doesn't actually listen when u ask a question, gives poor explanation or tells you just to google it.

He's a nice man, and is very accessible if you ever reach out to him. Unfortunately, his lectures are very disorganized, and they do not transition well between topics. You can pass, but it's not easy, and you have to use a lot of external resources and the textbook to get the grade you want.

Organic chemistry is a nightmare class! Jess was really helpful considering this class is not usually done online. Due to COVID had to take online, labs would have been better in person. I would recommend taking this class with Jess because he was very understanding also hes hilarious! Hard material to understand.

Because of COVID this CHEM 104 class had to be transferred to a fully online course, which teaching chem and taking chem online is no easy task. Jess did a GREAT job making adjustments so that we could succeed. He kept it fun despite circumstances and was always available and responsive. Only thing I would say is watch extra videos on YouTube.

I just finished this class in the middle of Covid. Not an ideal situation to take a chem lab, but Jess made it work. Tough topic- lots of reading, supplemented with videoed lectures and labs. He's quick to respond by email to any questions. I wish we could have taken him in person to see hear his dad jokes and see the dance moves. He's hilarious!

This class definitely takes a lot of time with reading, watching the films, and writing the responses but it doesn't feel like a lot if you enjoy the subject. She really reads everything and gives thoughtful and constructive feedback. One of my favorite teachers ever and we never even met in person.

Helen is an amazing teacher! I took her online course this semester and enjoyed each assignment every week! Her assignments are thoughtful and the material insightful. You can tell by her comments that she actually takes the time to read the work you did. I would definitely take a course of hers again.

Online Class with a really good text. Every assignment she gave good feedback and positive, constructive critique. Professor definitely knows her stuff. The homework wasn't too much because a lot of it was watching film to analyze- and you were given lots of choices. I found two new favorites in her suggestion making the whole course a win.

This was the perfect course to ease into for a returning online student and mom. Professor Fey's choice of text is a joy to read, her lecture notes are short and sweet, the movies are awesome, and the weekly assignment is a pleasure to write. The class requires a bit of self-drive, but her feedback makes it all worth while and keeps you focused.

The textbook is really interesting and well written with a clear knowledge of what people want to study. Her teaching is wonderful, she is clear, a good grader, encouraging, and helpful. She personalizes the grading responses which I think is incredibly helpful. If my major wasn't nearly completed I would switch to FS just because of her.

This class is very interesting, but when it comes to the teacher there are no clear cut questions in her assignments. They are confusing and jumbled. You have to solve a puzzle of word problems to finally get to the assignment. She does not teach on campus either so you can never meet with her to discuss anything.
Awesome professor! The movies were all enjoyable & good! I actually learned a lot from this class!

I got to see so many great films and learned plenty of new things! She is a great teacher. She responds as soon as possible to any questions (although she is very clear about what she wants). Also, the feedback she gives you on assignments is always full length and really detailed. Take this class for an easy A and to ACTUALLY learn something.

Really great class. Online courses can be annoying but honestly Fey does a great job at keeping things engaging. You have to write around 800 words for each week and 1500 minimum for the 3 exams. Also she's very open to answering questions are grades quickly. Take her if possible!!
Follow up and do the assignments and use the information from the book and you will do well. Easy to forget about online classes, but it isn't hard to do one assignment per week. Fun class

Her online courses are excellent! The material is relevant and the films are thought-provoking (you must find a way to view them on your own though). She has very helpful feedback, unlike other online professors, and quickly responds to questions. Her classes are not easy, but you get a lot out of them!

Took this class for an easy GE. That's not what I got. She's definitely pretentious and arrogant. Thats being said, I think I learned more in that class than any of my GEs. She's a very good teacher and is extremely knowledgeable. I'm minoring in Film Studies because of what she taught me. Great class but don't take her for an automatic A.
Really cool person, she really knows the deal and does not have crazy expectations. Very chill class. Not bad at all.

If your fulfilling your GE she wouldn't be your best choice, but if you're a film major and somewhat ambitious about the subject, take her! Tests aren't that hard although there're short essays but she's easy going on grading. As long as the basic ideas are mentioned it's at least a B paper. So...if you expect to learn, take her!

Not a terribly hard film class. The quizzes come straight from the book, almost word for word. The films are interesting, especially ones exploring sexuality. One paper due as the final. Helen isn't a very hard teacher and provides thought-provoking discussions. She looks like a female version of Tim Gunn and her voice is just as soothing.

She's one of the best in film at CC, her lecture's always clear and professional. Some may think she's uptight but i'll call it objectiveness since art is not absolute anyone could easily attaach an opinion to it and that could be usually misleading. For non-major the class coulb be harder than you thought, but for some who love film. TAKE HER!!!
Fey is very knowledgeable and passionate about films but she can be very moody and picks her favorite students in class. She offers no extra credit. Don't take her class if you are not a film major!

Very knowledgable about the world of film past and present. Very interesting lectures, with a good combo. of the technical and scholoarly aspects of film. Shes no joke when it comes to tests, but they are really not that hard. Best overall film teacher I've had!!!
nice teacher. a little uptight but still has a very nice way about her. fun class.

Extremely knowledgeable in her field. I learned quite a bit while taking her Film Genres class & would recommend it to anyone. Used the BlueBook for midterm & final exams, wrote one paper on a film sub-genre that interests you & sometimes grades your film notes....so pay attention to the flicks!

Fey is awesome. Before each quiz just skim through the chapter and read all the bold terms, & u'll ace it...the 1st half of the semester be SURE 2 chug a coffee...the 2nd half of the semester is better b/c they are all movies that are actually FROM this century...big lebowski for instance. awesome class...easy, but u learn a lot...actually go tho!

Very interested in what she does. I would skim the required chapters literally 5 minutes before class and ace each quiz. Easy class and good movies. Values everyone's opinions, but sometimes she has these weirdo trick questions on her quizzes that kinda ticked me off. All in all, a good, and easy teacher. I'd recommend her.
shes a good teacher but i dont think shes the best ive ever had. She can be a fiesty one.

I don't know what reason makes her a bad Prof., i personally think she is one of the best Prof in SBCC. She gives informations direct and encourages student to give their opinions in class. Those who dislike her are definetly weirdos.

She is very nice person and in my opinion great teacher. When you read everything for class(which is not much) you score 10/10 on quizes. When you listen to her and when you are engaged in it, it's not hard. Really interesting class, I would take it again. People that write that she is not good don't do anything for class.
she is a hard core teacher but she is definately one of the best...
No Comments

I really enjoyed Mrs. Fey's class. i thought she was nice, helpful, and interesting. she made me almost want to be a film major, but mine was already chosen. Genre films were really cool and she helped to give me a lot of insight. everyone that said she is a "butthead", you guys don't know what's up, and are pretty stupid. she was one of my favorit

She is very good. I enjoyed her Genre class very much and learned a lot. Well organized, starts on-time, and explains things clearly. Lot of class interaction. Encourgaes u to participate. Grades papers fair, & tests are easy and fair. Don't expect an easy A, but if put in effort sure to get an A.

Fey is very informative teaching many great films. Shows every type of genre made and encourages discussion. Makes thought provoking and specific paper assignments that are actually enjoyable to write.
This is an online course with tons of reading and multiple lectures. The subject and class material is interesting. This class is packed it literally could be a 2 part course.
I really enjoyed learning from Professor Foss, who is a clear expert in her field and has much wisdom to share. Her knowledge is encyclopedic!

Pros: interesting topics, extra credit opportunities, open note exams Cons: information overload, fast talker, heavy workload, no late work, excessive video watching, never updates canvas or grades anything online, would be more suited for an online class (which she obviously prefers), not helpful or reachable, kinda cranky/bad attitude

Do not take in person. She literally took her online class structure and just goes over it in person. There are no slides or note taking guides or anything during lecture- you're basically just getting talked at and then expected to know everything for the exam.

The worst online professor I've ever had. She doesn't keep up with grades, her lectures are all old and material is ancient. She is impossible to get ahold of outside of her one hour a week office hours. Doesn't answer emails or canvas messages. It's December and I still don't know my grade.

Professor Foss possess an in depth knowledge of the subjects that she teaches, and sometimes even gives alternate narratives, as different historians report different views. Her feedback on papers was astute. She encouraged me to get a degree in higher education, which I did!

Prof. Foss gives clear instructions on what's expected. Keep track of the assignments you turn in online or in-person just incase. Her study guides are clear, and if you study for her exams, you will get the grade you want.

Professor Foss is so passionate about the subject and great to learn from, everything from in person and online is accessible to notes to recorded lectures. She gives extra credit weekly along with the regular work, be ready to watch some old videos and some books. It's a bit of work for a 101 class, but I learned so much!

Mrs. Foss was and is an exceptional individual for teaching this course. She cares about the success of all of her students and gives ample time to turn in assignments. Extra credit options are made available throughout the semester and she encourages interactive engagement amongst students(by means of topic discussions). She is wise and admirable.

Hard for her to be empathetic/understanding. Hard for her to respond to your emails. Very inaccessible outside of class hours. Took her days to respond to my very urgent email, I emailed more times like she said to but it didn't help. Out of my 7-9 emails the entire semester she responded 2-3 times.

She was a great teacher! She is passionate about the subject and tries hard to engage the students in the material. Her test questions are not found anywhere online, so tests require study. She gives a lot of extra credit opportunities and is a fair grader. She is willing to make exceptions, gives good lectures and class is never boring!

Content is interesting but Prof. Foss does not teach well through an online platform. Her lack of tech skills makes class sloppy and unorganized. Also, the chosen textbook is dry and unclear, while the videos tend to be very old. Basically, it's as if she is making us read/ watch things she used when she was in college herself. Needs to improve!

I took two of Tina's classes several years and I found them to be very interesting. It is important for someone to enjoy this subject to do well and like the class. Tina has a wealth of hands-on info. and interfaces on a personal level with the local native american community. I found it a source of dismay that some of these review were so critical

NATA 101 was an amazing course. Tina Foss has an extensive knowledge of all things Native American. Show up, do your reading and you will be fine. There are many extra credit opportunities to cushion your grade if you choose to take advantage of them.

Very boring class. I was excited to take this course however my excitement faded after the first two classes. We watched an outdated movie with awful audio nearly every class. She gives handouts that have been copied so many times you can hardly read them. I found her rather rude to students when they would ask for clarification on something.

Really nice teacher, definitely knowledgeable and passionate about what she's teaching, just bad at teaching. She's all over the place during most of her lectures, jumps from one topic to another so it's hard to take notes or keep up like 15 mins into class. I gave up and just relied heavily on extra credit (which there is A LOT of).
Awesome lectures, seriously best teacher I've had at SBCC so far!

She is a nice professor/ person. If you ask for help she will help out. I have to say I learned more by just studying the review sheets she gives us way before exams.. Little boring. Of course I was very interested in learning more NAS. EASY A as long as you study review sheets!!!!

Great class and great prof. I have a degree in Native American Studies from SBCC. One of the few profs who actually accommodates disabled students. Doesn't walk around the classroom because she is disabled, but really willing to talkhelp students who go up and ask ?s. Easy to pass as long as you go to class and memorize review terms.

I would not recommend taking this class. I found out all the informatin from google since the book doesnt really talk abut the terms from the study guide. I love native american history, but I would not wanna have a class again with Foss..

She does not post anything online so keep track of your grades yourself and keep all your handouts. Her lectures are very unclear and hard to follow, however she really knows her stuff and does review before the test. Boring class, but not hard to pass

I highly recommend taking Prof. Foss' NATA-101, because she exposes the true legacy of U.S. Tyranny(genocide and dis empowerment),perpetuated against the Native American peoples. The documented revisionists texts had granted me counter history (non Euro-centric) to analyze.Read the books, take notes, do extra credit, and study to acquire an A.

All she does is ramble on about things that have nothing to do with the subject at hand. She does know a lot, but she chooses not to teach it and instead go off on tangents about nothing that's has to do with anything. I would not take a class under her. Go elsewhere.

She is a very nice lady, BUT I would not recommend her class. She spends about 90% of the semester standing in front of the class speaking. By mid semester you'll notice the class size will be reduced by more than half. I guess people got tired of falling asleep in class!

The worst class I have ever taken at SBCC. She gives you all the information you need to get good grades but her worksheets are very old and hard to read. All the materials she uses for class are extremely old. She just sits in front of the class and speaks. Most boring class ever. I do not recommend it!

If you are interested in the subject matter, this class is GREAT. Tina is so smart and knows her material very well. She does not use powerpoint or "exciting" media, but she is an amazing lecturer and I loved listening to her stories. Multiple choice on the exams is easy... Essays are in depth. Great class overall!

The class overall is pretty easy if you read the material and study the study guide. She basically tells you everything that will be on the test, you just have to attend class. It can be a little boring at times, but if you're interested in Native American art it will be more enjoyable. You have to study quite a few hours per test to make an A/B.

Really nice lady, is funny at times. I enjoyed her class and learned a lot. I am actually taking another one of her classes again next semester because i liked it so much. the only bad thing is that it gets boring at times. other than that, she's great! highly recommend taking one of her classes.

Foss is ok. She never stays on subject though and she just sit in front of class which is boring when your sitting watching her ramble for an hour and half. Tangents aside she basically "spoon feeds you the answers" (HER WORDS) which is true. Just go for the study guide, study it, and you'll get an A.

Excellent teacher who knows her subject. Be able to write essays and term papers, attend class, and read. Material covered supplemented by videos unavailable elsewhere. She reviews all material on exams. Don't expect power point presentations, or to be entertained by a stand up comic. This is a College Course, not a High School class for Slackers.

Ms. Gardinali was very sweet to me, and (I believe) tried to help me the best she could. However, I could never learn a language in her classes format. There were about 20 quizzes per week, all of which with had new material (you could take them however many times you would like), and to writing pages of vocab + hand drawing pictures sucks.

The workload for this course was unusually heavy for a 101-level language class, and the expectations felt disproportionate for beginners. Instructions regarding required Coach sessions were unclear, which led me to miss the first two sessions. Based on this experience, I would not choose to take another class with this instructor.

The professor assigns 30 homework assignments a week, which is ridiculous. I took this class hoping for a fun, light, and enjoyable experience after a stressful semester, but I was mistaken. Instead, we create silly handwritten portfolios, excessive amounts of homework and there are no open-book exams. I wish the class was more enjoyable.

She is an awful teacher, she asked me to upload the requirements for week 1 tree times! This meaning that It was her fault that the second week was still locked. I did everything she told us, and just made matters worse on everything. I have not had this problem with other teachers. She is just not a good online class teacher!

There is no teaching. Class is all digital. A huge amount of work. Must write every vocabulary word at least once in a hardcopy notebook, and grammar rules , and create a silly life "storyboard" in your notebook with handwriting, pictures, etc.. You then have to scan and upload all those pages. There are about 20 digital quizzes per week.
Prepare to work

I took Italian 101 and 102 from Laura and greatly enjoyed both of them. Although she is a tough grader, she ensures that everyone has ample access to resources and practice tests (all of which look almost exactly the same as the real deal) before any big exam. It's apparent that she loves her job and cares about her students.

Laura is an amazing teacher. I have had her for Italian 101, 102 and 103. She is the absolute best! She is very friendly and helpful as well as understanding. She always makes class fun and entertaining. I would recommend her class to everyone! You can also take some French classes with her!... In case anyone wants to.

I have taken Italian 101, 102, and 103 with Laura. She is honestly the best! The class requires time and practice, but thats the usual with any new language. She explains everything clearly and theres a lot of in class group activities that are always fun. She really does help everyone out and wants all her students to do well.

Laura is thebest professor I have had at SBCC! First of all, you call her by her first name. She cares about her students doing well in class and makes class fun. You have to work and practice to do well in the class, but that's what all languages take. Every class, I wish it wouldn't end. She is the sweetest person, I wish we could be BFF's.

she is very uptight and is not helpful at all. she doest like to help students. she is very mean and dosent realize that students are stressed out too. she is late to class just about every class. one time she didnt show up to class and sent an email half way through the class saying she was unable to attend. i warn you. do not have her as a teache

Laura is such a sweet teacher with a good sense of humor! She also accepts late work. Because she is actually from Italy, she knows about culture. I really looked forward to attending every class because every class was interesting, engaging, and funny! I got to know all my peers and we were like a family. I will for sure be taking Italian 102!

Laura by far was my favorite teacher this semester! My class was fun & engaging, we were like a family. She was so helpful and always there to answer your questions. She is interested in you learning and understanding the language more than anything. The class was a lot of work but, I feel like I actually can speak and understand the language now.

Laura is by far the best prof I've had. She is such a sweet person and really engages students when she teaches. Took 4 semesters with her and loved every second. A lot of work, yes, but that's what learning a language takes. Very clear and helpful- I always looked forward to Laura's classes- wish there were more!

Laura is is the coolest teacher I've had this semester. She is friendly, informative, and helpful. I looked forward to going to class everyday. She's pretty lenient with attendance, and late work. Just make sure you turn in your workbooks. And please actually use the Online Italian Lab. It totally helps. I highly recommend taking her class.

Excellent class & sweet, supportive, fun prof. Languages require work, build as you go along & there is a point in learning any language where you have to grapple with it without things being translated for you, which made it pretty challenging, but doable. I've taken several languages and found Gardinali's style of teaching highly effective.

She is a sweetheart she wants you to actually do well if you get lower then a C she lets you go to a tutor to get help & turn it in Im not saying this class is easy its pretty hard one thing i hated was she would put a word on the board in italian but wouldnt put in english she expected us to know it right then also the book is not user friendly
Laura is awesome you really learn a lot from her class! you defiantly have to work for an A and pay attention so you can learn! but she is awesome and the class was fun!

She's really nice however her assignments are really unclear. She's also from Italy so many of the concepts she's teaching in class may not seem as difficult to her, but really they are. She often put a word on the board, told you the definition, and then just expected you to know the word without any practice.
Great teacher, but you need to work hard to get a good grade.I would blank out most of the time in class, but thats my fault. All in all, good teacher.
Laura is such a sweetheart!! I have learned more in three months than I did in five years of middle/high school Spanish. Italian is incredibly fun and she makes the class simple to understand.

Really sweet person and entertaining lecturer. Class has a ton of work! She really cares about her students learning. I liked her a bit more when I had her for 101 at the beginning of high school, now that I'm in college and more serious about my major, I'm less enthused by the class work load.

If you're taking Italian at SBCC your options are limited to her and 1 other. She is a sincerely nice person BUT this was the hardest class I've had yet at CC. I have a 4.0 and granted I did get an A in her class but you really have to work for it. Very tough if this is your first foreign language class. Starts easy, gets really hard by the end.
Excellent! Makes learning language fun and interesting. I wish all teachers were like Laura. She is the best!
i love her. she is so sweet and a very good teacher. i've been taking italian with her for the past 2 semesters and am going to continue taking it next year. i definately recommend her!

i must say that i am having a hard time in this class...she has not spoken english yet in this class...i came into this class not knowing anything...b4 u start this class you better know a good bit of french...she won't stop for anyhting
she has been the best french teacher i have ever had. mayeb it's college, but i am learning quite quickly and everything is sticking. and she is multi-lingual. what more can i say?

Best language teacher I have ever had. Really made class interesting and fun. Come prepared; do your exercizes and you will really learn the language. She is very understanding and sweet. Don't except and easy A but as long as you participate and do most of your work you are in the clear. DEFINITELY WOULD RECOMMEND THIS PROFESSOR!
wonderful
strongly disagree! BORING class!!
I took his class, and I can't emphasize how great a human being he is. I love the way he teaches, and he's willing to help. I enjoyed all of his courses. He's the best professor ever!!

Gomez has honestly been the best teacher I've ever had. He's very good at teaching and will help you in whatever way you need it. He uses real life scenarios to make learning more interesting, and makes Chemistry fun to learn.
10/10 such a good prof! Would definitely take his class again. He's super nice and if you ever need extra help just go to his advisory :)
Professor Gomez is splendid at teaching.

He doesn't do canvas at all, like he doesn't even have a page. I mean if that's your thing you will like him. But he just talks and talks the whole time, his writing is very small and messy can't read it. If you want to know your grade you have to go to him directly, you have surprise quizzes. But again if that is your thing then add his class.

He is a character! One story to sum him up was he had emailed us 7 different articles with very little instruction on what to do with all the reading. I had gone up in class to ask him and he had me send him back an email to forward a 2nd time to the class because he forgot he had sent it. He is passable and a good stories in long lectures.

Philosophy major at this college, took him last semester. He seems like a genuine guy, but his lectures are not at all engaging or focused. Constant slip up of student names, due dates, and even philosophical content (ex: mixing up teleological vs deontological ethics) makes it seem as if he himself is just not present.

Professor Greene is the one of the coolest, kindest, most laid back, and funny teachers I've had this semester. Although the structure of his class could use some improvement and his lectures can be long and boring, as long as you pay attention somewhat and take notes, you should be fine. Our grade was based off a midterm, a final, and a term paper
Great teacher. Very kind. Highly recommend!

Phillip Greene is a great teacher, and I enjoyed his class thoroughly! Participate! It makes class so much more fun. The class structure isn't my favorite, you split into groups and discuss the reading and homework that week, but it ultimately doesn't matter as long as you study and take your own notes for the mid term and final.

(1) If you decide to take a philosophy class, PLEASE do not take Greene. He is just awful. He divides the class into groups, that you are stuck with for the entirety of the semester, and a majority of your discussions on the book and your homework is with them. So if you get a bad group (people who don't read or do the homework) you're in trouble

Greene was a nice guy and genuinely enjoyed lecturing philosophy, nothing about the class was hard except staying awake. Its a long class and sometimes its hard to hear about philosophy for so long! The hw you could skim the chapter and find the answers easy. Final was just some essay questions.

It was an alright class. If you're not prepared on a late monday class, don't take it. Small homework every week, and the midterm and final is in essay form. Pretty easy if you study. Let's you out about 30 min - 1 hour early, but still, it was such a boring class and I wouldn't have survived without making doodles in my book. Easy class though!

Phillip Greene was a phenomenal teacher. He has immense knowledge of the material and the class is set-up in a straight forward way enabling you to succeed without worry. If you show up every week, you can easily pass.

I loved his teaching style. He is very laid back and willing to work with students individually. He gives a light amount of homework and few tests. I did not find his class boring however, typically a three hour long class does tend to get tedious at times. But I did not find that to be a reflection on him. He is my favorite professor to date.
